title: A kávé készítésmódja (Coffee Preparation)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kávé
- forró víz
- tejszín

## Directions

1. A kávét bezárható, tengelye körül forgó kávéhengerben pergeljük, míg gesztenyebarna lesz, és izzadni kezd
2. Azonnal nagy, tiszta papírra öntjük, és gyorsabb meghűlés végett szétsimítjuk
3. Ha egészen kihűlt, tálba teszszük és könnyedén felrázzuk, hogy finom héja, mely a pergelés által a kávétól elvált, elrepüljön
4. A kávét megőröljük
5. Négy csészére hét dekagrammot veszünk az őrlött kávéból
6. A kávét a masinába teszszük, és a zúzóval lenyomjuk
7. Aztán csészényi forró vizet öntünk reá, és rögtön befedjük
8. Ha a víz keresztül szűrődött, még négy csésze forró vizet öntünk reá
9. Hat perc múlva a kávé átfolyt
10. Ha keresztül szűrődött, ezüstkanállal kell megkavarni és rögtön csészékbe tölteni

title: A Luby-sült (Luby's Roast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beef sirloin (500 grams), lean
- pork meat (500 grams), somewhat fatty, preferably from the shoulder
- veal sirloin (500 grams)
- salt
- fat (egg-sized piece)
- red onion (6 heads), sliced
- weak vinegar (1 glass)
- sugar (1 lump)
- small potatoes, whole
- bacon slices

## Directions

1. Take half kilo of lean beef sirloin and beat it as thin as possible with a mallet, being careful not to shred it but keeping it whole and nicely thin
2. Take half kilo of pork meat that is somewhat fatty, preferably cut from the shoulder, and carefully beat it flat without tearing it
3. Take half kilo of veal sirloin and beat it flat in one piece
4. When all three pieces of meat are prepared this way, salt them and place on a board with the beef slice on the bottom, the pork slice on the beef, and the veal on top
5. When stacked nicely, roll them together tightly so the meat takes on a cylinder shape
6. Tie it tightly around with clean, flat string to keep it from falling apart during cooking
7. Lard the smooth side of the cylinder with bacon slices in two rows
8. Place an egg-sized piece of fat in a pot and when melted, place the meat lengthwise in it
9. Add six sliced red onion heads and a glass of weak vinegar
10. Cover the pot and place it over even, strong heat
11. When the broth reduces but the meat is not yet browning, remove from heat and strain the onion broth through a sieve
12. Put it back in the pot with a lump of sugar and return the meat to it
13. Cover again and roast over high heat until the meat begins to brown slightly, being careful that the fat does not burn as this would ruin the pleasant flavor of the roast
14. When nicely browned, remove from the pot and remove the string
15. Cut into nice round slices, arrange on a long dish, and surround completely with browned small whole potatoes

title: A Puddingokra és Tálba Főttekre Való Tej Mártás (Milk Sauce for Puddings and Boiled Dishes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of good sweet milk
- vanilla
- ten egg yolks
- ten tablespoons of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Boil one liter of good sweet milk with a piece of vanilla
2. Put ten egg yolks and ten tablespoons of powdered sugar in a narrow tall dish
3. Beat them with a wooden spoon until the egg and sugar become foamy, which takes about half an hour
4. When the milk is hot, slowly pour it into the sugared egg while continuously stirring with a wooden spoon
5. Keep at an equal warm temperature until serving but not boiling and continuously stir with the spoon, because if placed in a very hot place and the egg milk mixture is left unstirred, it will curdle uselessly
6. Therefore pay great attention to preparing the sauce and make it only shortly before serving
7. If by chance there is not enough egg for this sauce, the foamy beaten egg and sugar can be added

title: Agyvelő-leves (Brain Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole calf brain
- beef broth
- butter
- ten to fifteen button mushrooms (champignons), coarsely chopped
- little finely chopped parsley
- half a cooking spoon flour
- crushed nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook one whole calf brain in beef broth
2. When cooked, strain through a sieve
3. Warm a piece of butter and braise ten to fifteen coarsely chopped button mushrooms with a little finely chopped parsley for a while
4. Sprinkle half a cooking spoon of flour over it and braise a little longer
5. Add the brain and pour good beef broth over it
6. A little crushed nutmeg may be added
7. Bring everything to a boil
8. Serve over brain dumplings or over fried, lengthwise-sliced bread

title: Agyvélő-tört (Brain Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two calf brains
- salt
- water
- butter
- fresh parsley
- onion
- pepper
- rusk
- beef broth
- two whole eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the calf brains in salted water and clean completely from fibrous parts
2. Chop finely
3. Melt a piece of butter and add chopped fresh parsley and finely chopped onion, browning slightly
4. Add the brain, a little salt, a little ground pepper, and one or two rusks crumbled fine, the quantity of brain should be about twice that of rusk
5. Pour on a little beef broth and cook until reduced, then cool
6. Mix in two whole eggs and place in a buttered mold
7. Set in a water bath and keep at even heat for half an hour
8. Meanwhile, prepare thin veal cutlets the size and shape of mulberry leaves using a wide knife
9. Soak the knife frequently in water to shape them easily
10. Once all are prepared and salted, coat well with melted butter, sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs
11. Place on a buttered pot lid and bake until nicely brown
12. When serving, turn the brain paste onto a round dish so the pieces overlap, with one edge resting on the brain to form a crown, or cut half-moons from butter pastry using a medium pastry cutter, coat with egg white, fry until golden brown and arrange around the turned-out brain paste glazed with good glaze
13. If the paste is made from two calf brains, use four eggs

title: Ajak-kenőcs (Lip Salve)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven dekagrams fresh pork fat
- four and one-half dekagrams white wax
- two dekagrams jasmine oil
- twelve grams beef marrow
- twenty drops of clove oil

## Directions

1. Melt seven dekagrams of fresh pork fat, four and one-half dekagrams of white wax, two dekagrams of jasmine oil, and twelve grams of beef marrow over gentle heat
2. Remove the mixture from the heat once melted
3. Add twenty drops of clove oil

title: Alma hálóköntösben (Apple in Nightgown)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Crumbly pastry
- Five or six tart apples
- 9 dekagram sugar
- A little ground vanilla
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare crumbly pastry and roll it out to the thickness of a knife
2. Cut rectangular slices from it
3. Peel the apples, cut them into cubes, put them in a pot
4. Add the sugar and a little ground vanilla and stew them well
5. Brush the edges of the pastry with egg white
6. Put as much filling in the center as the pastry can hold
7. Fold up the four corners of the pastry and glue them together with egg white
8. Brush with egg white, bake until beautifully golden yellow
9. Sprinkle with sugar and serve to the table

title: Alma hálóköntösben befőttel töltve (Apple in Nightgown Filled with Preserves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Buttered pastry
- Small masánszki apples
- One or two peeled apples
- Sugar
- Preserved currants or cherries
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare buttered pastry
2. Peel small apples and hollow out their cores and stems
3. Stew one or two peeled apples with sugar, press through a sieve
4. Mix with preserved currants or cherries
5. Fill the hollowed apples with this mixture
6. Cut a round leaf from another apple and place on top
7. Roll out the buttered pastry to the thickness of a knife
8. Cut rectangular pieces from it
9. Brush the corners with egg yolk
10. Place the filled apple in the center
11. Fold the four brushed corners over it
12. Brush with egg, bake until beautifully golden yellow like a roll

title: Alma sűrített lével (Apple with Concentrated Juice)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- six masanszki apples
- sugar
- three spoonfuls fine flour
- six egg yolks
- ten dekagrams crushed sugar
- a little vanilla
- four decilitres heavy cream
- five egg whites
- three spoonfuls apricot syrup
- ten dekagrams finely crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Cut six masanszki apples into thin slices, sprinkle well with sugar, and place in a porcelain or silver pan
2. In another pan, mix three spoonfuls fine flour smoothly with six egg yolks, add ten dekagrams crushed sugar and a little vanilla, thin it with four decilitres heavy cream, which is gradually mixed smoothly with the flour and egg and cook with constant stirring until it becomes a paste
3. Pour it over the apples and bake in the oven for a quarter hour
4. During this time, beat five egg whites stiffly with three spoonfuls apricot syrup and ten dekagrams finely crushed sugar, beat foamily
5. Make a crown with finger width and same height on the concentrated juice so that it is only visible in the middle, smooth the crown nicely with a knife, make deep cuts with a coffee spoon handle so it looks nicely braided, sprinkle with fine sugar, and place in a cooled oven
6. Serve to the table after half an hour

title: Alma vajastészta-szalagokkal (Apples with Puff Pastry Strips)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- several masanszki apples
- three to four more apples
- five dekagrams citronade
- seven dekagrams sugar
- a good spoonful of preserved apricot
- water
- egg white
- sugar
- narrow puff pastry strips
- preserved sour cherry with stem

## Directions

1. Peel several masanszki apples and hollow them out as deeply as possible so that the apple is empty but does not pierce through, it must have a thin bottom
2. Peel three to four more apples and cut them into cubes, chop five dekagrams citronade finely
3. In a pan, stew seven dekagrams sugar and a good spoonful of preserved apricot with the apple, citronade and a little water until it becomes marmalade
4. Fill the hollowed apples with this filling, brush with egg white, sprinkle with sugar, and wrap with narrow puff pastry strips like a spiral, brush the strips with egg white and bake nicely
5. Pile high on the plate, sprinkle with sugar and place on each one a preserved sour cherry with stem instead of a stem

title: Alma- vagy Körtekompót Más Módon (Apple or Pear Compote Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- apples or pears
- water
- lemon juice
- sugar
- currant or raspberry syrup (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the apples or pears, cut them in half, remove the seeds
2. Stew them with a glass of water, the juice of one lemon, and a piece of sugar
3. If you have currant or raspberry syrup, add 2 spoonfuls of it
4. Place the apples or pears on a dish and pour the liquid over them

title: Alma-hajkenő (Apple Hair Pomade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- good quality apples, unpeeled, cut into small pieces, with cores removed
- large raisins, cut into small pieces
- seven dekagrams each of apple and raisins
- twenty-eight dekagrams fresh unsalted butter
- seven dekagrams yellow wax

## Directions

1. Cut the apples unpeeled into small pieces, removing only the cores
2. Cut the large raisins into small pieces
3. In a crucible, cook seven dekagrams of apple and seven dekagrams of raisins with twenty-eight dekagrams of fresh unsalted butter and seven dekagrams of yellow wax with continuous stirring over gentle heat until a drop thrown onto glowing coals no longer sizzles
4. Press the pomade into a rose-water bottle and store for use

title: Alma-lepény (Apple Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Crumbly pastry
- Apples
- Broken sugar
- Little vanilla
- Fourteen dekagrams softened almonds
- Fine broken sugar
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare crumbly pastry and roll out to knife-back thickness
2. Place on a large flat plate
3. Peel apples and cut into thin slices
4. Densely line the cake bottom with apple slices
5. Sprinkle well with broken sugar and little vanilla
6. Sprinkle fourteen dekagrams softened and finely chopped almonds on the apples
7. Again sprinkle fine broken sugar
8. Place another sheet of pastry on top, no thicker than the first
9. Press together well so the two layers do not separate
10. Brush the top with egg white
11. Bake in steady heat
12. Alternative method: cut apples into small cubes, mix almonds with skin, sugar and vanilla together, and add twenty-eight dekagrams raisins

title: Alma-mártás (Apple Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three tart apples
- butter
- flour
- meat broth
- lemon zest
- lemon juice
- sugar
- fresh cream
- Liebig meat extract (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the tart apples and cut them into thin slices
2. Braise until soft in a little butter
3. Sprinkle a spoon of flour into the apples and brown it
4. Pour a spoonful of broth over it
5. Add the zest of half a lemon cut lengthwise, a little lemon juice, and enough sugar to make it sweet enough
6. Finally add two spoons of fresh cream, bring to a light boil and the sauce is ready
7. A teaspoon of Liebig meat extract gives an excellent flavor to this and other meat sauces

title: Alma-rétes (Apple Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- eggs
- butter
- warm water
- melted butter
- 4 large sour peeled apples
- 100 grams soaked broken almonds
- 100 grams sifted sugar
- 140 grams cleaned raisins
- breadcrumbs
- cinnamon
- candied lemon peel

## Directions

1. Prepare ordinary strudel dough with flour, eggs, a little butter, and warm water
2. Cover with a warm pot and let rise
3. Roll out as thinly as possible and brush with melted butter
4. Finely dice 4 large sour peeled apples
5. Mix 100 grams soaked broken almonds with 100 grams sifted sugar
6. Clean and wash 140 grams raisins and dry with cloth
7. On the rolled and brushed dough, first scatter apples, then almonds, sugar, raisins, and finally a little breadcrumbs
8. Gently fold together and shape into strudel
9. Brush with melted butter and place in a flat buttered pan
10. Bake until done; cinnamon and candied lemon peel may be added to the filling

title: Alma-torta (Apple Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-one dekagrams of fine flour
- fourteen dekagrams of finely ground sugar
- finely chopped lemon zest
- twenty-one dekagrams of butter
- one whole egg and one egg yolk
- sour cream as needed for moistening the dough
- five to six good large apples
- half a coffee cup of water
- a piece of sugar
- a little ground vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix together finely the flour, finely ground sugar, and finely chopped lemon zest.
2. Work in twenty-one dekagrams of butter.
3. Add one whole egg and one egg yolk.
4. Add enough sour cream to moisten the dough, which should not be very hard.
5. Work it together with a wide knife.
6. Fold it three times as you would puff pastry.
7. Peel five to six good large apples and cook them with half a coffee cup of water, a piece of sugar, and a little ground vanilla until they become a good marmalade.
8. Roll out the dough to knife-thickness and place on a cake sheet.
9. Spread the cooled apples on it in finger-thickness.
10. Make two finger-wide strips of dough around the edge to prevent the apples from running out.
11. Weave the entire cake with small dough rods.
12. Coat with egg white, sprinkle with sugar, and bake for three-quarters of an hour over lasting heat.

title: Almakompót (Apple Compote)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large uniform apples
- peach syrup
- water
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Peel large uniform apples, leaving the stem on
2. Dilute 2-3 tablespoons of peach syrup with water, diluting it enough for stewing the apples
3. Add a piece of sugar and squeeze in the juice of one lemon
4. Add the apples, bring to a boil without letting them fall apart
5. Place on a dish and add the sauce beneath

title: Almapép (Apple Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 tart apples
- sugar
- 2 crumbly bread rolls
- cream
- 90 grams finely ground almonds
- 90 grams finely ground sugar
- 1 lemon zest
- 70 grams butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, chop into small pieces and fry with sugar until it becomes a paste
2. Grate the bread rolls and moisten with cream
3. Take finely ground almonds and finely ground sugar
4. Chop lemon zest into small pieces
5. Whip the butter until foamy
6. Add the whole eggs and egg yolks one by one, beating together
7. Add the apple paste with the other ingredients and beat for half an hour
8. Finally fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
9. Pour the paste into a buttered mold sprinkled with fine breadcrumbs
10. Bake slowly
11. This pudding must bake somewhat longer than ordinary pudding, since the apple contains more moisture than the other mixture

title: Almapép franczia módra (Apple Pudding French Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10-12 peeled apples
- 3-4 tablespoons peach marmalade
- 100 grams finely ground and thinned sugar
- 140 grams rice
- 100 grams softened almonds
- 700 milliliters cream
- 70 grams butter
- sugar and ground vanilla
- 1 whole egg
- 5 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Cut the peeled apples into slices the thickness of a finger
2. Add peach marmalade and thinned sugar to the apples
3. Cook gently with repeated shaking until soft, but keep them whole and maintain their shape
4. Coarsely crush the rice and similarly the softened almonds
5. Rinse the rice and cook both in cream, stirring constantly until thick
6. Remove from heat and stir in butter while still warm
7. Cool and add enough sugar and vanilla to sweeten adequately
8. Whip the paste with one whole egg and five egg yolks for a quarter hour with continuous stirring
9. Finally fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
10. Pour half the paste into a buttered pot
11. Distribute the apple slices, placing less attractive ones evenly on the filled mixture
12. Pour the remaining paste over them
13. Bake slowly
14. Keep the remaining apple slices warm and cook the syrup until thick
15. Pour this syrup over the baked pudding when turned out onto a plate
16. Form a double ring from the apple slices, sprinkle with sugar and serve quickly to the table

title: Almaszeletek pistáciával (Apple Slices with Pistachios)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams butter
- flour for five-times beaten puff pastry
- apple marmalade
- fine sugar
- preserved peaches
- finely chopped pistachios

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry by beating butter five times with flour, rolled to knife-edge thickness
2. Place the entire rolled-out pastry on a baking sheet
3. Spread apple marmalade on it to half-finger thickness
4. Sprinkle with fine sugar and bake in a good hot oven
5. After baking, spread preserved peach on the apple surface
6. Sprinkle with finely chopped pistachios
7. Return to the oven for two minutes
8. Once completely cooled, cut into pieces three fingers wide and four fingers long
9. Sprinkle with fine sugar and serve on the table in neat order

title: Almaszeletek Pistáeziával (Apple Slices with Pistachio)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm butter
- apple marmalade
- fine sugar
- preserved peach

## Directions

1. Make a puff pastry turned five times from 28 dekagramm butter
2. Roll it to knife-thickness
3. Place the entire rolled dough on a sheet
4. Coat with apple marmalade to a half-finger thickness
5. Sprinkle with fine sugar
6. Bake in a good hot oven
7. Coat the apple surface with preserved peach

title: Almaszeletek spanyol fánkokkal (Apple Slices with Spanish Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams butter
- 17 dekagrams flour
- 10 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 9 dekagrams softened almonds
- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1 additional egg yolk
- 3 tablespoons thick cream
- Masánszki apples
- sugar
- 1 tablespoon preserved peach
- 3 egg whites
- 16 dekagrams sugar
- a little vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix butter, flour, and finely ground sugar well together
2. Crush the softened almonds and hard-boiled egg yolks to crumbs
3. Work these together with the butter mixture using a rolling pin until it becomes dough
4. Add one more egg yolk and three tablespoons thick cream, working well with a wide knife
5. Roll the dough to knife-edge thickness and cut into strips the length of a small span and two fingers wide
6. Place on a baking sheet, brush with sugared water, and bake at gentle heat
7. Peel Masánszki apples and cut into thin wedges
8. Cook the apple wedges with sugar and one spoonful of preserved peach into marmalade with continuous stirring
9. Draw shapes on parchment paper matching the dough strips but slightly narrower
10. Prepare Spanish fritter dough from three egg whites, 16 dekagrams sugar, and a little vanilla
11. Fill a pastry tube with this dough and pipe three fine strips lengthwise along the drawing, not at the edges so they don't expand too much during baking
12. Pipe cross-strips to create a lattice pattern
13. Sprinkle with fine sugar and place in a cooled oven to dry but remain white
14. Once dry enough to lift from the paper, spread apple marmalade knife-edge thickness on the slices
15. Top with the lattice made from Spanish fritter dough before serving

title: Almond Aspic (Mandola-kocsonya)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams sweet almonds
- 6 bitter almonds
- 3 liters water
- 1 liter well water
- 28 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 4.5 dekagrams isinglass, boiled down to 5 spoonfuls

## Directions

1. Pour 3 liters of water over 14 dekagrams sweet almonds and 6 bitter almonds and let stand 24 hours, after which they can be easily peeled.
2. Alternatively, if time is short, place in hot water to peel, then place in fresh water for a while to harden and make them brittle.
3. Remove, dry with clean cloth, and pound to paste in mortar.
4. Place the ground almonds in a porcelain pan, pour 1 liter of well water over them, and stir finely with silver spoon.
5. Strain through cloth, pour the strained liquid back over the almonds, and strain again, pressing with greatest force until all liquid is extracted.
6. Beat this almond milk with 28 dekagrams finely ground sugar.
7. Mix in 4.5 dekagrams isinglass that has been boiled down to 5 spoonfuls.
8. Strain again through cloth, pour into molds, and place on ice.

title: Almond Aspic Without Isinglass (Mandola-kocsonya vizahólyag nélkül)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two-thirds the amount of almond aspic
- 4 fresh egg whites
- isinglass (omitted)

## Directions

1. Prepare two-thirds of the almond aspic recipe without isinglass.
2. Whip 4 fresh egg whites in a brass bowl into stiff peaks.
3. Pour the almond aspic into the whites.
4. Whisk vigorously with a whisk and continue whisking over a hot basin of embers until the aspic mixes with the snow, becomes shiny and thick.
5. Immediately remove from heat, pour into small glasses or aspic bowls, and freeze on ice.
6. Once completely frozen, serve in the bowl without unmolding.

title: Almás étel más módon (Apple Dish Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- white house bread
- good wine
- crushed sugar
- butter
- finely chopped apple slices
- sugar
- chopped raisins
- small grapes
- almond
- lemon zest
- crushed cinnamon

## Directions

1. Moisten fine crumbs grated from white house bread with good wine, sprinkle in a few spoonfuls of crushed sugar
2. Place a finger-thickness layer of moistened crumbs in a butter-greased pan, dot with small pieces of butter, then place finely chopped and well-sugared apple slices on it
3. Finally place finely chopped raisins, small grapes, almond, lemon zest and crushed cinnamon, then repeat all of this and so on until the pan is full
4. Place in the oven and bake for three-quarters of an hour, turn out onto a plate and sprinkle with sugar

title: Amber Water
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 liters 70° purified alcohol
- 17 deciliters water
- 8 grams amber
- 14 dekagrams lemon peel
- 14 dekagrams cinnamon
- 6 grams vanilla
- 8 grams bergamot oil
- 10 drops rose oil

## Directions

1. Pour alcohol and water over crushed spices
2. Digest
3. Add fragrant oils

title: Ananász Czukorban (Pineapple in Sugar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram pineapple
- half kilogram finely ground and sifted sugar
- beef bladder for sealing
- clean paper

## Directions

1. For half a kilogram of fruit, the same amount of finely ground and sifted sugar is needed.
2. The bottles needed for this must be clean and dry; sprinkle sugar on the bottom of the jar to the thickness of a knife blade, peel the pineapple, and cut into slices of knife-blade thickness.
3. Place a layer of pineapple on the first layer of sugar, cover this again with ground sugar, place pineapple again, and so on until all is arranged.
4. Cover thickly with sugar on top, and seal well with beef bladder, under which you place clean paper, and set in a place well exposed to the sun, leaving it there until the sugar is completely dissolved and the juice becomes clear and transparent.
5. Then place in a cool, dry location.

title: Ananász-fagylalt (Pineapple Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapple
- 42 dekagrams pineapple syrup
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 4 deciliters water
- 3 lemons, juice

## Directions

1. Peel the pineapple and cut the finest flesh into pieces
2. Cook with a little water until it becomes completely syrup, then strain through a sieve
3. Cook 42 dekagrams of strained syrup with the same amount of sugar and 4 deciliters of water until the sugar is well dissolved
4. Pour into the syrup
5. Squeeze the juice of 3 lemons into it
6. Mix well and treat it as instructed in the previous section

title: Ananász-Kocsonya (Pineapple Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two large, ripe pineapples
- eight deciliters water
- thirty-five dekagrams sugar
- fourteen dekagrams sugar
- two deciliters water
- juice of three lemons
- one and one-quarter dekagrams dissolved fish glue

## Directions

1. Peel the two large ripe pineapples and crush the peel in a mortar
2. Pour eight deciliters water over it
3. Dissolve in a brass pan and strain
4. In this liquid cook thirty-five dekagrams sugar until it becomes invisible
5. Cut the pineapple into thin slices
6. Cook fourteen dekagrams sugar in two deciliters water until it begins to thicken
7. Place the slices into this syrup, bring to a boil for a few minutes
8. Set aside and let cool
9. Strain off the juice and mix with the already-cooked sugar
10. Add the juice of three lemons and one and one-quarter dekagrams dissolved fish glue
11. After mixing everything well, strain through a taut kitchen cloth
12. When the entire aspic mass is clear, pour two centimeters height into a mold that has been strongly packed in ice
13. Let it freeze, then arrange half of the pineapple slices on top
14. Pour over half a coffee cup of unfrozen juice, which must then freeze to hold the pieces in place
15. Pour aspic juice over it, and when it has frozen, arrange the second half of the pineapple slices on top
16. When these are also frozen as the first ones were, pour the final part of the aspic over it
17. Cover the whole with a tin plate loaded with ice and keep in the ice until, with care and speed, you turn it out onto the table

title: Angol Kocsonya (English Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagramm sugar
- 4 oranges
- 4 lemons
- 14 egg yolks
- 2 deciliter French champagne
- 8 egg whites
- 1 tablespoon rum
- 2 dekagramm dissolved isinglass
- almond oil
- large Spanish pastries

## Directions

1. Rub half of the sugar on an orange and the other half on a lemon
2. Squeeze the juice from 4 oranges and 4 lemons and strain through cloth
3. Crush the sugar and place in a pot and whisk together with the egg yolks, squeezed juice, and 2 deciliter French champagne
4. Place over charcoal fire and stir continuously until it becomes a thick mass
5. Meanwhile beat 8 egg whites into stiff peaks
6. Remove the pot from the coals and when the mixture begins to boil, fold in the beaten egg whites
7. Add 1 tablespoon of rum and 2 dekagramm dissolved isinglass
8. When cooled, pour into a deep mold that has been previously greased with pure almond oil
9. Place in crushed ice and freeze completely
10. Turn out onto a plate lined with a napkin and surround the edge of the jelly with large Spanish pastries arranged in a circle

title: Angol Kocsonya (Hopsz) (English Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- forty-two dekagrams sugar
- four oranges
- four lemons
- fourteen egg yolks
- two deciliters French champagne
- eight egg whites

## Directions

1. Rub one half of forty-two dekagrams sugar on an orange and the other half on a lemon
2. Squeeze out the juice from four oranges and four lemons
3. Strain the juice through cloth
4. Crush the sugar and place in a pot
5. Beat together with fourteen egg yolks, the squeezed juice, and two deciliters French champagne
6. Place on charcoal fire and stir continuously until it becomes a thick mass
7. In the meantime, beat eight egg whites into stiff snow
8. Remove the pot from the coals

title: Angol puncs (English Punch)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- jó kilogramm czukor
- másfél liter víz
- két czitrom sárgája finoman lehámozva
- két narancs sárgája finoman lehámozva
- egy palaczk rum
- egy palaczk fehér bor
- egy palaczk vörös bor
- négy czitrom leve
- négy narancs leve

## Directions

1. Jó kilogramm czukrot másfél liter vízzel, két czitrom és két narancsnak igen finoman lehámozott sárgájával egyszer felfőzünk
2. Azt lehabozzuk
3. Egy palaczk rummal, egy palaczk fehér borral és egy palaczk vörös borral összevegyítjük
4. Aztán négy czitrom és négy narancs levét belenyomjuk
5. Az egészet befödjük
6. Egy óráig forró hamun állni hagyjuk
7. Később tálba szűrjük
8. Forrón feladjuk

title: Angol tészta (English Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-one dekagrams of fine small tea rusks
- preserved apricots or raspberries for filling
- butter
- seven decilitres of good cream
- egg yolks
- fourteen dekagrams of sugar
- vanilla
- cream sauce or chocolate sauce

## Directions

1. Fill twenty-one dekagrams of fine small tea rusks with preserved apricots or raspberries; coat one half, place the other piece on top, and when all are filled this way, arrange them neatly in a butter-greased mold
2. Beat seven decilitres of good cream well with egg yolks, fourteen dekagrams of sugar, and a little vanilla, pour it on top, and set aside for an hour so that the rusks absorb it well
3. Then braise for three-quarters of an hour
4. At serving time, turn it out and add cream sauce or chocolate sauce to the edge

title: Angol-kalács (English Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagrams butter, whipped fluffy
- 4 egg yolks
- 21 dekagrams sugar, grated on lemon and crushed and sifted
- 21 dekagrams fine flour
- 4 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 3.5 dekagrams currants
- 3.5 dekagrams raisins
- 5 dekagrams quince paste, cut into small cubes

## Directions

1. Gradually mix egg yolks into whipped butter
2. Add crushed and sifted sugar and flour
3. Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Quickly add currants, raisins, and small cubes of quince paste to the batter
5. Fill buttered molds used for twice-baked bread
6. Bake at gentle heat
7. Turn out onto a sieve and cool
8. Cut into knife-width thick slices without cutting completely through
9. Sprinkle heavily with sifted sugar
10. Glaze with hot spatula
11. When sugar has hardened, cut slices completely through
12. Arrange on cloth-lined plate in a wreath or triangular shape of six to eight layers

title: Apricot Aspic (Baraezk-kocsonya)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 beautiful ripe apricots
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 4 deciliters water
- 1 lemon
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved and strained isinglass

## Directions

1. Peel 30 beautiful ripe apricots, remove pits, and place in a soup bowl.
2. In a brass pan, cook 42 dekagrams sugar with 4 deciliters water only until completely dissolved.
3. Pour the hot syrup over the apricots, cover, and let stand 24 hours.
4. Strain slowly without pressing, allowing liquid to flow drop by drop.
5. Mix in juice of 1 lemon.
6. Finally mix in 4.5 dekagrams dissolved and strained isinglass.
7. Strain through cloth lined with blotting paper, and if the mixture is beautifully clear, pour into molds and freeze.

title: Apró Dorongfánk (Small Stick Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram fine flour
- 3 dekagrams fresh butter
- salt
- 28 dekagrams finely ground almonds
- ground cinnamon
- finely chopped zest of 1 lemon
- 14 dekagrams powdered sugar
- 1 whole egg and 4 egg yolks
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Chop 3 dekagrams fresh butter into half kilogram flour, salt lightly and work well with the rolling pin
2. Mix in 28 dekagrams finely ground almonds, ground cinnamon, finely chopped lemon zest and 14 dekagrams powdered sugar thoroughly with the flour
3. Add 1 whole egg and 4 egg yolks, make the dough with a wide knife
4. Stretch to the thickness of a knife edge
5. Cut to the length and width of the baking sheet
6. Prepare fat in a small pot, tie the dough to the sheet with string and fry until golden yellow

title: Apáczafánkocska (Nun's Doughnuts)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 deciliters beautiful white melted honey
- 1.5 bread rolls, finely grated crumbs
- 10 dekagrams almonds with shell, crushed
- good portion of cinnamon
- cloves
- finely chopped zest of one lemon and one orange
- crumbly, slightly firm puff pastry dough
- egg white

## Directions

1. Into 2 deciliters of beautiful white melted honey, place the finely grated crumbs of 1.5 bread rolls, 10 dekagrams of almonds crushed with shell, a good portion of cinnamon and cloves, and the finely chopped zest of one lemon and one orange
2. Simmer slightly, then cool
3. Form hazelnut-sized balls from the mixture
4. Prepare crumbly, slightly firm puff pastry dough, roll it out like noodle dough, and place the balls inside
5. Brush the dough around the filling with egg white, dip the pastry tool into flour, cut out the doughnuts with it, and bake at gentle heat

title: Apáczafánkocskák más módon (Nun's Doughnuts Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams sugar
- coffee cup of water
- 21 dekagrams softened almonds
- zest of one orange
- zest of one lemon
- 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
- 3-4 cloves
- 10 dekagrams bread roll crumbs
- puff pastry dough
- egg white

## Directions

1. In a pot, cook 28 dekagrams of sugar with a coffee cup of water over coals until the sugar is completely dissolved
2. Crush 21 dekagrams of softened almonds, remove the zest from one orange, cook it in water, and chop it finely together with the zest of one lemon
3. Mix in 1 teaspoon of crushed cinnamon (which can contain 3-4 cloves) and 10 dekagrams of bread roll crumbs with the other ingredients
4. Add this mixture to the boiling sugar, keeping it on the fire with continuous stirring until the mixture has the proper thickness, then set it aside to cool
5. Prepare the dough in the same manner as in the previous recipe and treat the doughnuts in the same way
6. These doughnuts can be kept for a longer time in a box or covered with glass in a dry place
7. The balls made from the filling should be the size of a walnut

title: Aranybőralma-Marmelád (Golden-Skinned Apple Jam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- water
- sugar, for half kilogram of sugar use sixty-five decagrams of apple pulp
- cold water

## Directions

1. Cook the apples in water until they soften but do not fall apart; then place in cold water and when cooled, peel and press through a sieve.
2. Now place in a brass pan over gentle charcoal heat and stir constantly until thick.
3. Prepare the sugar as you would for other fruits and clarify it; add the apple when it begins to form heavy drops, mix well with the sugar and cook until a drop placed on a plate as a test does not run but remains firm.
4. If the sugar has cooked well, half an hour is sufficient; then when cooled, pour into glasses.

title: Aranyhaluska (Golden Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as for recipe 578
- melted fat for dipping
- butter for coating the pastry sheet and rim
- 90 grams almonds
- 140 grams raisins
- 90 grams sugar
- a little cinnamon
- egg white for brushing

## Directions

1. Prepare dough as for recipe 578 and roll out to the thickness of two knife-backs
2. Cut out small noodles about the size of a twenty, dipping the cutter in melted fat instead of flour
3. Coat the pastry sheet and its rim with butter
4. Blanch the almonds, clean them, and chop them finely with the skin; also chop 140 grams of raisins finely
5. Crush 90 grams of sugar and a little cinnamon finely and mix all together
6. Place a row of golden noodles on the pastry sheet so that each one overlaps the one before it, continuing until the bottom is completely covered
7. Sprinkle with the almond mixture and place another row of noodles in the same order
8. Continue in this way
9. Place in a warm location and let it rise well, brush with egg white, and bake at a sustained high temperature, as this dough requires more time and greater heat than other doughs

title: Aranyhaluska rákvajjal (Golden Noodles with Crayfish Butter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- an egg-sized piece of crayfish butter beaten fluffy
- 1 whole egg
- 3 egg yolks
- 400 milliliters lukewarm cream
- 37.5 grams fresh cheese yeast
- salt
- flour as needed
- melted crayfish butter for dipping and coating

## Directions

1. Beat the crayfish butter until fluffy
2. Add 1 whole egg and 3 egg yolks one after another
3. Add the lukewarm cream, fresh cheese yeast, salt, and enough flour so the dough can be rolled out well without being too stiff
4. Place in a warm location and let rise
5. Roll out to the thickness of two knife-backs and cut out small noodles with a cutter dipped in melted crayfish butter
6. Arrange the noodles row by row on a small pastry sheet coated with crayfish butter, with its rim also coated, so that the edge of one noodle rests on the next one
7. Once the bottom of the pastry sheet is completely covered, coat with melted crayfish butter and place the second row in the same order on top, coat with crayfish butter, and continue this way until everything is arranged
8. Let it rise in a warm place
9. Before putting it in the oven, coat with crayfish butter and bake at lower heat than the previous recipe so it does not lose its color but still bakes well
10. Turn out onto a platter and serve with warm sugared cream in a bowl

title: Aranykörték Párban (Golden Pears in Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- golden pears
- sugar
- water
- lemon juice
- 560 grams pears requires 14 dekagrams sugar and 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Peel the golden pears which are twice as large as muscat pears, clean the stem so that only half remains on the pear
2. Once peeled, coat each pear with lemon juice so they remain beautifully white, place them in water until all are peeled
3. Calculate 14 dekagrams sugar for 560 grams fruit, cook with 1 glass water only until the sugar dissolves

title: Articsóka zöld borsóval (Artichoke with Green Peas)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt
- water
- butter
- fresh parsley
- green peas
- roasted veal
- nutmeg flower
- fried goose liver (for garnish)

## Directions

1. Cook the artichokes in salted water
2. Remove the interior cleanly
3. Stew the green peas with butter and fresh parsley
4. Prepare a little butter sauce
5. Cut roasted veal into cubes
6. Add a little butter sauce to the veal with the green peas and a little nutmeg flower
7. Fill the artichokes with this mixture
8. Place them in the butter sauce and cook well
9. Garnish with fried goose liver

title: Articsóka-saláta (Artichoke Salad)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Oil
- Mixed spices
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the artichokes thoroughly, cut off the tips, and cook until tender
2. Clean out the rough fibres from inside
3. Place on an oiled rack, drizzle with oil, and sprinkle with mixed spices
4. Roast slowly
5. Arrange on a plate, dress with vinegar and oil, and serve cold

title: Asparagus in Butter Sauce (Spárga vaj mártásban)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt water
- butter sauce
- 2 deciliters sour cream
- fine breadcrumbs
- hot butter

## Directions

1. Clean the asparagus
2. Cook in salted water but not until too soft
3. Place in butter sauce or arrange in a platter with tips facing inward
4. Pour 2 deciliters of sour cream over it
5. Sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs and pour hot butter over it

title: Asparagus Soup (Spárgaleves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- asparagus
- frogs
- pea broth or pea water
- salt
- spices
- sour cream
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Braise butter, asparagus and frogs in a pot
2. Pour pea broth or pea water over it
3. Bring well to a boil, season with spices and salt
4. Add two tablespoons of sour cream
5. Serve on fried bread

title: Aszalt körték (Dried Pears)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe, large pears without defects
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Cook the ripe, large pears in a clean pot until they appear soft
2. Drain and peel them
3. Set aside the peels on a plate
4. Layer the pears in a press and press them
5. Place the press over a clean basin to catch the liquid
6. Let the pears press for a quarter hour, then press more strongly and let stand another quarter hour
7. Remove and place on a drying board, put in the oven for several hours until they turn a nice reddish-brown color
8. Cook the pressed pear liquid and peel with pieces of sugar until it becomes a thick syrup
9. Dip the pears well into this syrup and place in the oven to dry
10. Repeat the dipping in syrup two to three times, then dry thoroughly
11. Once cooled, layer in rolls and store in a dry place

title: Aszalt őszibaraczk (Dried Peaches)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large, ripe peaches
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and halve large, ripe peaches
2. Place on a drying board in a warm oven
3. When well dried and a nice light brown color, remove them
4. Press one slice flat against the other with your finger for each peach
5. Dip every peach in braided sugar
6. Place back on the drying board and put in the oven for two hours
7. Dip in sugar once more and dry again

title: Az Őzderék Páczolva (Marinated Venison Saddle)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one venison saddle
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- red onion
- cloves

## Directions

1. Remove the outer membrane-like skin from the venison saddle with a knife and place it in a dish.
2. To prepare the marinade for one whole venison saddle, put in a pot: two liters of water, one liter of vinegar, two tin spoonfuls of salt, one spoonful of pepper, six bay leaves, one large head of red onion, and six cloves.
3. Mix these together, bring to a boil, and boil for a quarter hour.
4. Pour this hot marinade over the venison in the dish, cover, and keep in a cool place.
5. The meat can be kept this way for one to two days or even longer, turning it twice daily.

title: Bableves (Bean Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three liters of water
- seven deciliters of small red beans
- two bay leaves
- two cloves of garlic
- thin roux
- one and a half liters of water
- salt and vinegar
- butter
- bread rolls

## Directions

1. Cook the beans, bay leaves, and garlic in three liters of water until soft
2. Press through a coarse sieve
3. Make a thin roux and cook again with one and a half liters of water
4. Add a little salt and vinegar
5. Pour over bread roll slices fried in butter and cut lengthwise

title: Baraczk-pereez (Apricot Pretzel Cookies)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams softened almonds
- 18 dekagrams crushed and sifted sugar
- 2 teaspoons canned apricots
- additional canned apricots as needed
- sugar for dusting
- fine powdered sugar
- snow
- lemon juice
- white paper cone

## Directions

1. Crush 14 dekagrams of softened almonds into fine dough without moistening them during crushing
2. On a board with a wide knife, thoroughly rub together the crushed almonds with 18 dekagrams of crushed and sifted sugar and 2 teaspoons of canned apricots
3. If the mixture is not moist enough, add a little more canned apricot preserves, which enhances the beautiful color of the dough, but it must not be too soft
4. Once the mass is well worked, dust the board with sugar and form small pretzels from the mixture
5. Place these on a tray lightly greased with wax
6. Mix fine powdered sugar with a little snow and lemon juice to make thick icing
7. Fill white paper cones with the icing and decorate the pretzels beautifully
8. Place in a gentle oven and dry them
9. These pretzels taste magnificent and have the property that in a dry place, in a box or covered with glass, they will keep for several weeks

title: Baraczk-pereez (Apricot Pretzel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams blanched almonds
- 18 dekagrams crushed and sifted sugar
- 2 coffee spoonfuls of preserved apricots

## Directions

1. Crush the blanched almonds into a fine dough without wetting the almonds during crushing
2. Crush the sugar and thoroughly rub it on a board with a wide knife together with the almonds and the preserved apricots
3. If the mixture is not moist enough, add a little more preserved apricot, which improves the beautiful color of the dough, but it should not be too soft
4. Once the mass is well worked, dust the board with sugar and form small pretzels from the mass

title: Baraczk-pép (Apricot Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Sixteen ripe, beautiful large apricots
- Two emperor's rolls (czipó)
- Milk
- Ten dekagrams softened almonds
- Seven dekagrams butter
- Six egg yolks
- Sugar to taste
- Four egg whites

## Directions

1. Peel the apricots and remove the pits
2. Stew the apricots in a pan with frequent shaking until soft
3. Press through a sieve and cool
4. Gently grate the crust of two emperor's rolls
5. Soak in milk, squeeze well, and chop finely
6. Finely grind ten dekagrams of softened almonds
7. Beat seven dekagrams of butter until fluffy
8. Mix in the six egg yolks one by one
9. First mix in the apricot, then the almonds and bread
10. Add enough sugar to taste sweet
11. When well mixed, gently fold in the stiffly beaten whites of four eggs
12. Pour into a pan lined with butter and bake

title: Baraczk-szeletek (Peach Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- five-times beaten puff pastry
- peach marmalade
- egg white
- finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Roll out the bottom layer of five-times beaten puff pastry to knife-edge thickness
2. Spread with peach marmalade to knife-edge thickness
3. Lay another layer on top
4. Glaze the same way as described for apple slices
5. Cut an elegant shape in the middle of each slice with a fine knife, going through the top layer only, which looks very nice when baked
6. Brush with a little egg white and sprinkle with finely ground sugar

title: Baraezk-Lepény (Apricot Tart)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- buttered dough
- apricots
- sugar
- egg

## Directions

1. Prepare buttered dough and roll out to good knife-width thickness
2. Cut circles the size of a coffee cup saucer
3. Arrange blanched apricots, halved in the middle and well sugared, on the circles
4. Make a small finger-width cut along the edge and fold up, creating a crown-shaped border
5. Brush with egg and when done, sprinkle with sugar and serve

title: Barna leves (Brown Broth)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- thin bacon slices
- chunks of beef for broth
- liver
- good quality bones, broken into pieces
- two heads of red onion
- several carrots
- kohlrabi
- kale
- parsley
- celery
- several peppercorns
- cloves

## Directions

1. Lay thin bacon slices in a pot
2. Add chunks of beef for broth, liver, and broken bones
3. Add two heads of red onion, several carrots, kohlrabi, kale, parsley, and celery, all sliced
4. Add several peppercorns and cloves
5. Cover the pot and let everything braise until it turns a beautiful brown color
6. To prevent burning, occasionally moisten with a little broth, but never stir so the broth stays clear
7. One hour before serving, pour regular broth over it and let it simmer slowly so the broth becomes strong
8. Strain and add meat dumplings or other garnish

title: Barna leves borjúmájból (Brown Broth with Veal Liver)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon cooking fat
- one teaspoon sugar
- red onion, sliced into rings
- veal liver, sliced
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Put one tablespoon cooking fat and one teaspoon sugar in a pot
2. Add sliced red onion rings and veal liver slices
3. Sauté until the onion turns red
4. Pour in meat broth and let it boil well
5. Once it has come to a boil, let it cool
6. Strain and remove the fat
7. Serve with cooked peas

title: Barna leves más módon (Brown Broth Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- meat and bones prepared in the regular manner with less vegetables
- four slices of bacon
- fatty bone
- hazelnut-sized piece of sugar
- piece of veal liver or spleen
- 12 dekagrams carrot, sliced into rings
- one parsley root
- celery
- kohlrabi
- one small head of red onion
- peppercorns
- cloves

## Directions

1. Prepare the meat and bones in the regular manner with less vegetables and let cook for one hour
2. In a pot, sauté the fatty bone with four slices of bacon until browned
3. Add a hazelnut-sized piece of sugar and a piece of veal liver or spleen
4. Add 12 dekagrams of carrot sliced into rings, parsley root, celery, kohlrabi, one small head of red onion, peppercorns, and cloves
5. Leave uncovered on the fire and let braise until the bacon turns brown, but be careful not to burn it
6. From time to time pour a spoonful of broth over the bones and vegetables
7. Let simmer until the vegetables are well braised, then brown with frequent stirring
8. When the meat is cooked, pour its broth over the bones and vegetables and bring to a boil together
9. Set aside to cool, then strain and remove the fat
10. Serve with meat dumplings
11. If not salted, the brown broth can be kept for 2-3 days

title: Barna Linezi Torta (Brown Almond Layer Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm almonds with shells
- zest of one lemon, finely chopped
- 28 dekagramm finely ground sugar
- 1 dekagramm cinnamon
- a few cloves
- 28 dekagramm fine flour
- 28 dekagramm butter
- 3 egg yolks
- cream for sweetness

## Directions

1. Finely grind almonds with shells
2. Finely chop lemon zest
3. Finely grind sugar, cinnamon and cloves
4. Work flour and butter well together on board with rolling pin
5. Mix in prepared ingredients
6. Add three egg yolks and enough cream for sweetness
7. Assemble dough with wide knife
8. Can make braided cake with preserves or layers baked and filled which is best
9. Dough should not be stiffer than necessary for easy preparation

title: Barnakáposzta gesztenyével (Red Cabbage with Chestnuts)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- red cabbage
- hot water
- salt
- pepper
- sugar
- butter or fat for roux
- flour
- roasted chestnuts
- sausage

## Directions

1. Wash the red cabbage thoroughly and pour hot water over it twice, then cook until soft and drain. Then chop finely and braise in a brownish roux for some time. Pour two large tablespoons of broth over it, add a small amount of pepper and a piece of sugar and braise for another hour. Finally arrange roasted chestnuts on top and add sausage at serving time.

title: Barnaleves rántott borsóval (Brown Broth with Fried Peas)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons flour
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat 3 eggs into 4 tablespoons of flour in a pot.

title: Barnaleves Rántott Borsóval (Brown Sauce with Fried Peas)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoons flour
- three eggs
- milk
- salt
- clear fat

## Directions

1. Beat three eggs into four tablespoons flour in a pot.
2. Mix the two together well.
3. Slowly pour in milk until the batter is as thin as a nice smooth crepe batter.
4. Season lightly with salt.
5. Melt clear fat in a frying pan.
6. Drop the batter in with a foam skimmer.
7. Shake the pan several times so the peas brown evenly on all sides.
8. Add to the soup only at the moment of serving, as they should not become too soft.

title: Barnára párolt borjú-karbonád (Brown Braised Veal Chops)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- bacon slices
- red onion
- carrot
- flour
- broth
- lemon zest
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the veal chops well, remove the membrane from the bone so the bone is completely clean where the meat begins
2. Beat well and shape into chops with a broad knife, season with salt
3. Place a few slices of bacon, sliced red onion, and carrot in a pot
4. Place the chops on top and braise slowly, turning several times so both sides brown
5. Remove and sprinkle flour into the braising liquid and brown well
6. Dilute with broth and cook the sauce for a good half hour
7. Strain through a sieve and remove the fat
8. Place the chops in with lemon zest and the juice of one lemon
9. Arrange neatly in a wreath shape and pour the sauce over

title: Barnára Párolt Kalarábi (Brown-braised Kohlrabi)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- sugar
- butter
- flour
- broth
- fried veal liver

## Directions

1. After cleaning and peeling the kohlrabi, cut into equal cubes
2. Brown a piece of sugar in butter
3. Add the kohlrabi and braise until nicely brown
4. Sprinkle a little flour over it and pour enough broth to make it not too soupy
5. Finish cooking with this
6. When serving, add fried veal liver to it
7. Prepare the fried liver as follows: take as much nice white veal liver as needed, blanch it slightly and cut each piece into two or three parts
8. Season with salt, dip in beaten egg, coat with fine breadcrumbs and fry quickly in lard until golden brown

title: Becsinált Jércze (Breaded Young Hen in Cream Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young hen
- butter
- flour
- broth
- sour cream
- lemon juice
- veal cutlets

## Directions

1. After cleaning and preparing the hens, blanch and slice them nicely.
2. Prepare the sauce as follows: melt seven dekagrams of butter, blend smoothly with two spoonfuls of flour, two spoonfuls of broth, half a spoonful of sour cream and the juice of one lemon.
3. The hens, which have been nearly cooked in broth beforehand, are only braised in this sauce with continuous stirring.
4. At the same time, shape beautiful cutlets from veal and cook their sauce until reduced.
5. Pile the hens high in the center of the dish and pour their sauce over them; arrange the cutlets around the edge in a wreath, poured with their own broth.

title: Becsinált mazsolával (Braised Fowl with Raisins)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- any poultry
- salt
- broth
- sugar
- raisins

## Directions

1. Cook the poultry in abundant salted broth until tender
2. When cooked, for three liters of broth, add sugar and raisins

title: Becsinált Mazsolával (Poultry with Raisins)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Any poultry
- Salt
- One and a half kilos of seedless raisins
- Three liters of cooking liquid
- Thin red roux
- Saffron, a good amount
- Lemon with peel, in quantity sufficient for sourness

## Directions

1. Cook the poultry in plenty of salted broth until soft
2. When the meat is cooked, add the seedless raisins to three liters of broth and cook until soft
3. Thicken with a thin red roux
4. The broth should not be thick
5. Add lemon with peel in sufficient quantity to make it appropriately sour
6. Add good saffron to achieve a beautiful yellow color

title: Beef Marrow Hair Pomade
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dekagrams beef marrow
- fresh water
- equal amount unsalted lard
- 4 dekagrams walnut oil
- pair of ox feet
- wine
- 14 dekagrams mutton tallow
- 28 dekagrams clarified lard
- fragrant oils
- 28 dekagrams beef marrow
- 4.5 dekagrams white wax
- 4.5 dekagrams walnut oil
- 10 dekagrams freshly pressed apple juice
- 4 grams nutmeg oil

## Directions

1. Boil 4 dekagrams beef marrow daily in fresh water
2. Mix with equal amount unsalted lard and melt together
3. Beat with foaming rod until mixture cools almost completely
4. Mix in 4 dekagrams walnut oil
5. Alternative method: chop a pair of ox feet, boil in water for four hours, cool, add small amount of wine, 14 dekagrams mutton tallow, and 28 dekagrams clarified lard, melt over gentle heat, add fragrant oils
6. Or: melt 28 dekagrams beef marrow at moderate heat with 4.5 dekagrams white wax and 4.5 dekagrams walnut oil, mix in by stirring 10 dekagrams freshly pressed apple juice and 4 grams nutmeg oil

title: Beefsteak (Beefsteak)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney (1 kilo) from the thick part
- pepper
- parsley (finely chopped, green)
- fat (1 wooden spoon)
- tarhonyá
- sour cabbage
- rice dish
- potatoes
- eggs
- horseradish
- beet juice
- parsley leaves
- gherkins

## Directions

1. Take one kilo of kidney from the thick part and clean it of membranes and sinews so it gets a neat round form but remains rounded
2. Sprinkle it with pepper and finely chopped green parsley
3. Prepare it in the morning this way and leave it in the cellar covered until evening
4. When needed, place a wooden spoon of fat in a flat iron pan over heat
5. In the smoking, hot fat, sear it quickly so it is pink outside but remains rare inside (or cook longer if preferred)
6. Arrange the seared meat lengthwise on a long dish and surround it alternating spoonfuls of: tarhonyá, sour cabbage, rice dish, potatoes and possibly more similar items
7. Add a talkerli-baked egg to each slice of meat, occasionally forming roses from scraped horseradish slices
8. Place a small pinch of white horseradish ribbon and a small pinch dipped in beet juice
9. Arrange parsley leaves on both sides as stems and place small gherkins neatly between

title: Berakott burgonya (Layered Potato Casserole)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ten to fifteen potatoes of equal size
- ten eggs
- ham, finely chopped
- sour cream, eight decilitres
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes so that they do not burst. Also cook ten eggs hard, then place in cold water and clean them. Cut the potatoes and eggs into slices. Take a casserole of about two liters capacity and layer as follows: a row of sliced potatoes salted, sprinkle finely chopped ham on top, then sliced egg and salt again, then ham again, potatoes, and so on until the dish is full. Pour eight decilitres of sour cream over the top, place in the oven and bake.

title: Bergamot Water
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 liters 72° alcohol
- 18 dekagrams bergamot oil
- 2 dekagrams lemon oil
- 1 dekagrams jasmine oil
- orange flower water as needed

## Directions

1. Dissolve bergamot oil, lemon oil, and jasmine oil in alcohol
2. Add orange flower water until alcohol is 5° strength

title: Besamel (Béchamel Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons flour
- one liter good fresh cream
- half a Spanish onion (if for meat broth)
- a piece of ham (if for meat broth)
- meat glaze tablet (if making strong meat béchamel)

## Directions

1. Beat three tablespoons flour with one liter of good fresh cream finely
2. Cook until the desired thickness is reached
3. Pass through a sieve and use for the intended purpose
4. If for meat broth, add half a Spanish onion and a piece of ham during cooking, and if you want to make the meat béchamel strong, add a meat glaze tablet
5. Béchamel can also be made simply by gently swelling flour in fat and cooking fresh milk to a certain thickness

title: Besammel-felfújt (Béchamel Soufflé)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 140 grams fresh melted butter
- 120 grams fine flour
- 400 milliliters cream or good milk
- salt
- 10 egg yolks
- 40 grams ground and sifted sugar with vanilla
- 6 egg whites
- butter
- peach or raspberry syrup, wine or milk sauce

## Directions

1. In a pot, brown the fine flour in the melted butter
2. When the flour foams, gradually add cream or milk with constant stirring
3. Stir the whole mixture until it separates from the pot, then season lightly with salt
4. Cool on a plate
5. Beat the paste with ten egg yolks until foamy
6. Mix in sugar ground with vanilla
7. Finally fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
8. Pour the mixture into a well-buttered mold and either steam for three-quarters of an hour or bake in a not too hot oven until a beautiful light yellow
9. Turn out onto a plate and pour peach or raspberry syrup, wine or milk sauce underneath

title: Bibornok-mártás (Sauce Rich - Warm Fish and Roast Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoonfuls of béchamel
- two egg yolks
- half a kilogram of crab butter

## Directions

1. Place two spoonfuls of béchamel in a pot and heat well
2. Beat in two egg yolks
3. Add the crab butter in small portions while continuously beating
4. When ready, serve hot

title: Birsalma párban (Quince Preserves in a Water Bath)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 dekagrams ripe quince
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 4 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Peel the quince and cut into slices two centimeters wide
2. Remove the seedy portion from the center and place in cold water
3. Boil the sugar with four deciliters of water until dissolved
4. Place the quince slices in the boiling syrup and stir continuously and very carefully with a silver spoon so the slices cook evenly
5. Cook until they can be pierced with a needle, but they must not become completely soft
6. Remove from heat and cool completely
7. Fill the jars, place them in a water bath (see recipe 961), and leave for at most three minutes

title: Birsalma sósborszeszben (Quince in Vinegared Spirits)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- quince or quince-pear
- 56 dekagrams sugar per 56 dekagrams quince
- 42 dekagrams sugar per 56 dekagrams quince
- strong vinegared spirits, same amount as the sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Peel the quince or quince-pear and cut into eight or more pieces depending on size.
2. Remove the seeds and place the quince in cold water.
3. Soften well in hot water and drain thoroughly.
4. Take 56 dekagrams quince to 42 dekagrams sugar and cook not too thickly.
5. Prepare the quince over three days as with other fruit, cooking the sugar quite thickly the last time.
6. Let cool and mix with an equal amount of strong vinegared spirits.
7. Pour over the quince already packed in glass jars.

title: Birsalma-fagylalt (Quince Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large ripe quinces, peeled and sliced thinly
- 42 decagrams sugar
- 7 deciliters fresh water

## Directions

1. Cook the quince slices with sugar and water in a preserving pan until the quince is completely softened
2. Cool it, then press through a clean, non-dense cloth
3. Mix with the juice of 2 lemons and cool completely
4. Pour into the ice cream mold and proceed as with other ice creams
5. For jelly made from the same mixture, use 1.5 decagrams of dissolved isinglass, strain through cloth and blotting paper as with all jellies

title: Birsalma-fagylalt (Quince Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large ripe quinces
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 7 deciliters fresh water
- juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Peel 4 large ripe quinces and cut into thin slices
2. Cook in a preserving pan with 42 dekagrams sugar and 7 deciliters fresh water until the quince is completely soft
3. Cool and press through clean, thin kitchen cloth
4. Mix with the juice of 2 lemons
5. Cool completely
6. Pour into freezing container and proceed as with other ice creams
7. The same mixture is used to make jelly, but for that use one and one-quarter dekagrams of dissolved isinglass, and strain as with all jellies through kitchen cloth and blotting paper

title: Birsalma-kocsonya (Quince Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter water
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 4 large ripe and beautiful yellow quinces
- 2 lemons
- 1.25 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar in water only until it dissolves
2. Wipe the four large ripe and beautiful yellow quinces clean with a cloth, but do not peel them
3. Cut them into slices as thick as a knife blade and cook them in the sugar until soft
4. When the sugar has well absorbed the quince flavor and the color is beautiful yellow, strain through a sieve but do not press
5. Press in the juice of two lemons
6. Mix in the dissolved isinglass
7. Strain through a stretched cloth
8. If it is beautifully clear, pour into a mold and freeze on ice
9. Alternatively, thin slices of quince preserved in sugar may be placed in the jelly in two or three rows, and then treated as other layered jellies

title: Birsalma-kompot (Quince Compote)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Three to four quinces
- Water
- Twenty-four dekagrams sugar
- Two deciliters water

## Directions

1. Bring the quinces to a slight boil in water, then place them in cold water
2. When cooled, peel them and divide into four segments, removing the cores
3. Cook twenty-four dekagrams sugar with two deciliters of water until the sugar forms heavy drops
4. Place the quinces in the sugar syrup, turning them several times so they soften on both sides without falling apart
5. Arrange on a serving dish and pour the light syrup over them

title: Birsalma-kompót (Quince Compote)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-4 quinces
- water
- 240 grams sugar

## Directions

1. Boil 3-4 quinces briefly in water
2. Place in cold water and chill
3. Remove skin and divide into 4 segments
4. Remove cores
5. Cook 240 grams sugar with 200 ml water until it forms heavy drops
6. Place quinces in sugar syrup and turn several times so both sides soften without falling apart
7. Arrange on a plate and pour the small amount of sauce underneath

title: Birsalma-levet Befőzni, Mely Fagylaltokhoz, Kocsonyákhoz és Kompotokhoz Alkalmas (Quince Juice Preserve for Ice Creams, Jellies and Compotes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful ripe quinces
- 1/2 kilogram sugar per 1/2 kilogram clear juice

## Directions

1. Peel beautiful ripe quinces, remove the cores, crush the quinces in a large mortar, press through cloth and let stand for one hour so the thick part settles, then strain the juice and prepare it like other juices.
2. Use the same amount of sugar for each 1/2 kilogram of clear juice.

title: Birsalma-Marmelád (Quince Jam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful, large, ripe quinces
- water
- sugar, same amount of quince pulp as sugar, half kilogram
- cold water

## Directions

1. Bake or cook beautiful large ripe quinces in water until soft, then press through a fine sieve, and cook the sugar, which you have dipped in cold water, until it threads.
2. For half a kilogram of sugar use the same amount of quince pulp; cook this with one glass of water until it threads, then add the pressed quince, cook for half an hour, then cool and pour into glasses.

title: Birsalma-mártás (Quince Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two large ripe, cooked quinces
- two deciliters sour cream
- half teaspoon flour
- one tablespoon stock
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and grate two large ripe, cooked quinces
2. Mix two deciliters of sour cream with half a teaspoon of flour and add one tablespoon of stock
3. Cook for half a quarter hour
4. Add the grated quince and enough sugar to make it sweet enough

title: Birsalma-ostya (Quince Wafer)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe quinces
- 32 dekagramm quince pulp
- 28 dekagramm sugar rubbed with lemon zest
- 1 lemon juice
- butter

## Directions

1. Scald beautiful ripe quinces with skin in water until softened
2. Peel and scrape the flesh with a knife without including the hard core
3. Mix finely 32 dekagramm of the scraped pulp strained through a sieve with 28 dekagramm sugar rubbed on lemon zest, adding the juice of 1 lemon
4. Spread very thinly on small porcelain plates coated with butter
5. Place the plates in a warm oven until the mixture dries enough to be pulled off
6. Roll onto round wooden sticks prepared for this
7. Brush the end with egg white so they stay together
8. Dry in a warm oven
9. Note: when rolling, the part of the wafer that lay on the buttered plate forms the outer side

title: Birsalma-pép (Quince Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 quinces
- 21 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 9 egg whites, whipped stiff

## Directions

1. Cook the quinces until soft
2. Peel them and pass through a sieve
3. Place in a bowl and cool
4. When cooled, add the finely ground sugar and stir for a quarter hour
5. Slowly fold in the stiffly whipped egg whites
6. Pour the mixture into a buttered porcelain mold
7. Mound it into a cone shape with a wide knife
8. Make six indentations
9. Set in a water bath and cook for half an hour
10. Note: Do not place a metal cover on top, as the paste would burn

title: Birsalma-pép más módon (Quince Paste, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large quinces
- 2 dry, finely grated rolls
- half a coffee cup heavy cream
- 9 dekagrams almonds, finely ground
- butter, whipped until frothy, egg-sized piece
- zest of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- sugar to taste
- 4 egg whites, whipped stiff
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the quinces and cool them
2. Peel and grate them
3. Moisten the grated rolls with the heavy cream
4. Finely grind the almonds and mix with the rolls and cream
5. Add the mixture to an egg-sized piece of whipped butter
6. Add the finely chopped lemon zest, two whole eggs, and four egg yolks
7. Stir everything for half an hour
8. Add sugar until sufficiently sweet
9. Gently fold in the stiffly whipped egg whites
10. Pour into a buttered mold sprinkled with fine breadcrumbs
11. Bake in the oven
12. This paste requires slightly longer baking than other pastes

title: Birsalma-Rudacskák (Quince Sticks)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- several large, ripe quinces
- 28 dekagrams sugar per 28 dekagrams quince pulp
- 1 glass water
- 18 dekagrams blanched and finely ground almonds
- 14 dekagrams citron peel, finely chopped
- wafer sheets
- lemon preserve

## Directions

1. Cook the quinces until soft but not bursting
2. Drain and let rest for a while
3. Peel and pass the flesh through a sieve
4. Take equal weights of sugar to quince pulp
5. Cook the sugar with a glass of water until very thick and forming large bubbles
6. Quickly add the quince pulp with the almonds and citron peel
7. Cook with continuous stirring for a few minutes until very thick
8. Cut wafer sheets into strips the length of a finger and two fingers wide
9. Spread the mixture onto the strips with a wet knife to the thickness of a little finger
10. Place on wax-coated baking sheets
11. Leave in a cooled oven until dried and easily peeled off
12. Coat with lemon preserve

title: Birsalma-Sajt (Quince Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven large, ripe quinces
- water
- sugar, same amount as quince, half kilogram

## Directions

1. Cook or bake seven large ripe quinces until soft; be careful they do not crack as they will then become watery.
2. When soft, cool slightly on a plate; then peel with a silver spoon, as they would darken from a knife, and press through a sieve.
3. For half a kilogram of quince use the same amount of sugar; cook this with one glass of water until it threads, then add the pressed quince, mix well with the sugar, and cook over gentle charcoal heat until the syrup separates from the silver spoon with which you must stir continuously so it does not burn.
4. Then while still warm place on coffee trays, spread nicely with a spoon, and leave on the tray for five or six days.
5. Then turn out onto a clean plate, let stand for six to eight days, turning over daily so it dries on all sides. The longer it stands, the better.

title: Birsalma-Sonka (Quince Ham)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half kilograms quince pulp
- one and a half kilograms fine sugar
- water, four deciliters
- aspic

## Directions

1. A portion of quince ham consists of one and a half kilograms of quince pulp and the same amount of fine sugar; cook the latter in a brass pan with four deciliters of water until it forms heavy drops.
2. Then add eighteen to twenty pressed quinces to the cooked sugar and cook with continuous stirring until it begins to gel slightly; this must be tested several times so the quince does not lose its white color by cooking too long.
3. If the test succeeds, put enough of it in a tin ham mold to form a layer of fat; spread it evenly quickly with a silver spoon and cool slightly; meanwhile pour enough aspic into the remaining mixture to give it a meat color; carefully place the red quince paste on the white, but the two must not mix; leave in a warm room for three or four days.
4. Test whether it could be turned out; if not, leave standing two or three more days, then turn out onto a clean board covered with tissue paper and keep in a dry place.
5. If the paper becomes damp after several days, replace it with dry paper. This ham is an excellent delicacy, as when cut into thin slices it has a beautiful appearance, and if the quince is pure and spotless, it perfectly resembles natural ham.

title: Birsalma-szeletek (Quince Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams quince
- 14 dekagrams softened and finely ground almonds
- 500 grams finely ground sugar
- 21 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 21 dekagrams softened and finely ground almonds
- 7 dekagrams bread crumbs moistened with 1 tablespoon honey wine
- 10 dekagrams finely ground chocolate dissolved in a pan with 1 spoonful water with repeated stirring

## Directions

1. Cook the quince and strain it
2. Let it stand covered for a while
3. Peel and press the flesh through a sieve
4. In a pan with continuous stirring, prepare a firm mixture from 500 grams quince, 14 dekagrams softened and finely ground almonds, and 500 grams finely ground sugar
5. Place this mixture on a clean board heavily sprinkled with sugar
6. Cool completely, sprinkle well with sugar, roll to twice knife-edge thickness
7. Cut into slices two fingers wide and a span long with a sharp knife
8. Place on paper sprinkled with sugar and dry in the drying oven
9. Meanwhile, prepare a mixture from 21 dekagrams finely ground sugar, 21 dekagrams softened and finely ground almonds, 7 dekagrams bread crumbs moistened with 1 tablespoon honey wine, and 10 dekagrams finely ground chocolate dissolved in a pan with 1 spoonful water with repeated stirring

title: Birsalma-szeletek mustban (Quince Slices in Must)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- good must
- quince

## Directions

1. Semi-cook good must and quince in desired quantities
2. Remove the foam and pour into a new clay pot
3. Bring to a boil a few times over gentle charcoal
4. Add cleaned quince cut into quarters
5. Cook until sufficiently soft, adding finely ground cinnamon and clove buds during cooking
6. When cooled, place in glass jars where it can be kept throughout the winter

title: Birsalma-torta (Quince Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogramm sugar
- 2 deciliter water
- 0.5 kilogramm quince pulp
- 18 dekagramm swollen and finely broken almonds
- 10 dekagramm candied citron, chopped
- 1 lemon, zest and juice finely chopped
- wafer paste sheets
- coarsely broken sugar
- lemon icing
- Spanish pastries
- green preserved fruits

## Directions

1. Cook 0.5 kilogramm of sugar with 2 deciliter of water in a brass bowl until it threads and produces large bubbles.
2. Prepare 0.5 kilogramm of pulp from well-ripened quinces, cook it, and press through a sieve.
3. Add the pulp to the well-cooked sugar, along with 18 dekagramm of swollen and finely broken almonds, 10 dekagramm of chopped citron, and the finely chopped zest and juice of 1 lemon.
4. Cook together over gentle heat with constant stirring until it becomes jelly-like.
5. Line a cake layer with large round wafers and spread the mixture to a two-finger height with a knife dipped in water, smoothing it evenly.
6. Sprinkle with coarsely broken sugar and leave in a cooled oven for 24 hours.
7. Coat with lemon icing and decorate the edge with Spanish pastries fastened with hardened sugar and green preserved fruits.
8. For the lemon icing: prepare it as pistachio icing, but rub the sugar on the lemon zest, finely crush it, and when the icing is well worked and clear and thick, incorporate the rubbed sugar and 2 teaspoons of lemon juice, stirring well.
9. Test the icing by placing a few drops on paper; if it does not spread and becomes firm in a few moments so it can be lifted off intact, it is good; if not, continue working it longer.

title: Birsalmaszeleteket becsinálni (Prepared Quince Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram well-ripened quince
- three deciliters water
- three deciliters vinegar
- half kilogram sugar
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Peel one kilogram of well-ripened quince and divide into four or six pieces
2. Remove the seeds and hard center portions
3. Place three deciliters water, the same amount of vinegar, and half kilogram sugar in a brass pan over fire
4. Add the quince pieces to keep them white
5. Before cooking, pierce each slice with a few pieces of cinnamon stick and two clove buds
6. Cook until soft, then remove and place in jars
7. Continue cooking the liquid until enough remains to cover the slices
8. When cooled, pour over the slices and store as done with others

title: Birsalmaszeletet Befőzni (Preserved Quince Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large, fully ripe, peeled quince
- sugar equal in weight to the quince
- four deciliters of water
- warm water

## Directions

1. Cut the whole ripe peeled quince into four pieces, or six if larger. Cut off the core. In a preserving basin, cook sugar equal in weight to the quince with four deciliters of water until the sugar dissolves. Add the quince slices and, turning them frequently, cook until completely soft but not falling apart. While the sugar is dissolving, the quince slices must lie in warm water to stay beautiful white in color. Remove the slices and continue cooking the liquid until it begins to thicken. Remove from heat, return the slices to the basin, and shake it strongly so the liquid covers everything. Cool slightly and pour while warm into clean preserving jars, leaving to stand for twenty-four hours. The liquid must completely cover the slices after settling. If the liquid is not thick enough after complete cooling, strain it slowly, cook it again, and pour it warm over the slices.

title: Blaskovics-vesepecsenye (Blaskovics Kidney Roast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- nice rested kidney roast
- salt
- 20 deka butter
- mixed vegetables, sliced into rings
- 3 bay leaves
- 1 whole lemon zest
- 1 ginger root
- a little whole black pepper
- 1/2 liter red wine
- water

## Directions

1. Lard a nice rested kidney roast, salt it, and set aside
2. Melt 20 deka butter in a pan, slice mixed vegetables into rings and place in the butter
3. Place the kidney roast on top, cover and braise until the vegetables turn yellow
4.
