When this has happened, add among the vegetables three bay leaves, the zest of one whole lemon, one ginger root, and a little whole black pepper, and pour in 1/2 liter red wine mixed with water and braise until the kidney roast becomes tender; if the broth becomes too thick, a little wine can be added
5. When the meat is tender enough, remove it, strain the broth through a sieve and pour it over the meat

title: Boeuf à la Mode (Braised Beef)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef without bones
- Ham
- Bacon, cut into finger-length and thickness pieces
- Green parsley, minced
- Small onion, minced
- Several mushrooms, minced
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bacon slices
- Red onion, sliced in rings
- Carrot, sliced
- Whole peppercorns, several
- Four deciliters white wine
- Good broth

## Directions

1. Lard the beef with pieces of ham and bacon
2. Prepare a mixture of minced green parsley, minced onion, several minced mushrooms, salt, and pepper
3. Roll the bacon pieces in this mixture and insert into the beef
4. Place the beef in a pot that is not much larger than necessary
5. Line the bottom of the pot with bacon slices, sliced red onion, and sliced carrot
6. Place the beef on top, cover tightly, and braise slowly for four hours, turning frequently to prevent burning
7. Pour four deciliters of white wine and the same amount of good broth over the meat
8. Continue braising until the meat is tender enough, strain the sauce through a sieve, and pour over the meat on the platter
9. This dish can also be served cold

title: Boeuf à la Mode, Second Method (Braised Beef)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Bacon and ham larded with vegetables
- Bacon slices
- Red onion
- Carrot
- Whole peppercorns, several
- Allspice
- White wine, four deciliters
- Broth, three serving spoons
- Braised cabbage

## Directions

1. Lard the beef with bacon and ham that have been rolled in minced vegetables
2. Line the pot with bacon slices, red onion, and carrot
3. Place the beef on top, add several whole peppercorns and allspice
4. Braise for five to six hours, pouring a small amount of broth over it from time to time to prevent burning and hardening
5. When it begins to become tender, pour four deciliters of white wine and three serving spoons of broth over it
6. Continue braising until tender, strain the sauce through a sieve, and remove the fat
7. Serve with braised cabbage as a crown

title: Bográcssos (Gulyás-style Stew)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt
- Paprika, preferably fresh in the pod
- Lamb meat, finely chopped
- Water

## Directions

1. Put salt and paprika in a kettle, then add finely chopped lamb meat
2. Pour in water to cover everything
3. Cook over gentle heat for one hour, then turn and continue cooking until it reduces to fat

title: Bor-eczet éretlen szőlőből (Wine Vinegar from Unripe Grapes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Unripe grapes, picked before ripening
- Water

## Directions

1. Press the unripe grapes that have been picked early
2. Place the juice in a pot and set it over heat, boiling for half an hour while carefully skimming off the foam
3. Pour into bottles and place in the sun for several days until fermentation begins
4. Pour into clean bottles, seal, and place in the cellar
5. This vinegar is especially good for chopped dishes

title: Bor-pép (Wine Pap)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 grated bread rolls
- 5 dekagrams butter
- 4 decilitres white wine
- 7 dekagrams butter
- 1 whole egg
- 5 egg yolks
- zest of 1 lemon
- 14 dekagrams ground sugar
- 4 egg whites
- fine breadcrumb powder

## Directions

1. Toast the grated bread in 5 dekagrams of butter without browning
2. Thin with 4 decilitres of white wine
3. Cook with continuous stirring and cool
4. Cream 7 dekagrams of butter and beat in the whole egg and 5 egg yolks one by one
5. Add the minced lemon zest and 14 dekagrams of ground sugar
6. When foamy, gently fold in the stiff foam of 4 egg whites
7. Fill a buttered mould coated with fine breadcrumb powder with the pap
8. Bake until done

title: Borjú-agyvelő citrom-mártásban (Calf's Brain in Lemon Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- red onion
- good broth
- lemon zest
- lemon juice
- calf's brain
- salted water

## Directions

1. Fry one tablespoon of flour in melted butter
2. Add red onion cut in half and dilute with good broth
3. When the sauce is cooked, remove the onion and add lemon zest and lemon juice
4. Cook the calf's brain in salted water, remove its membrane cleanly, and cut into equal pieces
5. Place in the sauce, bring to a boil for a few minutes, and serve

title: Borjú-agyvelő kompot (Calf's Brain Compote)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's brain
- salted water
- butter sauce
- 10-12 mushrooms
- butter
- green parsley
- twenty crayfish
- twenty asparagus tips
- butter pastry wreath or braised mushrooms

## Directions

1. Rinse the calf's brain in water, blanch it in salted water, remove its skin, and cut into large pieces
2. Prepare a butter sauce
3. Slice 10-12 mushrooms and braise with butter and green parsley
4. Clean the tails and claws from twenty crayfish
5. Cut the heads from twenty asparagus tips boiled in salted water
6. Place all of this together with the brain in the sauce
7. Bring to a boil several times and serve neatly
8. Place the asparagus, crayfish tails, and brain in the center of the plate, and arrange around it a butter pastry wreath or braised mushrooms

title: Borjú-fülek, finom ragoutval (Veal Ears with Fine Ragout)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 veal ears
- 100 grams beef marrow fat
- same amount flour
- 3 liters water
- red onion
- spices
- 20 grams salt
- 10 grams pepper
- white bread crumb
- hot fat
- veal tongue
- venison mushroom
- goose liver
- rooster comb
- other mushrooms
- 2/10 liter venison mushroom broth
- same amount mushroom broth
- poultry essence
- 1 liter brown sauce
- stuffed crayfish
- piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Blanch 4 veal ears in rapidly boiling water for 20 minutes.
2. Plunge in cold water, drain and clean carefully.
3. Prepare the broth: melt 100 grams beef marrow fat, mix in the same amount flour, pour 3 liters water over it.
4. Add pieces of red onion, few spices, 20 grams salt and 10 grams pepper.
5. Bring to boil once, stir with spoon, place cleaned veal ears in it and cook gently in covered pan until tender.
6. Remove and drain the moisture, dry carefully.
7. Cut a rectangular cone from white bread crumb 8 centimetres high with base 5 square centimetres and top 3 square centimetres.
8. Fry golden brown in hot fat and place in the center of the plate.
9. Arrange the veal ears on the four sides of it.
10. Prepare ragout from veal tongue, venison mushroom, goose liver, rooster comb and other mushroom.
11. Mix with the following sauce: in a pan put 2/10 liter venison mushroom broth, the same amount mushroom broth and poultry essence, add 1 liter brown sauce and reduce to half thickness.
12. Mix the ragout with this sauce and decorate the arranged veal ears with it and stuffed crayfish, surrounding with piquant sauce.

title: Borjú-Karbonád Káprival (Veal Cutlet with Capers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal cutlets
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs
- sour cream
- butter
- lemon zest
- capers
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the membrane completely from the cutlet bones, pound to shape nicely round, salt and coat with melted butter
2. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs and broil nicely
3. Place on a plate, pour three deciliters of good sour cream over it
4. Add a few pieces of butter, finely chopped lemon zest, and capers on top
5. Place coals above and below, bring to a boil and finally squeeze out the juice of one lemon over it

title: Borjú-kádka Vagdalékkal (Veal Cake with Chopped Meat)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- butter
- cartilage
- salt water
- ten to twelve mushrooms
- fresh parsley
- potatoes
- six large boiled floury potatoes
- eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare a light butter sauce
2. Braise the veal and cut into cubes
3. Cut cartilage boiled in salted water into similar pieces
4. Clean and chop ten to twelve mushrooms and braise with chopped fresh parsley and a little butter
5. Add everything to the butter sauce and cook until the sauce is completely reduced
6. Peel and cool six large boiled floury potatoes
7. When cooled, grate them and beat in a bowl with five decagrams of butter
8. Gradually add one whole egg and the yolks of four eggs, beating well
9. Mix in one whole egg white beaten to stiff peaks and salt
10. Fill buttered cake molds, lined with fine breadcrumbs, half full with the beaten potato
11. Place the chopped meat mixture in the center and top with more potato
12. Bake at low heat

title: Borjú-láb és fej tojással (Veal Leg and Head with Egg)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal legs cleaned
- veal head cleaned
- 1.5 liters water
- salt
- pepper
- 2 decilitres good sour cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 good spoonful flour
- hot meat broth
- 2 whole lemons

## Directions

1. Put the nicely cleaned veal legs and head to cook in 1.5 liters water for one hour.
2. Remove from broth and remove all bones from the meat.
3. Place back in the broth and season with salt and pepper, bring to boil again.
4. Meanwhile prepare a liaison in a deep bowl for one head and four legs: take 2 decilitres good sour cream and 4 egg yolks, 1 good spoonful flour.
5. Mix well so it is not lumpy.
6. Slowly pour the hot meat broth into the liaison in the bowl with continuous stirring.
7. Once incorporated, pour back into the warm pot, remove from strong heat as the egg will curdle.
8. At serving time squeeze the juice of 2 whole lemons into it, but not before as it would become bitter.
9. Serve in deep bowl.

title: Borjú-mell Friss Csiperke-gombával és Vajjal Töltve (Veal Breast Stuffed with Fresh Mushrooms and Butter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast, split
- Fresh button mushrooms, small and cleaned
- Butter, 125-150 grams
- Salt
- Hot water

## Directions

1. Stuff the split veal breast with small cleaned fresh button mushrooms and 125-150 grams of fine butter
2. Salt the mushrooms slightly before filling and sewing closed
3. Place in a baking pan, salt, and pour hot water over it
4. Bake, basting frequently
5. When the broth is beautifully brown, sprinkle a spoon of flour over it and pour in a very small amount of sour cream; the broth should be beautifully brown

title: Borjúczomb füstölt nyelvvel tűzve (Veal Leg Larded with Smoked Tongue)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized veal leg
- salt
- smoked tongue already cooked
- little marrow
- butter
- head of red onion studded with cloves
- half lemon rind and its juice
- 3 decilitres wine
- 2 decilitres broth
- 3-4 decagrams capers
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Wash and salt a medium-sized veal leg.
2. Cut already cooked smoked tongue and a little marrow into long pieces.
3. Make holes with a narrow knife and lard alternately with tongue or marrow.
4. Line a pan with butter, place red onion head studded with cloves, half lemon rind and its juice in it.
5. Place the leg on top, pour 3 decilitres wine and 2 decilitres broth over it.
6. Cover and braise slowly for two hours, turning several times.
7. When completely braised and crumbly, chop 3-4 decagrams capers.
8. Place the leg in another pan, strain the sauce over it and add the capers mixed with a few spoonfuls of sour cream.
9. Braise a little more, place the leg on dish and pour the sauce over it.

title: Borjúczomb szardellával (Veal Leg with Anchovies)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal leg
- Salt
- Four anchovies, cut lengthwise
- Garlic, cut very thinly

## Directions

1. Prepare and salt the leg
2. Make holes in it with a thin knife
3. Stud with lengthwise-cut anchovies and very thinly sliced garlic
4. Let rest for one hour
5. Roast over steady heat
6. Leave on the spit about half an hour longer than usual
7. Baste diligently with butter
8. Half an hour before serving pour six to eight spoonfuls of sour cream over it
9. Place on serving dish and pour sauce underneath

title: Borjúczomb sülve (Roasted Veal Leg)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal leg
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Clean the leg thoroughly and salt it
2. Let it rest in salt for two hours
3. Thread it onto a spit so the upper part is rounded and the shanks can be tied to the spit in proper direction
4. Roast over steady heat or in the oven
5. Baste with butter and broth
6. Finally drizzle only pure butter on it so it takes on a beautiful color and can be served glossy on the table
7. Pour the broth underneath if desired

title: Borjúfej frikasszirozva (Calf's Head Fricassee)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head
- salted water
- butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- medium-sized red onion
- green parsley
- broth
- grated nutmeg
- three eggs
- butter pastry for garnish

## Directions

1. Cook the calf's head in salted water until tender
2. Remove and place in cold water
3. Melt a piece of butter and fry two tablespoons of flour in it until golden
4. Add red onion cut in half and green parsley
5. Dilute with broth and cook the sauce
6. Cut the head into equal pieces and place in a pot
7. Strain the sauce over it
8. Add a little grated nutmeg and bring to a boil together
9. Before serving, beat three eggs well in a pan, gradually add the sauce, place the pan on a hot stove and stir until it thickens
10. Place the head on a plate, pour the sauce over it, and decorate the edge with a wreath made of butter pastry

title: Borjúhús ropogó (Veal Croquettes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram of veal
- 7 decagrams of bacon
- 1 head of red onion
- carrots, sliced into rings
- 2 whole eggs
- white or brown butter sauce
- wafers
- breadcrumbs, fine
- egg, beaten
- veal kidneys
- asparagus or artichoke, depending on availability
- mushrooms
- crayfish tails
- parsley, green and fried

## Directions

1. Clean and salt 1 kilogram of veal and cut into large pieces
2. Place 7 decagrams of bacon, 1 head of red onion, and sliced carrots at the bottom of the pot
3. Braise until the onion and carrots are completely soft and the veal becomes light brown, occasionally pouring stock over so it does not burn
4. When well braised, beat 2 whole eggs into it and remove from heat without stirring
5. Cool until the eggs set without hardening
6. When cooled, place the whole mixture with bacon and onion on a cutting board and chop finely
7. Place back in the pot and pour several spoonfuls of good white or brown butter sauce over it
8. Cook until thick and cool
9. When cooled, quickly dip wafers in fresh water and place a spoonful of chopped meat in the middle
10. Fold together well and roll in beaten egg and fine breadcrumbs
11. Fry in hot fat
12. Place a mixture made from veal kidneys, asparagus or artichoke, mushrooms and crayfish tails at the bottom of the dish
13. Pile the croquettes high and garnish with fried green parsley

title: Borjúhús-meridon (Veal Meridon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half a kilogram veal meat
- salt
- bacon slices
- red onion
- carrot
- meat broth
- green parsley
- five decagrams butter
- two tablespoons flour
- three deciliters milk
- one whole egg
- one egg yolk
- ragout ingredients: veal meat, veal breast, asparagus, shrimp tails, button mushrooms

## Directions

1. Clean and salt half a kilogram of veal meat, braise in a pot with bacon slices, sliced red onion, and sliced carrot, constantly adding a little broth so it does not brown
2. Once the meat is braised, chop finely, add finely chopped onion and green parsley, braise briefly with a little butter, then add the minced mixture and braise a little more
3. Make a béchamel from five decagrams butter, two tablespoons flour, and three deciliters of milk; mix in the minced mixture, season with salt, beat in one whole egg and one egg yolk, and mix well
4. Fill a butter-coated pot 2 centimeters high with the minced mixture and coat the entire perimeter of the pot 1 finger-width thick with the mixture
5. Prepare a ragout from veal meat, veal breast, asparagus, shrimp tails, and button mushrooms, cooking the sauce until thick, then beat in two egg yolks and fill the lined pot with the ragout

title: Borjúszegy Citrom-mártásban (Veal Loin in Lemon Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal loin
- cold water
- butter
- flour
- good stock
- lemon peel
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Blanch the veal loin
2. Place in cold water for a few minutes and rinse off the foam cleanly
3. Cut into thin slices
4. Melt butter in a pot, toast two spoonfuls of flour in it without browning it
5. Thin it with good stock and place the veal meat in it
6. Add lemon peel and the juice of one lemon
7. Cook until sufficiently tender

title: Borjúszegy Glacé-ban (Glazed Veal Loin)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful veal loin with ribs and cartilage cleaned
- finely chopped bacon in five or six rows
- ham pieces
- onion or nutmeg onion
- brown sauce
- 28 dekagrams veal
- bread soaked in milk and grated
- two or three egg yolks
- finely chopped onion
- fresh parsley finely chopped
- flour

## Directions

1. Blanch the veal loin, ribs, and cartilage as described in the first part, page 40
2. Lard it with finely chopped bacon in five or six rows
3. Cut the bacon with large sharp scissors so the whole resembles a spiny hedgehog
4. Place in a pot with ham pieces, onion or nutmeg onion, and fill with brown sauce
5. Cover and braise until tender
6. Remove the loin, strain the sauce through a sieve back into the pot, and braise until it becomes glacé with only a small amount of sauce remaining
7. Return the loin to the pot and coat well on all sides, especially where it is larded
8. Place in the center of the serving dish and pour the small amount of sauce over it
9. Prepare a wreath as follows: make a forcemeat from 28 dekagrams veal and bread soaked in milk and grated
10. Chop finely and grind to breadcrumbs
11. Add two or three egg yolks to make it into a paste
12. Add finely chopped onion and fresh parsley
13. Place on a flour-dusted board and make a thick sausage
14. Cook in beef broth, then cut into knife-thickness slices
15. Arrange these slices around the edge of the dish to form a nice golden wreath

title: Borjúszegy Spárgával, Rákkal és Zöld Borsóval Töltve (Veal Loin Stuffed with Asparagus, Crayfish, and Green Peas)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal loin cleaned
- bread
- milk
- asparagus
- crayfish
- crayfish butter
- parsley
- green peas
- egg
- egg yolk
- beef broth
- crayfish mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the loin and prepare the stuffing
2. Soak grated bread in milk
3. Blanch the asparagus and crayfish, clean and cut into small cubes
4. Make crayfish butter from the shells
5. Squeeze out the bread, chop finely and braise slightly with parsley
6. Add the asparagus with the crayfish tails and a little green peas and braise
7. Remove from heat, season and salt, cool slightly
8. Beat one egg and one yolk and mix well together
9. Stuff the loin and place in a pot on pebbles
10. Pour beef broth over it and braise until nicely tender
11. Before serving, prepare crayfish mushroom sauce and pour it over the loin

title: Borjúszegy-szágóval (Veal Loin with Sago)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal loin
- red onion sliced
- carrot
- bacon
- flour
- stock
- vinegar
- lemon peel
- sour cream
- sago
- butter pastry

## Directions

1. Remove the ribs from the loin and blanch
2. Place sliced red onion, carrot, and bacon in a pot and place the loin on top
3. Braise slowly until nicely browned
4. Remove and sprinkle two spoonfuls of flour on it and brown
5. Thin with stock and add a few spoonfuls of vinegar
6. Strain through a sieve
7. Return the loin to the pot, add finely chopped long lemon peel, three spoonfuls of good sour cream, and 3-4 dekagrams of sago that has been cleaned and washed
8. Braise until the sago is tender
9. When serving, place in the center of the dish, pour the sauce over it, and prepare a wreath of butter pastry

title: Borkifli (Wine-Soaked Crescent Rolls)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- six crumbly crescent rolls
- preserved cherries or raspberries without seeds
- sugar
- four deciliters good red wine

## Directions

1. Grate the crust of six crumbly crescent rolls and cut them in half lengthwise
2. Hollow out the interior and fill with preserved cherries or raspberries without seeds
3. Make a small stopper from the removed crumb
4. Dissolve sugar in four deciliters of good red wine in a deep dish to make it sweet
5. Pour the wine mixture over the crescent rolls and soften them in it
6. Turn them several times so they absorb the liquid evenly on all sides, but do not let them become too soft
7. Roll them in fine breadcrumbs and fry them quickly in fat until golden
8. Sprinkle with sugar and vanilla

title: Borkő-eczet (Tartar Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Good vinegar
- Boiling water
- 42 dekagrams alcohol
- Wine tartrate
- Burnt sugar or raspberry

## Directions

1. Prepare two small wooden barrels made of beech or oak wood with iron hoops
2. On the front side of the first barrel, above the tap, make an opening 3-4 centimeters from the barrel rim to serve as an air hole, with a diameter of 1½ centimeters
3. Place both barrels in a cellar that can be kept warm in winter
4. Fill the first barrel with good vinegar up to the air hole (approximately 7 liters)
5. Let the barrel rest for 14 days so the vinegar penetrates the wood well
6. Draw off 2 liters and place in the second barrel, replacing it with boiling water to which 42 dekagrams of alcohol are added
7. Every 3 weeks, draw off 2 liters and pour into the vinegar reserve in the other barrel, replacing with boiling water and alcohol in the same manner
8. If you wish to make this vinegar entirely equal to wine vinegar, dissolve a small amount of wine tartrate in it and color it with burnt sugar or raspberry

title: Borleves (Wine Soup for Fasting)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter of good white wine
- Sugar, a piece
- Cinnamon, a small amount
- Lemon peel
- Cloves, a few
- Egg yolks, four
- Sugar, for dusting
- Bread slices

## Directions

1. Bring one liter of good white wine to a boil with a piece of sugar, a small amount of cinnamon, lemon peel, and a few cloves
2. Before serving, remove the spices
3. In a bowl, mix together four egg yolks and slowly pour the hot wine into them so they do not curdle
4. Serve with bread slices dusted with sugar

title: Bormártás (Wine Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of table wine
- half a lemon peel
- broken cinnamon
- two cloves
- twenty egg yolks
- twenty dekagrams of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put half a lemon peel, a knife's point of broken cinnamon and two cloves in one liter of table wine
2. Boil together in a porcelain dish
3. While the wine heats, beat twenty egg yolks in a deep pot with twenty dekagrams of powdered sugar until completely foamy
4. When the wine is hot, slowly pour it into the egg and sugar mixture while continuously stirring the wine sauce with a wooden spoon so the entire pot becomes filled with thick foamy sauce
5. Do not cook further as the egg would curdle with the wine
6. Keep in a warm place and continuously stir with the spoon until serving, otherwise it cannot be beaten foamy again

title: Bormártás más módon (Wine Sauce, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sixteen egg yolks
- twenty five dekagrams of powdered sugar
- one liter of good wine
- half a lemon peel
- broken cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat sixteen egg yolks in a deep dish with twenty five dekagrams of powdered sugar until foamy
2. Put one liter of good wine in a ceramic or porcelain dish to boil
3. Add half a lemon peel and a knife's point of broken cinnamon to the wine
4. When the spiced wine is boiling, slowly pour it into the sugar and egg mixture while constantly stirring with a wooden spoon
5. Place the mixture at the edge of the fire where it stays continuously warm but not actively boiling
6. Prepare the wine sauce only shortly before serving as it will curdle if left standing
7. Continuously stir during this short time so it becomes thick and foamy

title: Borsó-pudding (Pea Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of dried peas, cooked until soft
- melted butter
- flour (one cooking spoonful)
- good meat broth (one serving spoon)
- cooked tongue, finely sliced and pickled
- roasted sausages, browned
- salt

## Directions

1. Press the cooked peas through a sieve
2. Brown one cooking spoonful of flour in melted butter until golden
3. Mix with the pressed peas
4. Add one serving spoon of good meat broth and cook until it becomes a thick paste, then cool
5. Grease a pan well with butter and fill it with the pea mixture to a height of 2 cm
6. Add a layer of cooked tongue sliced finely and pickled
7. Then add another layer of peas and a row of beautifully browned sausages cut into pieces and split through the middle
8. Continue alternating peas and pickled tongue until the pan is full
9. Steam for half an hour
10. Turn out onto a plate and decorate with finely chopped pickled tongue

title: Borsó-élesztő (Pea Yeast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon of peas
- 4 deciliters of boiling water

## Directions

1. Crush a tablespoon of peas
2. Pour into 4 deciliters of boiling water
3. Place the mixture in a warm location for one night
4. The next morning, foam will appear on the surface, which has all the properties of good yeast

title: Borsóvíz (Pea Broth)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 liter dried peas
- 3 liters fresh spring water
- carrot
- parsley root
- celery
- a few fried breadcrumbs
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put 0.5 liter dried peas in a pot and fill with 3 liters of clean spring water
2. Cook, adding carrot, parsley root, celery, a few fried breadcrumbs, and a little nutmeg
3. Simmer slowly and use as needed for fasting dishes and soups

title: Bosporos lé (Byzantine Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted poultry
- one tablespoon hot fat
- a good handful of breadcrumbs or good homemade bread crumbs
- small raisins
- cleaned finely broken almonds
- meat broth
- lemon slices
- optional: strong boiled wine

## Directions

1. Toast a good handful of breadcrumbs in one tablespoon hot fat until beautifully red
2. Add small raisins and cleaned finely broken almonds generously
3. Dilute with meat broth, sour with lemon slices
4. When it comes to a boil, add the roasted poultry pieces
5. Let cook for a little while until the meat is heated through, then remove from heat and keep warm until serving
6. Alternatively, dilute with good strong boiled wine, in which case only lemon zest is added

title: Bouquet Water
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 liters strong alcohol
- 4 grams clove oil
- 4 grams clove extract
- 4 grams bergamot oil
- 1 gram thyme oil
- 4.5 dekagrams rose extract
- 4.5 dekagrams orange extract
- 4.5 dekagrams cinnamon extract
- 4.5 dekagrams mignonette extract
- 4.5 dekagrams jasmine extract
- 4.5 dekagrams jonquil extract
- 4.5 dekagrams violet extract
- 4.5 dekagrams tuberose extract
- 1 gram amber essence
- 2 grams musk essence

## Directions

1. Dissolve clove oil, clove extract, bergamot oil, and thyme oil in strong alcohol
2. Add rose, orange, cinnamon, mignonette, jasmine, jonquil, violet, and tuberose extracts
3. Add amber essence and musk essence
4. Shake well

title: Braised Kale with Rice and Ham (Párolt Kelkáposzta Rizszsel és Sonkával)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- firm head of kale
- salted water
- bacon slices
- ham slices
- red onion
- carrot
- black peppercorns
- clove-pepper
- brown broth
- sour cream
- beef broth
- rice
- warm water
- butter or goose fat
- thin ham slices
- lean ham
- butter paper

## Directions

1. Wash a beautiful firm head of kale cleanly and boil in salted water for a few minutes without making it too soft
2. Strain and place on a sieve
3. In a larger pot put several bacon slices, the same amount of ham slices, chopped red onion and carrot, eight peppercorns and five clove-peppers
4. Cut the kale lengthwise in half and place it cut-side down in the pot
5. Cover and braise slowly, occasionally pouring a little brown broth on it so it does not burn
6. When the vegetable has a nice light-brown color on the bottom of the pot, carefully remove the kale
7. Mix a spoonful of sour cream with it and brown it with frequent stirring
8. Thin it with good beef broth and cook for a few more minutes until soft but not falling apart
9. Remove and set aside
10. Prepare the braised rice: soften ten decagrams of cleaned rice in warm water half an hour before use
11. Melt a piece of butter or goose fat and add the drained rice to it
12. Put half a red onion, a few peppercorns and salt in the middle of the rice and braise slowly, occasionally pouring one or two spoonfuls of good broth over it so the rice braises but does not boil, keeping the grains beautiful, white and whole while becoming soft without becoming soupy
13. Cover the bottom of a pot buttered with a paper that fits exactly
14. Cut thin slices from beautiful ham and leave a narrow strip of bacon on it
15. Cut the ham slices into triangular pieces and arrange them on the bottom of the pot so no space remains and the triangles fit together well
16. Finely chop a piece of lean ham
17. Remove the red onion and peppercorns from the braised rice
18. With a spoon, layer the rice smoothly and firmly in the pot, sprinkle thickly with chopped ham, then rice again and so on until the pot is full
19. Cover and steam for three-quarters of an hour
20. Before serving, strain the sauce in which the kale braised and let it braise a little
21. Carefully turn the kale out onto the plate, pour a little sauce on the bottom of the plate and serve with a beautifully prepared veal carbonade

title: Braised Sour Cabbage (Barnára párolt savanyú-káposzta)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons lard
- 3 tablespoons coarsely crushed sugar
- red onion, finely chopped
- sour cabbage
- sour cabbage juice
- a little flour
- a little broth

## Directions

1. Heat the lard in a pot
2. Add the coarsely crushed sugar and allow it to brown nicely
3. Add the finely chopped red onion and fry lightly
4. Press out the juice from the sour cabbage and add the cabbage to the browned sugar
5. Braise slowly, stirring frequently with a large fork, until it takes on a nice brown color
6. Sprinkle a little flour over it and braise again well
7. Pour a little broth over it, but it should not become soup-like
8. Cook together for some time

title: Braised Sour Cabbage Another Way (Párolt savanyú káposzta más módon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 firm heads of cabbage
- salt
- 1 tablespoon goose fat
- 1 tablespoon crushed sugar
- 1 teaspoon flour
- 2 tablespoons good white vinegar
- a few tablespoons sour cream
- broth

## Directions

1. Clean the outer leaves of the cabbage head and chop finely
2. Place in a bowl, salt it, and let it rest in the salt for a good hour
3. Heat 1 tablespoon goose fat and add 1 tablespoon crushed sugar
4. Brown the sugar with frequent stirring
5. Press out the cabbage, place in a pot and put in the oven
6. Stir frequently with a fork
7. When nicely browned, sprinkle with a small teaspoon of flour and braise for a short time
8. Pour 2 tablespoons good white vinegar over it and braise for a few more minutes
9. Pour in a few tablespoons of sour cream and the same amount of broth
10. Braise until it becomes a good sauce
11. The cabbage should be soft but not mushy, and should remain whole, so stir with a fork
12. If serving with pheasant: pluck, prepare and salt the pheasant, pierce the breast and roast quickly so the outside is completely brown but not fully cooked through; place in the finished cabbage and braise together for half an hour; when serving, place the pheasant in the center of the platter and arrange the cabbage around it
13. Alternatively, a capon can be used the same way

title: Braised Sour Cabbage with Rice or Potatoes (Párolt savanyú káposzta rizszsel, vagy burgonyával)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a little goose fat
- 2 tablespoons crushed sugar
- pressed sour cabbage
- 14 dekagrams rice
- a little butter or goose fat
- 1/2 peeled red onion, kept whole
- ham slices, triangular
- finely chopped ham
- butter for greasing the pot

## Directions

1. Melt a little goose fat in a pot
2. Add 2 tablespoons crushed sugar and brown it nicely with frequent stirring
3. Add the pressed sour cabbage and braise it frequently with a long fork until it becomes a nice golden yellow and soft
4. Meanwhile, braise 14 dekagrams of rice gently with a little butter or goose fat and half a peeled red onion that should remain whole, until soft
5. Line the bottom of a butter-greased pot with triangular ham slices arranged nicely side by side
6. Layer with braised rice, sprinkled densely with finely chopped ham
7. Add a layer of brown cabbage, similarly sprinkled with ham
8. Continue alternating layers until the pot is full
9. Cover with a fitting lid and braise for half an hour
10. If using potatoes instead of rice, cook and peel them, cut into thin slices and layer the same way as the rice, but pour fresh sour cream over each potato layer so they do not dry out
11. Turn out onto a platter and top with sausage or pickled tongue

title: Braised White Kohlrabi (Fehérre Párolt Kalarábi)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young kohlrabi
- fresh parsley
- fat
- chicken
- flour
- nutmeg flower
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Peel young kohlrabi and cut into thin knife-blade-thick slices
2. Cut out round slices with a small cutter
3. Braise with finely chopped fresh parsley and rendered fat, occasionally adding a little broth so they become soft while remaining white
4. Clean and portion one or two chickens
5. Braise the chicken pieces similarly with rendered fat
6. Sprinkle flour over the chicken to achieve the proper thickness
7. Add a little nutmeg flower and cook until the chickens are slightly tender
8. Add one or two spoonfuls of good sour cream to the kohlrabi
9. Once everything is braised tender, arrange the kohlrabi in the center of the plate and the chicken pieces beautifully around the edge

title: Broken Soup (Tört leves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large pike head
- salt water
- pea water
- pike
- bread soaked in milk
- eggs
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Made exactly as on meat days, except use a large pike head instead of chicken and pea water instead of meat broth
2. Serve with pike dumplings
3. For the pike dumplings: cook a good piece of pike, about one kilogram, in salted water
4. Clean of bones and skin
5. Add two beaten bread slices soaked in milk and squeezed out, three beaten eggs and green parsley

title: Brown Braised Sour Cabbage (Barnára Párolt Savanyú-Káposzta)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pork fat
- crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Heat two spoonfuls of pork fat in a pot
2. Add three spoonfuls of coarsely crushed sugar and when it is beautifully browned
3. [Recipe appears to be incomplete in the source text]

title: Bréz (Braising Liquid)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams (approximately 1/2 pound) ham
- 28 dekagrams fat

## Directions

1. Combine the ham and fat according to the proportions given

title: Buktatott Kalarábi Töltve (Layered Stuffed Kohlrabi)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tender kohlrabi
- salt water
- butter
- sour cream
- cooked kohlrabi
- good filling (as for stuffed kohlrabi)
- pickled tongue
- egg yolks
- coffee cup of sour cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the kohlrabi in salted water after peeling and salting it until it begins to soften slightly
2. Drain and cut into slices of knife-thickness width
3. Melt butter in a pan, quickly mix with a good spoonful of fresh sour cream
4. Add the chopped kohlrabi and braise for a quarter hour with frequent shaking
5. Keep the kohlrabi pale by braising on low heat
6. Prepare a good filling as needed for stuffed kohlrabi
7. Coat the bottom of a buttered pan alternately with a layer of kohlrabi and an equal-sized piece of finely chopped and cooked pickled tongue
8. When the first row is neatly arranged on the bottom, spread the filling smoothly over it
9. Add another row of braised kohlrabi slices, but without pickled tongue this time
10. Then add filling again and continue this way until everything is used
11. Beat three egg yolks with a coffee cup of thin sour cream and a little salt until well mixed
12. Pour this mixture over the kohlrabi and steam for one hour
13. At serving time, turn out onto a dish and pour good butter sauce underneath, which contains finely peeled and chopped kohlrabi cooked in it
14. Border the edges with pickled tongue

title: Buktatott Tojás (Filled Hard-Boiled Eggs with Crayfish and Fish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- finely chopped crayfish tails
- bread soaked in milk, grated
- 1 whole egg
- 3 tablespoons sour cream
- salt
- small piece of crayfish butter
- pike, finely shredded
- crayfish tails, cut lengthwise
- mushrooms
- green parsley
- green peas
- butter
- pepper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut 6 hard-boiled eggs in half lengthwise
2. Place in a mortar: chopped crayfish tails, grated milk-soaked bread, 1 whole egg, 3 tablespoons sour cream, salt, and a small piece of crayfish butter
3. Pound finely
4. Place half of this filling on a crayfish butter-greased dish
5. Prepare a ragout from: shredded pike, lengthwise-cut crayfish tails, and mushrooms
6. Stew this with green parsley and green peas in butter
7. Season with salt and a little pepper
8. Stew together with the crayfish tails
9. Fill the eggs with this ragout
10. Place the filled eggs in the dish
11. Top with the remaining half of the filling
12. Add a few tablespoons sour cream, a little crayfish butter, and fine breadcrumbs
13. Place in the oven and let heat through

title: Bukó Kácsa Sülve (Roast Wild Duck)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one wild duck
- salt
- marinade
- cream
- butter
- flour
- duck liver
- capers

## Directions

1. Pluck and singe the duck lightly, then clean thoroughly.
2. Remove the entrails, salt well, and if weather permits, marinate for one day.
3. Lard the duck and roast until golden-brown, basting frequently with the marinade and cream.
4. When roasted, finely chop the duck's liver and mix it into a roux made from melted butter and flour.
5. Add a few spoonfuls of the marinade and juice that dripped from the duck during roasting, along with two spoonfuls of cream.
6. Add capers and pour this sauce over the duck when serving.

title: Bundás kenyér (Fried Bread)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- several whole eggs
- salt
- white bread (sliced finger-thick)
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Beat several whole eggs and mix with a little salt
2. Dip finger-thick slices of white bread into this mixture and fry them in hot fat
3. It is tasty if the bread slice is dipped in milk

title: Burgonya borjú-aprólékkal (Potato with Veal Offal)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- veal offal
- parsley
- red onion, small head
- five anchovies, washed and cleaned of bones
- butter
- salt
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes until tender, peel and slice them
2. Cook veal offal until tender and chop it coarsely
3. Chop parsley, red onion, and anchovies finely
4. Melt butter and add all the chopped ingredients, add salt and braise everything together well
5. Place a few spoonfuls of sour cream and a few pieces of butter on a plate
6. Add a layer of potatoes, salt them
7. Add more sour cream and another layer of potatoes, continue until everything is arranged on the plate
8. Top with butter and sour cream
9. Garnish with anchovies cut lengthwise and bake until golden

title: Burgonya rostélyossal (Potatoes with Roasted Meat)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram of roasted meat
- salt
- lard
- red onion (two heads), finely chopped
- potatoes, cooked
- sour cream (two deciliters)
- butter or other fat
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Beat the roasted meat well, salt it, and set aside for half an hour
2. Put a little lard and the finely chopped red onion in a pan, add the roasted meat, and stew until tender, turning frequently
3. Once tender, remove and cut into equal oblong pieces
4. Return to the pan, add two deciliters of sour cream and stew a while longer
5. Slice the cooked potatoes
6. Grease a dish with butter or other fat, add a little sour cream
7. Layer the stewed roasted meat and onion that cooked with it, then a row of potatoes, then roasted meat again, and continue alternating until all ingredients are used
8. Top with sour cream and several pieces of butter, sprinkle with breadcrumbs
9. Place coals above and below so it browns nicely

title: Burgonya tejből (Potatoes in Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ten to twelve boiled potatoes
- seven deciliters good milk
- butter
- one small coffee cup cream
- eggs

## Directions

1. Peel the boiled potatoes and cut into thick slices
2. Bring the milk to a boil
3. Add the potatoes and cook until the milk reduces without turning to mush
4. Place several pieces of butter on top and pour a small coffee cup of cream over it
5. Brown quickly until nicely browned
6. Place an egg-based ring around the edge of the plate
7. Serve in the same dish
8. The potatoes should be neither soupy nor dry

title: Burgonya-kádka (Potato Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagrams good floury cooked peeled and cooled potato, grated
- 14 dekagrams finely powdered sugar
- 7 dekagrams finely powdered and soaked almonds
- zest of one lemon, finely chopped
- 2 whole eggs
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Place grated potato in a bowl with powdered sugar, blanched almonds, lemon zest, whole eggs, and egg yolks
2. Beat until well foamed
3. Fold in stiffly beaten egg white
4. Fill buttered molds halfway
5. Bake at gentle heat

title: Burgonya-mártás (Potato Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four to five medium-sized raw peeled potatoes
- goose fat or other fat
- flour
- green parsley, finely chopped
- meat stock
- two tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut the raw peeled potatoes into cubes
2. Brown a small amount of flour in goose fat or other fat
3. Add the potatoes and finely chopped green parsley to this
4. Mix well together
5. Thin with meat stock
6. Cook with two tablespoons of sour cream and serve

title: Burgonya-pudding sonkával (Potato Pudding with Ham)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams cooked starchy potatoes
- 10 dekagrams butter
- 3 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 28 dekagrams finely chopped ham
- salt
- grated Parmesan cheese
- hot butter

## Directions

1. Grate 28 dekagrams cooked starchy potatoes
2. Whip 10 dekagrams butter until fluffy
3. Add 3 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks one after another
4. Add the grated potatoes and 28 dekagrams finely chopped ham
5. Season with salt and stir well
6. Pour into a buttered basin and steam
7. After 1 hour remove the pudding, turn onto a plate, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese and pour hot butter over it

title: Burgonya-Pástétom (Potato Pâté)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crumbly pastry
- 8-10 boiled potatoes
- salt
- cooked ham, finely chopped
- grated Parmesan cheese
- sour cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare crumbly pastry and stretch it to knife-thickness
2. Line a pot with the pastry
3. Slice 8-10 boiled potatoes and salt them
4. Finely chop some cooked ham
5. Layer potatoes, then ham, then potatoes again in the pâté dish
6. Sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese
7. Add a little sour cream and butter on top
8. Continue layering until the pot is full
9. Prepare a pastry lid
10. Brush with egg white and bake slowly in the oven

title: Burgonya-pép (Potato Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramms cooked, floury potatoes
- 7 dekagramms butter
- 8 eggs
- 7 dekagramms finely ground almonds (some bitter)
- 10 dekagramms ground sugar
- zest of one lemon, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Crush the cooked potatoes and butter together in a mortar
2. Place in a bowl
3. Beat in the eggs one by one
4. Add the almonds, sugar and lemon zest
5. Stir until fluffy
6. Fold in the stiff peaks of 4 egg whites
7. Grease a cake pan with butter
8. Fill with the mixture
9. Bake slowly
10. When baked, remove the rim, sprinkle with sugar and serve to the table

title: Burgonya-rétes (Potato Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- eighteen dekagramm potato
- 14 dekagramm butter
- six egg yolks
- 7 dekagramm finely ground almond
- 10 dekagramm crushed sugar
- one lemon's peel, finely chopped
- three egg whites
- rétes-tészta (strudel dough)

## Directions

1. Cook and cool the potato, then grate eighteen dekagramm potato
2. Beat 14 dekagramm butter until fluffy
3. Add the potato to it
4. Mix in six egg yolks, 7 dekagramm finely ground almond, 10 dekagramm crushed sugar, finely chopped peel of one lemon, and finally fold in the stiffly beaten whites of three eggs
5. Stir everything together for half an hour
6. Stretch the strudel dough finely and spread the filling on it
7. Spread it to the thickness of a knife blade
8. Fold it together gently
9. Brush with butter and place in the oven to bake until golden
10. Note: this strudel must be served immediately after baking so it does not collapse

title: Burgonya-torta (Potato Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagramm cooked floury potatoes, grated
- 14 dekagramm finely ground sugar
- a little vanilla
- 10 dekagramm softened and finely ground almonds
- zest of 1 orange, finely chopped
- 4 whole eggs
- 8 egg yolks
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Cook and cool the best kind of floury potatoes and grate 24 dekagramm of them
2. Mix in a bowl with finely ground sugar, a little vanilla, softened and finely ground almonds, and finely chopped orange zest
3. Beat in 4 whole eggs and 8 egg yolks one after another, stirring continuously for half an hour until the mixture grows and becomes foamy
4. Meanwhile grease three equal cake sheets with the hoops together with butter
5. Fill the mixture in three equal parts and bake to a nice light brown
6. Remove from the sheet, place on a sieve and cool
7. Fill two sheets with preserves of your choice, stack them evenly and decorate the top sheet as desired
8. Note: this cake must be prepared on the day it will be used, as it dries quickly

title: Burgonyagombócz (Potato Dumpling)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 80 decagrams grated potato
- 21 decagrams fine flour
- 14 decagrams bread cut into small cubes
- 21 decagrams butter
- 2 or 3 eggs
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Mix together 80 decagrams grated potato with 21 decagrams fine flour lightly
2. Fry 14 decagrams bread cut into small cubes in 21 decagrams butter until stiff
3. Sprinkle these on the potato and mix lightly together
4. Beat 2 or 3 eggs and pour over and with flour's help make dumplings from it
5. Cook in salty water and serve as flour dumplings

title: Burgonyaleves (Potato Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- parsnip
- carrot
- celery
- water
- salt
- potatoes
- butter
- flour
- parsley, finely chopped
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put parsley, parsnip, carrot, and celery in a pot, fill with water, salt, and cook for two hours
2. Peel the potatoes, cut into cubes, and strain the broth over them
3. Cook until tender
4. Make a thin yellow roux with butter and flour
5. Add finely chopped parsley to it, let it swell with the broth, add nutmeg, and cook everything well
6. When the potatoes are tender, serve the soup over fried bread

title: Burgonyaleves húsnapon (Potato Soup on Meat Day)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 medium-sized potatoes
- 1 liter broth
- 1/2 roll
- Hot fat
- 3 eggs
- Broth

## Directions

1. Cook 3 medium-sized potatoes in 1 liter of broth and after cooling and peeling, grate them.
2. Cut half a roll into larger slices and fry them until crispy in hot fat, but not brown.
3. Cook 3 eggs hard, peel them, remove the yolks, and pound the remainder finely in a mortar.
4. Place in a pot and thin with broth, cooking for one hour.
5. Pass the whole mixture through a sieve and arrange on fried potato dumplings and marrow cubes the size of forints.

title: Burgonyaleves más módon (Potato Soup Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Several potatoes
- Water
- Green parsley, finely chopped
- Marjoram
- Red onion
- Roux
- Paprika
- Salt
- Milk

## Directions

1. Wash and peel several potatoes, cut them into cubes, and put them on to cook in water.
2. When the potato is soft, make a thin roux with finely chopped parsley green, marjoram, and red onion.
3. Brown it with paprika and pour over the potato cooking liquid.
4. Finally add salt and milk and serve.

title: Burgunya-polenta (Potato Polenta)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- potato pudding made with breadcrumbs
- butter
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. First prepare a potato pudding with breadcrumbs
2. Once cooled, cut into slices in the manner described for polenta
3. Layer in a buttered mold with grated cheese
4. Bake in the same manner as polenta

title: Bába-foga (Grandmother's Tooth)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 whole eggs
- salt
- flour
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Beat 6 whole eggs in a bowl and salt it and beat well by hand
2. Take so much flour that it is rather soft than stiff, but must be worked very much
3. On the kneading board roll between the hands and cut into cubes, cutting four sides on each
4. The fat in which it is constantly fried should not be very hot

title: Bárány citrommal (Lamb with Lemon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two liters of water
- one quarter of a lamb from the front
- salt
- a little parsley root
- carrot
- celery
- one small head of red onion
- several peppercorns
- one tablespoon of flour
- one egg-sized amount of lard
- half a glass of cold water
- two lemons

## Directions

1. Put one quarter of lamb from the front, cut into pieces, into two liters of water with salt, a little parsley root, carrot, celery, a small head of red onion, and several peppercorns
2. Cook the lamb for three-quarters of an hour
3. While it cooks, prepare a roux from one tablespoon of flour and one egg-sized amount of lard
4. When the roux is at its best, pour half a glass of cold water into it and immediately add it to the simmering lamb broth
5. After cooking with the roux for two minutes, strain through a sieve
6. Remove the meat from among the vegetables and place it in the clear broth
7. At serving time, squeeze the juice of two lemons into it, but not before, as it will become bitter

title: Bárány Citrommal (Lamb with Lemon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two liters water
- one quarter lamb from the front
- salt
- parsley root
- carrot
- celery
- small head red onion
- peppercorns
- flour
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Place one quarter lamb in two liters of water, cut into pieces
2. Add salt, a little parsley root, carrot, celery, a small head of red onion and a few peppercorns
3. Cook the lamb with these for three-quarters of an hour
4. While cooking, prepare a mixture from one spoonful of flour and one egg yolk

title: Bárányszegy Szarvasgombával (Lamb Chops with Morels)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three lamb chops, kept in fresh water
- red onion, sliced
- parsley root, chopped
- celery
- carrots
- butter
- bacon
- fine parsley
- morels, cleaned
- flour
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place the lamb chops in a copper pan lined with bacon and fat, along with red onion, parsley root, celery, carrots, and butter
2. Cover well and braise until tender
3. In a separate pot, melt butter and add finely chopped parsley
4. Add cleaned morels and soften
5. Add three spoonfuls of flour and braise together for a time
6. Pour the lamb stock over the mushrooms
7. Squeeze lemon juice over it
8. Add the lamb chops and simmer until fully cooked

title: Bárányszegy tárkony-mártásban (Lamb Cutlets in Tarragon Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb cutlets
- butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- red onion
- tarragon
- broth
- vinegar
- two tablespoons of sour cream
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut blanched lamb cutlets into pieces
2. Melt butter and fry one tablespoon of flour in it
3. Add red onion cut into four pieces and a bunch of tarragon
4. Dilute with broth
5. Add a little vinegar and two tablespoons of sour cream, as well as the lamb cutlets
6. Cook until sufficiently tender
7. Place the lamb on a plate and strain the sauce
8. Bring the sauce back to a boil
9. Beat two egg yolks well in a pan, add the sauce, and pour over the meat slices on the plate

title: Béka frikasszirozva (Fricasseed Frog)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- half red onion
- pea water
- lemon rind
- frogs
- sour cream
- eggs
- cold water
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. In a pot, melt a piece of butter, add a spoonful of flour and half a red onion and fry for only one minute so it does not brown
2. Dilute with pea water, add a little lemon rind and cook for a while
3. Add the frogs, add a spoonful of sour cream and cook a little longer
4. In a small pot, place two to three egg yolks, a teaspoonful of cold water and the juice of one lemon, strain the sauce over them, stir on the warm stove for a while longer, pour over the frogs and serve immediately

title: Béka kucsma-gombával s rákfarkkal (Frog with Kucsma Mushroom and Crayfish Tail)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- frogs
- butter
- kucsma mushrooms
- parsley
- crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Clean the frogs and place them in cold water
2. Prepare a light butter sauce
3. Sauté the cleaned kucsma mushrooms in butter with fresh parsley
4. Add the mushrooms to the butter sauce
5. Squeeze the frogs well and add them to the sauce along with the peeled crayfish tails
6. Bring to a simmer
7. Plate and arrange the crayfish tails around the edge of the platter

title: Béka kucsma-gombával s rákfarkkal (Frog with Morel Mushrooms and Crayfish Tails)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- frogs
- morel mushrooms (kucsma-gomba)
- butter
- green parsley
- vajas mártás (butter sauce)

## Directions

1. Blanch the frogs and place them in cold water
2. Prepare a little butter sauce
3. Braise the thoroughly cleaned morel mushrooms in butter with green parsley, place them in the butter sauce
4. Squeeze the frogs well and place them in the sauce as well

title: Béka petrezselyem-mártásban (Frog in Parsley Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- frogs
- butter
- flour
- fresh parsley
- pea broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean and soak the frogs thoroughly
2. Cut off the front legs and interlock the back legs together
3. Salt the frogs
4. Prepare a sauce with butter, flour made into a roux, and finely chopped fresh parsley
5. Pour pea broth over the sauce
6. Add the frogs to the sauce
7. Bring to a boil and serve

title: Békarostélyos burgonyával (Frog Grilled with Potatoes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large frogs
- butter, melted
- breadcrumbs, fine
- cooked potatoes
- bacon
- ham
- red onion, sliced
- carrots
- flour
- stock, 1 coffee cup
- lemon, juice of 1

## Directions

1. Cut thighs from large frogs and remove the bones, joining two thighs together
2. Re-insert the removed bone on the other side
3. Coat the small grilled pieces with melted butter and coat with fine breadcrumbs
4. Roast over gentle coals, pouring melted butter over several times until they become a beautiful golden brown color
5. Peel cooked potatoes and cut into walnut-sized pieces
6. Shape into rounds and slowly braise in a pot with several pieces of bacon, ham, red onion slices and carrots
7. Pour several spoonfuls of stock over them multiple times so they brown and soften nicely
8. Remove and sprinkle a little flour over them
9. Brown the flour, pour in one coffee cup of stock and cook well together
10. Squeeze the juice of one lemon into it
11. Arrange the potatoes in a ring shape on the dish
12. Strain the sauce through a sieve and place the grilled pieces in two rows in the middle of the dish
13. The sauce should not cover the grilled pieces, so take a small long dish and arrange them so that the bone shows from each one and the sauce is so little that it covers neither the potatoes nor the grilled pieces

title: Bélés (Layered Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- good puff pastry (made into 24 egg-sized pieces)
- fat
- fourteen dekagrams rice
- milk
- small raisins
- large raisins
- sugar
- cinnamon
- salt

## Directions

1. Divide good puff pastry into pieces the size of 24 eggs
2. Place two buttered baking sheets side by side, which will make the dough larger
3. Roll out the first piece of dough over these two sheets and brush with fat
4. Roll out seven more sheets of dough on top, so the bottom of the dough consists of eight such layers, each brushed separately with fat
5. Prepare the filling as follows: soak fourteen dekagrams of rice in milk, strain through a sieve so all moisture drains off, place this on the dough, adding small and large raisins
6. After sprinkling this with sugar and a little broken cinnamon, place eight more sheets of dough on top, each brushed separately with fat
7. Then comes the filling again prepared in the above manner, and on top, like before, eight more sheets of remaining dough
8. Trim the dough scraps protruding from the edge of the pan with a rolling pin or knife, cut the dough itself into cubes with a pastry wheel, brush the top with fat, bake over gentle heat and serve dusted with sugar
9. Bélés can also be filled with cheese or apples

title: Bélés Pástétom (Filled Pâté)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams veal from the thigh
- fat from near the kidney
- red onion, finely chopped
- lemon zest, finely chopped
- 1 bread roll, grated crust, soaked in milk and wrung out
- 3 eggs
- butter
- milk
- 2 whole eggs
- cooked veal sweetbreads
- udder
- lemon juice
- crumbly pastry

## Directions

1. Finely chop 500 grams veal from the thigh, fat from near the kidney, red onion, and lemon zest together
2. Soak a grated bread roll crust in milk, wring it out well, and add to the mixture
3. Remove small membranes and fibers from the mixture
4. Prepare a soft batter from 3 eggs, a small piece of butter, and milk
5. Mix everything finely together, remove from heat, stir in 2 whole eggs and let stand
6. Prepare a filling from cooked veal sweetbreads and udder, and fricassee this with 2 egg yolks and lemon juice
7. Prepare crumbly pastry and line a pâté dish and its bottom with it to one small finger's thickness
8. Place half of the filling in the center
9. Layer the fricasseed mixture on top
10. Shape the other half of the filling into a round form on top
11. Roll finger-thickness strips from the remaining dough and arrange them like a grill over the filling so the filling shows through everywhere
12. Brush with beaten egg and bake for three-quarters of an hour
13. Before serving the pâté, alternate asparagus tips and crayfish tails in the spaces of the grill where the filling shows through

title: Böjti gyökérleves (Fasting Root Vegetable Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley
- carrot
- celery
- onion
- pea broth
- butter
- 2 small tablespoons flour
- parsley, finely chopped
- egg dumplings or fried grated noodles

## Directions

1. Put parsley, carrot, celery, and onion in a pot and fill with pea broth, cooking for two hours
2. Melt a piece of butter, add 2 small tablespoons of flour and roast until golden
3. Add finely chopped parsley and brown it slightly
4. Dilute with the vegetable broth and cook for another half hour
5. Strain through a sieve
6. Make beaten egg dumplings and cook them in the soup, or prepare fried grated noodles by making common grated dough, melting butter with a little chopped parsley, frying the noodles until golden with frequent stirring, and cooking in the soup

title: Búzavirág-eczet (Wheat Flower Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- wheat flowers in bloom
- good vinegar

## Directions

1. When the flower is in full bloom, carefully pull off the petals, remove the white part, and let it dry.
2. Place it in a glass bottle, pour good vinegar over it, bind it well, and set it in the sun or in a warm place.
3. When the wheat flower begins to turn white in the vinegar, place fresh petals, pulled off just as carefully, in another bottle, strain the vinegar from the first flower onto it, and pour fresh vinegar on, bind the bottle, and similarly set it in a warm place.
4. Continue in this manner with fresh flowers until the vinegar has the desired color.
5. If you use good strong vinegar for this, not only will the color be much more beautiful, but you will need to pour it onto fresh flowers only two or three times instead of more.

title: Cardinal (Cardinal)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- finely peeled zest of two oranges
- half a kilogram of sugar cut into pieces
- two bottles of good but light wine
- juice of five oranges
- one bottle of champagne chilled on ice

## Directions

1. Put the orange zest in a bowl
2. Add the cut sugar
3. Pour the wine over it
4. Squeeze in the orange juice
5. Let stand for a few hours
6. Strain and add the chilled champagne
7. Fill glasses and serve very cold

title: Carrot with Kohlrabi (Sárgarépa Kalarábival)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- carrot
- kohlrabi
- butter
- fresh parsley
- flour
- good broth
- sugar
- calf brains
- bacon fat
- green parsley
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- broth
- wafer cookies
- fresh water
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Clean the carrot and kohlrabi and cut into slices
2. With a cutter make small crescents from each variety in equal quantities
3. Melt a piece of butter the size of an egg and add the above with finely chopped parsley and braise until soft without browning
4. Sprinkle a spoonful of flour over it, braise a bit longer and thin with good broth
5. Add a piece of sugar and cook well
6. Serve with breaded brain sausages prepared as follows: after cooking the brains in salted water, place in cold water, clean from the thin membrane and chop finely
7. Melt a piece of butter, add finely chopped green parsley and the brains, a little salt, pepper, a few handfuls of breadcrumbs and a little broth, and cook everything well
8. Cut wafer cookies in half, dip in fresh water, place a small piece of the brain mixture on it, fold together, dip in beaten egg, flour and breadcrumbs and fry beautifully in fat

title: Cedarwood Water
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 liters 72° alcohol
- 4.5 dekagrams bergamot oil
- 7 dekagrams lemon oil
- 1 dekagrams lavender oil
- 1.5 dekagrams Peruvian balsam
- 8 grams cinnamon oil
- orange flower water as needed

## Directions

1. Mix bergamot oil, lemon oil, lavender oil, Peruvian balsam, and cinnamon oil in alcohol
2. Add orange flower water to dilute alcohol to 28°
3. Filter

title: Celine Water
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dekagrams lemon peel
- 10 dekagrams orange peel
- 28 dekagrams black bread crust
- 4.5 dekagrams anise
- 4.5 dekagrams cinnamon
- 4.5 dekagrams caraway
- 1 dekagrams nutmeg flower
- 1 dekagrams lavender
- 4 grams vanilla
- 3 liters alcohol
- 2 grams bergamot oil
- 4 grams orange flower oil
- 80 drops jasmine oil

## Directions

1. Chop all solid ingredients finely
2. Pour 3 liters alcohol over them
3. Digest
4. Strain the liquid
5. Add 2 grams bergamot oil and 4 grams orange flower oil to the filtered liquid
6. Add 80 drops jasmine oil
7. Shake well

title: Cheese Cake for Tea (Sajt-lepény)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 litre milk
- 8 dekagrams butter
- 16 dekagrams flour
- 3 whole eggs
- 12 dekagrams grated Parmesan cheese
- salt
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 litre milk and 8 dekagrams butter into a saucepan and let them boil together well
2. Mix in 16 dekagrams flour and stir over the heat until the mixture falls from the spoon
3. Transfer the dough to a ceramic bowl, stir until cool and gradually mix in 3 whole eggs, 12 dekagrams grated Parmesan cheese and a little salt
4. Place finger-width thick, walnut-sized pieces of this dough onto a butter-greased sheet using a pastry bag, coat with egg yolk and bake in a moderate oven
5. When serving, sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese and serve hot with tea

title: China Water (for dyeing hair black)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4.5 dekagrams distilled water
- 2 grams silver nitrate
- 50 drops ammonia

## Directions

1. Dissolve silver nitrate in distilled water
2. Add ammonia extract
3. Comb hair well with this mixture
4. Dry in the sun
5. Do not let it touch the skin

title: China-pomádé (China Pomade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four dekagrams finely crushed red china
- almond oil (nine grams total used)
- twenty-seven grams cleaned and melted beef marrow

## Directions

1. First grind the four dekagrams of finely crushed red china with a small amount of almond oil
2. Add enough almond oil so that nine grams total is used
3. Add this mixture in small portions to twenty-seven grams of cleaned and melted beef marrow
4. Grind together until cooled
5. If desired, a fragrant oil can be mixed in

title: Citrom-eczet (Lemon Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful lemons with thin skin
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Take the yellow skin of beautiful lemons with thin skin and peel it very thinly, then cut it into small cubes.
2. Peel away the white and tough skin, and cut the lemon into slices, removing the seeds.
3. Place the lemon and its yellow skin in a glass bottle, pour wine vinegar over it, and place it in the sun or in a warm oven for a time, stoppered.
4. This vinegar gives a pleasant taste to many dishes.

title: Citrom-fagylalt, más módon (Lemon Ice Cream, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lemons
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- juice of 12 lemons
- 28 dekagrams sugar
- 4 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Gently rub 14 dekagrams sugar on 2 lemons
2. Place the yellow sugar in a bowl
3. Press the juice of 12 lemons onto it
4. Cook 28 dekagrams sugar with 4 deciliters water until it forms heavy drops
5. Cool the syrup
6. Mix the juice with the cooled syrup
7. Place in a freezing container

title: Citrom-mártás (Lemon Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow roux
- finely chopped red onion
- meat stock
- half a lemon's zest
- lemon juice
- two tablespoons fresh sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare a yellow roux and add the finely chopped red onion to it
2. When it has browned slightly, pour in enough stock so that it reaches the desired consistency
3. Cut the zest of half a lemon into long strips and add it along with the lemon juice
4. Cook well
5. Finally add two tablespoons of sour cream and the sauce is ready

title: Citrom-pép (Lemon Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramm softened and ground almonds
- juice of half a lemon
- 14 dekagramm sugar
- 2 tablespoons water
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- citronade
- finely chopped lemon zest
- butter

## Directions

1. Place the softened and ground almonds in a bowl and moisten with the juice of half a lemon.
2. Roughly crush 14 dekagramm sugar, pour 2 tablespoons of water over it, and cook until it thickens.
3. Pour the hot syrup over the almonds, mix well, and let cool.
4. Once completely cooled, beat in 6 egg yolks one after another, stirring for three-quarters of an hour.
5. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 3 eggs.
6. Add citronade and finely chopped lemon zest to the paste.
7. Pour into a buttered porcelain dish and bake slowly in the oven or cook in a bain-marie.
8. Note: this paste must be served at table immediately after baking, as it collapses otherwise.

title: Citrom-pép másképpen (Lemon Paste, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 well-ripened lemon
- 10 dekagramm sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil one well-ripened lemon until very soft, changing the water five or six times during cooking to remove the bitter taste.
2. Once so soft it can be easily mashed, cut into pieces and remove the seeds.
3. Press through a sieve and place in a bowl with 10 dekagramm sugar.
4. Beat well with 2 egg yolks.
5. Gradually fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 6 eggs.
6. Pour into a buttered porcelain dish and bake slowly in the oven at gentle heat or cook in a bain-marie.
7. Serve immediately at table, as this paste easily collapses.

title: Citrom-ívek (Lemon Wafers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagrams ground sugar
- water
- 28 dekagrams soaked and lengthwise-chopped almond
- 1 lemon, zest
- 1½ lemons, juice
- wafer sheets
- sugar
- icing

## Directions

1. Cook 21 dekagrams of ground sugar in a brass pan with 4 spoonfuls of water
2. Add 28 dekagrams of soaked and lengthwise-chopped almond and roast it slightly
3. Rub the zest of 1 lemon on sugar and add it with the juice of 1½ lemons
4. Mix well
5. Spread the roasted mixture on wafers of desired size in half-finger width
6. Place on a wax-covered pan
7. Sprinkle with ground sugar
8. Bake at gentle heat
9. Quickly bend over a form or rolling pin into wafer shape and coat with icing

title: Citronád-kádka (Candied Citron Custard Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter pastry
- 7 dekagrams candied citron, finely cut lengthwise
- 7 dekagrams almond, finely cut lengthwise
- 3 dekagrams pistachio
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 1 whole egg
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Prepare butter pastry
2. Finely cut lengthwise 7 dekagrams of candied citron, the same amount of almond, and 3 dekagrams of pistachio
3. Break up 14 dekagrams of sugar finely; place half in a bowl and add the finely ground almond to it
4. Mix the other half of the sugar with the chopped mixture
5. Beat 1 whole egg and 3 egg yolks onto this until well and foamy
6. Roll out the butter pastry to knife-thickness
7. Fill the molds (kádka) with it
8. Place some of the chopped mixture in each mold
9. Pour the egg mixture over as a covering instead of a lid
10. Bake in a cooled oven

title: Coffee Aspic with Cream (Kávé-kocsonya tejszínnel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 4 deciliters good black coffee
- 4 deciliters good cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 14 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 2 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Strain the coffee as cleanly as possible.
2. Mix the coffee with an equal amount of good cream.
3. In a pan, thoroughly beat together 6 egg yolks and 14 dekagrams finely ground sugar.
4. Add the mixed coffee to the eggs and sugar.
5. Place the pan on charcoal fire and stir constantly until it begins to thicken.
6. Remove immediately from heat to prevent curdling and pour into a clean pan.
7. Mix in 2 dekagrams dissolved isinglass.
8. Stir until cooled, then pour into molds where it remains until use.
9. Unmold as with other aspics.

title: Coffee Pastry (Kávés-fánk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters hot sour cream
- 10 dekagrams hot roasted coffee
- 6 spoonfuls fine flour
- 7 egg yolks
- sugar
- butter for greasing
- beaten egg
- breadcrumbs
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Pour the hot roasted coffee into the hot sour cream, cover and let stand for half an hour, then strain and cool
2. Mix finely with 6 spoonfuls of fine flour and 7 egg yolks
3. While continuously stirring cook it to a paste and add enough sugar to sweeten it well
4. When well cooked, remove from heat and cool
5. Spread the paste onto two large torte leaves greased with butter to a thickness of two knife-widths and cool completely
6. Place the torte leaves on the cooktop for a few minutes so the butter melts
7. Turn the paste out onto a floured board and cut out with medium-sized pastry cutters
8. Dip in beaten egg, coat with breadcrumbs and fry in hot lard until golden
9. Coat thickly with sugar and press with a hot spatula
10. Can be prepared the same way with chocolate, cooking 5 bars of chocolate in 7 deciliters of sour cream and treating it the same as the coffee pastry

title: Cold Soda with Apple (Hideg sodó almával)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- small peeled apples with centers removed
- water
- sugar
- lemon juice
- soda (as prepared in recipe 390)
- preserved currants
- Spanish fritters for garnish

## Directions

1. Cook small peeled apples with centers removed in water, sugar, and a little lemon juice until soft but not falling apart.
2. Place in deep bowls and fill with preserved currants.
3. Pour soda (prepared as in recipe 390) over the apples.
4. Place in a cold location.
5. Before serving, garnish decoratively with Spanish fritters.

title: Cologne Water
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 8.5 kilogram rosemary extract
- 1 kilogram mint extract
- 9 kilogram purified concentrated alcohol
- 3.5 kilogram purified concentrated alcohol
- 38 dekagrams lemon oil
- 8 grams rosemary oil
- 3.5 kilogram purified concentrated alcohol
- 8 grams rosemary oil
- 20 grams neroli oil
- 3.5 kilogram purified concentrated alcohol
- 1.5 grams clove oil
- 6 grams orange flower oil
- 6 grams orange peel oil
- 6 grams cedarwood oil
- 6 grams bergamot oil
- 6 grams rosemary oil
- 1.5 kilogram purified alcohol
- 1.5 kilogram mint extract
- 3.5 kilogram purified concentrated alcohol
- 10 dekagrams bergamot oil
- 12 dekagrams bergamot oil

## Directions

1. Mix rosemary extract with mint extract with continuous shaking
2. Add 9 kilogram purified concentrated alcohol
3. Let stand for several hours after stirring well
4. In a bottle, mix 3.5 kilogram purified concentrated alcohol with 38 dekagrams lemon oil and 8 grams rosemary oil
5. After several hours, unite this mixture with the first one by continuous strong mixing
6. Let entire mixture stand for twenty-four hours
7. Add 3.5 kilogram alcohol and 8 grams rosemary oil and drop in 20 grams neroli oil with continuous stirring
8. Shake mixture for about fifteen minutes
9. Let stand for thirty-six hours, shaking for fifteen minutes every twelve hours
10. Before each shaking, add 3.5 kilogram alcohol (total 8.5 kilogram)
11. Add 3.5 kilogram alcohol and 1.5 grams clove oil and digest
12. Alternatively: dissolve 6 grams each of orange flower oil, orange peel oil, cedarwood oil, bergamot oil, and rosemary oil in 1.5 kilogram purified alcohol and digest
13. Or: shake 1.5 kilogram mint extract well with 1.5 kilogram rosemary extract, after six hours add 3.5 kilogram purified concentrated alcohol, 10 dekagrams bergamot oil, and 12 dekagrams bergamot oil

title: Crayfish Sausage (Rák-kolbászka)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- common egg pancake
- asparagus heads, blanched
- braised mushrooms
- shredded pike
- pike liver
- crayfish butter
- flour
- 1 egg and 1 egg yolk
- salt

## Directions

1. Bake a common egg pancake and when cooled, cut each piece into several parts so the sausages will be finger-length
2. From blanched asparagus heads, braised mushrooms, shredded pike, and pike liver (which has been fried first), prepare a filling
3. Melt a piece of crayfish butter, add a little flour and the chopped filling
4. Braise slightly, then add 1 egg and 1 egg yolk
5. Mix well, remove from heat quickly and cool
6. Spread onto the divided egg pancake pieces to a knife-width thickness
7. Roll up and arrange nicely in a buttered baking dish
8. Half an hour before serving, beat 4 egg yolks and 4 deciliters of sour cream together
9. Pour over and cook in a water bath

title: Cseh tolka (Czech Tolka)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 70 grams butter
- 400 milliliters cream with 4 egg yolks well mixed
- 20 grams fresh cheese yeast
- 40 grams sugar
- a little salt
- flour as needed
- 4 egg whites beaten stiffly

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until fluffy
2. Add the cream mixed with egg yolks, fresh cheese yeast, sugar, and a little salt
3. Add flour until the dough is light, then fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
4. The dough should be approximately twice as thick as morzsóka dough
5. Place in a warm location and let it rise until it doubles in volume
6. Bake in the appropriate pan, top with preserves, sprinkle with sugar, and serve warm

title: Cseh tolka burgonyából (Czech Tolka made with Potatoes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large floury potatoes
- 1 whole egg
- 3 egg yolks
- a lump of butter about the size of an egg, beaten fluffy
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Cook and peel the potatoes, then grate them
2. Beat 1 whole egg and 3 egg yolks into the fluffy beaten butter
3. Add the grated potatoes and a little salt
4. Stir for a long time
5. If the dough becomes too stiff, add another egg, as it should not be stiffer than the previous recipe
6. Bake as you would the previous tolka
7. Use to accompany braised beef or as a side dish for other meats

title: Cseresznye pálinkában (Cherries in Spirits)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter liter fresh squeezed raspberry juice
- seven deciliters spirits
- eighty-four dekagrams sugar
- large black sour cherries with stems removed

## Directions

1. Mix the fresh squeezed raspberry juice well with the spirits
2. Completely dissolve the sugar in this mixture
3. Pour into a glass jar
4. Add as many large black sour cherries as needed so that the spirits still rises two finger-widths above the cherries
5. Tie the jar closed and store in a cool place

title: Cseresznye Párban (Cherries in Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful black, fleshy cherries
- sugar
- water
- 60 dekagrams cherries requires 15 dekagrams sugar and 2 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from beautiful black, fleshy cherries, wash and strain them
2. Calculate 15 dekagrams sugar for 60 dekagrams cherries, cook with 2 deciliters water until completely dissolved
3. Fill clean and uniform-sized jars with cherries up to their necks
4. Bind them with thoroughly washed cow bladder so tightly that no air can enter
5. Arrange and process as per instructions 961

title: Csiga-rétes (Spiral Strudel - Old Transylvanian Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- rétes-tészta (strudel dough)
- plum preserve
- fat for frying
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Stretch the ordinarily prepared thin strudel dough and fold it into four layers
2. Grease each layer slightly with fat
3. On the edge of the folded dough lying on the cloth, spread plum preserve very thinly and about half a finger's breadth wide using a knife
4. Fold the plum preserve-lined band onto the empty dough narrowly and cut with a knife so that you create one finger-width wide, plum preserve-filled dough strips from the entire large strudel dough
5. Once finished, cut the long plum preserve-filled dough strips into one span-length pieces
6. Form them into S-shaped, small spiral strudels
7. Put one spoonful of fat into a flat pan and place over high heat
8. When the fat is hot, fill the pan with spiral strudels
9. Watch carefully during baking as this fine dry dough browns very quickly
10. Transfer to a flat plate and serve without sugar; anyone who wishes can sprinkle sugar on their own

title: Csillagrendjel (Star Cookies)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites
- 28 dekagrams sugar
- 28 dekagrams shelled and crushed almonds
- finely chopped zest of one lemon
- juice of half a lemon
- wafer star shapes
