- Spanish cherry or other preserved fruit

## Directions

1. Beat 28 dekagrams of sugar with the foam of 3 egg whites into white, stiff icing
2. Mix for another quarter hour with 28 dekagrams of shelled and crushed almonds, the finely chopped zest of one lemon, and the juice of half a lemon
3. Coat the wafer star shapes with the mixture to the thickness of a knife blade, leaving one point in the center of the star free
4. Place a Spanish cherry or other preserved fruit on this point
5. Place the stars on a buttered sheet and bake in a gentle oven until light yellow

title: Csiperke-gombamártás (Mushroom Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- six to seven peeled button mushrooms
- green parsley, finely chopped
- butter
- flour
- good stock
- three to four tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove the interior of six to seven peeled button mushrooms
2. Slice the rest and chop finely with the green parsley
3. Braise in butter and sprinkle with flour
4. Once sufficiently evaporated, add one tablespoon of good stock and finally three to four tablespoons of sour cream
5. Bring to a gentle boil

title: Csipkebogyót Befőzni (Preserved Rose Hips)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe but not soft rose hips
- seventy dekagrams rose hips
- fifty-six dekagrams sugar
- two deciliters of water

## Directions

1. Select rose hips when completely ripe but not yet soft. Cut them in half and carefully scrape away the coarse inner part with a knife. Place in a pot that has already been boiled out. Pour enough water so they do not burn. Cook over gentle heat with continuous stirring until all the water has evaporated and the rose hips are soft, then press through a fine sieve. For seventy dekagrams of rose hips, take fifty-six dekagrams of sugar and cook it with two deciliters of water until it threads. Stir in the rose hip pulp and cook with continuous stirring for ten minutes, then pour hot into jars.

title: Csirke aux Crottons (Chicken with Croutons)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-3 large chickens
- thin bacon slices
- white paper
- chicken giblets
- chicken liver
- green parsley
- chopped onion
- 20-25 mushrooms
- butter
- soup stock
- salt
- pepper
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Salt 2-3 large chickens and clean well
2. Layer with thin bacon slices
3. Wrap in white paper and roast on a spit, being careful not to brown
4. Cook the giblets and when tender, finely chop with the liver, green parsley, chopped onion, and 20-25 mushrooms
5. Melt a piece of butter
6. Place the chopped mixture in the butter and braise for a while
7. Add 1 serving spoon of soup stock, a small amount of salt and pepper, and a few tablespoons of sour cream
8. Cook well
9. When the chickens are roasted, pour the sauce into the dish
10. Place the chickens on top
11. The chickens should not look roasted in appearance, but should remain beautifully white

title: Csirke Bajor Módra (Bavarian-Style Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large meaty young chicken
- thinly sliced bacon
- sour cream
- white wine
- whole peppercorns
- nutmeg flowers
- fine breadcrumbs
- butter
- red onion

## Directions

1. Take a large meaty young chicken suitable for cooking with a cream sauce
2. Pluck and clean the chicken
3. Lard with thinly sliced bacon
4. Carefully cut into two pieces
5. Line a pot with thinly sliced bacon
6. Add a small amount of sliced red onion
7. Place the chicken pieces with the larded side up
8. Add a small amount of sour cream, the same amount of white wine, a few whole peppercorns, and nutmeg flowers
9. Sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs and dot with butter pieces
10. Place in the oven and braise until tender and slightly browned

title: Csirke forrázva (Braised Chicken in Vinegar Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Small meaty young chickens
- Red onions
- Yellow carrots
- Green parsley
- Black pepper
- Cloves
- Water
- Vinegar
- Flour
- Butter
- Sour cream

## Directions

1. Select small meaty young chickens and prepare them, then blanch them.
2. Season lightly with salt and prepare them nicely.
3. Place in a pot with red onions, yellow carrots, green parsley, a little pepper, and a few cloves.
4. Fill with three parts water and one part vinegar.
5. Simmer until half cooked.
6. In the meantime, brown two spoonfuls of flour in hot butter, then strain the sauce over it.
7. Add two or three spoonfuls of sour cream.
8. Return the chickens to the pot and cook until completely tender.

title: Csirke frikasszirozva (Fricasseed Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Small chickens
- Butter
- Flour
- Red onion
- Green parsley
- Broth
- Egg yolks
- Salt

## Directions

1. Open and blanch small chickens.
2. Heat a piece of butter, add two spoonfuls of flour, a whole red onion, and a bunch of green parsley.
3. Dilute with broth and cook the sauce, then strain it.
4. Place the chickens in the strained sauce and simmer until cooked.
5. When ready to serve, place two egg yolks in a bowl, break them up, add the sauce and stir constantly over gentle heat to thicken.
6. Serve the chickens with the sauce poured over them.
7. It can also be prepared like fricasseed veal head with sour garnish.

title: Csirke kaporna-mártásban (Chicken in Caper Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Butter
- Flour
- Red onion
- Broth
- White vinegar
- Sour cream
- Capers
- Lemon zest

## Directions

1. Prepare the chickens nicely and blanch them.
2. In warm butter, brown two spoonfuls of flour and add a whole red onion.
3. Dilute with broth and cook the sauce.
4. Strain the sauce through a sieve.
5. Place the chickens in it.
6. Add one spoonful of good white vinegar, three spoonfuls of sour cream, a handful of capers, and finely chopped lemon zest.
7. Simmer until the chickens are tender, then serve with the sauce spooned underneath.

title: Csirke narancs-mártásban (Chicken in Orange Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Three small meaty chickens
- Finely chopped bacon
- Butter
- Flour
- Brown broth
- Orange zest
- Lemon zest
- Sugar
- Orange juice
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Blanch and prepare three small meaty chickens.
2. Lard with finely chopped bacon and roast until they turn a beautiful golden color.
3. Prepare a light roux with butter and flour.
4. Dilute with brown broth.
5. Add a little orange and lemon zest, a piece of sugar, and the juice of one orange and half a lemon.
6. Cook well.
7. Remove all fat from the sauce.
8. Place the chickens in it and simmer for a few more minutes.

title: Csirke paradicsomalma-mártásban (Chicken in Tomato Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Butter
- Flour
- Tomatoes
- Meat broth
- Sour cream
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Clean the chickens and cut them into the usual pieces.
2. Braise in butter and sprinkle a little flour over them.
3. Dilute with the strained tomatoes, which have been previously well cooked in meat broth.
4. Simmer the chickens until tender.
5. Finally, add a good spoonful of sour cream and a piece of sugar.
6. Arrange nicely on a platter and pour the sauce over them.

title: Csirke tejfellel és citrom-szeletekkel (Chicken with Sour Cream and Lemon Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Two small chickens
- Sour cream
- Lemon with peel
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Blanch two small chickens and rinse them several times with warm water.
2. Cut into the usual pieces and salt them, then let them rest in the salt for a quarter hour.
3. Pour good sour cream into a pot.
4. Cut a soup lemon thinly with its peel, carefully removing the seeds.
5. Layer half the lemon slices in the sour cream, then half the chicken pieces, then more sour cream.
6. Add the remaining lemon slices, then the rest of the chicken.
7. Top with sour cream and small pieces of butter.
8. Note: do not stir these chickens, only shake the pot gently and cook only until done, as otherwise they lose both flavor and appearance.
9. When serving, arrange the chickens neatly on a platter and pour the sauce underneath.

title: Csirke Vajas Mártásban Kartiflolával (Chicken in Butter Sauce with Cauliflower)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- small cleaned chickens
- butter
- flour
- good stock
- sour cream
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Prepare and blanch small chickens
2. Melt butter without making it hot
3. Add 2 tablespoons flour and lightly brown
4. Deglaze with good stock
5. Add 2 tablespoons sour cream and cook together well
6. Strain the sauce
7. Place the blanched chickens in the sauce and cook until sufficiently tender
8. Clean cauliflower and cook in salted water
9. Keep small rosettes whole, cut larger ones into pieces
10. Add the cauliflower to the sauce and cook together
11. For serving: place the chickens in the center of the dish and arrange the cauliflower around
12. Enrich the sauce with egg yolks (fricassée style) and pour over the chickens

title: Csirke Vér-Mártásban (Chicken in Blood Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken blood
- vinegar
- bacon
- red onion
- whole peppercorns
- cloves
- flour
- lemon juice
- chicken

## Directions

1. When killing the chickens, collect the blood in a bowl with a small amount of vinegar
2. Mix the blood with the vinegar so it does not coagulate
3. Pluck and clean the chickens and salt them well
4. Place bacon pieces in a pot
5. Cut red onion in half and place in the center
6. Pierce each piece with three cloves
7. Add a few whole peppercorns and cloves
8. Add the chicken pieces and braise but do not allow them to brown
9. Remove the chicken pieces
10. Dust with 2 tablespoons flour and brown
11. Deglaze with liquid and reduce
12. Add a small amount of lemon juice and cook well
13. Strain through a sieve
14. Return the chicken pieces and cook until tender
15. Mix the blood with the sauce and cook together for a few minutes more

title: Csirke zöld borsóval, rákfarkkal más módon (Chicken with Green Peas and Crayfish Tails, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Two chickens
- Small green peas
- Butter
- Crayfish tails
- Crayfish butter
- Sour cream
- Breadcrumbs
- Nutmeg flowers
- Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare two chickens as if you were going to stuff them.
2. For the stuffing: braise small green peas in butter.
3. Shell crayfish tails and cut them into small cubes, then braise in crayfish butter.
4. Add a little sour cream, breadcrumbs, a little nutmeg flower, and salt.
5. Stuff the chickens with this mixture.
6. Skewer on thin skewers and roast nicely.
7. These chickens can also be used for soup.

title: Csirke Zöldborsóval és Rákfarkkal (Chicken with Green Peas and Crayfish Tails)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- butter sauce
- 4 deciliters green peas
- butter
- parsley
- crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Blanch 2 chickens and cut into four pieces
2. Prepare a small amount of butter sauce
3. Place the chicken pieces in the sauce
4. Braise 4 deciliters green peas with butter and parsley
5. When cooked (text cuts off)

title: Csirke zöldborsóval és rákfarkkal (Chicken with Green Peas and Crayfish Tails)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Two young chickens
- Butter
- Sauce
- Four deciliters green peas
- Parsley
- Crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Blanch two young chickens and cut them into four pieces.
2. Prepare a light buttered sauce and place the chicken pieces in it.
3. Braise four deciliters of green peas with butter and parsley until softened.
4. Add the chicken to the peas along with shelled crayfish tails.
5. Simmer together for another quarter hour.
6. When serving, place the chickens in the center of the plate and arrange the peas around the edge like a wreath, garnished with crayfish tails, leaving about two finger-widths of the peas uncovered.

title: Csokoládé-csókok (Chocolate Kisses)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams good chocolate
- 7 dekagrams sugar
- 18 dekagrams blanched almonds
- egg white

## Directions

1. Finely crush the chocolate and sugar together
2. Blanch and chop the almonds - chop half finely and half into larger pieces
3. Prepare stiff peaks from egg white and beat the sugar and chocolate into a thick icing with it
4. Once well foamy, first beat in the finely chopped almonds, and once well mixed, beat in the coarsely chopped almonds
5. Using a coffee spoon, place onto wafer paper cut into rounds
6. Dry in a gentle oven

title: Csokoládé-fagylalt (Chocolate Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters good cream
- 6 tablets fine chocolate
- 14 dekagrams sugar

## Directions

1. Cook and stir 7 deciliters of good cream with 6 tablets of fine chocolate and 14 dekagrams of sugar until the chocolate is completely dissolved
2. Cool the mixture
3. Pour into the freezing container and treat it like the other ice creams

title: Csokoládé-fánk habos tejjel töltve (Chocolate Fritters Filled with Foamy Whipped Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm finely ground sugar
- 12 whole eggs
- 19 dekagramm fine flour
- whipped cream
- sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Put 28 dekagramm finely ground sugar into a brass bowl
2. Beat 12 whole eggs over it and beat well with the whisk
3. Place over gentle charcoal heat and beat continuously until the mixture becomes thick and white, handling the heat carefully so the mixture does not boil
4. When thick and white, remove from heat and beat a while longer
5. With the flat side of a cooking spoon, stir in 19 dekagramm fine flour
6. Fill a piping bag with the mixture and pipe small carnival fritter-sized drops onto white paper
7. Sprinkle with finely ground sugar
8. Place on a baking sheet and bake at gentle heat until beautiful and crispy
9. Once baked, carefully remove from the sheet
10. Fill with whipped cream that has been prepared beforehand with sugar and vanilla
11. Stack them two by two so they form a fritter shape
12. Arrange on a plate lined with a kitchen cloth
13. Serve quickly to the table
14. These fritters can also be filled with ice cream or strawberry foam

title: Csokoládé-Holdacskák (Chocolate Crescents)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-five dekagrams grated chocolate
- half kilogram finely ground and sifted sugar
- three egg whites

## Directions

1. On a rolling board, mix grated chocolate well with finely ground and sifted sugar
2. Work with a large kitchen knife into a smooth dough using three egg whites
3. If it does not hold together well, add a little more egg white
4. The dough should be rather firm than soft
5. Roll out to knife-width thickness
6. Form crescents with a donut cutter
7. Place on waxed sheet
8. Bake slowly at low heat

title: Csokoládé-kenyér (Chocolate Bread)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Sponge cake batter
- Preserved peaches or raspberries
- Chocolate coating

## Directions

1. Prepare sponge cake batter and spread smoothly two knife-widths thick on buttered sheet with wide knife
2. Bake at gentle heat
3. Cool and cut round leaves with a five-crown-sized cutter
4. Spread with preserved peaches or raspberries
5. Place another leaf on top
6. Dip entirely in chocolate coating with two forks
7. Place on parchment paper until completely dry
8. Peel gently from paper

title: Csokoládé-Kocsonya (Chocolate Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablets fine chocolate
- 7 deciliter cream
- 1 dekagramm dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Cook 6 tablets of fine chocolate in 7 deciliter of cream with continuous stirring
2. When slightly thickened, add 1 dekagramm dissolved isinglass
3. Pour into a mold and freeze on ice

title: Csokoládé-leves (Chocolate Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter milk
- 4 pieces of grated chocolate
- a little sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- toasted bread, cut into small slices

## Directions

1. Cook the milk with grated chocolate and sugar together, stirring well
2. Beat 2 egg yolks in a bowl and pour the soup over them
3. Serve over toasted bread slices

title: Csokoládé-parfait (Chocolate Cream Frozen Dessert)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams vanilla chocolate
- 0.3 liters hot milk
- 1.1 liters boiled and hot sweet cream
- 28 dekagrams crushed sugar
- 12 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Melt 28 dekagrams vanilla chocolate in 0.3 liters hot milk over charcoal fire, stirring slowly.
2. Pour in 1.1 liters boiled and hot sweet cream and cook everything together slowly for a quarter hour, then cool.
3. In the meantime, put 28 dekagrams crushed sugar in a pot and beat with 12 egg yolks until foamy.
4. Slowly pour the cooled chocolate into the egg mixture.
5. Strain through fine hair or silk sieve into a porcelain bowl.
6. Stir until completely cooled, then pour into the freezing mold and serve either as a whole block or in individual portions on ice cream plates.

title: Csokoládé-pép (Chocolate Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramms finely ground almonds
- 10 dekagramms finely ground sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 7 dekagramms finely grated chocolate
- fine ground vanilla
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Place almonds and sugar in a bowl
2. Beat in the egg yolks one by one and stir for a whole hour
3. Grind the chocolate finely with a little vanilla
4. Beat the 3 egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in
5. Fill a butter-greased mold
6. Bake until nicely done

title: Csokoládé-pép más módon (Chocolate Paste Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramms finely ground sugar
- 14 dekagramms almonds
- 14 dekagramms fine chocolate
- 10 dekagramms butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites
- 2 tablespoons apricot jam
- 4 egg whites (for topping)
- sugar

## Directions

1. Grind the sugar finely and moisten the almonds during grinding to keep them from becoming oily
2. Place the chocolate in a cooled oven until softened
3. Quickly mix in the butter, then the sugar and almonds
4. Slowly add 8 egg yolks
5. When beaten foamy, add the stiff peaks of 5 egg whites
6. Fill a deep and tall butter-greased mold
7. Bake at gentle but steady heat
8. Turn out half an hour before serving and cool slightly
9. Mix 2 tablespoons apricot jam with the same amount of sugar
10. Fold in the stiff peaks of 4 egg whites gradually
11. Fill a Spanish pastry bag and pipe strips into any indentations in the mold
12. Pipe a crown shape at the bottom, then successively smaller ones above, resembling a pineapple
13. Return to the oven until the meringue is dry
14. Serve quickly to the table
15. Note: Turn out onto a flat plate and place this on a plate covered with a tablecloth

title: Csokoládé-rétes (Chocolate Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- rétestésztát (strudel dough)
- three bars of chocolate
- 14 dekagramm softened almond
- 10 dekagramm sugar
- several spoonfuls melted butter
- five spoonfuls sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough as described in recipe 480 and stretch it finely
2. Dry it well
3. Grate three bars of chocolate and chop 14 dekagramm softened almond finely
4. Mix 10 dekagramm sugar with the almond
5. Pour several spoonfuls of melted butter over the dough
6. Sprinkle the chocolate and almond mixture over it
7. Pour five spoonfuls of sour cream over it
8. Fold it together
9. Pour cream into a baking dish to a thickness of one finger width
10. Place the strudel into the dish and bake it

title: Csokoládé-rétes más módon (Chocolate Strudel Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- rétestésztát (strudel dough)
- 10 dekagramm sugar
- six egg yolks
- 10 dekagramm finely ground almond
- two bars of chocolate
- four egg whites
- melted butter
- grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough
2. Mix 10 dekagramm sugar, six egg yolks, and 10 dekagramm finely ground almond together well
3. Grate two bars of chocolate
4. Mix the grated chocolate with the stiffly beaten whites of four eggs
5. Combine this with the egg yolk mixture
6. Stretch the dough finely and dry it
7. Brush with melted butter
8. Spread the filling onto it in the thickness of a knife blade
9. Fold it together gently
10. Place it in a baking dish brushed with butter
11. Sprinkle with grated chocolate and bake until golden

title: Csokoládé-sodó (Chocolate Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 liter boiled cream
- 3 egg yolks
- 10 dekagrams grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Bring 0.5 liter cream to a boil with 3 egg yolks and 10 dekagrams grated chocolate while continuously stirring

title: Csokoládé-szeletek (Chocolate Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams softened almonds
- 14 dekagrams grated chocolate
- 3 tablespoons water
- 14 dekagrams butter
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 7 egg whites
- 14 dekagrams granulated and sifted sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- 14 dekagrams fine flour
- 14 dekagrams butter
- orange zest
- 7 dekagrams finely ground and sifted sugar
- 2 teaspoons raspberry or currant marmalade

## Directions

1. Finely grind the softened almonds, moistening several times during grinding to prevent it from becoming oily
2. Cook the grated chocolate with three tablespoons water in a brass pan with continuous stirring into a thick paste
3. Beat butter until fluffy, finely grind the sugar
4. Mix the almonds and chocolate with the butter and sugar, stirring together for a good quarter hour
5. Fold in the stiff foam of seven egg whites
6. Spread the mixture thinly on a buttered baking sheet to pinky-finger thickness and bake slowly
7. Prepare a second mixture: beat granulated and sifted sugar rubbed with orange zest with four egg yolks for a quarter hour
8. Lightly fold in the stiff foam of four egg whites with 14 dekagrams fine flour
9. Meanwhile, melt 14 dekagrams butter, discard the whey, and when cooled, slowly fold in, stirring only until combined
10. Spread this mixture on a buttered baking sheet to pinky-finger thickness, sprinkle with sugar, and bake slowly
11. Once the second mixture is baked and cooled, cut into card-sized pieces
12. Spread the chocolate layer with peach marmalade and top with a white layer of the same size
13. Continue until all are filled
14. Mix 7 dekagrams finely ground and sifted sugar with 2 teaspoons raspberry or currant marmalade into icing
15. Fill a paper cone with a pinhead-sized opening with this icing
16. Decorate the slices and let dry

title: Csokoládé-torta (Chocolate Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 grams almonds
- 42 grams sugar
- Vanilla
- 5 grams crumbly crumbs
- 28 grams finely crushed chocolate
- Bowl of water
- 17 grams butter beaten until foamy
- 1 whole egg
- 15 egg yolks
- 7 egg whites
- Preserved peaches or raspberries
- Chocolate glaze

## Directions

1. Clean the almonds with cloth and crush them finely with their skins, pass through a fine sieve; crush what remains again, pass through the sieve and repeat until all is sifted
2. Crush 42 grams of sugar with a little vanilla and sift it together with 5 grams of crumbly crumbs, then mix everything together
3. Cook 28 grams of finely crushed chocolate with a cup of water in a brass pan with continuous stirring until it becomes completely smooth and child-porridge thick, then set aside
4. Put the almonds, sugar and crumbs into 17 grams of butter beaten until foamy, add 1 whole egg and 15 egg yolks, stir for half an hour
5. Now add the cooled chocolate and stir it in, then gently fold in the stiff foam of 7 egg whites
6. Butter two equal pans and fill each with half the mixture
7. Bake at gentle but sustained heat for three-quarters of an hour
8. Test with a knitting needle inserted into the bottom; if it comes out dry, the cake is baked; then cool the cake for a few minutes
9. Place one part of the cake on a appropriately sized cake leaf, fill with preserved peaches or raspberries, place the other cake on top and cover with chocolate glaze as found in the following recipe

title: Csokoládé-ívek (Chocolate Wafers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams soaked almond, cut in slivers
- 14 dekagrams finely ground and sifted chocolate
- 7 dekagrams finely ground and sifted sugar
- 3 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- wafer sheets
- butter
- icing

## Directions

1. Cut 14 dekagrams of soaked almond in slivers
2. Add 14 dekagrams of finely ground and sifted chocolate and 7 dekagrams of finely ground and sifted sugar
3. Beat stiff whites from 3 eggs
4. Place the almond, chocolate and sugar in a bowl
5. Stir in the beaten whites gradually, so the batter isn't too thin, as it must not spread
6. Cut wafer sheets two fingers wide and a span long
7. Spread the batter on with a knife
8. Dry at gentle heat on a butter-covered pan
9. When hardened, can be decorated with white or red icing

title: Csokoládés Szilva (Chocolate-Covered Fried Plums)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven deciliters dried plums
- hot fat
- three tablets chocolate
- ten decagrams powdered sugar
- sixteen to twenty apple slices
- candied cherries
- common pastry dough

## Directions

1. Wash the dried plums and scald without cooking them soft
2. When cooled and dried well, remove the pit
3. Place in hot fat and shake the pan continuously to fry quickly until crisp
4. Do not leave on heat too long as they burn easily
5. Grate three tablets of chocolate finely and mix with ten decagrams powdered sugar
6. As soon as the plums are removed from the pan and cleaned of fat, place in the chocolate mixture
7. Stir well with a spoon until completely coated
8. Heap high in the center of a platter
9. Cut out the core from sixteen to twenty apple slices with a small cutter
10. Dip in common pastry dough and fry quickly in hot fat
11. Let fat drip well and sprinkle heavily with powdered sugar until completely white
12. Place a candied cherry with stem in the opening in the center of each apple slice
13. Arrange apple slices around the edge of the platter

title: Csokoládéöntés torták és kisebb sütemények bevonására (Chocolate Glaze for Cakes and Smaller Pastries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 grams fine chocolate
- 18 grams sugar
- 2 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Coarsely crush 21 grams of fine chocolate with 18 grams of sugar and cook in a sugar pan with 2 deciliters of water with continuous stirring until it boils with large bubbles and becomes light child-porridge thickness
2. Remove from heat and stir quickly, continuously and as long as for punch preserve until it passes the test, that is, until a drop dripped on paper does not spread but hardens
3. Place the cake on a sheet of paper, pour the glaze evenly over it, quickly spread what runs down onto the sides of the cake so that it is completely covered and let it stand for a few hours
4. Decorate with whole fruits or in the following way: mix finely 1 spoonful of beautiful yellow peach syrup with 2 teaspoons of crushed sugar, fill this into a small syringe and when the chocolate or lemon coating has hardened, press either arches or star shapes onto it
5. These decorations can be made in two colors, that is with yellow peach and red raspberry syrup

title: Csuka osztrigával (Pike with Oysters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- water
- oysters
- fresh butter
- sour cream
- lemon zest
- lemon juice
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook pike in water and place on a plate.
2. Remove the oysters and simmer them slightly in fresh butter.
3. Add them to the pike along with sour cream, lemon zest, and the juice of one lemon, the oyster liquor and a little fine breadcrumbs.
4. Bring to a boil.

title: Csuka szardellával (Pike with Anchovies)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large pike, about two kilograms in the middle
- salt
- eight anchovies
- red onion
- fresh parsley
- lemon zest
- pepper
- sour cream
- lemon juice
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Salt a large middle piece of pike and let it stand for an hour.
2. Dry the fish with a clean cloth.
3. Chop finely together eight anchovies, one red onion, fresh parsley, and one lemon zest, add a little pepper and mix well.
4. Coat the entire piece of fish with this mixture.
5. Place in a baking pan, pour three deciliters of sour cream over it, add the juice of one lemon, several pieces of butter and a handful of breadcrumbs.
6. Place coals on top and cook well.
7. Serve on a plate and pour the sauce over it.

title: Csuka szardellával tűzve (Pike Larded with Anchovies)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- anchovies
- butter
- sour cream
- lemon juice
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Clean the pike in the usual way, fillet it and wash it thoroughly and salt it only very lightly.
2. Clean the washed anchovies of their bones and chop them lengthwise like bacon and lard the pike with them in two rows.
3. Place a piece of butter in a pan, add three spoonfuls of sour cream and the juice of one lemon and baste the fish with this several times during cooking.
4. When cooked, place it on the serving dish, add another spoonful of sour cream and a small piece of butter to it, bring to a boil, pour it over the fish and sprinkle lengthwise chopped lemon peel over it.

title: Csuka tejfeles tormával (Pike with Creamy Horseradish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- a little carrot
- parsley root
- two heads of red onion
- horseradish root
- flour
- butter
- fish broth

## Directions

1. After cleaning and filleting the fish, clean it completely of blood, salt it and let it stand for a while.
2. Cook a little carrot with parsley root and two heads of red onion cut into four pieces in salted water.
3. Place half a grated horseradish root in a pan, add a spoonful of flour, place a piece of butter in it and with a spoon of the broth in which the fish was cooked, bring the horseradish to a slight boil.
4. Place the fish on the serving dish and pour the sauce over it.

title: Csuka vágott mártással (Pike with Chopped Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salted water
- capers
- two anchovies
- butter
- ground black pepper
- pea broth
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of pike in salted water, remove its bones and chop it finely together with capers and two anchovies.
2. Melt a piece of butter, add the chopped mixture together with a little ground black pepper and braise it well.
3. When braised, fill it up with clear pea broth and a little vinegar and bring it to a boil.
4. Clean and salt a whole pike, melt a piece of butter in a pan, place the fish in it and braise it until it is braised on all sides.
5. Pour the sauce with the chopped mixture over it and bring it to a slight boil.

title: Csuka írósvajjal és petrezselyemmel (Pike with Brown Butter and Parsley)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned pike
- salt
- water
- cloves
- bay leaf
- hard-boiled eggs
- parsley
- brown butter

## Directions

1. Clean and split the pike
2. Cook in salted water with a few cloves and bay leaf
3. Carefully place on a serving dish
4. Sprinkle with finely chopped hard-boiled eggs and parsley
5. Pour hot brown butter over it

title: Császár-Bábakalács (Emperor Babka Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 grams butter
- 6 egg yolks
- salt
- 1 tablespoon ground sugar
- 36 grams yeast soaked in milk
- 2 deciliters lukewarm sour cream
- flour
- 90 grams almonds
- 90 grams ground sugar
- little cinnamon
- 100 grams raisins

## Directions

1. Mix 6 egg yolks one by one into 100 grams whipped butter, add little salt, 1 tablespoon ground sugar, 36 grams yeast soaked in milk, 2 deciliters lukewarm sour cream into a pot and mix well
2. Set aside while the butter and egg are whipped, and wait until the yeast begins to work, then strain it and slowly mix into the butter
3. Then mix in so much flour that it becomes a light dough but does not flow, being rather like funnel-cake dough
4. When you have stirred until it pulls from the spoon, cover and let rise in a warm place
5. Meanwhile finely grind 90 grams almonds cleaned with cloth with the skin, and well mix with the same amount of ground sugar and a little cinnamon, and chop 100 grams raisins together
6. When the dough has risen, butter the mold, fill it with half the dough and smooth it with a spoon dipped in flour, sprinkle evenly with the almond and sugar mixture, sprinkle the raisins on and cover with the rest of the dough, set in a warm place
7. When well risen, bake at gentle but lasting heat, turn onto a sieve and finely sprinkle with ground sugar

title: Császár-pudding (Emperor Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dekagrams butter
- 18 dekagrams sugar
- little vanilla
- 18 dekagrams soaked almonds
- cold water
- 10 dekagrams citron
- 1 whole egg
- 5 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Whip 9 dekagrams butter until fluffy
2. Pound 18 dekagrams sugar with a little vanilla and the same amount of soaked almonds, moistening with a little cold water so it does not become oily
3. Finely chop 10 dekagrams citron
4. Put everything into the whipped butter
5. Add 1 whole egg and 5 egg yolks one after another
6. Add the stiff whites of 4 eggs
7. Pour into a buttered mold with the bottom decorated neatly with citron and steam for three-quarters of an hour
8. Turn onto a plate and pour wine sauce around it

title: Császár-pép (Emperor Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams sugar in pieces
- 2 deciiliters water
- 28 dekagrams fresh sieved raspberry
- 5 dekagrams butter beaten to a foam
- 14 dekagrams softened and finely pounded almonds
- 7 dekagrams bread crumb soaked in milk
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Put the sugar pieces in a brass bowl and pour 2 deciiliters of water over them and let stand for a while
2. Place on a bed of coals and cook until it drips thickly
3. Add 28 dekagrams of fresh sieved raspberry and cook to a thick consistency
4. Remove from the fire and cool
5. Mix 5 dekagrams of foamy butter with 14 dekagrams of softened and finely pounded almonds and 7 dekagrams of bread crumb soaked in milk
6. Add 2 whole eggs and 4 egg yolks and stir together for half an hour
7. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
8. Fill a buttered torte wrapper with the paste and bake nicely

title: Császármadár sülve (Roasted Guinea Fowl)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- guinea fowl
- salt
- bacon
- butter
- oyster
- potato
- lemon peel
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the guinea fowl, wash and salt it
2. Insert a piece of bacon inside
3. Truss and roast until golden brown
4. Prepare a sauce with butter, oyster, potato, lemon peel and lemon juice
5. Place the roasted guinea fowl on a plate and pour the sauce underneath

title: Curd Cheese Cake with Crumbly Dough (Túrós-lepény omlós tésztából)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilograms flour
- 3/8 fat or butter
- 2 dekagrams yeast
- 1/2 coffee cup milk
- sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 coffee cup wine
- salt
- 2 egg yolks
- cream
- butter
- 2 egg whites
- curd cheese

## Directions

1. To 1/2 kilograms flour add 3/8 fat or butter and rub together well
2. Soak 2 dekagrams yeast in 1/2 coffee cup milk with three pieces of sugar to let it rise
3. With 4 egg yolks, 1/2 coffee cup wine, proper salt and the yeast, quickly knead a soft dough - if kneaded too long it becomes tough and hard, not crumbly
4. Let it rest for a few hours; when it has rested and appears to have risen, flatten it in a pan to the thickness of a knife blade, or slightly thicker
5. Force the curd cheese through thick cloth to remove the whey, then place in a bowl
6. With 2 egg yolks, a few spoonfuls of sweet sour cream, a piece of butter the size of an egg, and beaten foam from the 2 egg whites, mix the curd cheese together

title: Currant or Raspberry Cake (Ribizli vagy málna-lepény)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dekagrams of the finest currants or raspberries
- fruit juice
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean and wash 10 dekagrams of the finest currants or raspberries and dry them on a sieve
2. At the moment of placing the cake in the oven, fold the fruit into the batter, pour into the butter-greased pan, and immediately place in the oven to prevent the fruit from sinking to the bottom
3. For lighter fruits like raspberries this method works well, but for heavy cherries it is difficult
4. Press out 7 decilitres of juice from the fruit and cook it with 14 dekagrams sugar until thick, cool it, turn the cake out onto a plate, and coat it with the syrup, which should have the appearance of light jelly
5. This cake can be made either baked in a pan or in a baker's oven

title: Curraszszao-víz (Currassao Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams currassao orange peel
- 21 dekagrams orange blossoms
- 10 dekagrams Florentine iris root
- 14 dekagrams lemon balm
- 18 dekagrams jasmine flowers
- 4.5 dekagrams raisins
- 4.5 dekagrams clove buds
- paradise seeds
- 1 dekagram Peruvian balsam
- 1.5 liters alcohol
- 1 dekagram orange oil
- 4 grams bergamot oil

## Directions

1. Fill 1.5 liters of alcohol with the currassao orange peel, orange blossoms, Florentine iris root, lemon balm, jasmine flowers, raisins, clove buds, paradise seeds, and Peruvian balsam
2. Allow to digest
3. Strain the liquid
4. Add 1 dekagram orange oil and 4 grams bergamot oil to the strained liquid
5. Shake well

title: Czibere Soup Another Way (Czibere-leves más módon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half a handful wheat flour bran
- one liter water
- bread starter
- millet porridge
- sour cream
- eggs

## Directions

1. Scald half a handful of wheat flour bran with one liter of water
2. When cooled slightly, strain and add a little bread starter to the lukewarm liquid
3. Leave in a warm place where it will sour
4. If it becomes too sour, mix with a little water and bring to a boil
5. When boiling, add a tablespoon of millet porridge
6. In a soup bowl, beat one quarter liter of sour cream and an egg until frothy
7. Pour the hot soup over it and mix together

title: Czitrom-fagylalt (Lemon Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram sugar
- 13 deciliters water
- 1 lemon, thinly peeled rind
- 5-6 lemons, juice

## Directions

1. Boil 0.5 kilogram sugar with 13 deciliters water
2. Remove the foam
3. Add the rind of a thinly peeled lemon
4. When the sugar has cooled, squeeze the juice of 5-6 lemons into it
5. Freeze the whole thing

title: Czitrom-mártás (Lemon Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- yellowish roux
- finely chopped red onion
- meat broth
- lemon zest
- lemon juice
- fresh cream

## Directions

1. Prepare a yellowish roux and add finely chopped red onion
2. When this has browned a little, pour in enough broth for the proper thickness
3. Cut the zest of half a lemon into long thin pieces and add with its juice
4. Cook well together
5. Finally add two spoons of fresh cream and the sauce is ready

title: Czitrom-szeletek (Lemon Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lemon, juice
- 14 dekagrams finely crushed sugar
- 14 dekagrams finely chopped almonds
- Wafers

## Directions

1. Extract juice from lemon
2. Mix with finely crushed sugar
3. Let stand for 12 hours
4. Mix in finely chopped almonds
5. Dry the mixture on wafers
6. When cooled, cut into slices

title: Czitrom-víz (Lemon Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three liters of fresh water
- juice of three lemons
- twenty-two dekagrams of sugar
- finely peeled zest of lemon

## Directions

1. Pour the lemon juice into the water
2. Add the sugar and finely peeled lemon zest
3. Cover and let stand cold for one hour
4. Strain and serve

title: Czitronád-kádka (Citronade Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagrams citronade, finely and lengthwise chopped
- 7 dekagrams almonds, finely and lengthwise chopped
- 3 dekagrams pistachios
- 14 dekagrams powdered sugar
- 1 whole egg
- 3 egg yolks
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry
2. Finely and lengthwise chop the citronade, almonds, and pistachios
3. Pound 14 dekagrams sugar finely; place half in a bowl with the finely chopped almonds
4. Mix the other half of sugar with the chopped mixture
5. Beat 1 whole egg and 3 egg yolks into the mixture and whisk well
6. Roll out the puff pastry to knife-edge thickness
7. Fill the molds with it
8. Place some of the chopped mixture in each mold and cover with the beaten mixture instead of a lid

title: Czitronád-pép (Citron Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 stale, crumbly grated kipferl rolls
- 1 coffee cup cream
- 7 dekagramms softened and finely ground almonds
- 7 dekagramms butter, beaten until fluffy
- 1 whole egg and 5 egg yolks
- 10 dekagramms finely ground sugar
- 7 dekagramms citron, cut into laska shapes
- 3 egg whites
- fresh citron slices

## Directions

1. Pour cream over the grated kipferl
2. Mix the soaked crumbs with the almonds into the beaten butter
3. Add the whole egg and egg yolks one by one
4. Add the sugar and citron pieces
5. Beat the 3 egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in
6. Grease a pot with butter and line the bottom with white paper greased with butter
7. Cut thin slices from fresh citron and arrange them in a double star pattern on the bottom of the pot
8. Slowly add the beaten mixture
9. Steam for three-quarters of an hour
10. Turn out onto a plate, carefully remove the paper so the star is not damaged
11. Serve quickly to the table

title: Czitronád-rétes (Citron Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven decziliters fine flour
- one whole egg and one egg yolk
- 7 dekagramm butter
- salt
- milk
- half kilogram almond
- one lemon's juice
- lemon peel
- 14 dekagramm sugar
- 10 dekagramm candied citron
- five dekagramm pistachio
- melted butter
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. From seven decziliters fine flour, one whole egg, one egg yolk, 7 dekagramm butter, salt, and milk, prepare strudel dough
2. Work it well and let it rest for a while
3. Coarsely crush half kilogram of almond
4. Moisten it with one lemon's juice and chop the peel finely
5. Finely crush 14 dekagramm sugar
6. Finely chop 10 dekagramm candied citron and five dekagramm pistachio
7. Mix all ingredients together well
8. Stretch the dough finely
9. Brush with melted butter
10. Spread the filling smoothly on it
11. Fold it together
12. Place the strudel on a butter-brushed pastry paper
13. Sprinkle with sugar and bake

title: Czompó borral (Leg of Mutton with Wine)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- larger leg of mutton
- salt
- green parsley
- onion
- five to six mushrooms (csiperke)
- crushed pepper
- butter (piece the size of an egg)
- white wine (two deciliters)

## Directions

1. Clean the larger leg of mutton and cut it almost all the way through without separating it completely
2. Salt it and let it sit in the salt for one hour
3. Place it in a long pot
4. Finely chop green parsley, onion and five to six mushrooms together and add them to the pot with crushed pepper and a piece of butter the size of an egg all at once
5. Pour two deciliters of white wine over it
6. Cover the pot, place coals on top and cook through
7. Frequently pour the sauce over it with a spoon
8. When completely cooked, place it on a plate and serve with the small amount of sauce underneath

title: Czukorborsóleves (Sugar Pea Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter sugar peas
- ten dekagrams butter
- salt
- white pepper
- one and a half liters beef broth
- butter
- glaze

## Directions

1. Clean and wash one liter of sugar peas
2. Braise slowly with ten dekagrams butter, salt and white pepper
3. Press through a sieve
4. Slowly continue cooking with one and a half liters of beef broth until completely thickened so it runs off the spoon
5. Skim the fat well from the soup during cooking
6. Before serving heat for one minute with a lump of butter and a little glaze

title: Czukorborsóleves (Sugar Pea Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter sugar peas
- 10 deka butter
- salt
- white pepper
- 1 1/2 liters meat broth
- butter the size of an egg
- a little glaze

## Directions

1. Wash the sugar peas thoroughly and stew them slowly with 10 deka butter, a little salt, and white pepper until soft
2. Press through a sieve
3. Continue cooking slowly with 1 1/2 liters of meat broth until completely thickened so that it flows off the spoon
4. During cooking, skim the fat off the soup well
5. Before serving, bring to a boil for one minute with butter the size of an egg and a little glaze
6. Pour over bread slices fried in butter
7. The soup should have a nice light color

title: Czukorral bevont gesztenye (Glazed Chestnuts)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large Italian chestnuts
- 21 dekagrams broken sugar
- half a cup water

## Directions

1. Roast the large Italian chestnuts until the shells can be cleanly removed
2. Cook the broken sugar with half a cup of water until it becomes caramel (raw sugar), as one would cook it when making sugar tablets
3. Test if the caramel is ready by dipping a thin stick first in cold water then in the boiling sugar and again in cold water; the sugar should break cleanly and not be sticky, otherwise the chestnut will stick to the teeth
4. Once the sugar is properly cooked, dip the shelled chestnuts one by one into it
5. Place on a stone where they will become beautifully glass-like
6. Arrange immediately in the familiar boxes

title: Czukortorta (Sugar Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams butter
- 28 dekagrams flour
- 28 dekagrams powdered sugar
- a little vanilla
- zest of one lemon, finely chopped
- 5 hard-boiled egg yolks, sieved
- 3 egg yolks
- cream as needed for moistening
- 24 dekagrams sugar
- juice of half a lemon
- a little egg yolk
- jam of choice for filling
- lemon glaze

## Directions

1. Mix together butter, flour, powdered sugar, vanilla, lemon zest, and hard-boiled sieved egg yolks well
2. Place butter in the center and work together with a rolling pin
3. Add 3 egg yolks and cream as needed to moisten the dough
4. Combine the dough with a broad knife; the dough should not be stiff
5. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a little finger
6. Cut three equal layers from it and bake them nicely, then cool
7. Spread jam on each layer, assemble them together, and cover with lemon glaze
8. For the glaze: place 24 dekagrams sugar (rubbed lightly on lemon then finely powdered and sieved) in a bowl
9. Add juice of half a lemon and a little egg yolk and stir continuously
10. If the glaze is not thin enough, add more juice and stir for half an hour
11. When well foamed, cover the cake with it
12. In a cooled oven, slowly dry the glaze

title: Czukros tészta-táblácskák (Sugar Dough Tablets)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- zest of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- 12 spoonfuls of finely ground sugar
- 1 egg white, beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Mix the finely chopped zest of 1 lemon with 12 spoonfuls of finely ground sugar
2. Make the dough with the stiff egg white, which should not be soft
3. Roll out and cut into tablets
4. Place on a wax-coated tray
5. Bake at gentle heat

title: Dara-pép (Semolina Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 deciiliters cream
- semolina
- 7 dekagrams butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 10 dekagrams powdered sugar
- powdered vanilla
- 6 egg whites beaten stiff
- candied lemon peel
- preserved currants

## Directions

1. Cook semolina in 8 deciiliters of cream until it becomes a thick paste
2. Cool completely
3. Finely mix 7 dekagrams of butter with 8 egg yolks
4. Add the semolina paste and stir for a while longer
5. Mix in 10 dekagrams of powdered sugar with a small amount of powdered vanilla
6. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
7. Butter a pot well and place finely cut candied lemon peel on the bottom, forming a star shape
8. Put part of the mixture in, then preserved currants, then the rest of the mixture
9. Cook in a steam bath, carefully turn onto a plate and serve

title: Daramorzsóka (Semolina Porridge)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-quarter decilitres of fine semolina
- seven decilitres of milk
- five whole eggs
- two decilitres of milk
- vanilla
- sugar
- fourteen dekagrams of butter
- salt
- raisins

## Directions

1. Four hours before use, soak one and one-quarter decilitres of fine semolina in seven decilitres of milk, then set aside
2. Beat five whole eggs with two decilitres of milk and vanilla sifted with sugar, then mix with the soaked semolina
3. Melt fourteen dekagrams of butter in a wide shallow pan, pour the mixture into it with a little salt and braise it to a beautiful golden yellow
4. You can also add fourteen dekagrams of raisins during the braising
5. Serve the porridge immediately as it loses much of its quality from prolonged warming
6. Heap it high on the plate, sprinkling each layer well with sugar

title: Debreczeni Bélés (Debrecen-Style Filled Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pork caul fat lard
- flour
- eggs
- salt water
- filling options: cheese, rice, apple, cabbage, or jam

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as for other filled pastries
2. Brush the stretched dough generously with pork caul fat lard on a stretching board, not on the table
3. Do not cut off the edges but fold them back and stretch 6, 10, or even 12 layers on top of each other
4. When all layers are stretched, fold the entire sheet accordion-style into eight layers and allow to rest in cold winter weather for several days if needed
5. When ready to use, stretch to knife-blade thickness and fill with cheese, rice, apple, cabbage, or jam as desired
6. Gather all edges of the dough together to form a round filled pastry
7. Bake quickly in a hot oven

title: Derelye (Fruit Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- flour
- preserved plum jam
- salt
- brown butter

## Directions

1. Make a dough with four eggs and flour
2. Roll out the dough
3. Place small mounds of preserved plum jam at intervals
4. Cover with another piece of dough
5. Cut out semicircular shapes with a fluted pastry wheel
6. Cook in salted water
7. Rinse with cold water
8. Serve with hot brown butter

title: Derelye with Cheese Filling (Derelye)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight decagrams of curd cheese
- four eggs
- sugar
- sour cream
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix twenty-eight decagrams of curd cheese with four eggs and a little sugar
2. Add a little sour cream
3. Treat this filling the same way as the plum jam filling
4. Cut and cook as for fruit dumplings

title: Dinnye eczetben (Melon in Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- melon, peeled and sliced
- 1.5 liters vinegar per 500 grams melon
- 80 dekagrams sugar per 500 grams melon
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel the melon and cut into slices, place in a basin.
2. For each 500 grams of melon, cook 1.5 liters vinegar with 80 dekagrams sugar and pour over the melon.
3. Repeat this process.
4. On the third day, cook the vinegar with the melon together, then remove it and pack in a jar with cloves and cinnamon.
5. Cook the vinegar carefully with frequent skimming until slightly reduced and pour hot over the melon slices.
6. If the vinegar becomes thin again after a few days, cook it once more.

title: Dinnye Párban (Melon in Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- noble variety perfectly ripe fresh yellow melon
- sugar
- water
- 500 grams fruit requires 17 dekagrams sugar and 3 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Peel a most noble variety, perfectly ripe fresh yellow melon and cut into small slices
2. Calculate 17 dekagrams sugar for 500 grams fruit, cook well with 3 deciliters water, then cool
3. Pour over the arranged melon slices, bind the jars well and treat according to instructions 961

title: Disznófej-sajt (Pork Head Cheese)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh pork head
- 4-5 pieces of fresh pork skin, palm-sized
- salt
- one red onion, parboiled
- one whole lemon
- paprika
- black pepper
- clove pepper
- a few spoonfuls thin red blood
- cleaned stomach casing

## Directions

1. Boil the fresh pork head and fresh pork skin until tender
2. Let cool until half-cold, then remove meat from bones
3. Cut meat into long, thin slices and the pork skin into thin strips
4. Salt appropriately and gently mix the meat with skin so they do not break
5. Finely chop the parboiled red onion and squeeze lemon juice into it, grate in the zest
6. Add a little paprika, abundant black and clove pepper
7. Add a few spoonfuls of thin red blood to achieve a nice dark color
8. Gently fold everything together, stirring slowly so as not to break the meat and skin, until evenly colored and flavored throughout
9. Fill the cleaned stomach casing well with the mixture
10. Boil in hot salted water for half an hour
11. Pierce with a thin needle; if clear fat rather than red liquid comes out, it is done
12. Remove and press flat while warm
13. Either smoke it or use it fresh; dry in air for twenty-four hours for additional flavor

title: Disznótoros Káposzta (Pork Butcher's Cabbage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh pork meat, cut into pieces
- onion
- paprika
- fat
- salt
- hot water
- cabbage, boiled soft in water
- ground black pepper

## Directions

1. Fry the pork pieces until browned
2. Add paprika and onion in fat
3. Do not add liquid until the meat begins to cook in its own fat
4. Season with salt
5. Add hot water gradually if needed until the meat is tender
6. Let the liquid cook away so the meat has a roasted appearance
7. Add the pre-boiled cabbage and stir in
8. Add ground black pepper
9. Simmer together for fifteen minutes
10. When serving, sprinkle with ground black pepper

title: Diák-tészta (Student's Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Eighteen dekagrams flour
- Sixteen dekagrams butter
- Four deciliters hot milk
- Six egg whites, stiffly beaten
- Sugar
- Good concentrated broth

## Directions

1. Mix flour and butter well together
2. Cook in four deciliters hot milk
3. Cool
4. Mix in stiffly beaten egg whites from six eggs
5. Sweeten well
6. Pour into a mold
7. Place in steam bath
8. Put some coals on the lid
9. Serve with good concentrated broth poured over

title: Dió-torta (Walnut Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramm finely chopped walnuts
- 14 dekagramm soaked and finely ground almonds
- 28 dekagramm sugar
- a little vanilla
- 14 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. In bowl, stir finely chopped walnuts, soaked ground almonds, sugar and a little vanilla with fourteen egg yolks for half an hour, stirring constantly
2. Finally gently fold in stiffly beaten egg white foam
3. Butter two equal cake layers and their rings
4. Fill each with half the mixture and bake at gentle heat
5. Turn onto sieve and when cooled, fill one layer with preserves
6. Place second layer on top and coat with lemon or orange frosting
7. Note: cake layers should not be too thick or they will become heavy and fatty; thin layers make the cake light and fluffy
8. Walnuts should not be ground but only chopped together

title: Diós Lepény (Walnut Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams flour
- 7 dekagrams butter
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 1 egg yolk
- 28 dekagrams walnuts, finely chopped
- sugar for filling (same amount as walnuts)
- 7 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix flour and butter well
2. Add 3 tablespoons sugar and 1 egg yolk to make dough
3. Roll out and partially dry in the oven
4. Meanwhile prepare the filling: cook the finely chopped walnuts, equal amount of sugar, and 7 egg yolks until well thickened
5. Cool the filling slightly
6. Fill the pastry with the mixture
7. Once the pastry is baked and cooled, cut into portions

title: Diós Pite (Walnut Pie)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- stretched puff pastry
- butter
- walnuts, finely ground
- sugar
- lemon zest, grated
- raisins
- thick syrup

## Directions

1. Layer the thinly drawn and dried puff pastry into a greased baking pan, brushing each layer with butter
2. Between each layer, sprinkle finely ground sugared walnuts generously, grated lemon zest, and abundant raisins
3. Continue until the pan is full
4. Place in the oven to bake
5. When baked, pour thick syrup over it
6. Alternatively use the juice from any sugared preserves, boiled up
7. Return to the oven for about ten more minutes
8. Remove and turn onto a sieve
9. When half-cooled, slice as desired

title: Diós torta (Walnut Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams of finely ground sugar
- twelve egg yolks
- twelve dekagrams of walnut kernel, finely broken
- zest of half a lemon
- a little cinnamon
- twelve egg whites
- a handful of white flour or fine breadcrumbs
- butter and breadcrumbs for the mold

## Directions

1. Beat together the finely ground sugar and twelve egg yolks for half an hour.
2. Add the finely broken walnut kernel to the mixture.
3. Add the zest of half a lemon and a little cinnamon.
4. Finally add the twelve egg whites beaten into stiff foam, and a handful of white flour or fine breadcrumbs.
5. Beat well together.
6. Pour into a cake mold buttered and dusted with breadcrumbs.
7. Bake until done.

title: Diós torta (Walnut Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- walnuts
- butter
- sugar
- egg yolks
- egg white foam
- cornstarch
- preserved fruit
- lemon or orange icing

## Directions

1. Combine walnuts lightly into the mixture.
2. Butter two equal cake layers and their rims, fill each with half the mixture, and bake at gentle heat.
3. Turn out onto a sieve and once cooled, fill one layer with preserved fruit and place the other on top.
4. Coat with lemon or orange icing.
5. Note that the cake layers must not be very thick, otherwise they will easily become fatty and heavy; conversely, if baked in thin layers, the cake will be very light and airy.
6. The walnuts must not be broken but only chopped.

title: Diós-Lepény (Walnut Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagrams walnuts
- 1/2 teaspoon powdered cinnamon
- 1 orange, zest finely chopped
- 1 lemon, zest finely chopped
- 28 dekagrams sugar
- 2 deciliters water
- 25 dekagrams raisins
- crumbly dough
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Shell and finely grind the walnuts
2. Add ground cinnamon and finely chopped orange and lemon zests, mix well
3. Place sugar in a pot with water and boil until the sugar completely dissolves
4. Add the walnut mixture and keep on heat until the walnuts are well swollen and the whole mass becomes thick
5. Meanwhile, prepare crumbly dough
6. Roll out the dough in two pieces to knife-thickness
7. Place on the prepared baking dish
8. Spread the filling to two fingers' thickness
9. Sprinkle 25 dekagrams raisins on top
10. Cover with the other layer of dough, press edges together
11. Brush with egg yolk and bake
12. Remove from the oven while the dough is still crispy but the filling not dried out
13. The same may be made with almonds instead, but these should not be soaked, but ground with the skin on

title: Dióstészta almával (Walnut Dough with Apple)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter the size of an egg
- Two tablespoons flour
- Four deciliters hot milk
- Four apples
- Twenty walnuts
- Sugar
- Nine egg yolks
- Five egg whites, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. Make a béchamel: melt butter, add flour and mix until smooth
2. Gradually add hot milk while constantly stirring
3. Cook well and cool
4. Put in a basin when cooled
5. Peel apples and cut into cubes
6. Crush twenty walnuts coarsely and add sugar to taste
7. Beat nine egg yolks in one by one and stir until sufficiently fluffy
8. Finally mix in stiffly beaten egg whites
9. Pour into a buttered mold
10. Braise for one hour

title: Dobos torta (Dobos Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four egg whites
- seven dekagrams of sugar
- five egg yolks
- seven dekagrams of sugar
- ten dekagrams of flour
- creamy butter for the mold and flour for dusting
- fourteen dekagrams of butter
- twenty-eight dekagrams of sugar
- fourteen dekagrams of chocolate
- four egg yolks
- a little vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat four egg whites into half-stiff foam and mix in seven dekagrams of sugar.
2. Rub together seven dekagrams of sugar with five egg yolks until foamy.
3. Add ten dekagrams of flour to the egg yolk mixture.
4. Fold in the beaten egg whites.
5. Coat the cake mold with creamy butter and dust with flour.
6. Spread a knife's-thickness layer of the batter onto the mold and bake. Six to seven layers can be made from the given quantity.
7. For the filling: rub fourteen dekagrams of butter until foamy.
8. Cook twenty-eight dekagrams of sugar into syrup.
9. Add to the foamy butter fourteen dekagrams of dissolved and cooled chocolate, four egg yolks, and a little vanilla.
10. When the sugar syrup is also cooled, add it to the butter and mix together.
11. When the entire mixture is completely cooled, spread it on the sponge layers, placing a second layer on each coated layer.
12. For the icing: caramelize fourteen dekagrams of broken sugar to golden color and pour it over the top of the finished cake, smoothing it with a blunt knife.

title: Dorongfánk (Stick Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams flour
- 1 deciliter lukewarm sour cream
- 1.5 dekagrams brewer's yeast dissolved in sour cream
- 7 dekagrams melted butter
- 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
- salt
- beef fat
- finely chopped almonds
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make dough from flour, lukewarm sour cream, yeast, melted butter, eggs and egg yolks, and salt
2. Beat well and let rise
3. When slightly risen, place on a flour-dusted board and stretch to the thickness of a little finger
4. Cut into strips two finger-widths wide
5. Grease a spit with good beef fat, heat it, wrap the strips around it and press gently with your hand so they don't fall off
6. During frying, brush with butter and slowly fry until golden yellow
7. When half done, sprinkle with finely chopped almonds
8. Finish frying, remove from spit, sprinkle with sugar and fry the rest the same way

title: Dry Pea Soup (Száraz Borsó Leves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter dry peas
- three liters clean spring water
- carrot
- parsnip root
- celery
- a few fried bread crumbs
- a little nutmeg flower

## Directions

1. Put the dry peas in a pot and fill with three liters of clean spring water
2. Add carrot, parsnip root, celery, a few fried bread crumbs, and a little nutmeg flower
3. Boil slowly and use as needed

title: Dödölle (Millet Porridge)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven decilitres millet porridge (well rinsed)
- one and a half litres hot salted water
- seven dekagrams pork fat
- two decilitres sour cream
- crumbled salty cheese

## Directions

1. Cook the well-rinsed millet porridge in one and a half litres of hot salted water, stirring constantly to prevent burning, until it is so thick that the cooking spoon stands in it
2. Tear this into haluska-sized pieces, place them around the edge of a large bowl to cool so it keeps its shape
3. Once cooled, move them from the bowl's edge to the center with a knife, and always place new ones on the edge until all are torn
4. In another pot, melt seven dekagrams of pork fat, add two decilitres of good sour cream, pour this hot over the haluska, top with crumbled salty cheese, and shake the whole thing well

title: Egy kilogramm mézet alapú eczet (Vinegar Based on One Kilogram Honey)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram honey
- 40 dekagrams powdered tartar
- 1.5 liters good grain brandy
- 12 liters lukewarm rainwater or boiled well water

## Directions

1. Pour 1 kilogram of honey, 40 dekagrams of powdered tartar, and 1.5 liters of good grain brandy into a twelve-liter barrel.
2. Fill it completely with lukewarm rainwater or boiled well water and let it stand in a warm place for three months.
3. After this time, every month you can take out 5 liters of the best vinegar from the barrel, then pour 7 deciliters of brandy and 43 deciliters of water into it.

title: Egész Baraczkot Befőzni (Preserved Whole Apricots)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots that are firm and yellowish, not fully ripe
- half kilogram sugar
- apricots equal in quantity to the sugar
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. The apricots should not be fully ripe, but rather firm and yellowish. For half kilogram of sugar, take the same amount of apricots. Cook the sugar with one glass of water, clean it, and cook until it forms heavy drops but does not thread. Wash the apricots, dry them with cloth, halve them, place in the sugar, and bring to boil three times on all sides so they do not become soft and their skin remains beautiful and whole. With a silver spoon...

title: Egész baraczkot befőzni (Whole Preserved Apricots)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams apricots
- 500 grams sugar
- 1 glass water

## Directions

1. The apricots must not be completely ripe, but hard and yellowish.
2. Wash the apricots and dry them with cloth.
3. Cut them in half in the middle and place them in sugar.
4. Bring to a boil three times on all sides, so they do not soften and their skin remains intact.
5. Remove them with a silver spoon, cool them, and pour the syrup over them; the glass remains open.
6. After twenty-four hours, strain the syrup, bring it to a boil again, and pour it slightly warmer over the fruit.
7. Repeat this for three days; on the fourth day, boil the syrup again until it thickens, cool it, and pour it over.
8. On the fifth day, tie the glass closed.

title: Egész baraczkot más módon befőzni (Whole Preserved Apricots, Alternative Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots
- slaked lime
- white alum
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Use the best type of beautiful yellow apricots, not yet completely ripe.
2. Prepare clean water in a clean container, and strain slaked lime through clean cloth into the water until it becomes milky.
3. Peel the apricots, cut them in half, remove the pit, and place them in the lime water so they do not blacken.
4. While peeling, boil 7-10 liters of clean water with 10-12 dekagrams of white alum in a clean container.
5. Remove the peeled apricots from the lime water, wash them two or three more times in clean water, and boil them in the alum water for 3-4 minutes with constant stirring without softening them.
6. Remove them still firm and place on a sieve so the water drains well, then arrange on a clean tablecloth spread on a table.
7. For 1.5 kilograms of fruit, take 1 kilogram of sugar and boil it with three glasses of water until it throws heavy drops.
8. Arrange the apricots in a porcelain dish in a beautiful order and pour the boiled and cooled sugar over them.
9. Leave for twenty-four hours.
10. After this time, pour off the sugar, boil it again until it thickens, cool it, and pour it over the apricots again, leaving for another twenty-four hours.
11. On the third day, place the apricots in glasses and use the liquid that was on them for various purposes.
12. Again boil 1 kilogram of sugar with three glasses of water for 1.5 kilograms of fruit until it throws heavy drops, cool it, and pour over the apricots in the glasses.
13. Tie the glasses well and steam for 2-3 minutes.

title: Egész spanyol-meggyet befőzni (Preserved Whole Cherries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams sugar
- 1 glass water
- 500 grams cherries

## Directions

1. Boil 500 grams of sugar with one glass of water, removing the scum during boiling so it is completely clear, and boil until it throws heavy drops.
2. Then add 500 grams of the most beautiful type of cherry, having trimmed half of the stem, do not stir it with a spoon, but shake the pan several times so it mixes well with the sugar, and boil for a quarter hour.
3. Then remove the cherries with a slotted spoon and boil the juice for another quarter hour.
4. When removing from the fire, place the cherries back into it, and handle as you would other preserved items when filling.

title: Elfort élesztője (Elfort Yeast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 part finely ground barley malt
- 3 parts boiling water
- Good beer yeast (one sixteenth part)

## Directions

1. Pour one part of finely ground barley malt into a barrel
2. Add three parts of boiling water and stir for a while, then close the vessel with a lid
3. After standing for two hours, pour off the liquid from the malt
4. Boil for three hours
5. Pour back into the barrel and when cooled, add one sixteenth part of good beer yeast
6. After several days, fermentation is complete and the yeast has settled
7. If a small amount of surface yeast appears, remove it and later mix with the bottom yeast
8. Pour the prepared yeast into bottles; after 24 hours, pour off the liquid, cover the yeast with cold water, plug the bottles, and keep them in a vessel filled with cold water

title: Elsikkadt tojás sóskamártással (Poached Eggs with Sorrel Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- sorrel
- flour
- salt
- sour cream
- white vinegar
- water
- eggs

## Directions

1. Melt butter and add finely chopped sorrel
2. Braise until the liquid has evaporated
3. Sprinkle with a little flour and braise again
4. Season with salt and add sour cream
5. Simmer until fully cooked
6. In a pot, bring one part white vinegar and three parts water to a boil with salt
7. Crack eggs into the boiling liquid, being careful to keep them whole and not let them overcook
8. Place the sorrel sauce in a dish
9. Remove the poached eggs with a slotted spoon and carefully place in the sauce, keeping them whole

title: Elvert Darázsfészek (Whipped Wasp's Nest)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as in previous recipe
- flour
- preserved apricots, currants or sour cherries
- chopped nuts
- sugar
- raisins
- ground poppy seed
- lemon or orange zest
- cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the same dough as the previous recipe and let it rise well
2. Place on a flour-dusted board and roll out to a good thick knife-width
3. Cut into hand-width and arm-length pieces with a pastry cutter
4. Fill with preserved apricots, currants or sour cherries
5. Alternatively, mix ground nuts, sugar and raisins, or ground poppy seed, sugar and finely chopped lemon or orange zest, mixing with a few tablespoons of cream to a thick mass
6. Spread this filling on the pieces, roll them up
7. Place upright in a buttered baking dish or cake pan, buttering each roll so they separate when baked
8. Let rise in a warm place and bake at gentle but steady heat

title: Emperor's Crumbs (Császármorzsóka)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagrams butter, beaten fluffy
- 6 egg yolks
- 8 spoonfuls sour cream
- 7 dekagrams sugar, rubbed with lemon and ground
- salt
- 6 spoonfuls fine flour
- 6 egg whites
- butter for greasing
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Into 7 dekagrams of fluffy beaten butter, gradually mix 6 egg yolks and then 8 spoonfuls of sour cream, one spoonful at a time
2. Add 7 dekagrams of sugar rubbed with lemon, a little salt, and 6 spoonfuls of fine flour
3. Finally, when well mixed, add the stiff snow from 6 egg whites
4. Pour the batter into a buttered baking dish
5. Bake in the oven until golden yellow
6. Break up with two knives
7. Heap high on a plate and serve dusted with sugar
8. Alternatively, for Emperor's Boats: place the batter in a buttered baking dish, bake until light yellow, cut into diagonal cubes with a sharp knife and make a cross-cut on top, fry until golden in hot lard, fill the cut as desired with preserved fruit and serve dusted with sugar

title: Erős eczet (Strong Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 dekagrams tarragon
- 10 dekagrams shallot
- 7 dekagrams pearl onion
- 10 dekagrams bay leaf
- 7 dekagrams angelica root
- 10 dekagrams capers
- 10 dekagrams anchovy
- 7 deciliters strong vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop 20 dekagrams of tarragon, 10 dekagrams of shallot, 7 dekagrams of pearl onion, 10 dekagrams of bay leaf, 7 dekagrams of angelica root, 10 dekagrams of capers, and the same amount of anchovy.
2. Pour into a bottle and cover with 7 deciliters of strong vinegar, stop it well, and place it in the sun or next to a warm oven for four weeks.
3. Then press through cloth and filter the vinegar once more.

title: Erős leves - Consommé (Strong Broth - Consommé)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven egg whites
- half a liter white strong meat broth
- butter
- one young cabbage
- two kohlrabi
- two carrots
- fresh goose fat
- three-quarter part of a roll

## Directions

1. Beat seven egg whites well in a pot and mix with half a liter of white strong meat broth
2. Pour into a butter-greased pot and poach
3. Meanwhile, clean a young cabbage, two kohlrabi, and two carrots
4. Braise them with a little butter or fresh goose fat, but in such a way that they become brown, for which purpose pour a little meat broth over them several times
5. Finally, cut three-quarter part of a roll lengthwise and fry until golden in fat
6. When the consommé is solidified from braising, turn it out onto a clean board
7. Cut it with a dumpling cutter into crescent-shaped or round slices
8. Place the vegetables, the fried bread, and the consommé in a soup bowl
9. Pour brown broth over it

title: Erősebb fajta kávé-hab (Strong Coffee Foam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 tablespoons very strong, clear, cold coffee
- 10 dekagrams ground sugar
- 3.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- 7 deciliters excellent heavy cream
- crushed ice

## Directions

1. Place 7 tablespoons of very strong, clear, cold coffee in a basin with 10 dekagrams ground sugar
2. When dissolved, add 3.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass and set on ice
3. Beat 7 deciliters of excellent heavy cream with crushed ice in a brass basin using a whip until it becomes stiff foam
4. Quickly fold in the prepared coffee
5. If the flavor is very weak and color light, add more coffee
6. Pour into mold and freeze on ice

title: Estragon-eczet (Tarragon Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters good clean wine vinegar
- 5 dekagrams dried tarragon herb
- 15 grams raisins
- 1 dekagram ginger
- 10 cloves
- 2 dekagrams fresh garlic

## Directions

1. Pour 4 deciliters of good clean wine vinegar into a somewhat larger bottle.
2. Add 5 dekagrams of dried tarragon herb, 15 grams of raisins, 1 dekagram of ginger, 10 cloves, and 2 dekagrams of fresh garlic.
3. Place the bottle in the sun, and stirring frequently, let it stand for 4 weeks, then filter the vinegar through cloth.

title: Fagylalt hálóköntösben (Ice Cream in a Nightgown)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- vanilla ice cream
- raspberry, sour cherry, or currant ice cream

## Directions

1. Prepare vanilla ice cream and freeze it hard
2. In another mold, prepare raspberry, sour cherry, or currant ice cream and freeze it similarly; the vanilla ice cream mold should be only half full
3. Once frozen solid, repeatedly spread the ice cream on the inner sides of the mold, always taking the ice cream from the center so a deep hollow remains that does not reach the bottom, with the whole mold being newly coated in thickness
4. Remove 3 large spoonfuls of vanilla ice cream into an ice-chilled bowl, quickly fill the hollow with the liquid ice cream, spread it strongly and smoothly with a spoon, and cover the top with the removed vanilla ice cream
5. Close the mold, freshen the ice, and turn it rapidly in the ice for a quarter hour, drain the water well, cover the mold with ice, and leave it there until serving
6. Then quickly turn it out, cut into slices, and serve at once

title: Fagylaltak befőttek levéből (Ice Cream from Preserved Fruit Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters fruit syrup
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 4 deciliters water
- juice of 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Cook 14 dekagrams of sugar with 4 deciliters of water until the sugar is dissolved
2. Pour this hot mixture into 4 deciliters of fruit syrup and mix well until completely dissolved
3. Add the juice of 3 lemons
4. Treat it like other ice creams

title: Fahéj-fánk (Cinnamon Fried Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram of flour
- quarter kilogram of powdered sugar
- one good spoonful of ground cinnamon
- a few spoonfuls of good sour cream
- wine
- egg yolks
- hot fat
- cinnamon sugar

## Directions

1. Mix half kilogram of flour, quarter kilogram of powdered sugar, one good spoonful of ground cinnamon, a few spoonfuls of sour cream, the same of wine, and enough egg yolks to make a moderately stiff dough
2. Roll out to the thickness of a knife blade
3. Cut into small squares
4. Wrap around a rolling pin with thick string in a spiral shape
5. Fry in hot fat until brown
6. Dust with cinnamon sugar
7. Can also be filled with jam

title: Fahéj-ostya (Cinnamon Wafer)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm swollen and finely ground almonds
- 4 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 1/2 kilogramm ground sugar
- 1 dekagramm cinnamon
- 1 lemon, zest finely chopped
- 1/2 lemon juice
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix finely 28 dekagramm swollen and finely ground almonds with 4 stiffly beaten egg whites, 1/2 kilogramm ground sugar, 1 dekagramm cinnamon, the finely chopped zest of 1 lemon, and 1/2 lemon juice
2. Spread the dough knife-thickness by the spoonful onto a butter-coated sheet
3. Bake and while warm bend onto round wooden sticks

title: Fajdtyúk Sülve (Roasted Wood Grouse)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- wood grouse
- white paper
- butter
- marinade
- sour cream

## Directions

1. If the wood grouse is young, roast like a pheasant
2. Do not pluck the head but wrap it in white paper
3. Prepare it nicely
4. Lard the breast densely and roast
5. If the grouse is older, marinate and lard like haunch of venison
6. During roasting, baste several times with the marinade
7. When almost done, pour a few spoonfuls of sour cream under the marinade and baste several more times

title: Farsangi Fánk (Carnival Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagrams sifted flour
- salt
- 1 spoonful powdered sugar
- 2 deciliters lukewarm sour cream
- 5 egg yolks
- 7 dekagrams melted butter
- 1.5 dekagrams fresh brewer's yeast, softened

## Directions

1. Since these fritters are mainly made at carnival time and require a warm oven to rise properly, it is especially necessary to place the flour near a heated oven one day before use so it is dry and warm
2. Put 42 dekagrams sifted flour in a bowl, salt it, add 1 spoonful powdered sugar
3. Take 2 deciliters lukewarm sour cream, 5 egg yolks, 7 dekagrams melted butter and 1.5 dekagrams softened fresh brewer's yeast, mix everything well together
4. Take as much flour as needed for working the fritters
5. Since not all flour is the same, it is better to take more of it so the dough is not too hard; you can always add more flour later if necessary
6. Sift the above mixture through a sieve into a bowl and stir with the flour until the dough separates from the spoon and beat well for some time
7. Note that the dough should not be hard, although it is difficult to beat
8. Place the dough on a flour-dusted board and stretch to three knife-edge thicknesses, cut out the fritters with a floured fritter cutter and place them on a flour-dusted board
9. Cover with a light cloth so the dough does not dry out, then let rise well beside a warm oven, they must rise to such a height again
10. Particularly must take care that the dough does not

title: Farsangi Fánk More Another Way (Carnival Fritters Another Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 90 grams butter, creamed
- 6 egg yolks
- a pinch of salt
- 1 tablespoon granulated sugar
- 2 deciliters warm sour cream
- 13.5 grams fresh baker's yeast dissolved in the sour cream
- flour as needed for a light dough

## Directions
