1. Mix the creamed butter with egg yolks one by one, then add salt, sugar, and the warm sour cream in which the yeast has been dissolved
2. Add flour until a light dough forms
3. Stir until the dough separates from the spoon, then beat slightly
4. Let rise in a warm place
5. Once risen, place on a flour-dusted board and roll out to the thickness of a little finger
6. Cut out fritters and once they rise again, fry in hot fat

title: Fasirozott csirke (Ground Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful meaty chickens
- Bacon
- Grated roll soaked in milk
- Eggs
- Nutmeg flowers
- Salt
- Beef broth
- Mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. After blanching beautiful meaty chickens, let them soak for a while in warm water until they turn white nicely.
2. Split them in half along the back and remove all the bones up to the wings and legs.
3. Cut the meat, membrane, and all stringy parts together with a little bacon into small pieces.
4. Grate and milk-soak half a roll, wring it out well, beat two eggs into it, and stir over the fire until it becomes thick.
5. Crush the meat together with the roll in a mortar until fine.
6. Add a little nutmeg flower and salt.
7. Dry the chickens with a cloth, pour one or two spoonfuls of beef broth over them.
8. Braise slowly for half an hour and prepare a little mushroom sauce for them.
9. When serving, remove the bacon from the chickens, place on a platter, and pour the sauce over them.

title: Fasirozott Nyúl (Molded Rabbit)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of cold roasted rabbit
- piece of venison
- red onion
- bread soaked in beef broth
- egg yolks
- sour cream
- goose fat
- fine bread crumbs
- rabbit mold
- thin venison or rabbit loin slices
- brown roux
- stock
- vinegar
- lemon peel
- capers
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Chop finely cold roasted rabbit, venison, a little red onion and bread soaked in beef broth
2. Add two egg yolks and two spoonfuls of sour cream and mix everything well
3. Butter a rabbit mold and coat with fine bread crumbs
4. Place part of the minced meat in it
5. Line the center of the mold with thin venison or rabbit loin slices and place the rest of the minced meat on top
6. Bake nicely
7. Make a small brown roux, thin with stock, add a little vinegar, lemon peel, capers and finally one or two spoonfuls of good sour cream and cook
8. Turn the rabbit out onto a plate and pour the sauce over it

title: Fegyverfánk (Wafer Fritter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagramm butter beaten foamy
- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- 7 dekagramm swollen and finely ground almonds
- 9 dekagramm ground sugar with vanilla
- 4-5 tablespoons cream
- 7 dekagramm fine flour

## Directions

1. Beat 7 dekagramm butter foamy and slowly beat in 1 whole egg and 1 egg yolk
2. Mix in well 7 dekagramm swollen and finely ground almonds and 9 dekagramm ground sugar with vanilla
3. Add 4-5 tablespoons cream and finally 7 dekagramm fine flour mixed with the rest
4. Heat the wafer fritter iron on a flame, first coat with butter, wipe with paper, heat again
5. Place a small spoonful of dough on the iron, press the iron together well, turn several times over the fire so the dough is brownish-yellow on both sides
6. Quickly peel off with a knife and place in arch shape
7. Once all are baked, let sit in a warm place as they easily soften
8. Pile high on a plate and sprinkle with sugar

title: Fehér Kocsonya (Blanc Manger)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm blanched and finely ground almonds
- 1 liter hot cream
- sugar
- 2 dekagramm dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Place 28 dekagramm of blanched and finely ground almonds in a porcelain pot
2. Pour 1 liter of hot cream over them and whisk well
3. Strain through cloth into a tin-lined basin
4. Add enough sugar to make it sufficiently sweet
5. Mix in 2 dekagramm dissolved isinglass
6. Place in a deep porcelain cup and stir several times with a silver spoon to prevent a film from forming on top
7. Place on crushed ice or in a cellar and freeze

title: Fehér Kocsonya Kávéval (White Jelly with Coffee)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliter good cream
- 7 dekagramm coffee
- 8 egg yolks
- 18 dekagramm crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Heat 7 deciliter of good cream
2. Roast 7 dekagramm of coffee and place in a soup bowl
3. Pour the hot cream over it immediately and cover with a lid
4. Let stand for a quarter hour
5. Strain through a sieve
6. In a pot, whisk together 8 egg yolks with 18 dekagramm crushed sugar
7. Mix this with the cream
8. Pour into a deep porcelain cup and place in a water bath
9. Freeze in a cold place for a quarter hour before serving

title: Fehér Kocsonya Ostábla Alakban (White Jelly in Checkerboard Pattern)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- white jelly
- 2 tablets grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Prepare white jelly and pour into a flat rectangular dish
2. Place on ice and freeze completely
3. When well frozen, mark in advance and cut lengthwise six times and widthwise six times to form equal square cubes
4. Carefully remove every other cube with a spoon handle, leaving alternating cubes in place
5. Cook the removed cubes with 2 tablets of grated chocolate
6. When cooled, fill the empty spaces with the chocolate mixture
7. Return to ice and freeze again to form a checkerboard pattern

title: Fehér Mandola-ívek (White Almond Arches)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramm sugar
- 2 tablespoons water
- 14 dekagramm ground almonds
- 1 lemon, zest finely chopped and juice
- 1 egg white, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. Cook 14 dekagramm sugar with 2 tablespoons water until it threads
2. Add 14 dekagramm ground almonds, the finely chopped zest and juice of 1 lemon, and 1 stiffly beaten egg white
3. Dry the dough over charcoal heat until it can be bent
4. Remove from heat and let cool
5. Spread onto wafers of desired size
6. Bend the arches over a round baking sheet and dry them

title: Fehér mandola-ívek (White Almond Wafers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams sugar
- water
- 14 dekagrams ground almond
- 1 lemon, zest finely chopped and juice
- 1 egg white, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. Cook 14 dekagrams of sugar with 2 spoonfuls of water until it threads
2. Add 14 dekagrams of ground almond, the finely chopped zest and juice of 1 lemon, and the stiffly beaten white of 1 egg

title: Fehér törtleves (White Broken Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- About 1/2 kilogram of roasted capon, turkey, or veal
- Bread, cut into large pieces
- Fat
- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- Nutmeg flower, a small amount
- 1.5 liters good beef broth

## Directions

1. From roasted capon, turkey, or veal, take about 1/2 kilogram and remove all brown skin, then chop finely with a kitchen knife.
2. Fry large pieces of milky bread cut into cubes in fat until a light yellow color.
3. Pound the 4 hard-boiled egg yolks finely in a large mortar together with the chopped meat, then place in a pot.
4. Pour 1.5 liters of good broth over it, add a small amount of nutmeg flower, and let it simmer for a good half hour.
5. Pass through a sieve and pour into marrow dumplings.

title: Fehérré Párolt Csirke (Braised Chicken in White Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- bacon
- parsley root
- carrot
- whole pepper
- mace
- broth
- flour
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. After plucking the chicken, remove the breastbone, salt it well and prepare it.
2. Line a pot with bacon, parsley root, carrot, whole pepper and mace.
3. Place the chicken on top, cover with thin bacon slices, pour two spoonfuls of broth over it.
4. Place white paper on top, cover the pot and braise slowly.
5. Remove the two chickens, cover them again with bacon so they remain white in color.
6. Sprinkle flour over the braised vegetables and pour on as much broth as needed.
7. When well cooked, strain the sauce, add mushrooms, remove the bacon from the chickens, place it in the pot and bring to a boil once more.

title: Fehérré párolt csirke (Braised White Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Salt
- Bacon
- Parsley root
- Yellow carrot
- Whole pepper
- Nutmeg flowers
- Broth
- Flour
- White paper

## Directions

1. After blanching the chicken, extract the breastbone.
2. Salt it and prepare it well.
3. Line a pot with bacon, parsley root, yellow carrot, whole pepper, and nutmeg flowers.
4. Place the chicken on top.
5. Cover with thin bacon slices.
6. Pour two spoonfuls of broth over it.
7. Place white paper on the pot, then put the lid on top.
8. Braise slowly.
9. After braising, remove the two chickens, cover them again with bacon to keep them white.
10. Sprinkle a little flour on the braised roots and pour enough broth over them to make a sauce.

title: Fejes saláta (Lettuce Salad)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- firm lettuce heads
- salt
- pepper
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut nice firm lettuce heads into two or four parts
2. Wash thoroughly and place on a cloth
3. Put pepper and salt in the salad bowl
4. Pour two parts oil and one part vinegar
5. Turn the lettuce well in this mixture and serve

title: Fekete Cseresznyét Befőzni (Preserved Black Cherries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- best quality black, juicy, ripe cherries
- half kilogram sugar
- three deciliters of water
- crushed vanilla

## Directions

1. Pit the best quality black, juicy, ripe cherries so their juice is not lost. Cook half kilogram of sugar with three deciliters of water, skim it, and when completely clear, add one kilogram of pitted cherries with their juice. Cook until test drops stay in place on a plate, then cool and pour into jars as with other preserves. A little crushed vanilla may also be added.

title: Feketekávé-kocsonya mandula-tejjel (Black Coffee Jelly with Almond Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four deciliters strong coffee
- sugar
- three dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- twenty-one dekagrams almonds (including several bitter ones)
- four deciliters water
- two dekagrams isinglass

## Directions

1. Prepare four deciliters of strong coffee and sweeten well
2. Mix in three dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
3. When the coffee is clear and well mixed with the isinglass, strain through table linen
4. Pour into a melon-shaped mold in alternating sections: first fill one section, when frozen, fill another section of the mold, place on ice until frozen, and continue alternating
5. Prepare almond milk by making a milk from twenty-one dekagrams of almonds (including a few bitter ones) with four deciliters of water
6. Sweeten the almond milk well and mix in two dekagrams of isinglass
7. Strain once more
8. When the coffee in the mold is well frozen, slowly pour in the almond milk to fill the mold
9. Place on ice and freeze
10. When unmolding, work quickly and carefully so the coffee does not mix with the almond milk, keeping the sections in their own colors, one white and one black

title: Felföldi torta (Upland Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters flour
- 25 dekagrams lard
- 7 dekagrams finely chopped blanched almonds
- 28 dekagrams powdered sugar
- a little cinnamon
- a little cloves
- grated zest of one lemon and a little juice
- 1 egg
- 1.5 dekagrams yeast dissolved in milk
- jam for filling
- egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Mix flour with lard, blanched finely chopped almonds, powdered sugar, cinnamon, cloves, lemon zest and juice, egg, and yeast dissolved in milk
2. Place half the dough in a buttered pan
3. Fill the top with jam
4. Arrange the other half of the dough as small rods over the jam
5. Let it rise slightly in the pan
6. Brush with egg and bake

title: Felvert Bábakalács (Whipped Babka Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 140 grams butter
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 deciliter sour cream
- 36 grams cheese yeast
- little salt
- sugar
- 1 lemon rind
- 240 grams fine flour
- chopped almonds
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Whip 140 grams butter finely and mix in 10 egg yolks one by one
2. Mix 36 grams cheese yeast into 1 deciliter sour cream and slowly add this to the butter mixture
3. Salt lightly, rub a piece of sugar with a lemon rind, crush it and add with 240 grams fine flour
4. Stir only until the flour is well mixed
5. Fill a mold buttered and sprinkled with chopped almonds and breadcrumbs halfway, let it rise in a warm place and then bake slowly

title: Fenyőmadár sülve (Roasted Pine Bird)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pine birds
- salt
- butter
- thin bacon slices
- thin bread slices
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Pluck the birds and cut off the head and wings at the first joint
2. Salt and peck the feet
3. Cross them on the spit at the ribs, placing each bird next to the other
4. Place two thin slices of bacon between each bird and a thin bread slice between the bacon
5. Drizzle butter while roasting
6. Before fully roasted, sprinkle with breadcrumbs and finish roasting

title: Fiatal pulyka présben, ászpikkal (Young Turkey in Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 young turkey, capon-sized
- carrot
- red onion
- fresh parsley
- cloves
- salt
- whole pepper
- 2 parts white meat broth
- 1 part white wine
- aspic (prepared separately)

## Directions

1. Clean the young turkey properly and place it in a large pot where it fits comfortably
2. Add carrot, red onion, fresh parsley, a few cloves, salt, and whole pepper
3. Pour in 2 parts white meat broth and 1 part white wine
4. Cook slowly over gentle heat until fully cooked but not falling apart, removing scum during cooking to keep it clean
5. Remove and cool it completely, cleaning it well
6. Prepare aspic as described in recipe 315
7. Pour two-finger-widths of aspic into a basin or deep mold that will hold the turkey
8. Set on ice and let gel completely
9. Place the turkey on the aspic, breast-side down
10. Pour aspic over it to about halfway and let gel
11. Once gelled, fill completely so the turkey is entirely covered and let gel well
12. Before serving, briefly dip the basin in warm water, dry quickly with a cloth, and unmold onto a plate

title: Finom Bukta (Fine Gugelhopf)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 4 deka yeast
- lukewarm sugar milk
- 1 kilogram flour
- salt
- juice of half a lemon
- grated lemon zest
- warm milk as needed
- 4 egg whites
- peeled chopped almonds
- raisins
- vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks with 4 tablespoons powdered sugar and 4 tablespoons butter until foamy
2. Dissolve 4 deka yeast in lukewarm, sugared milk
3. Add 1 kilogram flour, salt, lemon juice and grated zest, and as much warm milk as it absorbs, and stir into a very soft dough, always in one direction, for a whole hour
4. Beat 4 egg whites into stiff peaks and fold into the dough
5. Heat the vessel in which it will rise and bake, and butter it and coat with breadcrumbs
6. Pour two finger-widths of dough into the vessel, then butter it, and sprinkle with peeled chopped almonds and raisins
7. Alternate layers of dough with almonds and raisins, filling the vessel halfway, and butter the top
8. Cover with a warm lid and let rise in a warm place
9. When the vessel is full, bake at appropriate heat
10. When done, sprinkle generously with vanilla sugar

title: Finom Bábakalács Meggyal (Fine Babka with Sour Cherries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams butter
- 7 egg yolks
- 1 deciliter cream
- 2 dekagrams yeast
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- fine flour
- salt
- brandied sour cherries
- chopped almonds
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 12 dekagrams butter until foamy, then gradually add 7 egg yolks one by one
2. Dissolve 2 dekagrams yeast in cream and mix with 1 tablespoon sugar, and let stand until the yeast begins to work
3. Slowly fold the yeast mixture into the beaten butter
4. Season lightly with salt and add enough fine flour to make a light dough
5. Stir until the dough leaves the spoon and beat it slightly more
6. Butter a mold and coat with chopped almonds, then fill halfway with the dough
7. With a spoon dipped in flour, smooth it out, then place a row of brandied sour cherries on top
8. Cover with the remaining dough, let rise, and bake at gentle heat
9. When done, turn out and sprinkle with sugar and serve

title: Finom füvek (Fine Herbs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- several button mushrooms, finely chopped
- green parsley, finely chopped
- red onion, finely chopped
- butter in small amount

## Directions

1. Mix together the finely chopped mushrooms, parsley, and red onion
2. Combine with a small amount of butter
3. Gently simmer
4. Use with various dishes

title: Finom gyümöleskenyér (Fine Fruit Bread)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half a kilogram each of: stewed plums, pressed pear, good figs, dates, fresh raisins
- 25 dekagrams citronade
- 25 dekagrams quince paste
- 25 dekagrams Milan chocolate softened in a warm oven
- 25 dekagrams stewed almonds
- 25 dekagrams walnuts
- 25 dekagrams Italian hazelnuts
- a small teaspoon of cinnamon
- brioche dough of a certain quantity
- egg for coating
- butter for coating the paper

## Directions

1. Cut the stewed plums, pressed pear, figs, dates, and fresh raisins into equal cubes, half a kilogram of each
2. Similarly cut the citronade, quince paste, and chocolate into cubes
3. Cut the stewed almonds, walnuts, and Italian hazelnuts into cubes
4. Place all the cut ingredients together in a large bowl with a small teaspoon of cinnamon
5. Knead into a quantity of brioche dough
6. Take about a bread-roll portion of the dough and knead it until all the cut fruit is well incorporated, since the dough merely serves to hold the other sweets together and should barely be noticeable
7. Shape into a long loaf
8. Wrap in rectangular sheets of brioche dough
9. Brush with egg
10. Place on paper coated with butter
11. Bake in a sustained hot oven

title: Finom liszt-morzsonya (Fine Flour Pasta Dish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1.5 liters milk
- fat or butter
- cinnamon sugar
- abundant raisins

## Directions

1. Make a soft dough from 3 eggs that must be pourable
2. Bring 1.5 liters milk to a boil
3. When boiling, pour the dough in from a good height so that it becomes long strips
4. When the milk is cooked, add abundant fat or butter
5. Sprinkle cinnamon sugar among it and add abundant raisins
6. Put it in the oven to brown
7. When serving, sprinkle with cinnamon sugar

title: Finom Mákos Kifli (Fine Poppy Seed Crescent)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.8 liters dry flour
- 42 dekagrams butter
- 12 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons milk
- 5 dekagrams fresh cheese yeast dissolved in lukewarm milk
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- lukewarm milk as needed
- salt as needed
- poppy seed filling (as for poppy seed strudel)
- beaten egg for brushing
- For walnut filling: shelled walnuts, good white honey, milk, finely chopped candied lemon zest, ground vanilla

## Directions

1. Place flour on a board and salt it
2. Work in the butter leaf by leaf using a rolling pin
3. Add 12 egg yolks, 6 tablespoons milk, 5 dekagrams fresh cheese yeast dissolved in lukewarm milk, 1 tablespoon sugar, and lukewarm milk
4. Mix into a dough, not too stiff
5. Use a wide knife to work the dough, avoiding touching it with bare hands
6. Once the dough comes together, form small pieces
7. Cover with cloth and let rise well
8. Prepare poppy seed filling as for poppy seed strudel
9. Roll out each piece to the thickness of a knife blade
10. Spread filling to the same thickness
11. Fold in half, place on buttered paper, and let rise slightly
12. Brush with beaten egg and bake
13. For walnut filling: chop shelled walnuts finely and mix with good white honey and enough milk to make it spreadable
14. Add finely chopped candied lemon zest and ground vanilla
15. Simmer briefly, then cool without letting it set completely so it can be spread

title: Finom Omlós Kifli (Fine Crumbly Croissant)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 750 grams flour
- 500 grams fat
- salt
- knife-tip amount of ground clove
- 1 coffee spoon ground cinnamon
- 6 egg yolks
- 30 grams yeast
- warm sugared water
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- sliced almonds
- cinnamon sugar
- clove sugar

## Directions

1. Take 750 grams flour and 500 grams fat, and with half the flour mix the fat, little salt, knife-tip of ground clove, 1 coffee spoon ground cinnamon together with a pastry roller, and set aside one egg-sized piece of this dough, rolling it out to half a finger thickness and putting it in a cold place
2. With the remaining flour, 6 egg yolks, 30 grams yeast dissolved in warm sugared water, and 3 tablespoons sugar and the egg-sized piece of fatty dough, knead a fine soft dough, working it for a long time until it becomes smooth and bubbly
3. Place in a warm location and let it rise well, then turn it onto a board and stretch it to the proper size, and fold in the cooled fatty dough, letting it rest for half an hour folded
4. Roll out three times at half-hour intervals, finally leaving it knife-edge thin
5. Sprinkle with sliced almonds, cinnamon sugar, and clove sugar
6. Cut into cubes with a hot knife and form croissants from the cubes, coat with egg, sprinkle almonds on top and bake quickly until nicely golden
7. It rises beautifully, fine and crumbly like puff pastry and layered

title: Finom thea-sütemény (Fine Tea Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup crushed sugar
- 1 cup crushed shelled almonds
- 1 cup flour
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- 1/2 lemon juice and grated zest
- 1 egg white
- powdered sugar
- paper for baking

## Directions

1. Make a good stiff dough from 1 cup crushed sugar, 1 cup crushed shelled almonds, 1 cup flour, 1 whole egg, 2 egg yolks, 1/2 lemon juice and grated zest
2. Roll out to knife-blade thickness
3. Beat 1 egg white with powdered sugar until stiff foam
4. Cover the rolled dough with this foam
5. With a warm knife cut into finger-thick rods
6. Place on buttered paper on a pan and bake at gentle heat until golden
7. Can also be covered with chocolate

title: Finom Élesztős Kifli (Fine Yeast Croissant)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters flour
- 280 grams butter
- salt
- 3 tablespoons ground sugar
- little vanilla
- 6 egg yolks
- 36 grams melted fresh cheese yeast
- little sour cream

## Directions

1. Shred 280 grams butter into 7 deciliters flour, then smooth it finely and salt it
2. Add 3 tablespoons ground sugar, little vanilla, 6 egg yolks and 36 grams melted fresh cheese yeast and little sour cream
3. Prepare the dough with a wide knife, it should not be very hard
4. Roll out to twice knife-edge thickness, cut out rectangular leaves from it
5. Fill with preserved apricots or currants, fold together and let rise
6. Brush with beaten egg, sprinkle with ground sugar and finely chopped almonds, and bake at gentle heat

title: Finom Új Ostya (Fine New Wafer)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm finely ground sugar with a small stick vanilla
- 21 dekagramm fine flour
- 4 eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 7 dekagramm melted butter
- cream

## Directions

1. Mix well in a bowl 28 dekagramm finely ground sugar with a small stick vanilla, 21 dekagramm fine flour, 4 eggs, and 4 egg yolks
2. Add 7 dekagramm melted butter and enough cream so the dough is light and liquid
3. Mix well again
4. Heat the iron, coat with a little butter, wipe with paper
5. Heat again, place a large teaspoon of dough on the iron
6. Bake until nicely golden-red
7. Remove the iron from the fire at once and either roll into thin tubes or shape into a funnel
8. Repeat until all is baked
9. Keep the fire even and the iron at constant heat

title: Finom élesztős rétes (Fine Yeast Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagramm butter
- six egg yolks
- 2 decziliters sweet sour cream
- one tablespoon yeast dissolved in three and one-half dekagramm sour cream
- two spoonfuls crushed sugar
- salt
- flour
- chopped almond
- citron
- raisins
- currants
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Beat 7 dekagramm butter until fluffy
2. Mix in six egg yolks
3. Add 2 decziliters sweet sour cream, one tablespoon yeast dissolved in three and one-half dekagramm sour cream, two spoonfuls crushed sugar, and salt
4. Mix together
5. Add enough flour to make the dough suitable for pâté
6. Quickly mix it with the rest
7. Let the dough rise in a warm place
8. Stretch it to the thickness of a knife blade
9. Fill it with chopped almond, citron, raisins, and currants
10. Fold it together
11. Place it on a butter-brushed pastry paper
12. Let it rise once more
13. Bake it

title: Finom élesztős tészta (Fine Yeast Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram flour
- 28 dekagrams butter
- salt
- 1 tablespoon ground sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons sour cream
- 14 dekagrams butter
- 3 deciliters sour cream with dissolved yeast

## Directions

1. Cut the butter into the flour and work finely with salt
2. Add the ground sugar, 8 egg yolks, sour cream, butter and yeast dissolved in sour cream
3. Prepare the dough with a wide knife and make rolls or other shapes from it

title: Fish Piece Soup (Halbeles leves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half liters water
- vinegar
- fish pieces, cleaned and cut
- fish roe
- two bay leaves
- thyme
- lemon peel
- one onion cut into four parts
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- black pepper
- pale yellow butter roux
- sour cream
- fried bread
- pike liver fried in butter

## Directions

1. Pour water and a little vinegar into a pot
2. Add the cleaned and cut fish pieces and fish roe
3. Add two bay leaves, thyme, lemon peel, one onion cut into four parts, salt, nutmeg flower and pepper
4. Cook for two hours
5. Prepare a thin pale yellow butter roux and lighten the soup with it
6. Cook for another half hour
7. Add a few tablespoons of sour cream
8. Before serving the soup on fried bread, remove the onion, thyme and bay leaves
9. Pike liver fried in butter can also be added

title: Fogoly Sülve (Roasted Partridge)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge
- salt
- thin bacon slices the size of a palm
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the partridge, salt it
2. Cut thin bacon slices the size of a palm
3. Fold the partridge's wings and legs neatly and prepare it
4. During roasting, baste it frequently with butter
5. Instead of wrapping it in bacon leaves, the partridge can also be larded with finely chopped bacon

title: Fogoly sülve (Roasted Partridge)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge
- salt
- thin bacon slices
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the partridge
2. Salt
3. Cut palm-sized thin bacon slices
4. Fold the wings and feet of the partridges neatly and prepare
5. Baste frequently with butter while roasting

title: Fogoly-szálmi Pástétomkákkal (Partridge Forcemeat with Pâté Croquettes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 partridges, cleaned
- salt
- thin bacon slices
- fried bread
- good brown broth
- half cup red wine
- partridge breasts
- half goose liver
- fine breadcrumbs
- fat
- butter
- green parsley, chopped
- red onion, chopped
- gravy
- small rolls from baker
- egg, beaten
- breadcrumbs
- fat

## Directions

1. Salt three cleaned partridges and wrap them in thin bacon slices, then roast them.
2. Cut the breasts from two partridges; grind the remaining parts together with the third partridge and fried bread in a mortar until it becomes a paste.
3. Put this in a pot, add a ladle of good brown broth and half a cup of red wine, and simmer until cooked.
4. Cut the reserved partridge breasts into pieces and prepare a separate mixture.
5. Cut the breast meat into cubes; similarly prepare half a goose liver rolled in fine breadcrumbs and fried in fat.
6. Melt a little butter, add finely chopped green parsley and a little red onion; add the cut meat and liver with three to four spoonfuls of buttered gravy and reduce until thick.
7. Have the baker make small rolls the size of a coin; scrape the crust, cut a cover the size of a groschen from the top, and remove the inside, moistening with sour cream but not making it too soft.
8. Dip these rolls in beaten, salted egg and breadcrumbs, then fry golden in fat.
9. Arrange the rolls on a sieve to drain.
10. Push the forcemeat through a fine sieve onto the bottom of a dish, arrange the fried rolls in a circle, fill with the prepared mixture, and top with the lids.
11. Work as quickly as possible so the dish does not cool.

title: Fojtott marhahús (Braised Beef)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- hosszúkásra vágott füstölt nyelv
- szalonna
- levesbe való marhahús
- friss szalonna
- vöröshagyma
- sárgarépa
- kevés citromhéja
- néhány egész borso
- fél liter bor
- ugyanannyi víz

## Directions

1. Hosszúkásra vágott füstölt nyelvvel és szalonnával levesbe való marhahúst megtűzdelünk és megsózzuk
2. Lábasba friss szalonnát, vöröshagymát, szeletekre vágott sárgarépát, kevés citromhéjat, néhány egész borsot teszünk
3. Aztán a húst beletéve, fél liter bor és ugyanannyi vízzel megpároljuk
4. Ha megfőtt, a mártást megszűrjük, ráöntjük és így föladjuk

title: Fojtott marhahús (Braised Beef)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef chunks
- Smoked tongue, cut lengthwise
- Bacon
- Salt
- Fresh bacon
- Red onion
- Carrot, sliced
- Lemon zest, small amount
- Whole peppercorns, several
- Marjoram
- Seven deciliters wine
- Small amount vinegar
- Water

## Directions

1. Lard the beef chunks with pieces of smoked tongue and bacon, then salt them
2. Place fresh bacon, red onion, sliced carrot, small amount of lemon zest, several whole peppercorns, and marjoram in a pot
3. Add the beef chunks
4. Pour seven deciliters of wine, a small amount of vinegar, and water over the meat
5. Cover tightly and braise slowly
6. When the meat is tender enough, remove it and skim the fat from the surface
7. Braise the vegetables well
8. Before serving, return the meat to the pot and braise briefly
9. Place on a serving platter, strain part of the sauce through a sieve over the meat, and serve the remaining sauce in a bowl

title: Fokhagymás-torma (Garlic Horseradish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- bread roll, sliced
- garlic cloves
- fatty meat stock
- grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Cook bread sliced into pieces with several cloves of garlic in fatty meat stock until it becomes fine enough to smooth
2. Then mix with grated horseradish and serve

title: Forgács-tészta. Csőrege (Shavings Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Fourteen dekagrams flour
- Seven dekagrams sugar powder
- Spoonful of wine
- Fat for frying
- Sugar for finishing

## Directions

1. Mix flour, sugar powder and a spoonful of wine well on a board
2. Roll out
3. Cut into two-finger-thick pieces with a dumpling cutter
4. Fry in fat
5. Finish with sugar and serve

title: Forrázott csirke más módon (Braised Chicken, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Fine breadcrumbs
- Hot butter

## Directions

1. Prepare and blanch the chickens as in the previous recipe and braise them in vinegar.
2. Place whole on a serving platter.
3. Sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs and pour hot butter over them.

title: Forrázott csuka tormával (Scalded Pike with Horseradish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a beautiful two kilogramme pike
- salt
- white vinegar
- water
- various roots
- lemon peel
- red onion
- whole peppercorns
- grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Salt a beautiful two kilogramme pike and leave it in salt for one hour.
2. You can leave the fish whole or cut it into pieces and then place it in a pan, pour one part white vinegar and three parts water over it, add all kinds of roots, lemon peel, red onion, a few whole peppercorns and cook slowly.
3. When cooked, immediately place it on the serving dish from the broth, sprinkle a good portion of grated horseradish over it, then pour a little of the hot broth in which the fish was cooked over it.
4. Cover with another plate, leave it covered for a while and then serve to the table.

title: Franchipare-hajkenő (Franchipane Hair Pomade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram mutton tallow
- seventy-five dekagrams vegetable oil
- thirteen dekagrams hair powder
- ten dekagrams jasmine oil
- three dekagrams bergamot oil
- two dekagrams cedarwood oil
- four grams clove oil
- two dekagrams musk dye

## Directions

1. Mix one kilogram of mutton tallow with seventy-five dekagrams of vegetable oil, thirteen dekagrams of hair powder, ten dekagrams of jasmine oil, three dekagrams of bergamot oil, two dekagrams of cedarwood oil, four grams of clove oil, and two dekagrams of musk dye well together
2. Dye the mixture to a brownish color

title: Franczia kétszersült (French Twice-Baked Biscuit)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 whole eggs with shells
- 10 spoonfuls of sifted sugar
- flour equal in weight to 7 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Weigh 10 whole eggs with shells and measure the same weight of sifted sugar
2. Measure flour equal in weight to 7 whole eggs
3. Separate the whites from the yolks of the 10 eggs
4. Beat the egg whites into stiff foam
5. Place in a bowl and beat in the egg yolks, then the sugar
6. Stir for half an hour
7. Quickly mix in the measured dry flour, stirring only until well combined
8. Coat the sheet mold with a little butter
9. Pour in the dough
10. Sprinkle the top with sifted sugar
11. Bake in a gentle oven for 10-12 minutes
12. Cut into slices of knife-blade thickness
13. Return to the oven and toast lightly
14. French sponge cake can also be made from this dough

title: Franczia rostélyos (French Roasted Beef)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted beef
- salt
- anchovies
- capers
- red onion
- green parsley
- lemon zest
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove the fat and bones from the roasted beef
2. Beat it well with the flat side of a knife and salt it
3. For one and a half kilograms of roasted beef, take six anchovies, wash them, and remove the bones
4. Add two decagrams of capers, two small heads of red onion, some green parsley, and lemon zest
5. Chop all of this finely
6. Melt a piece of butter, place the chopped mixture in it, and brown it slightly
7. Fill the roasted beef with this filling
8. Fold it together, place it in a pot, and braise until it browns
9. Pour three deciliters of sour cream over it and braise for another half hour
10. Strain off the fat and serve neatly

title: Franczia szelet (French-Style Kidney Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- besózott vesepecsenye
- nyers burgonyaszelet
- vörosha­gyma
- tejföl

## Directions

1. Félig süssük meg és vágjuk szeletekbe a besózott vesepecsenyét
2. Most rakjuk vissza a lábasba, azonban minden szelet közé rakjunk nyers burgonyaszeleteket és vörosha­gymát
3. Pároljuk barnára
4. Ha megpuhúlt, öntsünk rá tejfölt és forraljuk föl azzal
5. Levével és a burgonyával együtt tálaljuk föl

title: Franczia Tej Kocsonya (French Cream Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagramm crushed sugar
- vanilla
- 10 egg yolks
- 7 deciliter boiled and cooled heavy cream
- 7 deciliter best quality cream
- 2 tablespoons rum
- 2 dekagramm dissolved isinglass
- almond oil

## Directions

1. Whisk together 21 dekagramm crushed sugar with vanilla, 10 egg yolks, and 7 deciliter of boiled and cooled heavy cream in a glazed pot
2. Place over charcoal fire and cook with continuous stirring until it reaches the consistency of thick pancake batter
3. Pour into another vessel and continue stirring to prevent it from breaking
4. Cool completely
5. Whip 7 deciliter of best quality cream and fold in vanilla sugar
6. Mix in 2 tablespoons rum and 2 dekagramm dissolved isinglass
7. Combine with the cooled cream mixture
8. Pour into a mold greased with fine almond oil
9. Freeze on crushed ice

title: Franczia vajas metélt (French Butter Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- six decilitres of fine flour
- seven dekagrams of butter cut into thin slices
- salt

## Directions

1. Work six decilitres of fine flour well with seven dekagrams of butter cut into thin slices on a board with a rolling pin, salt it

title: Franczia ívek (French Wafers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 21 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- ½ lemon, juice
- 21 dekagrams soaked almond, finely cut lengthwise
- 2 dekagrams candied orange peel
- ½ lemon, zest
- 2 dekagrams candied citron, finely chopped
- wafer sheets
- butter

## Directions

1. Place the stiffly beaten whites of 3 eggs in a bowl with 21 dekagrams of finely ground sugar
2. Slowly press in the juice of ½ lemon
3. Stir until thick and beautifully white
4. Mix in 21 dekagrams of soaked almond, finely cut lengthwise, 2 dekagrams of candied orange peel, the zest of ½ lemon and 2 dekagrams of candied citron, all similarly finely chopped
5. Spread this mixture evenly on wafer sheets two fingers wide and a span long
6. Place on a wafer baking pan previously buttered
7. Bake at gentle heat

title: Franczia-pép (French Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciiliters milk
- common pancake batter
- lard for frying
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 deciiliters cream
- small amount of powdered vanilla

## Directions

1. Make ordinary pancake batter from 4 deciiliters of milk
2. Pass the batter through a skimming spoon with large holes into hot lard and fry the peas until golden
3. Remove the fried peas and continue until all the batter is fried
4. In a small pot, mix 14 dekagrams sugar with 8 egg yolks and add 8 deciiliters of cream and a small amount of powdered vanilla
5. Moisten the fried peas with milk and place in a buttered mold
6. Pour the cream and egg mixture over them
7. Cook in a steam bath for half an hour
8. Turn out onto a plate and serve

title: Franezia leveles torta (French Puff Pastry Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- five-times folded puff pastry made from approximately eighty dekagrams of butter
- cooked apricots for filling
- cooked currants
- four egg whites
- fine sugar
- pistachios, finely chopped
- coarse sugar
- egg wash
- cooked whole cherries for decoration

## Directions

1. Prepare five-times folded puff pastry from approximately eighty dekagrams of butter
2. Roll the pastry in three layers to the thickness of two knife-backs
3. Cut out two completely equal round leaves with a warm knife, sized as a 38 dekagram pastry leaf
4. Cut out a large circle from the center with a large doughnut cutter, leaving only a hand-width-sized smooth edge
5. Cut out two more completely identical leaves and cut them the same way, continuing until eight pairs are made, with each pair being about half a small finger-width smaller than the other
6. Brush every leaf with beaten egg, prick with a knife point, dust with finely ground sugar, and bake in a slightly cooled oven
7. Once baked, coat the largest leaf with cooked apricots to a knife-back thickness and place its pair on top
8. Fill the second pair with apricots and continue until all eight pairs are filled
9. Coat the edges of the joined leaves with foamy icing made from four egg whites to a knife-back thickness and roll over finely chopped pistachios on rice paper so the entire edge appears green with powder
10. Coat the second leaf similarly and roll it in coarsely ground sugar, the third again in pistachios, and so on until all eight pairs are powdered
11. Return to a cooled oven only until the foamy icing is dried, with the sugar remaining completely white and the pistachios a nice green
12. Once cooled, place the first two joined leaves on the intended plate, coat with cooked apricots, place the second double leaf on top so it occupies exactly the center of the first, coat its surface again with apricots and place the third leaf on top, continuing until all eight are stacked on top of each other
13. Decorate the final leaf with cooked whole cherries

title: Franezia leves (French Vegetable Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- firm head cabbage
- kohlrabi
- carrot
- equal parts of each
- four or five button mushrooms, thinly sliced
- butter or goose fat
- nutmeg
- pepper
- meat broth
- toasted bread, sliced lengthwise

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage, kohlrabi, and carrot into equal parts
2. Add the thinly sliced mushrooms
3. Heat butter or goose fat in a pot and add the cut vegetables
4. Braise for about half an hour
5. Add a little nutmeg and pepper
6. Pour meat broth over the vegetables
7. Cook until the vegetables are tender
8. Serve on toasted, lengthwise-sliced bread

title: French Buttered Noodles (Franczia vajas metélt)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 deciliters fine flour
- 7 dekagrams butter, thinly sliced
- salt
- 4 eggs
- 1 spoonful sour cream
- a little wine
- 7 dekagrams butter, beaten until fluffy
- 10 dekagrams sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Work the flour and butter slices together well on a board with a rolling pin
2. Season with salt
3. Knead with 4 eggs, a spoonful of sour cream, and a little wine
4. Work the not overly firm dough well and prepare dropped noodles from it
5. Cook these in sour cream and cool
6. Beat 7 dekagrams of butter until fluffy, then add 4 whole eggs one after another and beat each in well
7. Add the noodles with 10 dekagrams of sugar and vanilla, mix well
8. Place in a buttered baking dish and cook in a water bath

title: Friss cseresnyés torta (Fresh Cherry Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- nine egg yolks
- powdered sugar
- lemon
- quarter kilogram of blanched almonds
- whole lemon zest
- ground cinnamon
- ground cloves
- nine egg whites
- two crispy rolls
- butter
- flour
- one liter of black cherries

## Directions

1. Beat nine egg yolks with nine spoonfuls of powdered sugar and lemon juice until it thickens and becomes foamy
2. Finely chop quarter kilogram of blanched almonds and divide in half
3. Mix half into the egg yolk mixture
4. Add the grated zest of one whole lemon, one teaspoon of ground cinnamon, and a knife-point of ground cloves
5. Whip nine egg whites into stiff peaks
6. Add the sifted crumbs of two crispy rolls and fold in the meringue
7. Pour into a buttered and floured baking pan
8. Bake in moderate heat in the oven
9. Pit one liter of black cherries
10. When the cake has formed a crust on top, sprinkle the cherries on it and return to bake
11. The heavy cherries will sink partway into the soft dough
12. When the cake is done, whip four to six egg whites into stiff peaks
13. Sweeten well with sugar and coat the top of the cake with it, one finger thickness
14. Sprinkle the remaining ground almonds on top
15. When the meringue has turned golden, place on a sieve so the bottom does not soften

title: Friss vagy befőtt meggyből készült pép (Fresh or Preserved Cherry Pap)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 litres cherries
- 2 crumbly rolls, grated
- 11 dekagrams swollen, finely ground almonds
- zest of half a lemon
- sugar
- 7 dekagrams butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Wash the cherries, remove stems and pits, and chop coarsely
2. Moisten grated crumbly rolls with the cherry liquid
3. Add the finely ground almonds, minced lemon zest, and sugar to taste
4. Cream 7 dekagrams of butter and beat in 6 well-whisked egg yolks
5. Add the cherry mixture, almonds, and bread
6. Stir for a further quarter hour
7. Gently fold in the stiff foam of 4 egg whites
8. Put the mixture into a buttered mould and bake on a steady heat

title: Frog Soup (Békaleves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of butter
- finely chopped green parsley
- frogs
- flour
- pea broth
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- sour cream
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Melt butter and add finely chopped green parsley, braising slightly
2. Clean the frogs thoroughly and braise slightly in butter
3. Sprinkle a tablespoon or two of flour over them and braise slightly
4. Lighten with pea broth and add a little salt and nutmeg flower
5. When the soup is cooked and the frogs are tender, add two tablespoons of sour cream
6. Serve on fried bread

title: Fáczán Angol Módra (Pheasant English Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one fine pheasant
- lard
- ham
- lemon peel
- butter
- ham
- mushrooms, finely chopped
- flour
- brown stock
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Bone the pheasant while keeping it whole, stuff it, and restore it to its original shape.
2. Wrap it in paper with lard, ham, and lemon peel, and place on a buttered pot lid.
3. Put it in the oven.
4. To prepare the sauce: warm a small amount of butter in a pot, add ham and finely chopped mushrooms, sprinkle in a small amount of flour, and pour brown stock over it.
5. Simmer briefly, skim off the fat, and add lemon juice to make it tart.
6. Pour this sauce under the pheasant when serving.

title: Fánk Mandola-Tejből (Fritter with Almond Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 210 grams blanched almonds, soaked
- 5 deciliters water
- sugar to taste
- 3 tablespoons fine flour
- 1 egg for brushing
- breadcrumbs
- 4 deciliters milk
- 4 tablespoons fine flour
- 4 grated tablets chocolate
- 100 grams sugar

## Directions

1. Grind the soaked almonds fine and place in a soup bowl
2. Pour 5 deciliters water over it and let stand for an hour with frequent stirring
3. Wring out in a cloth and pound the almonds again, pour the almond milk over it, mix well and strain a second time for a strong almond milk
4. Mix in enough sugar so the taste is distinctly sweet
5. In a pot slowly mix 3 tablespoons fine flour with the almond milk, cook to a smooth paste with continuous stirring
6. When well cooked, spread on buttered sheet metal to finger thickness, cool and carefully turn onto a flour-dusted board
7. Cut out in crescent shapes with a flour-dusted cutter, brush with beaten egg and coat with fine breadcrumbs
8. Fry in fat to a beautiful golden color and dust with powdered sugar
9. For a more elegant presentation, make the following fritter to combine with it: in a pot set over low heat, slowly mix 4 deciliters milk with 4 tablespoons fine flour, 4 grated tablets chocolate and 100 grams sugar with continuous stirring
10. Cook until thick and smooth, then cool
11. Spread the paste evenly on buttered parchment with a knife moistened in water, cool completely, heat the parchment for a moment, then carefully turn onto a flour-dusted board and cut into crescents
12. Brush with egg and coat half with flour and half with breadcrumbs
13. Fry in hot fat and dust densely with finely grated chocolate
14. Arrange the white almond milk fritters and chocolate fritters alternately on a platter and serve

title: Fánk Tej Kocsonyával (Fritter with Milk Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablespoons fine flour
- 7 deciliters sour cream
- 8 egg yolks
- 140 grams granulated sugar
- vanilla
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- 1 egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Mix flour finely with sour cream, egg yolks, sugar, and vanilla
2. Cook to a smooth paste with continuous stirring, cool slightly
3. Spread thinly and evenly on buttered parchment paper using a wide knife moistened in water to the thickness of a little finger and cool
4. Warm the parchment slightly and carefully turn the milk jelly onto a flour-dusted board
5. Cut out coin-sized fritters with a cutter
6. Coat densely with fine breadcrumbs, flip with a wide knife and coat the other side with egg and breadcrumbs
7. Note that these fritters must not be touched by hand as they are very delicate and crumbs would be pressed into the milk jelly, causing loss of quality and appearance
8. Fry in hot fat to a beautiful golden color, drain the fat, dust with vanilla sugar and serve

title: Földieper-fagylalt (Strawberry Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 liters ripe strawberries
- juice of 2 lemons
- 30 dekagrams sugar
- 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Clean and press 1.5 liters of ripe strawberries through a sieve
2. Press in the juice of 2 lemons
3. Cook 30 dekagrams of sugar with 1 glass of water until it forms heavy drops
4. Combine with the strawberry mixture
5. Cool and pour into the freezing container

title: Földieper-hab (Strawberry Foam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters ripe strawberries, strained through a sieve
- 21 dekagrams broken sugar
- 1 liter good heavy cream
- 1.5 dekagrams dissolved and cooled isinglass

## Directions

1. Mix the strained strawberries with the broken sugar
2. Whip one liter of good heavy cream into foam
3. Mix the foam with the strained strawberries
4. Mix in the dissolved and cooled isinglass
5. Pour into a mold and freeze on ice
6. Before serving, turn out and decorate with a wreath of spun sugar
7. This foam can also be used for filling Spanish wind cakes

title: Földieper-Kocsonya Vegyített Eperfélékkel Berakva (Strawberry Jelly with Mixed Berries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagramm sugar
- 7 deciliter water
- 1.5 liter ripe cleaned strawberries
- 2 lemons
- alkormös for coloring if needed
- 4.5 dekagramm dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Cook 42 dekagramm sugar with 7 deciliter water until the sugar begins to form heavy drops
2. Add 1.5 liter of well-ripened cleaned strawberries and boil together with the sugar
3. Pass through a sieve and let drip
4. When cooled, add the juice of 2 lemons
5. If the color is not a beautiful pink, color with alkormös
6. Note that this jelly should be pale pink
7. Dissolve 4.5 dekagramm isinglass and mix in
8. Pass through stretched cloth and filter until completely clear, repeating if necessary
9. While this happens, bury a flat round mold in ice with a small round mold in the center, held down with a stone
10. Pour the jelly into the ring formed around the small mold and freeze for about one hour
11. When the jelly has set, pour hot water carefully into the small mold to remove the jelly
12. Once removed, fill the empty space with about 30 pineapple strawberries, the same number of beautiful wild strawberries, raspberries, and white and red currants that have been cleaned and sugared
13. Pour the reserved jelly over the fruit and return to ice to freeze
14. Unmold using the usual method

title: Földieper-Marmelád (Strawberry Jam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh, ripe wild strawberries
- sugar, half kilogram of sugar per half kilogram of strawberries
- water, two deciliters

## Directions

1. Select beautiful, ripe wild strawberries, press them through a sieve.
2. For half a kilogram of strawberries, use the same amount of sugar, which you cook in two deciliters of water until it threads.
3. Then add the pressed strawberries and stir constantly until it comes to a boil several times.
4. It is particularly important to note that it must not cook longer, as otherwise it will lose its beautiful color and acquire a bad taste; then when cooled, pour into glasses.

title: Földieper-pép (Strawberry Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters fresh strawberries
- 21 dekagramms sugar
- 3 tablespoons water
- juice of half a lemon
- sugar to taste
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Clean the strawberries and press through a sieve
2. Cook the sugar with 3 tablespoons water until sticky
3. Add the pressed strawberries and cook until thick
4. Place in a bowl and cool
5. Squeeze in the lemon juice and add sugar to taste
6. Stir for half an hour
7. While stirring, fold in the stiff peaks of 6 egg whites gradually
8. Quickly pile high on a butter-greased plate
9. Bake in a completely cooled oven for half an hour
10. Serve quickly to the table as it easily collapses

title: Földieper-szeletek (Strawberry Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cake dough
- butter
- 2 dekiliters ripe and stemmed fine sifted red strawberries
- finely ground and sifted sugar
- alkermes liqueur

## Directions

1. Prepare sponge cake dough, spread on a buttered baking sheet to twice knife-edge thickness, and bake at gentle heat
2. Cool, then cut into long slices or boat shapes
3. Fill with compote and place another slice on top
4. Prepare icing by beating ripe, stemmed, finely sifted red strawberries with finely ground and sifted sugar by the spoonful to prevent it from being too thin
5. Cover the slices or boats evenly with this icing
6. If the color is not attractive, add one or two drops of alkermes liqueur, but it should be at most pale pink
7. Dry in a cooled oven
8. These slices can also be covered with raspberry or strawberry compote, using raspberry or strawberry juice instead of lemon juice or rum

title: Fü-eczet (Herb Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tarragon vinegar
- basil vinegar
- bee balm vinegar
- rosemary vinegar
- lavender flowers
- several pieces of dried lemon and orange peel
- cinnamon
- nutmeg flower
- long pepper
- strong wine vinegar

## Directions

1. For this, take tarragon, basil, bee balm, and rosemary vinegars and lavender flowers, chop them coarsely together with several pieces of dried lemon and orange peel, and place all of this with a little cinnamon, nutmeg flower, and long pepper in a bottle, pour strong wine vinegar over it.
2. Bind the bottle with simple paper, perforate it, and leave the vinegar to absorb in the sun for 14 days.
3. Then filter through blotting paper and put it into small, well-stoppered bottles.
4. This vinegar is very good for minced meat dishes and sour sauces.

title: Függelék az Agyvelő-törthez (Accompaniment to Brain Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- brain paste
- butter
- pastry cutter

## Directions

1. Spread the brain paste prepared in the above manner onto a buttered baking sheet to the thickness of a little finger
2. Bake until nicely golden brown
3. Cut out with a pastry cutter in the size of a coin and use to surround the meat hash
4. Heap the meat hash in the center of the dish and enclose it with a crown made from the cut-out pieces

title: Fürj Rizszsel (Quail with Rice)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- quails, cleaned
- bacon slices
- piece of veal
- green parsley
- onion
- salt
- half glass white wine
- good broth

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned quails in a pot.
2. Add a few slices of bacon, a piece of veal, some green parsley, an onion, a little salt, half a glass of white wine, and several spoonfuls of good broth.
3. Braise over low heat.
4. Remove the quails and strain the sauce of fat.
5. Place the quails in the center of a dish, pour the sauce over them, and close in a rice border as described in recipe 119.

title: Fürj Sülve (Roasted Quail)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- salt
- 7 deciliters wine
- 7 deciliters vinegar
- cloves
- butter for basting

## Directions

1. Clean and pluck the quail well, rinse it thoroughly, salt it, and let it lie in salt for a while
2. Pour 7 deciliters of wine and the same amount of vinegar over it, add a few cloves, and let it marinate in the brine overnight
3. Roast it slowly and carefully
4. A small sauce can be made from the brine if desired

title: Fürj sülve (Roasted Quail)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- salt
- 7 decilitres wine
- 7 decilitres vinegar
- cloves
- brine

## Directions

1. Pluck the quail well and wash thoroughly
2. Salt and let sit in salt for a time
3. Pour 7 decilitres wine and 7 decilitres vinegar over it
4. Add cloves
5. Place in brine overnight
6. Roast slowly
7. A light sauce can be made from the brine

title: Főtt-torta (Boiled Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm sugar
- coffee cup of water
- 28 dekagramm softened almonds
- zest of half a lemon, finely chopped
- juice of half a lemon
- 1 egg white, stiffly beaten
- butter for greasing
- wafer
- candied fruit

## Directions

1. Cook sugar with coffee cup of water in a pan until it threads
2. Grind the softened almonds, finely chop the lemon zest
3. Add the almonds, lemon zest, lemon juice, and stiffly beaten egg white to the sugar and mix well
4. Cook until it becomes a solid mass
5. Spread half of the mass on a pyramid-shaped mold greased with butter and lined with wafer and bake with gentle heat
6. Form the other half into wreaths, bake similarly
7. When both are removed from the oven, glaze the wreaths in a nice order onto the cake with fondu sugar
8. Place candied plum in each wreath and decorate the cake with icing

title: Főzött dararétes (Boiled Semolina Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagramm flour
- one egg
- lukewarm water
- 7 dekagramm butter
- two whole eggs and three egg yolks
- four decziliters sour cream
- three decziliters semolina
- flour for dusting

## Directions

1. From twenty-eight dekagramm flour, one egg, and a little lukewarm water, prepare firm strudel dough
2. While it rests, beat 7 dekagramm butter until fluffy
3. Add two whole eggs and three egg yolks to it and mix well
4. Once finely mixed, add four decziliters sour cream and three decziliters semolina
5. Stretch the dough very thinly
6. Brush the filling halfway across it and fold it together
7. Using a wooden spoon handle dusted with flour, make indentations at equal distances and cut with a knife in two
8. Press the ends together well with your fingers
9. Boil the strudel pieces in hot water, but only until they float through, otherwise they will fall apart
10. Transfer to a plate and sprinkle each piece with finely chopped bread rolls fried in butter

title: Fűszer-torta (Spice Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagramm sugar
- 42 dekagramm almonds with skin, finely ground
- zest of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- zest of half an orange, finely chopped
- 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
- 2-3 cloves
- egg white
- butter or wax for greasing
- powdered sugar
- candied citrus peel

## Directions

1. Mix together the finely ground sugar and almonds with skin, finely chopped lemon and orange zest, ground cinnamon with cloves
2. On a board, knead the mixture with egg white using a large knife into a firm dough, being careful not to make it too soft or liquid
3. Sprinkle the board and dough with finely ground sugar and roll out to the thickness of a finger
4. Cut three equal sheets from it and place on a baking sheet greased with butter or wax
5. On one of the sheets before baking, press a lattice from Spanish puff paste using a pastry bag, as is customary for Linz torte
6. The dough for this must be quite firm and should be mixed with sugar and vanilla just as for puff cake dough
7. Around the edge of the sheet press small Spanish puff pastries so that the lattice perimeter forms a wreath shape
8. When the sheets have dried and the lattice has hardened, fill each with different preserves and stack them
9. Place the lattice on top, sprinkle with sugar, and arrange with preserved fruit
10. Bake slowly in a cooled oven until dry

title: Fűszeres rudacskák (Spiced Little Sticks)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- dough (as per recipe 685)
- white frosting
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Prepare dough as in recipe 685
2. Roll it out to the thickness of a knife blade
3. Cover with white frosting
4. Cut into sticks one finger long and wide
5. Decorate with pistachios
6. Place on a baking sheet coated with wax
7. Dry at gentle heat so the frosting does not lose its color but remains beautifully white

title: Fűszeres ívek (Spiced Wafers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams almond with skin, finely ground
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- good amount of cinnamon
- cloves
- lemon zest, finely chopped
- egg white
- wafer sheets
- butter
- icing

## Directions

1. Mix in a bowl: 14 dekagrams of almond with skin finely ground, the same amount of sugar, good amount of cinnamon and cloves, finely chopped lemon zest, beaten foamy with egg white (the batter should not be as thin as common beaten batter)
2. Spread on wafer sheets two fingers wide and a span long, to knife-thickness
3. Place on a buttered pan
4. Bake at gentle heat, or rather dry it
5. When completely hardened, spread part of stiffly beaten icing through a cone with a pinhead opening or paper cone in a nice pattern on the wafers
6. Mix the other part with a little food coloring and decorate the wafers nicely with it
7. Return to the oven where it dries completely

title: Galamb béliéivé (Stuffed Pigeon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a pigeon
- a little marrow
- finely chopped parsley
- lemon zest
- bread soaked in milk
- two eggs
- two whole eggs
- a full tablespoon sour cream
- several shelled and diced crayfish tails
- bacon
- red onion
- carrots
- oil-brushed paper
- flour
- good broth

## Directions

1. After cleaning and soaking the pigeon in water, cut it open along the back and remove the meat all the way to the head, legs and wings
2. Finely chop this meat together with a little marrow, finely chopped parsley, lemon zest, bread soaked in milk, and a mixture made from two eggs
3. Place in a bowl, then add two whole eggs, a full tablespoon of sour cream, and several shelled crayfish tails diced into cubes
4. Stuff the pigeons with this mixture and sew them shut so they regain their former shape
5. Place bacon, red onion, and carrots cut into slices in a pot, place the pigeons on top
6. Cover with oil-brushed paper and a lid and braise well, frequently pouring a little broth over them so the pigeons do not dry out
7. Remove the pigeons and braise the vegetables left in the pot until nicely brown
8. Sprinkle a little flour over them, dilute with good broth and cook down
9. Strain the sauce through a sieve, place the pigeons back in and serve on the table

title: Galamb Papírtekercsben (Squab in Paper Parcels)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-3 squabs
- salt
- parsley
- onion
- button mushrooms
- squab liver
- pepper
- anchovies
- bacon
- white paper
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean 2-3 squabs and split each lengthwise into two pieces
2. Season with salt and soften with a knife to make them slightly wider
3. Chop together parsley, onion, button mushrooms, squab liver, pepper, and anchovies for the stuffing
4. Slice bacon thinly
5. Place squab halves on white paper
6. Spread the inside of each squab piece with the stuffing
7. Continue this process until all squab pieces and stuffing are used
8. Cover the meat with additional bacon slices
9. Wrap in paper and fold the ends neatly
10. Make as many parcels as there are squab halves
11. Place on a grill, brush with butter, and roast slowly
12. Serve in the paper on the table

title: Galamb páezolva (Pickled Pigeon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- salt
- pickle
- finely chopped bacon
- sour cream
- two deciliters sour cream
- one tablespoon flour
- pickle
- pigeon drippings
- oblong-cut lemon zest

## Directions

1. After cleanly plucking the pigeons, season them well with salt
2. Prepare a good pickle and leave them in it for one day
3. Lard with finely chopped bacon
4. Roast slowly, basting several times with the pickle and sour cream as well
5. Prepare the sauce from two deciliters of sour cream, beating it with one tablespoon flour
6. Pour the pickle over it, as well as what dripped from the pigeon
7. Also add oblong-cut lemon zest
8. Heat slightly and serve the pigeons with the sauce poured over

title: Gelée Almával (Apple Gelée)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pippin apples
- 56 decagrams sugar
- 3 lemons, juice
- 6 decagrams isinglass
- 1 bottle white wine
- 1 lemon, zest

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and cook them with 14 decagrams sugar, one lemon zest, and a little wine or water, like compote, but so the apples do not fall apart
2. From the remaining sugar, lemon juice, lukewarm isinglass, and white wine, prepare a gelée
3. Layer this with the apples into a mold according to the method described in recipe 901
4. Preserved and well-drained fruit can be placed between layers

title: Gelée fehér borból (White Wine Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42-56 dekagramm sugar
- 1 bottle white wine
- 4 lemons
- 6 dekagramm isinglass or equivalent amount of aspic made from pork skin, gelatin, deer antlers, or calf's feet

## Directions

1. Pour 4 deciliter water over the sugar cut into small pieces
2. Once dissolved, bring to a boil
3. Squeeze the juice from the lemons into it and let it simmer briefly over the fire, skimming off the foam as cleanly as possible
4. Once cooled, combine in a porcelain or earthenware vessel with the aspic, which should be only lukewarm
5. Add the finely peeled zest from one lemon and the white wine, adding the latter very carefully
6. Let stand covered for half an hour, then strain through a cloth that has been rinsed in warm water
7. Test the firmness by the method described above, then pour into the mold and freeze on ice or in cold water
8. At serving time, hold the mold briefly in warm water, dry it, and turn the jelly out onto a dish

title: Gelée Földieperből (Strawberry Gelée)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilogram sugar
- 1.25 liters wild or garden strawberries
- 6 decagrams isinglass
- ½ bottle white wine or champagne
- 2 lemons, juice strained

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in 3 deciliters of water
2. Add the washed strawberries
3. Cover in a porcelain bowl and let stand for several hours
4. Strain the sugar through a cloth
5. Mix the lukewarm isinglass, wine, and strained lemon juice together
6. Layer the gelée with the fine garden strawberries (which have been washed and marinated with sugar) into a mold according to the method described in recipe 901, alternating layers
7. In winter, preserved strawberry syrup can be used instead of fresh strawberries, but use less sugar since the syrup is already sweetened with sugar

title: Gelée földieperből (Strawberry Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogramm sugar
- 1.25 liters wild or garden strawberries
- 6 dekagramm isinglass
- 0.5 bottle white wine or champagne
- 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in 3 deciliter water
2. Add the washed strawberries to it
3. Let stand in a porcelain bowl covered for several hours
4. Strain the sugar through a cloth and combine with the lukewarm aspic, wine and the strained juice from 2 lemons
5. Use beautiful garden strawberries that have been washed

title: Gelée likőrből (Liqueur Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- as described in recipe 897 with modifications: either half a bottle of wine with the same amount of liqueur replacing the missing wine, or an equivalent amount of water with half a bottle of liqueur instead of wine
- suitable liqueurs including maraschino, cherry water, crème de noyaux, parfait d'amour, vanilla or curaçao

## Directions

1. If using the first three liqueurs, the jelly remains white
2. If using the last three liqueurs, color the jelly with cochineal or other red coloring

title: Gelée madeirái vaniliával (Madeira Jelly with Vanilla)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42-56 dekagramm sugar
- 1 stick vanilla
- juice of 4 lemons
- 0.5 bottle Madeira wine
- 0.5 bottle white wine
- 6 dekagramm isinglass

## Directions

1. Once the sugar is dissolved, cook it with the vanilla
2. Let it simmer covered by the fire for half an hour
3. Once cooled, combine with the lukewarm aspic, the strained lemon juice, Madeira and white wine
4. Strain the entire mixture through a cloth
5. After testing the firmness, pour into the mold and freeze

title: Gelée narancsból (Orange Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 8-12 oranges
- 0.5 kilogramm sugar
- 2 lemons
- 0.75 bottle white wine
- 6 dekagramm isinglass

## Directions

1. Place the finely chopped sugar in a bowl, add the finely peeled zest from one orange, and let stand covered until the sugar is dissolved
2. Meanwhile, squeeze the juice from the oranges and lemons and strain together as described above
3. Combine the strained liquid with the lukewarm aspic, wine and sugar
4. Color the jelly red with cochineal or other coloring
5. Strain through a cloth, test for firmness, pour into the mold and freeze
6. To garnish with orange shells: hollow out the orange skins and scrape off the inner white side well, beat some of the orange jelly until white and foamy, fill the shells with this, once frozen cut into quarters and use to surround the jelly
7. Alternatively, segment the oranges like a compote, peel and remove the seeds, then marinate with sugar, pour a little orange jelly into the mold, freeze it, arrange a layer of orange segments on top, then jelly, continuing in layers until the mold is full

title: Gelée pezsgőből (Champagne Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagramm sugar
- 1 bottle champagne
- juice of 4 lemons
- 6 dekagramm isinglass or equivalent amount of aspic

## Directions

1. Combine the cooled sugar with the lukewarm aspic, strained lemon juice and champagne
2. Strain through a cloth
3. After testing the firmness of the jelly, place one quarter of the mixture in a porcelain vessel and set it on ice
4. Beat the remaining jelly with a whisk until it becomes white, foamy and begins to thicken
5. Fill the mold one quarter full with this beaten mixture
6. Once the beaten mass has almost completely frozen, pour the remaining strained jelly over it and completely freeze the whole on ice
7. To imitate champagne bubbles, combine the sugar with lemon juice, aspic and one quarter of the champagne and stir on ice until it begins to thicken, then slowly pour in the remaining champagne by spoonfuls, creating foam bubbles in the jelly, then pour into the mold and freeze on ice

title: Gelée Sárga Baraczкból (Yellow Apricot Gelée)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 yellow apricots
- 56 decagrams sugar
- 6 decagrams isinglass
- 3 lemons, juice
- ½ bottle white wine

## Directions

1. Proceed according to the method for autumn apricot gelée

title: Gelée Őszi Baraczкból (Autumn Apricot Gelée)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 apricots
- 56 decagrams sugar
- 6 decagrams isinglass or clarified gelatin
- ½ bottle white wine
- 3 lemons, juice strained
- 4 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in 4 deciliters of water
2. Add the peeled and halved apricots along with the pits
3. Let cook, skimming off the foam
4. Cover and let cool
5. Strain through a sieve, filtering the sugar through a cloth
6. Mix the lukewarm isinglass, strained juice of 3 lemons, and wine together
7. Layer the gelée with the apricots into a mold according to the method described above

title: Genois (Genoese Sponge)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramm butter
- 14 dekagramm broken and sifted sugar
- orange
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- 14 dekagramm fine dry flour
- apricot marmalade
- white frosting
- lemon juice
- redcurrant or raspberry preserves
- sugar

## Directions

1. Melt 14 dekagramm butter, remove the sediment and set aside to clarify completely but remain liquid, not solidify
2. Rub 14 dekagramm broken and sifted sugar on an orange, then beat in a bowl with 4 egg yolks for a quarter hour until foamy
3. Make stiff foam from 4 egg whites
4. At almost the same time, mix in 14 dekagramm fine dry flour with the mixture
5. Then quickly stir in the butter sediment only until well combined with the rest
6. Coat a cake tin with its ring with butter
7. Spread the mixture onto it in two knife-blade thicknesses smoothly
8. Bake at gentle heat
9. When baked and cooled, either cut with a two-forint-sized cutter or cut large boats from it
10. Coat with apricot marmalade
11. Using a small paper cone with an opening no larger than a pin head, pipe small dots of beautiful white frosting around the edge in an orderly pattern, leaving space for another dot between every two dots
12. Fill another cone with redcurrant or raspberry preserves that have been pushed through and cooked with sugar, which should be beautifully colored, and pipe red dots from the preserves between the white dots
13. If cutting the dough into boats, decorate the same way and arrange on a plate in a large star shape

title: Gesztenye-fagylalt (Chestnut Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 70 dekagrams Italian chestnuts
- 21 dekagrams sugar
- 2 deciliters water
- 3 tablespoons good rum
- 4 deciliters good cream
- finely crushed and well-salted ice

## Directions

1. Cook 70 dekagrams of Italian chestnuts until soft, peel them, and press through a sieve
2. Cook 21 dekagrams of sugar with 2 deciliters of water until it forms large bubbles while boiling and becomes thick
3. Finely beat the pressed chestnuts in a bowl with the cooked sugar
4. Add 3 tablespoons of good rum and mix well, making sure the mixture is very cold
5. Finally, gently fold in the stiffly beaten whipped cream of 4 deciliters of good cream
6. Immediately pour into the freezing container and place in finely crushed, well-salted ice
7. Do not turn, but let it freeze
8. During freezing, occasionally insert a stick around the edges of the container to mix the frozen mixture with the still-liquid part until the entire mass is frozen
9. Turn out, wrap with spun sugar columns, and place dipped chestnuts in the center

title: Gesztenye-pép (Chestnut Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Half kilogram Italian chestnuts
- Two deciliters good cream
- Eight dekagrams sweet almonds
- Twenty-one dekagrams sifted sugar
- Several spoonfuls cold cream
- Five dekagrams butter
- Five egg yolks
- Three egg whites
- Eight to ten roasted chestnuts
- Ten dekagrams sugar
- Half coffee cup water
- One lemon

## Directions

1. Roast the Italian chestnuts until soft without browning
2. Peel them and while warm grind until resembling dough
3. Put in a pan and mix smooth with two deciliters good cream
4. Boil with constant stirring
5. Clean eight dekagrams sweet almonds and grind as finely as the chestnuts
6. Press the chestnuts through a sieve and put in a pan with the almonds, twenty-one dekagrams sifted sugar, and several spoonfuls cold cream
7. Stew with constant stirring into a thick paste
8. Remove from heat and while warm mix in five dekagrams butter and cool
9. Gently mix in five egg yolks and stiffly beaten whites of three eggs
10. Butter the mold
11. Roast eight to ten chestnuts, peel them, and cut in half
12. Boil ten dekagrams sugar with half a coffee cup water until thick with large bubbles forming
13. Dip the chestnuts in this and place on marble, and when cool cut them in half
14. Line the mold with these so the sugar-coated side touches the mold side
15. Fill with the paste
16. Squeeze a lemon, strain, and boil with fourteen dekagrams sugar until large bubbles form
17. Cool, and when jelly-like, turn out the paste and coat with this glaze

title: Gesztenye-torta más módon (Chestnut Cake, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 large beautiful roasted chestnuts
- 21 dekagramm butter, beaten to foam
- 21 dekagramm ground sugar
- 14 dekagramm finely ground almonds
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Peel 15 large beautiful roasted chestnuts and chop finely
2. Add to the foamed butter with ground sugar, chestnuts, and finely ground almonds
3. Stir together and fill onto a cake sheet greased with butter
4. Bake slowly

title: Gesztenyetopta (Chestnut Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half kilogram of chestnuts
- fifty-six dekagrams of powdered sugar
- seven egg yolks
- one bar of grated chocolate
- seven egg whites
- butter for the mold
- jam or whipped cream for filling
- rum sugar icing for coating

## Directions

1. Peel the chestnuts and cook them until soft.
2. Strain them and press through a sieve.
3. Mix the pressed chestnut thoroughly with fifty-six dekagrams of powdered sugar, seven egg yolks.
4. Add one bar of grated chocolate and seven egg whites beaten into foam.
5. Bake in a buttered mold.
6. When baked, cut the cake with a sharp knife into six to ten slices.
7. Spread jam or whipped cream between the individual cake layers.
8. Cover the finished cake with rum sugar icing.

title: Grated Dough Filled with Sweet (Reszelt tészta édesen töltve)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- milk
- 7 dekagrams butter, beaten fluffy
- 7 dekagrams finely ground almonds
- 7 egg yolks
- 10 dekagrams sugar
- lemon or orange zest
- 4 egg whites
- preserved currants
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Make grated dough from 2 eggs and cook it in milk
2. When thick and well cooked, remove from heat and cool
3. Into 7 dekagrams of fluffy beaten butter add the same amount of finely ground almonds and 7 egg yolks, as well as 10 dekagrams of sugar rubbed with lemon or orange zest
4. Stir for half an hour
5. Add the cooled grated dough and finally the stiff snow from 4 egg whites
6. Pour half the mixture into a buttered baking dish
7. Fill with preserved currants
8. Pour the other half of the mixture over it
9. Bake nicely and serve dusted with sugar

title: Green Pea Soup (Zöldborsó-leves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tender green peas with pods
- butter
- a few fried bread slices
- two or three fried pike heads
- good clean pea broth
- salt
- fried bread croutons or fried bread cubes

## Directions

1. Braise tender green peas with pods until soft in butter
2. Pound the peas together with a few fried bread slices and two or three fried pike heads in a mortar
3. Place in a pot and pour good clean pea broth over it
4. Salt well and cook thoroughly
5. Strain through a sieve
6. Place in a pot and bring to a boil once more
7. Serve on fried bread croutons or fried bread cubes

title: Grog (Grog)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two liters of hot water
- two-thirds bottle of good arak
- forty dekagrams of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the hot water, arak, and sugar together
2. If it is still too strong, add warm water

title: Gulyáshús (Goulash Meat)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- meat cut into cubes, one kilogram
- water, one and a half litres
- onions, three
- caraway seeds
- salt
- carrot

## Directions

1. Cook the nicely cut meat in cubes with one and a half litres of water, three onions, caraway seeds, salt, and carrot in a pot for four hours. If the liquid cooks down too much, you can add water. Commonly, boiled potatoes or dumplings are also served with this.

title: Gulyáshús (Goulash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilo of beef chuck
- egg-sized amount of fat
- salt
- one large head of onion
- one teaspoon of paprika
- three liters of hot water

## Directions

1. Cut one kilo of beef chuck into large pieces
2. Place an egg-sized amount of fat in a pot and add the cut meat with salt, one large head of finely chopped onion, and one teaspoon of paprika
3. Braise covered for half an hour
4. Pour three liters of hot water over it and cook for one and a half hours until the meat is tender

title: Gulyáshús (Hungarian Goulash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram beef chuck (felsár-szélt)
- 1 egg-sized piece of lard
- salt
- 1 large onion, finely chopped
- 1 tablespoon paprika
- 3 liters hot water
- 1 plate of potatoes, peeled and quartered

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into large pieces
2. Place the lard in a pot and add the cut meat with salt, chopped onion, and paprika
3. Braise covered for half an hour
4. Pour 3 liters of hot water over it and cook for an hour and a half
5. When the meat is tender, add the potatoes
6. When the potatoes are cooked, the dish is ready to serve

title: Gulyáshús á la Rottenbiller Károly (Goulash à la Charles Rottenbiller)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- red onion in generous amount
- salt
- one whole tomato
- potatoes, peeled and chopped
- dumplings (optional, as alternative to potatoes)

## Directions

1. Chop the beef together as is customary, add a generous amount of red onion, and then place over heat as beef is usually prepared.
