Once you have removed the scum, salt sufficiently and add one whole tomato. When the tomato has become soft, remove it, press it through a sieve, and return it to the pot. When the meat begins to soften, add peeled and chopped potatoes and when these are cooked, serve the goulash. Instead of potatoes, separately made dumplings can also be served alongside the goulash.

title: Gyaluforgács (Wafer Shavings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 10 dekagrams fine flour
- 7 egg whites, beaten stiff
- butter for coating the sheet pan
- coarsely broken sugar for sprinkling
- any milk-based glaze

## Directions

1. Mix the finely ground sugar and fine flour well together
2. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
3. Spread the mixture onto a buttered sheet pan with a wide knife to paper-thin thickness
4. Place in a moderately warm oven for a few moments until a thin layer forms on top
5. Sprinkle evenly with coarsely broken sugar
6. Return to the oven until it becomes a light golden yellow
7. Cut into strips as long as a hand-span and as wide as a finger with a sharp knife
8. Roll quickly onto a cylindrical form of finger thickness to resemble wafer shavings
9. Heap high on a serving plate and serve with any milk-based glaze

title: Gyermekpép vanília-likőrrel (Children's Paste with Vanilla Liqueur)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cooking spoonfuls flour
- 7 deciiliters milk
- 7 dekagrams butter beaten to a foam
- 1 whole egg
- 6 egg yolks
- powdered sugar to taste
- powdered vanilla to taste
- 4 egg whites beaten stiff
- common wafer batter

## Directions

1. Mix 6 cooking spoonfuls of flour finely with 7 deciiliters of cold milk, adding the milk gradually to the flour so it does not become lumpy
2. Place on charcoal fire and stir constantly until thick, then cool
3. Mix the paste into 7 dekagrams of foamy butter
4. Add 1 whole egg and 6 egg yolks and mix together
5. Add powdered sugar and powdered vanilla to taste
6. Stir for half an hour
7. Finally fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
8. Place the paste in a buttered pot and put in a steam bath
9. Make small cones from common wafer batter, treat them like regular waffles but use a rolling pin that is sharpened fine at the bottom instead of a round one, bake the wafer smaller and wrap it around this rolling pin to make small cones

title: Gyermekpép-rétes (Baby Cereal Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- rétes-tészta (strudel dough)
- thick baby cereal
- 4 dekagramm butter
- two whole eggs and four egg yolks
- one lemon
- 10 dekagramm sugar
- four egg whites
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough and thick baby cereal
2. Beat 4 dekagramm butter until fluffy
3. Mix in the cooled baby cereal
4. Add two whole eggs and four egg yolks and stir for half an hour
5. Rub 10 dekagramm sugar on one lemon to obtain lemon sugar
6. Add this to the mixture
7. Finally gently fold in the stiffly beaten whites of four eggs
8. Stretch the dough finely
9. Spread the baby cereal filling on it to the thickness of a knife blade
10. Fold it together gently
11. Place it in a butter-brushed pan and bake until golden
12. When baked, serve with powdered sugar sprinkled on top

title: Gyümölcs-eczet (Fruit Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- rotted apples or rotted pears
- fruit scraps and peels
- good wine vinegar
- 2 deciliters of good wine vinegar per 6 liters
- 2 deciliters of brandy per 6 liters

## Directions

1. Collect rotted apples, rotted pears, and all manner of fruit scraps and peels in a water-tight barrel kept open in the cellar from autumn through winter
2. In spring or when you have time, press the collected fruit and place the juice in a clean vessel
3. Leave it to stand for 2-4 days so that the thick impure part gathers on top and can be removed
4. Place that juice in the vinegar barrel and put it in a moderately warm room, and after a few weeks you will obtain good strong vinegar
5. Alternatively: throw rotted fruit into a barrel standing in a warm location, shake it together and pour 16 liters of water into a 90-liter barrel
6. Bring the fruit temperature to 18-20° R if possible
7. During fermentation, stir the mixture well
8. After 3-4 weeks, depending on how warm or cool the barrel is, draw off the liquid and press out the remainder
9. Place the obtained cloudy liquid in another barrel, and for every 6 liters add 6 deciliters of good wine vinegar and 2 deciliters of brandy, and place the barrel in a warm location
10. Later, separate the fruit vinegar from its sediment

title: Gyümölcs-Rudacskák (Fruit Rods)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen dekagrams butter, beaten to foam
- twelve dekagrams sugar
- three egg yolks
- fourteen dekagrams flour
- three egg whites
- seven dekagrams currants
- seven dekagrams raisins
- ten dekagrams citron peel, chopped coarsely
- two tablets chocolate, chopped coarsely

## Directions

1. Add sugar and egg yolks to the foamed butter
2. Add flour, the stiff snow of egg whites, currants, raisins, citron peel, and chocolate
3. Spread on buttered sheet at finger thickness
4. Bake slowly
5. While still warm, cut into rods

title: Gyümölcsös rizs (Fruit Rice)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven decilitres of cream
- one and one-quarter kilograms of picked and washed rice
- eighteen dekagrams of pounded sugar
- salt
- three tablespoons of apricot or raspberry marmalade
- orange or lemon zest
- sugar
- lemon juice
- six egg whites
- butter
- candied fruits in syrup
- candied peach, orange, melon slices, pineapple, Reine Claude plums, Spanish cherries, or sour cherries

## Directions

1. Cook the picked and well-washed rice in seven decilitres of cream until soft, add eighteen dekagrams of pounded sugar and a little salt, then cool it
2. Meanwhile, mix three tablespoons of apricot or raspberry marmalade with sugar finely pounded and sifted on orange or lemon zest, then mix in the juice of half a lemon, and finally slowly mix in the stiffly beaten snow of six egg whites
3. Place one-third of the rice in a deep porcelain pan buttered with butter and spread it evenly, arrange candied fruits in syrup on it, such as apricot, orange, melon slices, pineapple, Reine Claude, Spanish cherry, or sour cherry
4. Spread the second third of the rice on top, arrange it again with fruit, and cover with the remaining rice
5. Then shape a peak from the apricot or raspberry foam on top of the rice with a silver spoon, and bake the whole in the oven at gentle heat for half an hour, then serve immediately on the table
6. If this dish succeeds, it has a very beautiful appearance and splendid flavor

title: Görög dinnye befőzése (Greek Melon Preserves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Greek melon with thick rind and very red flesh
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Select a Greek melon with thick rind and very red flesh
2. Cut off both ends of the melon to a width of two fingers, leaving the flesh intact on the rind
3. Prick the rind
4. Follow the same preservation method as described for yellow melon: leave in cold water overnight, spread on a cloth the next day to allow water to drain and the melon to dry, pass through a sieve and treat with sulfur to keep it light in color
5. Prepare a good thick syrup, pour into a bowl and let cool
6. Cook the melon slices in thin syrup for a short time, adding orange peel and thin sugar
7. For several days, boil down the liquid until the slices become transparent
8. Pour into glass jars, add a small amount of salicylic acid, tie down well and finally sterilize

title: Gőzmetélt (Steamed Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 420 grams flour, warmed
- a little salt
- 1 spoon broken sugar
- 200 milliliters lukewarm cream
- 5 egg yolks
- 70 grams melted butter
- 37.5 grams cheese yeast
- an egg-sized piece of butter
- an equal amount of sugar
- 200 milliliters milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 100 grams broken sugar
- a little vanilla
- 400 milliliters boiled cream

## Directions

1. Warm the flour in a bowl and salt it, add 1 spoon of broken sugar
2. Mix together the lukewarm cream, 5 egg yolks, melted butter, and cheese yeast
3. Take as much of the flour as needed for making the dough, then pour the entire mixture through a sieve onto the flour and stir until the dough separates from the spoon
4. Stir a little more, but it should not be too stiff even though it is difficult to work
5. Place on a well-floured board and roll out to the thickness of two knife-backs
6. Cut out the noodles with a medium-sized pastry cutter and let rise
7. Place an egg-sized piece of butter, an equal amount of sugar, and milk to a depth of two finger-widths in a flat, wide pan, bring to a boil, and add the noodles when well risen
8. Cover with clean paper and then with a well-fitting iron lid, steam for 15-30 minutes
9. While this is cooking, prepare the sauce: mix 5 egg yolks, 100 grams broken sugar, a little vanilla, and 400 milliliters boiled cream in a pot, place over coals and constantly stir so it does not curdle, until it begins to thicken
10. Pour into a bowl, place the noodles on a platter, sprinkle with sugar, and serve with the sauce

title: Hab-pép (Foam Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dekagrams preserved apricot
- 7 dekagrams finely powdered sugar
- juice of half a lemon
- 6 egg whites beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Place preserved apricot in a bowl
2. Add finely powdered sugar and stir for a quarter hour
3. Press in the juice of half a lemon
4. Gradually fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites by spoonfuls
5. Place the foam in the center of a buttered dish and heap it into a peak shape
6. Using the edge of a card, make five to six deep long cuts in the foam at proper distances from one another
7. Form a head from the foam and place on top
8. Bake in a very cool oven; when done in half an hour, serve immediately to the table as it collapses easily

title: Habart Linezi Torta (Whipped Almond Layer Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm soaked almonds
- 28 dekagramm sugar
- a little vanilla
- zest of one orange, finely chopped
- 28 dekagramm butter, whipped
- juice of half a lemon
- 4 egg yolks
- 28 dekagramm fine flour
- wafer

## Directions

1. Finely grind soaked almonds while moistening with water to prevent them from becoming oily
2. Add same amount of sugar, a little vanilla, and finely chopped orange zest
3. Whip butter until frothy and add lemon juice
4. Mix in egg yolks one by one
5. Mix in sugar, almonds and orange zest
6. Finally mix in fine flour
7. Make either a beautifully braided cake or three equal layers
8. Coat with egg white, sprinkle with sugar and bake
9. When baked, fill each layer with different preserves
10. Stack layers and either coat with orange frosting or decorate with candied fruit
11. For braided cake, use a band around the form since dough will flow

title: Habart mandolatorta (Whipped Almond Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams softened and finely pounded almonds
- 28 dekagrams finely pounded sugar
- 1 lemon, zest, finely chopped
- 2 whole eggs
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- butter for greasing the mold

## Directions

1. Place the softened and finely pounded almonds, the same amount of finely pounded sugar, and the finely chopped zest of one lemon in a bowl, into which beat 2 whole eggs and 6 egg yolks one after another and whip this for half an hour
2. The duration of whipping cannot be precisely determined; generally it is enough when the batter is already white and begins to rise in the bowl; it must never be whipped so long that it falls together again, otherwise the cake will be very hard
3. Once the batter is good, gently mix in the stiff snow of 3 egg whites, place the mixture in a mold greased with butter, and bake slowly
4. A whipped cake must never be exposed to great heat; once it is baked, which can be easily determined with a testing needle, turn it out and cool it and cover it with such a chocolate icing whose preparation method is found in no. 683

title: Habart Mandolatészta-Serlegecskék (Almond Paste Cup Cakes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- almond paste (as in recipe 693)
- butter
- whipped cream or ice cream for filling

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as in recipe number 693
2. Obtain special metal cup molds from a tinsmith with a thick center pin to leave a large opening
3. Pour the dough into cups greased with butter
4. Bake at low heat
5. Once cooled, fill the opening with whipped cream or ice cream just before serving

title: Habart Meggy-Lepény (Stirred Sour Cherry Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams soaked and finely ground almonds
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- lemon zest, finely chopped
- 5 dekagrams roll crumbs
- 1 egg
- 6 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites
- butter
- sour cherries

## Directions

1. Place the finely ground almonds and sugar in a bowl with finely chopped lemon zest
2. Add roll crumbs that are dry enough to grate
3. Beat 1 whole egg and 6 egg yolks over the mixture and stir for half an hour
4. Finally fold in stiffly beaten 5 egg whites
5. Coat a baking dish with butter
6. Line the bottom completely with sour cherries
7. Fill half with the batter
8. Bake in the oven until the dough is firm enough that the cherries do not sink
9. Slightly pull the baking dish out from the oven without removing it
10. Completely line the baked dough with more cherries
11. Top with the remaining batter
12. Bake completely
13. For the same cake with currants or raspberries: clean and wash 10 dekagrams of the finest berries and let them drain on a sieve until dry

title: Habart tejfelpép (Stirred Cream Pap)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 decilitres milk
- butter
- flour (amount to absorb 5-6 tablespoons of butter)
- sugar
- 8 dekagrams finely ground almonds
- vanilla
- 4 decilitres cream
- 6 egg yolks
- egg white foam

## Directions

1. Prepare wafer cones and many small pasta pieces
2. Make a béchamel from the milk
3. Add the pasta pieces (double the amount of the béchamel), the finely ground almonds, sugar to taste, and a small amount of ground vanilla to a pot
4. Pour 4 decilitres of cream over it and cook to a pap consistency with continuous stirring, thinning with more milk if too thick
5. Cool the mixture
6. Beat 6 egg yolks into the cooled mixture one by one until well foamed
7. Gently fold in the stiff foam of a few egg whites
8. Butter a mould and coat it densely with small pasta pieces
9. Fill with the pap and steam for three-quarters of an hour
10. When serving, turn out and arrange the wafer cones in a neat order, fill the cones with compote, and serve quickly

title: Habart élesztős tészta (Whipped Yeast Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams butter
- 2 deciliters fresh sour cream
- 5 egg yolks
- 14 dekagrams sour cream with dissolved yeast
- salt
- ground sugar
- half kilogram flour
- preserved fruit or berries for filling

## Directions

1. Beat the butter until foamy
2. Combine the sour cream, egg yolks, and yeast dissolved in sour cream and mix well
3. Strain through a sieve and add slowly to the beaten butter, one spoonful at a time
4. Never add more than one spoonful at a time and only after the previous one is well beaten in
5. Add a little salt and ground sugar
6. When all the mixture is combined, add half kilogram flour, beat well and prepare a stick dough
7. Spread the dough rolled to the thickness of a new hand on a butter-coated pastry leaf
8. Arrange with preserved fruit or berries and decorate with strips made from the remaining dough
9. Make a border around the edge of the leaf and bake at sustained heat
10. Before placing in the oven, it must rise like all yeast dough, then brush with egg white and sprinkle thickly with coarsely broken sugar and almonds

title: Habart-tojás spárgával és rákfarkkal (Scrambled Eggs with Asparagus and Crayfish Tails)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve eggs
- salt
- asparagus bundle boiled in salted water
- twenty crayfish tails
- ground pepper
- crayfish butter

## Directions

1. Beat twelve eggs with salt in a pot until well mixed
2. Cut the boiled asparagus into long pieces
3. Shell twenty crayfish tails and cut each one in the middle
4. Add the asparagus and crayfish tails to the eggs with a little ground pepper and mix well
5. Put the mixture into melted crayfish butter
6. Stir frequently and break into small pieces
7. Serve in a small round bowl, piling it high in the center and garnishing around it with mushrooms

title: Habos citrom-pép (Foamy Lemon Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagramm sugar lightly rubbed with lemon
- juice of 2 lemons
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Crush 18 dekagramm sugar lightly rubbed with lemon, place in a pan, and squeeze the juice of 2 lemons over it.
2. Keep on low heat until the lemon dissolves, then set aside.
3. Once cooled, beat in 8 egg yolks one after another.
4. Return to low heat and stir constantly until it begins to steam and becomes slightly thick.
5. Remove from heat and stir until cold.
6. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 8 eggs.
7. Heap into a porcelain pan to form a peak, smooth with a wide knife, and create spiral formations with a demitasse spoon.
8. Form a mushroom shape on top, sprinkle with fine sugar, and bake at very gentle heat or cook in a bain-marie.
9. Serve quickly at table once light brown, as this paste is delicate and collapses easily.
10. This paste can also be made with orange: for 8 eggs, use one large soup orange, lightly rub the sugar with the zest, squeeze the juice of one lemon and one orange over it, and otherwise prepare as with lemon.

title: Habos tej csokoládéval (Foamed Milk with Chocolate)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 liters thick heavy cream
- 14 dekagrams finest chocolate
- 2 deciliters water
- 14 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 2 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Set the thick heavy cream on ice 2 hours before use
2. Beat in a brass basin with a thick whip until the entire mass becomes foam
3. Cook 14 dekagrams of finest chocolate in 2 deciliters of water with continuous stirring until it reaches the consistency of child's porridge
4. Set aside
5. Mix this chocolate with 14 dekagrams of finely ground sugar and fold together with 2 dekagrams of dissolved isinglass into the foam
6. Pour into a mold, set on ice until frozen

title: Habos tej földieperrél (Foamed Milk with Strawberries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- same as foamed milk
- 28 dekagrams ripe strawberries, pressed through a sieve
- 28 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 3.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Prepare the same foamed milk as in the previous recipe
2. Mix 28 dekagrams of ripe sieved strawberries with an equal amount of finely ground sugar
3. Fold this together with the foamed milk and 3.5 dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
4. Pour into an attractive mold and freeze on ice
5. Before turning out, dip the mold for a moment in boiling water, dry it, and turn the foam out onto a plate

title: Habos vanília-fagylalt (Fluffy Vanilla Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters cream
- 7 deciliters good cream
- ground sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Prepare ice cream from 7 deciliters of cream as in the previous recipe
2. When cooled, whip 7 deciliters of good cream into stiff peaks
3. Quickly mix with finely crushed sugar and vanilla
4. Combine the whole with the cooled mixture, pour into the mold, and place on ice, where it freezes very quickly even without turning
5. If you want to freeze it quickly and strongly, turn it for a quarter hour

title: Habzó bor-eczet (Sparkling Wine Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 kilograms raw sugar
- 50 kilograms warm water
- Juice of a lemon and its finely cut peel
- 2 dekagrams cinnamon
- Half a kilogram of rinsed beer yeast
- 10 kilograms of the best wine vinegar, previously boiled
- 2 tablespoons yeast
- 2 kilograms salt and pepper sauce
- 4 grams crystallized wine tartrate per half kilogram
- 2 grams potassium bitartrate per half kilogram

## Directions

1. Dissolve the raw sugar in warm water
2. Add lemon juice and peel, cinnamon, and rinsed beer yeast
3. Place the mixture in a barrel, shake well, and set in a place with a temperature of 10-15° R.
4. After fermentation, strain the liquid
5. Mix the strained liquid with 10 kilograms of the best previously boiled wine vinegar and 2 tablespoons yeast
6. Pour the liquid into earthenware vessels and expose to a temperature of 20-25° R. until sufficiently sour
7. Mix the finished vinegar with 2 kilograms of salt and pepper sauce
8. After two days, pour into small bottles
9. To each half kilogram of vinegar add 4 grams of crystallized wine tartrate and 2 grams of potassium bitartrate
10. Close the bottles tightly after adding these substances and store in a cool place

title: Hal-karbonád (Fish Cutlet)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- about one kilogram pike from the middle
- salt
- water
- half kilogram carp
- flour
- butter
- half grated breadcrumbs
- milk
- small red onion
- lemon zest
- pepper
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cook about one kilogram of pike from the middle in salted water, only until the fish is not raw
2. Take half kilogram of carp, salt and coat in flour well, then fry in butter
3. Remove bones from both; soak half grated breadcrumbs in milk, wring out and chop fine with the fish
4. Similarly add a small red onion, a little lemon zest, and a little ground pepper
5. Mix well with three egg yolks
6. Shape small cutlets from this mixture

title: Hal-karbonád (Fish Cutlets)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram pike from the middle section
- half kilogram carp
- salt
- flour
- butter
- half grated roll soaked in milk
- small onion head
- lemon peel
- ground pepper
- three egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook the pike in salted water until just cooked through
2. Salt the carp and roll in flour, then fry in butter until done, removing all bones
3. Soak half a grated roll in milk and squeeze out the liquid
4. Mince the cooked pike and carp together with the squeezed bread
5. Add finely chopped small onion, lemon peel, and ground pepper
6. Mix everything well with three egg yolks
7. Form the mixture into small cutlets
8. Coat with melted butter, roll in fine breadcrumbs, and fry in fresh butter until golden
9. Before serving, melt a little butter with finely chopped lemon peel and lemon juice
10. Pour this hot sauce over the cutlets when serving
11. Alternatively, the same mixture can be formed into small sausages, dipped in beaten egg, coated with breadcrumbs, and fried, served with lemon sauce

title: Hal-kolbász (Fish Sausage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram pike from the middle
- salt
- water
- equal amount of carp
- flour
- milk
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- parsley
- egg yolks
- pepper
- cream

## Directions

1. Cook one kilogram of pike from the middle in salted water
2. Take an equal piece of carp, salt it, coat in flour, and fry
3. Remove bones from both fish and chop fine with breadcrumbs soaked in milk and wrung out
4. Meanwhile, braise finely chopped parsley in butter for a few minutes
5. Beat four egg yolks
6. Pound everything together in a large mortar until fine like dough
7. Add salt and pepper to taste
8. Place in a bowl and mix in three egg yolks and two spoons of cream
9. Fill a syringe and make sausages an ell long
10. Before cooking, place in warm water for several minutes, then fry nicely in butter
11. Serve hot
12. Note: sheep casings should be soaked and rinsed several times in warm water before filling

title: Halleves halászosan (Fisherman's Fish Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram of fish (best from bony parts such as head and tail) from catfish and carp, or possibly several kinds
- three roots
- one carrot
- vinegar
- fat
- flour
- two red onions, finely chopped
- crushed paprika
- bay leaf
- whole pepper
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut three roots and one carrot into thin long slices and place them in a pot suitable for making soup and cook until the vegetables are soft
2. Cut the fish into pieces, place it in the pot and pour on enough vinegar to make it sour and let the fish cook
3. In a pot, heat fat and when hot, fry one spoon of flour until bread-colored
4. Add two finely chopped red onion heads, a small spoon of crushed paprika, a little bay leaf and whole pepper and when wilted, dilute with the fish broth and boil for a short time
5. When the fish is cooked, add a little sour cream, bring to a boil again and then serve

title: Haluska tölcsértésztával, párban (Noodles with Cone-shaped Paste, Steamed)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dekagrams butter
- 1 whole egg
- 4 egg yolks
- flour
- salt
- hot milk
- sugar
- 4 deciliters milk
- flour
- sugar
- vanilla
- 9 dekagrams ground almonds
- 4 deciliters good cream
- 5 egg yolks
- peach or currant jam

## Directions

1. Cream 10 dekagrams butter
2. Add 1 whole egg and 4 egg yolks one after another and whisk
3. Season lightly with salt and add only enough flour to resemble light noodle dough
4. Fill a syringe with smooth round tube with this dough and pre-wet it with warm milk so the dough does not stick
5. Push the dough into hot milk cutting equal pieces with a knife continuously
6. Add a piece of sugar to the milk in which the extruded noodles are cooking
7. Remove with a skimming spoon when cooked and set aside
8. Make a thick pap from 4 deciliters milk, a few tablespoons flour, and sugar stirring continuously until smooth
9. When well cooked place a pot lid on iron legs with good coals underneath
10. When the tin-plated part of the lid which should be on top is well heated, quickly coat with butter
11. Place a spoonful of pap on it and spread out
12. Leave for a few minutes until it adheres to the bottom
13. Scrape it off with a wide knife leaving as much as a sheet of paper
14. When it begins to brown coat with butter and cut rectangular leaves
15. Roll these on a small wooden piece which is thicker on top and pointed below and about finger-length to form cone shapes
16. Continue until you have enough cones to abundantly fill the dough
17. Make large round sheets from the rest of the dough the size of the pot you plan to use
18. Coat the pot well with butter and on the bottom place a baked dough sheet
19. Add a row of noodles sprinkled with sugar, vanilla, and 9 dekagrams crushed almonds with shell
20. Then another sheet etc. until the pot is full
21. Whisk together 4 deciliters good cream or if the pot is larger somewhat more with sugar, vanilla, and 5 egg yolks and pour over
22. Place in a steam bath and leave for three-quarters of an hour
23. Then unmold and arrange the cones in nice order around it
24. Fill the cones with peach or currant jam

title: Halászlé (Fisherman's Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- various fish
- onion
- salt
- paprika
- water

## Directions

1. Procure various types of fish, clean them well, cut into pieces and place in a pot
2. Add plenty of onion, salt and paprika, cover with water
3. Cover and cook

title: Harlequin Cake (Harlekin-lepény)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- wafer batter
- puff dough
- 21 dekagrams soaked almonds
- sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons candied orange peel
- 2 bars finely grated chocolate
- 1.5 dekagrams green almonds
- quince slices in syrup
- 4 or 5 green preserved walnuts
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Using the waffle iron, bake 10-12 round wafers
2. Prepare puff dough and roll it as fine as noodle dough, line a wide flat baking pan with it smoothly
3. Finely crush 21 dekagrams of soaked almonds, moistening with water during crushing
4. Crush the same amount of sugar finely and sift it, then place both in a bowl, add 8 egg yolks and stir until white and fluffy
5. Divide into three parts: to the first part add 2 tablespoons of powdered sugar rubbed with orange, to the second part add 2 bars of finely grated chocolate, to the third part add 1.5 dekagrams of green almonds and the same amount of quince slices preserved in syrup and 4 or 5 green preserved walnuts, all cut into small cubes, and mix this with the almond mixture
6. Divide the stiff meringue from 6 egg whites into three parts and gently fold into each mixture
7. Place the portion with the orange sugar into the lined pan, cover with a wafer sheet, place the second mixture on top and cover again with a wafer sheet, finally place the fruited mixture in the oven and bake in gentle but sustained heat
8. When baked, turn out, cool slightly and serve dusted with sugar

title: Hasábos tészta (Layered Custard Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crumbly kifli cut into long pieces
- cream
- butter
- finely chopped almond
- raisins
- currants
- powdered sugar
- vanilla
- 2 deciliter good cream
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Dampen the kifli pieces with cream
2. Sprinkle the buttered porcelain mold with chopped almonds, raisins, currants, powdered sugar, and vanilla
3. Place one row of soaked kifli and sprinkle similarly with the mixture above
4. Continue until the mold is full
5. Beat together two deciliter cream, three egg yolks, sugar, and vanilla
6. Pour this mixture over the layered cake
7. Bake until done

title: Herczegnő-víz (Princess Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams English white lead
- 6 deciliters rose water
- 14 dekagrams alcohol
- 2 dekagrams camphor

## Directions

1. Rub 14 dekagrams English white lead finely with 6 deciliters rose water
2. Dissolve 2 dekagrams camphor in 14 dekagrams alcohol
3. Mix the two liquids together

title: Hermstádt élesztője (Hermstadt Yeast for Bread and Cake Baking)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram wheat malt
- 4 kilograms barley malt
- Water
- 21-34 liters of spiced liquor
- 14 liters with 10 dekagrams of good hops
- 7 kilograms coarse wheat flour
- Cold running water

## Directions

1. From coarsely ground wheat and barley malt, prepare the required amount of spiced liquor
2. Boil in a pot until only 12 liters remain
3. Before the liquid has reduced to this amount, add approximately 14 liters containing 10 dekagrams of good hops, then boil down to the stated amount
4. Strain the liquid and press out the remaining hops
5. Mix 7 kilograms of coarse wheat flour with enough cold running water to form a thick paste
6. Pour this into the prepared liquid and stir well together
7. Let this mixture stand in a place with a temperature of 14° R. for 30 hours, after which the yeast is ready for use
8. In summer it keeps for 3 weeks in a cool cellar; in winter for two months
9. To prepare fresh yeast, this yeast can be used to start fermentation

title: Hideg hal aszpikkal és majonézzel (Cold Fish with Aspic and Mayonnaise)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a middle piece of pike or pike-perch
- salt
- water
- white vinegar
- whole red onion
- carrot
- peppercorns
- cloves
- aspic

## Directions

1. Clean and salt a middle piece of pike or pike-perch
2. Cook in equal parts water and white vinegar with whole red onion, carrot, peppercorns, and cloves
3. Let cool, then cut into finger-thick pieces
4. Arrange evenly and high in the center of a round plate
5. Coat smoothly with mayonnaise using a wide knife so no fish is visible
6. Pour aspic into a wide pan and when set, turn out the jelly
7. Cut into triangular pieces of pinky-finger length with a slightly warm knife so the wider side of the triangle faces the mayonnaise
8. Place an aspic star on top of the mayonnaise in the center for a neat appearance

title: Hideg mustár-mártás (Cold Mustard Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- three anchovies, cleaned of bones and spines
- red onion
- green parsley
- mustard
- sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil four eggs hard
2. Add three anchovies cleaned of bones and spines
3. Chop together with red onion, green parsley and egg
4. Put in a bowl
5. Prepare with mustard, sugar and vinegar and serve

title: Hideg mártás masánszki almából és tormából (Cold Apple and Horseradish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four to six Massendie apples
- a small piece of horseradish
- a spoonful of crushed sugar
- white vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel and grate four to six Massendie apples
2. Grate a small piece of horseradish and place it in a bowl
3. Mix well with the grated apple and a spoonful of crushed sugar
4. Thin with white vinegar
5. Use twice as many apples as horseradish

title: Hideg Pástétom (Cold Pâté)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams bacon
- roasted venison or rabbit meat
- 5 bread rolls, crusts and insides
- good meat broth
- 5 egg yolks
- black pepper, ground
- nutmeg flower
- salt
- 3 goose livers
- butter
- 2 pheasants
- 2 partridges
- 10-12 truffle mushrooms
- green parsley
- 1 kilogram butter
- 750 dekagrams flour
- egg white
- aspic

## Directions

1. Finely chop 28 dekagrams bacon and roasted venison or rabbit meat, removing meat from bones
2. Grate 5 bread roll crusts and cook the insides in good meat broth
3. Mix the finely chopped meat with bacon
4. Add 5 egg yolks and mix well
5. Pass through a sieve
6. Add ground black pepper, nutmeg flower, and salt
7. Braise 3 goose livers in butter
8. Roast 2 pheasants and 2 partridges, cut them up, and remove meat from bones
9. Slice 10-12 truffle mushrooms and braise with butter and green parsley
10. Prepare crumbly pastry from 1 kilogram butter and 750 dekagrams flour (this pastry should be less crumbly than usual to hold the filling, so use more flour than butter)
11. Make a pâté of ordinary form with a much higher crown
12. Layer the filling with alternating rows: filling, truffle mushrooms, braised goose liver pieces, truffle mushrooms again, more filling, pheasant and partridge pieces, and so on until full
13. Close with pastry as usual
14. Decorate the top with pastry dough if desired
15. Brush with egg white and bake at steady heat for two hours
16. After removing from the oven, cool it
17. Cut the lid quite large and fill the interior of the pâté with aspic

title: Hideg Sodó Almával (Cold Soda with Apple)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- small peeled Masansky apples with centers removed
- water
- sugar
- lemon juice
- preserved currants
- soda from recipe 390

## Directions

1. Cook small peeled Masansky apples with centers removed in water, sugar, and a little lemon juice until soft but not falling apart
2. Place in a deep cup
3. Fill with preserved currants
4. Pour soda (as prepared in recipe 390) over the apples
5. Place in a cold location
6. Before serving, arrange decoratively with Spanish pastries

title: Hideg Tyúk a Legegyszerűbb Módon (Cold Chicken in the Simplest Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- beef broth
- anchovies
- fresh olive oil
- lemon juice
- strong vinegar
- lemon zest
- capers

## Directions

1. After nicely plucking and preparing two beautiful white chickens, let them stew until tender in good beef broth.
2. Finely chop two large anchovies and mix them with a little fresh olive oil, adding the juice of half a lemon and a little strong vinegar.
3. After the mixture is made, cut the cooled chickens neatly into pieces.
4. Dip each piece into the well-blended sauce and arrange neatly on a plate.
5. Sprinkle with finely shredded lemon zest and capers.

title: Hideg élesztős tészta (Cold Yeast Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee-cup amount of egg yolks
- same amount of melted butter
- same amount of lukewarm cream
- 14 dekagrams cream with dissolved yeast
- salt
- flour warmed in a bowl beforehand

## Directions

1. Prepare dough slightly firmer than puff pastry from the egg yolks, melted butter, lukewarm cream, yeast dissolved in cream, salt and flour
2. Beat the dough until it becomes fine and smooth
3. Place it in a cloth coated with butter, tie it loosely and place in cold water
4. Leave it in the water until the dough floats to the surface
5. Remove it, roll out to finger-thickness on a board well dusted with flour
6. Make rolls filled with preserves or other shapes, brush with egg white, sprinkle with sugared almonds and bake

title: Hollandi Tojás (Dutch Eggs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 large herring, well-soaked and skinned
- carp roe
- fat
- butter
- 5 tablespoons sour cream
- green parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Cut 7 hard-boiled eggs in half lengthwise
2. Chop the herring into small pieces
3. Remove and keep the roe from a carp
4. Fry both the herring and roe in fat
5. Spread 5 tablespoons sour cream on a buttered dish
6. Arrange the eggs neatly on the dish
7. Place a piece of herring and fried carp roe next to each egg half until the bottom of the dish is covered
8. Add a few tablespoons sour cream on top of the eggs
9. Sprinkle with finely chopped green parsley
10. Place coals on top so the sour cream boils

title: Holsteini tej (Holstein Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams softened almonds
- 7 deciliters good heavy cream, boiled
- water

## Directions

1. Finely grind 14 dekagrams of softened almonds, moistening slightly with water during grinding so it does not become oily
2. Add this to 7 deciliters of boiled good heavy cream and heat for several moments

title: Holsteini tej (Holstein Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams soaked almonds
- 7 deciliters boiled good cream
- 21 dekagrams sugar
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Finely grind the soaked almonds, moistening with a little water during grinding so it doesn't become oily
2. Add this to 7 deciliters of boiled good cream, cook together for a few moments, then strain through cloth
3. Dissolve 21 dekagrams sugar in it
4. Add the stiffly beaten snow of 5 egg whites to it
5. Place on charcoal fire and beat until the entire mixture becomes foam and has a shiny white color, taking care it does not begin to boil
6. When everything is well mixed, shiny and white, pour into shiny white coffee cups or deep glass bowls, place in ice and freeze
7. If you want to turn it out, mix 2 dekagrams dissolved isinglass into it, pour into a mold and freeze

title: Hospót (Mixed Vegetables)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- carrots
- turnips
- flour
- butter
- broth
- goose fat
- thin bacon slices
- cooked pickled tongue
- braised savoy cabbage
- ham

## Directions

1. Chop kohlrabi, carrots and turnips neatly
2. Dust each with a small amount of flour separately
3. Braise in butter, pour a small amount of broth over
4. Stir carefully so pieces do not break apart
5. Line the bottom of a mold lined with goose fat with thin bacon slices
6. Add a layer of kohlrabi and a layer of cooked pickled tongue cut into thin slices
7. Add a layer of braised savoy cabbage and again tongue
8. Finally add the turnips
9. Place the mold in a steam bath for fifteen minutes
10. Turn out onto a plate
11. Line the edge neatly with cut ham
12. Serve quickly to the table after removing the bacon from the top

title: Hospót más módon (Mixed Vegetables, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tender kohlrabi
- carrots
- green peas
- butter
- flour
- good broth
- sugar
- cauliflower

## Directions

1. Clean tender kohlrabi and carrots
2. With a small chisel, carve small balls from each, equal portions from each vegetable
3. Cook the same amount of green peas for a few minutes in salted water and drain
4. Melt butter and lightly brown a spoonful of flour
5. Thin with good broth and cook
6. Add the kohlrabi and carrot balls
7. Add a piece of sugar and braise until tender with frequent stirring
8. Add the green peas and cook the sauce completely
9. Be careful that the vegetables remain whole and do not become mushy
10. Prepare beautiful cauliflower divided into small florets
11. Cook tender in good meat broth, keeping them whole
12. When serving, heap the vegetables in the center of the plate like a mound
13. Arrange the cauliflower around the edge of the plate so it forms a wreath
14. Breaded chicken or veal cutlet is usually served with it

title: Hungária-szeletkék (Hungarian Cutlets)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh goose liver
- salt
- spices
- 1 white onion
- parsley greens
- several fresh mushrooms
- butter
- veal cutlets
- pork caul
- fat
- Croutons
- Sherry sauce
- caviar
- pastry rolls

## Directions

1. Press fresh goose liver through a sieve, add a little salt and spices, and mix well
2. Finely chop one white onion, a little parsley greens, and several fresh mushrooms, and braise in a little butter; when cooled, add to the goose liver purée and mix together
3. Now prepare the veal cutlets
4. Salt them and spread a little purée on one side and wrap in pork caul
5. Place in a pan and pour over a little fat, and bake in a hot oven for 10-15 minutes
6. Place under each cutlet a round Crouton, good Sherry sauce, and on each one a pastry roll filled with caviar

title: Hógula (Snow Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams flour
- salt
- 1 small spoon ground sugar
- 14 dekagrams butter
- 2 egg yolks
- sweet cream as needed for moistening
- sugar for sprinkling
- 7 dekagrams finely chopped almonds
- 4 deciliters cream
- 5 egg yolks
- sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with salt and ground sugar
2. Cut butter into small pieces and work into the flour with a rolling pin until well combined
3. Add two egg yolks and as much sweet cream as needed to moisten the dough
4. Combine the dough with a wide knife, roll out and fold in three times like puff pastry
5. Divide into smaller portions and roll out into round leaves
6. Cut with a pastry cutter and fry like ordinary snow pastry
7. Sprinkle with sugar and serve on the table
8. Alternatively, for use as a dessert, heap high on a dish and sprinkle with finely chopped almonds
9. Beat 4 deciliters cream with 5 egg yolks, sugar and vanilla until well combined
10. Pour over the pastry, place in the oven and bake for half an hour

title: Húsos Tészta (Meat Pasta)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful capon or young turkey breast
- 1.5 bread rolls, crusts and insides
- 2 deciliters milk
- 9 dekagrams butter
- 3 whole eggs
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- carrots, sliced
- red onion

## Directions

1. Cut the breast from a beautiful capon or young turkey
2. Grate 1.5 bread roll crusts and slice thinly
3. Moisten with 2 deciliters milk
4. Add 9 dekagrams butter
5. Place on a charcoal fire and braise with continuous stirring, beating in 3 whole eggs during cooking, until it becomes a thick paste that separates from the spoon
6. Cool it
7. In a large mortar, finely pound the cut capon breast with a little salt and nutmeg flower and pass through a sieve
8. In a pot, braise the rest of the capon with butter, several slices of carrot, and red onion until light yellow
9. From time to time pour a little broth on it and when the meat is tender, continue as directed

title: Ibolya Conserv (Violet Conserve)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilogram sugar
- 10 decagrams violet leaves, cleaned of all green parts
- 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in a glass of water, skimming the foam while boiling, until it threads
2. Remove from heat and let cool halfway
3. Pound the violet leaves very finely in a mortar and extract the juice
4. Pour the juice into the sugar and stir as quickly as possible with a new wooden spoon
5. Fill the mass into a case made of white double paper
6. When cooled, peel the conserve from the paper and cut into rod- or square-shaped pieces with a knife
7. Note: conserves should be stirred until the boiled sugar reaches the consistency and density of thick baby food; then mix in four spoonfuls of juice from any pressed fruit

title: Ibolya-kocsonya (Violet Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- small plate of spring violet leaves
- 7 deciliters hot water
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Tear violet leaves without leaving any green on them
2. Pour 7 deciliters of hot water over a small plate of violet leaves in a soup bowl
3. Cover well and let stand for 1 hour
4. Strain the leaves through a sieve
5. Dissolve 42 dekagrams of sugar in the liquid
6. When completely dissolved, add 4.5 dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
7. Strain the mixture through cloth and blotting paper
8. Pour into mold and freeze

title: Ikrás Káposzta (Cabbage with Roe)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cabbage
- red onion
- brown roux
- roe, blanched in salted water and fried in butter

## Directions

1. Once the sour cabbage is cooked soft, add it to a brown roux with red onion
2. Add the roe that has been blanched in salted water and fried in butter
3. Cook together well until fully combined

title: Ispékes leves (Spiced Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh pork
- calves' feet or pork feet
- salt
- red onion
- black pepper
- paprika
- sugar
- vinegar
- saffron

## Directions

1. Cut the pork and feet into pieces and put to cook, salting them
2. When the feet are tender, add red onion cut into cubes, using one large onion per foot
3. Cook together until the onion is soft
4. Make a nice pink thin roux
5. Add black pepper, a little paprika, and a tablespoon of powdered sugar to the roux
6. Thin the roux with the feet liquid and add vinegar
7. Add good saffron so the broth is a nice yellow color

title: Jeges kávé (Iced Coffee)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams of roasted coffee
- seven deciliters of good light coffee
- sugar to taste
- one and a half liters of good sour cream
- fourteen dekagrams of sugar

## Directions

1. Make good light coffee from the roasted coffee
2. Sweeten well and let cool in an ice cream mold without stirring
3. Mix the sour cream with fourteen dekagrams of sugar
4. Beat on ice until foamy
5. Just before serving, mix into the coffee
6. Serve in glass cups or bowls

title: Jeges mézes-kalács (Frosted Gingerbread)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites
- 14 dekagramm finely ground sugar
- 14 dekagramm finely ground almonds
- 7 dekagramm finely chopped citron peel
- wafer strips
- citron peel leaves

## Directions

1. Beat the stiff foam of 2 egg whites with 14 dekagramm finely ground sugar for one hour until it becomes thick frosting
2. Add 14 dekagramm finely ground almonds and 7 dekagramm finely chopped citron peel so it becomes a thick paste
3. Spread this paste onto strips cut from wafer, one finger long and two fingers wide, to a thickness of half a finger
4. Bake slowly in a cool oven
5. When the wafers are coated with the mixture, cut thin leaves from citron peel and arrange them on top in a beautiful pattern

title: Jeges puncs (Iced Punch)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- orange juice (half of total citrus)
- lemon juice (slightly more than half of total citrus)
- 1 glass lemon juice
- 4 glasses rice water
- 4 glasses crushed sugar
- half glass rum
- egg whites of 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Squeeze equal parts orange and lemon juice with slightly more lemon
2. Measure 1 glass juice, 4 glasses rice water, 4 glasses crushed sugar, and half glass rum
3. Mix well and begin to freeze
4. When half-frozen, gently mix in the stiff snow from 8 egg whites one at a time
5. Work well into the ice cream
6. Continue stirring and working throughout the freezing until the entire mixture becomes a stiff foam
7. Before serving, chill the glass container by burying it in ice, as it melts easily

title: Jonquille hajkenő (Jonquil Hair Pomade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram mutton tallow
- seventy-five dekagrams vegetable oil
- thirteen dekagrams hair powder
- two dekagrams musk dye
- two dekagrams bergamot oil
- four grams thyme
- four grams orange blossom oil
- twelve grams amber dye

## Directions

1. Take one kilogram of mutton tallow with seventy-five dekagrams of vegetable oil and thirteen dekagrams of hair powder, mix them together and dye to a pale yellow color
2. Then add two dekagrams of musk dye, the same amount of bergamot oil, four grams of thyme, the same amount of orange blossom oil, and twelve grams of amber dye

title: Juhtúrós burgonya (Potatoes with Sheep Cheese)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Several large floury potatoes
- Salt
- Butter
- Red onion, finely chopped
- Sheep cheese (juhtúró)

## Directions

1. Peel and wash the potatoes
2. Cook in salted water until soft
3. Slice the cooked potatoes and place on a plate
4. Pour over butter in which finely chopped red onion has been braised
5. Sprinkle with sheep cheese, toss and serve hot

title: Juhtúrós haluska (Sheep's Cheese Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram fine flour
- salt
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- water
- butter
- sheep's cheese
- fresh lard cracklings

## Directions

1. Place 1 kilogram fine flour in a large bowl
2. Season with salt
3. Beat in 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
4. Pour in a little water and mix everything with a cooking spoon
5. When the dough is well worked, throw noodles from a board with handle into boiling water using a knife
6. When cooked and risen to the surface, remove with a slotted spoon
7. Place in a pot coated with butter
8. Pour butter over and sprinkle with sheep's cheese and fine cracklings from fresh lard

title: Jázmin hajkenő (Jasmine Hair Pomade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven dekagrams fresh mutton tallow
- one and one-half kilograms vegetable oil
- two kilograms hair powder
- seven dekagrams jasmine oil

## Directions

1. Mix seven dekagrams of fresh mutton tallow, one and one-half kilograms of vegetable oil, and two kilograms of hair powder together by gentle melting
2. During continuous stirring, mix in seven dekagrams of jasmine oil

title: Jázmin-víz (Jasmine Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 liters purified 70° alcohol
- 2 kilograms fresh jasmine flowers
- 4 grams bergamot oil
- 4.5 dekagrams cinnamon, crushed to powder

## Directions

1. Add 2 kilograms fresh jasmine flowers, 4 grams bergamot oil, and 4.5 dekagrams crushed cinnamon to 5 liters purified 70° alcohol
2. Allow to digest

title: Jégfánkocskák (Frosted Little Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg whites
- 28 dekagramm sifted vanilla sugar
- 4.5 dekagramm hair powder
- 21 dekagramm cleaned and finely chopped sweet almonds
- 4.5 dekagramm candied orange peel
- 4.5 dekagramm citron peel
- 4.5 dekagramm pistachios
- wafer leaves

## Directions

1. Beat the stiff foam of 6 egg whites with 28 dekagramm sifted vanilla sugar for one hour until it becomes white frosting
2. Add 4.5 dekagramm hair powder, 21 dekagramm cleaned and finely chopped sweet almonds, 4.5 dekagramm candied orange peel, 4.5 dekagramm citron peel, and 4.5 dekagramm pistachios
3. Chop everything equally with the almond
4. Beat the whole mixture well
5. Spread this mixture onto leaves cut from wafers to the thickness of a small finger
6. Bake slowly at gentle heat

title: Jó házi kolbász (Good Homemade Sausage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 kilos mixed meat, finely chopped but not too fine
- half liter cold water
- quarter liter wine
- abundant black pepper, ground
- marjoram
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop the mixed meat well but not too finely
2. Mix together the meat, cold water, wine, abundant black pepper, marjoram, and salt
3. Work everything together thoroughly so that the spices, salt, water and wine penetrate evenly throughout
4. Fill casings with the mixture, packing firmly
5. Optionally add lemon or garlic to the filling
6. Smoke as soon as possible for more flavor
7. Smoke quickly to prevent the sausage from drying out

title: Jó házi írósvajas tészta (Good Homemade Butter Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams flour
- 250 grams fresh firm butter
- salt
- 1 tablespoon powdered sugar
- 1 hazelnut-sized piece of butter
- 1 teaspoon rum
- 4 egg yolks
- milk as needed
- egg wash
- blanched chopped almonds with sugar

## Directions

1. Take 250 grams of flour and 250 grams of fresh firm butter
2. Work half of the flour with the butter and a little salt using a rolling pin into a dough
3. When finely worked, roll out to the size of a plate and place on ice
4. Make strudel dough with the other half of flour, a little salt, one tablespoon powdered sugar, one hazelnut-sized piece of butter, one teaspoon rum, four egg yolks, and as much milk as it will take up
5. Work until small bubbles form on it and it separates from the board, which shows it is well worked
6. Shape into a loaf and let rest for one hour
7. Roll the dough quite thin
8. Place the butter dough on it and roll together quite thin
9. Fold as one folds a tablecloth and let rest for half an hour in a cold place
10. Roll out three times at half-hour intervals
11. Cut with a hot knife as desired
12. Brush with egg
13. Sprinkle generously with blanched chopped almonds with sugar
14. Bake quickly at a hot fire; small kipferl or cream-filled pastries can also be made from it

title: Jó Kalács Sütésmódja (Good Brioche Baking Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekaliters milk (lukewarm)
- flour (approximately 14 dekaliters by volume)
- 1.25 dekagrams fresh yeast dissolved in milk
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- salt as needed
- 1 egg yolk
- 18 dekagrams butter
- For finer brioche: 3 liters flour, 1.5 liters milk, 6 dekagrams softened cheese yeast, salt, 1 whole egg, 2 egg yolks, 28 dekagrams butter
- For economical good brioche: 1 liter fine flour, 1 whole egg, 1 egg yolk, 1 tablespoon sugar, 2 dekagrams cheese yeast dissolved in milk, 4 dekaliters lukewarm good milk, 7 dekagrams pure lard

## Directions

1. Prepare a starter (pâte) from 4 dekaliters lukewarm milk and 1.25 dekagrams fresh yeast dissolved in milk
2. Mix finely with more milk (this is essential with yeast)
3. Place in bowl and beat well, adding only as much flour as 4 dekaliters milk will wet
4. Set in a warm place until well risen
5. Warm the remaining milk and add 2 tablespoons sugar
6. Salt the flour and prepare the dough with the milk
7. Once the dough is well worked so no more flour or milk is needed, add the starter to the center and knead well
8. Note: knead with open fingers, not with fists as for bread
9. After kneading awhile, add 1 egg yolk and continue kneading
10. When dough separates from hand, move it to one side of the trough
11. Clean the trough and melt 18 dekagrams butter
12. Coat the trough and your hands with it and continue kneading
13. Coat the other side of the trough and repeat until all butter is kneaded in and dough forms bubbles
14. Then knead lightly with fists for a few minutes, fold together, dust with flour, cover and let rise well
15. Once well risen, form small pieces
16. Let them rest 15 minutes, then braid the brioche
17. Heat the oven while the dough rises so it is ready when the brioche is formed
18. The brioche should rise for at most 15 minutes before baking
19. Place on buttered paper and brush with beaten egg before putting in the oven
20. For finer brioche: use 3 liters flour to 1.5 liters milk; make starter from 4 dekaliters milk and 6 dekagrams softened cheese yeast; when well risen, salt flour, sweeten well, add 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks; prepare dough with remaining milk; knead 28 dekagrams butter in three portions using same method
21. For economical brioche: salt 1 liter fine flour in a bowl; in a pitcher whisk 1 whole egg, 1 egg yolk, and 1 tablespoon sugar; mix in 2 dekagrams cheese yeast dissolved in milk; add 4 dekaliters lukewarm good milk; pour this onto the flour and knead with right hand until dough is smooth
22. Now add 7 dekagrams pure cold lard and knead until hand is clean and dough forms small bubbles; cover with cloth and let rise in warm place
23. Divide the entire mass into 4 pieces on a flour-dusted board, knead them, and form two brioche from them

title: Jó Korai Tavaszi Eledel (Good Early Spring Dish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pea shoots
- young chicken or squab
- thin red roux
- fresh parsley
- ground black pepper
- paprika
- salted water
- sweet cream
- saffron
- nutmeg blossom
- fried bread cubes

## Directions

1. Roughly chop the pea shoots
2. Prepare a thin red roux
3. Cook the chicken or squab in salted water until done
4. Braise the chopped pea shoots in the roux for a few minutes
5. Dilute the roux with the broth from the chicken
6. Add the cooked meat and simmer for fifteen minutes
7. Before serving, pour in a glass of sweet cream
8. Pour into a serving bowl and sprinkle with saffron, nutmeg blossom, and fried bread cubes

title: Kabinet-pudding (Cabinet Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters boiled thick cream
- 21 dekagrams powdered sugar
- 3 grams powdered vanilla
- 10 egg yolks
- 4 deciliters good cream
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- candied cherries
- green almonds
- candied pear slices (half yellow, half red)
- large candied rowan berries

## Directions

1. When 7 deciliters of boiled thick cream has cooled, mix in 21 dekagrams powdered sugar, 3 grams powdered vanilla, and 10 egg yolks in a pot
2. Place on charcoal fire and stir constantly until it begins to thicken
3. Remove quickly from heat, pour into another pot, and continue stirring to prevent curdling
4. Let cool completely
5. Whip 4 deciliters of good cream on cracked ice with a whisk until stiff
6. Cool 4.5 dekagrams of dissolved isinglass completely
7. When the egg mixture is completely cold and thickened, fold in the whipped cream and isinglass so the entire mixture becomes foamy
8. Add more powdered sugar if needed
9. Coat the mold with fine fresh almond oil
10. Prepare candied cherries (half yellow, half red), cut pear slices into thin leaves and cut out circles and crescents with small cutters, also cut thin strips for stems, and prepare large candied rowan berries
11. Arrange a beautiful wreath of pear, rowan, and cherry around the mold's circumference
12. Cut green almonds into leaves and arrange a beautiful rose or star in the center with almond and red pear
13. Fill the mold halfway with foamy mixture
14. Place in cracked ice, cover the mold, and place ice on top so it freezes quickly
15. If too hard and fruit sinks, place remaining fruit (except rowan) in the center
16. Bury the mold well in ice and let freeze through
17. After 2-3 hours, dip the mold briefly in warm water and turn out

title: Kacsa Sülve (Roasted Duck)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Fatten the duck for at least one week before roasting
2. Pluck the feathers and scald with water
3. Carefully remove all remaining feathers
4. Always open the duck along the breast: remove the entrails
5. Clean the liver and gizzard and set aside
6. Salt inside and outside
7. Place in a roasting pan, add a little water
8. Put in a hot oven for one hour
9. Turn frequently and roast to a nice pink color
10. Prepare rice cake made with the liver and gizzard mixed in as a side
11. When serving, carve and arrange on a long platter; garnish with the liver-rice cake

title: Kajszinbaraczk Párban (Apricots in Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful yellow apricots
- sugar
- water
- 500 grams fruit requires 25 dekagrams sugar and 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Peel beautiful golden apricots, split them, and remove the pits
2. Calculate 25 dekagrams sugar for 500 grams fruit, cook with 1 glass water and clarify until it forms heavy drops, then cool
3. Arrange the apricots neatly in the jar, fill almost to the top with sugar, bind well and simmer for 2-3 minutes (per instructions 961)

title: Kajszinbaraczk-fagylalt (Apricot Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 40-45 large ripe apricots
- 0.5 kilogram sugar
- 7 deciliters water
- juice of 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Peel 40-45 large ripe apricots, split them, remove the pits, and place in a large soup bowl
2. Cook 0.5 kilogram of sugar with 7 deciliters of water until dissolved
3. Pour the hot mixture over the apricots, cover, and let stand for 24 hours
4. Strain through a sieve without pressing
5. Mix in the juice of 3 lemons
6. Treat it like the others

title: Kajszinbaraczk-Marmelád (Apricot Jam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram sugar
- one glass of water
- good soup apricots
- apricot syrup, same amount as sugar

## Directions

1. Cook half a kilogram of sugar with one glass of water, well clarified, until it threads.
2. Then pit good apricots, press them through a sieve, and add them to the clarified and cooled sugar, cooking over charcoal heat for a short while.
3. Test on a plate and if it does not run but quickly becomes gelatinous, it is good.
4. Then when cooled, pour into glasses as with the others.
5. The pitted apricots can be cut into small pieces and mixed directly into the sugar without sieving; for other purposes treat it as above; this latter version is even better for filled pastries.

title: Kajszinbaracзk-lepény angol tésztában (Apricot Pastry in English Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 280 grams fine flour
- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- salt
- 2 tablespoons broken sugar
- walnut-sized piece of butter
- 2 raw egg yolks
- 1 coffee cup fresh sour cream
- water if needed
- 280 grams clarified butter, chilled
- well-ripened apricots
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place 280 grams flour on a work surface
2. Press 3 hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve and add with salt and 2 tablespoons broken sugar, mixing well with flour
3. Make a well in the flour, add a walnut-sized piece of butter, 2 raw egg yolks, and a coffee cup fresh sour cream
4. Knead into dough, adding water if not moist enough; the dough should be firmer than strudel dough
5. Knead until it separates from your hands, fold together, cover with cloth, and let rest half an hour
6. Roll out to little-finger thickness
7. Work 280 grams chilled clarified butter until smooth, shape into a long sheet with your palm, and wrap in dough folding the dough over from all four sides as for butter dough
8. Cover and let rest 15 minutes; in summer place between two plates on ice
9. Roll out as thin as possible, fold together again, let rest, and repeat this process three times total
10. For the fourth rolling, roll to knife-blade thickness, cut 5 or 6 round leaves with a warm knife
11. Each leaf should be slightly smaller than your little finger in diameter
12. Make two-finger-width incisions of equal depth with a warm knife, brush incision edges with egg white, and fold the two sides up and together so the leaf looks woven into tabs
13. Brush with egg white and bake to beautiful roll color
14. One hour before baking, peel well-ripened apricots and cut in half at the center, or use canned apricots
15. Place on a large plate, sprinkle generously with sugar, turn several times
16. Strain the canned apricot liquid
17. When the leaves are beautifully baked, arrange apricots on the leaf up to the tabs, sprinkle well with sugar, and return to the oven for 8-10 minutes
18. Cook the apricot liquid with sugar in a pan, stirring frequently until it becomes light syrup
19. Arrange the filled leaves on a cloth-covered plate, largest leaf first and smallest last (so place the most beautiful apricots on the smallest leaf)
20. Coat the fruit with syrup using a knife, sprinkle the whole pastry with sugar, and serve warm

title: Kajszinbaraezkot Befőzni (Preserved Apricots - First Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- well-ripened apricots
- fifty-six dekagrams sugar
- one glass of water
- seventy dekagrams syrup

## Directions

1. Peel well-ripened apricots after removing the pits and press through a sieve. Cook them with fifty-six dekagrams of sugar and one glass of water. During cooking, continuously remove the scum to make it completely clean. When it begins to form heavy drops, add the syrup. For fifty-six dekagrams of sugar, add seventy dekagrams of syrup and cook quickly, always stirring well so it does not burn. When a test drop from the preserve does not run on a plate but stays in place, it is done cooking. Remove from heat and pour into jars.

title: Kajszinbaraezkot Befőzni Más Módon (Preserved Apricots - Second Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- best quality well-ripened apricots
- half kilogram apricots
- half kilogram finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the best quality well-ripened apricots and press through a sieve. For half kilogram of apricots, take the same amount of finely ground sugar and cook rapidly for half an hour in a brass preserving basin, then remove from heat and pour as hot as possible into jars. This preserve is particularly good for pastries that are filled after baking, as well as for carnival doughnuts. It has a beautiful appearance and perfectly clear flavor.

title: Kapor-mártás (Dill Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh dill, cleaned and washed
- rendered fat
- flour
- meat stock
- three to four tablespoons sour cream
- lemon juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Finely chop the clean dill as you would cabbage
2. Brown one tablespoon of flour in rendered fat
3. Add the dill to this
4. Thin with meat stock
5. When sufficiently cooked, add three to four tablespoons of sour cream
6. Cook a bit more and the sauce is ready
7. If you want to give this sauce a sour taste, cook it with a little lemon juice

title: Kaporna mártás (Caper Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- light brown roux
- ten to twelve decagrams capers
- good broth
- fresh cream

## Directions

1. Prepare a light brown roux
2. Coarsely chop the capers with a chopping knife and add to the roux
3. Braise for a few minutes
4. Loosen with a small spoonful of good broth
5. Add two to three spoons of fresh cream and cook well together

title: Kappan csigával (Capon with Snails)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- One capon
- Twenty-five snails
- Seven dekagrams anchovies
- One head red onion
- Fresh parsley, chopped finely
- Juice of one lemon
- Fourteen dekagrams butter, beaten until fluffy

## Directions

1. Cook the snails
2. Extract the snail meat from the shells
3. Mix snail meat with anchovies, red onion, parsley, lemon juice and fluffy butter, blending well
4. Stuff the capon with this mixture
5. Sew the capon closed so the stuffing does not fall out
6. Roast beautifully
7. Baste with sour cream and butter while roasting

title: Kappan főzelékkel (Capon with Vegetables)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a Styrian capon
- bacon slices
- strong beef broth
- eight white cauliflower rosettes
- salted water
- butter
- four large tomatoes
- meat hash filling
- eight pieces of good carrots
- capon broth
- three tablespoons butter sauce
- four egg yolks
- fresh butter
- half lemon juice

## Directions

1. After cleanest plucking, lard the capon
2. Place in a suitable pot, cover with bacon slices
3. Pour strong beef broth over it and braise slowly
4. Clean eight white cauliflower rosettes and cook tender in salted water with a little butter, then cover and set aside
5. Cut four large tomatoes in half, fill with meat hash, place in a pot
6. Pour beef broth over and place coals on top, braise until tender
7. Similarly cut eight pieces of good carrots neatly and braise tender with broth and sugar
8. Strain the capon broth and mix with three tablespoons butter sauce
9. Cook down to half
10. Beat well with four egg yolks, a piece of fresh butter, and juice of half a lemon
11. Heat through, strain into a small pot and keep warm
12. At serving time, cut the capon neatly into pieces
13. Arrange the vegetables around it and pour the sauce over

title: Kappan kagylócskákkal (Roasted Capon with Shellfish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a large capon
- salt
- yellow roux
- breadcrumbs
- anchovies
- fresh parsley
- lemon zest
- red onion
- two deciliters white wine
- fresh small clams

## Directions

1. Season the capon lightly with salt and prepare it
2. Roast until golden brown, but keeping it in a sauce
3. Prepare a yellow roux and toast breadcrumbs in it
4. Add finely chopped anchovies, fresh parsley, lemon zest, and red onion
5. Pour two deciliters of white wine over it
6. Cook together for a while
7. Rinse fresh small clams in wine
8. Add the roasted capon together with the clams
9. Cook together, transfer to a serving dish
10. Arrange the clams on top with the sauce

title: Kappan Kagylóeskákkal (Capon with Oysters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large capon
- salt
- yellow roux
- breadcrumbs
- anchovies
- parsley
- lemon zest
- red onion
- white wine
- fresh oysters

## Directions

1. Lightly salt a beautiful large capon and prepare it, then roast it brown while keeping it moist.
2. Make a yellow roux and fry a little breadcrumb in it, add several chopped anchovies, green parsley, lemon zest and a little red onion.
3. Pour two deciliters of white wine over and bring to a boil together for a time.
4. Rinse fresh oysters with wine, place them together with the roasted capon, bring to a boil together, dish it up and arrange the oysters with the sauce on top.

title: Kappan Szardellával (Capon with Anchovies)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- salt
- anchovies

## Directions

1. Clean the capon and salt
2. Let rest in salt for a time
3. Remove the bones from three anchovies and chop finely

title: Kappan szardellával (Capon with Anchovies)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Capon
- Salt
- Three anchovies
- Butter
- mixed well together

## Directions

1. Clean the capon and salt it
2. Let it rest in salt for a while
3. Remove the bones from three anchovies and chop finely
4. Mix finely with well-beaten butter
5. Coat the inside of the capon well with this mixture
6. Dry the capon and roast it
7. Baste frequently with butter
8. When beautifully golden, place on serving dish
9. Pour sauce underneath

title: Kappan Álkagyló-Mártásban (Capon in Mock Oyster Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- salt
- butter
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. When the capon is cleanly plucked, salt it, prepare it and roast it as above; then make a brown sauce with butter

title: Kappan álkagyló-mártásban (Roasted Capon in Mock Shellfish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a capon
- salt
- butter
- brown roux
- red wine
- meat broth
- six anchovies
- red carrot
- fresh parsley
- lemon zest
- crushed pepper

## Directions

1. Pluck the capon cleanly, season with salt, prepare and roast it as in the previous recipe
2. Prepare a brown roux with butter
3. Dilute half with red wine and half with broth
4. Finely chop six anchovies, red carrot, fresh parsley, and lemon zest
5. Add all to the roux with crushed pepper
6. Cook the sauce well
7. Add the capon and braise until tender
8. Serve

title: Kappan-tekeres (Capon Roll)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a stuffed, crumbly-fleshed capon
- seven dekagrams marrow
- seven dekagrams bacon
- two tablespoons fine herbs
- salt
- a handful of capers
- pickled tongue cut in pencil-like pieces
- equal amount of venison mushroom
- butter
- cloth
- acidic root vegetables
- aspic with cutout leaves

## Directions

1. After cleanest plucking, cut open along the back
2. With a sharp knife carefully remove the ribs from the flesh so the skin is not cut anywhere
3. Draw the legs into the body above the knee, develop the wings only to the first joint, similarly remove the rest
4. Spread the whole capon on a clean board and press as evenly as possible
5. Finely chop together seven dekagrams marrow, seven dekagrams bacon, two tablespoons fine herbs and a little salt
6. Pound into a paste, place in a bowl
7. Mix well with a handful of capers, the same amount of pickled tongue cut in pencil-like pieces, and an equal amount of venison mushroom
8. Spread on the capon skin, then roll it up in a cloth brushed with butter
9. Tie very securely and cook in acidic root vegetables for one and a half to two hours
10. Set aside in a cool place
11. For serving, remove the roll from the broth, unwrap it and cut into beautiful even slices
12. Arrange on a long platter and decorate with cutout aspic leaves

title: Kappanmell (Roasted Capon Breast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of a fine capon or young turkey
- one and a half bread rolls, skin
- two decilitres of milk
- nine dekagrams of butter
- three whole eggs
- salt
- mace
- carrot slices
- red onion

## Directions

1. Cut out the breast of a fine capon or young turkey. Grate the skin of one and a half bread rolls and cut into thin slices, moisten with two decilitres of milk, add nine dekagrams of butter, place over charcoal and stir continuously, beating in three whole eggs, and stew into a thick paste that should separate from the spoon, then let it cool.
2. Pound the cut capon breast with a little salt and mace finely together in a large mortar and press through a sieve.
3. Braise the rest of the capon in a pot with butter, a few slices of carrot and red onion until light golden, pour in a little broth from time to time, and when the meat is tender, remove the bones and cut into thin slices.
4. Put the forcemeat into a bowl and beat with five egg yolks until frothy, finally mix in the stiff snow of five egg whites and the cut meat, fill into a pot greased with butter, steam for one hour, then turn out and pour over it the broth that remained from braising the capon and has been cleared of all fat.

title: Kappanmell Osztrigával (Capon Breast with Oysters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- capon or young turkey breast
- oysters
- butter
- lemon zest
- lemon juice
- fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the capon or young turkey breast into small slices
2. Extract the oysters and place meat and oysters alternately in a buttered pot
3. Add finely chopped lemon zest and the juice of one or two lemons
4. Sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs and add a little butter
5. Place coals on the lid and braise slowly until very little sauce remains

title: Karfiol Összerakva (Assembled Cauliflower)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower, cleaned and chopped
- young chicken
- rice
- hard-boiled eggs, sliced
- salted water
- fat or butter
- breadcrumbs
- black pepper, ground
- fresh parsley, chopped
- paprika
- green onion
- sweet sour cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower and chicken separately in salted water
2. Cook the rice until tender
3. Grease a baking dish with fat or butter and coat with breadcrumbs
4. Layer the ingredients: cauliflower, rice with black pepper and parsley, chicken pieces, rice, egg slices, paprika and green onion, repeating until the dish is full
5. Sprinkle fat or butter and breadcrumbs between layers
6. Pour sweet sour cream over the top
7. Sprinkle with butter and breadcrumbs
8. Bake until fully cooked in the oven; serve in the baking dish

title: Karlsbadi Marhahús (Carlsbad Beef)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- about four and a half kilograms of top round beef
- salt
- bacon
- pickled tongue
- red onion
- four button mushrooms
- green parsley
- black pepper
- bacon slices
- lean ham slices
- two heads of red onion
- two carrots
- strong broth
- strong twine

## Directions

1. Pound a cleaned piece of top round beef until broad and salt it
2. Cut thin strips of bacon and pickled tongue
3. Finely mince red onion, four button mushrooms, and green parsley, add black pepper and mix well
4. Mix the minced bacon with this mixture
5. Remove the bones from the meat and make small holes in the inner side of the flattened meat without piercing through or damaging the outer side
6. Alternately stuff with the prepared bacon-tongue mixture, leaving a two-finger width gap between rows
7. Roll the meat tightly like a barrel and bind firmly with strong twine
8. Place bacon slices in a long pot, add one or two slices of lean ham, two heads of diced red onion and two carrots
9. Place the rolled meat on top and fill halfway with strong broth
10. Cover and braise for several hours, turning frequently and adding small amounts of broth as the sauce reduces
11. When the meat is completely tender, strain the sauce, remove the twine, and slice with a sharp knife
12. Arrange the slices on a dish, pour sauce over them, and garnish with small potatoes or mixed vegetables

title: Karlsbadi perecz (Carlsbad Pretzel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half kilograms of flour
- five deciliters of cold sour cream
- seven dekagrams of cheese yeast
- one tablespoon of crushed sugar
- salt
- thirty-five dekagrams of butter

## Directions

1. From one and a half kilograms of flour, five deciliters of cold sour cream, seven dekagrams of cheese yeast, and one tablespoon of crushed sugar, well mixed, prepare good firm sweet bread dough
2. Salt the dough
3. Once it has been kneaded until it separates from the hand, knead in thirty-five dekagrams of butter in pieces
4. Once all the butter is in and the dough is well kneaded, form small pretzels
5. Place these in a warm place and let them rise
6. Brush with melted butter
7. Bake in a cooled oven
8. These pretzels are very good with tea and coffee

title: Kartifiola vajasmártásban (Cauliflower in Butter Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- butter
- flour
- good broth
- sour cream
- nutmeg flower, finely ground

## Directions

1. Clean the cauliflower
2. If the florets are large, cut them into four pieces
3. Cook in salted water
4. Melt butter and brown two spoonfuls of flour in it
5. Thin with good broth, add two to three spoonfuls of sour cream and a small amount of finely ground nutmeg flower
6. Cut the cauliflower stem short and arrange the florets prettily in rose shape in the plate
7. Pour the well-cooked sauce over them and bring to a boil
8. When serving, be careful that the florets look beautiful
9. Can be served with breaded chicken or frog cutlets

title: Kartifiola vajban (Cauliflower in Butter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the cauliflower as described above
2. When cooked and drained, arrange the florets neatly in rose shape in the center of a warmed plate
3. Pour hot butter evenly over them
4. Serve quickly to the table
5. Can be garnished with golden-braised cabbage, which gives the dish a more beautiful color
6. Can also be served with breadcrumbs fried in butter

title: Kartifiola-, vagy virágos-káposztaleves (Cauliflower or Flowering Cabbage Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three medium-sized nice cauliflower heads
- beef stock
- butter
- flour
- nutmeg flower
- sour cream
- eggs
- bread

## Directions

1. Clean three nice medium-sized cauliflower heads, cut into equal pieces and cook in good strong beef stock
2. Heat a piece of butter in a pot, add two full spoons of cooking flour, bring to a slight boil, and pour the stock from the now soft cauliflower over it
3. Add little finely chopped nutmeg flower and two full spoons of sour cream
4. Before serving, place the cauliflower in the soup, thicken the soup with two egg yolks, and pour over fried bread and fried goose liver

title: Kartifiola-saláta (Cauliflower Salad)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful white cauliflower
- Salt
- Water
- Oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Use beautiful white cauliflower; pour boiling salted water over the whole rosettes
2. When they are sufficiently soft, strain off the water and rinse with fresh water so they remain beautifully white
3. Season lightly with salt, arrange neatly on a plate, and dress with vinegar and oil

title: Kaszás Lé (Smoked Pork Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked pork shoulder
