- Sour sour cream, a good amount
- Egg yolks, a few
- Wine vinegar
- Bay leaf

## Directions

1. Cut the smoked pork shoulder into pieces and cook in plenty of broth
2. When cooked, thicken with good sour sour cream, adding a few egg yolks
3. Sour with wine vinegar and add bay leaf

title: Kaszáslé (Pork Broth with Liaison)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three liters of water
- one kilo of fresh pork from the shoulder or leg
- salt
- three to four cloves of garlic
- four tablespoons of flour
- six tablespoons of good sour cream
- one pair of whole eggs
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Put three liters of water in a four-liter pot and add one kilo of fresh pork chopped into portions, either from the shoulder or leg
2. Add salt and three to four peeled cloves of garlic to the water
3. When the meat is very tender, strain the broth through a sieve to remove small bones that fell from the meat
4. Return the meat to the clean broth and prepare a liaison
5. In a bowl mix four tablespoons of flour, six tablespoons of good sour cream, and a pair of whole eggs, stirring well with the flour
6. When the liaison is smooth, slowly pour the hot meat broth into the bowl with the liaison
7. Return the broth to the pot and add enough vinegar to make it nicely sour
8. If desired, add finely chopped garlic after preparation
9. Keep in a warm place until serving, but do not boil for long as it may curdle

title: Kelkáposzta-Sarjak (Kale Sprouts)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kale sprouts
- salt water
- butter
- flour
- broth
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean the sprouts, wash them and cook in salted water
2. Drain and place in the prepared butter sauce and finish cooking with it
3. The sauce should cook well, especially if using this to garnish beef
4. Can also be prepared with sour cream

title: Keményítőliszt-eczet (Starch Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 kilograms of starch
- 350 kilograms of warm water
- 1 kilogram of malt flour
- 10 kilograms of water

## Directions

1. Mix 50 kilograms of starch with 350 kilograms of warm water
2. Cool to 55° R temperature
3. Mix in 1 kilogram of malt flour that has previously been mixed with 10 kilograms of water
4. Keep the mixture at 53° R temperature for 2.5 hours and dilute with water to vinegar

title: Kenyér-pép (Bread Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramms softened almonds
- 14 dekagramms sugar
- zest of one lemon, finely chopped
- ground cinnamon
- finely chopped citron
- 5 dekagramms good, not sour bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoons Tokay wine
- 10 dekagramms egg yolks
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Crush the softened almonds
2. Cook the sugar
3. Chop the lemon zest finely
4. Moisten the bread crumbs with Tokay wine
5. Stir all ingredients together with the egg yolks for half an hour
6. Beat the 4 egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in
7. Fill a butter-greased mold with the paste
8. Cook in a steam bath for three-quarters of an hour
9. Turn out and serve with wine sauce if desired

title: Kenyér-pép (Bread Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 140 grams softened almonds
- 140 grams sugar
- 1 lemon zest
- ground cinnamon
- candied citron
- 50 grams good unsour breadcrumbs
- 2 tablespoons wine (Menes)

## Directions

1. Grind the softened almonds
2. Cook the same amount of sugar
3. Chop the lemon zest finely
4. Add ground cinnamon, finely chopped candied citron, and good unsour breadcrumbs moistened with wine

title: Kenyérleves (Bread Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- good homemade bread, sliced very thinly
- soup fat or butter
- finely chopped onion
- good meat broth
- crushed pepper
- nutmeg
- egg per person
- sausage, chopped into small pieces
- finely chopped roots
- finely minced onion

## Directions

1. Cut good homemade bread into very thin slices
2. Heat soup fat or butter in a pot and warm it
3. Add finely chopped onion and bring to a boil
4. Throw in the sliced bread and lightly toast it similarly
5. Pour good meat broth over it
6. Add crushed pepper and nutmeg
7. Cook the soup well, being careful that the bread does not fall apart
8. Before serving, place one egg per person in the hot broth, which should not become hard
9. Add chopped sausage and finely cut roots
10. Sprinkle with finely minced onion

title: Kenyérleves más módon (Bread Soup, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- good homemade bread, sliced thinly
- strong meat broth
- butter
- egg per person

## Directions

1. Cook thinly sliced good homemade bread in strong meat broth
2. Strain the mixture through a sieve
3. Heat a piece of butter in a pot and pour the strained mixture into it
4. Bring to a boil
5. Poach one egg per person as soft-boiled eggs, taking care that the eggs remain whole
6. Warm the eggs in hot meat broth for a few minutes
7. Serve together with the bread soup

title: Kenyértorta (Bread Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams softened almonds
- the same amount of sugar
- one lemon, finely chopped zest
- knife-point amount of ground cinnamon
- two to three ground cloves
- ten dekagrams crumbs from well-baked, not sour black bread
- one to two coffee-spoonfuls of Málaga or Madeira wine
- one whole egg
- eight egg yolks
- four egg whites
- butter for coating the pan

## Directions

1. Finely grind the softened almonds, moistening slightly with a little water during grinding
2. Finely grind the same amount of sugar similarly
3. Prepare the finely chopped lemon zest, knife-point amount of ground cinnamon, and two to three ground cloves
4. Grate ten dekagrams of crumbs from well-baked, not sour black bread
5. Moisten the crumbs with one to two coffee-spoonfuls of Málaga or Madeira wine, making them only damp, not wet
6. Place all ingredients in a bowl, adding one whole egg and eight egg yolks one after another
7. Stir for half an hour until foamy
8. Gently mix in the stiff snow of four egg whites
9. Pour the mixture into a buttered pan or mold
10. Bake at not very high heat

title: Kerek, lapos kis szúróval kiszaggatott galuska (Round Flat Perforated Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter flour
- 1 liter crumbled curd cheese
- 4 eggs
- few tablespoons good sour cream
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix flour with crumbled curd cheese
2. Add four eggs, a few tablespoons sour cream, and salt, and knead into soft dough
3. Work quickly so the curd remains in pieces throughout the dough
4. On a well-floured board, roll out to half-finger thickness
5. Cut out with a small perforator (like a forint coin)
6. Boil in salted water until they float
7. Pour hot butter fried to a golden color over them
8. Sprinkle with red butter breadcrumbs and serve

title: Kifli-pép málnával leöntve (Kifli Paste with Raspberry Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 brittle kiflick
- 10 dekagramm softened and finely ground almonds
- 10 dekagramm ground sugar with vanilla
- 4 deciliters cream
- 7 dekagramm butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- citronade slices
- preserved sour cherry or medlar
- raspberry sauce

## Directions

1. Gently grate the crusts of 5 brittle kiflick and cut into slices.
2. Place in a pan with the almonds, sugar with vanilla, and cream.
3. Heat over coals, stirring constantly, until smooth paste forms.
4. Remove from heat and stir in 7 dekagramm butter while still warm, then let cool.
5. Once completely cold, beat in 6 egg yolks one after another.
6. Fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 4 eggs.
7. Butter a pan and line the bottom with white paper, then butter the paper again.
8. On the bottom of the pan, arrange a wreath of citronade slices, and inside arrange another wreath of preserved sour cherry or medlar pieces the size of a silver coin.
9. Carefully pour the paste over this and cook in a bain-marie for three-quarters of an hour.
10. If making the raspberry sauce from cooked fruit, clean it, press through cloth, and cook 4 deciliters of juice with 18 dekagramm sugar until the liquid is slightly thick.
11. Carefully turn the paste out onto a serving dish, carefully remove the paper lining, and pour the raspberry sauce over the bottom of the dish.

title: Kirakott hasábos tészta (Arranged Layered Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- brioche sliced thin
- cream
- butter
- currants
- soaked and separated almonds
- raisins
- pistachios
- 3 deciliter cream
- 10 dekagramm sugar
- vanilla
- 5 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pour cream over thin brioche slices and let them soak well
2. Butter a melon mold
3. Layer brioche pieces thickly sprinkled with currants
4. Layer brioche pieces arranged with soaked separated almonds
5. Layer brioche pieces with raisins
6. Layer brioche pieces with pistachios, then currants again, continuing until all the mold is filled with layers
7. Add a layer of soaked brioche sprinkled thickly with soaked finely ground almonds, sugar, and vanilla
8. Continue layering until the mold is full
9. Beat together three deciliter cream, ten dekagramm sugar, vanilla, and five egg yolks
10. Pour this mixture over the top and set in a water bath
11. Steam continuously for half an hour until the liquid evaporates
12. Carefully turn out and serve either plain or with cream sauce

title: Kirakott ászpik (Layered Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- aspic (prepared as in previous recipe)
- thinly sliced good Verona salami
- thinly sliced cold pheasant or woodcock, or turkey roast if unavailable
- thinly sliced ham

## Directions

1. Prepare aspic as in the previous recipe
2. Once it has set, pour an amount two-finger-widths thick into a basin and set on ice to gel
3. When set, arrange a layer of thinly sliced Verona salami to cover the bottom
4. Pour another two-finger-width layer of aspic over it and allow to gel
5. Arrange a layer of thinly sliced cold pheasant or woodcock, or turkey roast
6. Pour aspic over and let gel, then add a layer of thinly sliced ham
7. Pour aspic over and continue this process until the basin is full

title: Kis Muskatály-Körték Párban (Small Muscat Pears in Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- small muscat pears
- sugar
- water
- white vinegar
- 500 grams pears requires 25 dekagrams sugar, 1 glass water, and 2 tablespoons strong white vinegar

## Directions

1. For 500 grams of such small pears, take 25 dekagrams sugar
2. Clean the pears with a cloth and cut off half of the stem
3. Cook the sugar with 1 glass water until completely dissolved, add 2 tablespoons strong white vinegar and boil several times
4. Place the pears in, turn with a silver spoon, boil for a few minutes, then cool
5. Arrange the pears neatly in the jar, pour the liquid underneath, bind with white blotting paper and bladder, boil for 3 minutes, then cool completely in water

title: Kis Nádfánkok Tejszínhabbal (Small Reed Puffs with Whipped Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams not too hard butter
- 42 dekagrams flour
- salt
- 18 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks, sieved
- 2 more egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons cream
- 10 dekagrams soaked and well-dried almonds, finely chopped
- 10 dekagrams ground sugar
- egg white
- vanilla sugar
- sweet whipped cream

## Directions

1. Work the butter with the flour and salt on a board
2. Add the finely ground sugar, the 4 sieved hard egg yolks, and combine well
3. Add 2 more egg yolks and 2 tablespoons cream
4. Work everything into dough with a wide knife
5. Roll out to knife-blade thickness
6. Cut into strips two centimeters wide with a fluted cutter
7. Coat an ell-long metal form the width of half a bottle neck with butter
8. Wrap the strip around it so the second wrap is always on top of the first and nothing of the form shows
9. When all are rolled, mix the chopped almonds with ground sugar
10. Brush the puffs with egg white
11. Coat densely with almonds and sugar except on the side where the form lies
12. Coat the form with butter
13. Place the puffs on it
14. Bake to a nice roll color
15. Carefully remove from the form
16. Coat with vanilla sugar
17. Fill the opening with sweet whipped cream so the cream shows on both sides
18. Arrange neatly on a plate

title: Kocsonya muskatály-szölőbőL (Muscatel Grape Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe muscatel grapes
- juice of four lemons
- thirty-five dekagrams sugar
- four deciliters water
- four and one-half dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- finely crushed ice

## Directions

1. Remove the stem from ripe muscatel grapes
2. Extract juice from the berries by pressing through cloth until you have one liter of juice
3. Press the zest of four lemons through blotting paper several times
4. Cook thirty-five dekagrams of sugar with four deciliters of water until it forms heavy drops
5. Mix together with four and one-half dekagrams of dissolved isinglass and the other ingredients
6. Strain through table linen
7. Pour into a grape cluster-shaped mold that has been embedded in finely crushed ice
8. Allow to freeze for two hours, then carefully unmold onto a serving dish

title: Kocsonya muskatályborból (Muscatel Wine Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams sugar
- two deciliters water
- one-half liter muscatel wine
- juice of two lemons
- four and one-half dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Cook twenty-eight dekagrams of sugar with two deciliters of water until it forms large drops
2. Pour this into one-half liter of muscatel wine
3. Squeeze in the juice of two lemons
4. Add four and one-half dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
5. Strain through clean cloth as with other jellies
6. Pour into a mold, place on ice, and freeze
7. Prepare similarly from tokay or malaga wine, as well as from various liqueurs, especially good pineapple liqueur

title: Kocsonya nyers kávéból (Raw Coffee Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagrams best quality green coffee
- 7 deciliters hot water
- juice of 6 lemons
- 28 dekagrams sugar
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Pour 7 deciliters of hot water over 21 dekagrams of green coffee in a soup pot
2. Cover well and let stand for 1 hour
3. Strain through clean cloth
4. Add the juice of 6 lemons
5. Dissolve 28 dekagrams of sugar in the liquid
6. Add 4.5 dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
7. Strain through cloth
8. Pour into mold and freeze on ice

title: Kocsonya-befőttek levéből (Jelly from Preserved Fruit Juice)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters preserved fruit juice
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 4 deciliters water
- 3 lemons, for juice
- dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Cook 14 dekagrams of sugar in 4 deciliters of water, and as soon as the sugar is dissolved, pour it into the preserved juice and mix well
2. Squeeze the juice of 3 lemons into it
3. Fold in the dissolved isinglass and proceed as with other jellies
4. If the preserved juice is thick, take more water and pour the juice into the water sweetened with sugar once the sugar has dissolved, and boil for a few moments

title: Kocsonyázott Alma (Apple in Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- champagne
- water
- sugar
- isinglass
- cochineal juice
- preserved sour cherries

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and scoop out the centers slightly
2. Stew them in 2 parts genuine French champagne and 1 part water with 21 decagrams of sugar without letting them fall apart
3. When well softened, place on a prepared dish and put the approximately 7 deciliter sauce back on embers
4. Add 4.5 decagrams dissolved isinglass, or if you have ice, 3.5 decagrams is sufficient
5. Add cochineal juice for color, and strain the aspic through cheesecloth and blotting paper as usual
6. Fill the apples with whole preserved sour cherries and pour the aspic over them, being careful not to stain the apples, keeping them white
7. In the absence of good champagne, use only 7 deciliters of water, juice of 2 good lemons, and enough sugar to sweeten sufficiently
8. In summer, when fresh fruit is available, smaller apples can be used, and raspberry or currant syrup can be used instead of champagne

title: Kocsonyázott borjúfej (Jellied Calf's Head)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's head
- salt
- water
- green parsley
- carrots
- 2 heads red onion
- a little lemon zest
- whole pepper
- clove pepper
- 1 calf's foot
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the calf's head in half and cook it in salted water with green parsley, carrots, two heads of red onion, and a little lemon zest until it becomes very tender
2. Remove it and place it in cold water for a while
3. Cook the broth in which it was cooked with a little whole pepper and clove pepper and one calf's foot until it reduces to three deciliters
4. Add the juice of one lemon, strain through a sieve, and set the sauce aside slightly
5. Remove the bones from the calf's head and cut the meat into long pieces
6. Place into a mold, strain the sauce once more through clean cloth, pour it over the meat, and place in a cold location until it becomes jelly
7. Once jellied, turn it out

title: Kocsonyázott borjúfej (Jellied Veal Head)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal head
- salt
- water
- fresh parsley
- carrot
- 2 heads red onion
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cut the veal head in half down the middle
2. Cook it in salted water with fresh parsley, carrot, 2 heads red onion, and a little lemon zest until very tender
3. Remove it and place in cold water for a while

title: Kocsonyázott csuka vagy kecsege (Jellied Pike or Sterlet)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or sterlet
- salt
- white vinegar
- water
- red onion
- fresh parsley
- carrot
- lemon zest
- pepper
- cloves
- bay leaf
- thyme
- sturgeon bladder
- aspic
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Let the cleaned pike or sterlet lie in salt for an hour.
2. Pour one part white vinegar and three parts water into a pot, add red onion, fresh parsley, carrot, lemon zest, a few peppercorns, cloves, bay leaf and a little thyme.
3. Add the fish and two rings of beaten sturgeon bladder and cook until well done but not falling apart.
4. Then remove and cook the sauce until the sturgeon bladder dissolves.
5. Stretch a cloth over four legs of an upturned chair, pour the sauce over it and strain slowly so it is clear.
6. Place the fish on a plate, pour the sauce over it and place in a cold place so it becomes jelly.
7. Line the back of the fish entirely with aspic.
8. Hard boil two eggs, remove the yolks and press through a sieve; cut fine slices from the egg white and cut half-moons with a small cutter and decorate the entire fish as desired.

title: Kocsonyázott Kompót (Aspic Compote)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cream
- preserved peaches
- sugar
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Whip 7 deciliters of sour cream into foam and place on a sieve to drain well
2. Finely mash 3 spoonfuls of preserved peaches with 10 decagrams of finely pounded sugar, add the foam spoonful by spoonful
3. Mix in 1.5 decagrams dissolved isinglass

title: Kocsonyázott kompót (Jellied Compote)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 700 ml sour cream
- 3 tablespoons canned apricots
- 10 grams fine sugar
- 1.5 grams dissolved isinglass
- quince or Masánszki apple
- 3 oranges
- sugar
- water
- lemon

## Directions

1. Whip 700 ml sour cream into foam and place on a sieve to drain well
2. Mix 3 tablespoons canned apricots with 10 grams fine sugar, then add the foam little by little
3. Stir in 1.5 grams dissolved isinglass
4. Pour into a pan and freeze
5. Cook quince in water and sugar, or Masánszki apple with lemon, water and sugar
6. Prepare orange peel as described in the previous recipe
7. Peel orange clean, separate into segments and dip in concentrated sugar, then chill
8. Layer the compote at serving time
9. Top with jellied fruit cut into decorative shapes, arrange the orange peel neatly on top

title: Kocsonyázott kompót más módon (Jellied Compote, Alternate Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 180 grams fine sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 400 ml French champagne
- 1.5 grams dissolved isinglass
- several peeled quinces
- 2 medium quinces
- 140 grams sugar
- 1 glass water
- 2 oranges
- candied cherries
- greengage plums
- lemon

## Directions

1. Beat 180 grams fine sugar and 8 egg yolks with 400 ml French champagne in a pan
2. Place over charcoal fire and beat until thick and risen
3. Mix in 1.5 grams dissolved isinglass
4. Pour into a ring mold and place in broken ice
5. Slice peeled quinces and cut coin-sized round pieces from them
6. Cook 2 medium quinces with 140 grams sugar and 1 glass water until soft
7. Peel 2 oranges, cut into large cubes and sprinkle with sugar
8. Strain candied cherries and greengage plums from their preserving liquid
9. Once the sugar crown is frozen, turn it out into a prepared dish and immediately place on ice
10. Fill the ring opening by layering: quince, cherry, orange, and greengage plum
11. Prepare aspic from 2 oranges and 1 lemon, about 300 ml
12. Pour 2-3 spoonfuls of aspic over the fruit and let freeze
13. Add more aspic in layers, allowing each to freeze before adding the next
14. Once frozen solid, fill the entire opening with remaining aspic
15. Keep on ice until fully frozen and attractive in appearance

title: Kocsonyázott kompót párolt gyümölcsből (Jellied Compote from Stewed Fruit)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 grams isinglass
- water
- egg white
- 2 jars canned apricots
- sugar
- 1 jar small muscat pears
- lemon juice
- 1 jar Spanish cherries
- 1 jar autumn apricots

## Directions

1. Beat 40 grams isinglass well and tear into small pieces
2. Wash thoroughly in cold water and soak for several hours
3. Cook in 400-500 ml water until dissolved
4. Add fresh egg white during cooking, simmer for a few minutes, remove from heat
5. Let settle and strain the clear part through cloth
6. Divide into four parts and keep in a warm place
7. Select 12 finest apricots from a jar and place on a plate
8. Sprinkle well with crushed sugar and turn several times
9. Drain juice and sprinkle again so sugar penetrates well
10. Place remaining apricots with their juice in a bowl
11. Cook 140 grams sugar with 400 ml water until dissolved
12. Pour over apricots in bowl and let sit for several hours
13. Strain without pressing, add juice of a small lemon and one quarter of isinglass
14. Pass through stretched cloth
15. Put half in a pan, bury in ice and freeze
16. Once frozen, layer the sugared apricots on top
17. Pour remaining aspic over and freeze again
18. Select the finest pears from a jar and set aside
19. Place others in a bowl with 100 grams sugar and 200 ml water with half the pears
20. Once boiling, pour over the pears and let sit
21. Add juice of 1.5 lemons and another quarter of isinglass, strain through cloth
22. Pour over apricot layer and arrange pears same way as apricots
23. Select largest cherries from a jar and set aside on a plate
24. Sprinkle with sugar and strain
25. Place remaining cherries in a bowl with 140 grams sugar boiled in 100 ml water
26. Mix with another quarter of isinglass and strain through cloth
27. Pour over pear layer and arrange cherries
28. Select 12 finest autumn apricots on a plate
29. Sprinkle well with sugar and prepare like the first apricots
30. Add 1 lemon to the juice and remaining isinglass
31. Strain through cloth
32. Once cherry layer is frozen, pour and layer the final aspic
33. Note: the final aspic must be added slowly and only poured when already frozen to prevent fruit from floating up and mixing with other layers
34. Keep on ice until serving, use caution and speed when unmolding

title: Kocsonyázott rizs (Jelled Rice)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters good milk
- 14 dekagrams rice, well picked and cleanly washed
- 7 deciliters good heavy cream
- 4 egg whites
- 18 dekagrams sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. In a pot lined with butter, bring seven deciliters of good milk to a boil and cook fourteen dekagrams of well-picked and cleanly washed rice until soft
2. In a pot, beat seven deciliters of good heavy cream well with four egg whites
3. Place over gentle heat and stir until it comes to a slight boil
4. Add eighteen dekagrams of sugar and vanilla
5. Place the rice in a deep bowl, pour the mixture over it, mix well
6. Place on ice or in a cellar to freeze

title: Kocsonyázott sodó (Jellied Soda)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- 4 deciliters white old wine
- 2 dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- 21 dekagrams sugar rubbed on lemon

## Directions

1. In a pan, beat together the egg yolks, white old wine, dissolved isinglass, and sugar rubbed on lemon until foamy
2. Place on gentle coals and continue beating until it begins to thicken
3. Set the pan in cold water and continue beating until it cools slightly
4. Pour into a mold, freeze in a cold place, turn out onto a plate, and serve

title: Kocsonyázott Sürmű Leánysomból (Jellied Cornelian Cherry Preserve)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Cornelian cherries, stems removed
- water, slightly more than needed for cooking
- sugar, equal amount to the fruit

## Directions

1. Place the cornelian cherries, with stems removed, in a pot and pour over slightly more water than is necessary for them to cook.
2. Cook quickly over high heat so they retain their color, then press through a sieve.
3. Cook the sugar until it threads and forms heavy drops.
4. Take equal quantities of sugar and fruit; if you add the fruit to the sugar, it may only be brought to a boil a few times, then remove from the heat and when cooled, pour into glasses.

title: Kocsonyázott Sűrűié Bőralmából (Jellied Apple Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilogram sugar
- ½ kilogram apples
- water as needed

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the apples into pieces and cook, adding only as much water as is needed for cooking
2. When cooked, press through a thin cloth
3. Boil the sugar until it threads and forms large bubbles while boiling
4. Add the strained apple juice to the sugar and bring to a boil for a few minutes
5. Cool and pour into glasses

title: Kocsonyázott Sűrűié Leánysomból (Jellied Cloudberry Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cloudberries with stems removed
- equal amount of sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Place the cloudberries in a pot with slightly more water than needed for cooking
2. Boil rapidly over high heat so the color is preserved
3. Press through a sieve
4. Boil the sugar until it threads and throws heavy drops
5. Take equal amounts of fruit and sugar
6. When the fruit is added to the sugar, bring to a boil only a few more times
7. Remove from heat, cool, and pour into glasses

title: Kocsonyázott Sűrűié Ribizliből (Jellied Redcurrant Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ kilogram sugar
- ½ kilogram redcurrants, picked
- 2 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar in 2 deciliters of water until it threads, forming large bubbles while boiling
2. Wash the redcurrants and place on a fine-mesh sieve so the water drains off and they dry
3. When the sugar is ready, add the redcurrants, stir, bring to a boil two or three times
4. Pour onto a clean sieve standing over a soup bowl and let flow slowly without pressing through
5. Pour into glasses where it will gel
6. Prepared the same way from raspberries

title: Kocsonyázott tej piskótával bélelve (Jelled Cream Lined with Ladyfingers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Round, flat mold
- Ladyfinger biscuits
- 1 liter good heavy cream
- 18 dekagrams finely broken sugar
- 3 grams finely broken vanilla
- 1.25 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Line a round flat mold all around and on the bottom with beautiful ladyfingers, with the shiny side of the biscuit facing the mold wall
2. Line the openings with a double row so the cream cannot break through anywhere
3. Whip one liter of good heavy cream into stiff foam
4. When the entire mass has been whipped into foam, mix in the finely broken sugar and finely broken vanilla
5. Mix in the dissolved isinglass once it has cooled and been strained
6. Fill the lined mold with this mixture
7. Set on finely broken ice
8. When it has frozen solid, dip the mold for a few moments in hot water, dry with a cloth, carefully unmold with a knife, and turn out onto a plate

title: Kocsonyázott tejszín és csokoládéhab szeletekben (Jelled Cream and Chocolate Foam in Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Two tin molds
- Fresh almond oil
- Quince paste, cut into small cubes
- Green almonds
- 7 deciliters good heavy cream
- 25 dekagrams vanilla sugar
- 1.25 dekagrams dissolved and cooled isinglass
- One large tablet of Milan chocolate
- Quince paste slices preserved in sugar (alternative)

## Directions

1. Line two tin molds very thinly with fresh almond oil
2. Thickly scatter small cubes of quince paste and green almonds in one of the molds
3. Set seven deciliters of good heavy cream on crushed ice in a beating pan and whip it with a whisk into stiff foam
4. Mix in the vanilla sugar and the dissolved and cooled isinglass
5. Soften one large tablet of Milan chocolate in a pan by placing it in a warm but not hot oven
6. Quickly beat the softened chocolate with a new wooden spoon
7. Mix it with half of the cream foam
8. Pour it into the mold not scattered with fruit, shake well, and cover tightly with the lid
9. Wrap in parchment paper and set on crushed ice
10. Mix the pieces of quince paste and almond remaining with the second half of the foam along with any juice that settles from them
11. Pour this into the scattered mold, cover similarly, wrap, and bury in ice for a few hours
12. At serving time, remove the molds, wipe clean, dip quickly into hot water, wipe again, unmold onto a very clean board, and cut into slices as thick as a knife blade with a sharp knife
13. Cover the plate with a cloth and pile the slices high in alternating arrangement

title: Kocsonyázott torta (Jellied Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 grams crushed sugar
- Lemon juice
- Orange juice
- 28 grams peeled and sliced Masansky apples
- Rind of 1 orange
- Half a lemon rind
- 7 grams pistachios
- 7 grams blanched and swelled almonds
- 7 grams candied citronpeel
- 14 grams candied walnuts
- Wafer sheets
- Water

## Directions

1. Cook 42 grams of crushed sugar with lemon and orange juice instead of water in a brass pan until it threads
2. Add 28 grams of peeled and sliced Masansky apples to the sugar, constantly stir it while boiling so it becomes a paste without burning
3. Remove from heat and add the rind of 1 orange and half a lemon cut into large cubes, 7 grams of pistachios, the same amount of blanched and swelled almonds, the same amount of candied citronpeel, and 14 grams of candied walnuts, all coarsely chopped, and mix well
4. Line the cake leaf with wafers, make a ring around it and coat this as well with wafers, and fill with the jelly, which should be at least three fingers high

title: Kolbász (Sausage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pork
- salt
- ground pepper
- marjoram
- lemon zest
- water
- beef or pork casing
- pork fat

## Directions

1. Finely chop pork meat, salt it, season with ground pepper, marjoram and lemon zest
2. Dilute this thick mixture with water and place in a sausage stuffer, attach beef or pork casing to the end
3. Quickly fill the sausage in this manner and fry it in pork fat

title: Kolbász páczolt hagymával (Sausage with Pickled Onion)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven to nine white-skinned red onions
- goose fat
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- broth
- five pairs sausages
- fried breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Clean the onions and cut them into large pieces.
2. Heat a few spoonfuls of goose fat and add the onions with salt and pepper.
3. Braise them slowly over gentle coals until they become golden yellow.
4. Sprinkle flour over them and braise a little longer with the flour.
5. Pour a cup of broth over them and cook until the sauce is completely reduced.
6. Fry five pairs of sausages so that they brown nicely but do not overcook.
7. Add the sausages to the braised red onions.
8. Braise together for fifteen minutes and place in a tureen.
9. Border the edge with fried breadcrumbs cut into large triangles.

title: Kolozsvári Töltött Káposzta (Kolozsvár-Style Stuffed Cabbage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 heads of fermented head cabbage
- 1/2 kilo pork loin
- 1/2 kilo beef brisket
- 1/4 kilo bacon for larding
- 2 deciliters rice
- 1 whole egg
- 1/4 teaspoon ground pepper
- salt to taste
- 4 fillér worth of whole peppercorns
- 1/4 kilo smoked bacon
- 1 pig's ear
- 1/2 kilo pork with skin
- 1/2 kilo beef sirloin
- white wine (small glass if needed)
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Use fermented head cabbage, not shaved cabbage, as it has better flavor
2. Remove the leaves from 3 or 4 heads of fermented cabbage and trim the core from the back of each leaf
3. Set aside the thinner leaves and chop the remaining leaves thinly
4. Prepare the filling: finely chop 1/2 kilo pork loin and 1/2 kilo beef brisket
5. Finely chop 1/4 kilo bacon for larding
6. Combine the meat and bacon, then add 2 deciliters rice, 1 whole egg, 1/4 teaspoon ground pepper, and salt to taste
7. Mix the filling well by hand
8. Place filling in the reserved thin cabbage leaves, roll, and tuck in the ends
9. In one roll, wrap about 4 fillér worth of whole peppercorns and tie with string so it doesn't fall apart
10. In a 7-8 liter earthenware pot, layer 1/4 kilo smoked bacon on the bottom
11. Add a layer of chopped cabbage
12. Place on top: 1 pig's ear, 1/2 kilo pork with skin, and 1/2 kilo beef sirloin (these must be boiled first, skimmed, strained through a sieve, and the broth added)
13. Layer cabbage again, then the cabbage rolls, then cabbage until the pot is full
14. Pour water to cover the cabbage
15. If the cabbage is not sour enough, add a small glass of good white wine
16. Bring to heat and cook until tender, shaking and turning occasionally
17. When cooked, move to a cold place, covered with a sieve, until the next day
18. Reheat the next day
19. For serving: arrange the cabbage on the bottom of the dish, arrange the stuffed cabbage rolls, meat, and bacon on top
20. Pour the cabbage liquid into a pot, mix with sour cream, bring to a boil, and pour over the arranged cabbage
21. If cabbage remains in the pot, it can be reheated on the third day

title: Komló-élesztő (Hop Yeast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- A handful of very good hops
- 1 kilogram of water
- 1 egg white
- 25 dekagrams wheat flour

## Directions

1. Boil a handful of very good hops with 1 kilogram of water until it can be mixed with 1 egg white and 25 dekagrams of wheat flour

title: Kompót almapéppel (Compote with Apple Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 8-9 good quality apples
- 180 grams sugar

## Directions

1. Peel 8-9 apples, remove cores and chop coarsely

title: Kompót almapéppel (Compote with Apple Puree)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight to nine good quality apples
- Eighteen dekagrams sugar
- Stewed Spanish cherries with syrup and sufficient sugar
- Stewed apricots
- Ringlot plums

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, remove their cores, and cut them into rough pieces
2. Cook them in a pot with eighteen dekagrams of sugar, stirring frequently, until they form a thick puree, then cool
3. Spread the puree in the middle of a compote cup in the shape of a sugar loaf using a wide knife
4. Surround it with beautifully stewed Spanish cherries whose syrup has been simmered with sufficient sugar
5. Decorate the puree neatly with stewed apricots and ringlot plums, placing the most beautiful ones on top

title: Kompót aszalt meggyből (Compote with Dried Cherries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven deciliters dried and fresh cherries, washed
- Fourteen dekagrams sugar
- One glass white wine
- Two glasses water
- A little lemon or orange peel
- Juice of one orange
- Orange peel, chopped into long strips

## Directions

1. Cook the washed dried and fresh cherries with fourteen dekagrams sugar, one glass of white wine, two glasses of water, and a little lemon or orange peel until they soften
2. Remove them with a perforated spoon and pile them on a plate
3. Continue cooking the syrup for a while longer
4. Squeeze the juice of one orange into it, strain through cloth, and pour over the cherries
5. Place in a cold location
6. Before serving at table, sprinkle with finely chopped orange peel
7. Dried plums can be prepared the same way, using excellent quality fruit

title: Korhelyleves (Drunkard's Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- small amount of cabbage
- water
- small meat slices or smoked sausage cut into rings
- flour for roux
- broth
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place small amount of cabbage in a pot, cover generously with its own liquid and a little water
2. Add small meat slices or smoked sausage cut into rings
3. When cooked, make a thin roux with plenty of broth, keeping the sour taste
4. Season lightly with pepper
5. Serve the soup hot

title: Korhelyleves (Drunkard's Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- water
- smoked sausage cut into rings
- smoked meat cut into small slices
- thin roux
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Put cabbage in a pot with its own liquid and a little water
2. Add the smoked sausage rings and smoked meat slices
3. When cooked, make a thin roux so there is plenty of broth
4. Season lightly with pepper, keeping the sour taste
5. Serve hot

title: Korhelyleves más módon (Drunkard's Soup Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- fat
- red onion
- milk
- paprika
- sour cream
- broth
- rice
- fish
- frankfurter sausage

## Directions

1. Cook the cabbage
2. When soft, fry fat and chopped red onion in a pan with milk without browning
3. Add enough paprika to make it strong enough
4. Pour in the cabbage and sour cream and make as much soup as needed
5. Boil everything well together
6. Add rice and fish, as well as frankfurter sausage cut into pieces

title: Korhély-leves még más módon (Drunken Soup Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- One winter cabbage head
- Lard, a small amount
- Flour, a small amount
- Meat broth
- Egg yolks, six to eight
- Sour cream, a small amount
- Lard, a small amount
- Bread slices
- Roasted capon and chicken breasts, finely cut
- Veal and mutton

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves from a winter cabbage head and rinse well, then chop finely
2. In a copper pan, melt a small amount of lard, add a small amount of flour and then the cabbage, which should be braised gently
3. Transfer everything to a pot and fill with meat broth
4. Thicken by mixing in six to eight egg yolks, add a small amount of sour cream and lard
5. Cook slowly over moderate heat, being careful not to let the soup become too thick
6. When serving, add bread slices, finely cut pieces from roasted capon and chicken breasts, and veal and mutton

title: Korpa-torta (Bran Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagramm softened almonds, sliced
- 21 dekagramm almonds, cleaned and ground with skin
- 28 dekagramm ground sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 egg white, stiffly beaten
- 2 more egg yolks
- 1 more egg white, stiffly beaten
- 2 tablets grated chocolate
- 7 dekagramm citron peel, cut lengthwise
- butter for greasing
- orange icing
- green fruit for decoration

## Directions

1. Slice the softened almonds, clean the almonds with a cloth and grind with skin
2. Put in a bowl with ground sugar
3. Add 2 egg yolks and the stiffly beaten white of 1 egg and stir for a while
4. Then mix in 2 more egg yolks and the stiffly beaten white of 1 more egg, and continue this way until foamy
5. Add 2 tablets grated chocolate, the sliced almonds, and 7 dekagramm of lengthwise-cut citron peel
6. Grease the cake sheet with the hoop together with butter, fill in the mixture and place immediately in the oven so the almonds and citron do not settle
7. Bake for three-quarters of an hour with gentle but sustained heat
8. Cover with orange icing and decorate with green fruit

title: Kossuth-Kifli (Kossuth Crescent)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 dekagrams butter
- 25 dekagrams powdered sugar
- egg yolks
- 25 dekagrams light flour
- 3 egg whites
- grated lemon peel
- 1 egg white for brushing
- chopped almonds
- vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 25 dekagrams butter and 25 dekagrams powdered sugar with egg yolks until frothy.
2. Slowly add 25 dekagrams light flour.
3. Add the stiffly beaten whites of 3 eggs.
4. Add grated lemon peel.
5. Pour into a 25 cm square pan, spread with a knife, brush with 1 egg white, sprinkle with chopped almonds, and bake carefully.
6. Keep it pale, do not brown.
7. When done, cut into crescent shapes with a pastry cutter.
8. Place on a sieve and when cooled, arrange on a plate and sprinkle with vanilla sugar.

title: Koszorúk Mandolatejből (Crowns of Almond Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters milk
- 14 dekagrams almonds, soaked and ground
- 14 dekagrams rice
- sugar to taste
- 1 or 2 sheet cakes
- butter
- beaten eggs
- fine breadcrumbs
- fat for frying
- sugar
- preserved cherries

## Directions

1. Bring 7 deciliters of good milk to a boil, place 14 dekagrams of soaked and finely ground almonds in a soup pot, pour hot milk over, and let stand for 15 minutes.
2. Press through a clean linen cloth.
3. Prepare 14 dekagrams rice by cleaning it, rubbing well with a cloth, and grinding very finely in a mortar.
4. Bring the almond milk to a boil, add the rice with enough sugar to taste, and cook to a thick paste with continuous stirring.
5. Butter 1 or 2 sheet cakes and spread the hot paste with a wide knife dipped in milk to the thickness of a small finger.
6. Cool, then dust a board with flour and sugar, warm the cake slightly on the bottom, and unmold the rice.
7. Cut out circles with a medium donut cutter, then cut the center of each ring with a smaller cutter to form a crown.
8. Brush with beaten eggs, coat with fine breadcrumbs, and fry in fat to a bread-brown color.
9. When removed from fat, sprinkle thickly with sugar.
10. Arrange on a plate covered with a kitchen cloth in three rows in a cross pattern, placing a preserved cherry in the opening of each crown and serve hot.

title: Krém-kádka (Cream Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters cream
- 6 egg yolks
- sugar
- vanilla
- 4 to 6 pitted cooked cherries or stewed cherries in sugar
- gold leaf paper

## Directions

1. Line beautiful egg-shaped cake molds with shortcrust pastry
2. Fill with gold leaf paper shaped into a ball like the mold shape
3. Bake until golden brown in the oven
4. Meanwhile, prepare cream custard by mixing cream, egg yolks, sugar, and vanilla in a pot, stirring constantly over moderate heat until thickened
5. Set aside
6. When cakes are baked, remove the paper ball
7. Place 4 to 6 cherries in each cake that have been cooked in sugar and drained well
8. Fill the cakes with the cream custard
9. Return to the oven until the custard hardens
10. When serving, turn out onto a plate, sprinkle with vanilla sugar, and arrange on a platter so the cakes resemble bowls filled with cream custard

title: Kucsmagomba fasirozva (Puffball Mushroom Farce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- parsley
- puffball mushrooms
- 28 decagrams veal meat
- salt
- half a roll
- milk
- sour cream
- two egg yolks
- bread crumbs
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. In melted butter, braise finely chopped parsley, a handful of carefully washed puffball mushrooms, and 28 decagrams of veal meat
2. Once done, chop finely, salt, add half a roll soaked in milk and squeezed out, pound everything finely in a mortar, then add one or two tablespoons of sour cream and two egg yolks
3. Salt large puffball mushrooms and let them lie in salt for a while after washing them very thoroughly, dry them, remove the stem, fill with the minced mixture, attach the stem back, and place inside
4. In a pot, add finely chopped butter and a little green parsley, place the mushrooms on top, cover with a few pieces of fresh butter, sprinkle with bread crumbs, add a few more small pieces of butter, and bake slowly in the oven

title: Kucsmagombaleves (Mushroom Cap Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked veal innards
- parsley
- bread roll
- milk
- seven dekagrams butter
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- mace
- pepper
- one whole egg
- three egg yolks
- flour for dough
- egg white
- lard

## Directions

1. From cooked veal innards remove the larynx, spleen and membrane
2. Chop the rest finely with parsley and milk-soaked bread
3. Melt seven dekagrams of butter
4. Add a handful of breadcrumbs and fry until golden
5. Add the chopped mixture with salt, mace and a little pepper
6. Braise slightly
7. Beat in one whole egg and three egg yolks
8. Quickly stir and remove from heat
9. Continue stirring until eggs are well combined
10. Prepare a crumbly dough for the mushroom stems
11. Roll out to the thickness of a knife back
12. Cut into cubes two finger widths wide
13. Roll these onto the wooden spoon handle
14. Brush slightly with egg white to hold together
15. Shape mushroom caps from the chopped mixture above the stems
16. Roll in beaten egg and then breadcrumbs
17. Fry in lard
18. Place in soup bowl and pour brown broth over them

title: Kucsmagombaleves (Mushroom Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- green parsley
- butter
- flour
- pea broth
- salt
- sour cream
- toasted breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Wash the mushrooms several times until completely clean.
2. Stew them in butter with finely chopped green parsley.
3. Add a spoonful of flour and stew again.
4. Pour in as much pea broth as needed for soup.
5. Salt it and cook well.
6. Before serving, add a spoonful of sour cream and pour over toasted breadcrumb slices.

title: Kádka Töltelékkel (Pastry Case with Filling)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal or other leftover roast pieces
- bread roll, crust scraped off and soaked in milk
- green parsley, chopped
- lobster tail
- salt
- butter
- 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks
- lard pastry dough

## Directions

1. Chop veal or other leftover roast pieces finely.
2. Scrape the crust from half a bread roll, soak in milk, and squeeze dry.
3. Add finely chopped green parsley, lobster tail, and salt.
4. Beat butter until fluffy and mix in the above ingredients with 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks, stirring until it becomes a paste.
5. Prepare a lard pastry (see pastry section below) and roll it out as finely as lasagna dough.
6. Form flat pastry cases, fill with this stuffing, cover with the same dough, and bake in the oven until beautifully browned.
7. Turn the pastry cases out onto a dish and serve.

title: Kályha-fénymáz (Stove Polish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagrams benzoin resin
- 18 dekagrams storax
- 3 dekagrams laudanum resin
- 2 dekagrams turpentine
- rose oil
- clove oil
- lavender oil

## Directions

1. Powder 21 dekagrams benzoin resin, 18 dekagrams storax, and 3 dekagrams laudanum resin
2. Mix with 2 dekagrams turpentine
3. Place in a container, then place this container in another container filled with hot water
4. Allow to remain until the materials have melted
5. When the mixture is completely liquid, add rose oil, clove oil, and lavender oil

title: Káposzta-saláta szalonnával (Cabbage Salad with Bacon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender red head cabbage
- Salt
- Diced bacon
- White vinegar

## Directions

1. Take tender red head cabbage, remove the coarse ribs from the leaves, press the leaves together firmly, and slice them as finely as possible
2. Cut a piece of bacon into small cubes and fry it in a pan until beautifully golden
3. Remove the bacon and pour white vinegar over it, then bring it to a boil
4. Place the sliced cabbage in a basin, salt it, and pour the hot vinegar over it
5. Cover well and let it stand for a while
6. Strain off the vinegar, bring it to a boil again, and pour it over the cabbage as before, repeating this process once or twice more
7. Place on a warm plate, top with the fried bacon, add a little vinegar, and serve

title: Káposztás haluska (Cabbage Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Dumplings as described in recipe 526
- Cabbage, finely chopped
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare dumplings as described in recipe 526
2. Braise finely chopped cabbage in butter
3. When lightly browned, season lightly with salt and pepper
4. Mix with the dumplings and serve

title: Káposztás étek (Cabbage Dish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cabbage, three handfuls
- fat, one good spoonful
- rice groats, twenty-eight dekagrams
- water
- salt
- spices
- beef or pork, half kilogram
- sour cream, seven decilitres

## Directions

1. Place the sour cabbage in fat and cover it so it steams, but stir it frequently so it does not stick. In another pot, place the rice groats in fat and dilute with a little water, braise until it bursts. By the time the cabbage is soft and the rice is cooked, half a kilogram of either beef or pork should be cooked. Strain its broth (which can be used for other purposes) and chop the meat finely, keeping its salt and spices to make it tasty. Add salt and spices to the rice as well. Now prepare all three components together—the rice, cabbage, and meat—along with seven decilitres of good sour cream.

title: Káposztás-rétes (Cabbage Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 firm head of cabbage
- salt
- broken sugar
- lard
- chopped red onion
- pepper

## Directions

1. Slice and salt a firm head of cabbage, chop finely
2. Fry broken sugar in lard until browned
3. Add a little chopped red onion and fry
4. Add the cabbage from which you have first squeezed out the liquid, cook until red, season with pepper, and set aside to cool
5. Scatter thinly on half the dough, gently roll together, brush the top with fat, and bake until crispy

title: Káposztás-rétes más módon (Cabbage Strudel, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- chopped or grated salted cabbage
- hot water
- pepper
- sugar

## Directions

1. This version is only fine if the cabbage for filling is not fried in fat
2. Pour boiling water over the chopped or grated salted cabbage using a stick, and when half-cooled, pour onto a sieve so it drains well
3. Wring out thoroughly so not a drop of water remains
4. Spread on a flat plate
5. Scatter generously over the rolled and well-dried strudel dough with this white cabbage and sprinkle generously with peppered sugar
6. Roll together into strudel, brush the top with egg, bake at high heat to beautiful brown
7. This strudel is light, not fatty, and very fine

title: Kártya piskóta-tésztából (Playing Cards from Sponge Cake Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cake dough (as per recipe 673)
- butter
- common dough
- raspberry or apricot marmalade
- lemon or orange preserve

## Directions

1. Make sponge cake dough as described under recipe 673, which makes particularly good and beautiful cards
2. Spread the dough onto a baking sheet coated with butter to a knife-blade thickness
3. Make a border around the edge of the sheet from common dough so the sponge cake does not flow out during baking
4. Bake at gentle heat
5. When the dough has cooled, cut into small playing card shapes with a sharp knife
6. Fill half of the cards with raspberry or apricot marmalade
7. Place the remaining leaves on top nicely
8. Dip the cards quickly into lemon or orange preserve using two forks, place on white paper, and as quickly as possible before the preserve hardens, arrange them with pre-made suit and heart shapes
9. This way make one eye, two eyes, three eyes up to ten eyes

title: Kásás Bélés (Millet-Filled Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half kilograms flour
- one tablespoon lard
- two egg yolks
- salt water
- hot lard for brushing
- millet porridge cooked with pepper and plenty of lard, cooled

## Directions

1. Prepare a pastry dough with flour, lard, egg yolks, and salt water (or milk can be used instead of water for a less crusty dough)
2. Knead well until smooth, then stretch it out and let it rest
3. After resting, stretch it out on the table and brush with hot lard, then stretch all the remaining dough layers on top of each other
4. Divide into four parts and fold the parts on top of each other, then cut the resulting thick dough into ten-centimeter square pieces
5. Fill each square with a walnut-sized piece of cooled millet porridge, gather all four edges of the dough together to form small rounded filled pastries, pressing them slightly flat so the porridge spreads inside
6. Bake in a hot oven until beautifully golden—they should bake quickly and rise well

title: Kásás Hurka (Millet Sausage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pork fat layer and fat bed
- pork skin
- cooked millet porridge
- salt
- black ground pepper
- cooked red onion
- marjoram
- hot water

## Directions

1. Use the pork fat layer and fat bed, briefly boil them, and chop finely along with plenty of boiled skin
2. Mix an equal amount of cooked millet porridge with the meat and fat
3. Add proper salt, plenty of ground black pepper, cooked red onion as in liver sausage, and plenty of marjoram
4. Add a few spoonfuls of hot water to make the filling tender and easy to fill with a sausage filler
5. Secure both ends of the sausage with small wooden sticks and boil in hot salted water
6. To test if the sausage is cooked through, pierce it with a straw—if clear fat (not red liquid) comes out, the sausage is done and can be removed from the hot water

title: Kávé-fagylalt (Coffee Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams roasted coffee
- 7 deciliters hot cream
- 21 dekagrams sugar
- 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Place 14 dekagrams of roasted coffee in a soup pot and pour 7 deciliters of hot cream over it as soon as the coffee is added
2. Cover and let stand for half an hour
3. Strain into a pot into which you have already beaten 21 dekagrams of sugar well with 8 egg yolks
4. Place over charcoal fire and stir continuously until it thickens
5. Pour into another pot and stir for a while longer until cooled
6. Treat it like other ice creams

title: Kávé-hab (Coffee Foam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- very strong coffee
- 1 liter heavy cream
- 18 dekagrams sifted sugar
- 2 tablespoons strong black coffee
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- Spanish doughnuts for garnish

## Directions

1. Brew very strong coffee and filter it through fine cloth when completely clear
2. Add sugar generously and set on ice
3. Whip 1 liter of excellent heavy cream in a basin on ice until it becomes foam
4. Add sifted sugar to the basin, add 2 tablespoons of strong black coffee, and beat together
5. Gradually add the whipped cream
6. If the foam is too white, add more coffee so it becomes dark yellow with prominent coffee flavor
7. Finally, fold in the dissolved, cooled, and strained isinglass
8. Pour into a mold and set on ice
9. Before serving, turn out onto a plate and arrange neatly with Spanish doughnuts

title: Kávé-parfait (Coffee Cream Frozen Dessert)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagrams finest mocha coffee
- 1.4 liters very hot sweet cream
- 42 dekagrams finely crushed sugar
- 16 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Roast 42 dekagrams finest mocha coffee to a light brown.
2. Immediately pour it into 1.4 liters very hot sweet cream straight from the roaster, cover well, and allow to cool.
3. In the meantime, thoroughly blend 42 dekagrams finely crushed sugar with 16 egg yolks.
4. Slowly pour the strained coffee cream into the eggs.
5. Strain and beat again with a whisk until cooled.
6. Freeze in the mold to a butter-soft consistency.

title: Kávé-pép (Coffee Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams coffee
- 7 deciiliters cream
- 3 large cooking spoonfuls flour
- 10 dekagrams butter
- 8 egg yolks
- powdered sugar to taste
- 4 egg whites beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Roast the coffee well and place it hot into 7 deciiliters of hot cream
2. Cover well and let stand for half an hour so the cream absorbs the coffee aroma
3. Place 3 large cooking spoonfuls of flour in a pot, strain the cream through fine sieve onto the flour and mix well
4. Place on coals and stir until it begins to boil
5. Cook for a few more minutes and then cool
6. Beat 10 dekagrams of butter until foamy
7. Mix in the cooled paste and stir well with 8 egg yolks
8. Add powdered sugar to taste
9. Stir for another half hour
10. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
11. Place the paste in a buttered torte wrapper and bake for half an hour
12. Remove the ring, sprinkle with finely powdered sugar and serve quickly to the table

title: Kávé-pép más módon (Coffee Paste, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- strong coffee
- 7 deciiliters cream
- 9 dekagrams butter
- 4 cooking spoonfuls fine flour
- powdered sugar to taste
- 1 whole egg
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Mix strong coffee with cream in the proportion of 2 cups coffee to 7 deciiliters cream
2. Melt 9 dekagrams of butter in a pot and mix in 4 cooking spoonfuls of fine flour
3. Gradually add the hot coffee cream while stirring constantly, making a smooth paste; add the coffee gradually
4. When well cooked, cool it
5. Mix in powdered sugar to taste
6. Add 1 whole egg and 5 egg yolks one after another
7. Finally fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
8. Butter a mold or pot, pour in the mixture and bake slowly or cook in a steam bath

title: Kávé-torta (Coffee Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams sugar rubbed lightly with lemon
- 16 dekagrams fine cornstarch
- 14 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites
- 35 dekagrams sugar (for the ice)
- 1 cup strong coffee of the best quality
- 4 deciliters whipped cream

## Directions

1. Pound the sugar rubbed with lemon and sift it; similarly pound and sift the cornstarch
2. In a bowl, beat the sugar with 14 egg yolks for a good quarter hour until it becomes beautifully white and begins to rise
3. Beat 10 egg whites into stiff snow
4. First add the cornstarch flour and stir it in gently, then the egg snow, which must be mixed in very quickly
5. Grease a rimmed ring mold well with butter without any decorations; dust it lightly with fine flour
6. Similarly grease a baking sheet with butter
7. Fill the ring mold with the sponge batter so that it remains empty to a width of two fingers; spread the remaining batter on the baking sheet smoothly and evenly to the thickness of a knife blade with a wide knife
8. Bake both in a warm oven or stove at gentle heat until golden brown like a bread roll
9. Quickly remove the sheet and with a sharp knife cut out two round layers that fit exactly on the ring mold; place them back in the oven for a few moments, then turn out the ring, remove the layers from the sheet with a knife, place both on paper and keep near the warm oven
10. Pound 35 dekagrams sugar finely and sift it; brew a cup-full of strong, best quality coffee and set this, as well as 4 deciliters whipped cream, on ice
11. Place the sponge ring exactly on one of the layers; from the sugar and coffee, which you stir in only spoonful by spoonful, prepare a light ice, but it must not be liquid; cover the entire ring and the second layer, which you keep as a lid, with this
12. Once the ice has dried (which goes very easily on an economical stove, as you place the cake on a sieve near it), whip stiff snow from the chilled cream with a whisk, placing the whisk bowl on ice
13. Once this is done, put in 28 dekagrams sugar and as much ice-chilled coffee until the snow takes on a yellowish color and the coffee flavor becomes predominant
14. Mix the sugar and coffee well with the snow using the whisk, and fill the entire opening of the ring with this snow, which must already have dried; quickly place the lid on top, quickly cover the strip on the lid with the remaining ice, cut neat paper for the cake base, place the cake on it on a table covered with a tablecloth and quickly serve it to the table
15. The coffee snow must be filled into the ring only at the moment when the cake is served to the table, so that the ring does not soften
16. It can also be served without a lid, in which case the snow is filled into the ring and piled high at the moment it is served to the table
17. If the cake is prepared in a season when fresh strawberries can be obtained, the sponge ring can also be covered with strawberry pulp; pass the red strawberries through a fine sieve, place sifted sugar in a bowl, and mix the strawberry spoonful by spoonful with this; if it had a very pale color, one or two drops of cochineal can be mixed in; use care with the addition of strawberry syrup, so that it is not too thin
18. Cover the ring as with the coffee and let it dry; mix the strained strawberry in the bowl with finely pounded sugar for the snow, keep the cream snow on ice, and stir in the strawberry only slowly with the whisk and whip it up slightly again
19. Only so much may be added that the strawberry is still recognizable, so that the mixture is not too thin; give it color with cochineal
20. This cake can also be served without a lid, except that the snow is then filled and piled up at the moment of serving on the tablecloth

title: Kávés Lepény (Coffee Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- eighteen dekagrams fine flour
- fourteen dekagrams soft butter
- ten dekagrams finely ground sugar
- three hard-boiled egg yolks
- one egg yolk
- ten dekagrams sugar
- half cup of black coffee
- three egg whites
- seven dekagrams ground sugar
- jam for coating

## Directions

1. On a board, work the flour with the soft butter in layers
2. Mix finely ground sugar with flour
3. Add three hard-boiled egg yolks
4. Work everything well with the roller
5. Gather the mixture together and add one egg yolk, work well with knife
6. Roll out the smooth dough to three knife-widths thickness
7. Place on buttered sheet
8. Bake slowly at low heat
9. When baked and cooled, cut into two-finger wide and span-long strips
10. Coat the strips with jam
11. Cook ten dekagrams sugar with half cup black coffee until it begins to thread
12. When cooled, beat three egg whites to stiff snow
13. With the whisk, mix in seven dekagrams ground sugar
14. Add the cooked coffee
15. Spread the whole mixture smoothly on the strips
16. Dry in cooled oven
17. When dried, pile high on a cloth-covered plate

title: Kék Káposzta Gesztenyével (Red Cabbage with Chestnuts)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- red cabbage
- chestnuts (Italian, 500 grams for 8-10 servings)
- butter
- flour
- sausage

## Directions

1. Clean the red cabbage thoroughly and blanch it well with hot water
2. Cook until tender and drain
3. Prepare a brownish roux
4. Chop the cabbage finely with a knife and add it to the roux, then braise it together with the sauce for a while
5. Roast the Italian chestnuts until they shine beautifully
6. Heap the chestnuts in the center of the dish in a dome shape and arrange the cabbage in a wreath around them
7. Add sausage on the side

title: Kéz-kenöcs (Hand Salve)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- equal amounts of beef marrow and almond oil
- a small amount of lavender oil

## Directions

1. Mix equal amounts of beef marrow and almond oil over heat
2. Add a small amount of lavender oil for good fragrance
3. This salve is for rubbing into the hands to obtain fine, soft skin
4. In the evening before bed, rub the hands thoroughly with this salve, then put on gloves
5. In the morning, wash the pomade off the hands cleanly with almond meal and soapy water

title: Köményes fiatal sertés-czomb (Caraway Young Pork Leg)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young tender pork leg
- salt
- pepper
- carrots, sliced into rings
- celery, sliced into rings
- parsley root, sliced into rings
- considerable amount of caraway

## Directions

1. From a young tender pork leg, remove the bone without cutting the meat in two, then tie the leg into a nice round shape and salt it
2. Slice carrots, celery, and parsley root into rings, and place on a long baking sheet with pepper and a considerable amount of caraway to roast
3. When it begins to brown, pour half a liter of water over it and braise it until quite tender
4. Press the braised vegetables through a sieve and pour the pressed vegetables into the baking sheet under the roast
5. Slice the roast into nice rings, place in the center of the plate and pour the broth underneath, and serve very hot

title: Körte eczetben (Pears in Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-3 kilograms well-ripened, peeled Bergamot pears with stems removed
- 1.5 liters vinegar
- 80 dekagrams sugar
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. For 2 to 3 kilograms of well-ripened, peeled Bergamot pears, cook 1.5 liters vinegar with 80 dekagrams sugar and soften the pears in it.
2. Cook the vinegar slightly more with cloves and cinnamon and strain through cloth over the pears.
3. Repeat this process for two days or until the vinegar becomes thin again.
4. Then pour hot over the pears, cover with paper or bladder, and tie very firmly.

title: Köröm Paprikás (Paprika Pig's Feet)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh pork and pig's feet, as much as needed, cut into pieces
- Salt
- Red onion, one large onion per pig's foot piece, cut into cubes
- Black pepper
- Paprika, a small amount
- One tablespoon powdered sugar
- Vinegar
- Saffron, a good amount

## Directions

1. Cook the salted pig's feet pieces in water until soft
2. When the pig's feet are cooked soft, add the diced red onion and cook together
3. When the onion is also soft, prepare a beautiful red thin roux
4. Add black pepper, a small amount of paprika, and one tablespoon powdered sugar to the roux
5. Dilute the roux with the pig's feet cooking liquid
6. Pour in vinegar and add good saffron so that the broth becomes beautifully yellow

title: Köszméte- vagy egresmártás (Gooseberry Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and washed gooseberries
- butter or lard
- flour
- meat stock
- sugar
- three to four tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Braise the cleaned and washed gooseberries lightly in butter or lard
2. Sprinkle with as much flour as the quantity of gooseberries requires
3. Once this has evaporated sufficiently, add a little meat stock, sugar, and three to four tablespoons of sour cream
4. Cook until the gooseberries become soft but still remain whole
5. Can also be passed through a sieve

title: Köszmétés Bárány (Lamb with Salsify)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter lamb from the front
- various vegetables
- salt
- water
- flour
- sour cream
- salsify
- sugar
- parsley

## Directions

1. Put one quarter lamb with various vegetables in two liters of salted water to cook
2. Use vegetables such as those used in regular soup
3. While the lamb is cooking, prepare a mixture of one spoonful of flour and two deciliters of sour cream in a deep bowl
4. When the lamb is tender, slowly pour its broth into the mixture while continuously stirring
5. Place the meat in the sour cream broth and add one liter of nicely cleaned salsify, two spoonfuls of sugar and one teaspoonful of finely chopped parsley
6. Bring to a boil again
7. When the salsify is tender, remove from heat until serving
8. Serve in deep bowls

title: Közönséges gőzmetélt bajor módra (Common Steamed Noodles Bavarian Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams flour, warmed
- lukewarm milk
- 37.5 grams melted yeast
- a piece of melted butter about the size of an egg
- milk as needed for moistening
- 1 egg
- 2 egg yolks
- a little salt
- cream for steaming
- a little butter for steaming

## Directions

1. Place the warmed flour in a bowl and make a small dough from lukewarm milk and melted yeast, let it rise well
2. Add the melted butter and enough milk to moisten the dough
3. Beat in 1 egg and 2 egg yolks
4. Make the dough, which should be as stiff as bread dough
5. Beat it well, salt it lightly, cover it, and place in a warm location
6. Once it has risen about halfway, place on a floured board
7. Roll out to the thickness of two knife-backs
8. Cut into small pieces with a cutter and cover to let rise again
9. Pour cream to a depth of two finger-widths in a flat shallow pan, add a little butter, and bring to a boil
10. Add the noodles, cover with a lid that fits well, place clean paper on top, and coals above
11. Steam for about 15 minutes

title: Laska with Crayfish Butter (Laska rákvajjal)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- dough for noodles
- salted water
- melted crayfish butter
- 2 deciliters sour cream
- 3 egg yolks
- 4 spoonfuls crayfish butter
- crayfish tails, cut into cubes

## Directions

1. Prepare noodle dough from 2 eggs
2. Roll out thinly
3. Cut into laska shapes with a pastry wheel
4. Cook in salted water and drain
5. Place the laska in melted crayfish butter, stir well, and place on a buttered serving dish
6. Beat 3 egg yolks into 2 deciliters of sour cream with 4 spoonfuls of crayfish butter and mix well
7. Scatter cubed crayfish tails over the laska
8. Pour the sour cream mixture over it
9. Cover the top entirely with crayfish tails
10. Bake in the oven for a quarter hour

title: Laska-pástétom (Noodle Pastry with Filling)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- laska dough
- salted water
- butter
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- fat
- chopped filling or forcemeat
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Make fine chopped noodles from laska dough.
2. Cook them well in salted water and drain.
3. While still warm, mix in butter the size of a walnut.
4. Press the laska firmly into a large, wide pan spread with butter to a depth of two finger-widths and let it cool.
5. Cut out rounds the size of a forint using a pastry cutter.
6. Dip in beaten, salted egg, roll in breadcrumbs, and fry in fat.
7. Using a small cutter, make a hole in the center down to the bottom to create a crown shape.
8. Fill the opening with forcemeat or filling and serve hot, garnished with green parsley.

title: Lavendula-víz (Lavender Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- dried lavender flowers
- 4 grams vitriol oil
- strong purified alcohol
- various fragrant oils

## Directions

1. Fill three-quarters of a large pot with dried lavender flowers
2. Add 4 grams vitriol oil
3. Shake well
4. Allow to stand for one hour
5. Pour strong purified alcohol over it
6. Bind the bottle with a bladder
7. Allow to stand and digest for fourteen days
8. Various fragrant oils may be added

title: Lehámozott szilva eczetben (Peeled Plums in Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- 2 kilograms plums to 6 deciliters vinegar
- 60 dekagrams sugar
- whole cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Peel the plums either with a knife or by holding them in a strainer of hot water until the skin can be pulled off.
2. Once all are peeled, place in clean earthenware or glass jars.
3. For 2 kilograms plums, cook 6 deciliters vinegar with 60 dekagrams sugar, whole cinnamon, and cloves.
4. Skim it clean and pour cold over the plums.
5. Place the jars or crocks filled with plums into a vessel filled with cold water.
6. Set on fire and let stand for a quarter hour after the water boils, then let cool in the water.
7. After 8 to 12 days, cook the vinegar again and pour cold over the plums, just covering them completely.
8. Cover the jars with paper or bladder and tie very firmly.

title: Lemon Aspic (Czitrom-kocsonya)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 3 lemons
- 7 deciliters water
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Rub 42 dekagrams sugar very gently on 3 lemons.
2. Mix the sugar with 7 deciliters water.
3. Once the sugar has dissolved, mix in 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass.
4. Strain through cloth and freeze in molds like other aspics.

title: Lemon Aspic with Orange Slices (Czitrom-kocsonya narancs-szeletekkel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 beautiful ripe oranges
- lemon aspic (prepared as in recipe 808)

## Directions

1. Carefully peel 4 beautiful ripe oranges.
2. Divide each into 8 segments.
3. Pour lemon aspic into molds as with strawberry aspic, and carefully arrange orange segments in the center.
4. Fill completely with aspic and freeze.
5. Ensure fruit pits are removed and the aspic remains clean and transparent.

title: Lencseleves (Lentil Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- salt
- fried pike head or carp head or carp head
- fried breadcrumbs
- butter
- flour
- pike liver
- toasted breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Clean the lentils thoroughly, wash them, and cook them.
2. Salt them.
3. Make a brown broth, using fried pike head, carp head, or carp head.
4. Crush this with fried breadcrumb pieces in a mortar.
5. Make a brown roux with butter and flour.
6. Pour the lentils and the crushed mixture together with the broth into it.
7. Cook well.
8. Slightly sour it, strain through a sieve, salt, and serve over fried pike liver and toasted breadcrumb slices.

title: Lengyel-rostélyos (Polish Sirloin)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram lean loin
- salt
- one spoon cold fat
- paprika
- onions
- peppercorns
- three decilitres wine
- two decilitres vinegar
- cold water
- macaroni

## Directions

1. Cut slices from lean loin, beat them well with a mallet, and salt them
2. Place one spoon cold fat in a pot with a layer of meat
3. Sprinkle with a little paprika and thinly sliced onions
4. Add several peppercorns, then another layer of meat without paprika
5. Pour three decilitres wine, two decilitres vinegar, and several spoonfuls of cold water over the layered meat
6. Cover and cook until the meat is tender, occasionally adding a little wine and water if needed
7. Then roast until nicely browned, stirring constantly in the pot
8. Serve with its own fat and macaroni on the side

title: Lenmag'olaj saláta (Linseed Oil Salad)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- raw sour cabbage
- linseed oil
- hard-boiled egg

## Directions

1. Pour linseed oil over raw sour cabbage
2. Top with slices of hard-boiled egg
3. Serve alone or sometimes with scrambled eggs

title: Lentils with Partridges (Lencse Foglyokkal)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- salt
- thin wooden skewers
- large lentils
- butter
- red onion
- bacon
- lemon juice
- red wine

## Directions

1. Clean the partridges well and cut off their heads
2. Fold back their wings and salt them
3. Fit them with thin wooden skewers
4. Cook large lentils until soft so the grains remain whole
5. Strain the water from the lentils
6. Put a little butter and finely chopped red onion in a pot and add the lentils to braise
7. Roast or braise the partridges with bacon, lemon juice, red onion, and a little red wine
8. Pour the partridge sauce over the lentils and braise together for a short time
9. When serving, place the partridges in the center of the plate and arrange the lentils in a wreath shape around them

title: Leveles torta (Puff Pastry Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry folded five times
- twenty-eight dekagrams cake leaves
- egg white
- preserved currant jam
- preserved peach jam
- preserved rosehip marmalade
- egg white foam
- sugar

## Directions

1. Roll out puff pastry folded five times to two knife-widths thickness
2. From twenty-eight dekagrams of cake leaf, cut four leaves of the size of the cake leaf, coat them with egg white, place each one on the sheet, and bake slowly, but in such a way that they remain beautifully white, except for one leaf
3. Coat one leaf with preserved currant jam, place the other leaf on it, coat it with preserved peach jam, place the third leaf on it, coat it with preserved rosehip marmalade, finally place the last leaf on it, to which you place the following icing: Add to the stiff foam of fresh egg white in a small bowl enough sugar so that it becomes thin dough consistency

title: Leveles tészta (Layered Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large lemon
- 280 grams sugar
- 280 grams butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 280 grams fine flour
- 6 egg whites
- 3 torte sheets with rings
- butter
- preserved fruit
- 5 egg whites
- 2 tablespoons peach marmalade
- 100 grams finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Rub the sugar on a large lemon, finely grind the sugar
2. Whip the butter until foamy and add egg yolks one by one
3. Once the eggs are well beaten, add the sugar and beat for a quarter hour
4. Add the fine flour and the stiffly beaten egg whites
5. Butter three torte sheets with rings
6. Divide the batter into three equal parts on the torte sheets
7. Bake until a beautiful light yellow color
8. When baked and cooled, one sheet remains on the torte sheets, two are set aside on a sieve
9. Fill each sheet with preserved fruit and leave the top one empty
10. A quarter hour before serving, beat stiffly the egg whites of five eggs with two tablespoons peach marmalade and 100 grams finely ground sugar
11. Gradually fold in the egg whites and coat the top and sides of the pastry with a knife
12. Fill the remaining meringue into a Spanish pastry bag and pipe hazelnut-sized meringues in three rows around the edge of the top sheet
13. Place a large button in the center of the sheet, surround it with smaller meringues so they touch the button, keeping them small but beautifully tall, which requires a light and steady hand
14. Sprinkle with fine sugar and place in a cool oven or cold cooled oven so the meringue retains its color and does not harden too much

title: Leveles-tészta más módon (Puff Pastry Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagramms butter
- 4 tablespoons fine flour
- 4 deciliters warm milk
- sugar to taste
- a little vanilla
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- 4 dekagramms softened almonds
- 14 dekagramms finely ground and sifted sugar (for topping)
- preserves (for filling)

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and quickly stir in the flour, then cook with warm milk while continuously stirring until well cooked and a smooth thick paste forms
2. Cool and add sugar to taste and a little vanilla
3. Slowly fold in the egg yolks and the stiff peaks of 4 egg whites
4. Grease round copper pans lined with lead with a two-finger-wide rim
5. Fill with the paste to finger thickness
6. Bake at gentle heat
7. When well baked, turn out onto a clean board
8. Cut out 5-6 leaves with a medium-sized cutter
9. Place the first leaf on a plate, spread with preserves, add the second leaf and cover again with preserves, continuing until all leaves are stacked and filled
10. Mix 14 dekagramms finely ground and sifted sugar with stiff peaks of 3 egg whites and a little vanilla
11. Coat the entire pastry smoothly with this
12. Fill a Spanish pastry bag with the remainder and pipe small Spanish pastries the size of hazelnuts on the pastry in neat rows
13. Prepare 4 dekagramms softened almonds, dry well with a cloth and chop finely
14. Mix with the same amount of finely ground sugar
15. Sprinkle the entire pastry with this
16. Bake in a hot oven or a light golden yellow color

title: Leves agyvelő-gombóczezal (Brains Dumpling Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brains
- salt
- water
- parsley
- pepper
- butter
- bread roll
- milk
- five dekagrams butter
- two egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- beef broth
- brown or white broth

## Directions

1. Wash the veal brains thoroughly and cook in salted water
2. Remove the membrane and chop finely with parsley
3. Add salt and a little crushed pepper
4. Sauté in butter
5. Soak half a bread roll in milk and crush in a mortar
