6. Beat five dekagrams of butter until frothy
7. Mix the brains into the butter
8. Add two whole egg yolks
9. Add a little breadcrumb
10. Form small dumplings and cook in good beef broth
11. Place in soup bowl and pour brown or white broth over them

title: Leves Agyvelő-Kádkákkal (Soup with Brains in Molds)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two bread rolls
- milk
- four deciliters milk
- seven decagrams butter
- salt
- one egg
- three egg whites
- fine bread crumbs
- butter
- calf brain
- salt
- water
- green parsley
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. Rasp off the crust of two bread rolls, soften them in milk, and squeeze dry.
2. Place in a pot, pour four deciliters milk over them, bring to a boil over coals with continuous stirring until reduced to a puree.
3. Remove from heat and let cool.
4. Beat seven decagrams butter until fluffy.
5. Add a little salt, one whole egg, and the foam of three egg whites.
6. Place a little of the forcemeat in molds greased with butter and sprinkled with fine bread crumbs.
7. Fill the center with prepared brains and cover with the remaining forcemeat without completely filling.
8. The brains must first be cooked in salted water, then blanched, and braised with butter, finely chopped green parsley.
9. Cook the molds in steam for half an hour.
10. Unmold, place in a bowl, and pour brown sauce over it.

title: Leves Agyvelő-Pitével (Soup with Brain Pâté)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two bread rolls
- milk
- four deciliters milk
- calf brain
- ten decagrams butter
- eight egg yolks
- three egg whites
- butter
- fine bread crumbs
- salt
- water
- green parsley
- pepper

## Directions

1. Rasp off the crust of two bread rolls, cut into thin slices, place in a pot.
2. Pour four deciliters milk over them and let soften for an hour.
3. Place on coals and cook with continuous stirring until it becomes a puree.
4. Remove from heat and let cool.
5. Mix the cooled puree with prepared brains and ten decagrams butter beaten until fluffy.
6. Mix in eight egg yolks well beaten.
7. Gently fold in the foam of three egg whites.
8. Fill the forcemeat into a mold greased with butter and sprinkled with fine crumbs.
9. Cook by steaming for three-quarters of an hour.
10. Carefully unmold onto a clean board and cut into finger-thick slices.
11. Place in a soup bowl and pour brown sauce over it.
12. The brains must be cooked in salted water, placed in cold water, the membrane removed, finely chopped, and braised with butter, green parsley, a little pepper, and salt as needed before use.

title: Leves bekötöttel (Soup with Poached Meat Dumpling)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted veal
- Capon and turkey breast
- Roll soaked in milk
- 3 eggs
- Green parsley, finely chopped
- 7 dekagrams butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- Kitchen towel

## Directions

1. Chop roasted veal, capon breast, and turkey breast finely, soak a roll in milk and squeeze it out, beat 3 eggs, and chop green parsley finely.
2. Beat 7 dekagrams of butter until fluffy, throw in the chopped mixture, add 3 eggs one after another, salt, pepper, a little bread crumbs, and 2 tablespoons of good sour cream.
3. Mix everything well together, place the mixture on a kitchen towel greased with butter, tie it together, and boil in hot salted water for half an hour.
4. Carefully remove it, place it in a soup dish, and pour brown broth over it.

title: Leves burgonya-gombóczozal (Soup with Potato Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large starchy potatoes
- 7 dekagrams butter
- Green parsley, finely chopped
- 1 egg
- 3 egg yolks
- Salt
- Fine bread crumbs
- Hot fat

## Directions

1. Cook 4 large starchy potatoes, peel them, and grate them.
2. Beat 7 dekagrams of butter until fluffy, add finely chopped green parsley, 1 whole egg, and 3 egg yolks.
3. Mix everything well together, then mix in the potato with a little salt.
4. Form walnut-sized dumplings, roll them in fine bread crumbs, and fry them until a beautiful yellow in hot fat.
5. Place in the soup dish and pour potato broth over them.

title: Leves consommé-kádkákkal (Soup with Consommé Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 well-roasted chickens
- A piece of roasted veal
- 1 liter strong chicken broth
- 9 egg yolks
- Butter
- Consommé

## Directions

1. Chop 2 well-roasted chickens and a piece of roasted veal and pound finely in a mortar.
2. Pour 1 liter of strong chicken broth over it, pass through a sieve, mix together with 9 egg yolks, and pass through the sieve again.
3. Grease the dumplings with butter and fill with consommé and braise.
4. When the consommé sets, turn it out onto a soup dish and pour light brown broth over it.
5. To braise: Pour enough water into a deep pot so it reaches halfway up the mold; when the water boils, place the mold in it, cover with an iron lid, and place on the hot plate of the stove with as much heat as required.
6. For larger quantities of such a dish, it often must be cooked longer than an hour in this water, so if the water evaporates, it must be replaced with hot water, but the mentioned level must never be exceeded, as the contents would be in danger of the water overflowing it.

title: Leves csiperke-gombával (Soup with Mushrooms)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- button mushrooms
- butter
- green parsley, finely chopped
- flour
- meat broth
- nutmeg
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean the button mushrooms and cut into thin slices
2. Stew gently in a little butter with finely chopped green parsley
3. Sprinkle a little flour on top and when well reduced, add enough meat broth to make it like a thin sauce
4. Add a little nutmeg and a spoonful of sour cream
5. Let cook a little longer and serve over flour pastry or fried bread

title: Leves Dara-Kádkával (Soup with Semolina Cakes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- good meat broth
- semolina
- fourteen decagrams crayfish butter or other butter
- five eggs
- semolina puree
- salt
- butter
- fine bread crumbs
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Boil good meat broth in a pot.
2. Cook semolina into it until it becomes a thick porridge.
3. Remove from heat and let cool.
4. Beat fourteen decagrams crayfish or other butter until fluffy.
5. Mix in five eggs alternately with one egg and one spoon of puree.
6. Season lightly with salt.
7. Grease the molds with butter and sprinkle with fine bread crumbs.
8. When baked, unmold and place in a soup bowl.
9. Pour beef broth over it.

title: Leves darahaluskával (Soup with Semolina Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dekagrams butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 5 deciliters (a good measure) semolina
- Salt

## Directions

1. Rub 10 dekagrams of butter well, mix 4 egg yolks into it, and add 5 deciliters of semolina and a little salt.
2. When everything is well combined, throw the dumplings into the beef broth with a spoon.

title: Leves Felvert Daragombóccal (Soup with Whipped Semolina Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ten decagrams butter
- four eggs
- four deciliters semolina
- meat broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat ten decagrams butter until fluffy.
2. Slowly add four whole eggs.
3. Mix in four deciliters semolina.
4. Test with one dumpling first, as they can become either too soft or too hard.
5. If the test dumpling succeeds, make small dumplings.
6. Cook them in good meat broth.
7. Let them rest for a quarter hour.
8. Place in a soup bowl and pour the strained meat broth over them.

title: Leves Felvert Szalonnagombóczezal (Whipped Bacon Dumpling Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four dry bread rolls
- four egg yolks
- two deciliters of condensed milk
- twenty-one dekagrams of fresh bacon
- two whole eggs
- two egg yolks
- small handful of flour
- white meat broth

## Directions

1. Scrape the crust from four dry bread rolls and cut into small cubes
2. Mix four egg yolks with two deciliters of condensed milk and pour over the bread
3. Finely chop twenty-one dekagrams of fresh bacon with a kitchen knife and place in a bowl
4. Beat it with two whole eggs and two egg yolks until well foamed
5. Slowly fold in the soaked bread cubes
6. Add a small handful of flour, only as much as so they do not fall apart in the soup
7. Form dumplings from the mixture and place in good white meat broth

title: Leves húsgombóczozal (Soup with Meat Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams veal, roasted white
- 2 rolls, grated and soaked in milk
- 7 dekagrams butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- Salt
- Fine bread crumbs
- Fresh butter

## Directions

1. Chop 28 dekagrams of white-roasted veal finely, add 2 grated rolls soaked in milk (also chopped finely), and pound both finely in a mortar.
2. Beat 7 dekagrams of butter until fluffy, add 2 eggs and 1 egg yolk, and add the chopped mixture.
3. Season with salt and mix well; if still too soft, add a little fine bread crumbs.
4. Form small dumplings, fry them until a beautiful yellow in fresh butter, pour hot brown broth over them, and serve.

title: Leves Húsospaizskákkal (Soup with Meat-Filled Shield Shapes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight decagrams veal
- green parsley
- red onion
- butter
- flour
- meat broth
- two egg yolks
- thin milk cream
- egg
- fine bread crumbs
- fat
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Braise twenty-eight decagrams veal carefully so it remains as white as possible.
2. Finely chop green parsley and walnut-sized red onion.
3. Melt a piece of butter, add one teaspoon flour and brown slightly.
4. Add the chopped mixture and braise slightly.
5. Pour in half a serving spoon of meat broth and reduce until thick.
6. Mix in two egg yolks and remove from heat.
7. Cut thin slices of milk cream into knife-thick rings and spread with the meat filling.
8. Shape into shields and moisten with milk.
9. Dip in beaten egg and fine bread crumbs.
10. Fry golden brown in fat.
11. Serve with good meat broth.

title: Leves Húsvagdalék-Fánkkal (Soup with Meat Forcemeat Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted meat, especially turkey breast
- one or two button mushrooms
- half a bread roll soaked in milk
- green parsley
- salt
- egg yolk
- fine bread crumbs
- fat
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. Finely chop leftover roasted meat, especially turkey breast, one or two button mushrooms, half a bread roll soaked in milk, and green parsley.
2. Add a little salt and egg yolk and mix together.
3. Place the forcemeat on a board sprinkled with fine bread crumbs.
4. Stretch it to the thickness of a finger.
5. Cut out thumb-sized fritters with a cutter.
6. Fry light brown in fat.
7. Place in a soup bowl.
8. Pour hot brown sauce over it and serve at once to the table.

title: Leves lisztes lepénykével (Soup with Flour Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams butter
- 2 eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- flour
- 4 egg whites
- salt
- crab broth

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dekagrams butter until foamy
2. Beat in 2 whole eggs and 4 egg yolks
3. One after another, beat in enough flour to make a light dough and 4 egg whites beaten stiff
4. Season with salt
5. Butter a baking sheet or wide pot, put in the dough and bake until golden
6. When done, transfer to a board and cut out neatly with a cutter
7. Place in the soup bowl and pour crab broth over it

title: Leves Lúdaprólékkal (Soup with Goose Offal)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- green parsley
- six button mushrooms
- flour
- goose offal
- meat broth
- nutmeg
- toasted bread pieces
- goose liver

## Directions

1. Place butter, finely chopped green parsley, and six sliced button mushrooms in a frying pan.
2. Braise for a quarter hour.
3. Sprinkle two tablespoons flour over it.
4. Cook the goose offal in broth until soft enough.
5. Cut the meat into appropriate pieces, adding finely chopped nutmeg and cooking a bit longer.
6. Serve on long toasted bread pieces.
7. Cook the liver separately and chop it into pieces.
8. Add it to the soup.
9. This soup can also be made without button mushrooms.

title: Leves lúdmáj-pitével (Soup with Goose Liver Pie)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- 7 dekagrams butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 rolls
- milk
- green parsley, finely chopped
- red onion
- salt
- pepper
- fine bread crumbs
- 2 egg whites
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the goose liver and press through a sieve
2. Beat 7 dekagrams butter until foamy
3. Mix in 2 eggs and 2 egg yolks well beaten
4. Soak 2 rolls with crusts removed in milk
5. Add finely chopped green parsley, red onion, salt, and pepper
6. Add the soaked roll and fine bread crumbs
7. Once everything is mixed, fold in the foam from 2 egg whites
8. Pour the mixture into the pot, bake, and cut into slanted rectangles
9. Pour broken liver broth over it

title: Leves májgombóczczal (Liver Dumpling Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams veal liver
- bread roll
- milk
- parsley
- red onion
- seven dekagrams butter
- two whole eggs
- one egg yolk
- breadcrumbs
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Chop veal liver finely and press through a sieve
2. Soak bread roll in milk, squeeze out and chop finely with parsley and a pea-sized piece of red onion
3. Beat seven dekagrams of butter until frothy
4. Mix in two whole eggs and one egg yolk
5. Combine this with the liver and bread mixture
6. Add enough breadcrumbs to form nice small dumplings
7. Cook in good beef broth

title: Leves rizsliszt-kádkákkal (Rice Flour Dumpling Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- white broth
- seven dekagrams butter
- one whole egg
- six egg yolks
- salt
- four egg whites
- butter for greasing
- filling
- white broth

## Directions

1. Clean the rice and grind in a mortar to flour
2. Mix with cold white broth
3. Cook to a thick paste with constant stirring
4. Remove from heat and mix the still-hot paste with seven dekagrams butter
5. Let cool
6. Beat in one whole egg and six egg yolks
7. Add a little salt
8. When sufficiently mixed, fold in the stiffly beaten whites of four eggs
9. Grease dumplings with butter and fill halfway
10. Add filling to the center and cover with the above mixture without filling completely
11. Bake in oven and pour white broth over them

title: Leves rizsmájjal (Soup with Rice Liver)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver mixture
- flour
- hot broth
- brown broth

## Directions

1. Prepare the mixture as for goose liver pâté recipe number 53a, with the difference that one and a half spoonfuls of flour are added instead of egg white
2. Press this mixture through a larger grater with a wooden spoon into hot broth
3. To keep the rice beautifully whole and uniform do this in a pot
4. Remove gradually with a spoon as it cooks
5. Place in soup bowl and serve brown broth over it

title: Leves rák-kádkákkal (Crayfish Dumpling Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- bread roll grated and sliced thin
- four deciliters milk
- egg-sized piece of crayfish butter
- one whole egg
- three egg yolks
- salt
- twenty-five crayfish tails
- three egg whites
- bread rings
- crayfish butter
- broth

## Directions

1. Place thinly sliced grated bread in a pot
2. Pour four deciliters of milk over it
3. Cook with constant stirring to a paste and let cool
4. Beat an egg-sized piece of crayfish butter until fluffy
5. Add the cooked and softened bread
6. Beat in one whole egg and three egg yolks
7. Stir for half an hour
8. Add salt and twenty-five roughly chopped crayfish tails
9. Gently mix in the beaten whites of three eggs
10. Spread bread rings with crayfish butter and join them together
11. After half an hour turn into soup bowl and pour broth over them

title: Leves Spárgakádkával (Asparagus Dumpling Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- small bunch of asparagus
- twenty to thirty crayfish
- salted water
- half a bread roll
- milk
- piece of veal roast
- four dekagrams of butter
- one whole egg
- three egg yolks
- salt
- stiffly beaten egg whites from three eggs

## Directions

1. Cook the small bunch of asparagus and twenty to thirty crayfish in salted water
2. Soak half a bread roll in milk and squeeze it out
3. Chop this together with a piece of veal roast into small pieces
4. Whip four dekagrams of butter and mix in one whole egg and three egg yolks, stirring for fifteen minutes
5. Add a little salt and fold in stiffly beaten egg whites from three eggs
6. Fill buttered dumplings with this mixture
7. Place in steam for half an hour
8. Turn out into a soup bowl and pour good brown broth over it

title: Leves Szalonnagombóczezal Közönséges Módon (Bacon Dumplings Soup - Common Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen dekagrams of bacon
- three dried bread rolls
- four deciliters of flour
- two deciliters of milk
- one whole egg
- one egg yolk
- fresh green parsley
- good beef broth

## Directions

1. Chop fourteen dekagrams of bacon into cubes and similarly chop three dried bread rolls
2. Let the bacon melt in a pan, but only until it looks glassy
3. Strain off the fat, add finely chopped fresh green parsley and let it brown slightly
4. Add the bread roll to it and similarly brown it with continuous stirring
5. Remove from heat, pour into a flat bowl and let cool
6. Make a dough from four deciliters of flour, two deciliters of milk, one whole egg and one egg yolk, kneading it well
7. Add the cooled bread roll mixture, knead it together, and form dumplings
8. Cook in good beef broth all at once

title: Leves Szalonnagombóczezal Más Módon (Bacon Dumplings Soup - Another Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen dekagrams of bacon
- bread roll
- egg-sized amount of butter or fat
- fresh green parsley
- salt
- two whole eggs
- one egg yolk
- fresh water
- bread roll
- flour

## Directions

1. Chop fourteen dekagrams of bacon and a bread roll into small cubes together
2. Melt the bacon slightly and brown the chopped bread with it until it turns yellowish, then set aside to cool
3. Whip an egg-sized amount of butter or fat, fold in a little finely chopped fresh green parsley and a little salt
4. Mix in two whole eggs and one egg yolk
5. Add the cooled bread and bacon mixture
6. Finally add a bread roll soaked in fresh water, well squeezed out and finely chopped, and as much flour as is needed to hold the mixture together
7. Test with one dumpling before making them all

title: Leves tüdőtáskával (Lung Pocket Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal lung
- veal heart
- butter
- parsley
- red onion
- salt
- two eggs
- puff pastry dough
- flour
- white broth
- brown broth

## Directions

1. Cook veal lung and heart until tender
2. Remove the pharynx and chop the rest finely
3. Melt a piece of butter
4. Add finely chopped parsley and a little red onion
5. Sauté slightly
6. Add the chopped lung with a little salt
7. Fry briefly and stir well
8. Add two well-beaten eggs and remove from heat
9. Let cool
10. Prepare common puff pastry dough and roll out finely
11. Fill with the lung mixture leaving one and a half arm-lengths empty
12. Cut this empty part in half down the middle
13. Roll the dough from all sides so the empty dough covers the filled part several times
14. Press with a wooden spoon handle dipped in flour to make small indentations
15. Cut in half with a knife
16. Press the ends together
17. Cook the pockets in white broth
18. Place in bowl and pour brown broth over them

title: Leves vajashaluskával (Soup with Buttered Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagrams butter
- 4 egg yolks
- Egg white foam from 4 eggs
- Salt
- 8 tablespoons fine flour

## Directions

1. Beat 7 dekagrams of butter until fluffy, mix 4 egg yolks into it, add the foam from 4 egg whites, a little salt, and 8 tablespoons of fine flour.
2. As a precaution, you can test one dumpling cooked in hot beef broth; if it falls apart, add a little more flour.
3. Throw the dumplings into the hot broth with a spoon, cover, and let simmer for eight minutes.
4. Place individually in the soup dish and pour the broth over them.

title: Leves zsemlekádkákkal (Soup with Bread Molds)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 rolls
- 4 deciliters milk
- 8 dekagrams butter
- 1 egg
- 4 egg yolks
- salt
- 3 egg whites
- filling or ground meat

## Directions

1. Remove crusts from 2 rolls, cut into slices
2. Pour 4 deciliters of milk over them and cook to a fine paste with continuous stirring
3. Remove from heat and let cool completely after mixing in 8 dekagrams butter
4. Beat in 1 whole egg and 4 egg yolks one after another
5. Season slightly with salt
6. Finally gently fold in 3 stiffly beaten egg whites
7. Butter the molds, put a spoonful of paste in each, add a teaspoon of filling or ground meat on top
8. Cover with another spoonful of paste and bake nicely
9. Note: the bread paste must not be thin, otherwise the filling will sink to the bottom; generally the molds must be filled quickly and immediately placed in the water bath

title: Levesbe való rizslepény (Rice Cake for Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 decagrams rice
- 7 deciliters milk
- butter, egg-sized piece
- 6 egg yolks
- finely chopped parsley
- little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in milk until thick
2. Set aside to cool
3. Mix the butter with the egg yolks, finely chopped parsley, and a little nutmeg until fluffy
4. Add the cooled rice to the mixture
5. Grease a baking pan with butter and pour in the rice
6. Bake until light golden
7. Turn out onto a board and cut into equal square pieces
8. Serve with white or brown broth

title: Levestáblácskák utazásra (Portable Soup Tablets for Travel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram soup tablets
- 2.5 kilograms lean meat from the loin
- 6 liters water
- 1 old hen
- 2 veal feet
- onions, sliced
- veal shank
- veal knuckles
- 1 kilogram lean pork
- 1 to 1.5 kilograms mutton
- 4 deciliters water
- carrots
- parsley
- parsnip root

## Directions

1. Cook 0.5 kilogram soup tablets with 2.5 kilograms lean meat, 6 liters water, an old hen, and two veal feet until completely tender
2. In a large pot, layer sliced onions, veal shank, veal knuckles, lean pork, and mutton
3. Pour 4 deciliters water over it and add carrots, parsley, and parsnip root
4. Braise until the liquid is brown at the bottom
5. Pour the cooked meat broth over it and simmer slowly for 3-4 hours to keep the broth clear
6. When everything is very soft and the meat nearly inedible, strain through a fine sieve, then through cheesecloth, and transfer to another pot
7. Slowly reduce until it resembles a meat glaze (glace), from about 6 liters down to 2 liters
8. Strain through clean cheesecloth and pour onto a flat dish to cool
9. Once completely cooled and set, cut into pieces the size of chocolate tablets and lay on paper in a cool, airy place to dry
10. When completely dry, store in a dry place for later use
11. Note: if dissolving a tablet in hot water while traveling to quickly make broth, do not salt it further as it would become sour

title: Leánysom-kocsonya (Cornelian Cherry Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram ripe cornelian cherries
- 40 dekagrams refined sugar
- 1.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Cook the ripe cornelian cherries in the refined sugar for five minutes
2. Set the syrup aside and allow it to cool completely
3. Pass through a fine sieve
4. Mix with the dissolved isinglass
5. Pour into the desired mold
6. Set in crushed ice and freeze
7. The sugar should be cleaned with nine deciliters of water so that after mixing with the isinglass, it will fill a one-liter mold

title: Leánysom-kocsonya (Rowan Berry Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram well-ripened rowan berries
- 40 dekagrams refined sugar
- 3.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Cook 1 kilogram of well-ripened rowan berries with 40 dekagrams of refined sugar for 5 minutes
2. Set the syrup aside
3. When completely cooled, strain through a fine sieve
4. Mix with 3.5 dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
5. Pour into the appropriate mold
6. Place in cracked ice and freeze as with the above recipes

title: Linczi kenyér (Linz Bread)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- 12 egg whites
- 56 dekagrams sugar
- 14 dekagrams blanched almonds, finely chopped
- 1 lemon, zest finely chopped
- 56 dekagrams flour

## Directions

1. Place egg yolks in a bowl, whip the egg whites into stiff peaks
2. Finely crush the sugar and beat it together with the peaks and egg yolks using a whisk for a good hour until thick
3. In a pan, toast the blanched and finely chopped almonds until golden
4. Once cooled, mix into the beaten mixture
5. Add the finely chopped lemon zest and flour and mix everything well
6. Fill the dough into a buttered twice-baked bread form and bake slowly
7. Once baked and cooled, either slice like twice-baked bread or cut into small pieces

title: Linczi torta tojás nélkül (Linz Cake without Eggs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams softened almonds
- 28 dekagrams sugar with vanilla
- 1 lemon, zest
- 28 dekagrams butter
- cold water
- 1 lemon, juice
- 28 dekagrams flour

## Directions

1. Pound the softened almonds finely, moistening with water during pounding so that it does not become oily
2. Pound the same amount of sugar with vanilla finely and chop the zest of one lemon into small pieces
3. Beat 28 dekagrams butter until foamy, mixing in a spoonful of cold water during the beating, as well as the juice of one lemon, the almonds, and the sugar
4. Once well beaten into a foamy state, gently mix in 28 dekagrams flour
5. From this, prepare either a rod cake or a soup cake; in the first case, fill it with preserves, cover nicely with the rod, place the ring on top, coat with egg white, dust with coarsely pounded sugar, and then bake at sustained heat
6. This dough can also be used for small pastries, such as small cakes, pretzels, etc.

title: Linezi torta más módon (Linz Torte, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 dekagrams butter
- 26 dekagrams finely powdered sugar
- 3 whole eggs
- 14 dekagrams blanched finely chopped almonds
- 26 dekagrams fine flour
- a little chopped lemon zest
- jam for filling

## Directions

1. Beat butter until foamy
2. Add 26 dekagrams finely powdered sugar and stir for a quarter hour
3. Beat in 3 whole eggs, blanched finely chopped almonds, fine flour, and a little chopped lemon zest and mix well
4. Place half of the dough on a buttered sheet
5. From the other half, make small rods and arrange them nicely in a lattice pattern on top
6. Brush with egg wash and bake until nicely golden

title: Liszteskádka-leves (Flour Mold Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites beaten stiff
- 4-5 tablespoons fine flour
- salt
- crab broth

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dekagrams butter until foamy
2. Mix in 5 egg yolks and 5 stiffly beaten egg whites
3. Add 4-5 tablespoons fine flour and a little salt
4. Beat until foamy
5. Butter the molds, fill halfway with the mixture
6. Bake in the oven
7. Place in the soup bowl and pour crab broth over it

title: Lábatlan tyúk (Legless Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pancake batter (made much thicker and stiffer than usual)
- hot fat
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare pancake batter, but much thicker and stiffer than regular pancake batter
2. Take a rounded spoonful and place it in hot fat, and carefully, so it does not fall apart, fry it until golden
3. Serve dusted with sugar

title: Lúdaprólék-Leves Más Módon (Goose or Duck Offal Soup, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- goose or duck offal
- nutmeg
- rice broth
- flour
- green parsley
- pepper
- cold water
- goose liver
- button mushrooms

## Directions

1. Clean and finely chop goose or duck offal.
2. Cook with a little nutmeg until half tender.
3. Add a little rice broth.
4. When the meat is half soft, prepare a light roux with finely chopped green parsley and pepper added.
5. Thin with a little cold water so it is not lumpy.
6. Add to the offal and continue cooking.
7. Remove the goose liver, cut into nice long pieces.
8. Pour the soup over it and serve.
9. If available, button mushrooms can be added for good flavor, though the soup is good without them.

title: Lúdmáj rizs-abroncsban (Goose Liver in Rice Ring)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose liver
- 9-10 slices of red onion
- carrots, sliced into rings
- cloves
- 2 deciliters of sour cream
- breadcrumbs, a handful
- small pieces of butter
- 14 decagrams (1/4 pound) rice, cleaned
- parsley, finely chopped
- Spanish onion, quartered
- stock
- black pepper
- crayfish butter
- mushrooms, finely chopped
- crayfish tails

## Directions

1. Wash and salt the goose liver and place it in a pot
2. Place 9-10 slices of red onion and sliced carrots under the liver
3. Pierce the liver with cloves
4. Pour 2 deciliters of sour cream over it
5. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs and small pieces of butter
6. Roast for one hour
7. Remove the liver, clean it of sauce and fat
8. Pour a few spoonfuls of stock over the remaining vegetables and quickly reduce
9. Place the liver in a dish where the rice ring should already be prepared and pour the small amount of sauce over it
10. For the rice ring: melt a small piece of butter and add finely chopped parsley
11. Add 14 decagrams of cleaned rice to the butter
12. Add the Spanish onion quartered
13. Slowly braise the rice, occasionally pouring small amounts of stock so it does not break
14. When softened, remove the onion and add pepper and crayfish butter
15. In another pot, melt butter and add finely chopped parsley with several finely chopped mushrooms
16. Braise with a little black pepper until the sauce has evaporated
17. Sprinkle a little flour over it
18. Pour in one or two spoonfuls of sour cream and let it evaporate again
19. Form a rice ring in the dish and fill it with the braised mushrooms and crayfish tails
20. Leave two finger-widths of space between them so the whole resembles a ribbon
21. Place the goose liver in the middle

title: Lúdmáj Írósvaj-mártással (Goose Liver with Butter Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Two goose livers
- Butter
- Salt
- Clove, crushed
- Two egg yolks
- Sour sour cream
- Lemon-sugar
- Flour
- Beaten egg
- Bread crumbs
- Butter
- Broth
- Butter
- Hot sour cream
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Clean two goose livers well and cut into beautiful slices
2. Place half on a buttered baking sheet, salt, and flavor with crushed clove
3. Mix two egg yolks with sour sour cream and lemon-sugar, pour over the liver and bake in the oven
4. Flour the other half of the liver, dip in beaten egg then bread crumbs, fry in butter, and constantly baste with broth
5. Finally, melt butter with hot sour cream and sugar for the sauce, and pour over the baked liver when this sauce is ready

title: Lúdmáj-szálmi (Goose Liver Fricassee)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two beautiful goose livers
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- thin bacon slices
- onions
- flour
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Wash the goose livers and salt them, mixing pepper into the salt
2. Stud each liver with three or four cloves
3. Place thin bacon slices and two heads of onion cut into slices in a pot
4. Place the livers on top and cover with a few more thin bacon slices
5. Put live coals on the pot lid and braise slowly
6. When nicely golden, remove the livers and sprinkle a small spoonful of flour into the pot, allowing it to brown
7. Thin with a good ladle of beef broth and cook well
8. Remove the cloves from the livers and cut away all edge pieces from the liver
9. Cut thick slices from the center of the liver and arrange on the dish rim, keeping warm
10. When the sauce is well reduced, strain it
11. Press the onion, bacon, and liver remnants through a sieve into a small pot
12. Thin with one or two spoonfuls of sauce and cook well until it becomes a thick paste
13. Spread this paste on the bottom of the dish
14. Arrange the liver slices in a crown around the fricassee
15. Pour a few spoonfuls of sauce over the liver and serve hot

title: Macedoine (Masszedoán)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 lemons
- 8 deciliters water
- 35 dekagrams ground sugar
- 2 lemon peels
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Mix juice of 7 lemons with 8 deciliters water.
2. Dissolve 35 dekagrams ground sugar (rubbed on 2 lemon peels) in this liquid.
3. Mix in 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass.
4. Strain through stretched cloth until beautifully clear.

title: Magyar fogas (Hungarian Pike-perch)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike-perch
- salt
- string
- hot salted water
- dill vinegar
- butter
- nutmeg
- five egg yolks
- lemon
- green parsley

## Directions

1. After the fish has lain on ice for several days, clean it
2. Split it open and rinse well
3. Salt it thoroughly
4. Tie the fins with string and let it rest
5. One hour before serving, tie it in a special fish cloth
6. Place in hot salted water flavored with a few drops of dill vinegar and cook
7. Make a fine butter sauce with the fish broth, add nutmeg, and bring to a boil
8. Beat five egg yolks with one lemon together, combine with the butter sauce like a roux, and pour into a sauce cup
9. Place the fish on a cloth-covered board, then in an oblong dish
10. Remove the string and arrange on the table garnished with green parsley and served with sauce

title: Magyar pite (Hungarian Pita)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four and a half decilitres fine flour
- seven decilitres milk
- two whole eggs
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat four and a half decilitres of fine flour, seven decilitres of milk, and two whole eggs together well and salt lightly
2. Pour this mixture onto a buttered sheet, brush the top with butter, and let it bake over gentle heat
3. When baked, brush the top with butter

title: Magyar ponty (Hungarian Carp)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- carp middle sections
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- onion
- butter
- paprika
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean the carp sections, salt them, place in a pan, pour water and vinegar over them, just enough so the fish is not completely covered, and simmer slowly
2. Finely chop onion and braise it in butter until golden, add half a teaspoon of paprika which should also braise
3. Add two deciliters of sour cream to a little of the fish broth, bring to a boil, skim, and strain the sauce
4. Place the carp in a dish, pour a little of the sauce over it, and serve the rest of the sauce separately in a bowl

title: Majonéz (Mayonnaise)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two coffee cups melted aspic
- one small coffee cup pure olive oil
- knife-tip of ground pepper
- lemon juice
- one tablespoon white vinegar

## Directions

1. Pour the melted aspic, olive oil, ground pepper, lemon juice, and white vinegar into a brass cooking vessel
2. Place the vessel on ice
3. Beat with a whisk until it becomes a thick, shiny mass
4. Use for cold meat dishes and fish

title: Majonéz új módon (Mayonnaise, New Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- eight tablespoons finest oil
- eight tablespoons fine vinegar with lemon juice
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat eight egg yolks, eight tablespoons finest oil, and eight tablespoons fine vinegar with lemon juice and a little salt to a frothy consistency in a stone vessel.
2. Place the vessel in another vessel filled with hot water and put it on the fire.
3. When the water around the vessel boils, stir until the mayonnaise begins to thicken.
4. Then place in cold water and stir until it cools completely.
5. This mayonnaise always succeeds and is ready in a quarter hour.

title: Makaroni (Pasta Mold)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams pasta
- white broth
- 25 dekagrams butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 3 tablespoons sweet milk
- 4 whole eggs
- ham, finely chopped
- finely grated cheese
- butter for greasing the mold

## Directions

1. Cook the pasta in white broth.
2. Melt 25 dekagrams of butter, add 1 tablespoon flour and let it simmer slightly.
3. Increase with 3 tablespoons of sweet milk and remove from heat.
4. When cooled, beat in 4 whole eggs.
5. Add the cooked pasta and shake well.
6. Grease a mold with butter and pour in half the pasta.
7. Sprinkle a layer of finely chopped ham and finely grated cheese.
8. Add another layer of pasta and repeat three times until the mold is full.
9. Boil the mold in hot water for 1.5 hours, then serve.
10. Optional sauce: Stick cloves into an onion head and fry in a little fat, add 2 tablespoons flour, fill with good meat broth, strain through a sieve, and add sautéed button mushrooms.

title: Makaróni-pudding (Macaroni Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram macaroni
- white broth
- 25 dekagrams butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 3 tablespoons sweet milk
- 4 whole eggs
- ham
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cook 0.5 kilogram macaroni in white broth
2. Melt 25 dekagrams butter and add 1 tablespoon flour, let it simmer slightly
3. Enlarge with 3 tablespoons sweet milk and remove from heat
4. When cooled, mix in 4 whole eggs
5. Add the cooked macaroni and shake well
6. Pour half of the macaroni into a buttered mold
7. Sprinkle a layer of finely chopped ham and finely grated cheese on top
8. Add another layer of macaroni and repeat this three times until the mold is full
9. Cook the pasta in hot water together with the mold for 1.5 hours then turn out

title: Malacz Törzény (Roasted Suckling Pig)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig (3-4 weeks old)
- finely ground resin
- water
- lard-pieces
- bread roll with bacon pieces
- fine pins and thread
- bay leaf or similar seasonings

## Directions

1. Kill the pig with a thrust through the neck aimed at the heart
2. Dust finely with ground resin and scald with boiling water, then remove the bristles by hand
3. Fold the front legs under the breast and the hind legs under themselves
4. Stuff the belly cavity (after removing the intestines) with a bread roll studded with pieces of bacon and sew closed with fine pins and thread
5. Run the spit lengthwise through the pig so it exits at the snout without making a hole
6. During roasting, frequently baste with peeled bacon on a long fork
7. Keep the fire moderate so that no blisters form and the skin remains tender, but not so weak that the skin becomes soft and pale - it should be a beautiful golden-brown color, crispy and glass-like
8. After removing from the spit, take out the pins and thread
9. Make a deep cut between the head and neck to allow trapped steam to escape and prevent the skin from softening

title: Malacz vagdalékkal töltve (Suckling Pig Stuffed with Forcemeat)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- veal shoulder
- marrow
- bread roll
- milk
- four whole eggs
- salt
- black pepper
- nutmeg flowers
- cooked, pickled or smoked tongue
- pickled cucumber
- red onion
- carrot
- peppercorns
- allspice
- white wine
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the pig thoroughly, cut off the head and legs, and slit the belly lengthwise.
2. Separate the meat and bones from the skin carefully with a sharp knife without damaging the skin.
3. Chop finely 28 dekagrams each of veal shoulder, 14 dekagrams of marrow, and bread roll soaked in milk.
4. Pound this mixture in a mortar, pour four whole eggs over it, pound again, add salt, little crushed black pepper, and nutmeg flowers.
5. Pass through a fine sieve and spread the mixture inside the pig to the thickness of a thumb.
6. Cut cooked, pickled or smoked tongue into pieces the size of a small finger, and similarly cut pickled cucumber.
7. Arrange these in strips over the forcemeat, leaving spaces the width of a finger between them.
8. Roll up like a strudel, wrap in clean cloth, and tie both ends securely.
9. Place in a long pot with sliced red onion, carrot, a few peppercorns and allspice, and the cut-off head and legs.
10. Pour two liters of white wine and four deciliters of vinegar over it, salt it, cover, and cook until the head is tender.
11. Cool it, and when it has cooled and set, pour the cooking liquid over it and set aside until ready to use.
12. Before serving, remove from the cloth, dry it clean, and slice thinly.

title: Malaczot Sütni (Roast Suckling Pig)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one suckling pig
- salt
- ground pepper
- lard
- clean cloth
- twine

## Directions

1. After killing and scalding the pig, use water that is not too hot—only hot enough that you can safely insert your finger into it.
2. Remove the entrails and clean the pig thoroughly.
3. Salt it well and coat the interior with ground pepper.
4. Splay the thighs apart; bend the front legs inward at the joints so they lie backward and tie them with twine to maintain their natural position.
5. Thread the pig lengthwise onto a spit so that the spit passes through the neck and comes out through the nose without making a hole.
6. Stuff clean cloth into the mouth and sew it closed.
7. Roast for one hour over medium heat or in the oven, frequently basting with a piece of lard, then wiping clean with cloth after a few moments, and turning frequently to prevent blistering.
8. The skin should turn a beautiful golden-brown and be crispy and brittle.
9. When done, remove the spit, cloth, and twine, and quickly bring to the table.

title: Malaez-tokány (Piglet Stew)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-4 week old piglet
- 3-4 deka fresh bacon per kilo of meat, cut into small cubes
- 1-2 teaspoons black ground pepper per kilo of meat
- salt
- meat broth
- green parsley, finely chopped, as much as can be held with five fingers, per kilo of meat
- 1/2 small clove garlic per kilo of meat, finely chopped or ground in wooden mortar
- good paprika
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Cut the 3-4 week old piglet into small pieces and keep in a bowl filled with water
2. For each kilo of meat, render 3-4 deka fresh bacon cut into small cubes until the cracklings turn bread-yellow
3. Add 1-2 teaspoons of black ground pepper per kilo of meat to the fresh hot fat with the cracklings still in it
4. Immediately take the piglet meat from the water and place it in the fat so the pepper does not burn
5. Cover the casserole and let the meat braise
6. After half an hour, if the meat has absorbed the rendered liquid, pour in fat-free meat broth so the meat is always submerged about a quarter of its height
7. At this time, salt the meat and simultaneously add for each kilo of meat as much finely chopped green parsley as can be held with five fingers, and 1/2 small clove of garlic per kilo, finely chopped or ground in a wooden mortar
8. Continue braising until the meat is completely tender, occasionally pouring in broth
9. When the meat is done braising, taste the broth and add more salt if needed
10. Before serving, for 3-4 kilos of meat add 1/2 teaspoon good paprika and a little marjoram, then let the whole thing braise briefly without a cover until it reduces by half, and serve in a warm bowl and on heated plates

title: Maláta-eczet (Malt Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 liters barley
- water
- 40 liters water
- 7 deciliters yeast

## Directions

1. Soak 15 liters of barley in water.
2. When it swells, pour it onto a floor, cover it with something light until it begins to sprout, then spread it out, dry it again, and grind it coarsely.
3. Soak the ground malt in lukewarm water for several hours, then heat it well in hot water, then boil it for one hour.
4. For this amount of malt, calculate 40 liters of water.
5. After boiling, filter the liquid through a clean sack or sieve, at the bottom of which you have placed straw, into a container, and when it is half as warm as fresh milk, place it in a small barrel or trough, mix it with 7 deciliters of yeast, then cover and let it stand in a warm place.
6. Fermentation will soon begin, and when it no longer rises and no bubbling can be observed, and you have removed the yeast on top, draw off the fermented liquid through a tap, which is applied 3-5 centimeters above the bottom of the barrel, so that only clear vinegar flows out without the yeast and other cloudy parts.
7. The barrel into which you draw it off should first be rinsed with vinegar.

title: Mandola hálókabátban (Almonds in Lace Coat)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams finely crushed sugar
- 1 egg white
- wafer
- blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar into a stiff icing with the stiffly beaten egg white over half an hour
2. Cut round leaves from wafer, place a blanched almond on each and cover with a coffee spoonful of icing
3. Once all are prepared this way, bake or rather dry in a gentle oven and then arrange on a plate

title: Mandola-csókok (Almond Kisses)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams sugar with a little vanilla, crushed and sifted
- 2 egg whites
- 28 dekagrams blanched almonds, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar into a stiff icing with the stiffly beaten egg whites
2. Mix the blanched and finely chopped almonds into this mixture
3. Place spoonful-sized pieces on wafer paper placed on a baking sheet
4. Bake quickly in a gentle oven

title: Mandola-fagylalt (Almond Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams sweet almonds
- a few bitter almonds
- little cream
- 1 liter cream
- piece of vanilla
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 12 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Soak the sweet and a few bitter almonds, remove their skin, and finely grind or grate the almonds with a little cream
2. Boil 1 liter of cream with a piece of vanilla and 42 dekagrams sugar together
3. Mix thoroughly with 12 egg yolks and the almonds
4. On the fire beat to a thick foam (crème)
5. Strain through a fine sieve or cheesecloth
6. When cooled, freeze

title: Mandola-falatok (Almond Bites)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry
- 14 dekagrams blanched almonds
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 1 lemon, finely chopped rind
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cut rectangular leaves from puff pastry
2. Pound the blanched almonds and sugar together
3. Mix with finely chopped lemon rind and beat with egg yolks in a bowl for half an hour
4. Coat with egg whites
5. Place a teaspoon of almond paste in the center of each pastry leaf
6. Press opposite corners together
7. Place immediately on baking sheet
8. Brush with egg white
9. Bake at moderate heat until golden yellow
10. Serve warm sprinkled with sugar

title: Mandola-kifli (Almond Crescents)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams crushed and sifted sugar
- half an egg white
- a few drops of lemon juice
- 14 dekagrams blanched almonds, finely chopped
- 10 dekagrams coarsely chopped almonds
- 10 dekagrams crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar into a stiff icing with half an egg white and a few drops of lemon juice
2. Mix the blanched and finely chopped almonds into it
3. Previously mix 10 dekagrams coarsely chopped almonds with 10 dekagrams crushed sugar
4. Place a coffee spoonful of the beaten dough into the coarsely chopped sugar-almond mixture, press it wide with your palm, and shape into small crescents, then dust generously with almonds and sugar
5. Bake the crescents on waxed parchment in gentle heat

title: Mandola-lepény habarva (Almond Cake Mixed)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Crumbly puff pastry
- Butter
- Twenty-one dekagrams almonds (some bitter)
- Twenty-one dekagrams fine broken sugar
- One lemon
- Seven whole eggs
- Four egg yolks
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Prepare crumbly puff pastry and tear off twenty small pieces
2. Cover with warm cloth and let rise half an hour
3. Butter the plate needed
4. Stretch the pastry very finely, brushing each layer with melted butter; stretch out six layers
5. For the seventh layer prepare the filling: soften twenty-one dekagrams almonds (some of which should be bitter) and grind finely
6. Put the same amount of fine broken sugar and finely chopped zest of one lemon in the bowl
7. Mix in seven whole eggs and four egg yolks one by one and beat until foamy
8. Spread half this filling on the seventh layer of pastry
9. Then stretch six more layers on top, brush each with melted butter
10. Spread the other half of the filling on top
11. Finally stretch the remaining pastry on top
12. Cut off the pastry hanging over the edge, brush the top with butter, and bake slowly until the pastry is beautiful golden yellow and crispy

title: Mandola-Lepény Nyújtva (Stretched Almond Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- dough as in recipe 465
- 14 dekagrams almonds
- 14 dekagrams raisins
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- butter
- sour cream
- egg white
- powdered sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Prepare and handle the dough exactly as in recipe 465
2. Soak the almonds and chop finely
3. Remove seeds from raisins and chop similarly
4. Finely grind the sugar
5. Add desired flavoring to the filling
6. Mix all filling ingredients together
7. Fill the cake twice as in recipe 465
8. When the final layer is stretched, brush with egg white
9. Sprinkle with sugar
10. Bake in gentle heat
11. When done, sprinkle with powdered sugar and vanilla

title: Mandola-parfait (Almond Cream Frozen Dessert)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- milk
- sweet cream
- eggs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepared exactly the same way as hazelnut parfait.

title: Mandola-pép friss megggyel (Almond Paste with Fresh Cherries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams almonds
- 28 dekagrams sugar
- zest of half a lemon, finely chopped
- 1 whole egg
- 6 egg yolks
- fresh ripe cherries, with stems removed
- sugar for poaching the cherries

## Directions

1. Soften the almonds and crush the sugar, then sift the sugar
2. Chop the lemon zest finely and place everything in a bowl
3. Beat in the whole egg and six egg yolks one after another
4. Stir the mixture for half an hour
5. Wash the ripe cherries, remove the stems, and lightly poach them with sugar so they remain whole
6. Butter a mold and line it with paper, then butter the paper again
7. Arrange the cherries neatly on the bottom of the mold
8. Spread half the almond paste on top and set in a water bath
9. After five to ten minutes, when the paste has firmed and the cherries have not sunk, arrange the remaining cherries on top
10. Pour the remaining almond paste over them
11. Work quickly without removing the mold from the water bath
12. Cover immediately and poach whole for one hour

title: Mandola-pép pisztácziával (Almond Paste with Pistachio)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramm butter, beaten foamy
- 14 dekagramm finely ground almonds
- 10 egg yolks
- 7 dekagramm peeled pistachios
- 14 dekagramm citronade
- 14 dekagramm finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dekagramm butter until foamy.
2. Add 14 dekagramm finely ground almonds and beat in 10 egg yolks one after another, stirring for a full hour.
3. Cut 7 dekagramm peeled pistachios and 14 dekagramm citronade into long strips.
4. Add this mixture along with 14 dekagramm finely ground sugar to the beaten paste.
5. Stir until everything is well combined.
6. Pour onto a buttered torta leaf and bake evenly.

title: Mandola-pép pisztácziával (Almond Paste with Pistachios)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams butter, whipped until frothy
- 14 dekagrams finely ground almonds
- 10 egg yolks
- 7 dekagrams blanched pistachios
- 7 dekagrams citron, cut lengthwise
- 14 dekagrams finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Add the finely ground almonds to the whipped butter
2. Mix in the egg yolks one after another, stirring for a whole hour
3. Cut the blanched pistachios and citron lengthwise into the same size
4. Add the pistachios and citron together with the finely ground sugar to the whipped paste
5. Stir until everything is well combined
6. Spread on a buttered pastry sheet
7. Bake in even heat until golden

title: Mandola-pép töltött almával (Almond Paste with Stuffed Apple)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 17 dekagramm softened almonds
- 17 dekagramm sugar
- 5 dekagramm raisins
- zest of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- 8-10 large apples
- preserves
- 2 egg whites for meringue
- apricot for meringue

## Directions

1. Finely grind 17 dekagramm softened almonds and 17 dekagramm sugar.
2. Place in a bowl with 5 dekagramm raisins and finely chopped zest of 1 lemon.
3. Beat with 2 whole eggs and 4 egg yolks for half an hour.
4. Fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 4 eggs.
5. Peel 8-10 large apples, hollow them out, and parboil them briefly in water.
6. Place the apples in a porcelain dish that will also be used for serving.
7. Fill the apples with preserves as desired, sprinkle with sugar.
8. Pour the paste over the apples and place in the oven.
9. Once baked, prepare a meringue from the whites of 2 eggs and apricot.
10. Arrange the meringue around the edge of the dish, heap high in a crown with a wide knife.
11. Make incisions with a demitasse spoon about two fingers wide apart to resemble a plaited crown.
12. Return to the oven and bake for another half hour.

title: Mandolakifli más módon (Almond Crescents Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams blanched almonds, finely chopped
- 8 egg whites
- 56 dekagrams sugar, finely crushed and sifted

## Directions

1. Finely chop the almonds, taking care that the pieces are as equal as possible
2. Whip the 8 egg whites into stiff peaks and gently mix in the finely crushed and sifted sugar
3. Place a small spoonful of this mixture on the chopped almonds and gently roll it in the almonds by hand
4. Using a card, place on waxed parchment and form into a crescent shape
5. Continue until all the mixture has been rolled in the almonds
6. Bake the crescents in gentle heat

title: Mandolaleves (Almond Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven deciliters sweet milk
- fourteen dekagrams sugar
- a little vanilla
- twelve dekagrams swollen finely ground almonds
- four egg yolks
- toasted breadcrumb slices

## Directions

1. Heat seven deciliters of sweet milk with fourteen dekagrams of sugar and a little vanilla.
2. Add twelve dekagrams of swollen finely ground almonds.
3. Bring to a boil once.
4. Before serving, beat four egg yolks in a pot and mix them with hot milk.
5. Place toasted breadcrumb slices in the soup tureen and pour half the soup over them.
6. Whip the other half of the soup until frothy and pour it over the bread slices in the tureen until it is full of foam.
7. Then pour the rest of the soup into the tureen gradually, which should be placed over a bed of coals.

title: Mandolatej (Almond Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams of peeled sweet almonds
- two dekagrams of peeled bitter almonds
- water
- twenty-eight dekagrams of crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Finely grate the sweet and bitter almonds with water
2. Mix with two liters of cold water slowly
3. Let stand for a short time
4. Strain through a clean, well-soaked cloth
5. Mix the almonds again with a little water and press
6. Mix well with the crushed sugar
7. Strain again and pour into bottles and set on ice

title: Mandolatorta Tejszínhabbal (Almond Cake with Whipped Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm soaked and ground almonds
- 28 dekagramm sugar rubbed on lemon
- 1 whole egg and 7 egg yolks
- 3.5 dekagramm sifted bread crumbs
- 4 egg whites
- 7 deciliter good cream
- sugar for sweetness
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Whip soaked ground almonds with lemon sugar, whole egg and seven egg yolks until it becomes good and frothy, about half an hour
2. Add sifted bread crumbs
3. Finally fold in stiffly beaten egg white foam
4. Butter two equal cake layers and their rings
5. Fill mixture and bake slowly
6. Before serving, whip seven deciliters of good cream and add enough sugar and vanilla for sweetness
7. When almond layers are completely cooled, place on plate
8. Add half the whipped cream
9. Place second layer on top and remaining cream on top
10. Decorate edges with large Spanish pastry shell wreaths and serve quickly

title: Mandolás kádka (Almond Custard Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams soaked and finely ground almond
- 14 dekagrams ground sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 lemon, finely chopped zest
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix 14 dekagrams of soaked and finely ground almond with 14 dekagrams of ground sugar
2. Beat 2 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks into it alternately
3. When well and foamy, add the finely chopped zest of 1 lemon
4. Mix well
5. Fill a mold (kádka) buttered with this mixture
6. Bake at gentle heat

title: Mandolás-torma (Almond Horseradish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four deciliters good sour cream
- one small teaspoon flour
- one tablespoon stock
- a handful of soaked almonds
- a piece of sugar
- grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Mix the sour cream well with a small teaspoon of flour
2. Pour in one tablespoon of stock
3. Crush the soaked almonds and add them to the sauce together with a piece of sugar
4. Finally add grated horseradish

title: Mandolás-torma (Almond Horseradish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four deciliters good fresh cream
- small spoon flour
- meat broth
- handful swelled almonds

## Directions

1. Mix the fresh cream with a small spoon of flour well, pour in a spoonful of broth, add a handful of swelled almonds

title: Mandula-sodó (Almond Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 liter cream
- 16 dekagrams finely ground almonds
- little lemon zest
- 2 egg yolks
- little flour
- cold milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Add 16 dekagrams finely ground almonds to 0.5 liter cream with a little lemon zest
2. Place on heat and boil covered for a quarter hour
3. Mix 2 egg yolks with a little flour and cold milk
4. Pour the boiled cream over it
5. Add sugar and bring to a boil
6. Strain and serve as sauce for pudding or cake

title: Maraszkin fagylalt (Maraschino Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagrams sugar
- 4 deciliters water
- approximately 4 deciliters maraschino liqueur
- juice of 6 lemons

## Directions

1. Cook 21 dekagrams sugar in 4 deciliters water
2. Pour in approximately 4 deciliters maraschino liqueur
3. Press in the juice of 6 lemons
4. Proceed as with other ice creams
5. Work quickly and salt the ice heavily around the freezing container, as alcoholic ice creams freeze with difficulty

title: Marechal-hajkenő (Marechal Hair Pomade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram mutton tallow
- sixty-five dekagrams vegetable oil
- five hundred grams hair powder
- thirteen dekagrams jasmine oil
- two dekagrams cedarwood oil
- three dekagrams bergamot oil
- one dekagram orange blossom oil
- two dekagrams clove oil
- sixteen drops of cinnamon oil

## Directions

1. Combine one kilogram of mutton tallow with sixty-five dekagrams of vegetable oil, five hundred grams of hair powder, thirteen dekagrams of jasmine oil, two dekagrams of cedarwood oil, three dekagrams of bergamot oil, one dekagram of orange blossom oil, two dekagrams of clove oil, and sixteen drops of cinnamon oil

title: Marha-nyelv Mandolás Mártással (Beef Tongue with Almond Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- salt
- water
- bacon slices or red onion
- carrot
- cloves
- veal bones
- flour
- red wine
- broth
- ground almonds (5-7 dekagrams)
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue in salted water until tender
2. Clean the tongue of its skin
3. Place it in a pot with bacon slices or sliced red onion, sliced carrot, a few cloves, and veal bones
4. Braise these together until nicely browned
5. Sprinkle a spoonful of flour over it and brown it
6. Deglaze with half red wine and half broth
7. Cook well and strain the sauce through a sieve into another pot
8. Add 5-7 dekagrams of ground almonds to the sauce
9. Place the tongue back in and cook well
10. Before serving, garnish the tongue with almonds
11. Pour the sauce into the serving dish and place the tongue on top

title: Marhahús Barnán (Beef Browned in Sugar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of sugar
- wine
- cooked beef
- beef broth
- butter
- flour
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. In a pot, boil sugar and a small amount of wine until it turns chestnut brown
2. Remove the cooked beef from the cooking liquid, dry it well, and place it in the brown sugar
3. Brown it on all sides
4. Pour a small amount of beef broth over it
5. Add a small piece of butter rolled in flour and finely chopped lemon zest
6. Remove the fat and strain the sauce through a sieve over the meat

title: Marhahús Meggy- vagy Málnalével (Beef with Cherry or Raspberry Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of lean top round beef
- butter
- sugar
- glacé or brown broth
- raspberry or cherry syrup

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of lean top round beef until tender
2. Melt butter in a pot, add a few pieces of sugar, and brown it beautifully
3. Add a small amount of glacé or brown broth and let it simmer
4. Just before serving, add one or two spoonfuls of raspberry or cherry syrup
5. Simmer well with frequent turning, ensuring both sides become beautifully glossy

title: Marhahús papírtekercsben (Beef Wrapped in Paper)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Two kilograms roast beef
- Bouillon, two serving spoons
- Green parsley
- Small amount onion
- Eighteen mushrooms
- Butter
- Cracked pepper
- Salt
- White paper

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the beef, pound well, place in a pot, and pour two serving spoons of bouillon over it
2. Braise until the liquid has completely evaporated
3. Finely chop green parsley, a small amount of onion, and eighteen mushrooms
4. Mix with a piece of butter, cracked pepper, and salt
5. Coat the beef slices with this mixture
6. Wrap each slice in white paper that has been cut with decorative edges and folded
7. Butter another sheet of paper, place it on the roasting pan, arrange the paper-wrapped beef on it
8. Roast over gentle heat, turning frequently on all sides
9. When completely cooked, serve the beef in the paper wrappings

title: Marhahús Új Modorban (Beef in a New Fashion)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three kilograms of beef loin
- bacon
- salt

## Directions

1. For fourteen to sixteen people, take three kilograms of beef loin
2. Remove all membranes from the meat
3. Lard it in three to four rows
4. Salt it and prepare for braising as described previously
5. Take particular care that the bacon remains whole and the beef loin braised to a beautiful brown color

title: Marhahús új modorban (Beef in the Modern Manner)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three kilograms kidney roast beef
- bacon (to lard)
- salt
- beef broth
- butter
- lemon zest
- green peas
- salt water
- fresh parsley (finely chopped)
- beef broth
- sugar (crushed)
- hot butter
- three kilograms beef from the chuck
- sour cabbage
- macaroni
- salt water
- fresh hot butter
- lean finely chopped ham
- carrots (one and a half centimeter pieces)
- butter (half egg-sized)
- sugar (crushed)
- white broth
- salt
- white cooked meat
- artichokes
- mushrooms (button)
- green parsley
- butter
- cauliflower
- blue cabbage
- vinegar
- fresh bacon

## Directions

1. Clean the kidney roast beef of all connective tissue from the wider part
2. Pierce it in 3-4 rows, season with salt
3. Prepare for braising as previously described, ensuring the bacon remains whole and the beef roasts to a nice brown
4. Before serving, completely coat the beef in a small amount of sauce that should be glace-like consistency, then slice
5. Similarly cook three kilograms of chuck beef
6. Serve the beef surrounded by a crown of garnishes: green peas (boiled in salted water, mixed with finely chopped parsley, a little broth, and crushed sugar in hot butter, then shaken well and braised slightly to maintain their green color)
7. Prepare sour cabbage braised to a nice brown-yellow as described in the book
8. Cook macaroni in salted water, toss in fresh hot butter, sprinkle generously with lean finely chopped ham
9. Prepare carrots by cutting into one and a half centimeter pieces, shaping the bottom point finely and the top proportionally into a head
10. Melt half an egg-sized piece of butter, stir in a spoon of crushed sugar, brown to golden yellow, pour in a spoonful of white broth, add the carrots, season lightly with salt, shake several times until they become completely soft but remain whole
11. Before serving, cut the white-cooked meat and kidney roast beef into uniform pieces, arrange on a long platter in cross pattern so one row is white meat and the next is brown meat
12. Arrange the crown of garnishes around the meat (green peas, braised cabbage, macaroni with ham, carrots) in high, evenly wide strips

title: Marhahús-karbonád Szardellával (Beef Carbonade with Anchovy)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney steak
- salt
- butter
- lemon
- heavy cream
- brains
- parsley
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- eggs

## Directions

1. Trim all fibrous parts from the kidney steak and chop finely
2. Make small carbonades, salt them, coat with melted butter, and place in a pot
3. Squeeze lemon juice over and cover with two deciliters of heavy cream
4. Cover and place coals on top, braising until the sauce reduces
5. Place on a round dish
6. Cut triangular bread slices and fry them
7. Separately, cook brains in salted water, chop them finely, and braise with a little butter, parsley, salt, and pepper
8. Spread this mixture on the fried bread, place another piece of bread on top so the brain does not show
9. Arrange the bread pieces around the dish rim
10. Place the carbonades in the center in a circle, forming a crown

title: Marhahús-szeletek (Beefsteak)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7-8 large yellow potatoes
- 7 decagrams butter
- bagdány pecsenyé (beef)
- 3 medium red onions
- parsley, green and chopped
- black pepper, crushed
- fat, several spoonfuls

## Directions

1. Wash 7-8 large yellow potatoes, peel and slice them
2. Cook until completely soft in salted water
3. Drain and mix with 7 decagrams of butter
4. Press through a fine sieve and keep warm
5. Cut thin, round slices from well-beaten and cleaned beef
6. Salt them and mix in 3 medium red onions, a handful of chopped green parsley and a knife-point of crushed black pepper
7. Place several spoonfuls of fat in a flat pot and place the slices with the mixture in it
8. Braise until the slices are cooked; the red onion and parsley should not break apart but only become light yellow
9. At serving time, place the potato purée at the bottom of the dish and heap it in the middle
10. Coat the slices thickly with the braised red onion and parsley, first straining off the fat and mixing it into the purée
11. Arrange the slices nicely around the edge of the dish so they form a crown

title: Marhanyelv-frieandeaux (Beef Tongue Fricandeau)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- parsley
- onion for cutting
- yellow carrot
- salt
- finely chopped bacon
- bacon
- red onion
- yellow carrot
- ham
- veal shank
- brown gravy
- good vinegar

## Directions

1. Put the beef tongue in a pot with a bunch of parsley, cutting onion, yellow carrot and salt, and cook until the skin can be pulled off.
2. Lard it with finely chopped bacon in three or four rows.
3. Place bacon, red onion, yellow carrot, ham and veal shank all sliced into a pan.
4. Place the tongues in it and fill with brown gravy.
5. Cover the pan and braise gently until tender.
6. Remove the tongues and strain the gravy.
7. Pour two spoonfuls of good vinegar into it and cook until it becomes glace.
8. Place the tongue back in it, turn on all sides and the dish is ready.
9. Place braised small red onions around the edge of the plate.

title: Marrow Dough (Velős tészta)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dekagrams melted butter
- 3 cooking spoonfuls flour
- 4 deciliters hot milk
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- butter for greasing
- 1 to 1.5 calf brains
- salted water
- finely chopped red onion
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Roast the flour in the melted butter
2. Slowly pour in the hot milk while continuously stirring and cook until it becomes a smooth paste
3. Remove from heat and cool
4. Slowly mix in 6 egg yolks, season lightly with salt
5. Mix in the stiff snow from 3 egg whites
6. Grease a large flat baking dish or plate with a two-finger wide rim with butter
7. Place one third of the paste in it and bake
8. Meanwhile, scald the calf brain in salted water, clean it and cut into small slices
9. Braise with finely chopped red onion and fresh parsley, season with a little salt and pepper
10. Grease a baking dish with butter
11. Divide the baked paste into three equal slices
12. Place the first slice in the dish and fill it with the prepared brains
13. Add the second slice and fill again, then add the third slice and fill that as well
14. Finally cover with the remaining unbaked dough
15. Cook in a water bath for a good half hour
16. Turn out onto a platter and serve quickly
17. For a sweet version, place fresh and heavily sugared fruit such as apricots between the baked layers instead of brains, and sprinkle with sugar when baked; do not salt the paste but mix in vanilla sugar instead

title: Marsall-víz (Marshall Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram purified alcohol
- 7 dekagrams cinnamon essence
- 2 dekagrams sassafras essence
- 14 dekagrams bergamot oil
- 14 dekagrams amber essence
- 2 dekagrams jasmine essence
- 7 dekagrams ambergris essence
- 7 dekagrams musk essence

## Directions

1. Add all ingredients to 1 kilogram purified alcohol
2. Shake well

title: Masszedoán (Macedoine Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven lemon juices
- eight deciliters water
- juice of two lemons
- thirty-five dekagrams crushed sugar
- four and one-half dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- green, thinly sliced almonds
- beautiful, large preserved fruit in sugar

## Directions

1. Mix seven lemon juices with eight deciliters of water
2. In this mixture, dissolve thirty-five dekagrams of crushed sugar that has been rubbed on two lemons
3. Mix in four and one-half dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
4. Strain through a taut cloth until clear
5. Pour into a mold to a height of two centimeters and place on ice until it freezes
6. Arrange a dense wreath of green, thinly sliced almonds and beautiful, large sugar-preserved fruit around the edge of the mold
7. Arrange two rows of leaves, one row with tips pointing down and the other pointing up, with the fruit in the middle connecting with the leaves
8. Arrange a star in the same manner in the center of the mold
9. Pour a coffee-cup-sized amount of jelly on top
10. When this sets enough to hold the fruit in place, pour another layer of jelly so that jelly covers one-third of it
11. Layer in sliced quince or quince paste as previously described
12. For each new layer of fruit, initially pour only a small amount of jelly so the fruit does not rise, first securing it with little jelly, then pouring more on top
13. The third layer of jelly should be filled with whole or half sugar-cooked apricots, pour similarly small amounts of jelly on these until they freeze
14. Finally add the remaining jelly
15. Keep on ice until use and unmold as with the others

title: Mazsolás Kifli (Raisin Crescent)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters flour
- 0.5 kilogram butter
- 5 dekagrams melted cheese yeast
- 6 tablespoons sour cream
- 10 egg yolks
- lukewarm milk as needed
- salt as needed
- 1 kilogram raisins
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 2 tablespoons water
- 14 dekagrams candied citron, finely chopped
- candied lemon zest (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough in the same way as recipe 595
2. For the filling: chop the raisins finely
3. Cook the sugar in 2 tablespoons of water until it threads
4. Add the raisins to the syrup
5. Add the candied citron and mix well
6. Cool the filling
7. Fill the croissants with this mixture
8. Optionally add finely chopped candied lemon zest to the filling

title: Meg-gy Párban (Spanish Cherries in Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Spanish cherries
- sugar
- water
- 1 kilogram fruit requires 2 deciliters water for every 500 grams sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from beautiful Spanish cherries, wash and strain them
2. Place them in jars, fill more than halfway with cooked and cooled sugar water (calculating 2 deciliters water for every 500 grams sugar)
3. Treat them as you would regular cherries

title: Meggy mézbe berakva (Black Cherries Preserved in Honey)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- larger black cherries with stems removed
- 3 dekagrams cinnamon per 4.25 liter barrel
- 1 dekagram cloves per 4.25 liter barrel
- 2 liters good white honey
- clean linen cloth
- clean linen for the bung

## Directions

1. Prepare a barrel slightly smaller than one used for cucumbers.
2. Wash and dry large black cherries after removing their stems.
3. For a 4.25 liter barrel, crush 3 dekagrams cinnamon and 1 dekagram cloves.
4. Divide the spices equally among three small bags made from clean linen and sew them closed.
5. Place one spice bag on the bottom of the barrel.
6. Cook 2 liters of good white honey, skim it clean, and let it cool.
7. Pour some of the cooled liquid honey on the barrel bottom and place some cherries on top.
8. Add another spice bag on the cherries, then the remaining cherries, and place the third spice bag and remaining honey on top.
9. Leave two finger-widths of space at the top of the barrel.
10. Place clean linen on the bung, drive it in firmly, and store in a cool place.

title: Meggy-fagylalt (Sour Cherry Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cherries
- 42 decagrams sugar
- 2 deciliters water
- 3 lemons
- 4 deciliters cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the sour cherries clean, crush them in a mortar, and press through cloth
2. Cook 42 decagrams sugar in 2 deciliters water until the sugar is completely dissolved
3. Press the juice of 3 lemons into the cherry juice, mix well, and proceed as with other ice creams
4. If you want to mix it with cream, use 4 deciliters of good cream per 7 deciliters of juice, dissolve 14 decagrams sugar in it, and mix it with the cherry juice; otherwise proceed as with other ice creams, except that if cream is added, the lemon juice is omitted

title: Meggy-kocsonya (Sour Cherry Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful ripe sour cherries with stems removed
- 7 deciliters sour cherry juice
- juice of 2 lemons
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 2 deciliters water
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Wash ripe cherries and crush in a mortar with their pits
2. Extract the juice
3. Use 7 deciliters of cherry juice with the strained juice of 2 lemons
4. Cook 42 dekagrams of sugar with 2 deciliters of water until it forms heavy drops
5. Mix the cooked sugar with the cherry juice
6. Add 4.5 dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
7. Strain through cloth and blotting paper
8. Pour into mold and freeze

title: Meggy-kádka (Sour Cherry Custard Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams sifted sugar
- 7 dekagrams soaked and finely ground almond
- 4 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- 1 lemon, finely chopped zest
- 7 dekagrams breadcrumb (kifli)
- butter
- sour cherries

## Directions

1. Stir together for one hour: 14 dekagrams of sifted sugar, 7 dekagrams of soaked and finely ground almond, 4 whole eggs, 4 egg yolks, and 4 egg whites beaten stiffly
2. Finally add the finely chopped zest of 1 lemon and 7 dekagrams of breadcrumb (kifli)
3. Mix this well with the rest
4. Fill a buttered mold (kádka) with this mixture
5. Place 5 sour cherries in each one
6. Bake at gentle heat

title: Meggy-Lepény (Sour Cherry Tart)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crumbly dough
- ripe sour cherries
- sugar
- egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare crumbly dough and roll out to three knife-widths thickness
2. Place on a baking sheet, make a finger-width border cut along the edge and fold up all around
3. Pit beautiful ripe sour cherries, strain their juice, and arrange on the tart
4. Sprinkle with sugar, brush the folded edges with egg white, and bake
5. When done, cut into equal portions, sprinkle with sugar and arrange neatly on a platter

title: Meggy-lepény más módon (Sour Cherry Pastry, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 70 grams butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 140 grams finely ground almonds
- 140 grams finely ground sugar
- 4 egg whites
- 145 grams fine buttered breadcrumbs
- 500 grams ripe fresh sour cherries
- butter for greasing
- fine breadcrumbs for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Beat 70 grams butter until fluffy
2. Add 8 egg yolks one by one, mixing well after each addition
3. Fold in 140 grams finely ground almonds and 140 grams finely ground sugar
4. Mix in 4 egg whites beaten stiff
5. Stir the whole mixture together for one hour
6. Add 145 grams fine buttered breadcrumbs
7. Finally fold in 500 grams ripe fresh sour cherries
8. Butter the pastry sheets and sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs
9. Fill with the prepared mixture
10. Bake for half an hour

title: Meggy-leves (Sour Cherry Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven deciliters of sour cherries
- A small amount of wine
- Four deciliters of wine
- Four deciliters of water
- Broken cinnamon
- Sugar
- Lemon peel, a small amount
- Toasted bread points

## Directions

1. Put the sour cherries in a pot with a small amount of wine and braise until reduced to a paste
2. Press through clean cloth
3. In a pot, pour four deciliters of wine and four deciliters of water over the paste
4. Flavor with broken cinnamon, sugar, and a small amount of lemon peel
5. Bring to a boil and serve into toasted bread points

title: Meggy-levet Befőzni (Cherry Juice Preserve)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- 1/2 kilogram sugar per 1/2 kilogram juice

## Directions

1. Crush the cherries, express the juice, strain it, take the same amount of sugar for each 1/2 kilogram of juice, otherwise treat it exactly as described in recipe 936.

title: Meggy-pép (Cherry Pap)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- finely ground vanilla
- 28 dekagrams ground sugar
- 28 dekagrams fine flour
- fresh cherries

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and beat in the egg yolks and whole eggs one by one
2. Add finely ground vanilla and ground sugar
3. Gently fold in the flour
4. Pour half the batter into a well-buttered mould
5. Arrange a row of fresh cherries on top
6. Sprinkle the cherries with sugar
7. Cover with the remaining batter
8. Bake for one hour

title: Meggy-rétes (Sour Cherry Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- 7 deciliters ripe sour cherries
- 14 decagrams coarsely crushed almonds
- 14 decagrams sugar
- 1 crumbly white bread, grated
- lemon peel, finely chopped
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough
2. Pit 7 deciliters of ripe sour cherries, stew them slightly and strain the juice
3. Mix 14 decagrams coarsely crushed almonds, 14 decagrams sugar, grated crumbly bread and finely chopped lemon peel
4. Stretch the dough finely and brush with melted butter
5. Spread the cherries evenly on it
6. Sprinkle the rest of the filling on top
7. Fold gently together
8. Place on a buttered parchment paper
9. Brush with melted butter and bake

title: Meggy-rétes tészta, élesztős tésztával (Sour Cherry Strudel with Yeast Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients
