- 9 decagrams butter, beaten until fluffy
- 6 whole eggs
- 1.5 decagrams soaked yeast
- 2 spoonfuls broken sugar
- a little salt
- 4 deciliters warm sour cream
- about 1 liter flour
- 14 decagrams plumped almonds
- 14 decagrams sugar
- 7 deciliters stewed sour cherries

## Directions

1. Beat 9 decagrams butter until fluffy and add 6 whole eggs one by one
2. Add 1.5 decagrams soaked yeast, 2 spoonfuls broken sugar, a little salt, 4 deciliters warm sour cream and enough flour so the dough is neither too thin nor too stiff
3. Let the dough rise well covered
4. Meanwhile grind 14 decagrams plumped almonds and 14 decagrams sugar
5. Stew 7 deciliters cherries, pit them and strain
6. When the dough has risen well, place on a cloth sprinkled with flour and stretch to knife-edge thickness
7. Only at the moment of use, mix the sugar and almonds and sprinkle on the dough
8. Place the strained cherries on top and sprinkle with broken sugar
9. Fold gently the edge of the dough that remained unfilled
10. Stretch the dough lengthwise so the strudel is not too thick
11. Place on a buttered baking sheet
12. Let rise for another quarter hour
13. Brush with egg white and sugar
14. Bake in gentle but sustained heat
15. When baked, place on a sieve and cool
16. Serve dusted with sugar
17. This can also be made the same way with crushed walnuts, sugar, vanilla and chopped raisins

title: Meggy-sodó (Cherry Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter fresh cherries
- 0.5 liter water
- 0.25 liter wine
- sugar
- 1 piece cinnamon
- lemon zest
- clove
- 1 tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Crush 1 liter of fresh cherries together with their pits in a mortar
2. Cook in 0.5 liter water and 0.25 liter wine
3. Strain through a sieve
4. Add sugar, 1 piece cinnamon, lemon zest, clove, and 1 tablespoon flour mixed with a little water
5. Bring to a boil again and serve alongside cake or pudding

title: Meggyet eczetbe becsinálni (Spanish Cherries in Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams Spanish cherries with stems half-cut
- 500 grams finely crushed sugar
- 1 coffee cup good white vinegar

## Directions

1. Place a few spoonfuls of sugar in a preserving jar.
2. Layer alternately: cherries, sugar, cherries, sugar, until all is used.
3. Pour 1 coffee cup of good white vinegar over it.
4. Tie the jar and leave in the sun for five to six days during which time the sugar dissolves.
5. Strain the liquid and cook it until it forms heavy drops.
6. Cool it, pour over the cherries, and tie closed only after 24 hours.

title: Meleg bor (Warm Wine)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- good white or red wine
- sugar to taste
- several cloves
- a piece of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Sweeten the wine to taste
2. Add the cloves and cinnamon
3. Bring to a boil
4. Remove the spices
5. Serve hot in glasses or in bottles wrapped with cloth

title: Meleg Szalonpudding (Warm Bacon Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- four and one half decilitres of good sour cream
- vanilla
- powdered sugar
- ten dekagrams of candied fruit of various types
- six egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat eight egg yolks with four and one half decilitres of good sour cream
2. Add finely ground vanilla and powdered sugar to taste
3. Place the mixture over charcoal fire and stir until it begins to thicken
4. Remove quickly and stir for a few minutes so it does not curdle
5. Set aside until completely cooled
6. Cut ten dekagrams of various candied fruits into small cubes and mix into the cream
7. Gradually fold in the stiff whites of six eggs
8. Line a buttered mould with seven dekagrams of fine ladyfinger biscuits, placing them with their shiny sides facing outward and standing upright
9. Place the mixture in the mould
10. Steam for three quarters of an hour
11. Turn out and serve immediately

title: Meleg tojásos puncs (Warm Egg Punch)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half a bottle of Bakateri or other white wine
- half a bottle of arak
- eighty dekagrams of crushed sugar
- juice of two oranges and two lemons
- finely peeled zest of one orange
- eighteen eggs
- seven deciliters of water

## Directions

1. In a deep vessel, combine six whole eggs and twelve egg yolks with the sugar, stirring well together
2. Slowly add the wine, arak, and water, as well as the zest of one orange
3. Place the vessel in a pan over charcoal fire
4. Beat the mixture with birch twigs tied together until it thickens completely and fills the vessel with fine foam
5. Remove from heat and continue beating for six more minutes
6. Strain through a fine sieve into glasses

title: Menyhal kifejtett kagylóeskákkal és szarvasgombával (Lamprey with Shelled Shellfish and Mushrooms)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lamprey
- salt
- butter
- flour
- onion
- lemon peel and juice
- pea broth with vegetables
- 25 small shellfish
- 6-8 mushrooms
- fresh parsley
- 0.5 cup good wine
- 3-4 tablespoons sour cream
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clean the lamprey and form it into a ring, wash and salt it
2. Melt butter, stir in 2 teaspoons flour, add half an onion and a piece of lemon peel, dilute with pea broth in which roots have been cooked
3. Add the lamprey and bring to a boil so it remains whole, then strain the sauce and add lemon juice and bring to a boil again
4. Meanwhile, shell 25 small shellfish and cut 6-8 mushrooms into leaves, braise with fresh butter and finely chopped green parsley
5. Add 0.5 cup good wine, 3-4 tablespoons sour cream, the shelled shellfish and braise together for several minutes
6. In a small cup, beat 2 egg yolks well, add the lamprey sauce, place the cup on a bed of coals and strain the sauce with continuous stirring
7. Place the lamprey in the center of the dish, pour a little sauce over it and arrange the shellfish and mushrooms in a wreath around the lamprey

title: Menyhal osztrigával (Lamprey with Oysters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lampreys
- salt
- oysters
- pike liver, minced
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- pea broth
- lemon juice and peel
- sour cream

## Directions

1. After opening the lampreys, salt them and lard them so they maintain their shape and cook them well seasoned
2. Take oysters from their shells, cook them through, add minced pike liver and braise both together in butter
3. Brown some breadcrumbs in butter, pour 2 tablespoons pea broth over it, add lemon juice and peel and 2 tablespoons sour cream
4. Arrange lampreys on a plate, pour the sauce over, bring once to a boil and serve

title: Metélt fánkocsaka (Fried Noodle Cakes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Four deciliters flour
- One egg
- Two egg yolks
- One liter hot cream
- Ten dekagrams finely ground almonds
- Vanilla
- Butter
- Flour for dusting
- Beaten egg for dipping
- Breadcrumbs
- Fat for frying
- Sugar and vanilla for serving

## Directions

1. Make common noodle dough from flour, egg and egg yolks
2. Roll out and cut wide noodles
3. Cook in one liter hot cream
4. Add finely ground almonds and vanilla, cook until cream is completely reduced
5. Spread on a buttered baking sheet to the thickness of a finger and cool
6. Turn out onto a flour-dusted board
7. Break into small fried cake pieces
8. Dip in beaten egg, coat with flour and fine breadcrumbs
9. Fry until golden yellow in fat
10. Sprinkle with sugar and vanilla when serving

title: Metélőhagyma-mártás (Chive Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- three fresh anchovies or one herring
- egg yolks
- fine olive oil
- two tablespoons finely chopped chives
- white vinegar
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil three eggs hard
2. Finely chop the whites
3. Clean and wash three fresh anchovies or one herring, cut into cubes
4. Smooth the egg yolks with fine olive oil
5. Add two tablespoons of finely chopped chives and the above ingredients to this
6. Thin with white vinegar and mix well
7. A little sugar can also be added

title: Millefleur (Millefleurs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram strong alcohol
- 4 grams orange blossom oil
- 1 gram clove oil
- 0.5 kilogram bouquet water
- 14 dekagrams rose water
- 2 grams benzoin dye
- 1 gram ambergris essence
- 1 gram musk essence

## Directions

1. Dissolve 4 grams orange blossom oil and 1 gram clove oil in 0.5 kilogram strong alcohol
2. Add 0.5 kilogram bouquet water, 14 dekagrams rose water, 2 grams benzoin dye, 1 gram ambergris essence, and 1 gram musk essence
3. Mix well
4. Strain

title: Millefleur Hair Pomade
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram fresh mutton tallow
- 75 dekagrams vegetable oil
- 13 dekagrams hair powder
- 4.5 dekagrams amber coloring
- 3 dekagrams orange flower oil
- 2 dekagrams cedarwood oil
- 2 dekagrams musk coloring
- 70 drops cinnamon oil
- 4 grams Peruvian balsam
- 4 grams rosewood oil
- 2 grams clove oil
- 4 grams thyme oil

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients with the fresh mutton tallow base

title: Millefleur-hajkenő (Millefleur Hair Pomade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram fresh mutton tallow
- seventy-five dekagrams vegetable oil
- thirteen dekagrams hair powder
- four and one-half dekagrams amber dye
- three dekagrams orange blossom oil
- two dekagrams cedarwood oil
- two dekagrams musk dye
- seventy drops of cinnamon oil
- four grams Peruvian balsam
- four grams rosewood oil
- two grams clove oil
- four grams thyme oil

## Directions

1. Combine one kilogram of fresh mutton tallow with seventy-five dekagrams of vegetable oil, thirteen dekagrams of hair powder, four and one-half dekagrams of amber dye, three dekagrams of orange blossom oil, two dekagrams of cedarwood oil, two dekagrams of musk dye, seventy drops of cinnamon oil, four grams of Peruvian balsam, four grams of rosewood oil, two grams of clove oil, and four grams of thyme oil

title: Mogyoró-fagylalt (Hazelnut Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams large Italian hazelnuts
- 7 deciliters good cream
- 21 dekagrams ground sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the large Italian hazelnuts similarly to almonds
2. Clean in cold water
3. Grind to a fine paste
4. Boil 7 deciliters of good cream
5. Place the ground hazelnuts in a soup pot
6. Pour the cream warm over it
7. Let stand for one hour, stirring frequently
8. Add 21 dekagrams ground sugar
9. Stir frequently
10. When the sugar has melted and the mixture has cooled, strain through cloth
11. Pour into the container and treat as with the other ice creams

title: Mogyoró-parfait (Hazelnut Cream Frozen Dessert)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagrams hazelnut kernels
- milk
- 1.4 liters hot sweet cream
- 16 egg yolks
- 42 dekagrams powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Finely grind 21 dekagrams hazelnut kernels in milk, then pour into a pot.
2. Pour in 1.4 liters hot sweet cream and stir until boiling, then allow to cool.
3. Thoroughly blend 16 egg yolks with 42 dekagrams powdered sugar.
4. Press the cream through a clean kitchen cloth and pour into the eggs.
5. Stir over gentle charcoal fire with a whisk until nearly boiling, but it must not boil.
6. After straining, continue stirring until cooled, then pour into the freezer.

title: Mogyoró-rudacskák (Hazelnut Sticks)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams hazelnut mixture
- 14 dekagrams sugar for the frosting
- Egg white
- Few drops lemon juice
- White frosting

## Directions

1. Prepare dough as specified in recipe 766
2. Spread two knife-widths thick on sugar-sprinkled board in rectangular leaf shape
3. Cover smoothly with white frosting
4. Coat baking sheet with wax beforehand
5. Cut frosted hazelnut dough into sticks two centimeters wide and finger length
6. Place on sheet with knife
7. Dry in completely cool oven
8. Peel from sheet with knife and arrange neatly on plate
9. Finely pound and sift 14 dekagrams sugar per 28 dekagrams hazelnut mixture
10. Beat frosting with egg white and a few drops lemon juice in small bowl until stiff

title: Mogyoró-rudacskák más módon (Hazelnut Sticks Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams shelled hazelnuts, dried well in cloth
- 28 dekagrams sifted sugar
- egg white
- apricot marmalade
- lemon icing

## Directions

1. Mix the hazelnuts well with the sifted sugar
2. On a board with a wide knife, work it well with egg white added slowly, so the dough is not too soft
3. Stretch out on a sugar-dusted board to two knife-blade widths thick
4. Cut with a sharp knife dipped in sugar into finger-length and thumb-wide sticks
5. Place on thinly waxed parchment and dry in a well-cooled oven
6. Remove with a knife and cool
7. Fill with apricot marmalade, place another on top, and dip in lemon icing

title: Mogyoró-torta (Hazelnut Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams of hazelnuts
- twenty-eight dekagrams of sugar
- eleven egg yolks
- five egg whites
- three identical cake leaves and their hoops
- butter for coating
- apricot syrup for filling
- preserved raspberries for filling
- lemon icing for coating
- quince paste or candied quince
- green almond for decoration
- preserved apricot or a beautiful apple for decoration

## Directions

1. Shell the hazelnuts, rub well in cloth, and grind them.
2. Similarly grind twenty-eight dekagrams of sugar and sift it.
3. Place the sugar in a basin with eleven egg yolks and beat until white and well foamy.
4. Add the ground hazelnuts and beat well.
5. Fold in the stiff foam of five egg whites.
6. Coat three identical cake leaves and their hoops with butter.
7. Fill with the mixture and bake over gentle heat.
8. To test if well baked, after half an hour insert a large needle into the center of the cake; if the needle remains clean, turn the cake out onto a sieve and cool it.
9. When completely cooled, coat the first layer with apricot syrup, place the second on top, fill it with preserved raspberries, and cover with the third layer.
10. Cover the entire cake evenly with lemon icing.
11. Cut long slices from quince paste or candied quince.
12. Slice green almond lengthwise and arrange a star in the center of the cake.
13. Place a whole preserved apricot or a beautiful apple in the center of the almond star.
14. Arrange the quince paste strips at finger-width intervals around the edge of the cake, placing the green almond leaves beside them as if serving as a stem.
15. Place the cake on a plate covered with a neatly folded napkin.

title: Mornay-mártás (Mornay Sauce - Warm Fish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- milk
- grated Parmesan cheese
- butter (20-25 dgr more)
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Make a roux from butter and flour
2. Dilute with milk to obtain a fairly thick béchamel
3. Simmer the béchamel while stirring
4. Add one weak tablespoon of grated Parmesan cheese per three tablespoons of béchamel
5. Slowly mix in 20-25 dgr of butter while continuously stirring with a whisk to obtain a smooth sauce
6. After adding the cheese, do not stir over heat, only keep it warm
7. Season with pepper and salt
8. Use with pre-cooked fish, meat, or eggs by pouring the thick sauce over them, sprinkling with Parmesan cheese and baking in a hot oven for one to two minutes

title: Morzsatorta (Crumb Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- forty-two dekagrams flour
- twenty-eight dekagrams butter
- twenty-eight dekagrams softened almonds
- twenty-eight dekagrams finely ground sugar
- one lemon, finely chopped zest
- cooked cherries or currants for filling
- egg white
- coarse sugar

## Directions

1. Work the flour well with the butter using a rolling pin
2. Mix together the softened almonds, finely ground sugar, and finely chopped lemon zest
3. Prepare the pastry so it holds together
4. Roll to a finger-width thickness
5. Place on a pastry leaf
6. Fill with cooked cherries or currants
7. Make a lattice pattern on top
8. Surround with a band
9. Brush with egg white
10. Dust with coarse sugar
11. Bake at gentle heat

title: Multiply-Baked Cake (Többször sült lepény)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams butter
- flour
- fresh sour cherries
- powdered sugar
- 7 decilitres cream
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- vanilla
- 10 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make a crumbly dough from 28 dekagrams butter and roll it as thin as a knife blade
2. Place it on a flat prepared pan and prick it with a fork to prevent it from puffing up during baking
3. Bake in the oven until golden
4. Cover with enough fresh sour cherries to cover the bottom and sprinkle well with powdered sugar and bake through
5. When the dough is well baked, prepare 7 decilitres of cream with 14 dekagrams sugar, a little vanilla and ten egg yolks, whisk together in a pan over gentle heat until it begins to thicken
6. Pour half of this mixture carefully over the fruit, quickly place in the oven until set, then pour the remaining half over and place in the oven again
7. When set, remove, cool, cut into large cubes and serve
8. This cake can be made the same way with apricots or raspberries - peel the apricots, cut them in half, cover the dough with them, sprinkle well with sugar and bake; when baked, pour the same sauce over it as before; note that the inner edge of the pan should be well coated with egg white and the dough pressed together firmly at the edges or it will fall off; for the sauce use a coffee spoon of sifted vanilla

title: Mushroom Soup (Gombaleves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- clove mushrooms
- pea broth
- butter
- green parsley
- flour
- black pepper
- nutmeg flower
- sour cream
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Wash the clove mushrooms several times in warm water
2. Pour pea broth over them and cook in it
3. Pour the broth into another pot
4. Braise the mushrooms in butter and green parsley
5. Sprinkle a cooking spoon of flour over them
6. Pour the pea broth over it
7. Add a little ground pepper, nutmeg flower and a good tablespoon of sour cream
8. Serve on fried bread

title: Muskatály-szőlő-kocsonya kőmagú gyümölccsel (Muscatel Grape Jelly with Stone Fruits)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters grape juice
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- common water
- juice of 3 lemons
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- 2 ripe, peeled apricots cut into 4 pieces
- 4-5 mirabelle plums
- ripe blue grapes

## Directions

1. Embed a ring-shaped mold with a center opening in ice
2. Remove ripe flavorful grapes from their stems and press the juice through cloth
3. For 7 deciliters of juice, use 42 dekagrams of sugar
4. Cook the sugar with a cup of common water until the sugar begins to thread
5. Pour the juice into the sugar mixture and blend well
6. Press the juice of 3 lemons and mix with 4.5 dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
7. Strain through stretched cloth lined with blotting paper
8. Fill the outer form's empty space to the edges
9. Leave on ice until firm
10. In the empty center, carefully arrange 2 beautiful ripe peeled apricots cut into 4 pieces, 4-5 mirabelle plums, and ripe blue grapes dusted with sugar
11. Fill with remaining jelly and freeze
12. Turn out onto a plate similar to other jellies

title: Muskatály-szőlő-kocsonya kőmagú gyümölcscsel (Muscatel Grape Jelly with Stone Fruits)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- well-ripened flavorful grapes
- forty-two dekagrams sugar
- seven deciliters grape juice
- juice of three lemons
- four and one-half dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Use a ring-shaped mold with a center opening that has a bottom, embedded in ice
2. Remove ripe flavorful grapes from their stems and extract the juice by pressing through cloth
3. For seven deciliters of juice, prepare forty-two dekagrams of sugar
4. Cook the sugar with a cup of ordinary water until it begins to form threads
5. Pour into the juice and mix well together
6. Squeeze in the juice of three lemons
7. Add four and one-half dekagrams of dissolved isinglass

title: Muskotályszőlő-fagylalt (Muscat Grape Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- muscat grapes
- 7 deciliters grape juice
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 1 glass water
- juice of 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Remove muscat grapes from stems and press them
2. Cook 42 dekagrams of sugar with 1 glass of water over 7 deciliters of juice until it forms heavy drops
3. Pour this into the juice
4. Add the juice of 4 lemons
5. Pour into the freezing container and freeze

title: Muskotályszőlő-fagylalt (Muscat Grape Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- muscat grapes
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 1 glass water
- juice of 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the muscat grapes and squeeze them
2. Cook 42 dekagrams sugar with 1 glass water until it forms heavy drops
3. Pour this syrup onto the grape juice
4. Add the juice of 4 lemons
5. Pour into a freezing container and freeze

title: Mustárvaj (Mustard Butter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- several boiled, salted and peppered egg yolks
- butter in appropriate quantity
- several spoonfuls of good quality mustard

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with butter until foamy
2. Finally mix in several spoonfuls of good quality mustard

title: Muszka csónok (Russian Boats)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams crushed sugar
- 14 dekagrams finely chopped almonds
- 1.5 dekagrams finely ground cinnamon
- 1.5 dekagrams cloves
- finely chopped zest of one lemon
- egg white
- fine sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. On a board, make dough from 14 dekagrams of crushed sugar, 14 dekagrams of finely chopped almonds, 1.5 dekagrams of finely ground cinnamon, 1.5 dekagrams of cloves, and the finely chopped zest of one lemon, using the stiffly beaten white of one egg
2. Dust the board with fine sugar and form boats, wreaths, or whatever shapes you like from it
3. Bake on a sheet greased with wax in a gentle oven and store dry

title: Mágnás-pörkölt (Magnate's Goulash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- meat for pörkölt
- white wine, two decilitres
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as pörkölt with lamb meat. Just before serving, pour two decilitres of white wine over it, add a small amount of pepper, and when it boils, serve.

title: Máié (Cornmeal Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half litres fresh cornmeal
- hot water
- a handful of wheat flour
- fat

## Directions

1. Pour one and a half litres of fresh cornmeal into a bowl and scald with hot water, let it become quite stiff, and set it aside for two hours in a warm place, the so-called nest
2. Then mix in a handful of wheat flour and let it cool for a couple of hours
3. Now spread the dough thinly (about pinky-finger thickness) onto a sheet thickly greased with fat, then pour fat generously over the top
4. Bake máié in very hot heat and cut into cubes

title: Májas Hurka (Liver Sausage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- raw liver soaked in cold water
- pork caul fat
- large red onion
- lard
- black pepper
- paprika
- cooked millet porridge
- black pepper
- hot water
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak raw liver in cold water and chop finely with a chopping knife, removing any vessels and knots
2. Remove all fat from the white liver and briefly boil and finely chop a handful of clean caul fat
3. Braise a large red onion in lard with pepper and a small amount of paprika
4. Mix in a moderate amount of cooked millet porridge, plenty of black pepper, a few spoonfuls of hot water to make the filling loose, and proper salt
5. The liver filling should be well-fatted and loose so the sausage will be tender
6. Add plenty of spices
7. Fill the intestines with a sausage filler, secure the ends with small wooden sticks, and boil in hot salted water

title: Május-ital (May Drink)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh sweet woodruff (Asperula odorata) picked before flowering
- weak white wine
- sugar to taste
- one orange sliced into rings

## Directions

1. Let the fresh woodruff wilt in the shade for 5-6 hours
2. Clean it of thick stems
3. Put in a bowl and cover with weak white wine (seven deciliters per handful of herb)
4. Sweeten to taste
5. Add the orange sliced into rings
6. After standing for one hour, pour into glasses and serve with the orange slices

title: Mákos rétes (Poppy Seed Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 decagrams butter, beaten until fluffy
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 spoon broken sugar
- 2 deciliters sour cream
- 1.5 decagrams fresh cheese yeast dissolved in the sour cream
- a little salt
- 7 deciliters flour
- 4 deciliters poppy seeds, finely ground
- 21 decagrams broken sugar
- a little cinnamon
- peel of half a lemon and half an orange, finely chopped
- 8 spoonfuls good sour cream

## Directions

1. Beat 14 decagrams butter until fluffy and add 8 egg yolks one by one
2. Well mixed, add 1 spoon broken sugar, 2 deciliters sour cream, fresh cheese yeast, a little salt and 7 deciliters flour
3. Mix everything well, knead the dough finely and let it rise slightly
4. When the dough has risen, place on a board well sprinkled with flour and stretch to knife-edge thickness
5. Finely grind 4 deciliters poppy seeds, mix with 21 decagrams broken sugar, a little cinnamon, and finely chopped peel of half a lemon and half an orange
6. Sprinkle the dough thickly with this filling, pour 8 spoonfuls of good sour cream over it and fold together
7. Place in a buttered flat dish and let it rise
8. Brush with butter, bake and sprinkle generously with sugar before serving

title: Málna- vagy Ribizli-levet Befőzni (Raspberry or Currant Juice Preserve)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram clear juice from raspberries or currants
- 1/2 kilogram sugar
- 4 decilitres water

## Directions

1. Press the juice of these fruits through cloth, then strain through filter paper.
2. Cook 1/2 kilogram clear juice with 1/2 kilogram sugar and 4 decilitres water, clean it well and cook until it threads and throws large bubbles.
3. Add the juice, cook on a gentle charcoal fire for a good quarter hour and carefully remove the foam.
4. Test by dropping a few drops on a plate, and if it no longer spreads, the juice is sufficiently cooked.
5. This juice must not cook any longer as otherwise it would lose flavor and color; then cool it, pour into jars, and put in a cool place.

title: Málna-, Ribizli-, vagy Meggy-levet Készíteni Kocsonyához és Fagylalthoz (Raspberry, Currant, or Cherry Juice for Jelly and Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries, currants, or cherries
- 1/2 kilogram broken sugar per 1/2 kilogram juice

## Directions

1. Press the raspberries, currants, or cherries through a clean cloth and strain the juice through filter paper so it is beautifully clear, doing this in a cold place so the juice does not boil.
2. Measure the juice and take the same amount of broken sugar for each 1/2 kilogram of juice, place the sugar with the juice in a clean white soup bowl and stir with a silver spoon until the sugar is completely dissolved.
3. Pour the juice into a dry glass bottle, filling only to the neck, then quickly stopper it firmly, cork it, and keep in a cool place.
4. If preparing the juice from cherries, crush them in a mortar so the seed flavor is also perceptible, press the juice through cloth, strain it and treat it the same way as above.

title: Málna-eczet (Raspberry Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 dekagrams fresh good raspberries
- 2 kilograms well-boiled vinegar

## Directions

1. Crush 3 dekagrams of fresh good raspberries slightly, and mix them in a bottle with 2 kilograms of well-boiled vinegar.
2. When the vinegar becomes beautifully red and has a pleasant raspberry smell, carefully pour off the liquid without pressing it, then let it settle.
3. Filter through grey blotting paper, fill small bottles completely with it, bind them tightly with bladder, and place them in the cellar in sand.

title: Málna-fagylalt (Raspberry Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- 7 deciliters milk
- 28 dekagrams sugar
- juice of 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Press the raspberries through clean cloth
2. Dissolve 28 dekagrams of sugar in 7 deciliters of milk
3. Add the juice of 4 lemons to the mixture
4. Pour the mixture into the freezing container and treat it like the other ice creams

title: Málna-fagylalt (Raspberry Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- 7 deciliters raspberry juice
- 28 dekagrams sugar
- 4 lemons, juice

## Directions

1. Press the raspberries through clean cloth
2. Dissolve 28 dekagrams sugar in 7 deciliters of juice
3. Add the juice of 4 lemons to this
4. Pour the mixture into the container and treat as with the others

title: Málna-Kocsonya (Raspberry Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- 7 deciliter raspberry juice
- 2 lemons
- 35 dekagramm sugar
- 4.5 dekagramm dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Press the cleaned raspberries through cloth
2. To 7 deciliter of raspberry juice, add juice of 2 lemons and 35 dekagramm sugar dissolved in the juice
3. Mix in 4.5 dekagramm dissolved isinglass
4. Pass through clean cloth
5. Pour into a mold and freeze on ice

title: Málnalevet Eczettel Befőzni Betegek Számára (Raspberry Juice Preserve with Vinegar for the Sick)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 liters raspberries
- 4 decilitres good white vinegar
- 56 dekagrams sugar
- 4 decilitres water

## Directions

1. Clean the raspberries, wash them, put them in a clean vessel; pour 4 decilitres of good white vinegar over 1.5 liters of raspberries and let it stand for several hours.
2. Press through cloth and strain; cook 56 dekagrams of clear juice with 70 dekagrams sugar and 4 decilitres water until the sugar threads and throws large bubbles, then add the juice, cook and treat it the same as the previous recipe.

title: Málnát Befőzni (Preserved Raspberries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram beautiful, large ripe raspberries
- half kilogram sugar
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. For half kilogram of beautiful large ripe raspberries, use the same amount of sugar, which cook with one glass of water over embers until it forms a jelly-like consistency when tested, then remove from heat and cool. Add the raspberries and briefly bring to a rapid boil over high heat without stirring or shaking, so the fruit remains whole. If making a sauce or filling, press the raspberries through a sieve and proceed as above.

title: Másfél kilogramm erős méz alapú eczet (Vinegar Based on One and a Half Kilograms Strong Honey)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kilograms strong honey
- 25 dekagrams red powdered tartar
- water
- 17 liters good brandy
- 0.5 kilogram rye bread
- 2 liters strong vinegar

## Directions

1. Pour 1.5 kilograms of strong honey and 25 dekagrams of red powdered tartar into a pot filled with water.
2. Keep the liquid at a boil with slow stirring until the tartar dissolves, then filter through cloth.
3. Cool this decoction until you can hold your hand in it, then pour it into a vinegar barrel and add 17 liters of good brandy.
4. Cut 0.5 kilogram of rye bread into small pieces, cover it with 2 liters of strong vinegar, heat it almost to boiling, then pour the mixture into the barrel, shake it well, place the barrel in a warm spot, and after 6-8 weeks carefully draw off the vinegar.

title: Méz-eczet (Honey Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 kilograms water
- 75 dekagrams honey
- 25 dekagrams tartar

## Directions

1. Take sweet water obtained from honey pomace, place it in sour earthenware pots, and set them next to the oven, by which means after 4-6 weeks you will obtain good vinegar.
2. Alternatively, take 12 kilograms of water with 75 dekagrams of honey (this amount is necessary for twelve liters), place them together on the fire, remove the foam, and add 25 dekagrams of tartar.
3. Boil the mixture for a time, then strain it through cloth into a barrel and place it in a warm spot so it boils for a few moments.
4. Draw off the now-ready vinegar, clarify the cloudy part in a pot with bladder, and pour the vinegar together with the clarified part into a small barrel.
5. Once it has sufficiently soured, draw it off into bottles, which, well-stoppered, will keep for a long time.

title: Mézeskalács (Honey Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams flour
- 14 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- the finely chopped zest of one lemon
- cinnamon
- a small amount of finely ground cloves
- hot honey
- split almonds

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with finely ground sugar, the finely chopped zest of a lemon, cinnamon, and a small amount of finely ground cloves
2. Make a dough with hot honey to the consistency of butter dough
3. Work the dough for a good half hour
4. Let it rest for three-quarters of an hour
5. Roll out to half a finger thickness
6. Shape into boats or other forms
7. Top with split almonds
8. Bake quickly in a well-heated oven

title: Mézhajkenő (Honey Hair Pomade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams honey
- seven dekagrams pork fat
- fourteen dekagrams Peruvian balsam
- seventy-five centigrams cedarwood oil
- seventy-five centigrams nutmeg oil
- four and one-half dekagrams musk

## Directions

1. Melt together twenty-eight dekagrams of honey, seven dekagrams of pork fat, and fourteen dekagrams of Peruvian balsam
2. During cooling, mix in seventy-five centigrams of cedarwood oil, the same amount of nutmeg oil, and four and one-half dekagrams of musk

title: Napoleon-torta csokoládéval bevonva (Napoleon Cake Coated with Chocolate)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm chocolate, softened in a warm oven
- butter
- 28 dekagramm almonds, shelled, broken, and sifted
- 28 dekagramm sugar, broken with vanilla and sifted
- 16 egg yolks
- egg white foam
- 14 dekagramm cornstarch
- apricot marmalade
- chocolate glaze
- various preserved fruits

## Directions

1. Soften 28 dekagramm chocolate in a warm oven and blend finely in a bowl with a piece of butter.
2. Add another piece of butter and blend smoothly, then continue alternating butter additions until 28 dekagramm of butter is fully incorporated.
3. Slowly add 28 dekagramm of shelled, broken, and sifted almonds, the same amount of sugar broken with vanilla and sifted, and 16 egg yolks.
4. Stir this mixture foamily for half an hour.
5. Finally fold in the stiffly beaten egg white foam and 14 dekagramm of cornstarch.
6. Prepare four equal, low-rimmed cake layers, butter them, dust with flour, fill with the mixture, and bake in a gently heated oven.
7. Once fully baked, turn out onto a sieve and cool.
8. Place on parchment paper and spread apricot marmalade to the thickness of a knife blade.
9. Stack the layers on top of each other and trim neatly.
10. Coat the entire cake evenly with chocolate glaze and set aside to dry.
11. Once completely dry, decorate with various preserved fruits.

title: Narancs-fagylalt (Orange Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 2 oranges
- juice of 10 oranges
- juice of 3 lemons
- 4 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Gently rub 14 dekagrams sugar on 2 oranges so it does not become bitter
2. Squeeze the juice from 10 oranges and 3 lemons
3. Cook the 14 dekagrams sugar with 4 deciliters water
4. When dissolved, pour into the orange juice
5. Mix well
6. Cool completely and pour into freezing container

title: Narancs-hab (Orange Foam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 6 large oranges and 8 small oranges, for juice
- 4 lemons, for juice
- fresh egg white
- 4.5 dekagrams dissolved, strained, cooled isinglass

## Directions

1. Rub 42 dekagrams of sugar on 3 oranges, being careful not to rub too deeply lest it become bitter
2. Squeeze the juice from 6 large and 8 small oranges and mix it with the juice of 4 lemons
3. Pour over the sugar to dissolve it
4. If not sweet enough, add a little more sugar
5. Pour this juice into a deep preserving basin, set on ice, add fresh egg white, and beat with a whip until the entire mass becomes foam
6. Cool the juice very well on ice beforehand and keep it on ice during beating
7. If the mixture will not come together, add a little more egg white
8. Finally, fold in 4.5 dekagrams of previously strained and cooled dissolved isinglass
9. Pour into a mold, set on ice until frozen
10. The same method can be used to make foam from any fresh fruit, such as raspberries, currants, or cherries; press the fruit through cloth, dissolve the sugar with it, and proceed in the same manner

title: Narancs-pép (Orange Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagrams almonds
- fresh water
- 2 oranges
- 21 dekagrams sugar
- 1 whole egg
- 4 egg yolks
- juice of 2 lemons
- 4 egg whites, whipped stiff

## Directions

1. Grind the almonds, sprinkling lightly with fresh water during grinding to prevent them becoming oily
2. Rub the sugar on two oranges, then grind it finely
3. Place the ground almonds and sugar in a bowl
4. Beat in the whole egg and four egg yolks
5. Stir in the lemon juice and beat everything for one hour
6. Finally fold in the stiffly whipped egg whites
7. Spread the paste on a buttered pastry sheet and bake
8. When nearly finished baking, whip two tablespoons of raspberry marmalade with seven dekagrams of ground sugar until very frothy
9. Fold in the stiff egg white meringue of four eggs, a little at a time, ensuring it is not watery
10. Remove the pastry sheet rim
11. Shape the meringue into a crown on top of the paste, piling it high with a knife
12. Make indentations every two finger-widths with a card edge to resemble a braided crown
13. Sprinkle with ground sugar
14. Return to the oven
15. Work very quickly to prevent the paste from collapsing

title: Narancs-torta (Orange Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large or 3 small oranges, zest grated
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 3 egg yolks
- 5 dekagramm butter, beaten to foam
- 28 dekagramm swollen and finely broken almonds
- 28 dekagramm sifted sugar
- 1.5 dekagramm fine flour
- preserved sour cherries
- egg white
- coarsely broken sugar

## Directions

1. Grate the zest of 2 large or 3 small oranges and finely pound it in a mortar with 6 hard-boiled egg yolks.
2. Beat 5 dekagramm of butter to a foam, then gradually blend in 3 egg yolks one after another.
3. Add 28 dekagramm of swollen and finely broken almonds, and stir for half an hour.
4. Add the same amount of sifted sugar and stir for another quarter hour.
5. Add the orange zest mixed with egg yolks, and mix everything well with 1.5 dekagramm of fine flour.
6. Divide the mixture into two parts, place in buttered cake layers, spread evenly with a knife, and fill with preserved sour cherries.
7. Pipe the third part in a ring shape over the top.
8. Brush the cake with egg white and sprinkle with coarsely broken sugar.
9. Bake slowly and decorate with icing.

title: Narancsjég-ívek (Orange Ice Arches)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oranges, zest
- 28 dekagramm sugar
- 1/2 orange, juice
- pistachios, coarsely ground

## Directions

1. Rub 2 orange zests on 28 dekagramm sugar, crush and sift it
2. In a bowl, beat together with 1/2 orange juice for about 15 minutes
3. Add a little more juice and continue beating until the ice becomes thick and does not flow
4. Spread the ice knife-thickness onto wafers cut into long strips
5. Sprinkle with coarsely ground pistachios
6. Dry in very gentle heat

title: Narancsvirág-víz (Orange Blossom Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kilograms fresh orange blossoms, without sepals and stems
- 2 liters alcohol
- 4 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Digest 3 kilograms fresh orange blossoms with 2 liters alcohol and 4 deciliters water

title: Narancsvíz Bishof (Orange Water Bishof)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one bitter orange
- half a sweet orange
- seven deciliters of red wine
- half a kilogram of sugar
- juice of one orange

## Directions

1. Finely peel the bitter orange and half the sweet orange
2. Add the peels to the red wine
3. Cover and let stand for eight days
4. Boil the sugar, dripping the juice of one orange over it
5. When cooled, mix in the strained wine
6. Pour this extract into bottles and add to warm red wine

title: Naranes-parfait (Orange Blossom Cream Frozen Dessert)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dekagrams roasted orange blossom
- 1.4 liters hot sweet cream
- egg yolks
- sugar

## Directions

1. Add 5 dekagrams roasted orange blossom to 1.4 liters hot sweet cream.
2. Follow the procedure already known from previous recipes.
3. Note: when slowly pouring the cream into the egg yolks, stir very carefully so that the mixture does not break.

title: Naranes-sodó (Orange Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 liter cream
- orange zest
- sugar
- 5 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Bring 0.5 liter cream to a boil with orange zest and sugar
2. Pour over 5 egg yolks while continuously stirring
3. Bring to a boil again

title: Nyelv lengyel-mártásban (Tongue in Polish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tongue
- salt
- water
- bacon slices or red onion
- yellow carrot
- allspice
- veal bones
- flour
- red wine
- broth
- 5-7 decagrams ground almond

## Directions

1. Cook tongue in salted water until tender.
2. When cleaned of skin, place in pan.
3. Add bacon slices or red onion, yellow carrot sliced, few allspice and veal bones.
4. Braise all until nicely browned.
5. Sprinkle with spoonful of flour and brown it.
6. Dilute with half red wine and half broth.
7. Cook well, strain sauce through sieve into another pan.
8. Add 5-7 decagrams ground almond to it.
9. Place tongue in it and cook well.
10. Before serving, lard the tongue with the almond, pour sauce into dish and place tongue on top.

title: Nyelv nyárson sütve szardella-mártással (Tongue Roasted on Spit with Anchovy Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh large tongue
- bacon
- anchovy
- butter
- 2 decilitres sour cream
- bread crumbs
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Blanch fresh large tongue until tender, then remove skin.
2. Do not remove from pot until wanting to put on spit; similarly must serve immediately from spit to table.
3. After removing skin, lard neatly with two parts bacon and one part anchovy.
4. Thread on thin spit.
5. Melt a piece of butter, add 2 decilitres sour cream.
6. During roasting baste the tongue with this mixture three or four times.
7. Sprinkle with bread crumbs, squeeze lemon juice over it, baste once more with the sauce.
8. Roast quickly, place on dish and pour sauce under it.

title: Nyers czukor-fagylalt (Raw Sugar Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams sugar
- 0.5 glass water
- 1 liter hot cream

## Directions

1. Cook 28 dekagrams of sugar with 0.5 glass of water until it becomes raw sugar, that is, when placed on stone it can be cut and is slightly yellowish but not bitter
2. Pour 1 liter of hot cream over it and cook the sugar in the cream until completely dissolved
3. Cool and pour into the freezing container

title: Nyomott körték (Pressed Pears)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- parchment pears
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Peel the parchment pears, remove the core leaving the stem intact
2. Cook the pears in sugared water until soft
3. Remove them one by one and drain
4. Place on paper-covered sheet metal and dry in a well-cooled oven
5. Press lengthwise between two wooden boards until wide and flat
6. Dry again thoroughly and press even thinner
7. Repeat this process three to four times until completely flat and knife-blade thin and well dried
8. Layer in rolls, placing finely ground sugar between each layer
9. Fill the rolls completely and store in a dry place until needed

title: Nyomott Perfikati vagy birsalma-kompot (Pressed Quince Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- quince
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare quince marmalade, cooking it well and allowing it to cool
2. Form into balls the size of a walnut
3. Roll flat on paper sprinkled with fine sugar in a dry place in the air
4. Roll thinner each day with a roller, sprinkling with sugar, until wafer-thin
5. Store in rolls for future use

title: Nyárson Sült Galamb (Skewered Roasted Squab)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- salt
- bacon slices
- small salad

## Directions

1. Clean the pigeons and salt
2. Wrap in bacon slices
3. Thread three on a skewer
4. Roast to a nice pink color
5. When removed from the spit, remove the bacon and serve
6. Can be decorated with small green salad

title: Nyárson sült galamb (Spit-roasted Pigeon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons
- Bacon slices
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean the pigeons and salt them
2. Wrap them in bacon slices
3. Thread three pigeons onto a spit
4. Roast until beautifully golden brown
5. Remove from spit and take off the bacon wrapping
6. Serve garnished with small green salad

title: Nyárson sütött sertésderék (Roasted Pork Loin on a Spit)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three kilograms pork with skin
- salt
- fat for basting

## Directions

1. Wash the pork with skin and salt it
2. Make deep cuts lengthwise and then crosswise into the skin with a sharp knife
3. Thread onto a spit
4. Roast to golden color, frequently basting with its fat
5. When well roasted, the skin should be crispy like glass but still tender to bite

title: Nyélczés mandola-kenyér (Almond Bread with Finely Chopped Pieces)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams blanched almonds
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- a little vanilla
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Finely chop the blanched almonds
2. Crush the sugar with a little vanilla and sift it
3. Whip the 3 egg whites into stiff peaks and combine with the sugar
4. Beat together for half an hour until it becomes a thick, foamy icing, but it should not be thin; it is safer to add only half of the stiffly beaten egg whites at first, and if after some beating it is still very thick, add the remaining egg white
5. Once the icing is foamy, add the almonds and mix with the rest
6. Using a spoon, form small peaked shapes on wafer paper
7. Bake in a gentle oven; when well dried, break off the wafer paper and pile high on a plate

title: Nyélczés Mandolatorta (Almond Cake with Sliced Almonds)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagramm sugar
- 2 deciliter water
- 500 dekagramm soaked almonds, sliced
- 7 dekagramm chopped pistachios
- 10 dekagramm chopped citron
- 1 lemon with finely chopped peel and juice
- 3 egg yolks
- wafer for cake base

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and water until it forms a thread
2. Add the sliced soaked almonds, chopped pistachios, chopped citron, and lemon peel and juice
3. Remove from heat and let cool slightly
4. Mix in three egg yolks
5. Line cake form with wafer
6. Shape the cake and bake at gentle heat
7. Decorate with frosting and candied fruit

title: Nyélczés mandolatorta (Chopped Almond Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 2 deciliters water
- 500 grams softened and chopped almonds
- 7 dekagrams chopped pistachios
- 10 dekagrams chopped citron
- 1 lemon, zest and juice
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pour 2 deciliters of water over 42 dekagrams sugar and cook until it threads; then add 500 grams of softened and chopped almonds, 7 dekagrams chopped pistachios, 10 dekagrams chopped citron, 1 lemon with both its finely chopped zest and juice
2. Let it stand a little, remove from the fire and cool it; then mix in 3 egg yolks

title: Nyélczés Mandolatorta Szeletekben (Sliced Almond Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagramm finely ground and sifted sugar
- egg whites
- lemon juice
- almonds, sliced
- chocolate frosting
- 10 dekagramm sugar rubbed with one or two oranges
- egg white for paste
- wafer strips

## Directions

1. Whip sugar, lemon juice and egg white into thick frosting
2. Gently mix in sliced almonds
3. In separate bowl, whip chocolate frosting without lemon juice and mix in sliced almonds
4. Butter a pyramid cake form
5. Cut wafer into finger-wide strips and coat with filling to knife-blade thickness
6. Line form alternately with white and dark strips
7. Dry in cooled oven
8. Make hard paste from sugar rubbed with orange and egg white
9. Roll out to two knife-blade thicknesses and cut leaf shapes with small pastry cutter
10. Cut centers with tiny cutter to form wreaths
11. Place wreaths on buttered sheet and dry in cooled oven
12. Glue wreaths to cake with hardened sugar
13. Place preserved green plums, preserved walnuts, or preserved green almonds alternately in each wreath

title: Nyúl Sülve (Roast Rabbit)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- red onion
- lemon juice
- peppercorns
- lard
- cream
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. After removing the entrails and cleaning the rabbit thoroughly, remove its blue skin and salt it well.
2. Prepare a marinade from vinegar, water, red onion, lemon juice, and a few peppercorns; boil this together for half an hour.
3. Place the rabbit in this hot marinade and leave it for one to two days.
4. Before roasting, lard the back and thighs with lard, then roast slowly, basting frequently with the marinade.
5. When the rabbit is halfway cooked, pour a few spoonfuls of cream over it.
6. When fully roasted and placed on the plate, surround it with the thinnest possible lemon slices from which the seeds have been removed.
7. Strain the sauce and pour it under the rabbit.

title: Nyúl Sülve Más Módon (Roast Rabbit, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- vinegar
- water
- red onion
- lemon peel
- roots
- whole black pepper
- salt
- lard
- cream
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. After the rabbit is beheaded, washed, and carefully cleaned of its bluish membranes, salt it well.
2. Boil vinegar, water, red onion, lemon peel, some roots, and whole black pepper together for half an hour to make a marinade.
3. Pour this hot marinade over the rabbit and let it sit for one or two days to become properly tender.
4. Before threading onto the spit, neatly lard the back and thighs.
5. Roast slowly on the spit, basting frequently with the marinade during cooking.
6. When the rabbit is halfway cooked, pour a few spoonfuls of cream over it.
7. When perfectly roasted and arranged neatly on the plate, cover it with thinly sliced lemon without seeds, arranged in overlapping fashion lengthwise.
8. Strain the sauce and pour it under the roasted rabbit.

title: Nyúl Vadlében (Rabbit in Game Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit, cleaned
- Parsley, a few sprigs
- Carrot, sliced
- Red onion, sliced
- Water, half
- Wine vinegar, half
- Hungarian pepper
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Lemon peel, a small amount of each

## Directions

1. Clean the rabbit and marinade it in the following marinade: chop into slices a few sprigs of parsley, carrot, and red onion
2. In a container, prepare half water and half wine vinegar
3. Add Hungarian pepper, thyme, bay leaf, and lemon peel, a small amount of each
4. Bring to a good boil and when sufficiently boiled, pour over the already cleaned rabbit and cover well
5. When cooled, bring to a boil again and pour off twice more

title: Nyúl vadlében (Rabbit in Game Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- parsley stems
- carrot
- red onion
- water
- wine vinegar
- Hungarian pepper
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lemon zest
- smoked bacon
- flour for thickening

## Directions

1. Clean the rabbit thoroughly and marinate it. Prepare the marinade by chopping parsley stems, carrot, and red onion into slices. In a pot, combine equal parts water and wine vinegar and bring to a boil. Add Hungarian pepper, thyme, bay leaf, and lemon zest in small amounts. Allow to boil well, then pour the hot marinade over the cleaned rabbit and cover well. Once cooled, bring to a boil again and pour off the liquid twice more, then set aside. It is best if the rabbit stays in the marinade for three days. When ready to prepare the rabbit, remove it from the marinade and lard it well. Place it in a roasting pan, add a small amount of onion, parsley, and carrot from the marinade and roast it. While roasting, baste frequently with the marinade liquid. (Do not roast with bay leaf and lemon zest as this will make it taste bitter.) When roasted, pass the vegetables through a sieve and place them in a pot to brown. Crush half a lump of sugar and add it, stirring constantly until it becomes a dark brown. When very dark, pour half a liter of good sour cream over it. Add a slice of lemon zest, a bay leaf, and capers. Pour this sauce over the rabbit and bring to a boil once more.

title: Nápolyi kifli (Neapolitan Croissants)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cake dough
- butter for greasing
- strong dough for border
- apricot or raspberry syrup
- 3 egg whites
- 16 dekagrams sugar
- small amount of vanilla
- orange preserve
- 42 dekagrams fine sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare sponge cake dough and grease a large sheet with butter
2. Make a border around the edge of the sheet from strong dough to prevent the sponge cake dough from running off
3. With the help of a large knife, spread the sponge cake dough on the sheet to the thickness of a knife blade and bake it at gentle heat
4. Once baked, use a small pastry cutter to cut out crescent shapes or croissants, fill them with apricot or raspberry syrup, place another on top, and continue until all are filled
5. Prepare Spanish thread dough from 3 egg whites, 16 dekagrams sugar, and a small amount of vanilla
6. Fill a pastry bag with the mixture; the tube must have a very small opening and the lines must be very fine
7. Press thin lines crosswise into the croissants, with the space between lines only as wide as a knife blade, then add another line
8. Once all are piped, place in the oven to dry as quickly as possible without browning
9. Meanwhile, prepare orange preserve from 42 dekagrams fine sugar, with the difference that when the sugar that has been worked for a long time is white and has the consistency of child-paste, add 2 teaspoons of finely ground sugar first rubbed on an orange and 4 teaspoons of orange juice
10. Once the croissants are dried, dip the upper side entirely in the preserve and place them upper-side down on paper until they harden, then pile them high on a plate
11. The surest test that the sugar is properly made into preserve is if during cooking it forms large bubbles, and during the working you see that what flows off the spoon is white and thick

title: Oglio (Oljó) leves (Oglio Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large onion
- kohlrabi
- carrot
- four hard cabbage heads
- several peppercorns and clove pepper
- eighty decagrams (1 1/2 pounds) soup meat
- two calf feet
- one rooster
- bacon slices

## Directions

1. Cut onion, kohlrabi, carrot into slices
2. Cut four hard cabbage heads into four equal parts
3. Cut the eighty decagrams of soup meat, two calf feet, and one rooster into pieces
4. Slowly braise all of these in a pot lined with bacon slices until nicely browned on the bottom
5. During cooking frequently pour little meat stock over so it does not burn
6. Cook for one and a half hours and then set aside to settle
7. Strain and serve in small cups at table
8. From the same soup are also made the fat slices, except they must be well cleaned of fat and cooked until thickened, which when cooled can be cut up and stored

title: Olasz polenta (Italian Polenta)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liter salted water
- corn meal for polenta
- butter
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Boil salted water in a pan
2. Add cornmeal and cook until thick dough forms
3. Work the dough as finely as possible with a spoon
4. Spread on a board and shape into a round ball
5. Wrap in a cloth and let rise for one hour
6. Cut into knife-thickness slices using strong thread
7. Layer the polenta slices in a buttered mold, sprinkling each layer with grated Parmesan cheese
8. Top the last layer with cheese
9. Bake at high heat for one hour
10. Drain the fat and carefully separate with a knife
11. Turn out and serve with a golden-brown and crispy crust

title: Olasz Rántotta Tejfellel (Italian Omelet with Sour Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon lard
- small amount of red onion
- a knife point of paprika
- eggs
- salt
- fresh parsley
- chives
- sour cream
- breadcrumbs
- grated Parmesan cheese
- diced ham

## Directions

1. In one tablespoon of lard, braise a small amount of red onion until golden
2. Add a knife point of paprika
3. Break the eggs carefully into the hot lard, being careful not to break the yolks
4. Salt them individually
5. Sprinkle finely chopped fresh parsley, chives, and paprika over the yolks
6. Pour sour cream over the eggs to cover them
7. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs and bake quickly
8. Bake in the same dish in which it will be served to the table
9. Make it even more delicious by adding grated Parmesan cheese and diced ham

title: Olasz saláta (Italian Salad)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Multiple varieties of green salad
- Several cooked potatoes
- A piece of eel
- A large herring, well-soaked and cleaned
- Several spools of fish
- Several hard-boiled eggs
- Red beets, finely chopped
- Aspic

## Directions

1. Cut the fish and eel into slices and arrange them neatly in a salad cup with various green lettuces and boiled potatoes
2. Remove the yolks from several hard-boiled eggs and press the whites through a sieve
3. Cut the whites into strips and use a small cutter to make half-moon shapes from them
4. Add finely chopped red beets and use these to decorate the salad beautifully
5. Create three rings: the first from egg yolk, the second from red beets, and the third from egg white, with aspic placed in the center

title: Olasz saláta más módon (Italian Salad, Another Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Two herring, cleaned of skin
- Two pieces marinated eel
- Two spools of fish
- Seven dekagrams tuna
- Seven dekagrams marinated salmon
- Four to five sardelles
- Small seafood crustaceans
- Capers
- Pistachios
- Vinegar with oil

## Directions

1. For five or six servings, cut two cleaned herrings, two pieces of marinated eel, two spools of fish, seven dekagrams of tuna, the same amount of marinated salmon, and four to five sardelles into slices
2. Arrange these pieces together neatly on a flat plate so that the whole has an attractive appearance
3. On top, as decoration, place small seafood crustaceans, capers, and pistachios as desired
4. Pour vinegar mixed with fine oil over the entire dish

title: Olasz saláta más módon (Italian Salad, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two herrings, cleaned of skin
- two pieces of marinated eel
- two spools of fish
- seven dekagrams of tuna
- seven dekagrams of marinated salmon
- four to five anchovies
- small sea crabs for decoration
- capers for decoration
- pistachios for decoration
- wine vinegar
- fine oil

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned herrings, marinated eel pieces, spools, tuna, marinated salmon, and anchovies into slices.
2. Arrange these pieces together beautifully on a flat plate so that the whole has an attractive appearance.
3. Decorate the top with small sea crabs, capers, and pistachios according to taste.
4. Pour wine vinegar and fine oil over the entire dish.

title: Olasz-mártás (Italian Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three hard-boiled egg whites
- three fresh sardines or one herring
- salt
- fine oil
- two spoons finely chopped shallot onion
- white vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop the hard-boiled egg whites finely and the sardines or herring (which must first be cleaned of salt and washed) into cubes
2. Whisk the hard-boiled egg yolks well with fine oil
3. Add the finely chopped shallot onion and the ingredients above
4. Loosen with white vinegar and mix everything well together

title: Orange Aspic (Narancs-kocsonya)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 2 orange peels
- 10 oranges
- 2 lemons
- 4 deciliters water
- 4.5 dekagrams isinglass

## Directions

1. Gently rub 42 dekagrams sugar on 2 orange peels; do not rub hard or it will become bitter.
2. Scrape off the rubbed sugar with a knife and place in a soup bowl.
3. Squeeze juice from 10 oranges and 2 lemons and dissolve the sugar in this juice.
4. May add 4 additional deciliters of water if desired.
5. Once sugar is completely dissolved, add 4.5 dekagrams isinglass and mix well with the juice.
6. Strain through a large, double-stretched cloth as with other aspics, pour into molds, and place on ice.
7. If decorating this aspic, pour only 2 centimeters height into mold and freeze on ice; then arrange candied orange segments in star pattern around mold, add another 2 centimeters of mixture and freeze; once firm, pour remaining mixture on top and keep on ice until use.

title: Ostya (Wafer)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagramm swollen almonds
- 10 dekagramm sugar with vanilla
- a teaspoon of flour
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 deciliters cream

## Directions

1. Pound 7 dekagramm swollen almonds very fine; similarly pound 10 dekagramm sugar with vanilla
2. Place in a pot with a teaspoon of flour and 2 whole eggs
3. Mix the dough well
4. Slowly pour in 2 deciliters cream
5. Once well mixed, bake the wafers over a flame fire on a wafer iron
6. The small roller on which you quickly rotate them should not be thicker than a little finger

title: Osztriga, csuka- vagy menyhalmájjal (Oysters with Pike or Carp Roe)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or carp roe
- salt
- fine breadcrumbs
- fat for frying
- oysters
- butter
- ground pepper
- lemon peel
- lemon juice
- bread for coating

## Directions

1. Salt the pike or carp roe and cut into small pieces
2. Roll in fine breadcrumbs and fry in fat
3. Open the oysters and place them in a butter-greased pot in alternating layers of roe and oyster
4. Sprinkle each layer with ground pepper and finely chopped lemon peel, adding pieces of butter
5. Squeeze the juice from two lemons into the pot
6. Cover with hot coals on top and let it steam slowly
7. When serving, arrange triangular fried bread slices around the edge of the plate or use puff pastry rings for garnish

title: Osztrigával Töltött Sült Kappan (Roasted Capon Stuffed with Oysters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four decagrams butter
- handful of breadcrumbs
- twenty shucked oysters
- salt
- mace
- capon
- flour
- good broth
- capon drippings
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Melt four decagrams of butter and mix with a handful of breadcrumbs, twenty shucked oysters, salt, and mace
2. Stuff the capon with this mixture
3. Roast the capon nicely
4. Prepare a golden roux from a little butter and flour
5. Add a spoonful of good broth, the capon drippings, twenty oysters, and lemon juice
6. Simmer well
7. Pour the sauce underneath when serving

title: Pacsirta rostélyon sütve (Grilled Lark)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lark
- salt
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Pluck the lark and cut off the head
2. Carefully remove the innards without removing the fat
3. Soak in water for a quarter hour
4. Salt and place on a skewer over the grill
5. Roast while frequently basting with butter
6. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs

title: Paczal (Offal Hash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- carefully selected paczal (tripe)
- hot water
- salt
- vegetables
- half liter good sour cream
- a few tablespoons fine flour
- a few hard-boiled eggs
- fresh parsley leaves
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Rinse the carefully selected paczal thoroughly with hot water and broth
2. Slice it thinly on a board
3. Place in hot water with salt and cook well
4. Add vegetables to it
5. Prepare a binding from half liter good sour cream and a few tablespoons fine flour
6. Chop a few hard-boiled eggs into cubes and mix in
7. Carefully add the binding, sprinkle in fresh parsley leaves
8. Bring to a boil and serve
9. Alternatively, instead of the sour cream binding, prepare a fatty breadcrumb roux and use that to bind it, season with a little lemon juice and serve

title: Pain Eperből (Strawberry Pain)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 decagrams fine garden or wild strawberries, sifted
- 42 decagrams sugar
- 2 deciliters water
- 6 decagrams isinglass or equivalent gelatin
- 1-2 lemons, juice

## Directions

1. Chill a smooth cylindrical mold on ice
2. Add a few spoonfuls of well-strained and slightly thickened wine gelée and rotate the mold on all sides until covered with a layer of gelée
3. Place the mold back on ice and decorate with raw or preserved fruit as desired
4. In a porcelain bowl, combine the strained strawberry purée with the sugar syrup (made by cooking sugar in 2 deciliters of water to a not very thick syrup), the isinglass or gelatin, and lemon juice
5. Stir this mixture on ice in the porcelain bowl until it becomes thick
6. Fill the prepared mold with this mass and freeze
7. A test is necessary here as well; if the gelée becomes too stiff, add a little water

title: Palacsinta gyermekpéppel (Pancakes with Baby Cereal)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve to fifteen pancakes
- three tablespoons flour
- milk
- seven dekagrams butter
- one whole egg and four egg yolks
- handful of swollen ground almonds
- seven dekagrams finely ground sugar
- vanilla
- three egg yolks
- two deciliters sour cream
- sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Cook twelve to fifteen pancakes
2. In a saucepan, mix three tablespoons flour smoothly with milk and place over heat, stirring until it begins to thicken, then cook a little longer
3. Pour onto a plate and cool
4. Beat seven dekagrams butter until foamy, add the cereal, then beat in one whole egg and four egg yolks one at a time
5. Add a handful of swollen finely ground almonds, seven dekagrams finely ground sugar and a little vanilla, and stir for another quarter hour
6. Spread the mixture on the pancakes, roll them up and cut into square pieces
7. Arrange the cut pancakes in even rows in a buttered saucepan
8. Mix three egg yolks with two deciliters sour cream, a little sugar and vanilla, pour over and steam for half an hour

title: Palacsinta habart mandolás tésztával (Pancake with Beaten Almond Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- eighteen very thin pancakes
- fourteen dekagrams softened almond
- lemon zest
- fourteen dekagrams sugar
- one whole egg
- three egg yolks
- four egg yolks
- crushed sugar
- two decilitres good cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Make or bake eighteen very thin pancakes, crush fourteen dekagrams softened almond finely, chop lemon zest finely, place this and fourteen dekagrams sugar in a bowl, beat one whole egg and three egg yolks on it one after another and beat until foamy
2. Spread the almond paste on the pancakes and fold them, cut into pieces of finger thickness
3. Grease a pan or porcelain plate with butter, stand the pancakes upright around the sides in two or three rows, put four egg yolks, a little crushed sugar and two decilitres good cream in a pot, mix well and pour over
4. Place in the oven and brown on top
5. The same way the pancakes can be filled with raisins and small grapes, or with preserved sour cherry, crushed nuts, cinnamon, sugar

title: Palacsinta porhanyós tésztában (Pancakes in Crumbly Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sixteen to twenty pancakes
- four deciliters sour cream
- nine dekagrams finely ground almonds
- sugar
- vanilla
- four egg yolks
- crumbly dough

## Directions

1. Cook sixteen to twenty pancakes and cut them into strips
2. Bring four deciliters sour cream to a boil, add nine dekagrams finely ground almonds, a piece of sugar, a little vanilla and the cut pancakes and cook through well, but the strips should remain visible and not become a paste
3. Cool, beat four egg yolks over it and mix well, noting that it should be thicker than common cereal
4. Roll out crumbly dough as thin as lasagna dough, place in a saucepan or mold without wrinkles, add the pancake mixture, cover with dough and bake
5. When done, turn out onto a platter

title: Palacsinta rizszsel (Pancakes with Rice)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ten dekagrams rice
- seven deciliters milk
- ten dekagrams finely ground almonds
- seven dekagrams butter
- two whole eggs and four egg yolks
- sugar
- zest of lemon-sugar rubbed orange
- two tablets fine chocolate
- three tablespoons apricot syrup
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook ten dekagrams washed rice in seven deciliters milk until soft enough, adding ten dekagrams finely ground almonds during cooking
2. Thicken and cool
3. Beat seven dekagrams butter until foamy, add the rice, then beat in two whole eggs and four egg yolks one at a time and stir for half an hour
4. Add enough sugar for sweetness
5. Divide the mixture into three parts: the first remains as is but add zest of lemon-sugar rubbed orange; to the second add two tablets of fine chocolate; to the third add three tablespoons of apricot syrup mixed smooth and stir well for color
6. Fold stiffly beaten egg whites from four egg whites into each part in three portions
7. Butter a round mold, cook the pancakes as thin as possible and line the entire mold with them
8. Layer the orange-flavored portion, cover with pancakes, add the chocolate portion, cover with pancakes, and finish with the final portion of cereal, cover with pancakes and bake in the oven until nicely browned
9. When done, turn out onto a platter and serve at once

title: Palacsinta szilvával töltve (Pancake Filled with Plum)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four decilitres milk
- three eggs
- preserved plum

## Directions

1. Make thin pancakes from four decilitres milk and three eggs and fill them very thinly with preserved plum

title: Palacsinta szilvával töltve (Pancakes Filled with Plums)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four decilitres of milk
- three eggs
- preserved plums for filling
- butter
- sour cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. From four decilitres of milk and three eggs, make thin pancakes
2. Fill them very thinly with preserved plums
3. Fold them together and cut them in half in the middle, as well as trim the uneven two ends
4. Place a little butter and good sour cream on a plate, then arrange the filled pancakes in a star shape on the bottom of the plate
5. Again add sour cream and butter, then pancake, and so on, until everything is arranged
6. Finally add a little more sour cream and small pieces of butter on top
7. Let it come to a boil slightly and serve sprinkled with sugar on the table

title: Palacsinta túróral töltve (Pancakes Filled with Curd Cheese)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four decilitres of milk
- three eggs
- fourteen dekagrams of butter beaten until frothy
- two whole eggs
- six egg yolks
- one kilogram of grated curd cheese
- four decilitres of good sour cream
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. From four decilitres of milk and three eggs, make thin pancakes
2. Prepare the filling: beat fourteen dekagrams of butter until frothy, mix in two whole eggs and six egg yolks, then combine with one kilogram of grated curd cheese, four decilitres of good sour cream, a little salt, and enough sugar to sweeten it well
3. Fill the pancakes with this mixture
4. Fold them together, trim the two ends, place them in a butter-greased mold, and bake them in the oven
5. Serve dusted with sugar on the table

title: Palacsinta-tekercs (Rolled Pancakes with Veal)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Half kilogram stewed veal
- half red onion
- little fresh parsley
- clarified goose fat or butter
- flour
- half serving spoon good broth
- fine pancakes
- egg white
- fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Chop the stewed veal finely with half red onion and a little fresh parsley.
2. In clarified goose fat or butter, brown a spoonful of flour, add the chopped veal, pour half a serving spoon of good broth over it, and cook down until thick.
3. Meanwhile, make fine pancakes and cut them into squares.
4. Spread filling on each pancake, roll them up, dip in beaten egg and fine breadcrumbs, then fry in fat.
5. When serving, stack them in a cone shape.

title: Palacsinta-tekercs vaddal (Rolled Pancakes with Game)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fine pancakes
- leftover roasted venison or rabbit
- little lemon zest
- bread roll
- milk
- half lemon juice
- one or two egg yolks
- egg white
- breadcrumbs
- fat
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Make fine uniform pancakes.
2. Chop leftover roasted venison or rabbit finely, along with a little lemon zest.
3. Remove the crust from a bread roll, soak half of it in milk, squeeze it well, and chop it finely with the game meat.
4. Place this mixture in a bowl, add half lemon juice and one or two egg yolks depending on the amount of filling.
5. The filling should be spreadable but not thin.
6. Spread the filling smoothly on each pancake with a knife, but not quite to the edges.
7. Brush the ends with egg yolk, roll up, and trim the ends.
8. Dip in beaten egg, roll in breadcrumbs, and fry in fat.
9. When cooked, place on a plate and top with fried green parsley.

title: Papine-fagylalt (Almond and Hazelnut Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams soaked almonds
- 14 dekagrams large hazelnuts
- fresh walnuts (optional, in season)
- 1 liter well-boiled cream
- 42 dekagrams sugar

## Directions

1. Crush 14 dekagrams soaked almonds and 14 dekagrams hazelnuts to a fine paste
2. If walnuts are in season, peel them and add a good handful to the mixture
3. When everything is finely crushed, pour 1 liter of well-boiled cream, not hot but warm, over it
4. Mix the nuts well with the cream
5. Press through cloth
6. Dissolve 42 dekagrams sugar in it
7. Cool and proceed as with other ice creams

title: Paprikás Borjúhús Galuskával (Paprika Veal with Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh parsley
- red onion
- butter
- salt
- veal, cut into cubes
- flour
- meat broth
- paprika
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Chop parsley and red onion together and braise in butter
2. Season with salt
3. Add the cubed veal and braise
4. Dust with flour
5. Moisten with meat broth
6. Braise until tender
7. Add paprika and a few spoonfuls of sour cream

title: Paprikás borjúsült egy darabban (Paprika Veal Roast in One Piece)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tender veal from the hind part
- fat
- onion
- black pepper
- clove pepper
- paprika
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Put fat in a pot and fry abundant onion and a few black and clove peppercorns until golden, add one teaspoon of paprika
2. Beat the veal well but not so it falls apart, salt it and coat in flour, place immediately in the hot fat
3. This roast should cook quickly, three-quarters of an hour is enough
4. To serve, pour a little broth underneath, and when sliced onto a plate, strain the sauce through a sieve onto it while hot
5. The sauce should be a nice red color; the veal remains white from the quick cooking, looks very nice and is tender and fine

title: Paprikás csirke (Hungarian Paprika Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- onion
- butter
- paprika
- flour
- chicken broth
- sour cream
- parsley

## Directions

1. Singe the chickens, cut them up, wash clean, cut into uniform pieces and lightly salt
2. Finely chop an onion and braise it in butter in a copper pan until golden
3. Add the chicken pieces and half a tablespoon of paprika
4. Cover and let simmer, shaking frequently until tender
5. Dust with a little flour, add a little chicken broth and a few spoonfuls of sour cream
6. Let simmer slowly, skim the foam carefully and serve with fresh parsley

title: Paprikás csirke, valódi magyar (True Hungarian Paprika Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- fresh bacon
- rose paprika
- water
- sour cream
- parsley
- dumplings

## Directions

1. Singe the chickens and cut into regular pieces (from one chicken: 2 thighs, 2 second thighs, 1 bishop's cut, 1 beggar's purse, the head and neck, 2 wings with giblets and liver, and 4-5 breast pieces if the chicken is larger; small chicks should be quartered)
2. Place the cut meat in a bowl filled with water
3. Chop fresh bacon into small cubes (2-3 decagrams per chicken) and render it in a pot until breadcrumb-colored
4. For each chicken, add 1 teaspoon of rose paprika to the hot fat where the rendered bacon remains, stir quickly and add the chicken with plenty of water
5. Cover and braise, if the liquid reduces completely it can be increased with fat-free broth
6. Season with salt at mid-cooking, and when the chicken is sufficiently tender, add 1-2 tablespoons of sweet unsoured sour cream per medium chicken
7. Once it comes to a boil, add a good pinch of finely chopped green parsley for each chicken, let it come to a boil again, serve and garnish with fine whipped dumplings

title: Paprikás-hal szegedi módon (Szeged-style Paprika Fish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- red onion
- paprika
- fat
- water
- fish
- salt

## Directions

1. Fry the red onion and paprika in fat
2. Pour four deciliters of water over it and bring to a boil
3. Add the salted fish and cook for half an hour
4. Do not stir with a spoon, but shake it occasionally so it does not burn

title: Paradicsom-alma saláta (Tomato Salad)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three firm tomatoes
- salt
- red onion
- oil
- strong vinegar

## Directions

1. With a sharp knife, slice two or three firm tomatoes into thin rounds
2. Place these in a soup plate
3. Salt heavily and sprinkle with finely chopped red onion
4. Layer more tomato slices on top, salt these
5. Sprinkle with red onion and pepper
6. Finally pour oil and plenty of strong vinegar over everything
7. Place the salad in a cool place where it must stand for at least an hour to be palatable
8. Served with beef it is particularly tasty

title: Paradicsomalma-leves (Tomato Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- red onion
- vegetables and roots
- water
- roux
- salt

## Directions

1. Crush the tomatoes and place in a pot with red onion and vegetables
2. Pour in a little water and let cook well
3. Make a roux thick or thin to taste
4. Press the tomatoes through a sieve and add to the roux
5. Boil everything well together and salt to taste
6. If desired, add noodle dumplings, short pasta, or rice to the soup

title: Paradiesomalma-mártás (Tomato Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- six to seven tomatoes
- butter
- flour
- meat stock
- sugar
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean six to seven tomatoes well and wash them
2. Break each into several pieces
3. Braise tender in a pot
4. Melt a piece of butter and add one tablespoon of flour to it and brown slightly
5. Pass the braised tomatoes through a sieve and thin the roux with it
6. If too thick, add a little more meat stock, then add a piece of sugar and sour cream

title: Pauláni Táblácskák (Paulani Cookies)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams finely ground and sifted sugar
- 14 dekagrams flour
- 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
- 12 ground cloves
- zest of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- zest of 1 orange, finely chopped
- 2 tablespoons Marsala or Malaga wine
- candied almonds

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar, flour, cinnamon, cloves, lemon zest, and orange zest on a board
2. Pour the wine over the mixture
3. Work it into a firm dough using a wide knife
4. Roll out to the thickness of a knife blade
5. Cut into strips two finger-widths wide
6. Cut boat shapes from these strips
7. Place a half-puffed almond in the middle of each boat
8. Coat the baking sheet with wax
9. As soon as the cookies go into the oven, brush with sugar water
