10. Dry at very gentle heat

title: Pecsenye-gombócz (Roast Meat Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram beef roast, finely chopped
- 10 deka rice, cooked in salted and fatty water
- 1 teaspoon ground pepper
- zest of half a lemon, grated
- salt
- 15 deka white bacon, chopped
- 2 whole raw eggs
- hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Chop the beef roast very finely
2. Cook the rice in salted and fatty water and cool it
3. Add the rice to the chopped meat, season with ground pepper, grated lemon zest, and salt
4. Chop the bacon and add it
5. Mix well with two whole raw eggs and form into dumplings the size of eggs
6. Flatten them slightly on a board so they cook through more easily
7. Fry them in not too much hot fat until cooked through
8. Can be served with a sauce of sour cream and lemon juice, or without sour cream; surround with macaroni or tarhunya

title: Pergelt-torta (Caramelized Almond Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 dekagramm sugar, finely ground
- 500 dekagramm softened almonds, sliced
- almond oil
- lemon
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Finely grind the sugar and slice the softened almonds and dry in a lukewarm oven
2. Place the ground sugar in a brass preserving pan over strong charcoal fire, stirring continuously with a cooking spoon
3. Break up the small pellets that form, until the sugar completely melts and begins to turn yellowish
4. Quickly add the dried almonds and stir until both are well mixed and have taken on a light breadcrumb color
5. Quickly fill the caramelized mixture, carefully greased with fine almond oil, into a goblet or basket shape
6. Press the entire form as thin as possible with a lemon
7. When completely cooled, carefully turn out and place on a plate nicely covered with a kitchen cloth
8. Fill the entire opening high with whipped cream, which gives it a very beautiful appearance

title: Pesti almás-lepény (Pest-style Apple Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter fine flour
- Ten dekagrams butter
- One whole egg
- Milk
- Fourteen dekagrams softened almonds
- Twenty-one dekagrams raisins
- Fourteen dekagrams sugar
- Little cinnamon
- Sour, crumbly, peeled apples
- Butter for brushing
- Egg white
- Fine vanilla
- Sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry from one liter fine flour, ten dekagrams butter, one whole egg, and milk
2. Work it well and tear off twenty-five small pieces (cipócskák)
3. Cover with a warm cloth and let rise for half an hour
4. Finely chop fourteen dekagrams softened almonds
5. Remove pits from twenty-one dekagrams raisins and chop roughly
6. Finely grind fourteen dekagrams sugar and little cinnamon and mix well together
7. Cut sour, crumbly, peeled apples into very thin slices
8. Butter a flat plate prepared for this purpose
9. Stretch out one piece at a time over the plate
10. Brush each layer over the plate with melted butter and continue until eight layers are stretched out
11. Line with the chopped apples and half of the filling on top
12. Then again eight layers of pastry, each brushed with butter individually, then again a row of apples and filling
13. Finally stretch out the remaining nine pieces
14. Smooth the pastry hanging over the plate edge with a rolling pin so the layers stay together
15. Brush the top layer with egg white and pierce the pastry with vanilla a few times so it does not puff up during baking
16. Put in the oven and bake until nicely light brown
17. When baked, sprinkle with sugar and serve; can also be served cold

title: Pirított mandola (Roasted Almonds)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 dekagrams sugar
- half a cup of water
- 500 grams almonds, cleaned with cloth

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar with half a cup of water until it threads and throws large bubbles during cooking
2. Add the cleaned almonds and constantly stir with a wooden spoon over gentle embers until the almonds have absorbed all the sugar and dried
3. Be careful that they do not become oily; as soon as they begin to pop, remove from heat and cover the pan until they cool
4. If you want the almonds to be red, mix in cochineal juice

title: Pirított tejes haluska (Fried Milk Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagrams butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- flour as needed
- salt
- 7 deciliters cream
- sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Cream together 7 dekagrams butter
2. Add 2 whole eggs and 3 egg yolks one after another
3. Season with salt and add enough flour to bind the dough but keep it soft
4. Bring 7 deciliters cream to a boil with a little sugar
5. Drop noodles evenly into the cream with a spoon
6. When cooked, remove and place in a deep porcelain dish or pot
7. Beat 4 egg yolks in a small pot
8. Pour the cream with crushed sugar and vanilla into the egg yolks and whisk well
9. Pour over the noodles
10. Place in the oven until the noodles brown and the cream becomes thick
11. Sprinkle with sugar and serve

title: Pirított ívek (Roasted Wafers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams soaked almond, cut in slivers
- 28 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 1 orange, zest
- 1 lemon, zest and juice
- pistachio, finely chopped
- water

## Directions

1. Mix 28 dekagrams of soaked almond cut in slivers with 28 dekagrams of finely ground sugar
2. Finely chop the zest of 1 orange and 1 lemon and add with their juices to the almond and sugar
3. Mix well and let stand for half an hour so it swells well, as it must be moist
4. Place 3 spoonfuls of this mixture in a pan or brass bowl
5. Roast until light brown, stirring constantly so it doesn't burn and gets even color
6. Then place by the spoonful on a board moistened with water
7. Spread with a knife into thin wafer shape
8. Sprinkle with finely chopped pistachio
9. When hardened, remove and prepare the rest of the wafers the same way

title: Piskóta Papírtokban (Sponge Cake in Paper Cases)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen dekagrams finely ground sugar
- four egg yolks
- four egg whites
- seven dekagrams fine dry flour
- fine ground sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Beat fourteen dekagrams of finely ground sugar with four egg yolks for fifteen minutes
2. Mix in the stiff snow of four egg whites
3. Finally add seven dekagrams of fine dry flour and mix well
4. Fill paper cases about halfway or slightly higher
5. Sprinkle with finely ground sugar
6. Bake at low heat

title: Piskóta-pép (Sponge Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lemon or orange
- 140 grams sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 70 grams fine flour or sifted potato starch
- 5 egg whites
- butter
- sour cherry or currant syrup cooked in sugar
- chocolate made from cream

## Directions

1. Lightly rub the sugar on a lemon or orange, grind finely and sift
2. Put with two egg yolks in a bowl and beat for three-quarters of an hour
3. Gently fold in seven dekagrams fine flour or sifted potato starch with the stiffly beaten whites of five eggs
4. Pour the whipped mixture into a buttered mold and steam for three-quarters of an hour
5. Do not give too much heat on top so it does not brown, but only steams out
6. When serving, turn out onto a plate and pour sour cherry or currant syrup cooked in sugar around it
7. Or prepare a chocolate cream from chocolate cooked in cream, beat it into a mousse, and heap it high in a wide ring so the beautiful yellow pudding rises only in the center

title: Piskóta-torta (Sponge Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- forty-two dekagrams of sugar rubbed on an orange
- twenty-two egg yolks
- twenty-one dekagrams of fine flour or the finest ground and sifted cornstarch
- twelve egg whites
- three identical cake leaves and hoops
- butter for coating
- jam for filling
- chocolate icing or fresh raspberry icing for coating
- seventeen to twenty-one dekagrams of powdered sugar
- fresh strawberries for the raspberry icing

## Directions

1. Grind the sugar rubbed on orange and sift it.
2. Beat it in a basin with twenty-two egg yolks for one hour evenly.
3. Gently fold in twenty-one dekagrams of fine flour or the finest ground and sifted cornstarch, as well as the stiff foam of twelve egg whites.
4. Coat three identical cake leaves and hoops with butter.
5. Divide the batter into three equal parts and bake slowly over gentle heat.
6. When well baked, turn the cake leaves out onto a sieve, cool completely.
7. Then coat every layer with different preserves, stack them on top of each other, and cover with either chocolate icing or fresh raspberry icing.
8. For the raspberry or strawberry icing: the strawberries should be small and beautifully red.
9. Sift seventeen to twenty-one dekagrams of powdered sugar finely on a sieve.
10. Press the fruit through clean cloth and stir into the sugar by spoonfuls so the icing is not too thin.
11. When the icing is foamy, coat the cake smoothly and evenly with a knife and dry it completely in a cool oven.

title: Piskóta-torta tejfelhabbal (Sponge Cake with Whipped Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams sugar
- sponge cake dough
- butter
- ten dekagrams sugar
- vanilla
- eight deciliters sour cream
- whole fruits for garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare sponge cake dough using twenty-eight dekagrams of sugar as described in the previous recipe
2. Butter a small cake leaf with the ring together
3. Fill it with a few spoonfuls of dough, at most three knife-widths thick, this leaf should be the topmost and smallest
4. Spread the remaining dough onto two cake leaves in the same manner
5. After buttering these, make a ring around the cake leaves from common dough to prevent the sponge cake dough from flowing, apply it to the same thickness as the first, sprinkle with sugar, and bake at gentle heat until light roll-colored
6. When the dough is baked, cut out six leaves, each one a fish-bone width smaller in diameter, so that the one you filled in the cake leaf is the smallest
7. Remove the rings from these
8. Prepare a butter dough from ten dekagrams of sugar and a little vanilla, fill it into a Spanish piping bag, and pipe beautiful Spanish fritters onto the edge of the smallest leaf so that it forms a nice wreath
9. From the remaining butter dough, pipe small fritters the size of Italian hazelnuts onto white paper, place these on a sheet in a completely cooled oven so they remain beautifully white but harden and become crispy
10. Put eight deciliters of sour cream into the whipping bowl, place it on ice, and whip with the whisk until the entire mass becomes stiff foam, then add enough finely broken sugar and vanilla so that it is sweet enough, and quickly stir this in with the whisk
11. Remove the cake slightly earlier from the oven so it cools, fill every leaf with the sour cream foam to a good knife-width thickness so that the foam comes between all six layer-cake leaves
12. Arrange beautiful whole fruits on the top leaf of the cake, and place the small fritters around the edges of the other six leaves in a circle
13. Once the cake is completely assembled and filled, place it on a cake leaf, cover the plate with a tablecloth, and quickly serve it on the table

title: Piskótatészta-serlegek tejszínhabbal (Sponge Cake Cups with Whipped Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams sugar for sponge cake dough
- butter for greasing
- sugar for dusting
- 7 deciliters good cream

## Directions

1. Prepare sponge cake dough from 14 dekagrams of sugar, spread it smoothly on a well-buttered sheet with a wide knife to the thickness of a knife blade
2. Dust with sugar and bake at gentle heat until light bread-yellow
3. Quickly cut long strips with a sharp knife, each as wide as the palm and as long as a span
4. Place the sheet back in the oven for a few moments, then pull it out without removing it completely, so the dough does not harden and become brittle; therefore the sheet must always be kept warm
5. Peel off the cut strips one by one with the knife and quickly bend them over a small wooden roller such as those found in toy shops, so that the two ends of the dough come together
6. Hold it together in your hand until the dough has hardened, then carefully remove it so it does not break
7. Place it on a sieve in a warm place so it does not soften, and continue until all are bent
8. Good cream will be prepared separately

title: Piskótatészta-tekercs (Sponge Cake Roll)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen dekagrams sugar
- sponge cake dough
- butter
- preserved peach or raspberry marmalade
- egg white
- powdered sugar
- coarse sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as described in recipe 672, fourteen dekagrams of sugar is sufficient for ten to twelve people
2. Pour this dough onto a long baking sheet buttered with butter and smooth it evenly with a knife to a knife-width thickness
3. Bake at gentle heat until the dough becomes dark yellow
4. Then coat entirely with preserved peach or raspberry marmalade and place it in the oven again for a moment so that the dough softens and can be rolled together
5. Remove the baking sheet and roll the cake along its length into a roll so that the roll is beautifully thick
6. Place it in the already cooled oven for eight to ten minutes
7. When this time has elapsed, remove it, let it cool, and let it harden
8. With a sharp knife, cut the entire roll into knife-width thick slices and arrange them in three or four wreaths on the plate
9. Before cutting the roll, coat it completely with snow using a large feather, roll it in coarsely broken sugar, place it in the cooled oven again, but only until it dries, then when cooled, cut it

title: Piskótatészta-tekercs más módon (Sponge Cake Roll Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams sugar
- orange zest
- fifteen egg yolks
- eighteen dekagrams flour
- seven egg whites
- fourteen dekagrams sugar
- butter
- fifteen to twenty medium apples
- eighteen dekagrams broken sugar
- three spoonfuls preserved peach syrup
- twenty-one dekagrams walnuts
- half orange peel
- half lemon peel
- cinnamon
- poppy seed

## Directions

1. Rub twenty-eight dekagrams of sugar on orange, break it, sift it, beat fifteen egg yolks into it, and stir for an hour
2. Then lightly mix in eighteen dekagrams of flour, finally add the stiff foam of seven egg whites
3. Since this amount is too much for one roll, prepare it from fourteen dekagrams of sugar as follows: Spread the dough thinly onto a long sheet buttered with butter and bake it at gentle heat until roll-colored
4. Peel fifteen to twenty medium apples beforehand, remove their cores, cut them roughly into pieces, and cook into marmalade with eighteen dekagrams of broken sugar with frequent stirring
5. Then finely mix in three spoonfuls of preserved peach syrup, let it cool, coat the roll with it to a knife-width thickness, place it in the oven again until it can be rolled together, and treat it as before
6. This roll can also be filled with walnuts: cut twenty-one dekagrams of beautiful walnuts finely, as well as the peel of half an orange and half a lemon, stir this with broken cinnamon and twenty-one dekagrams of sugar dissolved in a coffee cup of water, and cook only until the sugar has penetrated everything well and the filling can be spread well
7. Filled with poppy seed it is also very good and beautiful, as it resembles a yellowish-black ribbon; the poppy seed, once broken, is prepared in the same way as the walnuts

title: Piskótás tészta (Sponge Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen dekagrams of finely pounded sugar
- four egg yolks
- four egg whites
- ten dekagrams of fine dry flour
- butter
- preserved cherries or rowan berries
- quince paste
- pistachios
- raisins
- sour cream
- egg yolks
- sugar
- vanilla
- preserved apricots
- old white wine
- French reduced stock

## Directions

1. Beat fourteen dekagrams of finely pounded sugar well with four egg yolks; when it begins to foam, add the stiff snow of four egg whites and ten dekagrams of fine dry flour
2. Pour the dough into a buttered pan and bake it slowly
3. When fully baked, carefully trim the brown crust with a thin knife and cut the whole into slices like rusks
4. Prepare the cake one day before using, as it cuts better then
5. Use a mold with an opening in the middle to form a wide, tall crown; butter it well
6. Line the bottom with whole preserved cherries or rowan berries in sugar syrup, then with quince paste cut into small triangles and softened pistachios arranged neatly
7. Build strong walls from the cut sponge cake in the entire mold, cut the remaining sponge cake and a little more quince paste into small cubes, mix with small raisins, fill the mold with this and press it down lightly
8. Half an hour before serving, beat two decilitres of sour cream with five egg yolks, pounded sugar, and vanilla smoothly, pour it on top and set it in a steam bath
9. Before serving, beat two spoons of preserved apricots with the same amount of pounded sugar and four egg yolks well, pour in five tablespoons of white old wine, put it on a low fire and whip with a whisk until it becomes a thick mass
10. Turn the cake out onto a plate, fill the opening with French reduced stock, and arrange the remaining sauce around the edge of the cake in a crown shape with a spoon

title: Pisztráng kékre főzve (Trout Cooked Blue)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- salt
- red onion
- bay leaves
- lemon zest
- whole pepper
- white wine vinegar
- water
- oil

## Directions

1. Do not remove the trout from the water until you want to use it
2. Strike it on the head, open it and remove the insides, quickly rinse it so the blood comes out and sprinkle it well with good white wine vinegar
3. Then in a pot of equal parts water and white wine vinegar add salt, red onion, bay leaves, lemon zest and several whole pepper corns and bring to a boil
4. Place the trout in it and bring to a boil; then remove from heat and pour fresh vinegar over it again, cover the trout and serve cold or hot as desired
5. If serving cold, cool it in its broth and serve with vinegar and oil

title: Pogácsa (Hungarian Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four decilitres of flour
- four decilitres of finely chopped lard
- salt
- two egg yolks
- three tablespoons of sour cream
- two tablespoons of wine
- two dekagrams of yeast dissolved in milk

## Directions

1. On a work surface with a rolling pin, work together four decilitres of flour, four decilitres of finely chopped lard, and a little salt.
2. Add two egg yolks, three tablespoons of sour cream, two tablespoons of wine, and two dekagrams of yeast dissolved in milk, and knead by hand until it becomes smooth dough, roll out very thin, fold together, roll out again, and repeat this three times until at the fourth rolling out a strip the width of a finger remains.
3. Cut out with a medium-sized doughnut cutter and arrange nicely on a baking sheet, place in a warm spot to rise.
4. When risen, make light cuts on the surface of the pogácsa with a knife, brush with egg and bake until nicely golden. This very simple dish can be enjoyed cold and warm, and is especially a favorite food of men.

title: Ponty (Carp)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- middle sections of carp
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- finely chopped red onion
- butter
- paprika
- fish stock
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean the middle sections of carp and salt them
2. Place in a pan and add water and vinegar, but only enough to not completely cover the fish
3. Allow to cook slowly
4. Sauté finely chopped red onion in butter until golden
5. Add half a teaspoon of paprika and let it stew
6. Add a little fish stock and two deciliters of sour cream
7. Bring the sauce to a boil and skim off foam
8. Strain the sauce
9. Place the carp in a serving dish and pour a little sauce over it
10. Serve the remaining sauce separately in a cup

title: Ponty cseh módra (Carp Bohemian Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a beautiful carp
- vinegar
- beer
- parsley root
- red onion
- celery
- bay leaf
- thyme
- whole peppercorns
- allspice
- cloves
- lemon peel
- sugar
- walnuts
- black bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the carp thoroughly, fillet it, and collect its blood in a pot, pour a little vinegar into the fish so that all blood comes out of it and cook it.
2. Prepare the broth in a pan: put two parts beer and one part vinegar into it, as well as parsley root cut lengthwise, red onion, celery, bay leaf, thyme, a few whole peppercorns, allspice, cloves, lemon peel, a piece of sugar and a few walnut meats; cover and cook for one hour.
3. Roast a handful of black bread crumbs in butter, add it, also add the blood, cut the fish into pieces and add it to the broth; if the sauce is still thin, a little grated gingerbread can be added.
4. When the fish has cooked for a good quarter hour, it is ready; then place it on the serving dish, strain the sauce over it and serve to the table.

title: Ponty kocsonyázva (Carp in Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a beautiful piece of carp
- salt
- white wine vinegar
- water
- bay leaf
- carrot
- lemon peel
- whole peppercorns
- red onion
- cloves
- fish bladder
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cut a beautiful piece of carp into pieces and salt it.
2. Make the broth as follows: put two decilitres white wine vinegar, one and a half litres water, bay leaf, carrot, lemon peel, whole peppercorns, red onion cut into small pieces, three to four cloves and two decagrams of fish bladder into a pan and cook it all slowly.
3. Place the fish in it and cook it, place it on the serving dish, strain the broth and cool it.
4. Then beat two whole eggs into the broth together with their shells and place it on embers again and bring to a slow boil.
5. Stretch cloth over the legs of an overturned chair, carefully pour the broth over it and strain it slowly so that it becomes clear; then pour half of it into a pan or mould and let it set, arrange it with sea crabs, place the fish on top and then pour the other half of the broth over it.

title: Ponty kocsonyázva cseh módra (Carp Aspic in Czech Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg live carp
- 0.5 cup strong vinegar
- 1 large carrot
- 1 onion
- parsley root
- small piece of celery
- 1 liter hot beer
- 1 tablespoon honey
- cloves
- clove pepper
- coarsely ground pepper
- half a lemon peel
- walnuts
- carp blood

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the live carp into large pieces and carefully collect the blood in a bowl with half a cup of strong vinegar
2. Cut the carrot, onion, parsley root, and celery into fine strips and brown them nicely in a clean pan over flame with continuous stirring
3. Transfer to a pot, pour over 1 liter hot beer and 1 tablespoon honey
4. Add cloves, clove pepper, coarsely ground pepper, and half a lemon peel cut lengthwise, add the fish pieces, pour in a small cup of vinegar and cook the fish well
5. Place the fish pieces in a deep dish, but continue cooking the head separately
6. In another pot, brown 2 tablespoons honey or sugar, and when nicely browned, add a handful of coarsely crushed walnuts and the reserved carp blood to the sauce
7. Bring to a boil several times, then pour over the fish and let it set in a cold place

title: Ponty kékre főzve (Carp Cooked Blue)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- hot vinegar
- vinegar
- water
- cloves
- whole peppercorns
- lemon peel
- parsley root
- salt
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Fillet the carp, wash it, cut it into pieces and pour hot vinegar over it so that it becomes a beautiful blue colour.
2. Make the broth from vinegar and water so that it has a good sour taste, add a few cloves, whole peppercorns, lemon peel, parsley root and salt, bring to a boil, place the fish in it, cover and cook slowly.
3. When cooked, place blotting paper on it and let it stand for a while, then place it on the serving dish and arrange torn green parsley on top or around the edge of the dish.
4. Mirror carp is best for this because it has few scales. It can also be served cold with vinegar and oil.

title: Ponty sóskamártásban (Carp in Sorrel Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- salt
- melted butter
- sorrel sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the carp and make cuts lengthwise on both sides without cutting all the way through
2. Salt it, coat with melted butter, grill on a gridiron and baste several times with butter
3. When nicely browned and cooked through, place good sorrel sauce on a plate and arrange the fish on top

title: Ponty zöld mártással (Carp with Green Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- peppercorns
- allspice
- thyme
- green parsley
- sorrel
- five anchovies
- capers
- three hard-boiled eggs
- table oil

## Directions

1. After the carp has lain in salt for one hour, scald it with a broth made of half vinegar and half water, into which peppercorns, allspice and thyme have been cooked, and then cool it.
2. Cook a handful of green parsley and the same amount of sorrel until soft and squeeze out the water well.
3. Remove the bones from five water-soaked anchovies and pound them finely in a mortar with a similar amount of capers and three hard-boiled eggs and press through a sieve.
4. Mix well with a few spoonfuls of fine table oil and vinegar and pour it over the dish.

title: Porhanyó tészta (Crumbly Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams flour
- 28 dekagrams butter
- 2 egg whites
- 1 full tablespoon wine
- sour cream as needed for moisture
- salt

## Directions

1. Into 28 dekagrams lightly salted flour, slice the same amount of butter
2. Work the butter with the flour using a rolling pin, but do not touch with the hands
3. Add two egg whites, one full tablespoon of wine, and enough sour cream to make it moist
4. Mix well with a large knife so everything is combined
5. The dough should not be stiff
6. Roll out three times as thin as possible
7. Fold in four parts on the same side
8. Use no more flour than absolutely necessary
9. Then use the dough as desired
10. Note that soft butter should be used for crumbly pastry

title: Potpourri (Potpourri)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dekagrams lavender flowers
- 10 dekagrams rose petals
- 10 dekagrams jasmine flowers
- 10 dekagrams orange blossoms
- 10 dekagrams lemon peel
- 10 dekagrams orange peel
- 3.5-5.5 dekagrams cinnamon
- 3.5-5.5 dekagrams paradise seeds
- 3.5-5.5 dekagrams nutmeg flowers
- 3.5-5.5 dekagrams clove buds
- 2 dekagrams Peruvian balsam
- 9 dekagrams Florentine iris root
- 2 dekagrams benzoin resin
- 8 grams bergamot oil
- 7 centigrams musk
- 2 liters 65° alcohol
- 4 deciliters rose water
- 4 grams vinegar-spirit
- 20 drops ambergris dye

## Directions

1. Chop all ingredients finely
2. Pour 2 liters 65° alcohol and 4 deciliters rose water over the mixture
3. Allow to digest
4. Strain
5. Add 4 grams vinegar-spirit and 20 drops ambergris dye

title: Potpourri Hair Pomade
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram fresh mutton tallow
- 75 dekagrams vegetable oil
- 65 dekagrams hair powder
- 3 dekagrams lavender oil
- 3 dekagrams bergamot oil
- 3 dekagrams orange flower oil
- 4 grams clove oil

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients with the fresh mutton tallow base

title: Potpourri-hajkenő (Potpourri Hair Pomade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram fresh mutton tallow
- seventy-five dekagrams vegetable oil
- sixty-five dekagrams hair powder
- three dekagrams lavender oil
- three dekagrams bergamot oil
- three dekagrams orange blossom oil
- four grams clove oil

## Directions

1. Combine one kilogram of fresh mutton tallow, seventy-five dekagrams of vegetable oil, sixty-five dekagrams of hair powder, three dekagrams of lavender oil, three dekagrams of bergamot oil, three dekagrams of orange blossom oil, and four grams of clove oil

title: Potyka-kocsonya (Pike Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- carrot
- red onion
- bay leaf
- lemon zest
- whole peppercorns
- dill
- two decagrams of isinglass

## Directions

1. Cut up the pike and salt it
2. In a pot, combine one and a half liters each of vinegar and water
3. Add carrot, red onion, bay leaf, lemon zest, whole peppercorns, dill, and two decagrams of isinglass
4. Bring to a boil
5. Add the fish and cook until done
6. Place in a serving dish
7. Strain the broth through a kitchen towel stretched over the legs of an inverted chair
8. Pour half of the broth into a deep mold and allow to set
9. Arrange the pike on top, decorated with olives
10. Serve the pike aspic at table

title: Potyka-kocsonya (Pike Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- carrot

## Directions

1. Cut up the pike and salt it
2. In a pot, combine one and a half liters of vinegar and the same amount of water, add carrot

title: Pudding angol módra (English-style Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 17 dekagrams soaked almonds
- cold water
- 17 dekagrams sugar
- 7 dekagrams black bread
- 1 tablespoon malaga or Menes wine
- lemon zest
- little cinnamon
- 1 whole egg
- 5 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Grind 17 dekagrams soaked almonds, moistening with a little cold water to prevent them from becoming oily
2. Finely grind the same amount of sugar
3. Grate 7 dekagrams black bread and moisten with 1 tablespoon malaga or Menes wine
4. Finely chop lemon zest with a little cinnamon and put in a bowl
5. Add 1 whole egg and 5 egg yolks one after another, stirring until fluffy, which takes about half an hour
6. Add lightly whipped whites of 5 eggs
7. Pour into a buttered basin or mold and steam
8. After three-quarters of an hour turn onto a plate and pour wine sauce over it

title: Pudding-fagylalt à la Nesselrode (Nesselrode Pudding Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram peeled chestnuts
- piece of vanilla
- milk
- 1.5 liters cream
- 12 egg yolks
- 35 dekagrams sugar
- 28 dekagrams raspberry jelly
- 14 dekagrams cleaned raisins
- 14 dekagrams currants
- 7 dekagrams candied lemon peel cut into cubes
- white wine
- sugar
- 4 deciliters whipped cream
- sugar
- 1 glass maraschino

## Directions

1. Cook 0.5 kilogram of peeled chestnuts with a piece of vanilla in milk until soft and press through a sieve
2. Beat 1.5 liters of cream with 12 egg yolks and 35 dekagrams of sugar over fire until foamy, then press through a sieve
3. Add 28 dekagrams of raspberry jelly and combine with the chestnut purée
4. Briefly heat 14 dekagrams of cleaned raisins, 14 dekagrams of currants, and 7 dekagrams of diced candied lemon peel with a little white wine and sugar, then let cool
5. When the chestnut mixture has frozen solid, mix in 4 deciliters of whipped cream, the raisins and candied lemon peel
6. Turn the freezing container a few more times
7. Pour the ice cream into a tall or dome-shaped mold
8. Close the mold with its lid and place between salt and ice for about 2 hours
9. Turn the ice cream onto a platter
10. Serve separately a sauce of whipped cream mixed with sugar and a small glass of maraschino

title: Puffadt tejfeles haluska (Puffy Sour Cream Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters sour cream
- 6 whole eggs
- 30 dekagrams flour
- 1.5 liters milk
- butter
- fine breadcrumbs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Whisk together sour cream and 6 whole eggs finely
2. Add 30 dekagrams flour and salt to make noodle dough
3. Bring 1.5 liters milk to a boil in a pot
4. Drop noodles into boiling milk using a spoon and cook until done
5. Remove with a slotted spoon so they remain whole
6. Place on a plate coated with butter and sprinkled with fine breadcrumbs
7. Sprinkle breadcrumbs on top again
8. Place in the oven and brown nicely
9. Can be served sprinkled with sugar

title: Puhintás torta (Leavened Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/8 kilogram butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 4 spoonfuls powdered sugar
- 3 decagrams yeast
- warm milk
- 1 kilogram flour
- salt
- abundant lemon zest
- 1/8 kilogram raisins and small grapes
- 8 egg whites
- grated chocolate
- powdered sugar
- vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Beat together 1/8 kilogram butter and 8 egg yolks with 4 spoonfuls powdered sugar
2. Dissolve 3 decagrams yeast in warm milk
3. Take 1 kilogram flour and mix with the yeast mixture, appropriate salt, abundant lemon zest, and as much milk as it absorbs to make a good soft dough
4. Work the dough for a long time, always stirring in one direction to make it fine and soft
5. When worked, mix in 1/8 kilogram raisins and small grapes
6. Butter and flour a large pan and pour the dough into it, then let it rise in a warm place
7. When well risen and the pan is full, put it into the oven to bake
8. When nearly done, beat 8 egg whites until stiff, mix with grated chocolate and powdered sugar, and thickly coat the baked dough
9. Return to the oven until the foam dries on it
10. Slice when half cold, as the knife would crush the fine soft dough when hot, and sprinkle with vanilla sugar

title: Puliszka (Polenta)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- one litre cornmeal
- fourteen dekagrams bacon slices
- parmesan cheese (same amount as bacon)
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil water in a one-and-a-half litre pot, and when it boils well, pour out one quarter
2. Salt the water remaining in the pot, place a cooking spoon in the center, and around it pack in one litre of sweet cornmeal, and let it cook for half an hour more
3. Meanwhile, fry out fourteen dekagrams of bacon slices and grate the same amount of parmesan cheese onto a plate
4. Into the bottom of a warmed bowl, place one quarter of the melted bacon fat and cheese, then one quarter of the cooked cornmeal, then bacon fat and cheese again, then cornmeal, and so on
5. Finally, place the bacon slices and remaining cheese on top

title: Pulyka Béllelve (Stuffed Turkey)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young large turkey
- veal
- goose liver
- bacon
- fresh mushrooms
- button mushrooms
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- eggs
- white wine

## Directions

1. Blanch a young but fairly large turkey and split its skin lengthwise down the back.
2. Using a sharp knife, carefully extract the legs to the thigh and wing, being careful not to damage the skin.
3. Blanch twenty-eight dekagrams of veal, one goose liver, a small amount of bacon cut to the size of peas, several finely chopped fresh mushrooms and button mushrooms.
4. Braise with butter and green parsley, add finely minced onion, salt and pepper.
5. Place everything in a bowl, beat in two whole eggs and egg yolk, mix well with the rest.
6. Stuff the turkey with this mixture and sew the skin closed at the back.
7. Cover the turkey with thin bacon slices, wrap well with string and place in a pot that should not be larger than the turkey.
8. Pour three spoonfuls of good broth over it, two deciliters of white wine and cook slowly.
9. When the turkey is sufficiently tender, remove it and place where it will not cool.
10. Skim the fat from the sauce, strain through a sieve and cook until only a small amount remains.
11. Remove the bacon from the turkey, place on the plate, pour the small amount of sauce underneath and arrange small braised potatoes around the edge.

title: Pulyka- és récze szárnyvagdalék (Turkey and Duck Wing Meatballs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey and duck wings and giblets
- green parsley
- carrot
- leek
- butter sauce
- 30-40 crayfish tails
- asparagus
- veal
- roll soaked in milk
- marrow
- red onion
- green parsley
- salt
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook turkey and duck wings and giblets with green parsley, carrot, and leek until tender
2. Prepare a light butter sauce
3. Cut the wings and giblets finely and place in the sauce
4. Add 30-40 crayfish tails and finely chopped asparagus
5. Combine veal, half a roll soaked in milk, a little marrow, red onion, green parsley chopped finely, and salt with two egg yolks
6. Form small dumplings from this mixture
7. Fry them until golden brown
8. Add to the wings and giblets in the sauce
9. Bring slightly to a boil together and serve

title: Pulykakarbonád Fricandeauban (Turkey Chops Fricandeau)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young large turkey
- salted water
- bacon
- ham
- veal knuckle
- onion
- brown broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Blanch a young large turkey in salted water; remove the thighs as large as possible, remove the wings and breast on both sides, keeping the wing as a chop.
2. Remove the thighbone up to the middle of the joint; the upper part of the thigh should remain nicely exposed, as should the upper part of the wing.
3. Stud densely with finely chopped bacon, cut the bacon short so it looks nicely curled.
4. Place a piece of ham and veal knuckle in a pot with a little onion, pour three to four spoonfuls of brown broth over, cover and braise until the chops are well tender.
5. Remove them, strain the sauce through a sieve, skim the fat and cook until it becomes glaze.
6. Return the chops to it, squeeze the juice of one lemon over them, turn the chops well in the glaze, then arrange on a plate, pour the small amount of sauce over them and decorate with a wreath made from butter pastry.
7. It can also be served with sorrel sauce.

title: Puncs (Punch)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fél kilogramm apróra vágott czukor
- egy czitrom leve
- három-négy narancs leve
- kis pohárnyi rum fele
- egy dekagramm orosz thea
- másfél liter forró víz
- rum

## Directions

1. Fél kilogramm apróra vágott czukrot tálba teszünk
2. Egy czitromnak és három-négy narancsnak levét, és kis pohárnyi rum felét hozzáadjuk
3. Ezt beborítva, meleg helyre állítjuk
4. Aztán egy dekagramm orosz theára másfél liter forró vizet öntünk
5. Azt pár perc múlva leszűrjük
6. A többihez öntjük, és azonnal feladjuk
7. A rumot üvegben külön adjuk melléje, hogy ki-ki saját ízlése szerint használhassa

title: Puncs-fagylalt (Punch Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tablespoon rice
- 1 liter water
- juice of several lemons
- 1 glass lemon juice
- 4 glasses crushed sugar
- half glass rum
- 4 glasses rice water

## Directions

1. Cook a tablespoon of rice in 1 liter water, skim well
2. When rice has burst, remove from fire
3. Squeeze juice from several lemons
4. Measure 1 glass lemon juice, 4 glasses crushed sugar, half glass rum, and 4 glasses rice water
5. Place in a bowl but stir frequently until sugar dissolves and the mixture cools
6. Proceed as with other ice creams
7. Rice water may be replaced with light Holland or corn tea

title: Puncs-Fánk (Punch-Filled Cakes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cake batter
- fine sugar for sprinkling
- twenty-eight dekagrams coarsely ground sugar
- two deciliters water
- two teaspoons lemon juice
- two teaspoons sugar rubbed on lemon
- two teaspoons good rum
- preserved apricots or raspberries for filling

## Directions

1. Fill a pastry cone with sponge cake batter
2. Pipe small coin-sized cakes onto white paper
3. Sprinkle with fine sugar and place on baking sheet
4. Bake at low heat
5. When cooled, peel off with knife
6. Fill half of each cake with preserved apricot or raspberry at the bottom, cover with another cake
7. For the filling: pour two deciliters water onto twenty-eight dekagrams coarsely ground sugar in a deep brass pan
8. Place on charcoal fire, stir several times and cook until sugar completely dissolves and thickens slightly
9. Test by passing a slotted spoon through it and blowing hard on it; if many small bubbles flow through like soap bubbles, the sugar is ready
10. Remove from heat
11. Take a new cooking spoon, dip halfway into the sugar and continuously rub against the pan's side, keeping the spoon in the empty pan area, working only what flows from the dipped spoon, doing this quickly and without pause
12. When sugar begins to develop a white skin, quickly stir it off and continue the previous method until sugar becomes white and thick
13. Add two teaspoons lemon juice, two teaspoons sugar rubbed on lemon, and two teaspoons good rum
14. Mix well and thoroughly until well combined with the sugar
15. The filling is good when white and thick like light baby pap
16. Dip the filled cakes one by one into the filling, turning both sides with two four-pronged forks
17. Place on white writing paper with the forks and leave until firm
18. Remove and arrange neatly on a plate

title: Puncs-kocsonya (Punch Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lemons
- four cups crushed sugar per one cup lemon juice
- one-half cup rum per one cup lemon juice
- four cups boiled rice water or corn tea or Dutch tea (the former is more pleasant due to vanilla flavor)
- seven deciliters of corn kernels
- eighteen deciliters water
- five dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- alkanet for coloring (optional)

## Directions

1. Squeeze the lemons and strain the juice through clean cloth
2. Place all the above items in a soup bowl: one cup lemon juice, four cups crushed sugar, one-half cup rum, and four cups boiled rice water or corn tea
3. Rinse seven deciliters of corn kernels and boil them with eighteen deciliters of water for about an hour or longer
4. Strain and prepare the punch with this liquid
5. Stir the items in the bowl frequently until the sugar is completely dissolved
6. Add five dekagrams of dissolved isinglass mixed with the rest
7. Strain this jelly through table linen
8. Optionally color it red with alkanet, then place in a mold and freeze on ice

title: Párolt bárányhús (Braised Lamb)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two lamb legs
- two heads of red onion
- carrot
- thyme
- bay leaf
- three tablespoons of brown broth
- veal bones
- lard
- flour
- vinegar
- lemon zest
- six large cucumbers

## Directions

1. Blanch two lamb legs and thread them
2. Place sliced red onion and carrot in a pot, put the legs on top
3. Add thyme, bay leaf, three tablespoons of brown broth, and chopped veal bones
4. Cook until it becomes glace
5. Heat a tablespoon of lard and place the braised onion and carrot with one tablespoon of flour in it
6. Brown well and dilute with broth
7. Add a little vinegar and lemon zest and cook well
8. Strain through a sieve
9. Cut six large cucumbers into leaves and add to the sauce and bring to a boil
10. Place the legs in the glace, turning them so they are coated on all sides, especially on the side where they were threaded
11. Place on a plate, pour the sauce on the bottom, and arrange the small cucumbers around

title: Párolt cseresznye csokoládéval (Stewed Cherries with Chocolate)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Good broth
- Black cherries
- One and a half liter cherries
- Two deciliter sweet cream
- Finely grated fine chocolate

## Directions

1. Remove stems from black cherries and wash them
2. Put in a pot and stew until soft
3. Pour two deciliter sweet cream over the one and a half liter of cherries
4. Bring to a boil with the cherries
5. Add finely grated fine chocolate to a bowl
6. Cook the mixture until the chocolate is dissolved
7. Pour onto a plate
8. Arrange sponge cake pieces neatly around it

title: Párolt galamb (Braised Pigeon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three pigeons
- salt
- bacon slices
- red onion
- carrots
- flour
- good broth
- good vinegar
- lemon zest
- whole peppercorns
- capers
- two tablespoons sour cream
- butter pastry

## Directions

1. Season two or three pigeons with salt and prepare them nicely
2. Place several slices of bacon, red onion, and carrots in a pot, place the pigeons on top and braise until nicely browned
3. Remove them, sprinkle a little flour over them, brown, dilute with good broth and a little good vinegar
4. Add a little lemon zest and several whole peppercorns, cook the sauce well and strain through a sieve
5. Add fine capers and two tablespoons of sour cream
6. Place the pigeons back in and braise a little more
7. Decorate with a garland of butter pastry

title: Párolt marhahús (Braised Beef with Vegetables)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Six to eight slices of bacon
- One whole carrot, sliced
- Red onion
- Good broth
- Young kohlrabi, several
- Large carrots
- Butter
- Sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook the beef as usual
2. When it begins to become tender, place six to eight bacon slices, one whole sliced carrot, and red onion in a pot
3. Place the beef on top, cover, and braise slowly
4. From time to time pour a small amount of good broth over it and turn on all sides so it browns beautifully
5. Take several young kohlrabi and large carrots and using a special vegetable scoop (the kind used for kohlrabi), cut them into small balls
6. Melt a piece of butter in the pot with the meat, add a piece of sugar, and when it dissolves, add the carrot and kohlrabi balls
7. Braise until tender, pouring a small amount of broth frequently over them to prevent browning
8. Before serving, remove the fat from the sauce, strain through a sieve, and arrange the braised kohlrabi and carrot around the platter as a border
9. Place the meat in the center, pour the sauce over, and the dish is ready
10. If serving with potatoes, treat the meat the same way but add a few slices of ham, boil small potatoes, peel them, fry them in butter, and use them as a crown around the meat

title: Párolt Marhahús Burgonyakéreggel (Braised Beef with Potato Crust)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef
- bacon slices
- carrot slices
- red onion slices
- flour
- good broth
- red wine
- carrot
- butter
- green parsley
- sugar
- three large potatoes
- egg yolks
- salt
- whole eggs

## Directions

1. Place the cooked meat in a pot lined with bacon, carrot, and red onion slices
2. Turn frequently so it browns on all sides
3. Remove the meat and sprinkle with flour
4. When the flour is browned, dilute with half good broth and half red wine
5. Simmer until well reduced and strain
6. Using a special tool, cut small balls from carrot and braise them gently with butter, green parsley, and sugar without browning
7. Cook three large potatoes, peel, and grate them when cooled
8. Whip in a piece of butter, mix with finely chopped parsley, potatoes, salt, two whole eggs and two egg yolks
9. Coat the inner part of the meat evenly with this mixture and bake in the oven until the crust browns nicely
10. Serve the meat in the center of the dish, place the brown sauce at the bottom, and arrange the braised carrots around the meat like a wreath

title: Párolt Marhahús Rizskéreggel (Braised Beef with Rice Crust)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful piece of beef
- bacon slices
- red onion slices
- carrot slices
- flour
- good broth
- seven to ten decagrams of rice
- white broth
- lean ham
- green parsley
- two deciliters of sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook a beautiful piece of beef until tender and place in a pot with a bottom lined with bacon, red onion, and carrot slices
2. Cover and braise slowly, turning frequently so it browns on all sides
3. Remove the meat and sprinkle with a spoonful of flour, browning it
4. Pour good broth over it and reduce the sauce to a thick consistency
5. Meanwhile, cook seven to ten decagrams of rice in white broth until tender
6. Coat the top part of the meat with the rice using a wide knife
7. Finely chop a piece of lean ham and green parsley, mix with two deciliters of sour cream
8. Pour this mixture over the rice crust and place in the oven
9. Before serving, strain the brown sauce into the serving dish, place the meat on top without damaging the crust

title: Párolt palacsinta (Steamed Pancakes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pancakes
- butter
- seven dekagrams swollen and finely ground almonds
- ten dekagrams finely ground sugar
- vanilla
- six egg yolks
- four deciliters sour cream
- two deciliters strained strawberries, raspberries or currants
- ten to fourteen dekagrams sugar

## Directions

1. Cook pancakes and cut them into fine strips
2. Thickly sprinkle a buttered saucepan with the strips, leaving about one finger's width of space at the top
3. In a bowl, combine seven dekagrams swollen and finely ground almonds, ten dekagrams finely ground sugar, a little vanilla and six egg yolks, mix well with four deciliters sour cream
4. Pour over the pancakes and steam
5. When serving, turn out onto a platter
6. Cook two deciliters strained strawberries, raspberries or currants with ten to fourteen dekagrams ground sugar until thick and coat the top of the pancakes smoothly with this

title: Párolt reszelt tészta (Braised Grated Noodle Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 7 deciliters hot milk
- 10 dekagrams butter
- vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Cook grated noodle dough made from 2 eggs in 7 deciliters hot milk
2. When well cooked and thick, place in a pot in which 10 dekagrams butter has been melted
3. Mix together and braise with continuous stirring
4. When serving sprinkle with vanilla sugar

title: Párolt rostélyos (Braised Roasted Beef)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon or fat
- green parsley
- garlic
- lemon zest
- peppercorns
- roasted beef
- vinegar
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Place a few slices of bacon or fat, green parsley, a small piece of garlic, lemon zest, all chopped finely, and several peppercorns in a pot
2. Wash the roasted beef well, beat it, and salt it
3. Place it in the pot, cover it, and braise slowly
4. Frequently pour a spoonful of liquid over it so it has only very little sauce
5. Strain off the fat, add a little vinegar and one to two spoonfuls of sour cream
6. Braise well; the sauce should be scant and thick
7. For half a kilogram of roasted beef, use two small heads of red onion, a small bunch of parsley, and a small piece of garlic

title: Párolt rák (Braised Crayfish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- butter
- fresh parsley
- garlic
- salt
- sour cream
- white wine

## Directions

1. Rinse the crayfish thoroughly
2. Melt butter and add finely chopped parsley and garlic
3. Add the crayfish and salt
4. Braise for a while
5. Add several tablespoons of sour cream and white wine
6. Continue braising until the crayfish turn red

title: Párolt Récze Vereshagymával (Braised Duck with Red Onions)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- bacon slices
- ham
- red onions
- carrot
- flour
- broth
- white vinegar
- lemon zest
- butter

## Directions

1. After plucking the duck, salt and prepare it.
2. Slice bacon, ham, red onions and carrot into rings and place in a pot.
3. Place the ducks on top and braise slowly until soft and browned on all sides.
4. Remove the ducks, sprinkle two small spoonfuls of flour on them and brown them.
5. Dilute with good broth, add a little white vinegar and cook the sauce well.
6. Strain the sauce through a sieve, return the ducks to it, add a little lemon zest and braise slightly more.
7. Boil 30-40 walnut-sized red onions in water for a minute, having cut off a leaf from top and bottom, then place in cold water and peel the upper half.
8. Heat a piece of butter, place the onions in it, turn them frequently so they brown evenly, add a little brown broth and salt, cook until tender without falling apart.
9. When serving, place the ducks in the center of the dish and arrange the braised onions in a wreath shape around them.

title: Párolt tekercsek (Braised Rolls)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beef for broth
- beef marrow
- salt
- water
- green parsley
- butter
- red onion
- breadcrumbs
- bacon
- carrot
- thread
- flour
- vinegar
- lemon zest
- sour cream
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut pieces from beef broth meat that are as long as a knife is wide but not thicker than a knife spine
2. Beat them and salt them
3. Pour boiling salted water over beef marrow, peel off the membrane cleanly, chop it finely, and braise it in butter with green parsley
4. Add a little chopped red onion, salt, pepper, a handful of breadcrumbs, and some liquid, and bring to a slight boil and cool
5. Spread this filling on the meat, roll it up, and tie it with thread
6. Place bacon, red onion, and carrot in a pot, put the rolls in, and braise until their sauce has evaporated and they brown
7. Remove them, place one to two spoonfuls of flour in the pot, brown it, dilute it with good liquid, add vinegar and lemon zest, cook the sauce well, strain it, place the rolls back in, add two spoonfuls of sour cream, and braise slightly
8. Arrange the rolls in a line on a serving dish, strain the sauce over them, and border the edge of the dish with crescents made from short pastry, or after cutting off both ends of the rolls, pile them high in the center of the dish, pour the scant sauce underneath, and surround them with a wreath of mushroom or truffle, which has been browned in butter first

title: Párolt Vesepecsenye (Braised Kidney)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney front part
- salt
- bacon
- red onion
- carrot
- broth
- vinegar
- sour cream
- parsley
- red onion (1 head)
- garlic
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the front part of the kidney and salt it
2. Place sliced bacon, sliced red onion, and sliced carrot in a pot with the kidney on top
3. Braise slowly, pouring a little broth over it occasionally to prevent it from burning or browning too much
4. When nicely brown and tender, add a little vinegar and one or two spoons of sour cream and braise a while longer
5. Remove and slice thinly
6. Finely chop parsley, one head of red onion, and a little garlic
7. Place a few spoons of sour cream and butter in a pot, then alternate layers: a layer of kidney, sprinkled with the finely chopped mixture, then butter, sour cream and a spoon of vinegar, then kidney again, until everything is used
8. Finally, place coals on top and braise slowly

title: Párolt vesepecsenye savanyú uborkával (Braised Kidney with Sour Cucumber)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- A szép darab vesepecsenyét
- finoman vágott szalonna
- vereshagyma
- sárgarépa
- 3-4 szem szegfűborso
- ugyanannyi borsszemet
- két kis kanál liszt
- két szedőkanál jó lé
- két kanál jó ecet
- ugyanannyi tejfel
- kevés citromhéja
- 20-25 nagyobb savanyú uborka
- kis csésze jó tejfel

## Directions

1. Miután a vesepecsenyét megmostuk, hártyájától megtisztítottuk, a kövérséget és csontokat leszedtük
2. Finoman vágott szalonnával öt sorban megtűzünk és lábasba teszünk
3. A lábas fenékét szalonna-, vereshagyma- és sárgarépa-szeletekkel raktuk ki
4. 3-4 szem szegfűborsot és ugyanannyi borsszemet teszünk bele
5. A tűzött oldalnak felül kell jönnie
6. Befedjük, parázst teszünk fölibe és addig pároljuk, míg szépen megbarnúl
7. Kiveszszük, két kis kanál lisztet hintünk reá, melyet többszöri felkavarás mellett megbarnítunk
8. Aztán két szedőkanál jó lével feleresztjük
9. Két kanál jó eczetet, ugyanannyi tejfelt és kevés citromhéjat teszünk bele
10. Jól elfőzzük, aztán a mártást átszűrjük
11. A vesepecsenyét ismét beletesszük és még negyedóráig pároljuk
12. A mártásból kávéscsészényit más lábasba teszünk
13. 20-25 nagyobb savanyú uborkát szeletekre vágunk
14. A mártásba teszszük, kis csésze jó tejfelt adunk hozzá
15. Gyakori felrázás közben addig főzzük, míg a mártás jó öregre elfőtt
16. Az uborkának szép egésznek kell maradnia
17. Tálaláskor a vesepecsenyét a tál közepére helyezzük
18. A mártást felibe és az uborkákat a tál szélére köröskörűi

title: Párolt ürüczomb (Braised Lamb Leg)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb leg
- anchovy fillets
- bacon
- thread
- green parsley
- onion
- garlic
- peppercorns
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. If the leg has been in water for a while, remove the bone from the meat and pull off the fatty membrane
2. Drill holes through it with a knife and lard alternately with anchovy and bacon
3. Cut the bacon to the thickness and length of half the little finger
4. Wrap the leg tightly with strong thread so the bacon and anchovy do not fall out
5. Place in a pot that should not be larger than the leg
6. Add a bunch of green parsley, onion, a little garlic, and several peppercorns
7. Braise slowly for three to four hours
8. When the leg is tender, remove the thread and unwrap it, strain the sauce and clean the leg of fat
9. Chop green parsley, onion, several anchovies, and hard-boiled egg finely, add to the sauce
10. Pour a little broth over, return the leg to the sauce and braise a little longer

title: Pörkölt Bárányhús (Lamb Paprika Stew)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty-eight decagrams of fresh lard, cut into small cubes
- One small head of red onion, finely chopped
- Paprika
- Salt
- One quarter of a lamb, cut into beautiful pieces and rinsed

## Directions

1. Melt the lard cubes in a pot until the cracklings are beautifully yellow
2. Sauté the finely chopped red onion and paprika
3. Add salt and the lamb pieces cut into beautiful pieces and rinsed, to the lard mixture
4. Cover and let cook; there is no need to add broth because the meat will create its own liquid under the cover

title: Pörkölt hal (Fish Pörkölt)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fat or butter
- red onion
- paprika
- fish
- salt

## Directions

1. Heat a spoonful of fat or butter in a pot
2. Sauté three heads of red onion cut lengthwise until softened
3. Add a knife-point of red paprika for color
4. Add the chopped and salted fish
5. Cover and allow to cook

title: Pörkölt Káposzta Oldalossal (Braised Cabbage with Pork Belly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 decagrams bacon, sliced
- 1 kilogram pork belly, cut lengthwise

## Directions

1. Fry the sliced bacon and pork belly together in a pot
2. Add cabbage and braise
3. Stir frequently and cook until the cabbage is well-browned

title: Pörkölt malacz (Pork Goulash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig, cut into pieces
- smoked bacon, twenty-eight dekagrams, cut into cubes
- red onion, finely chopped
- paprika
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the suckling pig into nice pieces and wash it, then place in a dry pot so that the water drains from it. In the meantime, fry the diced bacon in a pot until crispy. Brown the finely chopped red onion in the rendered fat, and briefly do the same with the paprika so it takes on a nice color. Then add the meat, salt it, and stir frequently during cooking so it does not stick. Do not cover it, otherwise it will produce too much liquid and will not cook down completely to the fat sauce. Prepare pörkölt chicken, duck, turkey, etc. in the same manner.

title: Pörkölt marhahús (Beef Goulash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beef, preferably kidney steak, one and a half kilograms, finely chopped
- water, four decilitres
- salt
- paprika

## Directions

1. Cook the finely chopped beef with four decilitres of water, salt, and paprika until it cooks down to its fat.

title: Pörkölt Pulyka (Turkey Paprika Stew)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one cleaned turkey
- one egg-sized amount of lard
- one head of red onion, finely chopped
- one tablespoon of paprika
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned turkey into small pieces.
2. In a pot, melt the lard; when hot, add the finely chopped red onion and paprika.
3. Add the turkey meat to the paprika-lard mixture and salt it.
4. Cover and braise, stirring often, for one to two hours (depending on whether the turkey is young or old), until it browns.
5. Add small amounts of water from time to time to prevent the meat from drying out and browning too much.
6. Serve in a long dish and border with rice mixed with the turkey's finely chopped liver and giblets.

title: Remoulade-mártás (Remoulade Sauce - Cold Fish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolks
- vinegar
- oil
- pickled cucumber
- capers
- tarragon
- green parsley
- caviar
- one to two boiled egg yolks
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare exactly as tartar sauce, except without mustard
2. Add the following ingredients: pickled cucumber, capers, tarragon, green parsley, whole caviar, and one to two boiled egg yolks pressed through a sieve, with a small amount of vinegar

title: Ribizke-mártás (Currant Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7-72 liters of currants
- cold water
- a little butter or lard
- a piece of sugar
- meat broth
- sour cream
- a little flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the seeds from the currants and wash them in cold water
2. Stew them slowly in a little butter or lard, well covered, with a piece of sugar added
3. Pour meat broth over them
4. Mix sour cream with a little flour and combine the two mixtures
5. Salt lightly and bring to a boil

title: Ribizke-mártás (Currant Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half to three-quarter liter currants
- butter or lard
- sugar
- meat stock
- sour cream
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the seeds from the currants and wash them in cold water
2. Braise gently in butter or lard (well covered) and add a piece of sugar to it
3. Pour meat stock over it
4. Mix sour cream with a small amount of flour
5. Combine both mixtures, season lightly with salt and bring to a boil

title: Ribizli-fagylalt (Currant Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- currants
- 7 deciliters currant juice
- half kilogram sugar
- 3 deciliters water
- juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Press currants through cloth
2. Cook half kilogram sugar in 3 deciliters water until half-reduced
3. Pour into the currant juice
4. Mix in the juice of 2 lemons
5. Place in freezing container

title: Ribizli-kocsonya (Currant Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large ripe currants
- forty-two dekagrams sugar
- four deciliters water
- seven deciliters currant juice
- juice of two lemons
- four and one-half dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- finely crushed ice

## Directions

1. Remove ripe currants from their stems and extract their juice
2. Cook forty-two dekagrams of sugar in a brass pan with four deciliters of water until it forms heavy drops
3. Add seven deciliters of currant juice, stir well
4. Squeeze the juice of two lemons into it and cool slightly
5. Mix in four and one-half dekagrams of dissolved isinglass and strain the entire mixture through clean cloth
6. If not sufficiently clear and transparent, repeat the straining
7. Pour into an attractive mold, place on finely crushed ice
8. When sufficiently frozen, unmold onto a serving dish and present at table

title: Ribizlit befőzni (Preserved Currants)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- currants
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Remove beautiful ripe currants from the stem.
2. For 500 grams of sugar, boil with one glass of water until it throws large bubbles.
3. Take the same amount of stemmed currants and treat them as you would raspberries.

title: Ribizlit egész fürtökben befőzni (Preserved Currants in Whole Bunches)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- currants in bunches
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Prepare the sugar as in the previous recipe; for 500 grams of sugar, take the same amount of bunched currants.
2. Select the most beautiful ones, bring to a boil five or six times, then carefully remove with a slotted spoon onto a sieve, placing the drained juice with the rest.
3. Boil until thick, then remove from the fire, carefully place the currants into it and cool slightly.
4. Before it cools completely, fill into glasses and tie them as you would other preserved glasses.

title: Ringlót pálinkában (Greengage Plums in Brandy)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 slightly unripe greengages
- cloth for cleaning
- 2 parts fresh water
- 1 part white vinegar
- 1 kilogram sugar
- 1 liter water
- 1.5 liters good brandy

## Directions

1. Clean 150 slightly unripe greengages carefully with a cloth and prick them all over with a needle.
2. Soften them in a mixture of 2 parts fresh water and 1 part white vinegar for three to four hours.
3. Cook 1 kilogram sugar with 1 liter water and skim it well.
4. When the syrup is sufficiently clear, place the greengages in a clean basin, let the syrup cool slightly, and pour it over them.
5. Leave for 24 hours so the fruit absorbs the sugar well.
6. Strain the syrup, place the fruit in glass jars, cook the syrup again until it becomes thick, let it cool, and pour it over the fruit.
7. Pour 1.5 liters of good brandy over this quantity, tie the jars closed, prick the paper with a needle, and store.
8. When the fruit settles to the bottom of the jar, it is ready to eat.

title: Ringlót pálinkában (Greengage Plums in Spirits)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one hundred fifty not quite ripe greengages
- cloth
- needle
- two parts fresh water
- one part white vinegar
- one kilogram sugar
- one liter water

## Directions

1. Clean one hundred fifty not quite ripe greengages with a cloth and prick all over with a needle
2. Soften them in two parts fresh water and one part white vinegar for three to four hours
3. Cook one kilogram sugar with one liter water and skim it well

title: Ringlót Párban (Greengages in Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- largest beautiful green ripe greengages
- sugar
- water
- cooked sugar water

## Directions

1. Wipe the largest beautiful green ripe greengages individually and pierce several times with a needle
2. Place them in the necessary jar, fill halfway with cooked and cooled sugar water, bind well and simmer for 3-4 minutes (per instructions 961)
3. All varieties of plums are prepared the same way, removing the pit first, then arrange neatly, fill halfway with cooked sugar water, and treat as with the others
4. For greengages, add 1 small spoonful of fine rum to each jar, which enhances the good flavor

title: Ringlót-szilvát befőzni (Preserved Greengage Plums)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- greengages
- water
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil the greengages in two parts water and one part vinegar, but allow only a few boils so the skin does not crack and the stem does not fall out.
2. Then place in fresh water.
3. For 500 grams of sugar, allow 25 dekagrams of fruit.
4. Boil the sugar with one glass of water, clarify it, and boil until it throws heavy drops.
5. Place the greengages on a sieve so they drain well, then into the sugar, and bring to a boil with it for one moment and remove from the fire, pour over them, and leave open for twenty-four hours.
6. After this time, strain the syrup from them well and boil it again, cool it, pour over the fruit; repeat this procedure for three days.
7. The greengage must not be completely ripe, and for this reason those at the end of July are the best and most suitable.

title: Risotto all'Italiana (Italian-style Rice)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams rice
- 3 carrots
- 3 kohlrabis
- 2 half heads of cabbage
- breast of roasted chicken or turkey
- half a large goose liver, fried in fat
- salt, pepper, nutmeg
- fresh butter or goose fat
- 1 tablespoon good meat broth
- meat broth for serving

## Directions

1. Wash the rice and clean the vegetables, cutting them into thin slices
2. Cut the chicken breast and goose liver similarly into thin slices and season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
3. Coat a pot with fresh butter or goose fat
4. Layer rice, then vegetables, then meat and liver, and repeat until the pot is full
5. Cover with rice and dot with a few pieces of butter
6. Pour half a tablespoon of good meat broth over it
7. Cover the pot with a lid and braise in the oven until the rice and vegetables are soft, the rice remaining whole and not becoming soupy; add more broth if needed
8. Cover the pot with a folded kitchen towel and serve in a soup tureen with strained meat broth

title: Rizs angol módon (Rice English Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- eighteen dekagrams rice
- warm water
- seven dekagrams raisins
- five dekagrams currants
- four crumbly sour apples
- eighteen dekagrams ground sugar
- butter
- preserved cherries

## Directions

1. Soften eighteen dekagrams rice in warm water for a quarter hour, then sieve
2. Clean, wash and dry seven dekagrams raisins and five dekagrams currants
3. Peel four crumbly sour apples, cut into slices and sift eighteen dekagrams ground sugar
4. Line the bottom of a buttered saucepan thickly with apple slices
5. Add a layer of heavily sugared rice, then some raisins and currants, then another layer of apples, sugar, rice and a layer of preserved cherries, then repeat with apples

title: Rizs angol módon (Rice English Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 180 grams rice
- 70 grams raisins
- 50 grams currants
- 4 tart apples
- 180 grams crushed sugar
- butter
- preserved sour cherries
- preserved fruit with syrup

## Directions

1. Soften the rice in warm water for a quarter hour, then strain through a sieve
2. Clean, wash and sort the raisins and currants
3. Peel the tart apples, slice them into pieces, and sift the crushed sugar
4. Line the bottom of a buttered pot thickly with apple slices
5. Make a layer of heavily sugared rice, then a small amount of raisins and currants
6. Make another layer of apples, sugar, and rice, then a layer of preserved sour cherries
7. Add more apples and the remaining raisins and currants
8. Top with a final layer of sugared rice
9. Fill the pot with well-sugared preserved fruit syrup so that it covers the rice
10. Place in a warm oven and leave until the liquid is completely absorbed
11. While cooking, prepare cone-shaped pastry
12. Turn the rice out onto a plate and decorate beautifully with the pastry

title: Rizs csokoládéval, vagy tejsodóval (Rice with Chocolate or Custard)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagrams rice, blanched
- 1 liter heavy cream
- 4 tablets finely grated chocolate
- 8 dekagrams softened and finely ground almonds
- 10 dekagrams sugar
- whole preserved cherries
- 4 deciliters heavy cream
- 5 egg yolks
- 10 dekagrams ground sugar
- finely ground vanilla

## Directions

1. Cook the blanched rice in one liter of heavy cream
2. Add the finely grated chocolate, softened almonds, and sugar
3. Cook until the rice is soft but the grains remain whole
4. Pile high in the center of a dish
5. Decorate with whole preserved cherries
6. Bake in a cooled oven
7. For the custard: Whip four deciliters of heavy cream with five egg yolks, ten dekagrams ground sugar, and a little finely ground vanilla
8. Place over low heat and stir constantly until it begins to thicken
9. Remove quickly from heat to prevent curdling
10. Pour the custard over the baked rice

title: Rizs tölcsértésztával, párban (Rice with Cone-shaped Paste, Steamed)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- good milk
- 10 dekagrams softened and finely ground almonds
- sugar
- 7 dekagrams butter
- 14 dekagrams rice
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Make cones and round sheets the same way as for the noodle dish
2. Cook rice in good milk
3. Add 10 dekagrams softened and finely ground almonds and enough sugar to make it sufficiently sweet
4. When the rice is soft cool it and place in 7 dekagrams creamed butter
5. Mix 2 whole eggs and 4 egg yolks with 14 dekagrams rice and finally beat in 4 egg whites

title: Rizs-fagylalt (Rice Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 dekagrams rice, well washed
- 7 deciliters cream
- piece of vanilla
- 21 dekagrams fine sugar
- 4 deciliters cream

## Directions

1. Cook 56 dekagrams washed rice in 7 deciliters cream with a piece of vanilla cut into small pieces until the rice is soft
2. Press finely
3. Beat 21 dekagrams finely crushed sugar with the rice in a bowl until frothy
4. Add 4 deciliters cream
5. Pour into freezing container
6. Bury in well-salted ice and let freeze
7. During freezing, scrape down around the container twice without stirring
8. When turned out, line the bottom with two rows of Spanish fritters arranged like a crown

title: Rizs-pudding (Rice Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams rice
- 9 dekagrams ground almonds
- milk
- 10 dekagrams butter
- 1 whole egg
- 4 egg yolks
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- little vanilla
- 8 egg yolks
- 7 deciliters cream

## Directions

1. Cook 14 dekagrams rice with 9 dekagrams ground almonds in milk and cool
2. Whip 10 dekagrams butter until fluffy
3. Add the rice, 1 whole egg and 4 egg yolks
4. Mix well and pour into a buttered mold and steam
5. Pour cream sauce over it, made as follows: pound 14 dekagrams sugar with a little vanilla and mix well with 8 egg yolks in a pan
6. Slowly add 7 deciliters of thick boiled cream
7. Place the pan over coals and keep stirring until it begins to thicken
8. Remove quickly from heat and pour over the pudding

title: Rizs-pép csokoládéöntéssel (Rice Pap with Chocolate Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- rice pap (prepared unfilled)
- cream custard or chocolate sauce
- 4 bars fine chocolate
- 4 decilitres cream
- ground sugar
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Prepare rice pap as before, but without filling
2. Cook the pap in a steamer pot
3. For the chocolate sauce: boil 4 bars of fine chocolate in 4 decilitres of cream until thickened
4. In a separate pan, whisk together a small amount of ground sugar and 3 egg yolks
5. Pour the chocolate into the egg mixture with continuous stirring
6. Place on the fire and whisk until foamy
7. Pour the chocolate sauce around the cooked pap

title: Rizsabroncs (Rice Crown Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dekagrams rice
- 7 deciliters milk
- 7 dekagrams ground almonds
- 5 dekagrams fresh butter
- 6 egg yolks
- sugar and vanilla to taste
- 4 egg whites
- 10 large apricots
- 18 dekagrams sugar
- 2 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Grind 10 dekagrams of cleaned rice very finely in a mortar.
2. Bring 7 deciliters of milk to a boil, add the rice and 7 dekagrams of ground almonds, and stir continuously until a thick paste forms.
3. Remove from heat and stir in 5 dekagrams fresh butter, then cool.
4. Add 6 egg yolks one by one and sugar with vanilla to taste, beat until frothy.
5. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 4 eggs.
6. Pour into a buttered ring mold and steam for three-quarters of an hour.
7. Meanwhile, peel 10 large apricots, cut in half, and remove pits.
8. Cook 18 dekagrams sugar with 2 deciliters water until it forms heavy drops, add apricots and simmer only a moment so they remain whole.
9. Remove with a spoon to a plate, sprinkle with powdered sugar, and set aside in a warm place.
10. Cook the syrup until it becomes a light jelly.
11. Unmold the pudding onto a plate, fill the ring opening with apricots, coat with jelly, and pour the rest over the fruit.

title: Rizsgombóczleves (Rice Dumplings Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams rice
- water
- meat broth
- butter
- 7 dekagrams butter
- 1 egg
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- flour
- brown broth

## Directions

1. Wash the picked rice in lukewarm water well
2. Put in a pot with a little meat broth and a piece of butter and stew until soft
3. Beat 7 dekagrams butter until foamy
4. Mix in 1 whole egg and 3 egg yolks as well as the cooled fat
5. Season with salt and add a little flour to hold it together
6. Form small dumplings and cook them in white meat broth
7. Once cooked, remove and place in the soup bowl
8. Pour brown broth over it

title: Rizskádka irósvajas tésztában (Rice Cake in Puff Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramm rice
- 7 deciliter milk
- 9 dekagramm ground almond
- butter for frothing
- 10 dekagramm sugar rubbed with orange zest
- 1 whole egg
- 4 egg yolks
- puff pastry

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in milk until well softened
2. Add ground almond to the cooked rice and let cool
3. Place the cooled rice in frothed butter
4. Add the sugar rubbed with orange zest, beaten until frothy
5. During stirring, add one whole egg and four egg yolks
6. Prepare puff pastry and roll it out as if making noodles
7. Line long, flat molds with puff pastry
8. Fill each mold with the rice mixture
9. Cover with pastry and bake until golden brown

title: Rizskádka irósvajas tésztában (Rice Pudding in Butter Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Fourteen dekagrams rice
- Seven deciliters milk
- Nine dekagrams ground almonds

## Directions

1. Cook fourteen dekagrams rice in seven deciliters milk
2. Add nine dekagrams ground almonds when rice is well cooked

title: Rizskásás rétes (Rice Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- 14 decagrams rice
- 8 deciliters milk
- 7 decagrams butter, beaten until fluffy
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- broken sugar
- a little vanilla

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough
2. Cook 14 decagrams rice in 8 deciliters milk until soft
3. Mix 7 decagrams butter beaten until fluffy with 5 egg yolks and the stiff peaks of 3 egg whites
4. Add broken sugar and a little vanilla
5. Fill the stretched dough with this mixture
6. Fold the strudel together
7. Place in a buttered baking dish
8. Bake until golden brown
9. Sprinkle with sugar when serving

title: Rizsleves (Rice Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned pullet
- green parsley
- butter
- meat stock
- rice

## Directions

1. Cut cleaned pullet into pieces, salt lightly and let stand in salt
2. Place the chicken with finely chopped green parsley in a piece of melted butter and braise slowly, but to keep it nicely white, frequently pour little meat stock over it until soft
3. Then pour over one or two more spoonfuls of meat stock and braise slightly more

title: Rizsleves (Rice Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned chicken
- Salt
- Green parsley, finely chopped
- Butter
- Beef broth
- 14 dekagrams (1/4 pound) rice
- Nutmeg flower, a small amount
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned chicken into pieces and salt it lightly, allowing it to sit in the salt.
2. Place the chicken with finely chopped green parsley into a piece of melted butter and braise it slowly, pouring a little broth over it several times so it remains beautifully white until tender.
3. Pour another spoonful or two of broth over it and braise briefly.
4. Wash the carefully selected rice in lukewarm water and cook it in good beef broth for half an hour before serving so it becomes tender but remains whole, adding a small amount of nutmeg flower.
5. Beat 2 egg yolks in a small dish with a little broth, stirring well.
6. Place the chicken with the rice in a soup pot, pour this broth over it and serve.

title: Rizsliszt-pép (Rice Flour Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams rice, washed and dried
- 7 deciliters cold milk
- 7 dekagrams softened and finely ground almonds
- 10 dekagrams butter
- sugar rubbed on an orange
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites, whipped stiff
- whole preserved Spanish cherries
- quince paste

## Directions

1. Grind the washed and dried rice into fine flour
2. Place in a pot and gradually stir in the cold milk until smooth
3. Add the softened almonds and place over low heat
4. Cook with constant stirring into a thick, smooth paste
5. Remove from heat and while warm, stir in the butter and sugar rubbed on the orange
6. Cool
7. Stir in the egg yolks one after another
8. Fold in the stiffly whipped egg whites
9. Arrange whole preserved cherries and quince paste in a double star pattern on the bottom of a buttered mold or pot
10. Carefully fill with the paste
11. Set in a water bath for three-quarters of an hour and turn out
12. Surround the edge of the paste with cherry or raspberry syrup made with boiled sugar

title: Rizsmeridon (Rice Meridon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen dekagrams of well-washed rice
- seven decilitres of milk or good broth
- ten dekagrams of butter
- one whole egg
- five egg yolks
- thirty shrimp tails and claws
