- two or three soft-cooked goose or turkey forcemeat
- one piece of cooked calf's head
- salt
- three egg whites
- white paper
- asparagus heads

## Directions

1. Cook fourteen dekagrams of well-washed rice in seven decilitres of milk or good broth; when cooked tender, let cool and place in ten dekagrams of butter beaten until frothy, then gradually mix in one whole egg and five egg yolks one after another.
2. Add the shelled tails and claws of thirty shrimp, as well as two or three portions of soft-cooked goose or turkey forcemeat and one piece of cooked calf's head cut into strips, a little salt, and finally gradually three egg whites beaten stiff.
3. Grease a pot with butter, place white paper on the bottom of the pot, cut to fit, brush with a little butter, arrange neatly with whole shrimp tails and asparagus heads cooked in salted water, place the rice in it and cook in a water bath for one hour, carefully turn out onto a serving dish, remove the paper, and bring immediately to the table.

title: Rizsmeridon más módon (Rice Meridon Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen dekagrams of rice
- seven decilitres of milk
- ten dekagrams of butter
- one whole egg
- five egg yolks
- salt
- three egg whites
- butter sauce
- three cooked calf's tongues
- fifteen to twenty shelled shrimp tails
- six button mushrooms braised in butter
- asparagus or artichoke

## Directions

1. Cook fourteen dekagrams of rice in seven decilitres of milk, when cooled, mix with ten dekagrams of butter, one whole egg and five egg yolks, season lightly with salt, and finally gently mix in three egg whites beaten stiff.
2. Meanwhile, prepare a butter sauce, cut three cooked calf's tongues, fifteen to twenty shelled shrimp tails, six button mushrooms braised in butter, and a few asparagus or artichoke cooked in salted water into large cubes, place in the butter sauce and cook well.
3. When the sauce is completely cooked, mix two egg yolks with two tablespoons of cold broth and stir into the mixture, bring to a boil once more and let cool.
4. Arrange the bottom of a well-greased pot neatly with shrimp tails and asparagus heads, place half of the beaten rice into it, then the ragout in the middle and the other half of the rice on top, then place in a water bath where it should remain for three-quarters of an hour.

title: Rizsmorzsóka (Rice Crumb)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty-eight dekagrams rice
- One and one-half liters milk
- Twenty-one dekagrams butter, beaten fluffy
- Four whole eggs
- Four egg yolks
- Ten dekagrams powdered sugar
- Vanilla
- Optional: raisins and currants

## Directions

1. Pick over and wash rice
2. Cook in one and one-half liters milk until not quite soft
3. Beat butter until fluffy
4. Mix in four whole eggs and four egg yolks one by one
5. Add cooled rice, powdered sugar and vanilla
6. Do not stir vigorously so rice grains remain whole
7. Pour into a buttered dish
8. Place in oven and bake until golden brown
9. Break apart with a spatula
10. Sprinkle with sugar and serve
11. Optional raisins and currants may be mixed into the dough

title: Rizspástétom (Rice Pâté)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams rice
- milk
- crayfish butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- veal ear
- asparagus
- crayfish tails
- button mushrooms
- butter
- meat broth
- crumbly pastry

## Directions

1. Cook 14 dekagrams rice in milk until thick and cool it
2. Beat crayfish butter and add 4 egg yolks to it
3. Add the cooled rice and 4 egg whites and mix gently
4. Prepare a diced filling from veal ear, asparagus, crayfish tails, and button mushrooms
5. Melt some crayfish butter and brown the filling in it
6. Beat 2 egg yolks in a small cup, dilute with cold meat broth, pour over the filling and mix well
7. Prepare crumbly pastry and line a pot with it
8. Place half of the rice mixture in the pot
9. Layer the filling on top
10. Spread the remaining rice mixture on top and smooth it nicely
11. Prepare a pastry lid
12. Brush with egg yolk and bake slowly

title: Rose Salve
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 part white wax
- 4 parts lard
- 1 part rose water

## Directions

1. Melt white wax in a crucible
2. Add 4 parts lard and melt both together
3. When cooled, add 1 part rose water
4. Use for sore breasts and chapped skin

title: Rostély-tészta (Lattice Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven deciliters of fine flour
- salt
- twenty-eight dekagrams of lard
- twenty-one dekagrams of sifted sugar
- zest of one lemon, finely chopped
- a little cinnamon
- crushed clove and pepper
- one large egg

## Directions

1. Mix seven deciliters of fine flour with salt and twenty-eight dekagrams of lard
2. Add twenty-one dekagrams of sifted sugar, finely chopped zest of one lemon, a little cinnamon, and crushed clove and pepper
3. Work all this well together with a rolling pin
4. Make a hole in the middle of the whole, crack a large egg into it and make fine dough with a wide knife
5. Roll out to the thickness of the little finger and place on four sheet pans
6. Create a lattice on top with thin dough sticks
7. Brush with beaten egg
8. Bake to a nice light brown
9. Once removed from the oven, cut into equal rectangular pieces and arrange neatly on a plate

title: Rostélyon Sült Borjúszegy Paradiesommártással (Grilled Veal Chop with Tomato Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chop
- salt
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the chop in a press, weighting it with something flat
2. When cooled, cut into thin slices
3. Salt the slices lightly
4. Dip in egg and coat with breadcrumbs
5. Fry quickly in butter over high heat
6. Arrange on a plate in circles
7. Pour tomato sauce in the center

title: Rostélyon Sült Borjúszegy Paradiesommártással (Grilled Veal Chop with Tomato Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- nice marbled beef cut
- fat
- fresh parsley
- red onion
- lemon zest
- whole pepper
- vinegar
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Beat the beef, wash and salt it
2. Place fat, fresh parsley, red onion, lemon zest, and whole pepper in a pot
3. Add the beef and braise slowly with a small amount of liquid
4. When the meat is tender, pour vinegar and sour cream over it

title: Rostélyos kirántva (Breaded Fried Beef Roast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beef roast slices
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- fat

## Directions

1. Take well-beaten roast slices, remove the bones, dip them in beaten egg
2. Coat thoroughly with breadcrumbs and fry quickly in hot fat
3. When placing the slices on a plate, garnish with fresh green parsley

title: Rostélyos savanyú tejfellel (Braised Beef Roast with Sour Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beef roast (rib cut)
- fat
- fresh parsley
- onion
- lemon zest
- whole black pepper
- vinegar
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Beat a nice rib cut of beef, wash and salt it
2. Put fat, green parsley, red onion, lemon zest and whole pepper in a pot, add the roast and braise slowly, pouring in a little broth
3. When the meat has softened, pour vinegar and sour cream over it

title: Rudas Kenyér (Almond and Pistachio Biscotti)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams powdered sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 7 dekagrams blanched almonds, chopped
- 14 dekagrams ground almonds
- 7 dekagrams pistachios
- 4.5 dekagrams citron peel, chopped
- zest of 1 orange, chopped
- a little flour
- paper tubes for baking

## Directions

1. Beat the powdered sugar with the whole eggs and egg yolks in a bowl
2. Add the chopped blanched almonds, ground almonds, pistachios, and citron peel
3. Add the orange zest
4. Mix well with a little flour
5. Fill paper tubes with the mixture
6. Bake gently
7. When baked, remove and cut into equal bars like twice-baked bread
8. Dry in the oven

title: Rábaközi perecz (Raba Valley Pretzel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 decagrams flour
- 5 whole eggs
- 5 egg yolks
- 4 spoonfuls sweet sour cream
- salt
- cream and butter

## Directions

1. Mix well together 42 decagrams flour with 5 whole eggs and 5 egg yolks, 4 spoonfuls sweet sour cream, and a little salt
2. Beat all parts of the dough with the axe handle for a good hour
3. Form small pretzels from it and place them in hot water, 7-8 on a cooking spoon (head down) in the center of the pot
4. When the water brings the pretzels up, lift the spoon and remove them, quickly drain the water, place the pretzels on a board and press down by hand one by one to correct form, continuing until all are cooked
5. Turn them in creamy butter, place on a sheet close together, and bake in a moderately hot oven
6. This amount makes about 80 pretzels, which are good for coffee and tea and have the advantage of keeping for weeks without hardening or losing their flavor

title: Rácz Pite (Rácz Pie)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams soaked almonds
- 28 dekagrams raisins
- powdered cinnamon
- 14 dekagrams dried currants
- 14 dekagrams powdered sugar
- dough as in recipe 412
- egg yolk
- sour cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Finely chop the soaked almonds and the same amount of raisins
2. Add a little powdered cinnamon, 14 dekagrams dried currants, and 14 dekagrams powdered sugar
3. Mix all well together
4. Prepare dough as in recipe 412 and handle the same way
5. When the dough is drawn out, filled, and prepared up to the egg brushing step, cut it with a sharp knife into large, long cubes
6. Bake in gentle heat in the oven
7. As soon as it is baked, remove at once from the baking dish, as it would soften and lose its beautiful appearance
8. Cut the pieces completely through and arrange on a serving dish in a double star shape
9. Sprinkle with sugar
10. A spoonful of sour cream should be mixed into the butter used for spreading these cakes, otherwise they would be too dry

title: Rák-rétes (Crayfish Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- forty crayfish tails and claws
- crumbly bread roll
- sour cream
- crayfish butter
- one whole egg and two egg yolks
- four to five spoonfuls

## Directions

1. Extract the tails and claws from forty crayfish
2. Chop them coarsely
3. Grate crumbly bread roll and moisten it with sour cream
4. Beat a piece of crayfish butter until fluffy
5. Mix in one whole egg and two egg yolks
6. Add the moistened bread crumbs and the chopped crayfish pieces
7. Add four to five spoonfuls (of sour cream or broth)

title: Rák-rétes (Crayfish Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 crayfish tails and claws
- crumbly white bread, grated
- sour cream
- crayfish butter, beaten until fluffy
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- 4-5 spoons fresh sour cream
- strudel dough
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Shell and coarsely chop the crayfish tails and claws
2. Mix grated crumbly bread with sour cream
3. Beat crayfish butter until fluffy and mix in 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks
4. Add the moistened breadcrumbs and chopped crayfish tails with 4-5 spoonfuls of fresh sour cream to the butter mixture and stir well
5. Prepare strudel dough, stretch it finely and dry it
6. Brush with melted butter
7. Spread the filling on the dough and fold together
8. Brush the top with melted crayfish butter
9. Bake at gentle heat until golden

title: Rákba való más töltelék (Crayfish with Different Filling)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- rice
- pepper
- green parsley
- salt
- fat
- crayfish butter

## Directions

1. Calculate at least two larger or three smaller crayfish per person
2. For each crayfish take one spoon of rice, add a pinch of ground pepper, a pinch of finely chopped green parsley leaves, little salt, and per person one hazelnut-sized piece of fat
3. Mix these well together
4. Fill the back of the crayfish halfway; do not fill completely because when it cooks it will swell and if there is no space the rice will remain hard, and the tail will be difficult to push back since it must be used to close the filling so it does not run out during cooking
5. After the stuffed crayfish cooks for about half an hour, scatter the remaining spiced rice that was left over from the filling into the soup so it cooks too until it softens nicely but is not too soft
6. When done, strain the crayfish butter and add to it

title: Rákleves (Crayfish Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 36-40 medium-sized crayfish
- lightly salted water
- parsley
- piece of red onion
- crushed pepper
- thyme
- bay leaves
- basil
- half a liter light table wine
- fish broth (consommé)
- 100-150 grams white bread crumb

## Directions

1. Wash the crayfish carefully
2. Put lightly salted water in a casserole and add parsley, sliced red onion, crushed pepper, a little thyme, a few bay leaves, and a pinch of basil
3. Add half a liter of light table wine
4. Before the liquid boils, add the crayfish and let cook until completely done, stirring frequently
5. Remove the tails from the cooked crayfish and extract the meat from them
6. Grind the cooked torsos with their meat and the claws finely in a mortar
7. Place this ground mixture in a pot and pour the liquid in which the crayfish were cooked over it
8. Add 2 liters of fish broth (consommé) and 100-150 grams of white bread crumb
9. Bring the whole to a boil and cook for a good hour on a careful fire so it does not boil away
10. After one hour, strain through a fine sieve and set the broth aside

title: Rákleves (Crayfish Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- pea water

## Directions

1. Prepare the soup the same way as on meat days, but instead of meat broth use pea water.
2. Serve the soup with crayfish tails on top.

title: Rákleves (Crayfish Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- crayfish shells and claws
- butter
- fish stock (consommé)
- 2 liters fish stock
- 100-150 grams white bread crumb
- parsley
- eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from the cooked crayfish and set aside the shells
2. Pound the cooked trunk with the meat inside and the claws finely in a mortar
3. Place the pounded mixture in a pot and pour over it the liquid in which the crayfish were cooked
4. Add 2 liters of fish stock (consommé) and 100-150 grams of white bread crumb
5. Bring the whole to a boil and cook for about an hour on a careful fire so it does not reduce too much
6. After an hour, strain through a fine sieve and set the stock aside
7. Prepare crayfish butter: place the cooked crayfish shells and claw shells in a pot, fry with parsley and butter, then pound in a mortar, pour over with hot thin meat stock, set in a cold place, and after cooling the crayfish butter will gather on top of the liquid and should be removed and set aside
8. Stuff the nicer crayfish trunks, 2-3 pieces per person
9. The filling is made from bread soaked in milk, worked with butter, 8 egg yolks, little breadcrumb and crayfish butter, from which small dumplings are also made
10. Cook the dumplings in the prepared stock together with crayfish butter
11. Before serving, add a few spoonfuls of sour cream to the whole soup according to its quantity, and serve the whole soup hot with the picked crayfish meat

title: Rákleves más módon (Crayfish Soup Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty crayfish
- beef stock
- butter
- flour
- bread

## Directions

1. Cook about thirty crayfish in beef stock for six people
2. Remove the meat from the shells and pound both finely in a mortar
3. Heat a piece of butter and add the pounded crayfish shells, braise them in the butter for a while
4. Strain this crayfish butter through fine cloth, return the remaining shells to the stock in which the crayfish were cooked and let boil well
5. Heat the extracted crayfish butter, add two full spoons of flour and bring to a slight boil without browning
6. Pour over the same stock in which the crayfish shells were cooked, strain through a sieve and serve on fried bread
7. Break up the crayfish tails and claws before putting them in the mortar

title: Rákleves vagdalékkal (Crayfish Soup with Forcemeat)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty crayfish
- pike head
- toasted breadcrumbs
- good pea broth
- asparagus head
- stewed mushrooms
- pike liver
- pike meat
- crayfish butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Scald thirty crayfish.
2. From fifteen crayfish that have been crushed, remove the small pieces.
3. Flesh the other fifteen crayfish, setting aside their tails and claws.
4. In a mortar, crush two fried pike heads and several slices of toasted breadcrumbs.
5. Pour good pea broth over it and bring to a boil.
6. Strain through a sieve.
7. Prepare the blanched asparagus head, stewed mushrooms, pike liver, and finely torn pike meat, as well as the crayfish tails.
8. Melt a piece of crayfish butter in a pot.
9. Sprinkle a little flour over it.
10. Pour the prepared ingredients into it, add the strained crayfish broth, bring to a boil once more, and serve on toasted breadcrumb pieces.

title: Rákmeridon (Crab Mousseline)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two grated rolls
- two decilitres of cream
- 90 grams of crab butter
- 40-50 crab tails and legs
- two decilitres of young green peas
- butter
- salt
- five egg yolks
- one whole egg
- three egg whites

## Directions

1. Soak the grated rolls in two decilitres of cream
2. Beat 90 grams of crab butter until fluffy
3. Squeeze out the bread crumbs well and add them to the butter
4. Add the crab tails and legs, cut in half only in the middle
5. Cook two decilitres of very young green peas in butter so that they do not lose their color
6. Slowly add this, a little salt, five egg yolks and one whole egg
7. When beaten until fluffy, gently mix in the stiff whites of three eggs
8. Coat the mould with crab butter
9. Line it with whole crab tails, green peas or asparagus tips
10. Fill with the mixture
11. Steam for one hour

title: Rákmeridon (Shrimp Meridon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two grated bread rolls
- two decilitres of sour cream
- nine dekagrams of shrimp butter
- forty to fifty shrimp tails and legs
- two decilitres of young green peas
- butter
- salt
- five egg yolks
- one whole egg
- three egg whites
- shrimp butter for the mold
- whole shrimp tails
- green peas or asparagus heads

## Directions

1. Soak two grated bread rolls in two decilitres of sour cream; then beat nine dekagrams of shrimp butter until frothy, squeeze out the crumbs well and add them, as well as the shelled tails and legs of forty to fifty shrimp, which are cut only in the middle.
2. Braise two decilitres of completely young green peas in butter so that it does not lose its color, add this, a little salt, five egg yolks and one whole egg gradually, and when beaten frothy, gently mix in three egg whites beaten stiff.
3. Grease the mold with shrimp butter, arrange neatly with whole shrimp tails, green peas or asparagus heads, fill the whole mixture in.

title: Rákmorzsóka (Crayfish Crumb Dish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4.5 deciliters fine flour
- 7 deciliters cold milk
- salt
- 10 dekagrams crayfish butter
- 8 whole eggs
- 24 crayfish tails chopped fine
- 12 dekagrams crayfish butter
- sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Whisk flour and cold milk together slowly in a pot
2. Season with salt
3. Add 10 dekagrams melted crayfish butter, 8 whole eggs, and 24 finely chopped crayfish tails and mix well
4. Melt 12 dekagrams crayfish butter in a wide flat pot
5. Pour the batter into it
6. Place in a moderately hot oven watching that it does not burn on the bottom
7. When the batter is golden on top, stir it and braise until it no longer sticks to the roof of the mouth
8. Serve immediately as it will otherwise dry out
9. If desired sweet, sprinkle with sugar when braising and serving

title: Rákvaj (Crayfish Butter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish scraps (shell, legs, body)
- whole small crayfish (with intestines and stomach removed, scissors and tail cut off for other uses)
- cooked crayfish shells
- water
- butter (half the amount of crayfish)

## Directions

1. Crush the crayfish scraps, shell, legs and small whole crayfish (after removing intestines and stomach) finely
2. Cook with a little water
3. Mix with butter equal to half the weight of the crayfish, while hot
4. Strain through cloth and let cool

title: Rákvaj (Crayfish Butter) - Alternative Method
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crushed crayfish
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Mix crushed crayfish with butter and cook in water
2. Strain through cloth
3. Cool, after which the crayfish butter rises to the surface of the water and can be removed

title: Rántott Aszalt Szilva (Fried Dried Plums)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- dried plums
- almond kernels
- flour
- sugar
- wine
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Wash the plums and cook until softened but still whole
2. Remove the pit and replace it with a blanched, pitted almond that has been soaked
3. Prepare a light batter from flour, sugar, and wine
4. Dip the plums in the batter
5. Fry in hot fat until golden
6. Sprinkle with sugar and serve

title: Rántott baraczk (Fried Apricot)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful large apricots
- A few apricots
- A piece of citronage
- Citron peel
- Sugar
- Wine
- Sugar
- Flour
- Fat

## Directions

1. Peel beautiful large apricots and remove their pits
2. Finely chop a few apricots, a piece of citronage, and citron peel together
3. Add a piece of sugar and stew slightly
4. Fill the apricots with this mixture
5. Fasten together with small wooden skewers
6. Dip in thin pastry made from wine, sugar, and flour
7. Quickly fry in fat until beautifully golden
8. Sprinkle with sugar and serve warm to the table

title: Rántott baraczk más módon (Fried Apricot Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve large ripe apricots
- 14 dekagram finely ground sugar
- Two tablespoons cream

## Directions

1. Peel and halve twelve large ripe apricots
2. Place in a porcelain bowl
3. Sprinkle with 14 dekagram finely ground sugar
4. Pour two tablespoons cream over them
5. Let stand for two hours, shaking several times so the liquid permeates well
6. Dip in batter, coat in fine breadcrumbs
7. Fry in hot fat until beautifully golden
8. Cook the remaining juice into a syrup
9. Arrange the fried apricots nicely on a plate and pour the syrup over them
10. Sprinkle with coarsely ground sugar
11. The same method can be used for orange slices: peel the white skin well from the oranges and divide into six slices

title: Rántott burgonya (Fried Potato)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover veal roast or cooked veal
- butter
- flour
- broth
- sour cream
- nutmeg
- mushrooms
- asparagus or artichoke
- medium potatoes
- eggs
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of leftover veal roast or cooked veal into small cubes.
2. Melt a piece of butter the size of half an egg and add half a spoonful of flour.
3. Brown it slightly, then mix with one cup of good broth and a spoonful of sour cream seasoned with nutmeg.
4. Add the chopped meat, stewed mushrooms, and asparagus or artichoke cut into cubes.
5. Braise everything together until the sauce is reduced.
6. Cook medium-sized potatoes until soft, peel them, let them cool, and cut off the top and bottom so they stand on the plate.
7. Hollow them out slightly, beat two or three eggs depending on the number of potatoes, dip the potatoes in the eggs, then in breadcrumbs mixed with an equal amount of flour.
8. Fry in fat and quickly fill with the prepared filling while still hot.

title: Rántott Csibe (Fried Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- flour
- egg thinned with water
- fine breadcrumbs
- hot fat
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the chicken and cut into pieces
2. Leave in salt for a time
3. Coat each piece one after another in flour
4. Dip in egg thinned with water
5. Coat with fine breadcrumbs
6. Fry in hot fat
7. Arrange on a platter
8. Sprinkle with fried parsley
9. Serve warm

title: Rántott gesztenye (Fried Chestnuts)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- About sixty Italian chestnuts
- 7 dekagram butter
- 37.5 dekagram butter
- 14 dekagram ground sugar
- Two deciliter cream
- Six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Roast and clean about sixty Italian chestnuts, removing all black spots with a fine knife
2. Cut them in half
3. Finely crush one half and mix with 7 dekagram butter
4. Press through a sieve
5. In a pot, mix this paste with 37.5 dekagram butter, 14 dekagram ground sugar, and two deciliter cream
6. Place on gentle heat and stir continuously until the paste becomes slightly firm
7. Gradually add six egg yolks while stirring
8. Continue cooking for a few more minutes with constant stirring
9. Place the paste on a buttered board and cool
10. When completely cooled, form balls the size of the chestnut halves removed
11. Flatten the paste, place a chestnut in the center
12. Fold the other half of the paste over it and continue until all are done
13. Dip in beaten egg, coat in fine breadcrumbs
14. Fry in hot fat until beautifully golden
15. Sprinkle with sugar and vanilla, serve warm to the table

title: Rántott Kelkáposzta (Breaded Kale)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- firm kale heads
- salt water
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- flour
- lard
- butter
- sour cream
- bone marrow
- onion
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the kale heads in salted water until tender and drain
2. Allow to cool, then cut each head into four equal parts
3. Beat two eggs well and dip the kale into them
4. Coat half in breadcrumbs and half in flour, then fry in lard until golden brown
5. Place small pieces of butter on a serving dish, arrange the breaded kale on top
6. Add a good portion of sour cream on top and a little butter
7. Place coals on top and let simmer for half an hour
8. Prepare the bone marrow patties as follows: after the marrow is cooked, clean it from its membrane
9. Chop some onion and fresh parsley finely
10. Melt a little butter in a pan, add the marrow, onion, and parsley
11. Season with salt and pepper, mix well and brown lightly
12. Cut two rolls into slices of finger-thickness, shape them round or square
13. Coat with a knife-thickness layer of marrow and cover with another slice, continuing as needed
14. Quickly dip these filled slices in milk, beat two or three eggs and dip the patties into them
15. Sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs and fry until golden brown
16. Serve the bone marrow patties alongside the breaded kale

title: Rántott mandola (Fried Almond)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagram fine flour
- 14 dekagram ground sugar
- 14 dekagram almonds ground with shells
- 9 dekagram butter
- A little ground cinnamon
- Fat

## Directions

1. Work together on a board with a roller: 18 dekagram fine flour, 14 dekagram ground sugar, 14 dekagram almonds ground with shells, 9 dekagram butter, and a little ground cinnamon
2. Roll out to the thickness of a small finger
3. Cut out almonds with the appropriate mold
4. Fry in hot fat until beautifully golden
5. Place on a plate, sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon, serve warm to the table

title: Rántott Meggy (Fried Cherries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe cherries
- flour
- sugar
- wine
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Tie the ripe cherries together in groups of four
2. Cut their stems evenly
3. Prepare a thin batter from flour, sugar, and wine, like breadcrumb dough
4. Dip the cherries well into the batter
5. Place in hot fat and shake the pan so they do not settle too quickly
6. Transfer to a strainer and place absorbent paper underneath to absorb the fat
7. Sprinkle with sugar
8. Arrange on a platter with the stem end up
9. Serve warm

title: Rántott meggy (Fried Cherry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful ripe cherries
- Flour
- Sugar
- Wine
- Fat

## Directions

1. Tie beautiful ripe cherries together in groups of four
2. Cut their stems evenly
3. Prepare a thin batter from flour, sugar, and wine as thin as breadcrumb pastry
4. Dip the cherries well into this
5. Put in hot fat and shake the pan so they don't settle too quickly
6. Place on a strainer and put absorbent paper underneath

title: Rántott menyhal mártással (Breaded Lamprey with Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lampreys
- salt
- flour
- lemon juice
- sour cream
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- pea broth

## Directions

1. Open the lampreys, salt them and lard them so they keep their nice round shape
2. Let them sit briefly in salt and dry them, dust with flour and bread them nicely
3. Squeeze lemon juice over, add sour cream, butter-fried breadcrumbs, a little butter, finely chopped lemon peel and a little pea broth
4. Braise briefly and serve

title: Rántott palacsinta almával töltve (Fried Pancake Filled with Apple)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four decilitres milk
- crispy sour apple
- sugar
- one spoonful preserved apricot
- seven dekagrams softened almond
- seven dekagrams raisin
- egg
- fine breadcrumbs
- fat
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make fine pancakes from four decilitres milk, then peel crispy sour apple, cut it into thin slices, pair them, add enough sugar to make it sweet enough, also add a spoonful of preserved apricot, remove from heat and cool
2. Chop seven dekagrams softened almond and the same amount of raisin together into fine oblong pieces and mix this with the apple, then fill the pancake with it, namely: place a piece of filling on each pancake and spread with a knife, then fold it together, cut in the middle, as well as cut off both ends, dip in beaten egg, sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs and fry nicely golden in fat and serve sprinkled with sugar

title: Rántott ponty (Breaded Fried Carp)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned carp
- salt
- flour
- egg
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned fish into nice pieces
2. Salt the pieces
3. Roll in breadcrumbs mixed with flour
4. Fry quickly in butter

title: Rántott sodralék burgonyával (Fried Veal Rolls with Potatoes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- starchy potatoes
- two decagrams butter
- three whole eggs
- green parsley
- salt
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- fat
- parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cook and peel starchy potatoes, grate them cold on a grater
2. Beat two decagrams of butter until fluffy for four or five potatoes, break in three whole eggs, add the grated potatoes with salt and green parsley, and form an elongated roll
3. Dip into beaten egg and bread crumbs and fry light brown in fat
4. Optionally mix a little parmesan cheese with the bread crumbs and finish with fried green parsley

title: Rántott szarvacsok (Fried Stuffed Rolls)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crumbly rolls
- veal meat or roast leftovers
- green parsley
- red onion
- flour
- meat broth
- sour cream
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- fat

## Directions

1. Grate the crust of crumbly rolls, cut them in half in the middle, and hollow them out
2. Coarsely chop veal meat or roast leftovers, green parsley, and red onion; add half a tablespoon of flour and fry briefly, then add the chopped mixture, pour a tablespoon of broth over it, and cook until there is no liquid remaining
3. Fill the rolls with this stuffing, cover with the hollowed-out top, pour a little sour cream over them, and let soften, turning frequently so they absorb the liquid evenly without falling apart
4. Beat a few eggs, dip the rolls in the beaten egg, coat with bread crumbs, and fry golden brown in fat
5. Garnish with fried green parsley when serving

title: Rántott tekenösbéka (Breaded Turtle)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle
- salt
- water
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- fresh lard
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Cook, clean, and cut the turtle into pieces as described previously
2. Beat several eggs
3. Dip the turtle pieces in the egg
4. Coat with fine bread crumbs
5. Fry in fresh lard until golden
6. Garnish with fried parsley and serve

title: Rántott tojás (Fried Stuffed Eggs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- butter
- green parsley
- anchovies
- sour cream
- breadcrumbs
- flour
- salt
- fat for frying
- lemon juice
- onion

## Directions

1. Peel hard-boiled eggs and cut a small portion from the bottom so they stand upright
2. Cut a lid from the top and hollow out the yolk
3. Crush the yolk finely in a mortar
4. Cream butter and mix with finely chopped green parsley, minced anchovies, crushed yolk, sour cream, breadcrumbs, and salt
5. Fill the eggs with this mixture
6. Dip the filled eggs in beaten egg
7. Coat half with breadcrumbs and half with flour
8. Fry in fat
9. Make a sauce from lemon juice, anchovies, green parsley, and chopped onion mixed into thin roux and thinned with pea broth, adding sour cream
10. Boil everything together until the sauce is thick
11. Place the eggs in a dish and pour the sauce over them

title: Róbert-mártás (Robert Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoon of Liebig meat extract
- finely chopped lemon zest
- several peppercorns
- allspice
- a few bay leaves
- meat broth
- one tablespoon of French mustard
- a little anchovy butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook a teaspoon of Liebig meat extract, finely chopped lemon zest, peppercorns, allspice, and bay leaves well in meat broth
2. Add one tablespoon of French mustard, a little anchovy butter, and lemon juice
3. Bring to a boil again
4. Press through a sieve
5. If very thick, dilute with a little hot meat broth

title: Római puncs (Roman Punch)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 kilogram sugar
- 7 deciliters water
- 2 lemons, rind
- 3 lemons, juice
- 4 deciliters fine arrack
- 1 bottle champagne
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Boil 0.5 kilogram sugar in 7 deciliters water
2. Remove the foam
3. Add the rind of 2 lemons rubbed on sugar
4. When the sugar has cooled, squeeze the juice of 3 lemons into it
5. Strain the whole through a sieve
6. Freeze hard
7. During continuous turning of the container, mix in 4 deciliters fine arrack, 1 bottle champagne, and the stiffly beaten snow of 4 egg whites, incorporating the latter most easily with a whisk
8. Pour this punch into champagne glasses and serve regularly alongside roast

title: Rózsa-eczet (Rose Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh rose petals or violet, carnation, orange blossom, bee balm, jasmine leaves
- good wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Pick fresh rose petals or violet, carnation, orange blossom, bee balm, jasmine or similar flower leaves (depending on which type of vinegar you wish to make)
2. Clean them of any insects that may be on them
3. Place them unwashed in a bottle so that it fills three-quarters of the bottle
4. Pour good wine vinegar into it almost completely full
5. Tie it with perforated paper and leave it in the sun for 10-14 days to infuse
6. Then strain it and place it in a clean, well-stoppered bottle
7. Full violet leaves are worth more than the sucker ones
8. These vinegars are good not only for salads but for all kinds of chopped dishes and sauces

title: Rózsa-kocsonya (Rose Petal Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven dekagrams freshly picked rose petals
- small amount of cochineal
- forty-two dekagrams purified sugar
- four deciliters good cherry water
- juice of three lemons
- five and one-half dekagrams dissolved isinglass
- finely crushed ice

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over freshly picked and separated rose petals with a small amount of cochineal and forty-two dekagrams of purified sugar
2. Cover well and let stand
3. When completely cooled, strain through cloth dampened in warm water into a porcelain cup
4. Mix with four deciliters of good cherry water, juice of three lemons, and five and one-half dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
5. Pour into a mold embedded in finely crushed ice
6. Cover well, place ice on top, and let freeze for two hours
7. Unmold in the usual manner
8. Handle all flower jellies in this manner; for jellies made from carnations, use carnation pepper

title: Rózsa-víz (Rose Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful rose petals
- water
- vitriol spirit

## Directions

1. Spread cloth on an earthenware dish
2. Place good quality rose petals on the cloth
3. Cover with a tortoiseshell lid with edges and good seal
4. Place hot coals on the lid
5. Keep the fire going until the petals are dried
6. The true juice will evaporate from the petals and drip through the cloth into the dish
7. When the petals are dried, remove the cloth, clean it from the dried petals, put the water in a container, seal it well, then continue the procedure with fresh petals
8. Alternatively: Pour water into a dish and add rose petals, then add two to three drops of vitriol spirit, which is sufficient to impart both color and the fragrance of roses to the water

title: Rózsafánk (Rose Doughnut)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two whole eggs
- two egg yolks
- two tablespoons of crushed sugar
- two tablespoons of sour cream
- a little salt
- fine flour

## Directions

1. Put two whole eggs, two egg yolks, two tablespoons of crushed sugar, two tablespoons of sour cream, and a little salt into a pot and mix well
2. On a board, prepare a light dough from fine flour and knead it
3. Roll this out into a thin sheet, but do this carefully so the dough does not tear
4. Fold in half and cut with a rose-shaped cutter or a medium-sized doughnut cutter if rose-shaped is not available
5. Stack five such sheets on top of each other, which then form the doughnut
6. Brush the middle of each sheet with egg white and press together in the middle with your finger
7. Once the sheets are cut, make five cuts into the edge of each so they look like rose petals
8. Cover with a cloth so they do not dry out
9. Fry in fat until a nice golden color, turning several times during frying to achieve an even color
10. Carefully remove and place on a sieve covered with absorbent paper
11. Once all are fried, place one whole preserved sour cherry or currant in the middle of each doughnut
12. Sprinkle with finely crushed sugar and arrange neatly on a plate

title: Rózsafánk (Rose Fritter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons granulated sugar
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- a pinch of salt
- fine flour for light dough

## Directions

1. Place 2 whole eggs, 2 egg yolks, 2 tablespoons sugar, 2 tablespoons sour cream, and a pinch of salt in a bowl and mix well
2. On a board prepare a light dough from fine flour
3. Roll out to a thin sheet very carefully so the dough does not tear
4. Fold in half and cut out with a rose-shaped cutter or a medium-sized fritter cutter if the rose cutter is not available

title: Rózsafánk más módon (Rose Doughnut, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven deciliters of flour
- seven dekagrams of butter
- six tablespoons of crushed sugar
- twelve egg yolks
- three tablespoons of sour cream
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Cut seven dekagrams of butter into seven deciliters of flour
2. Work well with a rolling pin
3. Add six tablespoons of crushed sugar, twelve egg yolks, three tablespoons of sour cream, and a little salt
4. Prepare the dough with a wide knife and work it well
5. Otherwise, handle it as with the previous doughnut

title: Salzburgi haluska (Salzburg Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Four deciliters cream
- Two dekagrams butter
- Flour
- Six egg yolks
- Three egg whites, stiffly beaten
- Salt
- One and one-quarter dekagrams powdered sugar
- Vanilla
- Four additional deciliters cream for cooking

## Directions

1. Put cream and butter in a pot and bring to boil
2. Mix in flour to make a firm, hot dough
3. While still warm, mix in six egg yolks and stiffly beaten egg whites
4. Add salt, powdered sugar and vanilla, mix well
5. Bring four additional deciliters of cream to boil
6. Drop hazelnut-sized dumplings into the boiling cream
7. Remove cooked dumplings and keep warm; repeat until all are cooked
8. Mix six egg yolks with cold cream and blend with the cream the dumplings were cooked in
9. Add sugar and vanilla
10. Place dumplings in a buttered dish, pour sauce over
11. Bake in steam bath for half an hour
12. Unmold and serve

title: Saláta vajas mártásban (Salad in Butter Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- head lettuce, beautiful yellow
- butter
- sour cream
- breadcrumbs
- veal cutlet for garnish

## Directions

1. Take beautiful yellow head lettuce, remove the green leaves
2. Cut each head into four pieces
3. Wash several times until clean
4. Blanch in salted water, but it should not be too soft
5. Drain the water
6. Coat a plate with butter
7. Add four to five spoonfuls of sour cream
8. Add lettuce and continue layering
9. Top with sour cream and several small pieces of butter
10. Sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs
11. Place in the oven and cook through
12. Garnish with veal cutlet

title: Sarlotte (Charlotte)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 12-15 peeled apples
- 4-5 tablespoons apricot syrup
- 10 dekagramm sugar
- coffee cup of water
- butter
- knife-thickness slices of sweet bread

## Directions

1. Cut peeled apples into thin slices
2. Place apricot syrup, sugar, and water in a pot
3. Set over moderate heat and stew the apples frequently shaking, until soft without falling apart and the syrup is well mixed with the apples
4. Line a well-buttered mold with knife-thickness slices of sweet bread
5. Layer one row of apples, then one row of bread slices drizzled with melted butter
6. Continue alternating until the mold is full
7. Cover the top with bread, pour melted butter over it
8. Bake until the top bread is golden and crispy
9. Turn out and sprinkle with sugar

title: Ser-élesztő (Beer Yeast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh, strong, lukewarm beer
- A small amount of yeast
- Flour

## Directions

1. Mix fresh, strong, lukewarm beer with a small amount of yeast and flour
2. Allow the mixture to ferment for 10-12 hours

title: Sertéspecsenye barna lével (Pork Chops with Brown Gravy)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- hot fat
- one head red onion
- one parsley root
- carrot
- celery
- paprika
- black pepper
- pork meat (white chop or leg)
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. In hot fat, cut one head of red onion, one parsley root, carrot, and celery into thin slices
2. Quickly braise until soft
3. When softened, brown with a little paprika and ground black pepper
4. Add the cleaned and salted pork meat
5. Pour a little water underneath
6. Braise quickly until tender
7. If the liquid reduces, pour in hot salted water by the spoonful so the vegetables do not burn, but the gravy must be dark brown
8. When the meat is tender, remove it and slice it, placing on a hot platter
9. Pour hot water over the browned vegetables and bring to a boil
10. Strain the gravy through a sieve, mashing the browned vegetables through it over the sliced meat
11. This produces a very pleasant sauce, the main thing being that it should not burn but be dark brown

title: Sodralék csiperke-gombával (Veal Rolls with Button Mushrooms)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal thigh
- roast leftovers
- bacon
- cooked ham
- green parsley
- onion
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- two egg yolks
- two tablespoons sour cream
- butter
- white wine
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices from veal thigh, soften them well, and set aside
2. Finely chop roast leftovers, a small piece of bacon, cooked ham, green parsley, onion, and mushrooms; add salt, pepper, two egg yolks, and two tablespoons sour cream, and mix well
3. Spread this mixture on the veal slices and roll them up
4. Line a pot with a few bacon slices, place the rolls on top, cover with bacon slices, place embers on the lid, and braise for two hours
5. Strain the fat, cut the rolls lengthwise in half, and arrange on a platter so the filling shows
6. Clean small button mushrooms, melt a piece of butter, add green parsley and mushrooms, braise until the sauce evaporates, dust with a little flour, braise again, then add half a coffee cup of white wine, the same amount of broth, and two full tablespoons of sour cream; cook until only little sauce remains
7. Skim the fat from the mushroom sauce, arrange the mushrooms in a wreath around the edge of the platter, and place the rolls in the center

title: Sodó-fagylalt (Wine Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams sugar rubbed on 2 oranges
- 12 egg yolks
- 4 deciliters good Somlyai wine
- 4 egg whites
- 21 dekagrams powdered sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Finely crush the sugar rubbed on 2 oranges and beat well with 12 egg yolks
2. Stir in 4 deciliters of good Somlyai wine
3. Place over charcoal fire and stir until it begins to thicken
4. Pour into a porcelain dish and cool
5. Place in the freezing container
6. When the ice cream has become a solid mass, bury it in ice for more than an hour
7. Meanwhile, beat 4 egg whites to very stiff peaks so no liquid remains
8. Mix in 21 dekagrams of finely sifted crushed sugar and a little vanilla gently and quickly with the flat side of a cooking spoon without stirring
9. Fill this mixture into a pastry bag and pipe beautiful tall Spanish puffs onto paper
10. Sprinkle finely with sugar using a small tulle bag
11. Bake in a cooled oven without allowing them to brown
12. Leave in the oven until they peel off the paper easily and dry
13. Turn the ice cream onto a platter and arrange the puffs in a spiral shape, using the largest ones for the first coil, then progressively smaller ones, with the smallest at the top so it resembles an artichoke

title: Somot befőzni (Preserved Serviceberries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams sugar
- 2 deciliters water
- 500 grams ripe serviceberries

## Directions

1. Boil 500 grams of sugar with two deciliters of water until it throws heavy drops, removing the scum during boiling.
2. Wash 500 grams of ripe serviceberries, scald with boiling water, and drain well.
3. Place them in the boiling sugar, do not stir much so they do not separate from the seeds.
4. After boiling for a quarter hour, remove them with a slotted spoon, and boil the juice until it is thick.
5. Then remove from the fire, place the serviceberries back in, and handle the same as the previous one when filling.

title: Sonka-pástétom (Ham Pâté)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram of cooked ham
- two decilitres of milk
- two eggs
- sour cream
- butter
- crumbly pastry

## Directions

1. Chop half kilogram of cooked ham finely, removing the fat, prepare a pancake from two decilitres of milk and two eggs, and cut slightly wider than thin slices, place in a bowl, mix the larger part of the chopped ham with a large coffee cup of good sour cream, but so that it remains somewhat liquid.
2. Line a pot greased with butter with crumbly pastry, roll out the pastry to the thickness of a thin knife blade and line the pot smoothly so that it does not wrinkle, sprinkle the bottom of the pastry with the chopped ham and prepare a lid from pastry, brush with egg white and bake.

title: Sorrel Soup (Sóskaleves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter or lard
- flour
- three handfuls sorrel
- salt
- three egg yolks
- butter
- three tablespoons sour cream
- fried bread slices or pea paste fried in fat

## Directions

1. Make a light, thin roux with butter or lard in a pot
2. Clean and finely chop three handfuls of sorrel and place in the roux
3. Braise slightly and thin or thicken with liquid as desired
4. Before serving, beat three egg yolks, a little butter and three tablespoons of sour cream together
5. Pour the hot soup over it
6. Serve on fried bread slices or pea paste fried in fat

title: Sour Cherry Cake with Meringue (Meggy-lepény)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites
- egg yolks
- sour cherries
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat together one whole egg and six egg yolks, stirring for half an hour
2. Fold in stiff meringue made from five egg whites
3. Grease a baking pan with butter and line the bottom with whole sour cherries
4. Fill pan halfway with the mixture and bake in the oven until the batter has set so firmly that the cherries do not sink into it
5. Pull the pan slightly out of the oven without removing it completely, cover the cooked batter entirely with more sour cherries, add the remaining batter on top, and bake completely through

title: Sour Cherry Cake with Yeast Dough (Meggy-lepény élesztős tésztából)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams butter
- 9 egg yolks
- 10 dekagrams soaked almonds
- 3 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 2 decilitres cream
- 1.5 dekagrams fresh yeast
- salt
- flour
- sour cherries

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dekagrams butter until fluffy, add 9 egg yolks one by one, stirring well
2. Mix in 10 dekagrams soaked and finely ground almonds, 3 tablespoons powdered sugar with the butter and eggs well
3. Incorporate 2 decilitres of good cream and 1.5 dekagrams of fresh yeast soaked in cream, add a little salt and enough fine flour to make a light dough that should not be runny but should not be very stiff either, beat with flour until the dough pulls away from the spoon
4. Grease the mold or pan with butter, wash the sour cherries (having removed their stems beforehand) and stew with powdered sugar, strain the juice well
5. Line the entire mold with this juice, place the smaller portion of dough on top and let it rise well
6. When the dough has risen in the mold, place in the oven and bake just enough so that the cherries do not sink; now pull the mold out slightly without removing it from the oven, place the remaining cherries on top, carefully cover with the remaining dough, bake until golden, and when baked, turn out and sprinkle with sugar

title: Spanyol gyümölcs-pép (Spanish Fruit Pap)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- bread slices
- Malaga or Menezes wine
- butter
- ripe apricots
- sugar
- fresh fruit or compote

## Directions

1. Dampen the bread slices with Malaga or Menezes wine
2. Line a buttered pot with bread slices so no gaps remain
3. Make a layer of peeled and sliced ripe apricots, sprinkled with sugar
4. Add another layer of bread slices
5. Add a layer of fresh fruit or compote (but not marmalade)
6. Continue layering until the pot is full
7. Cover with bread slices and bake until nicely browned
8. Carefully turn out onto a plate, sprinkle with sugar, and serve

title: Spanyol Kenyér (Spanish Bread)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- elongated rolls
- preserved apricots
- beaten eggs
- fine breadcrumbs
- fat for frying
- sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut elongated rolls into knife-blade-thin slices.
2. Spread with preserved apricots and place another slice on top.
3. Soak in well-sweetened red wine until saturated.
4. Dip in beaten eggs and roll in fine breadcrumbs.
5. Fry in fat and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Spanyol Kenyér Más Módon (Spanish Bread Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- elongated rolls
- preserved currants
- red wine, sweetened
- flour
- sugar
- cinnamon
- fat for frying
- finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Cut rolls into slices as before.
2. Fill with preserved currants and soak in sweetened red wine.
3. Make a thin batter from red wine, flour, sugar, and cinnamon.
4. Dip the filled roll slices into the batter and fry in fat.
5. Sprinkle immediately with finely ground sugar and brown with a hot spatula.
6. Note: Do not soak these slices as strongly in wine as the previous recipe, or they will fall apart and absorb fat.

title: Spanyol Szélfánk Tejszínhabbal (Spanish Meringue Puffs with Whipped Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 24 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- a little vanilla
- pistachio nuts, soaked and finely chopped
- 4 deciliters cream
- vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Quickly combine with the finely ground sugar and vanilla
3. Fill a pastry bag with the mixture
4. Pipe half-egg-sized meringue puffs onto baking paper
5. Place on a baking sheet
6. Bake in a gentle oven until white and firm
7. Leave in the oven until hardened and easily peeling from the paper
8. Chop soaked pistachio nuts finely
9. Brush the lower edge of each meringue with egg white
10. Densely coat with chopped pistachios
11. Return to the oven to dry
12. Whip the cream to stiff peaks
13. Add vanilla sugar to taste and mix well
14. Carefully hollow out the meringue puffs without piercing through
15. Fill with the whipped cream
16. Stack two together with cream holding them
17. The green pistachio strip goes in the middle of the puff for a neat appearance
18. Arrange on a cloth-lined plate and pile high and neatly

title: Spanyol széltorta (Spanish Wind Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 dekagramm fine sugar
- 2 grams vanilla
- 11 egg whites
- fine broken sugar

## Directions

1. Very finely pound 50 dekagramm of fine sugar with 2 grams of vanilla and sift it.
2. Beat 11 egg whites in a copper bowl to very stiff peaks, whisking the sugar in rapidly and gently.
3. The foam must be so stiff that if the bowl is inverted, it remains motionless, achieved by constantly lifting the whisk high during beating, and whipping faster and harder as it becomes stiffer.
4. Draw four round layers on two sheets of white paper, each slightly smaller in diameter than a pinky finger.
5. Spread the mixture smoothly onto the traced layers to the thickness of a finger using a knife.
6. Using a pastry bag, pipe small pastries around the edges of the layers.
7. For the fourth, smallest, and top layer, pipe four fine ribbons crossing each other, pipe a large tall button in the center, surround it with pea-sized Spanish pastries, and fill the areas not crossed by ribbons with similar nut-sized pastries placed side by side, then pipe similar pastries along the edge.
8. Dust all layers with finely broken sugar through organdine, and bake in a cooled oven; after one to two hours, the paper can be peeled off with a thin knife.
9. Fill the layers with preserved fruit, placing the largest at the bottom, then smaller ones, and the smallest on top decorated with whole preserved fruits.
10. Note that the oven should be neither very hot nor very cold; the best test is if a bare arm can be held in it.
11. This method applies to Spanish wind paste as well.

title: Spanyol széltésztából készült szarvasok fagylalthoz (Stags Made from Spanish Wind Paste for Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Spanish wind paste as made for cake number 705
- 8 dekagrams almonds
- 8 dekagrams sugar
- fine vanilla
- almonds and sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Prepare Spanish wind paste as made for cake number 705
2. Soak 8 dekagrams of almonds, rinse them several times, dry them and chop finely
3. Grind 8 dekagrams of sugar with fine vanilla and mix with the almonds
4. Draw small finger-sized stags on paper
5. Fill the pastry tube with Spanish wind paste in portions and pipe out following the drawn lines so that each subsequent line overlaps the previous one
6. Sprinkle densely with almonds and sugar
7. Place on a sheet pan
8. Bake in a gentle oven; the oven must not be hot so that they remain beautifully white
9. Leave in the oven until they separate easily from the paper
10. Heap high on a serving plate and present at table alongside ice cream
11. Can also be served as a pastry, but make them slightly larger and before serving, coat the side that was on the paper with some marmalade

title: Spanyol- vagy erős barna-mártás (Spanish or Strong Brown Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- all kinds of roots (parsley, carrot, celery)
- red onion, sliced into rings
- meat scraps (best is veal)
- diced fresh bacon
- ground black pepper
- bay leaves
- flour
- Liebig meat extract
- meat broth
- white wine (one good tablespoon)
- rum (one teaspoon)
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Brown all kinds of roots, red onion sliced into rings, meat scraps, and diced fresh bacon with a little pepper and bay leaves
2. Sprinkle with a little flour, as much as the fat from the mixture will absorb
3. Once the flour is browned, pour in meat broth strengthened with Liebig meat extract and cook well
4. Before serving, add one good tablespoon of white wine, one teaspoon of rum, and lemon juice
5. Press through a sieve
6. Bring to a boil once more and serve

title: Spanyol-meggyet eczettel befőzni (Preserved Cherries with Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams sugar
- 3 deciliters water
- cherries

## Directions

1. Boil 500 grams of sugar with three deciliters of water, well clarified.

title: Spanyol-meggyet Eczettel Befőzni (Spanish Cherry Preserve with Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram sugar
- 3 decilitres water
- 1/2 kilogram Spanish cherries
- 1 coffee cup good white vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 kilogram sugar with 3 decilitres water, clean it well and cook until it forms heavy drops.
2. Take 1/2 kilogram Spanish cherries for 1/2 kilogram sugar, trim their stems very finely, add them to the sugar and cook for approximately fifteen minutes, being careful that they remain whole.
3. Remove the cherries with a skimmer and continue cooking the syrup, pour in 1 coffee cup of good white vinegar and cook until the syrup becomes thick.
4. When the cherries have cooled, place them in a glass jar, pour the cold syrup over them and leave it open for twenty-four hours, then seal it like other preserves.

title: Spanyol-Meggyet Szárítani (Spanish Cherries Dried)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Spanish cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the Spanish cherries and place them on a drying board
2. Once they are dried so that no more juice comes out of them, remove the pit
3. Press two or three together and press them with your fingers to at least the width of a krajcár
4. Place them in the cooled oven for a few hours, sprinkle with sugar while warm, and pack into containers

title: Spiced Pear (Golden Pear Preserves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 56 dekagrams pears
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- glass of water
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and trim the stem so that only half remains on the pear
2. Brush each pear with lemon juice so they remain beautifully white
3. Place them in water while you finish peeling all of them
4. Boil the sugar with a glass of water only until the sugar is completely dissolved
5. Place the pears in the boiling syrup and turn them with a spoon so they cook evenly
6. Cook until they can be pierced with a needle, but they should not become very soft
7. Remove from heat and cool completely
8. Arrange them nicely in jars and pour the sugar water over them so the pears are completely covered
9. Tie the jars closed and place them in a water bath, boiling for two minutes
10. These pears are very beautiful for garnishing compotes

title: Spárga-saláta (Asparagus Salad)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Cleaned asparagus
- Yellow leafy salad
- Salt
- Oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Tie the cleaned asparagus in a bundle and cook it
2. Drain the water from it and arrange it on a plate so the asparagus heads point inward, then cool completely
3. Clean a beautiful yellow leafy salad thoroughly
4. Arrange it on the plate in a star shape around the asparagus
5. Salt it, pour oil mixed with good vinegar over it, and serve after a quarter hour

title: Spékelt nyúlszeletek (Larded Rabbit Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit, cleaned
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned rabbit into pieces two fingers thick
2. Lard the pieces
3. Let them sit in a sour brine for several days
4. Braise them
5. Glaze them
6. Serve in peppery or tomato sauce

title: Spékelt nyúlszeletek (Larded Rabbit Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned rabbit
- bacon for larding
- sour pickle brine
- butter for braising
- burnt wine
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- pepper
- red onion
- bay leaf
- parsley
- sour cream for sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned rabbit into slices two fingers thick
2. Lard the pieces with bacon
3. Leave in sour pickle brine for several days
4. Braise the rabbit
5. Glaze it
6. Serve in pepper or tomato sauce
7. Alternatively, prepare a sauce with sour cream when the rabbit is tender

title: Strasbourg Doughnut (Strassburg'i fánk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams of good firm butter
- eight egg yolks
- five dekagrams of cheese yeast soaked and mixed in sour cream
- a coffee cup of sour cream
- seven dekagrams of sugar crushed on lemon zest
- salt

## Directions

1. Place twenty-eight dekagrams of good firm butter, eight egg yolks, five dekagrams of cheese yeast soaked and mixed in sour cream, a coffee cup of sour cream, seven dekagrams of sugar crushed on lemon zest, and salt into a bowl
2. Beat this with a cooking spoon until the mixture is well combined and resembles a paste
3. Note that this dough should not be stirred, only beaten with a spoon until well combined
4. Once done, add enough flour so the dough resembles sweet bread dough
5. The flour should be beaten in and not stirred in
6. Once the dough is finely worked, let it rise slightly
7. Finally, place on a floured board, cut into egg-sized pieces, form into round rolls, place on buttered paper
8. Sprinkle with puffed, coarsely chopped almonds, sugar, and vanilla
9. Bake at a gentle heat

title: Striped Orange Aspic (Csíkos narancs-kocsonya)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 orange shells
- half the orange aspic from recipe 812
- one-third the almond aspic from recipe 810
- cream aspic

## Directions

1. Carefully hollow out 7 oranges and place in clean cold water.
2. Prepare half the orange aspic and one-third the almond aspic from recipes 812 and 810; these two aspics should hold better with isinglass than before.
3. Remove orange shells from water and dry well with clean cloth.
4. Place on a sieve set over broken ice.
5. Into each shell, pour 1 spoonful of cream aspic; once frozen, pour 1.5 spoonfuls orange aspic over it, then 2 spoonfuls almond aspic, continuing this way to the middle.
6. Then continue layering until completely filled, ensuring layers are equal in height and always well frozen.
7. Before serving, divide each orange into 6 parts with a sharp, thin knife and arrange neatly on a plate.

title: Stuffato, vagy Olasz Marhahús (Stuffato, or Italian Beef)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three kilograms of beef chuck
- fourteen decagrams of bacon
- salt
- ground cinnamon
- garlic
- four deciliters of white wine
- seven deciliters of beef broth
- eight medium tomatoes
- sugar
- macaroni

## Directions

1. Trim excessive fat from three kilograms of beef chuck, pound well, and salt
2. Cut fourteen decagrams of bacon into long pieces and dust lightly with salt and cinnamon
3. Finely mince garlic, crush it, and mix with bacon
4. Lard the meat as done for beef à la Modenese
5. Place the meat in a pot and add four deciliters of white wine and seven deciliters of beef broth
6. Braise slowly and continuously for four hours, turning frequently
7. After four hours, add eight medium tomatoes that have been braised and strained, along with a piece of sugar
8. Continue braising slowly for another hour
9. Skim off the fat, arrange the meat on a serving dish, garnish with macaroni, and serve the remaining strained sauce in a bowl on the side

title: Stuffed Kohlrabi (Töltött Kalarábi)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young kohlrabi
- salted water
- roasted or braised meat
- fresh parsley
- red onion
- goose fat
- flour
- veal
- butter sauce
- breaded chicken or goose liver

## Directions

1. Cook the young kohlrabi in salted water until half-softened
2. Cut off a small lid from the top and hollow it out well
3. Finely chop roasted or braised meat, fresh parsley, and a piece of red onion
4. Melt a small spoonful of goose fat, brown a tablespoon of flour in it
5. Add the chopped parsley and red onion, brown them slightly
6. Add the chopped veal meat and mix well
7. Pour a little broth over it and cook well
8. Fill the hollowed-out kohlrabi with this filling
9. Place it in the prepared butter sauce
10. Shake it frequently and let it cook for a quarter hour
11. Arrange beautifully on a plate and serve with breaded chicken or goose liver

title: Stuffed Sour Cabbage (Töltött savanyú káposzta)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kilogram young pork
- a small piece of bacon
- a handful of washed rice
- a little salt
- ground pepper
- 2 whole eggs
- cabbage leaves
- finely chopped cabbage
- thin gravy
- 3-4 tablespoons sour cream
- finely chopped dill
- ham slices

## Directions

1. Place the pork and bacon in a bowl
2. Add a handful of washed rice, a little salt, ground pepper and 2 whole eggs
3. Mix everything well together
4. Fill cabbage leaves with this mixture, roll them up tightly
5. Place in a pot with finely chopped cabbage
6. Put over heat and cook for 1-2 hours
7. Prepare a thin gravy, add the cabbage and cook for at least another hour
8. Add 3-4 tablespoons sour cream and finely chopped dill and braise a little more
9. When serving, surround the cabbage with the filling and add ham slices

title: Sweet Stuffed Cabbage (Édes káposzta töltve)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 soft head of cabbage
- salt water
- 28 dekagrams braised veal, chopped into pieces
- 1/2 goose liver, breaded and fried
- breadcrumb coating
- a little fresh parsley
- 1/2 red onion, grated
- 1/2 bread roll, soaked in milk and squeezed out
- salt
- ground pepper
- 1-2 tablespoons good sour cream
- egg yolk
- butter for lining the mold
- boiled cabbage leaves

## Directions

1. Cook the soft head of cabbage in salted water, cool it and separate into individual leaves
2. Prepare the filling: chop finely together the braised veal cut into pieces, half a goose liver breaded and fried, a little fresh parsley, grated half a red onion, and half a bread roll soaked in milk and squeezed out
3. Place in a bowl and season with salt and ground pepper
4. Add 1-2 tablespoons of good sour cream and egg yolk and mix well
5. Line a butter-greased mold completely with boiled cabbage leaves
6. Spread part of the filling smoothly on top
7. Add cabbage leaves again and continue alternating until the mold is full
8. Cover the top with cabbage leaves and braise in steam for 45 minutes
9. Turn out onto a platter
10. Serve with a sauce prepared as follows: melt a piece of butter the size of half an egg, mix in a spoonful of flour and brown to a dark yellow; dilute with a tablespoon of broth, add 4-5 whole peppercorns, a little nutmeg, and 2 tablespoons of brown broth; cook well, strain through a sieve and cook until thick; pour the thin sauce on the bottom of the platter, place the stuffed cabbage in the center and arrange nice cutlets around it

title: Szalagos Kocsonya (Striped Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four deciliters good cream
- fish glue
- four deciliters currant juice
- juice of half a lemon
- eighteen dekagrams sugar
- two dekagrams dissolved fish glue
- fourteen dekagrams swollen, finely broken almonds
- a few bitter almonds among them
- four deciliters best cream
- sugar to taste
- two dekagrams dissolved fish glue

## Directions

1. Prepare a chocolate aspic from four deciliters of good cream, mixing it with fish glue
2. Pour into a mold and freeze on ice
3. Press currants through a cloth, take four deciliters of currant juice
4. Add the juice of half a lemon to the currant juice
5. Dissolve eighteen dekagrams sugar in the liquid
6. Mix in two dekagrams dissolved fish glue
7. Strain the mixture through a cloth stretched on four chair legs with blotting paper in the middle
8. When the chocolate aspic is completely frozen, pour the currant mixture on top
9. Soak fourteen dekagrams almonds, including a few bitter ones, in four deciliters of best cream
10. Let it stand with the almonds for a few minutes, mixing well
11. Strain through cloth and add enough sugar to make it sweet enough
12. Mix in two dekagrams dissolved fish glue
13. Strain once more and slowly pour over the already frozen currant aspic
14. Freeze the whole mixture
15. When unmolding, pay special attention so the form is not left in water too long, lest the stripes melt and the colors mix together

title: Szalonka sülve (Roasted Woodcock)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcock
- salt
- thin bacon leaves
- butter
- goose fat
- onion
- parsley
- pepper
- bread slices
- fat

## Directions

1. Clean the woodcock and remove the innards, keeping the liver and entrails aside
2. Wash thoroughly and salt
3. Prepare and tie thin bacon leaves on the breast
4. Roast
5. Chop the entrails and liver finely together
6. Melt butter and goose fat, add chopped onion and parsley, heat slightly
7. Add the chopped entrails and liver, season with salt and a pinch of pepper
8. Simmer
9. Cut bread into knife-thickness slices and fry in fat
10. Arrange the woodcocks on a plate, spread the simmered filling on the bread slices, and arrange around the edge of the plate

title: Szalonka-szálmi (Woodcock Forcemeat)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks, cleaned
- bacon slices
- bread fried in fat
- good broth
- woodcock intestines
- green parsley, chopped
- red onion
- butter
- large bread triangles
- fat

## Directions

1. If the woodcocks are well cleaned, remove their intestines and set aside.
2. Wrap the woodcocks in bacon slices and roast them.
3. Cut out the finest pieces of breast; grind the remaining parts together with fried bread in a mortar until it becomes a paste.
4. For two woodcocks, use less than one bread roll.
5. Pass through a fine sieve, put in a pot, fill halfway with good broth, and simmer until it becomes a paste.
6. Meanwhile, chop the woodcock intestines finely and stew them in butter with finely chopped green parsley and a little red onion.
7. Cut large triangles from bread and fry them in fat.
8. Spread the stewed mixture thickly on these bread pieces.
9. Place the pieces of breast cut from the woodcocks on the bottom of a dish, arrange the forcemeat and the spread bread slices neatly around the edge of the dish.

title: Szardella-mártás (Anchovy Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven dekagrams anchovies, skinned
- green parsley, finely chopped
- red onion, finely chopped
- yellow roux
- good stock
- ground pepper
- two tablespoons sour cream
- lemon juice
- lemon zest (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the skinned anchovies and wash them, remove their spines
2. Chop finely together with green parsley and red onion
3. Prepare a yellow roux
4. Add the chopped mixture to it and brown
5. Thin with one to two tablespoons of good stock
6. Add ground pepper and finally two tablespoons of sour cream
7. Season with lemon juice
8. A little lemon zest can also be added

title: Szardellavaj (Anchovy Butter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- finely chopped anchovy
- butter (two to three times the amount of anchovy)
- vinegar (if serving plain)

## Directions

1. Grind finely chopped anchovy with two to three times as much butter
2. Press through a sieve
3. Use for various dishes, especially sauces and fillings
4. If enjoying plain, add a little butter and vinegar during preparation

title: Szardellavaj (Anchovy Butter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- finely chopped anchovies
- two to three times as much butter

## Directions

1. Grind the finely chopped anchovies together with the butter
2. Press through a sieve
3. Used for various dishes, especially sauces and fillings. If to be enjoyed on its own, add a small amount of butter and vinegar during preparation

title: Szarvasczímer sülve (Roasted Venison Haunch)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch
- salt
- brine
- bacon slices
- fat
- paper
- caper sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the venison haunch and remove the skin
2. Beat it well to tenderize
3. Salt generously and place in brine for two to three days
4. Lard with bacon slices
5. Roast and cover with fat-greased paper to prevent the bacon from burning
6. Baste several times with the brine
7. When well roasted, place on a plate and pour caper sauce over it

title: Szegedi halászlé (Szeged-style Fisherman's Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fish (carp, catfish, sturgeon and other river fish)
- fresh bacon
- onion
- salt
- paprika
- water

## Directions

1. For ten to twelve people, procure at least six to seven kilos of fish, which should include carp, catfish and sturgeon, and may also include two or three other types of live river or stream fish
2. Clean the fish alive and cut off their tails below the anus; pour the fish blood into the cauldron, remove the intestines and rinse the fish
3. Cook the fisherman's soup best outdoors or at an open hearth in an iron cauldron hung on a chain
4. For each kilo of fish, chop 2-3 decagrams of fresh bacon into small cubes and for each kilo of fish add one to two medium onions cut into thin slices, which should be lightly fried in the fat
5. At the bottom of the cauldron, layer the fish heads and firmer-fleshed fish so that there is a small layer of onion at the bottom, then a little salt and paprika and a row of fish, thus all the fish and onion are layered and salted and paprikaed in layers
6. When everything is layered, pour very little water over it and do not stir during cooking, but occasionally rock the cauldron by its handles in a circle
7. The fisherman's soup cooks for just half an hour and is best served at the table in the cauldron itself, where the chef who knows where each type of fish is located can best serve each person individually

title: Szegedi harcsás leves (Szeged-style Catfish Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- fat
- flour
- red onion
- paprika
- catfish

## Directions

1. Cook the cabbage for one hour
2. Prepare a thin roux and fry the red onion and paprika in it
3. Dilute the cabbage in this mixture
4. Clean and rinse the catfish, cut it into pieces and add it
5. Cook together for a while, but in such a way that it is not very thick and enough broth remains

title: Szegedi paprikás-hal más módon (Szeged-style Paprika Fish, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- small piece of sterlet
- small catfish
- carp
- red onion
- paprika (Szeged, bright red)
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well with a knife on the outside
2. If the fish has scaled skin, remove the scales; if it has smooth skin, scrape off the slimy part with a knife
3. Cut each fish open at the belly, remove the intestines and cut the whole fish into pieces two fingers thick
4. Place three small heads of red onion cut into thin slices in a pot for three fish
5. No fat is needed for this fish
6. Salt the fish, place it on the onion, sprinkle with one tin spoon of beautiful bright red Szeged paprika and pour on enough water to cover the fish
7. Place on high heat, cover and do not stir until serving, as the fish has weak flesh that boils very quickly and would fall apart if stirred
8. It needs to cook for twenty minutes and then it is done
9. When serving, use a deep plate and quickly pour the entire fish into it at once so the flesh does not fall apart
10. Serve a spoon alongside this paprika fish

title: Szegedi Tejfeles Lepény (Szeged Sour Cream Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams flour
- 28 dekagrams butter
- 3 eggs
- 4 fillérs yeast
- milk
- salt
- 1 liter sour cream
- 12 eggs

## Directions

1. Spread flour on a pastry board and crumble butter into it
2. Add 3 whole eggs, yeast dissolved in milk, salt, and enough milk to make a stiff dough
3. Cover and place in a warm spot to rise
4. Mix 1 liter sour cream with 12 whole eggs, 28 dekagrams flour, and salt
5. Roll out the dough to finger-thickness
6. Place in a greased baking pan
7. Spread the sour cream mixture evenly over it
8. Brush the top with egg mixed with sour cream
9. Press down the edges with your fingers
10. Bake until done, about 45 minutes
11. Remove from oven, cut into square pieces, and serve hot
12. Sugar may be served on the side

title: Szegy Tormával Behúzva (Loin of Veal with Horseradish Filling)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful piece of loin, about four kilograms
- almond-based horseradish sauce
- four egg yolks
- melted butter
- fine breadcrumbs
- green peas
- carrot

## Directions

1. Cook a beautiful piece of loin, about four kilograms, until tender and remove from broth and strain
2. Prepare an almond-based horseradish sauce, made thicker than for beef
3. Mix four egg yolks into the horseradish sauce
4. Coat the loin with this mixture to the thickness of a finger
5. Drizzle with melted butter and sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs
6. Half an hour before serving, place in the oven and bake until golden brown
7. Serve with green peas and carrots on the side

title: Szilva-kompot (Plum Compote)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh plums
- Water
- Sugar
- Lemon peel

## Directions

1. Peel the fresh plums finely, place them in a pot, and pour a little water over them
2. Add a piece of sugar and bring to a boil several times
3. Transfer to a designated cup
4. Add lemon peel to the sauce and cook until it thickens
5. Strain it, pour over the plums, and serve cold at table
6. To give these last two compotes a more elegant appearance, they can be decorated with a row of white and a row of red orange slices

title: Szilva-pép (Plum Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagramm butter
- half kilogramm sponge cake, roughly crumbled
- 7 egg yolks
- 14 dekagramm sugar, rubbed with lemon
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 7 dekagramm butter until foamy.
2. Add half kilogramm of roughly crumbled sponge cake.
3. Beat in 7 egg yolks one after another.
4. Add 14 dekagramm sugar that has been rubbed with lemon zest.
5. Once all is beaten until foamy, fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 5 eggs.
6. Butter a mold and cook in a bain-marie for three-quarters of an hour.

title: Szilvás Lepény (Plum Tart)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fine yeast dough
- ripe blanched plums
- sugar
- chopped almonds
- cinnamon
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Roll out fine yeast dough to small finger width and place on a flat baking sheet
2. Arrange ripe, blanched and halved plums densely side by side
3. Sprinkle with broken sugar, finely chopped almonds, cinnamon and lemon zest
4. Let rise and bake
5. When done, cut into pieces like the previous recipe and arrange on a platter with sugar sprinkled on top

title: Szilvás-rétes (Plum Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- 35 ripe plums
- cinnamon
- lemon peel
- crumbly white bread, grated
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough
2. Cut 35 ripe plums into thin rings
3. Finely grind a little cinnamon
4. Finely chop lemon peel and grate crumbly white bread
5. Stretch the dough finely and brush with melted butter
6. Spread the plums and remaining filling on it

title: Szilvás-rétes (Plum Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- thirty-five ripe beautiful plum stones
- a little cinnamon
- lemon zest
- crumbly kifli
- melted butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough, then cut thirty-five ripe beautiful plum stones into thin rings and crush a little cinnamon, chop lemon zest finely and grate crumbly kifli
2. Stretch the dough finely, brush with melted butter, sprinkle the plums and the other ingredients on it, fold it gently together and, having sprinkled with butter, place in a butter-greased pan
3. When baked, serve to the table sprinkled with sugar

title: Szilvát Eczetbe Befőzni (Plum Preserve in Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients
