- 1.5 liters white vinegar
- 1 kilogram 80 dekagrams sugar
- 6 dekagrams cinnamon
- 2 grams cloves
- 2.5 kilograms very ripe plums

## Directions

1. Cook 1.5 liters white vinegar with 1 kilogram 80 dekagrams sugar, 6 dekagrams cinnamon, and 2 grams cloves (break the last two roughly) into a syrup, strain it, and dry the remaining spices on paper.
2. When the syrup has cooled, place 2.5 kilograms of very ripe beautiful plums, pierced gently with a needle, into a preserve jar, pour the cold syrup over them and leave open for 10 days.
3. On the 11th day, remove the plums and cook the syrup down to thickness again, then finally add the plums and cook very gently with it, then carefully place in a jar, sprinkling the spices dried during the ten days in layers and pouring the syrup over it.
4. Seal the following day and keep in a cool place.

title: Szultán-víz (Sultan Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram purified alcohol
- 3.5 dekagrams Peruvian balsam essence
- 7 dekagrams clove dye
- 7 dekagrams tolu balsam essence
- 3.5 dekagrams jonquille essence
- 5 dekagrams hyacinth essence
- 5 dekagrams reseda essence
- 2 dekagrams ambergris essence
- 1 dekagram musk essence
- 14 dekagrams rose essence
- 14 dekagrams orange essence

## Directions

1. Add all ingredients to 1 kilogram purified alcohol
2. Shake well

title: Szágóleves (Sago Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven to nine dekagrams sago
- five dekagrams butter
- flour
- cold white broth
- two egg yolks
- fresh sour cream

## Directions

1. Warm five dekagrams butter without making it hot
2. Add the sago and braise with frequent stirring, keeping it beautifully white
3. Pour in a small spoonful of fine flour and stir several times
4. Braise a few more minutes
5. Pour cold white broth over it and let it cook until the grains are large and shiny like glass
6. Beat two egg yolks with a good spoonful of fresh sour cream
7. At the moment of serving pour this mixture into the soup

title: Szálmi Nyúl- vagy Őzhúsból (Forcemeat of Rabbit or Venison)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted rabbit or venison
- two rolls, sliced wide
- lard
- bones from the meat
- a few bacon slices
- carrots
- onions
- one tablespoon flour
- one part white wine
- two parts good strong broth
- puff pastry
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. After roasting the rabbit or venison, cut out the most beautiful back pieces knife-thin and set aside
2. Remove remaining meat from bones and fry it
3. Slice two rolls into wide slices and fry in lard with the minced meat
4. Pound together in a large mortar until it resembles paste, then place in a pot
5. Meanwhile, braise the bones with a few bacon slices, carrots, and onions until nicely brown
6. Sprinkle with one tablespoon flour and when brown, cook well with one part white wine and two parts good strong broth
7. Strain the sauce, add one part to the ground meat, bring to a boil, stir well and pass through a sieve
8. Add the reserved back slices and boil together for a few minutes
9. The forcemeat should be pea-thick
10. Prepare puff pastry and cut out a round piece the size of a plate with a warm knife
11. Coat the outer edge with egg yolk and place strips of pastry around it
12. Cut remaining pastry into coin-sized pieces and arrange on the strips so each overlaps the previous one to form a tall crown
13. Coat everything carefully with egg and bake nicely
14. When removed from the oven, fill the crown with the forcemeat, pile the slices on top and serve quickly

title: Száraz borsóleves (Dry Pea Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 liter dried peas, large and beautiful
- 1.5 liters fresh water
- a piece of butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- finely chopped parsley
- meat broth
- salt
- noodle dough with flour

## Directions

1. Wash the peas and cook until completely tender in 1.5 liters fresh water
2. Heat a piece of butter and add flour, roasting until golden
3. Add finely chopped parsley and let it swell slightly
4. Pour the meat broth with the peas over it and cook well
5. Pass through a sieve and return to the pot
6. Salt to taste and serve over noodle dumplings

title: Szárcsa barna mártásban (Coot in Brown Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- coot
- vinegar
- water
- thyme
- lemon peel
- red onion
- salt
- butter
- flour
- pea broth
- wine

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the coot and cut into several pieces
2. Rinse several times
3. Prepare a marinade of half vinegar and half water with thyme, lemon peel, red onion, and salt
4. Bring the marinade to a boil and add the coot pieces, braise until tender
5. Prepare a brown sauce: make a light roux with butter and flour
6. Dilute with half the marinade in which the coot was cooked and half pea broth
7. Bring to a boil, thin with wine
8. Add the coot pieces and braise a bit longer
9. Strain the sauce through a sieve
10. Plate the coot and pour the brown sauce over it

title: Szárcsa más mártásban (Coot in Another Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- coot
- butter
- carrot
- parsley root
- Spanish onion
- lemon peel
- cloves
- bay leaf
- vinegar
- pea broth
- flour
- wine

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the coot and cut into several pieces
2. Rinse well
3. Coat a pot with butter and add carrot, parsley root, Spanish onion, lemon peel, cloves, and bay leaf
4. Place the coot on top and braise
5. Pour vinegar and pea broth over it and bring to a boil
6. Make a yellow roux
7. Add the braised coot with sauce and a little wine to the roux
8. Bring to a boil
9. Plate and pour the sauce over it

title: Szárcsa sülve (Roasted Coot)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- coot
- water
- brine
- salt
- sour cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the coot
2. Soak in water for one hour
3. Place in brine for one day
4. Salt and prepare
5. Roast well, basting frequently with brine during cooking
6. Toward the end, baste with sour cream and butter
7. Place on a plate and pour the sauce over it

title: Száresaleves (Stockfish Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- stockfish
- parsley
- carrot
- leek
- celery
- cabbage
- various vegetables
- salt
- butter
- flour
- potato dumplings

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the stockfish and place it on the fire as if you were making beef broth, treating it in the same manner.
2. Calculate one stockfish as equivalent to half a kilogram of meat and measure the water accordingly.
3. Add parsley, carrot, leek, celery, cabbage, and various other vegetables.
4. Salt and cook as long as beef broth.
5. Strain it.
6. Make a thin roux from butter and flour, mix it with the broth, and cook well.
7. Prepare fried potato dumplings, place them in the tureen, and pour the soup over them.

title: Szárított Mandolatorta (Dried Almond Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagramm soaked and ground almonds
- 28 dekagramm sugar
- zest of a beautiful lemon with its juice
- 3.5 dekagramm coarsely chopped citron
- 1 whole egg and 2 egg yolks
- wafer
- egg white for coating
- crushed sugar for sprinkling
- whole cherries with stems for decoration

## Directions

1. Dry soaked ground almonds with sugar in a copper pan over charcoal fire with constant stirring until they stick together
2. Finely grate lemon zest and add with juice
3. Add chopped citron
4. Add whole egg and two egg yolks and mix well
5. Fill three-quarters of the mixture into a pyramid cake form lined with wafer
6. Form small wreaths from remaining mixture
7. Arrange wreaths on the wafer in a beautiful order
8. Coat with egg white and sprinkle with crushed sugar
9. Bake at gentle heat
10. When done, decorate with frosting
11. Place whole cherries with stems in the wreaths

title: Székely-gulyás (Székely Goulash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pork, cut into small pieces
- red onion, finely chopped in generous amount
- paprika, small amount
- cabbage
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Place the pork cut into small pieces in a pot, add a generous amount of finely chopped red onion and a small amount of paprika, and braise with cabbage until soft. When sufficiently soft, pour sour cream over it so it becomes soupy, cook briefly, and it is done.

title: Szénén, illetőleg rostélyon sült (Charcoal-Grilled Meat)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram young beef brisket or kidney loin with skin and flap
- salt
- paprika

## Directions

1. Take a large pile of hot charcoal from the store-stove and place it on the stove top, using hardwood charcoal only, not coal
2. Place a low wire mesh screen on four legs over the charcoal
3. Cut the meat into pieces the size of a plate and salt them
4. Arrange the meat in a single layer on the wire mesh above the charcoal
5. Keep the burning charcoal lively beneath the mesh, as the meat must cook continuously on high heat or it will become spoiled
6. When one side is nicely browned, flip it with a fork; when both sides are browned, remove it to small white boards
7. Serve each person on such a board so they may cut it themselves as they prefer, consuming it hot
8. Pour paprika and salt to the edge of each small board with a teaspoon for each person
9. This can also be made with lamb brisket, which is much more tender, or with beautiful large beef rib steaks; fatty meat is better

title: Szőlőrétes (Grape Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe grapes
- bread crumbs
- butter
- strudel dough
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Seed ripe grapes
2. Fry bread crumbs in butter
3. Make common strudel dough and roll it out
4. Sprinkle with grape seeds, bread crumbs, sugar, and cinnamon
5. Roll up lightly and bake

title: Sárga baraczk eczetben (Yellow Apricots in Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and halved apricots
- 1 liter vinegar per 2 kilograms fruit
- 80 dekagrams sugar per 2 kilograms fruit

## Directions

1. Take peeled and halved apricots and cook once with vinegar prepared with sugar, calculating 1 liter vinegar and 80 dekagrams sugar per 2 kilograms of fruit.
2. Then treat them as with melon.

title: Sárga Baraczk Eczetsben (Yellow Apricots in Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and halved apricots
- vinegar
- sugar
- 2 kilograms fruit requires 1 liter vinegar and 80 dekagrams sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the peeled and halved apricots once with sugar and vinegar (calculating 2 kilograms fruit to 1 liter vinegar and 80 dekagrams sugar)
2. Then treat them as you would melon

title: Sárgarépa-eczet (Carrot Vinegar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- carrot pulp left over from sugar making
- water
- vinegar mother or vinegar-soaked bread
- 25 liters of liquid
- 7-10 decagrams of tartar
- 4 decagrams of cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Pour water over the carrot pulp left from sugar making so that it covers it completely
2. After 2-3 weeks, press the mixture that has turned to vinegar fermentation and place the juice in a location of 10-15° R temperature
3. To promote sour fermentation, add a small amount of vinegar mother or vinegar-soaked bread, tartar and cream of tartar to 25 liters of liquid
4. Remove the rising foam
5. Once the liquid no longer foams, draw off the vinegar several more times, and if it is not yet strong enough, add a little tartar and place it in open air
6. When the vinegar has the proper sourness, clarify it with egg white, draw it off, and seal it airtight

title: Sódar-cotelettek (Herring Cutlets)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lat butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 6 lat pressed cooked potato
- salt
- a little nutmeg
- 10 lat finely chopped cooked herring
- 4 egg whites, well beaten
- Madeira sauce

## Directions

1. Mix together 6 lat butter, 4 egg yolks, 6 lat pressed cooked potato, salt, a little nutmeg, and 10 lat finely chopped cooked herring
2. Add the well beaten whites of the four eggs and place the whole mixture in a greased smooth mold, and bake in the oven until nicely brown
3. Turn onto a plate, crown the top and serve the small cutlets already braised and made from herring farce
4. Serve Madeira sauce separately in a cup

title: Sódar-Kádkák (Smoked Goose Pancake Dumplings)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pancakes
- cooked lean smoked goose (sódar)
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 deciliters good sour cream
- butter
- smoked goose

## Directions

1. Cut pancakes into haluska (noodle) shapes
2. Chop cooked lean smoked goose into small pieces
3. Mix the chopped goose with the pancakes in a bowl
4. Beat 3 egg yolks with 2 deciliters good sour cream thoroughly
5. Mix this into the goose and pancake mixture
6. Butter special dumplings molds (kádkák) and coat densely with finely chopped smoked goose
7. Fill with the goose-pancake mixture
8. Braise in a water bath

title: Sódar-tekeres (Corned Beef Roll)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- cooked lean corned beef
- fresh sour cream
- butter
- veal roast or chicken breast
- cartifole (artichoke?)
- mushrooms
- crayfish tails
- butter sauce
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Make thin round omelet slices suitable for rolling from six eggs.
2. Chop cooked lean corned beef finely and mix with enough fresh sour cream to make it spreadable.
3. Spread this mixture knife-thick on the egg slices and roll them up.
4. Trim both ends to make them uniform.
5. Line a round or oval mold buttered with the egg rolls standing upright side by side.
6. Prepare a butter sauce with diced veal roast or chicken breast (removing brown parts), boiled cartifole cut into small pieces (reserving a few smaller rosettes), three small cleaned mushrooms, and 15-20 crayfish tails cut into cubes, braised until the sauce reduces completely.
7. Beat two egg yolks into the sauce and set aside.
8. Fill the mold with the forcemeat mixture and steam.
9. When turning out onto the plate, remove the egg slices from the top and arrange the forcemeat attractively with the cartifole rosettes and crayfish tails.
10. These rolls can also be made with forcemeat containing marrow.

title: Sódaros pogácsa (Ham-Filled Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sixty dekagrams of flour
- four dekagrams of good butter
- two egg yolks
- two tablespoons of sour cream
- salt
- white old wine
- seventy-five dekagrams of finely chopped lean ham
- six dekagrams of grated Parmesan cheese
- sweet sour cream

## Directions

1. Work together with a knife sixty dekagrams of flour, four dekagrams of good butter, two egg yolks, two tablespoons of sour cream, a little salt, and as much white old wine as needed for crumbly pastry, then let rest in a cool room or cellar for two to three hours, then roll out to the thickness of a knife blade.
2. For the filling, take seventy-five dekagrams of finely chopped lean ham, six dekagrams of grated Parmesan cheese, and enough sweet sour cream to hold the mixture together.
3. A teaspoon of this filling goes into each pogácsa, which are first cut out with a larger and then, when the filling is already in, with a smaller pogácsa cutter.
4. Brush the tops of the pogácsa with egg and bake until nicely golden-red.

title: Sódaros rétes (Ham Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- a piece of lean ham, finely chopped
- melted butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 deciliters sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare strudel dough, work it well, cover with a warm baking dish and let it rest
2. Meanwhile, finely chop a piece of lean ham
3. Stretch the dough finely and brush with melted butter
4. Mix 2 whole eggs with 2 deciliters sour cream
5. Pour this over the dough and spread evenly on all sides
6. Fold gently together
7. Place in a buttered baking dish
8. Sprinkle the strudel with melted butter
9. Bake until golden brown

title: Sódartekeres (Ham Roll)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dekagram butter
- 5 egg yolks
- salt
- 7 dekagram fine flour
- 3 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- ham, chopped
- good Parmesan cheese, grated
- 2 spoonfuls grated Parmesan cheese
- 2 spoonfuls sour cream
- sour cream
- ham
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Beat 10 dekagram butter until fluffy and gradually add 5 egg yolks one after another.
2. Add a little salt, 7 dekagram fine flour, and 3 egg whites stiffly beaten.
3. Spread the mixture evenly on a buttered pan to the thickness of a knife back and bake over low heat.
4. Chop some ham finely and grate good Parmesan cheese.
5. When the dough is light breadcrumb-yellow, remove the pan and sprinkle with ham and 2 spoonfuls of grated Parmesan cheese, then pour 2 spoonfuls of sour cream over it.
6. Return the pan to the oven for a few moments until it pulls up nicely.
7. Remove and cut the edge of the dough with a knife, then roll it up, not too tightly.
8. Spread with sour cream, sprinkle with ham and a little Parmesan cheese, and return to a not-too-hot oven for a few more moments.
9. Before serving, cut into slices three knife-widths wide with a sharp knife and arrange crosswise on a dish.
10. These rolls can also be used well for garnishing ragout.

title: Sóska-mártás (Sorrel Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one plate of clean, finely chopped, washed sorrel
- rendered goose fat
- flour
- meat stock
- one coffee cup good sour cream
- one coffee spoon Liebig extract

## Directions

1. Place the clean, finely chopped, washed sorrel in melted goose fat and braise
2. Sprinkle flour over it and braise well
3. Add only a very small amount of stock and one coffee cup of good sour cream, as well as one coffee spoon of Liebig extract
4. Cook a bit more

title: Sörleves (Beer Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter white beer
- a little cinnamon
- lemon peel
- sugar to taste
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 deciliter good hot milk foam
- breadcrumbs, fried and cut into long pieces

## Directions

1. Cook the beer with cinnamon, lemon peel, and sugar until well reduced
2. Beat 6 egg yolks in a bowl and slowly pour in 1 deciliter of good hot milk foam, stirring well
3. Serve over fried breadcrumb pieces

title: Süllő majonézzel (Pike-perch with Mayonnaise)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 kg pike-perch
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter
- 4 cooked egg yolks
- crushed ice
- 1 cup fresh olive oil
- small lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon aspic
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the pike-perch, cut into slices two fingers wide
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, braise in melted butter and place in a cold location
3. Before serving, arrange the fish slices in a wreath shape on a plate
4. For the mayonnaise: press 4 cooked egg yolks through a sieve, mix on crushed ice with 1 cup fresh olive oil, a little lemon juice, 1 tablespoon aspic and a little vinegar until smooth
5. Coat the fish piece with this mayonnaise, and decorate the plate rim with either finely chopped aspic or small pastry shapes

title: Süllő zöld petrezselyemmel (Pike-perch with Green Parsley)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike-perch
- salt
- salted water
- various root vegetables
- fine breadcrumbs
- 10 decagrams butter
- green parsley
- small potatoes

## Directions

1. Clean the pike-perch and let it sit in salt for an hour
2. Cook in salted water with various root vegetables, taking care it does not fall apart
3. Place on a long platter, sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs
4. Melt 10 decagrams butter, add finely chopped green parsley and pour over the fish
5. The platter rim can be decorated with small potatoes fried in butter

title: Sült Borjúhús, Kapon- és Pulyka-Mellet (Roasted Veal, Capon, and Turkey Breast Forcemeat)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted veal
- capon breast
- turkey breast
- bread roll
- milk
- three eggs
- green parsley
- seven decagrams butter
- three eggs
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- two tablespoons sour cream
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. Finely chop the roasted veal, capon breast, and turkey breast.
2. Soak bread roll in milk and squeeze dry.
3. Chop three eggs and finely chop green parsley together.
4. Beat seven decagrams butter until fluffy.
5. Add the chopped meat mixture to the beaten butter.
6. Beat in three eggs one at a time.
7. Add salt, pepper, a little bread crumbs, and two tablespoons of sour cream.
8. Mix everything together well.
9. Place the forcemeat mixture on a cloth greased with butter, tie it up.
10. Cook in boiling salted water for half an hour.
11. Remove carefully and place in a soup bowl.
12. Pour brown sauce over it.

title: Sült borjúszegy (Roasted Veal Loin)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal loin
- pork for larding
- flour
- butter
- stock
- burnt wine
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- pepper
- red onion
- bay leaf
- parsley
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove the skin
2. Lard the loin with pork
3. Roll in flour
4. Roast in butter
5. When browned, pour stock and burnt wine over it
6. Add salt, nutmeg flower, pepper, red onion, bay leaf and parsley
7. When the veal loin is tender, make a sauce with sour cream

title: Sült borjúszegy (Roasted Veal Loin)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal loin with skin
- bacon for larding
- flour
- butter
- meat stock
- burnt wine
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- pepper
- red onion
- bay leaf
- parsley
- sour cream for sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the veal loin
2. Lard the loin with bacon
3. Roll in flour
4. Roast in butter
5. When browned, pour stock and burnt wine over it
6. Add salt, nutmeg flower, pepper, red onion, bay leaf, and parsley
7. When the veal loin is tender, prepare a sauce with sour cream

title: Sült Csibe (Roasted Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned chicken
- salt
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Press down and remove the breastbone of the cleaned chicken
2. Salt it
3. Bend the legs in
4. Roast until nicely golden brown, basting frequently with butter
5. If desired, sprinkle with breadcrumbs during roasting
6. Roast a bit longer
7. Serve quickly to the table
8. Note: Chicken is more tender and flavorful if killed one day before use, bled, placed in cold water and left for twenty-four hours

title: Sült csuka mártással (Roasted Pike with Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half kilogram pike
- salt
- four anchovies
- fresh parsley
- half head red onion
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean and salt a pike weighing one and a half kilograms and let it stand for an hour.
2. Chop finely together four anchovies, a little parsley and half a red onion head.
3. Beat a piece of butter and add the chopped mixture, mix well.
4. Coat the inside of the fish with half of this mixture and tie it shut.
5. Roast it nicely.
6. During roasting, pour sour cream and the remaining mixture over it.
7. Once roasted, place on a plate with the sauce underneath.

title: Sült czompó mártással (Roasted Lamprey Loin with Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large lamprey loin
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter
- butter
- 2 deciliters sour cream
- lemon juice and peel
- capers
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Clean the large loin, cut lengthwise but not all the way through so it remains whole
2. Season with salt mixed with ground pepper, coat with melted butter and roast on a gridiron
3. When cooked through, place on a plate, add some butter, pour 2 deciliters sour cream over it
4. Add lemon juice, finely chopped lemon peel, a little capers, a handful of breadcrumbs and some butter, top with coals, bring to a boil and serve

title: Sült czompó mártással (Roasted Leg of Mutton with Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large leg of mutton
- salt
- crushed pepper
- melted butter
- sour cream (two deciliters)
- lemon juice
- lemon rind, finely chopped
- capers, finely chopped
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the large leg of mutton and cut it lengthwise, but not all the way through, so it remains whole
2. Season it with salt mixed with crushed pepper
3. Coat it with melted butter and roast it on a grate until fully cooked
4. When roasted through, place it on a plate, add a few pieces of butter on top
5. Pour two deciliters of sour cream over it and add the juice of one lemon
6. Add finely chopped lemon rind, a little capers, a few handfuls of breadcrumbs and a few pieces of butter
7. Place coals on top, bring it to a boil and serve

title: Sült Fáczán (Roast Pheasant)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one pheasant
- salt
- lard
- butter
- lemon peel
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. After scalding the pheasant, remove the entrails and clean thoroughly.
2. Salt it well and lard it with finely chopped lard.
3. Roast slowly, basting frequently with butter.
4. When done, decorate with lemon peel and serve with lemon juice poured over it.

title: Sült Galamb (Roasted Squab)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young fat pigeons
- butter
- breadcrumb crusts
- milk
- fresh parsley
- salt
- eggs

## Directions

1. Kill young fat pigeons, pluck with feathers, scald and clean
2. Salt and let rest for half an hour
3. For two pigeons, prepare stuffing as follows: beat butter until foamy
4. Grate one and a half breadcrumb crusts and soak in milk, squeeze well
5. Add finely chopped fresh parsley and a little salt
6. Mix well together
7. Beat two eggs and one egg yolk and add
8. Stir for a while longer
9. Fill the pigeons with the stuffing using a funnel, tie and roast for half an hour
10. Baste with butter during roasting

title: Sült nyúl tejfellel (Roasted Rabbit with Sour Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- hot vinegar
- water
- carrots
- parsley root
- onion, sliced
- celery
- bay leaf
- ginger
- cloves
- peppercorns
- lemon zest
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Wash and skin the rabbit thoroughly
2. Lard it nicely
3. Pour hot vinegar and water over it
4. Add sliced carrots, parsley root, sliced onion, celery, bay leaf, ginger, cloves, peppercorns and lemon zest
5. Let the rabbit sit in this marinade for one or two days, turning it several times during this period
6. When ready to cook, thread the rabbit onto a spit, tie its legs up
7. Roast, basting alternately with butter and sour sauce, then with sour cream at intervals
8. When well roasted, place on a platter and garnish with lemon slices
9. Strain the liquid in the pan and serve separately in a small dish

title: Sült Pulyka (Roasted Turkey)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- salt
- bacon
- bread
- milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the turkey
2. Remove the breast and rinse several times
3. Cook well
4. Lard the breast neatly with bacon
5. Soak bread in milk and place in the crop
6. Roast at an even temperature
7. If the turkey is large, almost two hours are needed for roasting
8. Baste with butter during roasting
9. Serve the drippings that fall from it at the table

title: Tarhonya-készítés (Tarhonya Pasta)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven deciliters of flour
- one whole egg
- water

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with one whole egg and water into a very stiff dough, more dry than wet
2. Knead the dough well
3. Press through a coarse-mesh sieve
4. Then press through progressively finer sieves to create tarhonya of various sizes
5. Dry in the sun
6. Use in soups, with meat, with chicken, with pörkölt, etc.

title: Tarhonyás Becsinált (Lamb with Egg Noodles in Sour Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Young lamb meat
- Broth
- Sour cream
- Flour, a small amount
- Vinegar
- Fresh tarragon leaves
- Spices, to taste

## Directions

1. Cook tender lamb meat in plenty of broth until soft
2. When cooked, thicken with plenty of sour cream and a small amount of flour
3. Sour with vinegar and add fresh tarragon leaves
4. Season generously with spices to taste

title: Tatárka-pohánka leves (Buckwheat Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two deciliters buckwheat
- two egg whites
- good meat broth
- two egg whites
- piece of fresh butter
- two full spoons of fresh sour cream

## Directions

1. Mix two egg whites into the buckwheat
2. Dry the mixed buckwheat well in a warm place
3. Once completely dry, rub with a knife until it returns to its previous form
4. Cook in good meat broth and let simmer for a few minutes
5. In a pot, beat two egg whites, a piece of fresh butter, and two full spoons of fresh sour cream together well
6. Pour the broth into this mixture
7. Serve

title: Tejben főtt hab krémmel (Milk-Poached Meringue with Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- ten egg whites
- sugar
- vanilla
- milk
- sixteen egg yolks
- seven deciliters milk
- sugar
- vanilla or grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Beat ten egg whites until stiff peaks form, adding enough sugar and vanilla for sweetness and flavor
2. Using a spoon, cook dumplings of this meringue in milk and heap them high on a platter
3. Once all dumplings are cooked, strain the milk
4. For the cream: beat sixteen egg yolks well with seven deciliters of the lukewarm milk in which the dumplings were cooked
5. Add enough sugar for sweetness and a little vanilla or grated lemon zest
6. Bring to a boil while constantly stirring until it thickens slightly
7. Transfer from one container to another to cool and prevent curdling
8. Once cooled, pour the cream around the dumplings on the platter

title: Tejes csokoládé (Hot Chocolate with Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- négy dekagrammos vaníliás csokoládétáblácska
- csészényi jó tej

## Directions

1. A csokoládét papíron durvára összevágjuk
2. Serpenyőbe teszszük és kevés tejjel a tűzön felolvasztjuk
3. Finoman elhabarjuk
4. A többi tejet is hozzáöntjük
5. Néhány perczig forrni hagyjuk
6. Aztán a csokoládékannába töltjük
7. Felfőzzük
8. Néhány perczig nyugodt állás után a habverővel habossá kavarjuk
9. Csészékben feladjuk

title: Tejfel-pép (Sour Cream Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablespoons flour
- 4 deciliters heavy cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place the flour in a pot and gradually pour in the heavy cream, stirring well to avoid lumps
2. Add a little sugar and bring to a boil with constant stirring
3. When thickened, place a large pot lid inverted as a triangle underneath with a strong charcoal fire below
4. When well heated, butter one side quickly
5. Spoon some of the paste onto it and bake for one minute
6. Scrape with a knife and leave only paper-thick layer on the lid
7. Leave until it browns, then butter again
8. Cut into pieces and peel off with a knife, shaping into small wafers
9. Work quickly to prevent burning

title: Tejfeles burgonya (Sour Cream Potatoes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- several potatoes
- half kilogram meat
- several eggs
- seven decilitres sour cream
- breadcrumbs
- pepper
- salt
- fat

## Directions

1. Cook several potatoes in their skins
2. While they cook, roast half kilogram meat and cook several eggs hard
3. Peel the boiled potatoes and cut into slices, chop the meat very finely
4. Cut the eggs into slices
5. Prepare a buttered and breadcrumb-lined pot
6. Layer in the pot: one layer of potatoes (salted), then a layer of chopped meat and eggs with sour cream; repeat layers of potatoes, meat, and eggs
7. Add a little pepper and plenty of grated breadcrumbs between each layer
8. Place in the oven for quarter of an hour
9. The potatoes can also be prepared this way with only sour cream, without meat and eggs

title: Tejfeles fagylalt (Cream Ice Cream with Wafers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- thin child's porridge
- sugar
- vanilla
- butter
- good cream
- more sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Prepare a thin child's porridge and sweeten well with sugar
2. Add finely crushed vanilla
3. Place a flat sheet on embers and heat it
4. Butter the sheet
5. Spread a small amount of the porridge on it and spread with a knife
6. Leave for a moment until it sticks
7. Scrape with the knife very thin, leaving only paper thickness
8. When it begins to color, quickly cut it and roll as small wafers
9. Fold what can be folded; break the rest into pieces and continue cooking until a good-sized wafer piece is ready
10. Prepare a thin porridge from good cream and sweeten strongly
11. Add a good amount of finely crushed vanilla
12. Cool and place in freezing container
13. Freeze with continuous stirring and working
14. When beginning to freeze, place the wafer pieces in the container and work well into the ice cream
15. When completely hard, keep in ice for one more hour
16. Turn out and decorate with the small wafers

title: Tejfeles Farsangi Fánk (Sour Cream Carnival Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters good sweet sour cream, warmed
- 10 egg yolks
- 13.75 grams fresh baker's yeast dissolved in sour cream
- 1 tablespoon granulated sugar
- 1 liter fine dry flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Add egg yolks, yeast dissolved in sour cream, and sugar to the warmed sour cream and mix well together
2. Place 1 liter fine flour in a bowl, salt it, strain the above mixture into it
3. Beat the dough, place on a flour-dusted board
4. Handle it separately in the same manner as recipe 555

title: Tejfeles haluska rákfarkkal (Sour Cream Noodles with Crayfish Tails)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagrams butter
- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons sour cream
- salt
- flour
- 7 deciliters milk
- crayfish tails
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cream 7 dekagrams butter
2. Add 4 eggs one after another
3. Add 4 tablespoons sour cream, salt, and enough flour to make it properly firm
4. Bring 7 deciliters milk to a boil
5. Drop noodles into milk with a spoon and cook
6. Remove and place on a buttered plate
7. Place crayfish tails between them and pour sour cream over
8. Decorate the top with nicely fried crayfish tails

title: Tejfeles Leves (Sour Cream Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Melted butter, a good cooking spoon's worth
- Flour, a good cooking spoon's worth
- Red onion, finely chopped
- Seven deciliters of sour cream
- Seven deciliters of pea broth
- Salt
- Nutmeg, finely chopped
- Clove, finely chopped
- Toasted bread points

## Directions

1. In melted butter, lightly fry a good cooking spoon's worth of flour until yellowish
2. Add finely chopped red onion
3. Mix in seven deciliters of sour cream and seven deciliters of pea broth together and blend well
4. Boil for a short time, then season with salt, finely chopped nutmeg, and clove
5. Serve into toasted bread points, as with the previous soup

title: Tejfeles tolka - Dalkerln (Sour Cream Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliter fresh sour cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons flour
- sugar
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Pour three tablespoons of fresh sour cream into a pot
2. Gradually stir in six egg yolks and six tablespoons flour
3. Add sugar to taste
4. Pour in the remaining sour cream
5. Stir well and fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
6. Bake in the appropriate mold until done
7. Sprinkle with fine sugar and serve

title: Tejfeles-rétes (Sour Cream Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- strudel dough
- 10 decagrams butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 3 deciliters sour cream
- 4 egg whites
- 14 decagrams cleaned raisins
- 7 decagrams chopped almonds or fine breadcrumbs
- 7 decagrams broken sugar

## Directions

1. Stretch strudel dough finely and let it dry for 15 minutes
2. Beat 10 decagrams butter with 4 egg yolks until fluffy
3. Stir in 3 deciliters sour cream
4. Fold in the stiff peaks of 4 egg whites
5. Brush the stretched dough with this mixture
6. Sprinkle with 14 decagrams raisins, 7 decagrams chopped almonds or breadcrumbs, and 7 decagrams broken sugar, leaving about a hand's breadth empty at one edge
7. Fold gently
8. Place in a buttered baking dish
9. Bake slowly

title: Tejleves (Milk Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter good milk
- handful of swollen finely ground almonds
- a little vanilla
- sugar
- three egg yolks
- toasted breadcrumb slices

## Directions

1. Cook one liter of good milk with a handful of swollen finely ground almonds and a little vanilla.
2. Sweeten to taste.
3. Before serving, beat three egg yolks well and pour the hot milk over them.
4. While pouring the milk onto the eggs, stir vigorously so that the whole does not curdle.
5. Serve over toasted breadcrumb slices.

title: Tejszínfagylalt narancs vagy citrom illattal (Cream Frozen Dessert with Orange or Lemon Flavor)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 orange or 1 large lemon, peel and pith
- 1.4 liters strong boiling sweet cream
- eggs and sugar as in previous recipes

## Directions

1. Peel 1 orange or 1 large lemon and carefully remove all the white inner pith from the colored peel so that no white pith remains.
2. Place the orange or lemon peel in 1.4 liters of strong boiling sweet cream, cover the pot, and allow to cool.
3. Follow the same preparation method as the previous cream frozen desserts.

title: Tekenösbéka czitrom-mártásban (Turtle in Lemon Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle
- butter
- flour
- pea broth
- red onion
- lemon peel
- lemon juice
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook the turtle and cut into four pieces
2. Melt butter and brown a spoonful of flour in it
3. Dilute with pea broth
4. Add a head of red onion (which is removed before serving)
5. Reduce the sauce
6. Strain the sauce
7. Season with lemon peel and juice
8. Add a little sour cream
9. Add the turtle pieces and bring to a boil for several minutes
10. Plate neatly

title: Tekenösbéka tulajdon héjában (Turtle in Its Own Shell)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle
- salt
- water
- butter
- bread crumbs
- lemon peel
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook the turtle in salted water until very soft
2. Open the shell and remove the meat into four pieces
3. Set aside the liquid found in the shell
4. Carefully separate the liver from the gallbladder without breaking it
5. Place several pieces of butter in the shell
6. Add the liquid found in it
7. Add the meat cleaned of its skin
8. Add a little bread crumbs, several pieces of butter, chopped lemon peel, and lemon juice
9. Place in a pot
10. Put hot coals on top
11. Bring to a vigorous boil and the dish is ready

title: Tekenösbéka vérmártásban (Turtle in Blood Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle
- salt
- water
- butter
- flour
- red onion
- cloves
- fish broth or cooking water
- lemon peel
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. After bleeding the turtle, place in hot salted water and set aside the blood in a cup
2. Cook the turtle until its shell can be opened (test several times)
3. Remove the meat from both shells, peel off the outer skin, and cut into desired pieces
4. Make a light brown sauce with butter and flour
5. Add a head of red onion studded with cloves
6. Dilute with fish broth or the water in which the turtle was cooked
7. Add lemon peel and half a lemon's worth of juice
8. Add the turtle pieces and cook a bit longer

title: Tekenősbéka-pástétom (Turtle Pâté)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter pastry
- egg white
- turtle
- salted water
- butter sauce
- boiled asparagus
- shrimp tails
- button mushrooms

## Directions

1. Prepare a butter pastry and roll it out to the width of a knife blade, make a bowl pâté from it, brush with egg white and bake nicely.
2. Clean the turtle as already described, cook in salted water, prepare a light butter sauce, add boiled asparagus, shelled shrimp tails and button mushrooms, as well as the turtle, and braise together.
3. When the pâté is baked, place on a serving dish, carefully cut off the lid and fill in the mixture nicely, put the lid back on, and bring quickly to the table.

title: Tekercsek mandolatésztával (Rolls with Almond Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- ten dekagrams of puffed almonds
- ten dekagrams of sugar
- lemon or orange zest
- one and three-quarter dekagrams of fresh butter
- four egg yolks
- egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare butter dough
2. First, grind ten dekagrams of puffed almonds into fine dough
3. Crush the same amount of sugar rubbed on lemon or orange, crush it, and grind again with one and three-quarter dekagrams of fresh butter, four egg yolks, and the almonds until it becomes a paste
4. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a knife blade
5. Cut finger-length rectangular pieces with a warm knife
6. Brush the edge with egg white
7. Then spread almond paste on it with a knife, but not quite to the edge
8. Fold together, press the two ends slightly with the knife back and fry in fat
9. The fat should not be very hot so the rolls fry slowly to a nice golden-brown color
10. Sprinkle with vanilla sugar and neatly arrange on a plate covered with a cloth

title: Tepertős Pogácsa (Crackling Scones)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram fresh, finely chopped cracklings
- one kilogram flour
- two deciliters sour cream
- a few tablespoons milk
- egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Rub the finely chopped cracklings together with the flour well between your hands
2. Knead together with sour cream and a few tablespoons of milk so that the dough is rather firm than soft
3. Roll out the dough to one and a half finger thickness
4. Cut out with a scone cutter and place on a baking sheet
5. Brush with egg and bake until slightly browned

title: Tepertős pogácsa (Cracklings Biscuits)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram good fresh finely chopped cracklings
- one kilogram flour
- salt

## Directions

1. To half kilogram of good fresh finely chopped cracklings, take one kilogram of flour and rub it well between two hands

title: Terrapin Soup (Tekénősbékaleves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four terrapins
- butter
- green finely chopped parsley
- flour
- pea broth
- black pepper
- nutmeg flower
- sour cream
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Cut off the heads, feet and tails of three or four terrapins
2. Scald, remove the meat, cut finely and cook in salted water
3. Place butter and finely chopped green parsley in a pot
4. Braise the terrapin well in this
5. Sprinkle with a little flour and pour as much pea broth as needed over it
6. Add a little black pepper, nutmeg flower and two tablespoons of sour cream
7. Cook slightly more and serve on fried bread

title: Thea (Tea)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fél csésze legjobb fajta thea
- forró víz

## Directions

1. Tíz csésze tartalmú theakannába fél csésze legjobb fajta theát adunk
2. Azt pohárnyi forró vízzel leöntjük
3. Két perc múlva a felöntést jó forró vízzel ismételjük
4. A kannát teletöltjük
5. Pár perczig nyugodtan állni hagyjuk
6. Aztán feladjuk a theát
7. Később még kanálnyi theát adunk hozzá
8. A kannát újólag megtöltjük forró vízzel
9. Négy perc múlva a legizletesebb és legzamatosabb thea ismét kész
10. A felöntést még egyszer ismételhetjük

title: Thea-parfait (Tea Cream Frozen Dessert)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.4 liters boiling sweet cream
- 3.5 dekagrams fragrant tea
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 16 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Place 3.5 dekagrams fragrant tea in 1.4 liters boiling sweet cream, cover well, and cool.
2. Then proceed as above with 42 dekagrams sugar and 16 egg yolks.

title: Theakészítés más módon (Tea Preparation - Alternative Method)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pecco thea vagy souchong thea
- forró víz

## Directions

1. A világos úgynevezett pecco theából több, a sötét (souchong) theából kevesebb veendő egy-egy csészére
2. A gyengébb theából nem tetés kávés kanálnyi, az erősebből fél kanállal egy-egy csészére
3. Ha 4–10–15 csésze thea készül egyszerre, akkor nem kell 4–10–15 külön adagra való theát venni, hanem elégséges 3–7–11 külön adagra való mennyiség
4. A víz teljesen tiszta, zsiradékmentes edényben forralandó
5. Mihelyt buzogva forr, a thea-kannában levő theára gyorsan reá öntendő
6. Befedendő, és azonnal servirozandó
7. A theának a szerint, a milyen ereje van, 1½–2–3–4, sőt a zöld theáknál 5 perczig kell a leforrázás után állani
8. Ha három csészénél több töltendő meg, akkor a sorba rakott csészék előbb balról jobbfelé töltessenek meg félig
9. Aztán folytatólagosan jobbról-balra lesz a theás csésze teletöltve

title: Tiroli kenyér (Tyrolean Bread)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams softened butter
- 28 dekagrams blanched and ground almonds
- 28 dekagrams imperial flour
- 14 dekagrams sifted sugar
- 1 dekagram anise
- juice and finely chopped zest of 1/2 orange
- 5 egg yolks
- a little genuine Tokay wine

## Directions

1. Work 28 dekagrams of softened butter, the same amount of blanched and ground almonds, the same amount of imperial flour, 14 dekagrams of sifted sugar, 1 dekagram of anise, the juice and finely chopped zest of 1/2 orange, 5 egg yolks, and a little genuine Tokay wine into a smooth dough in a mixing bowl
2. Knead lightly by hand on a board for a while longer
3. Roll the dough into thin layers
4. Fold in half three times, then roll out again to knife-blade thickness
5. Cut into equal boat shapes
6. Place on butter-coated sheet metal and bake at gentle heat until light golden
7. When cooled, brush with fine apricot marmalade and place another on top so that the two lower parts of the boats are joined by the apricot marmalade

title: Tiroli rétes (Tyrolean Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- buttered dough
- 28 decagrams plumped almonds
- 28 decagrams sugar
- peel of 1 orange and 1 lemon, finely chopped
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 10 decagrams finely chopped candied lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare buttered dough and stretch it long, like dough for noodles
2. Finely grind 28 decagrams plumped almonds and 28 decagrams sugar
3. Finely chop the peel of 1 orange and 1 lemon
4. Place everything in a bowl, add 2 whole eggs and 4 egg yolks
5. Beat for half an hour until well fluffy
6. Pour onto the buttered dough and spread with a knife
7. Sprinkle with 10 decagrams finely chopped candied lemon
8. Fold gently together
9. Place on a buttered parchment paper
10. Brush with egg white
11. Bake in strong heat

title: Tisztított czukortorta (Refined Sugar Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams sugar
- 2 deciliters water
- 28 dekagrams blanched and finely chopped almonds
- 7 dekagrams citronade
- 7 dekagrams quince marmalade
- 4 preserved walnuts
- zest of two lemons
- wafers
- powdered sugar
- preserved fruit for decoration

## Directions

1. Cook sugar and water together in a copper pan until clarified
2. When the sugar has cooked enough to begin to thread, add the blanched and finely chopped almonds, citronade, quince marmalade, preserved walnuts, and lemon zest
3. Stir until it begins to harden
4. Place the whisked mixture on a wafer-lined cake base
5. Cool, sprinkle with powdered sugar, and decorate with preserved fruit

title: Tobacco Hair Pomade (against hair loss)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 grams tobacco or powdered tobacco
- hot water
- 10 dekagrams clarified beef marrow

## Directions

1. Place tobacco in a container
2. Pour hot water over it
3. After ten hours, strain and press the infusion
4. Let the liquid settle
5. Evaporate until 45 centigrams remain
6. Mix with 10 dekagrams clarified beef marrow
7. Scent as desired

title: Tojás burgonyával (Eggs with Potatoes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- butter
- sour cream
- eggs
- salt
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Slice the cooked potatoes
2. Add pieces of butter and several tablespoons of sour cream
3. In a baking dish, alternate layers of potatoes and eggs
4. Season each layer with salt, butter, and sour cream
5. Continue layering until the dish is full, ending with a potato layer
6. Cover everything with sour cream
7. Add pieces of butter on top and sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs
8. Place hot coals underneath and on top, and bake until golden brown

title: Tojás rákleves (Egg Crayfish Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 eggshells
- 1/4 kilo stewed rice
- 1/4 kilo veal lung stewed and finely chopped
- 1/8 kilo salted bacon cut into tiny cubes
- ground black pepper and clove pepper abundantly
- one head onion finely chopped
- green parsley
- one whole egg raw
- little good saffron
- nutmeg flower
- thin red roux
- sweet sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean the eggshells nicely
2. Prepare the filling: for 20 eggshells mix 1/4 kilo stewed rice, 1/4 kilo veal lung stewed and finely chopped, 1/8 kilo salted bacon cut into tiny cubes, ground black and clove pepper abundantly, one head onion finely chopped, green parsley, one whole raw egg, little saffron and nutmeg flower
3. Mix these together well
4. Fill the eggshells very firmly with the filling
5. Place in a pan with the openings facing upward
6. Pour hot water over them and cook until soft
7. Make a thin red roux
8. Pour abundant sweet sour cream over the soup
9. The fine egg crayfish soup is ready

title: Tojás Vagdalékkal (Poached Eggs with Vegetable and Fish Ragout)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of pike cooked in salted water
- 18 asparagus spears
- mushrooms, finely chopped
- green parsley
- crayfish tails
- salt
- melted butter
- sour cream
- 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook pike in salted water, remove bones, and chop finely
2. Cook 18 asparagus spears in salted water
3. Stew finely chopped mushrooms with green parsley
4. Cook and peel a few crayfish tails
5. Chop everything into small cubes and sauté in melted butter
6. Add salt, a few tablespoons sour cream, and stew well
7. Carefully pour 8 eggs into boiling salted water so they stay whole
8. Cook only until they begin to set, but do not let them harden
9. Serve the ragout piled high in the middle of the plate
10. Arrange the eggs around it in a wreath shape, lifting them out with a slotted spoon

title: Tojás Ülepedve (Poached Eggs with Anchovies and Cucumber)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 6 tablespoons sour cream
- 12 eggs
- 6 well-soaked sardelles (anchovies), cleaned and deboned
- sour cucumber, cut into long slices

## Directions

1. Butter a dish and add 6 tablespoons sour cream to it
2. Break 12 eggs into the dish, taking care to keep them whole
3. Clean the sardelles (anchovies) and remove their bones
4. Cut them into oblong pieces and place them among the eggs
5. Cut sour cucumber into long slices and arrange them around the eggs so they form a green wreath
6. Place enough coals on top so that the eggs cook through but do not harden

title: Tojás-lepény (Egg Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 hard-boiled eggs
- 10 dekagrams butter, beaten to foam
- bread soaked in milk
- green parsley, finely chopped
- 2 whole eggs and 2 yolks
- 2 deciliters sour cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop the 10 hard-boiled eggs finely
2. Mix into the foamed butter: bread soaked in milk, finely chopped green parsley
3. Beat in 2 whole eggs and 2 yolks
4. Add 2 deciliters sour cream with a little salt
5. Place the mixture in a buttered pan
6. Bake nicely
7. When done, turn out onto a plate and serve at table

title: Tojás-sodralék (Egg Roll with Vegetables and Fish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 eggs
- salt
- butter
- mushrooms, finely chopped
- green parsley
- piece of fried pike, deboned
- 15-20 asparagus spears
- 20 crayfish tails and claws
- 3 eggs for the mixture
- sour cream
- crayfish butter

## Directions

1. Beat 10 eggs in a pan and salt them
2. Melt butter in a pancake pan and add a small portion of the beaten eggs
3. Let it spread in the pan and cook like a pancake; repeat until all egg is used
4. Prepare the filling: stew finely chopped mushrooms with butter and green parsley
5. Chop the fried, deboned pike into small pieces
6. Cook asparagus in salted water and cut into oblong pieces
7. Chop 20 crayfish tails and claws into cubes
8. Make a mixture from 3 eggs and beat it finely
9. Add all of this to the stewed mushrooms
10. Add a few tablespoons sour cream and stew slightly
11. When the egg pancakes are cooked, fill them with the mixture and fold them together
12. Cut off the edges and cut in the middle and divide
13. Arrange the egg rolls neatly on a dish greased with crayfish butter, with 2-3 tablespoons sour cream poured in
14. Salt slightly
15. Pour another tablespoon of sour cream on top
16. Decorate with crayfish tails
17. Place in the oven, let it heat through for a few minutes, and serve quickly

title: Tojáspalacsinta (Egg Pancake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pancake batter
- four eggs
- fifteen crayfish tails with claws
- half a roll soaked in milk
- butter
- green parsley
- mushrooms
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare pancakes and set aside
2. Make a batter from four eggs
3. Shell and clean fifteen crayfish tails and claws
4. Coarsely chop the crayfish, the bread soaked in milk, and mushrooms that have been braised in butter with green parsley
5. Cream butter with several tablespoons of sour cream
6. Mix in the chopped ingredients
7. Spread the filling on the pancakes and place on a plate
8. Add sour cream and butter on top, place hot coals above, and brown lightly

title: Tojáspohárka (Egg Cups)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve hard-boiled eggs
- bread soaked in milk
- crayfish tails
- crayfish butter
- three whole eggs
- salt
- sour cream
- mushrooms
- green parsley
- prepared fish
- nutmeg
- sour cream for topping
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Hard-boil twelve eggs and peel them
2. Cut off the top and bottom to make lids
3. Hollow out the yolks without breaking the whites
4. Chop the yolks and cut lids finely with bread soaked in milk
5. Crush several crayfish tails, crayfish butter, and three whole eggs with salt in a mortar
6. Mix with six tablespoons of sour cream
7. Fill the egg whites with this mixture
8. For the sauce: dice mushrooms and cut cooked good quality fish into small cubes
9. Combine with green parsley, crayfish tails, and a little nutmeg
10. Braise lightly in crayfish butter
11. Place this mixture on a buttered plate
12. Stand the filled eggs on top
13. Pour two deciliters of sour cream over them
14. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs and crayfish butter
15. Bake until golden brown

title: Tojásszék-pép (Egg Chair Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- 14 dekagrams softened almonds
- 7 dekagrams butter beaten to a foam
- 1 whole egg
- 4 egg yolks
- powdered sugar to taste
- vanilla to taste
- 3 egg whites beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Pound the hard-boiled eggs and softened almonds separately until fine
2. Mix the pounded mixture into the foamy butter
3. Add the whole egg and four egg yolks and mix together
4. Add powdered sugar and vanilla to taste
5. Gently fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
6. Place in a buttered dish, form a ring around the edge, and bake slowly

title: Tojásszék-pép (Egg Yolk Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- 14 dekagramms softened almonds

## Directions

1. Crush the hard-boiled eggs finely and separately crush the softened almonds
2. Combine the crushed ingredients with 7 dekagramms butter

title: Tok czitrommártásban (Squash in Lemon Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- squash
- salt
- butter
- flour
- red onion, divided into four parts
- pea water
- lemon rind
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. After cleaning and salting the squash, let it sit in the salt for half an hour
2. In a pot, melt a piece of butter and fry two full spoons of flour in it
3. Add the red onion divided into four parts
4. Dilute with pea water and add the squash, cooking until completely done
5. Add a little lemon rind and just before serving, squeeze the juice of one lemon over it

title: Tok frikasszirozva (Squash Fricassee)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- red onion head, divided into four parts
- lemon rind
- pea water
- lemon juice
- cleaned squash
- eggs

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of butter and fry a full spoon of flour in it until golden
2. Add the red onion head divided into four parts and lemon rind
3. Fill with pea water and cook until done
4. Add the juice of one lemon and cook the cleaned squash in this sauce in such a way that it does not fall apart
5. Before serving, beat two egg yolks in a pot, strain the sauce over them, place on coals, stir constantly until it begins to thicken
6. Pour it over the plated squash and serve immediately

title: Tok héringgel (Pike with Herring)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a nice piece of pike
- salt
- white vinegar
- water
- red onion
- parsley
- carrot
- bay leaf
- herring
- breadcrumbs
- cream

## Directions

1. Leave a nice piece of pike in salt for one hour
2. Cook in equal parts white vinegar and water with red onion, parsley, carrot, and bay leaf
3. When cooked, cut into thin slices
4. Place alternating layers of pike and herring in a pan (having removed herring skin and bones and soaked well, then cut into long pieces)
5. Pour a little breadcrumbs and one spoon of good cream over them
6. Continue alternating layers of pike and herring, pouring with lemon juice and cream until the pan is full
7. Cover the pan and steam for one hour
8. Carefully turn out onto a plate so it does not fall apart
9. Border the plate with puff pastry ribbons rolled to knife-edge thickness and pricked with a medium-sized doughnut cutter
10. Brush edges with egg white and overlap the leaves so one covers half of the other
11. Arrange the entire border around the food in the center of the plate

title: Tokok kenyértorta-tésztából (Cases from Bread Cake Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- bread cake dough (as per recipe 680)
- lemon frosting
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Pour bread cake dough (as per recipe 680) into small paper cases
2. Bake at gentle heat
3. When baked, coat with lemon frosting and beautifully decorate with finely cut pistachios
4. Place again in the oven that has cooled to the appropriate temperature so the frosting just dries
5. Serve in the cases to the table

title: Tolófánk (Choux Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters hot milk
- 4 deciliters fine flour
- a piece of butter
- 10 egg yolks
- salt
- 1 spoonful of powdered sugar
- 6 egg whites, whipped stiff

## Directions

1. Cook the hot milk with the fine flour together
2. Let it become a thick, smooth paste with continuous stirring, adding a piece of butter
3. When cooled, stir in the egg yolks one at a time
4. Add salt, 1 spoonful of powdered sugar
5. Finally fold in the whipped egg whites
6. Handle as described in the preceding recipe

title: Tormamártás hidegen (Cold Horseradish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- finely grated horseradish
- hot meat broth
- sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Place the finely grated horseradish on a clean plate and put in the oven for a short time so the excessive strength of the horseradish evaporates
2. Pour hot meat broth over it
3. Add a spoonful of sugar and a few drops of vinegar and bring to a boil
4. Cool before serving

title: Tortácskák (Almond Tartlets)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams butter, well mixed
- 2 whole eggs
- 14 dekagrams blanched and ground almonds
- 14 dekagrams ground sugar
- zest of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- 14 dekagrams fine flour

## Directions

1. Beat 2 whole eggs into 14 dekagrams of well-mixed butter and blend well
2. Add 14 dekagrams of blanched and ground almonds, the same amount of ground sugar, and the finely chopped zest of 1 lemon, and mix everything well
3. Finally add 14 dekagrams of fine flour and stir until well combined
4. Pour the mixture into buttered tartlet molds
5. Bake in a gently heated oven until golden
6. Turn out of molds. Can be frosted with icing or made into a cake

title: Tortácskák (Torte Cakes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams well-mixed butter
- 2 whole eggs
- 14 dekagrams soaked almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and eggs together well
2. Add the soaked almonds

title: Tortácskák fehér linezi tésztából (Tartlets from White Linzer Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams flour
- 28 dekagrams butter
- 14 dekagrams blanched almonds, soaked
- 14 dekagrams ground sugar
- zest of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- 4 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- jam for filling

## Directions

1. Work 28 dekagrams of butter into thin pieces into 28 dekagrams of flour on a board, working well with a rolling pin
2. Add 14 dekagrams of blanched, finely ground almonds, the same amount of finely ground sugar, the finely chopped zest of 1 lemon, and 4 hard-boiled egg yolks
3. Bind the dough together with 1 whole egg using the rolling pin
4. Roll out to knife-blade thickness
5. Fill one half with cooked currants
6. Cut star shapes from the center of the other half with a small cutter
7. Brush the edges of the filled dough with egg white
8. Place the cut-out piece on top
9. Brush with beaten egg and bake until golden

title: Tortácskák masánszki almával (Tartlets with Masansky Apples)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crumb dough (morzsatészta)
- dried peas for blind baking
- fine sugar
- small masansky apples
- water
- sugar
- lemon juice
- cochineal or carmine for coloring
- gelatin for setting

## Directions

1. Roll crumb dough to knife-blade thickness
2. Line flat molds with the dough and place a thin dough ring on the edge
3. Fill the molds with dried peas, place in oven and bake golden
4. When well baked, remove the peas from the molds and take the molds out of the forms, sprinkle with fine ground sugar
5. Peel small masansky apples after removing the core, keeping them whole
6. Put water, sugar, and lemon juice in a pot and stew the apples until soft
7. Remove the apples and boil down the liquid to a thick jelly, color with cochineal
8. Remove from heat and cool
9. Fill the molds with apples and cover with the jelly

title: Tortácskák más módon (Almond Tartlets, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams blanched almonds, soaked
- 14 dekagrams ground sugar
- 14 dekagrams fine flour
- zest of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- 14 dekagrams butter
- 2 egg yolks
- cream as needed for moistening the dough

## Directions

1. Finely grind 14 dekagrams of soaked blanched almonds and sift 14 dekagrams of ground sugar, reserving the coarser portions to sprinkle on finished pastries
2. Mix the sugar and almonds with 14 dekagrams of fine flour and the finely chopped zest of 1 lemon, combining well on a board
3. Cut 14 dekagrams of butter into thin pieces and work into the mixture
4. Add 2 egg yolks and enough cream to moisten the dough as needed
5. Work the dough well until smooth and even
6. Form small tartlets and fill with jam of your choice
7. Make a small lattice on top
8. Brush the tartlets with egg white, sprinkle with the reserved coarse sugar
9. Bake in a gentle oven

title: True Czibere Soup (Valódi czibere-leves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- well-worked black bread dough made with yeast or sourdough
- water
- clean wheat bran
- rice
- eggs
- egg yolks
- sour cream
- butter
- cream of tartar

## Directions

1. From well-worked black bread dough made with yeast or sourdough, set aside about one liter
2. Let it rise in a slightly warm place for 6-8 hours
3. Place in an 8-9 liter earthenware pot and mix by hand with 2 liters of water
4. When well mixed, thicken with clean wheat bran to form a soft dough consistency
5. Cover with a lid and leave in a warm place for 48 hours, stirring three times daily with a wooden spoon
6. On the third day, fill the pot with cold water and let it settle for a couple of hours
7. When settled, strain off as much as needed into an earthenware pot and bring to a boil
8. For each liter of czibere, cook a handful of rice, and when tender, strain off the water
9. For each liter of czibere, beat an egg and egg yolk with a tablespoon of sour cream
10. Temper by first adding a small amount of hot czibere so it does not curdle, then add to the whole pot
11. Stir well, season with salt, add the cooked rice and bring to a boil
12. When boiling, add a tablespoon of melted butter per liter, and if not sour enough, a little cream of tartar
13. Pour into small bread cubes and serve
14. Serve oil at the table and those who like it can pour a spoonful into their bowl
15. Refill the pot with the same amount of clean water and keep in a warm place for later use
16. One starter can be strained eight times if kept in a good place

title: Tutti frutti mandolatejből (Tutti Frutti from Almond Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 42 decagrams almonds (including a few bitter ones) per 7 deciliters water
- 7 deciliters water
- 28 decagrams crushed sugar
- 14 decagrams quince paste
- 14 decagrams green plums preserved in sugar
- 14 decagrams preserved sour cherries
- 7 decagrams green walnuts

## Directions

1. Prepare strong almond milk by swelling the almonds and leaving them in cold water for half an hour, grinding them finely into a paste, thinning with 7 deciliters water, setting aside for half an hour, pressing through cloth, grinding again, pouring the almond milk over again, pressing through, and dissolving 28 decagrams crushed sugar in it
2. Cut the quince paste, preserved green plums, preserved sour cherries, and green walnuts into cubes
3. Pour the almond milk into a pan and proceed as with other ice creams; when already half-frozen, mix in the preserved fruits and freeze completely

title: Tutti-frutti fagylalt (Tutti Frutti Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 orange and 1 lemon, zest rubbed on sugar
- 2 oranges and 2 lemons, juice pressed
- 500 grams sugar
- 4 deciliters water
- preserved walnuts
- green fruit (green almonds, ringlots, quince paste, etc.)
- equal parts of each fruit, cut into small cubes

## Directions

1. Rub the zest of 1 orange and 1 lemon on sugar, not too deeply
2. Press the juice of 2 oranges and 2 lemons
3. Cook 500 grams sugar in 4 deciliters water until the sugar is well dissolved
4. Pour it into the juice, dissolve the rubbed sugar similarly in the juice, and mix well
5. Add preserved walnuts and various types of green fruit, such as green almonds, ringlots, quince paste, etc., using equal parts of each, cut into small cubes
6. Proceed as with other ice creams
7. When it begins to thicken, add the chopped fruit and its juice, and work it well into the ice cream; the more fruit you add, the better

title: Tárkony-mártás (Tarragon Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh tarragon, cleaned and finely chopped
- hot meat broth
- one deciliter of sour cream mixed with a little flour
- salt
- lemon juice or vinegar (optional, if not sour enough)

## Directions

1. Pour hot meat broth over the finely chopped, well-washed tarragon
2. Add a deciliter of sour cream mixed with a little flour
3. If not sour enough, add a teaspoonful of lemon juice or vinegar
4. Cook until thickened to half consistency and salt
5. Alternatively, stew the finely chopped fresh tarragon leaves first in butter or lard, then add the meat broth, flour-thickened sour cream, and a little vinegar

title: Tárkony-mártás (Tarragon Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tarragon, leafed off and finely chopped, well washed
- hot meat stock
- one deciliter sour cream
- flour
- lemon juice or wine vinegar (optional)
- salt

## Directions

1. Pour hot meat stock over the leafed off, finely chopped, well washed tarragon
2. Add one deciliter of sour cream mixed with a little flour
3. If not sour enough, supplement with one coffee spoon of lemon juice or wine vinegar
4. Cook to half-thick consistency, season with salt and serve

title: Tárkonyos bárány (Tarragon Lamb)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of lamb from the front
- two liters of salted water
- one egg-sized amount of lard
- one large tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. Place one quarter of lamb from the front in two liters of salted water to cook
2. While it cooks, prepare ingredients with one egg-sized amount of lard and one large tablespoon of flour

title: Tárkonyos Bárány (Tarragon Lamb)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter front of lamb
- two liters salted water
- one egg-sized amount of lard
- one large tablespoon flour
- one cup cold water
- one teaspoon chopped fresh tarragon leaves
- six tablespoons vinegar
- a few tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Boil the quarter front of lamb in two liters of salted water
2. While it cooks, prepare a nice brown roux from one egg-sized amount of lard and one large tablespoon flour
3. When the roux is hottest, pour in one cup cold water and immediately add it to the hot lamb broth
4. Add one teaspoon chopped fresh tarragon leaves and six tablespoons vinegar
5. Boil for five minutes with these, then remove from high heat until serving
6. Pour in a few tablespoons of sour cream and serve in a deep bowl

title: Tártár-mártás (Tartar Sauce - Cold Fish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three egg yolks
- a few drops of vinegar
- two to three decis of oil
- pepper
- French or English mustard
- good quality vinegar

## Directions

1. Salt the egg yolks
2. Add a few drops of vinegar
3. While continuously stirring with a whisk, add the oil drop by drop (be careful as adding too much oil at once will cause it to separate)
4. When finished, pepper and add French or English mustard
5. Dilute to the proper consistency with good quality vinegar

title: Tépett csuka savanyú káposztával (Shredded Pike with Sour Cabbage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- a little red onion
- pike
- salted water
- a little butter
- two heads of red onion
- sour cream
- breadcrumb
- milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the cabbage in the usual way, thicken it with a little red onion and cook it down with the sauce.
2. Scald the pike with salted water, remove its bones and tear it into small pieces.
3. Melt a little butter, add two heads of red onion cut into thin slices and brown it nicely to a golden colour.
4. On a plate, arrange several spoonfuls of sour cream, a little butter and one portion of the shredded pike, salt it, then add some of the browned onion, then again three or four spoonfuls of sour cream, one spoonful of breadcrumb, again some of the fish, and so on until everything is used up.
5. Pour the last layer with milk and braised onion, place several pieces of butter on the breadcrumb, put it in the oven and cook for a quarter hour, then serve to the table together with the cabbage.

title: Tépett csuka savanyú káposztával (Shredded Pike with Sour Cabbage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cabbage
- red onion
- pike
- salt water
- butter
- red onion
- sour cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the cabbage in the usual way, add a little roux with red onion and cook the sauce through.
2. Pour salted boiling water over the pike, remove its bones and shred into small pieces.
3. Melt a little butter, add two heads of red onion cut into thin slices and roast until a nice golden color.
4. Then place several spoonfuls of sour cream, a little butter and one portion of the shredded pike on a plate, salt it, and add the roasted onion.

title: Tészta almából gyermek-péppel (Cake with Apple and Baby Cereal)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four decilitres milk
- five dekagrams butter
- six egg yolks
- ten dekagrams sugar grated on lemon zest
- four egg whites
- six masanszki apples
- butter
- fine breadcrumbs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare thick baby cereal from four decilitres milk, place the cooled cereal in five dekagrams butter beaten foamy, beat in six egg yolks and mix well, also ten dekagrams sugar grated on lemon zest, and finally mix in the stiff peaks of four egg whites
2. Peel six masanszki apples and cut them into oblong pieces and mix them into the beaten cereal
3. Pour the mixture onto a butter-greased, fine breadcrumb-sprinkled torte sheet, bake in a warm oven, turn out onto a plate and sprinkle with sugar

title: Tészta almával (Cake with Apple)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fifteen masanszki apples or eight larger apples of a different kind
- sugar
- seven dekagrams softened almond
- fourteen dekagrams sugar
- seven dekagrams citronade
- half a roll
- four egg yolks
- four egg whites
- butter
- fine breadcrumbs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel fifteen masanszki or eight larger apples of a non-sweet kind, chop finely and stew gently in sugar so they soften a little
2. Crush seven dekagrams softened almond and fourteen dekagrams sugar, chop seven dekagrams citronade finely and grate half a roll, mix all this well with four egg yolks and mix in the stiff peaks of four egg whites
3. Pour the mixture into a butter-greased, fine breadcrumb-sprinkled pan, place in the oven and bake for three-quarters of an hour
4. Turn out onto a plate and serve sprinkled with sugar

title: Tésztás Kása (Noodle Millet)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter millet porridge
- quarter kilogram bacon
- three liters hot water
- salt
- one large sheet of dried pasta, broken into small pieces

## Directions

1. Dice the bacon finely and render it until crispy
2. Put the millet porridge into the hot fat (do not wash it, only rub it well in cloth)
3. Let it fry in the fat for a few minutes
4. Pour three liters of hot water over it and salt it
5. When the porridge is half cooked, add the broken dried pasta
6. When the porridge and pasta have absorbed all the liquid, stir in the pasta and let it simmer gently for another quarter hour, shaking occasionally but not stirring

title: Tököt befőzni (Preserved Pumpkin)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkin
- salt
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the pumpkin and salt it
2. Squeeze out the salty water from the pieces
3. Bring vinegar and water to a boil
4. Fill jars halfway with this mixture and place the cut pumpkin pieces in them
5. Tie the jars well closed and place in a water bath, leaving them there until the pumpkin begins to boil
6. In winter or when using, add to a roux and prepare like in summer with a little sour cream

title: Töltött bagdány-pecsenye (Stuffed Bagdany Roast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or veal bagdany
- salt
- hard-boiled eggs
- salted pork, finely chopped
- green parsley
- black pepper
- saffron
- salt

## Directions

1. Salt the bagdany
2. Slit the skin around it
3. Split the meat lengthwise in a cross pattern
4. Grate 6-8-10 hard-boiled eggs according to the size of the meat
5. Add a good piece of chopped salted pork chopped very finely, green parsley, abundant black pepper, one knife-tip of good saffron and salt
6. Mix these together
7. Fill the bagdany with this mixture
8. Roast until nicely browned
9. Optionally pound the meat to make it more tender

title: Töltött borjúczomb, melyen a vesepecsenye is rajta van hagyva (Stuffed Veal Leg with Kidney Attached)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal hind quarter, upper part
- Six extracted anchovies
- Chopped meat from the removed upper part
- Red onion, small amount
- Lemon zest
- Marrow
- Fresh parsley, chopped
- Salt and spices
- Sour cream
- Oysters, extracted
- Potatoes, as available by season
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the upper part of the hind quarter down to the kidney, leaving the lower part on the leg
2. Deeply split the entire leg with a long knife
3. Prepare the filling: chop six extracted anchovies, the chopped meat from the removed part, small amount of red onion, lemon zest, marrow, and green parsley; salt and spice it; chop all finely, pound in mortar and add a little sour cream; extracted oysters and potatoes can be added according to season
4. Fill the roast with a large syringe, pierce the leg if desired, seal the openings with lard, and roast slowly
5. Cook butter and sour cream and baste the roast with it
6. Make a good sauce from the drippings with oysters and potatoes

title: Töltött burgonya (Stuffed Potato)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- veal, cut into small cubes
- butter sauce
- asparagus, chopped
- crayfish tails
- nutmeg flower, a small amount
- sour cream
- butter
- sausage for garnish

## Directions

1. Peel cooked potatoes and cool them
2. Cut off a piece from each potato so it stands upright on a plate, and cut a lid from the top
3. Carefully hollow out the potatoes
4. Put the diced veal in butter sauce along with chopped asparagus and crayfish tails and a small amount of nutmeg flower, cook everything well so nothing of the sauce can be seen
5. Fill the potatoes with this mixture
6. Replace the lids
7. Place on a plate, add sour cream and butter, and bake until they turn a nice golden color
8. Garnish with sausage if desired

title: Töltött Csiga (Stuffed Snails)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large snails
- salt
- green parsley
- sardelle (anchovy), cleaned and rinsed
- lemon zest
- snails, part of them
- butter, beaten
- lemon juice
- fine breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the snails and remove from their shells
2. Salt them and let sit in salt for a while
3. Rinse the shells thoroughly
4. Clean the snails of slime and rinse the tails
5. Chop finely: green parsley, cleaned and rinsed sardelle, a little lemon zest, and a part of the snails
6. Mix into beaten butter
7. Add a little lemon juice and a small handful of fine breadcrumbs
8. Mix everything well
9. Fill the snail shells with this mixture, alternating with snail halves, until the shell is full
10. Place on a tin plate
11. Put in the oven and the dish is ready

title: Töltött csirke (Stuffed Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two cleaned chickens
- two rolls
- milk
- butter
- green parsley
- 5-6 eggs
- salt
- spices

## Directions

1. Soak two rolls in milk and squeeze out
2. Braise the squeezed rolls with green parsley in butter
3. When the filling has cooled, beat in 5-6 eggs
4. Season the filling with salt and spices
5. Stuff the chickens through the neck opening
6. Tie them closed
7. Roast on a spit or in a pan until golden brown

title: Töltött csirke magyarosan (Hungarian Stuffed Chicken)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- hard-boiled eggs
- soaked roll
- bacon
- black pepper
- saffron
- parsley
- chives

## Directions

1. For one chicken, grate three hard-boiled eggs, add one-quarter of a soaked roll, one-eighth kilo of bacon cut into small cubes, a good amount of black pepper, and a good pinch of saffron, along with plenty of green parsley and chives
2. Mix this mixture together and stuff the nicely fattened chicken with it, whose skin has been well lifted
3. The stuffing is not runny and must be pushed into the chicken by hand
4. When cooked, it is very fine, tender and extraordinarily delicious

title: Töltött csuka (Stuffed Pike)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike with skin intact
- red onion
- fresh parsley
- anchovies
- grated bread soaked in milk
- butter
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- three eggs
- sour cream
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Clean the pike of scales and cut it in half along the back, removing all its meat along with the bones, but the head and tail must remain on the spine and the entire skin must remain intact.
2. Remove the bones from the meat and chop it finely with red onion, fresh parsley, and several anchovies cleaned of skin and bones.
3. Add grated bread soaked in milk and squeezed, a piece of butter, a little nutmeg, pepper, salt and three eggs, and mix until as smooth as dough.
4. Fill the pike's skin completely with this filling and sew it shut along the back.
5. Place the pike in a well-buttered pan and roast until a beautiful golden color, basting five or six times with fresh sour cream or butter during roasting.
6. Once roasted, place the fish upright on a plate and slice it beautifully and evenly with a sharp knife, pour the sauce over it and sprinkle evenly with thinly sliced lemon zest.
