7. Pike can be prepared the same way, and if carp is to be used this way, only the sour cream and lemon zest should be omitted, so the fish should be basted during roasting only with melted butter.

title: Töltött cukorsuvegecsek rizsből (Filled Sugar-glazed Rice Pastries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 dekagrams rice
- 7 deciliters cream
- 7 dekagrams finely ground almonds
- sugar to taste
- flour for dusting
- canned apricots for filling
- egg white
- beaten egg
- fine breadcrumbs
- fat for frying
- grated chocolate or sugar for coating

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in cream until soft
2. Add the finely ground almonds and sugar to taste
3. When the rice is soft, let it cool slightly
4. Place it on a board dusted with flour and flatten into triangular pieces with a knife
5. Fill each piece with canned apricot
6. Brush the edges with egg white and roll into a small sugar-glass shape
7. Dip in beaten egg, then in fine breadcrumbs
8. Fry in hot fat
9. When removed from fat, dip in grated chocolate or sugar
10. Heap high on the serving dish

title: Töltött czukorsüvegeskék rizsből (Filled Sugar-Glass Shapes with Rice)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve dekagrams of rice
- seven deciliters of sour cream
- seven dekagrams of finely crushed almonds
- sugar
- flour
- preserved apricot
- egg white
- beaten egg
- fine bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook twelve dekagrams of rice soft in seven deciliters of sour cream
2. Add seven dekagrams of finely crushed almonds and enough sugar to make it sufficiently sweet
3. Once the rice is soft, cool it slightly and place it spoonful by spoonful on a board dusted with flour
4. With a knife, flatten into triangular pieces
5. Fill with preserved apricot
6. Brush the edge with egg white and roll it together in the shape of a small sugar-glass
7. Finally, dip into beaten egg and then into fine bread crumbs and fry in fat

title: Töltött fahéjas fánk (Filled Cinnamon Doughnut)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-eight dekagrams of butter
- forty-two dekagrams of flour
- fourteen dekagrams of crushed sugar
- three egg yolks
- sweet sour cream
- preserved apricots
- cinnamon sugar

## Directions

1. On a board, work together twenty-eight dekagrams of butter, forty-two dekagrams of flour, and fourteen dekagrams of crushed sugar well with a rolling pin
2. Add three egg yolks and enough sweet sour cream so the dough achieves proper firmness
3. Work this finely with a wide knife, not by hand
4. Fold together and roll out three times as with butter dough
5. At the third rolling, roll half the dough slightly thicker than noodle dough and brush with preserved apricot
6. Roll out the other half of the dough in the same way and place it on top
7. Cut into rectangular sheets four fingers wide and tie them to a cinnamon doughnut tube with string
8. Fry in fat and sprinkle thickly with sugar and cinnamon
9. Note that the sheets should not be filled completely to the edges with preserve, so it does not run out during frying; it is better to first cut the sheets and then fill each one separately, brush the outer part with egg white, place the other sheet on top and press slightly along the edge

title: Töltött fehérpecsenye (Stuffed Veal Breast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- szép fehérpecsenye
- négy szardellas
- kevés szalonna
- zöld petrezselyem
- sárgarépa
- szalonna
- vörosha­gyma
- sárgarépa
- petrezselyemgyökerek
- kevés ecet
- lé
- citromhéja
- egész borso
- két babyérlevele
- kanálnyi liszt
- jó levé
- kevés ecet

## Directions

1. Szép fehérpecsenyét megveregetve és megsózva egy napig hűvös helyen hagyu­nk
2. Aztán négy szardelláta, kevés szalonnát, zöld petrezselymet, sárgarépát vágunk együtt apróra
3. A fehérpecsenyét hosszában felvágjuk és a vagdalékot beletöltjük
4. Bevarrjuk és finomra vágott szalonnával megtűzzük
5. Aztán lábasba szeletekre vágott szalonnát, vöröshagymát, sárgarépát és petrezselyemgyökeret teszünk
6. Erre a fehérpecsenyét, kevés eczetet és levet öntünk reá
7. Citromhéjat, egész borsot és két babyérlevelete adunk hozzá
8. Addig pároljuk, míg elég porhanyó lesz
9. Ekkor kiveszszük, kanálnyi lisztet hintünk a gyökerekre és megbarnítjuk
10. Jó levessel feleresztjük, kevés eczetet adunk hozzá
11. A fehérpecsenyét ismét beleteszszük és még egy ideig pároljuk

title: Töltött fehérpecsenye más módon (Stuffed Veal Breast, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kiló vastag fehérpecsenye
- 10 deka fehér szalonna
- három keményre főtt tojás
- egy kávés kanál tört borso
- só
- kevés apróra vágott citromhéja
- egy késhegynyi tört szerecsendió-virág
- egy egész nyers tojás
- egy kanál zsír
- nehány kanál víz

## Directions

1. Vegyünk 2 kiló, lehetőleg vastag fehérpecsenyét
2. Éles késsel vágjuk ki közepéből a húst
3. A gömbölyű pecsenye hártyás külső része, a mennyire lehetséges, vékony zacskóforma legyen
4. Az ebből kivágott pecsenyét tegyük deszkára és vagdaljuk jól meg
5. Továbbá aprítsunk még a pecsenye közé 10 deka fehér szalonnát és három keményre főtt tojást
6. Tegyünk a töltelékbe egy kávés kanál tört borsot, sót, kevés apróra vágott citromhéjat és egy késhegynyi tört szerecsendió-virágot
7. A tölteléket egy egész nyers tojással gyúrjuk jól össze
8. Tömjük meg vele a nyers fehérpecsenyezacskót
9. Ha teletömtük, varrjuk be czérnával a végét
10. A húst sózzuk meg és kevés borssal is hintsük be
11. Tegyük egy kanál zsírral együtt lábasba
12. Öntsünk még reá nehány kanál vizet
13. Gyakran megforgatva pároljuk egy óra hosszáig, hogy minden oldala piros legyen
14. Ha a külső hús puha, lehet tálalni
15. Szép, vékony szeletekbe vágjuk
16. Egymás mellé csínosan rakva, hosszú tálban szokás tálalni
17. Körítsük párolt vegyes zöldséggel

title: Töltött Főtt Tojás (Stuffed Hard-Boiled Eggs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- 1/2 bread roll, grated and soaked in milk
- butter the size of half an egg
- green parsley, finely chopped
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the hard-boiled eggs in half lengthwise
2. Finely crush the yolks in a mortar
3. Mix the grated, milk-soaked bread, butter, chopped parsley, crushed yolks, salt, and sour cream together well
4. Fill the egg whites with this mixture
5. Place small pieces of butter in a dish
6. Stand the eggs in the dish
7. Pour sour cream over the top and add a few pieces of butter
8. Bake in the oven until nicely browned

title: Töltött főtt tojás tejfellel (Stuffed Boiled Eggs in Sour Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean and peel the hard-boiled eggs
2. Cut them lengthwise in half

title: Töltött Főtt Tojás Tejfellel (Stuffed Hard-Boiled Eggs with Sour Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 hard-boiled eggs
- 1/2 bread roll soaked in milk
- 10 deca fresh bacon, chopped into very small cubes
- 1 tablespoon sour cream
- 1 whole raw egg
- green parsley
- celery leaves
- onion, finely chopped
- 3 types of greens, 1 tablespoon total
- black pepper and clove pepper, a knife point of each
- salt
- paprika red fat for drizzling
- breadcrumbs
- 1/2 liter salted sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut the hard-boiled eggs in half lengthwise and grate the yolks
2. Soak the bread roll well in milk and chop it finely
3. Chop the fresh bacon very finely into small cubes
4. Chop the green parsley, celery leaves, and onion finely; the total of these three greens should be 1 tablespoon
5. Mix together: the grated egg yolks, soaked bread, bacon, sour cream, raw egg, greens, black pepper, clove pepper, and salt
6. Form small balls the size of the egg yolk
7. Roll in breadcrumbs
8. Place the hard-boiled egg halves in a greased and breadcrumbed dish
9. Fill with the small balls
10. Pour 1/2 liter of salted sour cream over
11. Drizzle with paprika red fat
12. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs
13. Bake in the oven for 1/2 hour
14. Serve in the same dish it was cooked in

title: Töltött galamb (Stuffed Squab)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young fat squabs
- salt
- butter
- milk
- rolls
- green parsley
- saffron
- two whole eggs
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Inflate young fat squabs by blowing through the feather sockets to plump them
2. Pluck them
3. Let them sit in salt for half an hour
4. For two squabs, melt a piece of butter over coals
5. Soak one and a half rolls in milk and chop together with green parsley, saffron and salt
6. Mix everything together
7. Beat in two whole eggs and two egg yolks
8. Stuff the squabs with the filling
9. Thread them onto a spit
10. Roast slowly until golden brown

title: Töltött Kalarábi (Stuffed Kohlrabi)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tender kohlrabi
- salt water
- cooked or braised meat
- fresh parsley
- red onion
- goose fat
- flour
- broth
- butter sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the tender kohlrabi in salted water until half-cooked
2. Cut off a small lid from the top and hollow it out well
3. Chop cooked or braised meat, fresh parsley and a small piece of red onion finely
4. Melt a small spoonful of goose fat, fry a spoonful of flour in it
5. Add the chopped parsley and onion, brown lightly
6. Add the chopped veal meat, mix well
7. Pour a little broth over it and cook well
8. Fill the hollowed kohlrabi with this mixture
9. Place in the prepared butter sauce
10. Let simmer with frequent shaking for a quarter hour
11. Arrange neatly on the dish and serve

title: Töltött Kelkáposzta (Stuffed Kale)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- firm, tender, not too large kale heads
- salt water
- cooked veal
- duck liver (already breaded and fried)
- roll (soaked in milk)
- fresh parsley
- red onion
- cooked kale
- salt
- pepper
- good broth
- eggs
- twine
- sauce
- fried veal feet

## Directions

1. Clean the kale heads and hollow them out carefully
2. Cook in salted water and drain the liquid completely
3. Place on a sieve to drain thoroughly
4. Prepare the stuffing: chop cooked or braised veal, fresh parsley, and a small piece of red onion finely
5. Melt a small spoonful of goose fat in a pan, fry a spoonful of flour in it
6. Add the chopped parsley and onion, brown lightly
7. Add the chopped veal, mix well
8. Pour a little broth over it and boil well
9. Beat two eggs and add them while stirring continuously until a thick mass forms
10. Cool the mixture
11. Form this into a thick sausage shape
12. Separate the cabbage leaves and place the sausage inside
13. Arrange the leaves neatly so it forms a kale head again
14. Tie with twine and place in the prepared sauce
15. At serving time, cut the heads in half and place neatly on the dish
16. Pour the sauce over them and serve with fried veal feet

title: Töltött laska (Filled Ribbon Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter milk
- Three eggs
- Crayfish butter (rákvaj)
- Crayfish tail
- One bread roll
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Bring one liter milk to boil
2. Cook short ribbon noodles made from three eggs in the milk and cool
3. Butter a dish with crayfish butter
4. Beat crayfish butter the size of half an egg until fluffy
5. Mix with a bread roll soaked in milk and crayfish tail
6. Place half of the noodles on the dish
7. Pour the mixture over
8. Place remaining noodles on top with more crayfish butter
9. Bake until golden
10. Sprinkle with sugar and serve

title: Töltött pisztráng (Stuffed Trout)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- pike
- small red onion
- bread roll soaked in milk
- lemon zest
- fresh parsley
- salt
- pepper
- one or two eggs
- melted butter
- breadcrumbs
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove the entrails from the trout, clean it and salt it, then let it stand for a while.
2. Take a piece of pike, carefully remove its bones, and chop it finely with a small red onion, milk-soaked and squeezed-out half bread roll, a little lemon zest and fresh parsley.
3. Add salt and ground pepper to the mixture, beat one or two eggs into it and mix everything well.
4. Dry the trout with a cloth, spread the filling on it, coat with melted butter, sprinkle with breadcrumbs, place in a buttered baking pan.
5. Put in the oven, baste several times with butter so they acquire a nice color.
6. Place on a buttered plate, pour a little sour cream over it and simmer slightly.

title: Töltött rizs-pép (Filled Rice Pap)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 decilitres boiling cream
- 12 dekagrams rice
- 10 dekagrams butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 7 dekagrams finely ground almonds
- vanilla
- 10 dekagrams ground sugar
- 4 egg whites
- breadcrumb powder
- apricot compote

## Directions

1. Cook the rice in boiling cream until soft
2. Cool and crush fine in a mortar
3. Mix the creamed butter with 6 egg yolks, the finely ground almonds, vanilla, and sugar
4. Fold in the stiff foam of 4 egg whites
5. Butter a mould and coat with fine breadcrumb powder
6. Fill half with the pap and stuff with apricot compote or other filling
7. Cover with the remaining pap and bake slowly

title: Töltött rostélyos (Stuffed Roasted Beef)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted beef
- salt
- green parsley
- red onion
- lemon zest
- sour cream
- bacon
- thyme
- bay leaves
- peppercorns
- clove pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Remove the fat and bones from the roasted beef, beat it well with the flat of a knife, and salt it
2. Chop one slice of roasted beef finely and mix it with green parsley, red onion, and lemon zest, and salt it
3. Mix this filling with sour cream so that it can be spread well
4. Spread this filling on a piece of roasted beef to the thickness of a knife spine
5. Place another piece of roasted beef on top, fasten it in four places, and do the same with the rest
6. Place a pot with a few slices of bacon, red onion, thyme, and bay leaves and line the bottom
7. Place the roasted beef pieces in the pot with several peppercorns and clove pepper, braise until tender, and serve
8. Cook the sauce until golden, sprinkle a spoonful of flour on top, brown it, add a spoonful of liquid and several spoonfuls of sour cream
9. Strain it over the roasted beef and cook once more

title: Töltött rostélyos bajor módra (Stuffed Sirloin Bavarian Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful sirloin
- salt
- several anchovy fillets
- capers
- onion
- green parsley, finely chopped
- butter
- two or three egg yolks
- broth
- sour cream
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Beat and salt the sirloin well
2. Remove anchovy fillets and add finely chopped capers, onion, and green parsley
3. Beat butter until foamy, add two or three egg yolks, then the finely chopped mixture and mix well
4. Fill the sirloin with this mixture and roll it up
5. Place in a pot, add a little broth and braise until crumbly
6. Pour six spoonfuls of sour cream over it and braise a little longer
7. Strain off the fat and sprinkle with lemon zest before serving

title: Töltött rák (Stuffed Crayfish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- salt
- parsley
- caraway seeds
- rice
- butter
- 30 crayfish tails
- egg yolk
- sour cream
- white wine

## Directions

1. Cook the crayfish with salt, parsley, and caraway seeds
2. Clean and shell the crayfish
3. Braise rice in butter with finely chopped parsley
4. Chop 30 crayfish tails coarsely and add with salt
5. Cool the mixture
6. Mix with egg yolk and three tablespoons of sour cream
7. Stuff the crayfish shells with this mixture
8. Replace the lower shell so the crayfish retains its whole shape
9. Press the shell firmly together
10. Place on a platter
11. Add six tablespoons of sour cream, several tablespoons of white wine, and caraway seeds
12. Place hot coals above and below the crayfish
13. Bring to a boil and the dish is ready

title: Töltött tojás (Stuffed Eggs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- six hard-boiled eggs
- bread soaked in milk
- butter
- green parsley
- salt
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut six hard-boiled eggs in half lengthwise
2. Crush the yolks finely in a mortar
3. Soak half a roll in milk and squeeze it out
4. Cream together egg-yolk-sized pieces of butter
5. Mix with finely chopped green parsley, crushed yolk, half the soaked bread with its soaking milk, and salt
6. Add two tablespoons of sour cream
7. Mix everything well together
8. Fill the egg white halves with this mixture
9. Place small pieces of butter in a dish and set the eggs in them
10. Pour sour cream over the top and add more butter pieces
11. Bake in the oven until nicely browned

title: Töltött Tojás (Stuffed Eggs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 eggs
- bread roll soaked in milk
- egg yolks, grated
- sour cream
- tablespoon powdered sugar
- teaspoon cinnamon
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- 7 deciliter sour cream
- saffron
- cinnamon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Hard boil the eggs and cut them in half, removing the yolks.
2. For ten eggs, chop less than one bread roll that has been soaked in milk.
3. Grate the egg yolks and add them to the bread.
4. Add a little sour cream, a tablespoon of powdered sugar, and a teaspoon of cinnamon, and mix together.
5. Butter a flat dish and sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
6. Place the halved eggs on the dish and fill them with the prepared mixture.
7. Take 7 deciliter of sour cream for ten eggs, mix in saffron, cinnamon, and sugar, and pour over the eggs in the dish.
8. Place in the oven and brown until golden, then serve in the same dish.

title: Töltött Tojás (Stuffed Eggs)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- bread roll (zsemla)
- milk
- sour cream
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Hard boil eggs, cut in half, and remove the yolks
2. For 10 eggs, soak bread roll in milk and chop
3. Grate the hard-boiled egg yolks and add to the bread
4. Add a little sour cream, 1 tablespoon powdered sugar, and 1 teaspoon cinnamon, mix together
5. Butter a shallow dish and sprinkle with bread crumbs
6. Place the halved eggs in the dish
7. Fill the eggs with the prepared mixture

title: Tört leves (Broken Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- pike head
- pea broth
- breadcrumbs
- soaked in milk
- eggs
- green parsley
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the soup the same way as on meat days, but instead of chicken use a large pike head, and instead of meat broth use pea water.
2. Serve the soup with pike dumplings prepared as follows: Cook a nice piece of pike, about one kilogram, in salted water.
3. Clean the pike from bones and skin.
4. Chop two pieces of breadcrumbs soaked in milk and pressed out, three beaten eggs, and chopped green parsley finely together.
5. Beat seven dekagrams of butter until fluffy.
6. Add the chopped mixture to the butter and mix well.
7. Add one whole egg and three egg yolks, mix everything well.
8. Season with a little salt.
9. Do not knead the dough too firmly.
10. Shape small dumplings, roll them in fine breadcrumbs, and fry them.

title: Tört májleves (Chopped Liver Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 decagrams calf liver
- three-quarter part of a roll
- two eggs
- fat
- good meat broth
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fry the calf liver, roll, and two eggs in fat
2. Pound finely in a mortar
3. Place in a pot and pour good meat broth over it
4. Add a little nutmeg
5. Cook for half an hour
6. Strain through a sieve and return to the pot
7. Cook a little longer
8. Serve over fried liver dumplings

title: Tört pudding (Pounded Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams butter
- 14 dekagrams sugar
- 14 dekagrams almonds
- 1 lemon zest
- 1 roll crust
- milk
- 8 egg yolks
- 4 egg whites
- wine sauce

## Directions

1. Finely pound 14 dekagrams butter, the same amount of sugar and the same amount of almonds together
2. Finely chop the zest of 1 lemon
3. Grate the crust of 1 roll, soak in milk and squeeze out well
4. Pound everything finely in a mortar
5. Put in a bowl and add 8 egg yolks one after another
6. Stir for half an hour
7. Add the stiff whites of 4 eggs
8. Pour into a buttered basin, steam and serve with wine sauce

title: Törtleves (Broken Bread Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- half a roll
- two hard-boiled eggs
- good meat broth
- marrow, finely chopped
- fried liver
- bread roll

## Directions

1. Cut chicken and half a roll into pieces and bread them
2. Pound two hard-boiled eggs together with the breaded pieces in a mortar
3. Place in a pot and pour good meat broth over it
4. Let cook for one hour and strain through a sieve
5. If the soup is too thick, thin it with a little clear broth
6. When serving, add finely chopped marrow that has been cooked until tender, fried liver, and bread

title: Török tej-kocsonya (Turkish Milk Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters good heavy cream
- 5 tablespoons apricot or raspberry marmalade
- 14 dekagrams finely broken sugar
- A few drops of almond liqueur (if using raspberry)
- 1.5 dekagrams dissolved and cooled isinglass

## Directions

1. Set seven deciliters of good heavy cream on ice in a brass pan and whip it with a whisk until the entire mass becomes stiff foam
2. In a pan, beat five tablespoons of apricot or raspberry marmalade with fourteen dekagrams of finely broken sugar until frothy
3. If using raspberry, add a few drops of almond liqueur to give it a pale pink color
4. Gently and slowly mix the foam with this mixture
5. Add the dissolved and cooled isinglass
6. Bury the mold in ice, pour in the mixture, and freeze on ice
7. For serving, top the foam with Spanish fritters or close with a wreath made from and dried puff pastry

title: Törött Rizsabroncs Vajastésztában (Broken Rice Puffs in Butter Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 dekagrams rice
- butter
- 4 deciliters cream
- vanilla sugar to taste
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- butter puff pastry cases
- apricot preserve

## Directions

1. Grind 7 dekagrams of cleaned rice very finely.
2. Heat butter in a pan, add the rice and fry, then pour 4 deciliters cream over it and stir until thick.
3. If too thick, add more cream; it should have the consistency of a soufflé.
4. Add vanilla sugar to taste.
5. Remove from heat, beat in 3 egg yolks until frothy, then slowly fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 3 eggs.
6. Line round molds with butter puff pastry, add a good spoonful of mixture, top with a little apricot preserve, then fill completely with the remaining mixture.
7. Place in the oven and bake slowly.

title: Túró-Fánkocska (Cheese Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter pastry dough
- egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare butter pastry dough — see below
2. After folding 3 times, roll out to the thickness of a knife blade
3. Cut out with medium-sized fritter cutters in the desired quantity
4. Place on a baking sheet
5. Brush the center with egg white

title: Túró-fánkocska (Cottage Cheese Fritters)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry (prepared as described below)
- Egg white
- Seven dekagrams butter
- Four egg yolks
- Cottage cheese (enough to fill a small coffee cup), finely grated
- Salt
- Three tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry and fold it three times, then roll out to knife-thickness
2. Cut out medium-sized rounds with a donut cutter and place on a baking sheet
3. Brush the centers with egg white
4. Cut smaller rounds with a slightly smaller cutter (small enough to fit inside the first) and place them on top of the first rounds
5. Beat seven dekagrams butter until light, then add egg yolks one at a time
6. Mix in the finely grated cottage cheese, season lightly with salt, and blend in three tablespoons sour cream; the mixture should be thick, not thin
7. Using a knife dipped in sour cream, form the filling into pointed mounds on the centers of the fritters
8. Cut rounds from the second set of pastry pieces using the same smaller cutter, then cut out a small center with an even smaller cutter to create a crown shape
9. Place the crown on top of the cottage cheese mound to partially enclose it
10. Brush the pastry with egg white and bake until a beautiful light golden color

title: Túró-lepény (Cheese Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams flour
- 3/8 lard or butter
- 20 grams yeast
- half coffee cup milk
- 3 pieces sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- half coffee cup wine
- salt to taste
- cottage cheese
- 2 egg yolks
- a few tablespoons sour cream
- one egg-sized piece of clarified butter
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Take 500 grams flour and 3/8 its weight in lard or butter, rub well together
2. Dissolve 20 grams yeast in half a coffee cup milk with 3 pieces of sugar
3. Mix the yeast mixture with 4 egg yolks, half a coffee cup wine, salt, and quickly knead into a soft dough—if kneaded too long it becomes tough and not crumbly
4. Let rest for several hours until visibly risen
5. Flatten to knife-blade thickness on a baking sheet
6. Press cottage cheese through thick cloth to remove whey, then place in a bowl
7. Mix the pressed cheese with 2 egg yolks, a few tablespoons of sour cream, one egg-sized piece of clarified butter, and whipped egg white, adding a pinch of salt
8. Spread the cheese mixture finger-thick over the dough on the baking sheet, sprinkle with flour and butter generously over the flour so a beautiful brown crust forms on top while the cheese stays soft
9. Fold the edges of the dough back onto the sheet about a finger's width and press with your fingers to create a decorative border
10. Brush the folded edge with egg and bake at high heat until beautifully browned

title: Túrós csúsza (Cheese Csúsza)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- eggshell measure of water
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat 4 eggs into a bowl, pour water measured with eggshell into the middle, and knead with as much flour as it absorbs, until the dough becomes shiny from kneading
2. This should be rather stiff than soft, although it is harder to roll out
3. Divide into two portions

title: Túrós csúsza (Cheese Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- water (eggshell measure)
- flour (as much as the dough will absorb)

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs into a bowl, add water measured with an eggshell, and knead with flour until a stiff dough forms and becomes shiny from kneading
2. Divide the dough into two pieces
3. Roll out the dough slightly thicker than a knife's edge and cut the entire sheet into finger-wide strips
4. Tear these strips into small pieces with your fingers and cook in already boiling water
5. Cook only until they rise well to the surface of the water and boil for 10 minutes
6. Remove with a slotted spoon, drain the water well, rinse with cold water, place in a bowl, salt it, pour hot fat over it, top with cheese, a little sour cream, and nicely fried bacon cracklings, and serve hot

title: Túrós pogácsa (Cheese Scone)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kilogram flour
- 1/2 kilogram fat
- salt
- water
- 3/4 kilogram cow cheese, crumbled
- egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Mix 3/4 kilogram flour with 1/2 kilogram fat by rubbing together
2. Mix with salty water to make a soft dough
3. Suddenly knead in the same amount of crumbled cow cheese as there was flour, so that the cheese remains in pieces
4. Form into the size of an egg and make long scones from it
5. Cut, brush with egg, bake until nicely golden, and serve hot

title: Túrós rétes (Cheese Strudel)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 decagrams butter
- 6 egg yolks
- lemon zest
- 4 deciliters cheese
- sugar
- sour cream
- 6 egg whites
- strudel dough

## Directions

1. Break 7 decagrams butter over coals
2. Add 6 egg yolks, lemon zest, 4 deciliters cheese, sugar, and sour cream
3. Finally add the foam from 6 egg whites
4. Pour the strudel dough with butter
5. Spread the filling on it, roll up the strudel and bake in the usual manner

title: Túrós-metélt (Cheese Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half liters of dried curd cheese
- butter
- three whole eggs
- three egg yolks
- salt
- flour
- hot milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Finely combine the dried curd cheese with butter
2. Mix in three whole eggs, three egg yolks, and a little salt
3. Make a dough with flour
4. On a floured board, form the dough into noodles the length of a finger and thick
5. Fry in a bronze pan with butter until golden brown
6. Pour into hot milk
7. Season well with sugar and let it boil

title: Tüdős Hurka (Lung Sausage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lung soaked in cold water and briefly boiled
- spleen
- small amount of raw liver
- caul fat cleaned in cold water
- large red onion
- black pepper
- quarter kilogram cooked rice
- cloves
- black ground pepper
- marjoram, sifted through a sieve
- salt
- hot water

## Directions

1. Soak the lung in cold water and briefly boil it, then clean it from the bronchi
2. Finely chop the spleen and small amount of raw liver with a chopping knife
3. Briefly boil and finely chop the cleaned caul fat
4. Braise a large red onion in lard with black pepper until soft, then chop very finely
5. Mix the cooked rice, a small amount of cloves, black ground pepper, plenty of marjoram (sifted), proper amount of salt, and a few spoonfuls of hot water with all the ingredients to create a tender mass
6. Fill the clean intestines with a sausage filler to seven-handspan-length pieces
7. Secure both ends of the sausage with thin wooden sticks
8. Boil in hot salted water

title: Tőkehal szardellával (Cod with Anchovies)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram of cod
- salt water
- eight anchovies
- green parsley
- capers
- butter
- sour cream
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook one kilogram of cod in salted water
2. Finely chop eight anchovies, green parsley and a little capers together
3. Place a few pieces of butter on a plate and add one to two spoonfuls of sour cream
4. Tear the cod into small pieces and arrange one layer on the plate
5. Then arrange one layer of the chopped mixture with a little breadcrumbs, several spoonfuls of sour cream and one to two pieces of butter
6. Continue in this manner until the plate is full
7. Sprinkle the whole with breadcrumbs, sour cream and butter
8. Place in the oven and bake until nicely golden

title: Tűzött harcsa (Larded Catfish)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large piece of catfish
- salt
- finely minced bacon
- veal slices
- ham pieces
- parsley
- scallion
- peppercorns
- 2 deciliters white wine
- 2 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Wash the catfish, salt it and let it sit in salt for an hour
2. Lard it with finely minced bacon in five or six rows
3. Place in a pot with veal slices, ham pieces, a bundle of parsley, scallion, whole peppercorns, 2 deciliters white wine and the same amount of water, cover and cook slowly
4. When the fish is cooked, remove it, strain the sauce through a sieve, remove fat and reduce until 2 deciliters remain
5. Return the fish to the sauce to warm through completely, serve on a plate and pour the sauce over

title: Tűzött vesepecsenye (Roasted Kidneys with Marinade)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- red onion
- carrot
- lemon zest
- bay leaves
- peppercorns
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and bones from a nice kidney and salt it
2. Prepare a marinade from one part vinegar and two parts water
3. Add one head of red onion, carrot, lemon zest, two bay leaves, and several peppercorns to the marinade and cook it well
4. Pour the hot marinade over the kidney, cover it, and let it stand in a cool place for 12-24 hours
5. Pierce the kidney in three to four rows
6. Remove the red onion and carrot from the marinade and place them in a pot with the kidney on top
7. Place coals on top and braise slowly for two hours until the color becomes yellowish-brown
8. Pour four deciliters of sour cream over it and braise for another quarter hour until the sauce becomes a nice yellow color
9. Place the kidney on a serving dish and pour the sauce underneath

title: Uborka-mártás (Cucumber Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cucumbers
- light brown roux
- meat broth
- fresh cream
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cut the sour cucumbers into thin slices as much as needed
2. Prepare a thin light brown roux and loosen it with good broth
3. Add the cucumber with two to three spoons of fresh cream
4. Add a little lemon zest and bring to a boil
5. The cucumber can also be grated

title: Uborka-saláta (Cucumber Salad)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled cucumber
- Salt
- Minced garlic
- Fine oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Slice the peeled cucumber very thinly and salt it
2. Let it stand for several hours
3. Strain off the salty water
4. Mix in minced garlic
5. Pour fine oil and vinegar over it and mix well
6. Sprinkle with crushed pepper

title: Uborkát becsinálni (Preserved Cucumbers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized cucumbers
- fresh water
- walnut-sized alum
- dill seeds
- currant leaves
- cucumber herb
- ironwort
- half vinegar
- half good white wine
- several handfuls of salt
- 3 dekagrams whole pepper
- 3 dekagrams white ginger

## Directions

1. Place medium-sized cucumbers, preferably small rather than large, in fresh water and leave them there for 2-3 hours, pouring fresh water over them several times
2. Drain them, place on cloth, and dry them
3. The barrels in which you place them must be very clean
4. Put a walnut-sized piece of alum in each barrel and the usual herbs: currant leaves, cucumber herb, and especially a lot of ironwort, as this preserves the firmness and green color of the fruit
5. After lining the bottom of the barrel with this, place a good layer of cucumbers on top, then herbs again, then cucumbers, and so on until the barrel is full
6. Boil equal parts vinegar and good white wine with several handfuls of salt, three dekagrams of whole pepper, and three dekagrams of white ginger in a clean earthenware pot
7. Pour this mixture hot over the cucumbers, then seal tightly immediately
8. Check occasionally, and if there is barrel mold on top, remove it; if necessary, pour a little more wine and vinegar
9. Shake this container several times during use
10. For households where only small quantities of cucumbers are needed, it is better to preserve them in glass jars rather than barrels; the procedure remains the same, except that the jar, since the vinegar is not absorbed, should be tied tightly closed with paper and a bladder and stored in a dry place like cooked fruit

title: Uborkát télre sósvízbe eltenni (Cucumbers in Brine for Winter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- szép, jó nagy uborkák
- szőlőlevél
- kapor
- szőlő-kocsány
- timsó
- folyóvíz
- só

## Directions

1. Szép, jó nagy uborkákat tisztán megmosunk és megszárítunk
2. Ezeket kis hordóba teszszük, melynek fenekén aknás lyuk van a csap számára
3. A hordó fenekére maroknyi szőlőlevelet, kaprot és szőlő-kocsányi teszünk
4. Az utóbbiból pedig jó részt, melyet ha megmostunk, kissé összezúzunk, hogy savanyú ízét hamarább közölje az uborkával
5. Aztán darabka timsót teszünk
6. Erre tiz uborkát teszünk, aztán ismét szőlőlevelet, kaprot; ismét uborkát, míg a hordó megtelt
7. Ekkor folyóvizet öntünk reá, melyben annyi sót oldunk fel, hogy jól megérezzék rajta
8. A hordót jól bedugjuk, meleg helyre állítjuk
9. Két hétig minden nap megrázzuk
10. A csapon minden tíz nap után sósvizet töltünk utána

title: Ugyanaz baromfiból (Brown Broth with Poultry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole chicken or capon
- water
- small amount of clean fat
- one teaspoon ground sugar
- carrot, sliced into rings
- parsley root
- celery
- red onion
- peppercorns
- kale, if available

## Directions

1. Put the whole chicken in a pot and pour in enough water to cover it, then cook
2. In a pot, put a small amount of clean fat and one teaspoon ground sugar
3. Add carrot sliced into rings, parsley root, celery, red onion, and peppercorns, and if available, a little kale
4. Cover and sauté on the fire with frequent stirring
5. When browned, add to the chicken
6. When the chicken is cooked tender, strain the broth and return it to the fire
7. Cook fine noodles in the broth and serve with the shredded chicken meat

title: Ugyanaz csontokból (Brown Broth from Bones)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- bones
- vegetables and seasonings as for brown beef broth

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as brown beef broth, using bones instead of meat
2. This is particularly useful when serving roasted meat, braised beef, game roasts, etc., as the bones and other scraps from these can be made into a very good broth by this method
3. Lung meat is best for this broth

title: Vaddisznófej (Wild Boar Head)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- vaddisznófej
- eczetes víz
- kevés bor
- só
- vörosha­gyma
- sárgarépa
- citromhéja
- kakukfű
- kis babyérlevele
- nehány egész borsszemé
- zöld babyérlevele
- becsinált veresrépa
- egy kemény tojás
- zöld petrezselyem
- citromhéja
- édes mártás vagy hideg torma vagy ecet és olaj

## Directions

1. Miután a fejet nehány óráig áztattuk, forró vizet öntünk reá
2. A nyelv hártyáját lehúzzuk
3. A fejet lehető legszebben megtisztítjuk
4. Kendőbe kötvén, fazékba teszszük
5. Eczetes vizet és kevés bort öntünk reá
6. Sót, vörösha­gymát, sárgarépát, citromhéjat, kakukfüvet, kis babyérlevelete és nehány egész borsszemet adunk hozzá
7. Puhára főzzük
8. Aztán kiveszszük, asztalkendőt összehajtunk, a fejet rátesszük
9. Mellé zöld babyérlevelekets, fölibe becsinált veresrépát és egy kemény tojás vékonyra vágott fehérét, zöld petreszely­met és citromhéjat rakunk csínosan
10. Aztán édes mártást vagy hideg tormát vagy eczetet és olajat is adunk mellé

title: Vaddisznóhús elkészíteni (Prepared Wild Boar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- vaddisznóhús
- két rész veres bor
- egy rész ecet
- só
- babyérlevele
- vörosha­gyma
- kevés citromhéja
- sárgarépa
- minden fűszerből egy keveset
- befőzött ribizli vagy rózsacsipke

## Directions

1. Ha a vaddisznóhúst jól kiáztattuk, fazékba teszszük
2. Két rész veres bort és egy rész eczetet öntünk reá
3. Sót, babyérlevelete, vörösha­gymát, kevés citromhéjat, sárgarépát és minden fűszerből egy keveset adunk hozzá
4. Addig főzzük, míg porhanyó lesz
5. Aztán befőzött ribizliből vagy rózsacsipkéből mártást készítünk
6. A vaddisznóhúst feltálaljuk, a levéből keveset reáöntünk
7. A mártást külön csészében adjuk fel az asztalra

title: Vadgalamb sülve (Roasted Wild Pigeon)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- wild pigeon
- salt
- vinegar
- wine
- onion
- bay leaf
- lemon peel
- bacon
- sour cream
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and salt the pigeon
2. Make a brine from vinegar, wine, onion, bay leaf and lemon peel
3. Place the pigeons in the brine for two to three days, especially if they are old
4. Lard with finely chopped bacon and roast slowly
5. Baste frequently with brine and sour cream during roasting
6. Prepare the sauce by making a roux with a few spoonfuls of flour in melted butter
7. Pour enough brine over to make the sauce sufficiently thick
8. Add the drippings from the pigeons and a few spoonfuls of sour cream, and bring to a boil
9. Place the roasted pigeons on a plate and pour the sauce underneath

title: Vadmártás (Game Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley root and other roots
- bay leaves
- capers
- black pepper
- red onion
- diced bacon
- flour
- a little flour for browning
- Liebig meat extract (5 grams)
- salt
- sour cream (several spoonfuls)
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Stew parsley root and other roots with bay leaves, capers, black pepper, red onion, and diced bacon until all moisture from the roots and onion has evaporated
2. Sprinkle with a little flour and brown it, stirring constantly
3. Pour in a little marinade and bring to a boil
4. When well cooked, press through a sieve
5. Add 5 grams of Liebig meat extract
6. Season with salt
7. Finally, pour in several spoonfuls of sour cream
8. If too thick, dilute with meat broth and cook for another quarter hour
9. Serve separately in a bowl with roasted meat or game

title: Vadpecsenyeként páczolt őzderék (Marinated Venison Roast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch
- 2 liters water
- 1 liter vinegar
- 2 tin spoons salt
- 1 spoon peppercorns
- 6 bay leaves
- 1 large onion
- 6 cloves
- thin bacon slices
- 1 tin spoon flour
- 2 decilitres sour cream
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Remove the outer membrane from the venison with a knife and place in a bowl
2. In a pot, combine 2 liters water, 1 liter vinegar, 2 tin spoons salt, 1 spoon peppercorns, 6 bay leaves, 1 large onion, and 6 cloves
3. Bring the mixture to a boil and cook for a quarter hour
4. Pour the hot brine over the venison in the bowl while hot
5. Cover and place in a cool location
6. Turn the meat in the brine twice daily
7. The meat can be cooked after just one day, but keep longer if possible
8. When ready to roast, place on a board and lard with thin bacon slices along the spine in four rows and on the sides
9. Place in a roasting pan and pour 1 liter of strained brine over it
10. Roast in a hot oven for one hour, basting several times
11. In a bowl, combine 1 tin spoon flour with 2 decilitres sour cream and mix together
12. Pour this mixture into the roasting liquid and simmer for a few minutes
13. Cut the venison with a sharp knife across the backbone so the bone is severed in each slice
14. Arrange in a long dish, garnish with lemon slices, and pour the sauce underneath

title: Vadrueza tűzve és sülve (Larded and Roasted Wild Duck)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- wild duck
- brine
- salt
- bacon slices
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean the wild duck well
2. Place in brine for several days
3. Salt and lard neatly on the breast in six rows
4. During roasting, baste frequently with brine and sour cream
5. Serve with the sauce that dripped from it

title: Vadászleves (Hunter's Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Strong beef broth, completely defatted
- Partridge or woodcock, cleaned of feathers and innards
- Beef broth
- 4 egg yolks
- Nutmeg flower, a small amount

## Directions

1. Prepare a very strong beef broth completely free of fat, which should be strained through a kitchen towel and kept warm.
2. Pound a cleaned partridge or woodcock (with innards included) raw in a mortar very well.
3. Pass through a sieve together with a tablespoon of beef broth, 4 egg yolks, and a small amount of nutmeg flower.
4. Mix into the prepared broth and arrange into small game meat dumplings to serve.

title: Vagdalt becsinált (Minced Meat Balls in Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- veal and pork together, chopped
- smoked bacon, a small amount, cut into very small cubes
- raw rice, two tablespoons
- flour, one tablespoon
- one whole egg
- half a bread roll, soaked and chopped
- ground black pepper, a small amount
- salt
- hot fat for frying
- flour for breading
- hot water
- lemon or vinegar
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Chop the veal and pork together with a knife, and a small amount of smoked bacon into very small cubes. Mix together two tablespoons of raw rice, one tablespoon of flour, one whole egg, half a bread roll soaked and chopped, a small amount of ground black pepper, and the proper amount of salt with the chopped meat. Form into dumplings the size of apples, roll them well in flour, and fry them a nice golden color in hot fat. Once fried, remove them and make a light roux from the fat, dilute it with hot water, and place the cooked dumplings in it. Once they have cooked through, season with lemon or vinegar and generously add sour cream before serving.

title: Vagdalék Barna Abroncsban (Forcemeat in Brown Ring Mold)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram veal
- bacon slices
- carrots
- cloves
- onion
- good brown stock
- half a bread roll (zsemle)
- 5 egg yolks
- glace (lész)
- lemon juice
- ring mold (abroncsforma)
- butter
- cooked veal sweetbread (borjúkedes)
- roasted veal
- crayfish tails
- asparagus or artichokes
- button mushrooms (csiperke-gomba)
- parsley

## Directions

1. Braise 1 kilogram veal with bacon slices, carrots, cloves, and onion, occasionally adding good stock, until nicely browned
2. Remove the meat and chop finely
3. Soak half a bread roll in brown stock, squeeze out, and add to meat
4. Pound the mixture with 5 egg yolks in a mortar until it becomes a paste
5. Place in a pot, add a little brown stock and lemon juice and a little glace, cook well until it resembles a purée
6. Pass through a sieve
7. Butter a ring mold and fill it with this mixture
8. Braise in a water bath for three-quarters of an hour
9. Meanwhile, cut cooked veal sweetbread and roasted veal into cubes, include crayfish tails, asparagus or artichokes in small pieces, some sliced button mushrooms, and finely chopped parsley
10. Braise this garnish mixture in butter and cook well
11. Turn out the brown ring mold onto a serving dish
12. Fill the center opening with the garnish mixture
13. Alternate crayfish tails, asparagus tips, and artichoke rosettes around the ring for service

title: Vagdalék Borjúhúsból (Minced Veal Hash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roasted veal
- butter the size of an egg
- one tablespoon flour
- good broth
- one tablespoon sour cream
- a little ground nutmeg
- asparagus
- mushrooms
- butter
- fresh parsley
- artichokes or more asparagus
- salted water

## Directions

1. Cut cold roasted veal into large cubes
2. Melt butter the size of an egg, add one tablespoon flour and brown it slightly
3. Dilute with good broth, add one tablespoon sour cream and a little ground nutmeg
4. When the sauce is ready, add the veal cubes, as well as 25-30 pieces of asparagus and mushrooms that have been braised in butter with fresh parsley
5. Braise everything together until no sauce remains
6. Pile in the shape of a mound in a round dish and decorate with a crown made of puff pastry
7. If preparing in a season when asparagus is not available, use artichokes instead, boiled in salted water and cut into pieces

title: Vagdalék Kagylóban (Forcemeat in Shells)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 decagrams butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 3 deciliters good milk
- ground veal or meat (vagdalék)
- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- butter for greasing
- calf's sweetbread ragout
- veal ragout
- asparagus
- crayfish tails
- button mushrooms (csiperke-gomba)
- marrow carbonate for garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare a béchamel sauce from 5 decagrams butter, 2 tablespoons flour, and 3 deciliters good milk
2. Mix the forcemeat (vagdalék) into the béchamel, season with salt
3. Beat in 1 whole egg and 1 egg yolk, mix well
4. Butter a shell dish (kagylókat) generously
5. Prepare a ragout from calf's sweetbread (borjúkedes), asparagus, crayfish tails, and button mushrooms, reduce the sauce until thick
6. Beat 2 egg yolks into the ragout
7. Fill the buttered shells with forcemeat to a thickness of one finger all around
8. Fill the center with the ragout
9. Cover with the remaining forcemeat to finger thickness
10. Braise for one hour
11. Turn out onto a serving dish and garnish with marrow carbonate

title: Vagdalék sonkával és rántott burgonyával (Minced Meat with Ham and Fried Potatoes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- veal meat
- veal breast
- green parsley
- button mushrooms
- artichokes or asparagus
- potatoes
- seven decagrams butter
- one whole egg
- three egg yolks
- salt
- fine bread crumbs
- ham

## Directions

1. Heat a little butter and add finely diced veal meat and veal breast with green parsley-braised button mushrooms and artichokes or asparagus according to season; braise until only little sauce remains
2. Meanwhile, cook four or five good starchy potatoes, peel them, cool them, and grate them
3. Beat seven decagrams of butter until fluffy, add one whole egg and three egg yolks one by one, add a little salt and grated potato to form flat round croquettes about two knife-widths thick and the size of a one-forint silver coin
4. Dip into beaten egg and fine bread crumbs and fry golden in fat
5. Beat two egg yolks into the minced meat sauce and arrange on a round platter; densely cover with the fried potatoes sprinkled with finely chopped ham and border the platter with ham

title: Vagdalék tekenösbékából (Turtle Ragout)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- turtle
- salt
- water
- butter
- flour
- pea broth
- parsley
- peas
- mushrooms
- 12 crayfish tails
- butter pastry

## Directions

1. Cook the turtle in salted water until soft
2. Break the shell in two and remove the cooked turtle
3. Remove the black skin from the legs and other parts
4. Carefully remove the liver and organs without breaking the gallbladder
5. Cut the turtle into four pieces
6. Make a light butter sauce diluted with pea broth
7. Melt butter and braise parsley, 2 deciliters of peas, 12 finely chopped mushrooms, and 15-20 crayfish tails
8. When everything is braised, add to the turtle and cook well together
9. There should be only a little sauce when served
10. Can be surrounded with a crown made of butter pastry

title: Vagdalék-fánk vagy Vajas Táska (Meat Rissole)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram braised veal, not browned
- green parsley
- 1 onion
- butter or goose fat
- small teaspoon flour
- half ladle broth
- lard pastry dough
- egg white
- fine breadcrumbs
- hot fat
- fried parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Chop half a kilogram of braised veal finely, along with some green parsley and one onion.
2. Melt a little butter or goose fat, mix in a small teaspoon of flour, lightly brown the chopped parsley and onion, then add the chopped veal.
3. Pour half a ladle of broth over it and simmer until it becomes a thick paste.
4. Roll out lard pastry thinly and cut into pieces the width of the palm and length of a finger.
5. Brush one end with egg white and coat lengthwise with filling.
6. Roll up and press the two ends together firmly so they do not open during frying.
7. Brush the whole thing with egg white, sprinkle with fine breadcrumbs, and fry golden in hot fat.
8. Drain on clean absorbent paper and arrange neatly in a dish.
9. Garnish with fried parsley.

title: Vagdalék-kádkák (Chopped Meat Cases)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted turkey
- half calf sweetbread
- six mushrooms
- twenty crayfish tails and claws
- crayfish butter
- green peas
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Chop roasted turkey, half calf sweetbread, six mushrooms, and the tails and claws of twenty crayfish into small cubes
2. Make fourteen decagrams of crayfish butter from the shells
3. Braise the mushrooms in the crayfish butter with three deciliters of green peas and finely chopped fresh parsley
4. Add the chopped ingredients and continue cooking

title: Vagdalék-kádkák (Minced Meat Pastries)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked turkey
- half a veal breast
- six button mushrooms
- twenty shrimp tails and claws
- fourteen decagrams shrimp butter
- three deciliters green peas
- green parsley
- four deciliters sour cream
- one and a half crumbly rolls
- two eggs
- two egg yolks
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked turkey, veal breast, button mushrooms, and shrimp tails and claws into small cubes
2. Make shrimp butter from the shells
3. Stew the button mushrooms with the shrimp butter, green peas, and finely chopped green parsley
4. Add the minced mixture, stew briefly, and season with salt; let cool
5. Pour the sour cream over the crumbly roll crumbs, add two eggs and two egg yolks, and mix well
6. Coat the pastry molds with shrimp butter and fill them with the minced mixture, coat with the crumbly dough, add a little shrimp butter on top, and bake slowly

title: Vagdalékgombóczleves (Ground Meat Dumplings Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- a pair of veal sweetbreads and udder
- a few button mushrooms
- butter
- 3 egg yolks
- milk
- nutmeg
- salt
- egg white
- bread crumbs
- white broken broth

## Directions

1. Blanch the veal sweetbreads and udder slightly, chop finely with a few button mushrooms
2. Melt a piece of butter and put in the chopped mixture
3. Beat 3 egg yolks into a little milk and whisk well
4. Pour over the mixture, add nutmeg and salt, mix well
5. Cut square wafers, brush with egg white, place half a spoon of the mixture on each
6. Form into round dumplings, roll in egg yolk then bread crumbs
7. Fry in hot fat
8. Place in the soup bowl and pour white broken broth over it

title: Vagdalékok Borjúreczében (Minced Meat in Veal Mold)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 38 dekagrams veal
- red onion
- bacon
- leftover roasted turkey or capon if available
- bread soaked in milk
- parsley
- salt
- black pepper
- egg yolks
- eggs
- butter
- veal mold
- roux
- brown stock
- lemon peel
- sour cream
- capers
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Braise the veal with red onion and bacon
2. If there is leftover roasted turkey or capon, add it as well
3. Chop the braised meat with bacon, red onion, roast, and bread soaked in milk and parsley finely together
4. Add salt and ground pepper
5. Mix in two egg yolks and two eggs
6. Butter a pot, place the veal mold in it and then the minced meat
7. Cover the top again with veal mold
8. Cover and bake nicely brown in the oven
9. Make a small roux, thin with brown stock, add a little lemon peel and cook well
10. Finally add two spoonfuls of sour cream, small capers and lemon juice and cook a little more
11. Turn the minced meat out onto a plate, pour the sauce over it, and garnish with small cut-out lemon peel

title: Vaj-fagylalt (Butter Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 dekagrams sugar
- large orange, rind
- 8 egg yolks
- 7 deciliters hot cream

## Directions

1. Finely rub sugar on a large orange
2. Beat well with 8 egg yolks
3. During continuous beating, slowly pour in 7 deciliters hot cream
4. Beat on a gentle fire until it begins to thicken
5. Quickly pour into another pot so it doesn't curdle
6. Beat in that pot until it cools
7. Strain through a sieve
8. Place in the container and treat as with the previous one

title: Vajas pástétom vagdalékkal (Butter Pastry Cake with Filling)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter pastry dough as described in recipe 372
- egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare butter pastry dough as described in recipe 372
2. Roll out to about knife-blade thickness
3. Cut out a round or rectangular piece
4. Brush the outer edge about one finger-width with egg white
5. Roll out another piece of dough similarly
6. Place it on top
7. If the piece is round, cut out small coin-sized pieces with a small doughnut cutter and arrange them on the pastry edge, always so that the second piece covers half of the first
8. If rectangular, cut two-finger-wide strips with a hot knife from the dough and place them on the edge

title: Vajas Pástétom Vagdalékkal (Puff Pastry Patty with Minced Meat Filling)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry dough as described in recipe 372
- egg white
- 28 dekagrams veal
- 3-4 veal sweetbreads
- 6 button mushrooms
- green parsley, finely chopped
- 20-30 shelled crayfish tails
- asparagus or artichoke, boiled in salted water
- nutmeg flower
- butter sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry dough as described in recipe 372 and stretch it to knife-thickness
2. Cut out a round or rectangular piece and brush the outer edge with egg white to one finger's width
3. Stretch another piece of dough similarly and place it on top
4. For round pastry: cut small donut-sized pieces and arrange them on the edge of the pastry, overlapping so the second piece always covers half of the first
5. For rectangular pastry: cut two-finger-width strips from the dough and place on the edge, trimming neatly at the corners
6. Make a round incision in the center of the pastry the size of a drinking glass using a warm knife
7. Place a double button made from dough on top
8. Brush the pastry with egg white and bake in the oven until golden
9. Once baked, cut out the lid with a sharp knife and carefully remove it
10. Quickly extract the dough that spreads between the two pieces
11. Fill the pastry with the prepared filling
12. For the filling: cook 28 dekagrams veal and 3-4 veal sweetbreads, then braise 6 chopped button mushrooms with finely chopped green parsley; separately cut 20-30 cooked crayfish tails and boiled asparagus or artichoke into cubes, and braise these in butter sauce with nutmeg flower until the sauce reduces completely
13. Carefully fill the pastry with the filling using a spoon
14. Replace the lid and serve on the table
15. Alternative: the pastry can also be filled with rabbit or venison instead

title: Vajas tészta-tok (Butter Pastry Case)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter pastry dough
- 8 eggs
- crayfish tails, halved lengthwise
- ham, cut into large cubes

## Directions

1. Prepare the butter pastry dough as described below
2. Roll it out to the thickness of a knife blade
3. Line a square form smoothly and evenly without looking
4. Fill with clean paper and bake quickly
5. Prepare a mixture from 8 eggs to which on fasting day only crayfish tails halved lengthwise are mixed in, or on meat days ham is cut into large cubes and placed in it
6. When the case is baked, remove the paper and turn out from the form
7. Place on a long dish and pour the mixture into it

title: Vajas Tészta-tok (Butter Pastry Cases)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter pastry
- eight eggs
- crayfish tails (on fast days)
- ham cut into large cubes (on meat days)
- pickled tongue slices
- sausage slices
- frog escalope (on fast days)

## Directions

1. Prepare the butter pastry and roll it out to the thickness of a knife blade
2. Place it smoothly into a four-cornered mold without creases
3. Fill with clean paper and bake quickly
4. Meanwhile, prepare a mixture from eight eggs
5. On fast days, mix in crayfish tails cut lengthwise; on meat days, cut ham into large cubes and add
6. Once the case is baked, remove the paper and turn out from the mold
7. Place on a long dish and fill with the egg mixture
8. Arrange the crayfish tails or ham cubes on top
9. Edge the dish rim with slices of pickled tongue and sausage; on fast days use frog escalope instead

title: Vajastorta levelekben (Butter Cake in Layers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry dough
- Egg whites
- 90 grams blanched almonds
- 90 grams finely crushed sugar
- Preserved fruit

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry dough and roll it to knife-blade thickness
2. Cut three equal-sized leaves from the dough
3. Cut beautiful arches into them with a warm knife
4. Brush with egg white, but not all the way to the edges so the dough will rise
5. Chop the blanched almonds finely and mix well with an equal amount of finely crushed sugar
6. Sprinkle the leaves with this mixture and bake at sustained heat
7. Once baked, brush each leaf with preserved fruit and stack them on top of each other
8. Top the final leaf with preserved cherries or decorate with icing

title: Vajastorta őszibaraczkkal (Butter Cake with Peaches)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry dough
- Butter
- Egg white
- 24 peaches
- Finely crushed sugar
- Sugar for syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry dough and work it as described in the previous recipe, rolling it to little finger thickness
2. Cut out a leaf with a warm knife, then cut a smaller leaf from it such that the first has a border two fingers wide
3. Stretch the smaller piece so it matches the crown in size
4. Place on a butter-coated pastry leaf and brush the edge with egg white as far as the crown requires, then place the crown on top, doing this with great care to maintain the round shape
5. Peel 24 beautiful peaches and cut them in half
6. Coat one half with finely crushed sugar and line the bottom of the cake all the way to the crown with these pieces
7. Bake in a moderately cooled oven for half an hour
8. If the ring has risen high, sprinkle it with fine sugar on top and all around and glaze it to a red-golden color with good flame fire which is kindled in one corner of the oven for this purpose
9. Cook the other half of the peaches in a syrup made from 140 grams of sugar until it is jelly-thick
10. Arrange the peaches neatly in the cake and when serving, pour the syrup over it

title: Vajastészta (Butter Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams flour
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- 1 tablespoon white vinegar or 2 tablespoons wine
- water as needed
- salt

## Directions

1. Salt the flour on the kneading board
2. Beat in an egg, two tablespoons of sour cream, and one tablespoon of white vinegar or two tablespoons of wine
3. Add a little water if not wet enough; the dough should be somewhat stiffer than ordinary strudel dough
4. Knead until it separates from the hand, then form into a round loaf
5. Continue kneading while shaping
6. Cover with a cloth and let rest for fifteen minutes
7. Roll out to the thickness of a knife blade
8. Knead 28 dekagrams of butter well and form into a wide sheet
9. Place this in the middle of the dough
10. Fold the dough over it from all four sides
11. Dust the board lightly with flour
12. Beat the dough with a rolling pin and stretch as thin as possible
13. Fold in four parts on the same side and brush off the flour
14. Use minimal flour when dusting to prevent sticking
15. Roll lightly and let rest for fifteen minutes in a cool place (in hot weather place between two plates on ice)
16. Roll out again
17. Repeat this process three times; the fourth time shape as desired

title: Vajastészta másképp (Butter Pastry Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams flour
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 full tablespoon sour cream
- juice of half a lemon
- 28 dekagrams butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Salt 28 dekagrams of flour on the kneading board and divide into two parts
2. From one half make a light dough, stiffer than strudel dough
3. Add one egg yolk, one full tablespoon sour cream, and juice of half a lemon
4. Knead until it separates from the hand
5. Shape into a loaf and set aside
6. Into the other half of flour work 28 dekagrams of butter and stretch with the rolling pin until the butter and flour are completely united
7. Roll the dough to knife-blade width
8. Shape the butter into a long piece and place in the middle of the dough
9. Fold the dough over from all four sides
10. Beat wide with the rolling pin and stretch as thin as possible
11. Fold together again
12. Repeat this three times, using as little flour as possible when folding
13. The fourth time stretch thin and use as desired

title: Vanilia-fagylalt (Vanilla Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 21 decagrams sugar
- 1.5 grams ground vanilla
- 8 egg yolks
- 7 deciliters boiled cream

## Directions

1. In a pan, mix well the sugar, ground vanilla, and egg yolks
2. Pour on 7 deciliters of boiled cream and mix well
3. Place on a charcoal fire and stir continuously until it begins to thicken
4. Quickly remove from fire, pour into another pan, and stir for a while longer to prevent curdling
5. When cooled, press through a fine sieve, pour into the ice cream mold, and proceed as with other ice creams

title: Vanilia-parfait (Vanilla Cream Frozen Dessert)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 vanilla pod, finely chopped
- 1.6 liters good sweet cream
- 18 egg yolks
- 35 dekagrams finely crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Chop 1 vanilla pod finely and cook with 1.6 liters good sweet cream for eight minutes, then remove to cool.
2. In the meantime, beat 18 egg yolks with 35 dekagrams finely crushed sugar for a quarter hour.
3. Slowly pour the vanilla cream into the egg mixture.
4. Beat over charcoal fire with a whisk until the eggs thicken or the mixture begins to boil.
5. Remove from fire, strain through silk sieve, and continue stirring until completely cooled.
6. Fill the mold with the mixture and freeze until it reaches the firmness of frozen butter. For smaller tables, the above quantities should be used proportionally.

title: Vanilia-sodó (Vanilla Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 liter cream
- 16 dekagrams sugar
- little vanilla
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Put 0.5 liter cream on heat with 16 dekagrams sugar and a little vanilla and bring to a boil
2. Mix 2 egg yolks with 1 tablespoon flour
3. Pour the hot cream over it while continuously stirring
4. Return to heat and bring to a boil while continuously stirring

title: Vanilia-sürűlék (Vanilla Custard)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- seven egg yolks
- seven decilitres of cream
- sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Make a thin batter from seven egg yolks
2. Bring seven decilitres of cream to a boil and add a piece of sugar and a little pounded vanilla
3. When it is boiling, carefully pour in the batter, boil for a few minutes, and remove with a skimming spoon
4. When everything has been cooked this way, pile it up high in a deep plate and sprinkle every layer with sugar, almonds, and finely pounded vanilla
5. Crown the top with small, thinly sliced almonds and brown it nicely in the oven
6. When baked, pour vanilla custard (crème) around the edge of the plate

title: Vanilia-ívek (Vanilla Arches)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagramm finely ground sugar
- 14 dekagramm ground and swollen almonds
- a small amount finely chopped lemon zest
- 1 teaspoon ground vanilla
- 2 dekagramm fine flour
- 2 egg whites, stiffly beaten

## Directions

1. Mix well 14 dekagramm finely ground sugar, 14 dekagramm ground and swollen almonds, a small amount finely chopped lemon zest, 1 teaspoon ground vanilla, 2 dekagramm fine flour, and 2 stiffly beaten egg whites
2. Spread the mixture onto a wax-coated baking sheet
3. Place in a not very hot oven
4. When nicely golden, cut into square pieces
5. Quickly bend over a small wooden roller
6. Repeat until all are bent, then place in a warm location so they do not soften

title: Vaniliás koszorúcskák (Vanilla Wreaths)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 dekagrams sugar
- 18 dekagrams fine flour
- 14 dekagrams butter
- 7 dekagrams blanched almonds, soaked
- 7 dekagrams sugar
- a little vanilla
- egg for glazing
- apricot syrup
- candied cherries for garnish

## Directions

1. Mix 10 dekagrams of sugar with 18 dekagrams of fine flour on a board
2. Work 14 dekagrams of butter into the flour and sugar with a rolling pin until the butter absorbs the flour
3. Work repeatedly with a wide knife until fine dough forms, adding no liquid
4. Roll the dough to knife-blade thickness
5. Cut small wreaths with a doughnut cutter and make a small hole in the center with a small cutter to form a wreath shape
6. Finely chop 7 dekagrams of soaked almonds and finely grind the same amount of sugar with a little vanilla, mix together
7. Brush the wreaths with beaten egg and sprinkle densely with the almond and sugar mixture
8. Brush the side of the wreaths not covered with almonds with apricot syrup
9. Place another wreath on top
10. Pile high on a plate and place candied cherries in the opening of the wreaths
11. Pretzels can also be made from this dough, sprinkled with almonds and sugar

title: Vaniliás rudacska theához (Vanilla Rod for Tea)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pieces grated chocolate
- powdered sugar
- shelled almonds finely chopped
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/2 lemon juice
- crushed sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Measure 3 pieces grated chocolate by the cup
2. Take the same amount of powdered sugar and the same amount of finely chopped shelled almonds
3. Work together with 3 egg yolks and 1/2 lemon juice until very stiff
4. Place on crushed sugar so it does not stick to the board
5. Flatten with a sugared knife to finger thickness
6. With a sugared knife cut into finger-thick rods
7. Place on a buttered and floured pan and bake at gentle heat

title: Vanilla Hair Pomade
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram fresh mutton tallow
- 75 dekagrams vegetable oil
- 25 dekagrams hair powder
- 7 dekagrams Peruvian balsam
- 10 dekagrams vanilla tincture

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Vanília-Kifli (Vanilla Croissant)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 280 grams butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 70 grams sugar
- finely ground vanilla
- 36 grams melted cheese yeast
- 2 deciliters lukewarm sour cream
- salt
- flour
- apricots or currants
- egg white
- sugar

## Directions

1. Whip 280 grams butter until foamy and mix in 8 egg yolks one by one
2. Add 70 grams sugar, finely ground vanilla, 36 grams melted cheese yeast, 2 deciliters lukewarm sour cream, little salt and flour
3. Beat lightly and let the dough rise
4. Place on a flour-sprinkled board, roll out and cut out triangular leaves from it
5. Fill with apricots or currants and fold together, brush with egg white, sprinkle with sugar and serve at table

title: Vanília-kocsonya ostyával berakva (Vanilla Jelly with Wafers)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- vanilla jelly (from previous recipe)
- small wafers
- 6-7 wafer sheet slices

## Directions

1. Pour 2 centimeters height of vanilla jelly into a small round mold
2. Place in cracked ice and freeze
3. Meanwhile, bake small wafers and 6-7 slices from wafer sheet batter
4. Place the pot with remaining jelly on ice and semi-freeze
5. When the jelly in the mold has frozen, place a round wafer sheet on it
6. Spread a spoonful of semi-frozen jelly on it
7. Continue this way until all mixture is filled in
8. Freeze firmly, turn out onto a plate, and arrange in 3 rows with small wafers

title: Vaníliás kocsonya (Vanilla Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters good cream
- 18 dekagrams sugar
- 1.5 grams vanilla
- 9 egg yolks
- 3.5 dekagrams dissolved isinglass

## Directions

1. Boil 7 deciliters of good cream and remove from heat
2. Cool completely
3. Finely pound 18 dekagrams of sugar with 1.5 grams of vanilla
4. Mix this in a pot with 9 egg yolks
5. Slowly add the cream, blending well
6. Place on charcoal fire and stir constantly until it begins to thicken
7. Transfer to a clean pot and stir until cooled to prevent curdling
8. Add 3.5 dekagrams of dissolved isinglass
9. Mix well, pour into mold, and freeze on ice

title: Vaníliás tej-kocsonya (Vanilla Milk Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagrams sugar
- Broken piece of vanilla
- 14 dekagrams egg yolks
- 2 deciliters boiled heavy cream

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar with broken piece of vanilla and fourteen dekagrams of egg yolks
2. Slowly pour two deciliters of boiled heavy cream onto it and beat well until it begins to thicken
3. Remove the pan from heat
4. Pour the milk jelly into a cup and cool it
5. Sprinkle well with finely broken sugar and brown with a hot spatula

title: Vaníliás tej-kocsonya (Vanilla Milk Jelly)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 dekagrams sugar
- ground vanilla pod
- 14 dekagrams egg yolks
- 2 deciliters heavy cream, boiled

## Directions

1. Beat together the sugar, ground vanilla, and egg yolks
2. Slowly pour in the boiled heavy cream and beat well until it begins to thicken
3. Remove the pan from heat, pour the milk jelly into cups, and let it cool
4. Sprinkle generously with finely ground sugar and brown it with a hot spatula

title: Vaníliás Táblácskák (Vanilla Tablets)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fifty-six dekagrams fine sugar
- three grams vanilla
- five eggs
- zest of one orange, finely chopped
- fifty-six dekagrams finest flour

## Directions

1. Grind the fine sugar with vanilla very finely
2. Add five eggs and the finely chopped orange zest to the mixture
3. Beat everything together for one hour
4. Add the fifty-six dekagrams finest flour to the dough
5. Place the dough on a floured board
6. Roll out to the thickness of two knife-blade widths
7. Press the necessary form onto it and cut through with a knife
8. Place the tablets on a tray greased with wax
9. Bake at gentle heat for ten minutes, so that their color remains white

title: Vaníliás-víz (Vanilla Water)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 dekagrams good vanilla pods
- 7 dekagrams Peruvian balsam
- 3.5 dekagrams cinnamon
- 4 grams bergamot oil
- 1.5 liters 72° alcohol
- 4 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Pour 1.5 liters 72° alcohol and 4 deciliters water over the vanilla pods, Peruvian balsam, cinnamon, and bergamot oil
2. Allow to digest

title: Vegyes Befőtt (Mixed Preserved Fruit)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram sweet, juicy black cherries, pitted
- half kilogram morello cherries
- half kilogram sugar

## Directions

1. Pit half a kilogram of sweet, juicy black cherries and similarly pit half a kilogram of morello cherries.

title: Vegyes Befőtt (Mixed Preserved Fruit)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half kilogram sweet, juicy black cherries
- half kilogram sour cherries
- half kilogram sugar
- one glass of water
- half kilogram raspberries

## Directions

1. Pit the black cherries and sour cherries, collecting their juices. Cook the sugar with one glass of water, skimming it until completely clear. Add the cherries and their juices, and cook for one hour. Add the raspberries and cook with the other fruit until a drop tested on a plate no longer runs but stays in place. Remove from heat and pour into dry, pre-warmed glasses so they do not crack. Leave the glass open for twenty-four hours, then seal with paper.

title: Vegyes halétek (Mixed Fish Dishes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- various fish: pike, carp, sterlet, sturgeon, zander
- salt
- yellow roux
- red onion
- red wine
- water
- fresh parsley
- green onion
- lemon zest
- cloves

## Directions

1. Clean various fish such as pike, carp, sterlet, sturgeon and zander, and let them lie in salt for half an hour.
2. Cut them into pieces.
3. Prepare a little yellow roux, place a red onion cut in half in it.
4. Mix with half red wine and half water.
5. Add fresh parsley, green onion, a little lemon zest and cloves, and cook the sauce well.
6. Add the fish and simmer slowly.
7. Once cooked, place on a plate, strain the sauce, but it must cook well, and place triangular fried bread slices on the edge of the plate.

title: Vegyes kompot aszalt gyümölcsökből (Mixed Compote with Dried Fruits)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried Spanish cherries or small prune plums
- Dried autumn apricots
- Dried and pressed pears
- Water
- Sugar
- A little lemon peel

## Directions

1. Cook the dried Spanish cherries or prune plums, dried autumn apricots, and dried and pressed pears in water with sugar and a little lemon peel
2. When serving, pile the cherries or prune plums high in the middle, then arrange the apricots and pears around them, keeping their stems intact

title: Vegyes Kompót (Mixed Compote)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- large white cherries
- sugar
- water
- lemon juice
- large sour cherries
- small pears
- rum
- large peaches

## Directions

1. Stew large white cherries in sugared water and lemon juice
2. Remove the stems from large sour cherries and stew them separately in water and sugar
3. Peel small pears that ripen at the same time and stew them separately in water and sugar without stems
4. When soft, add a spoonful of rum to them, simmer for a few more minutes and cool
5. Boil 9 decagrams of sugar with a cup of water until the sugar begins to thread
6. Clean 5 large peaches, cut them in half, remove the pits, dip them in the sugar, and boil until the sugar coats them completely, being careful they do not fall apart
7. When serving the compote, place the white cherries in the center of the dish, piled high, arrange the sour cherries in a wreath around the cherries, arrange the peaches around this, and finally the small pears, which are left whole, and to keep them beautifully white, brush them with lemon juice after peeling; these form the outer wreath, arranged so their stems point upward

title: Vegyes Kompót Más Módon (Mixed Compote Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- emperor pears
- water
- sugar
- lemon juice
- large autumn peaches
- serviceberries
- oranges
- vanilla liqueur
- isinglass

## Directions

1. Cook larger pears such as emperor pears, peel them, cut them in half, and stew them with water, sugar, and lemon juice
2. Boil 10 decagrams of sugar with a coffee cup of water until it threads, peel 5 large autumn peaches, cut them in half, and add them, being careful they do not overcook but remain whole
3. Cook 4 deciliters of serviceberries in water with enough sugar for sweetness
4. Peel the zest from 2 oranges and cut it into long strips, boil this in water until soft
5. In a pan, boil 9 decagrams of sugar with a little water until it threads, drain the orange zest, and with a fork dip it carefully in the thickened sugar so it does not break, bring to a boil for a moment and cool
6. When serving, make the first wreath from the pears, the second from the peaches, pile the serviceberries in the middle, place the orange zest on the edge of the pears, and if well set, stand it up straight with a fork
7. Finally, mix a coffee cup of good vanilla liqueur with half a cup of water, a little sugar, and 1.5 decagrams dissolved isinglass, strain this through cheesecloth, freeze it well, and when set, turn it out and cut small half-moons from it, arranging these on the compote in neat order

title: Vegyes tejhab (Mixed Cream Foam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter good heavy cream
- ground sugar
- vanilla
- freshly pressed raspberry juice
- 10 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- a little egg white
- strong, cooled black coffee
- sugar
- 4.5 dekagrams isinglass

## Directions

1. Place 1 liter of good heavy cream in a large brass preserving basin, set on ice or cool in a good cellar
2. Beat with a whip until the entire mass becomes thick foam
3. Divide into three parts
4. Leave the first part white and add enough ground sugar and vanilla to make it sufficiently sweet
5. Mix the second part quickly with freshly pressed raspberry juice, 10 dekagrams finely ground sugar, and a little egg white
6. Mix the third part with strong, previously cooled black coffee and enough sugar to make it sufficiently sweet (2-3 tablespoons of coffee with a third of the foam is enough to give it the proper brown color and good coffee flavor)
7. Dissolve isinglass, strain it, cool it, and divide it among the three parts
8. Pour the coffee foam first into a round mold or basin and set on ice
9. While the first is freezing, place the other two foams in a cool place
10. When the first has frozen, pour the red foam on top, freeze again, and finally add the white foam
11. Before serving, dip the mold briefly in warm water and carefully turn the foam out onto a plate

title: Velőgombóczleves (Marrow Dumplings Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams bone marrow
- 2 rolls
- milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- chicken broth

## Directions

1. Clean the bone marrow and cut one-third into cubes and the rest into slices
2. Place in a bowl to melt and beat slightly until foamy
3. Add 2 grated rolls soaked in milk, 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks one after another
4. Add the marrow cubes as well
5. Mix everything together well
6. Form small dumplings and cook them in chicken broth
7. Once cooked through, serve

title: Velőgombóczleves más módon (Marrow Dumplings Soup, Another Way)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 rolls
- milk
- 10 dekagrams bacon, finely chopped
- green parsley, finely chopped
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- fine bread crumbs
- bone marrow

## Directions

1. Soak 1 1/2 rolls in milk
2. Finely chop bacon and crush together with finely chopped green parsley
3. Mix in 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
