4. Season with salt
5. Squeeze out the bread well, chop finely, and mix with the other ingredients
6. Finally add enough fine bread crumbs so the mixture holds together
7. Clean the bone marrow designated for this well, rinse in water, and chop into hazelnut-sized pieces
8. Form small dumplings and place a piece of marrow in the center of each
9. Cook in hot broth until the marrow is cooked through

title: Vereshagyma-mártás (Red Onion Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two heads red onion
- one coffee spoon crushed sugar
- rendered fat
- strong good vinegar
- stock

## Directions

1. Coarsely chop two heads of red onion and put with one coffee spoon of crushed sugar into a small amount of melted fat
2. Brown it nicely
3. Thin with stock
4. Add one tablespoon of strong good vinegar
5. Cook well and pass through a sieve

title: Veresrépa-mártás (Beet Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon flour
- two deciliters sour cream
- one part beef stock
- one part cooked beets
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix one tablespoon of flour with two deciliters of sour cream
2. Add one part beef stock, one part cooked beets, vinegar and sugar
3. Cut the cooked beets into cubes or long pieces and add them shortly before serving
4. Bring to a boil once or twice and it is ready
5. This sauce should not cook long as it will otherwise lose its beautiful color

title: Vert élesztős tészta (Beaten Yeast Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams fine flour
- 2 deciliters cream
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 dekagram dissolved yeast
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- salt
- 28 dekagrams firm butter
- preserved apricots, currants or poppy seeds for filling
- egg white
- ground sugar
- almonds

## Directions

1. Divide the flour into two parts
2. From the larger part, prepare dough on a board with cream, egg yolks, dissolved yeast, sugar and salt; this dough should be firmer than puff pastry
3. Knead it with your hand until it separates from the hand, form into a ball and set aside covered with a cloth
4. Salt the other half of the flour, place firm butter in the middle, cover with flour and smooth with a rolling pin until the flour and butter are completely mixed, being careful not to touch the butter but always separating it with a wide knife and stretching it into a long strip
5. When done, place the dough on a board dusted with flour, roll out to the thickness of two knife-backs, place the butter in the middle and fold the dough over it from all four sides
6. Roll out and fold together four times like puff pastry
7. Note that this dough should not rest but should be rolled out and folded quickly one after another
8. This dough is especially good for pot pies and yeast cake, but particularly for rolls: roll out to knife-back thickness, cut out round leaves with a large cutter and brush the edges with egg white
9. Fill with preserved apricot, currants or poppy seeds, fold together and form into roll shapes
10. Place on a baking sheet and let rise slightly
11. In the meantime, blanch almonds, dry and chop finely and mix with an equal amount of ground sugar
12. When the rolls have risen, brush with egg yolk, sprinkle thickly with the almond sugar and when baked, sprinkle again with sugar and serve on the table

title: Vese velővel (Kidneys with Marrow)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kidneys
- marrow, cleaned
- hot fat
- salt
- pepper
- eggs (one per kidney used)

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the kidneys, remove the stones by cutting them out, then grate them and place in hot fat together with the cleaned marrow. Salt them and for each kidney used, beat one egg over them. Finally sprinkle with pepper and serve.

title: Vesepecsenye Angol Módra (Kidney Roast, English Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney
- salt
- wine
- vinegar
- water
- lemon zest
- bay leaf
- cloves
- red onion
- pepper
- capers (3 dekagrams)
- sardelles
- bacon
- parsley
- sour cream (2 deciliters)

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the kidney, wash it thoroughly, and salt it
2. Prepare a marinade with wine, vinegar, water, lemon zest, bay leaf, and a red onion studded with a few cloves
3. Boil the marinade and pour it hot over the kidney with a few peppercorns
4. Leave it overnight in the marinade
5. The next day, braise it until very tender using the method previously described
6. Finely chop capers, lemon, cleaned sardelles, a small piece of bacon, parsley, and one onion head taken from the marinade
7. Fry this mixture in butter and add it to the meat
8. Add 2 deciliters of sour cream
9. Braise gently, skim off the fat
10. Place the kidney on a serving dish and strain the sauce over it

title: Vesepecsenye Apró Burgonyával (Kidney with Small Potatoes)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney
- salt
- bacon
- red onion
- carrot
- small potatoes (walnut-sized, peeled)
- flour (2 spoons)
- broth
- vinegar
- lemon zest
- sour cream (2 full spoons)

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the kidney, salt it, and lard it nicely
2. Place bacon, sliced red onion, and sliced carrot in a pot, with the meat on top
3. Braise slowly, turning frequently so the meat does not brown too much and softens on all sides
4. Add peeled, walnut-sized potatoes
5. When the meat and potatoes are browned and tender, remove them
6. Sprinkle flour (2 spoons) over the braised vegetables, brown it, and fill with broth
7. Add a little vinegar and lemon zest and cook the sauce well
8. Skim off the fat and strain through a sieve
9. Put the meat and potatoes back in, add 2 full spoons of sour cream and cook well
10. When serving, arrange the potatoes like a crown around the edge of the plate and place the kidney in the center

title: Vesepecsenye nyúl módjára sütve (Kidney Roast Prepared Like Rabbit)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney
- brine used for rabbit
- salt
- bacon slices
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove the membrane from the kidney and cut off the bones
2. Marinate as one would marinate rabbit
3. Lard in four rows
4. Roast for two hours over steady heat
5. Baste frequently with the brine during roasting
6. Before fully cooked, baste several times with sour cream to give it a nice colour
7. Prepare the same sauce as for rabbit, with which capers may be mixed

title: Vesepecsenye-szeletek (Kidney Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney
- salt
- bacon
- red onion
- carrot
- clove pepper
- flour
- vinegar
- sour cream
- anchovies
- capers
- lemon zest
- hazelnuts

## Directions

1. Clean the meaty part of the kidney well from skin and fat
2. Wash it and cut it into small pieces
3. Salt it and shape round slices with a knife
4. Place bacon, red onion, and carrot cut into slices in a pot and line the bottom of the pot with them
5. Place the slices in the pot with several clove peppers and braise covered until they become a nice light brown color
6. Remove them and sprinkle a spoonful of flour on top
7. Add two spoonfuls of vinegar and the same amount of sour cream
8. Cook well and strain through a sieve
9. Soak two anchovies in water and remove their bones
10. Chop two decagrams of capers, a small head of red onion, and a hazelnut-sized piece of red onion finely together
11. Add this to the sauce, place the slices back in, and cook well

title: Vetrecze (Reheated Beef Roast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover beef roast
- hot fat
- cold water
- vinegar
- pepper
- onions

## Directions

1. Place thinly sliced leftover beef roast into hot fat
2. Season with salt and add a little cold water and several spoonfuls of vinegar
3. When the liquid has reduced and the fat begins to fry, add a little pepper and several onions sliced thinly
4. When the onions begin to brown, it is ready to serve
5. Serve with potato puree

title: Vágott Mandola-ostya (Cut Almond Wafer)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- sugar
- 1 egg
- milk
- 28 dekagramm swollen and cut almonds
- 28 dekagramm finely ground sugar
- 3 egg whites, stiffly beaten
- lemon juice
- 1 lemon zest finely chopped
- 1 orange zest rubbed with sugar
- 7 dekagramm candied citron cut into small cubes
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a dough from a small amount of flour, sugar, 1 egg, and milk and bake leaves like a wafer
2. Stack these on top of each other and place a plate on top so they stay straight
3. Mix together 28 dekagramm swollen and cut almonds and 28 dekagramm finely ground sugar with 3 stiffly beaten egg whites, a little lemon juice, the finely chopped zest of 1 lemon, the zest of 1 orange rubbed with sugar, 7 dekagramm candied citron cut into small cubes, and a little ground cinnamon
4. Mix all well together
5. Cut the baked wafers into beautiful rounds
6. Coat with the dough
7. Bake on a sheet to a beautiful light yellow and immediately bend onto round wood

title: Vágott Metélt-Lepény (Chopped Noodle Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams soaked almonds
- lemon juice
- 21 dekagrams powdered sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- 1 egg
- flour
- lard
- cinnamon or lemon zest

## Directions

1. Finely grind half of the soaked almonds and pour lemon juice over them
2. Cut the other half into long, fine pieces
3. Place powdered sugar in a bowl, add the finely ground almonds and 6 egg yolks, and stir together for half an hour
4. Beat 3 egg whites into stiff peaks and fold into the mixture
5. Prepare a chopped noodle dough from 1 egg, 1 egg yolk, sugar, and flour
6. Fry the noodles in lard until golden
7. Break the noodles into small pieces and mix with the almond pieces
8. Season with cinnamon or lemon zest and mix well
9. Coat a flat baking dish with butter and line it with puff pastry
10. Fill with the almond mixture
11. Cover with rounds of puff pastry
12. Bake slowly until golden and crispy
13. Turn out onto a serving plate and sprinkle with sugar

title: Vágott metélt-lepény (Cut Noodle Cake)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty-eight dekagrams softened almonds
- Lemon juice
- Twenty-one dekagrams broken sugar
- Six egg yolks
- Three egg whites
- One egg
- One egg yolk
- Sugar
- Flour
- Fat for frying

## Directions

1. Grind half of twenty-eight dekagrams softened almonds finely and pour lemon juice over it
2. Cut the other half into long, fine pieces
3. Put twenty-one dekagrams broken sugar in a bowl, add the finely ground almonds and yolks of six eggs
4. Stir together for half an hour
5. Mix in the stiffly beaten whites of three eggs
6. Prepare cut noodles from one egg, one egg yolk, sugar, and flour
7. Fry in fat until beautiful golden

title: Véres hurka (Blood Sausage)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- throat roast
- lungs
- larynx
- small amount of rendered intestine fat
- cooked millet porridge
- salt
- black pepper, ground
- parboiled red onion
- marjoram
- hot water

## Directions

1. Chop together the throat roast, blood vessel pieces from the lungs, and larynx while warm
2. Add small amount of rendered intestine fat
3. Mix in cooked millet porridge in proportion to the meat and fat
4. Season with salt, abundant black pepper, parboiled red onion and marjoram
5. Add hot water by spoonfuls to make the filling thin and easy to stuff
6. Fill the intestine casings with the mixture using a sausage stuffer
7. Seal both ends of the sausage with small wooden sticks
8. Cook in hot salted water
9. Test for doneness by piercing with a straw; when clear fat rather than red water comes out, the sausage is cooked

title: White Turnip (Fehérrépa)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young white turnips
- butter
- crushed sugar
- brown broth
- glace
- ham

## Directions

1. Peel the young white turnips, wash them and cut each into five or six equal parts shaped like crescents
2. These slices should be the length and thickness of a small finger
3. In melted butter brown two spoonfuls of crushed sugar until golden
4. Add the turnips and when they are well mixed with the sugar, pour half a serving spoon of brown broth over them
5. Shake well and braise until the turnips are soft but do not fall apart
6. Finally add a serving spoon of glace and cook this into a large sauce
7. Turn the turnips on all sides, pour the little sauce into a plate, arrange the turnips on it in triple wreath form with a fork, and garnish the edge of the plate with finely chopped ham

title: Wine Soup (Borleves)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter good white wine
- three deciliters water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- six egg yolks
- three tablespoons sweet sour cream
- fine bread pieces
- fine sugar
- caramelized sugar

## Directions

1. Mix one liter of good white wine with three deciliters of water so it is not too strong
2. Add sugar and cinnamon
3. Cook for half an hour
4. Before serving, remove the spices
5. Beat six egg yolks in a pot and add three full tablespoons of sweet sour cream
6. Beat well and pour the wine in slowly and gradually
7. Place in a soup bowl and add finely cut, generously sprinkled with fine sugar, and caramelized (the melted sugar is like glass) bread pieces with a hot spatula
8. Quickly bring the soup to the table so the bread does not soften

title: Zeller-saláta (Celery Salad)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- cress (Kresse), endive, or rampion
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Cut celery into slices
2. Cook it and place in a bowl
3. Wash the cress (Kresse), endive, or rampion and place on a sieve on top
4. Prepare with vinegar and oil

title: Zellerleves (Celery Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one celery
- fat or butter
- nutmeg flower
- sugar
- meat stock
- egg yolks
- sweet sour cream
- bread

## Directions

1. Cut one celery into small thin cubes
2. Put a small piece of fat or butter in a pot and when hot add the celery and braise it slowly until soft
3. When soft, pass through a sieve, bring to a boil with little nutmeg flower, one piece of sugar, and little meat stock
4. In the soup bowl mix 3 egg yolks with 3/4 liter of sweet sour cream, pour the celery with hot meat stock over it on fried bread cubes or pea pasta

title: Zsemle-pudding Bor-sodóval (Roll Pudding with Wine Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- two rolls, grated peel
- milk
- ten dekagrams of soaked almonds
- fourteen dekagrams of raisins and small dried grapes
- seven dekagrams of butter
- two whole eggs and four egg yolks
- three egg whites
- citron or orange peel
- sugar
- white paper

## Directions

1. Soak the grated peel of two rolls in milk
2. Crush ten dekagrams of soaked almonds
3. Clean fourteen dekagrams of raisins and small dried grapes
4. Beat seven dekagrams of butter until fluffy
5. Squeeze the rolls well, chop finely and add to the butter
6. Add two whole eggs and four egg yolks one after another and stir for a quarter hour
7. Fold in the stiff whites of three eggs
8. Add the almonds, raisins and small grapes
9. Add a piece of sugar rubbed with citron or orange peel and mix well
10. Line a pot with butter, cut a white paper to fit the bottom and butter it
11. Arrange attractively with raisins and soaked almonds on the bottom
12. Add the pudding mixture and cook in steam for one hour
13. Turn the pudding onto a plate and carefully remove the paper
14. Pour the prepared wine sauce around it
15. For the sauce: crush sugar rubbed with citron, put in a pot with ten egg yolks, mix with twenty tablespoons of good white wine and place over glowing coals
16. Stir the sauce continuously until it thickens and rises, then remove quickly from the fire and pour onto the plate

title: Zsemlepástétom-Vagdalék (Breadroll Pâté Hash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- round rolls the size of pastries
- eggs
- fine breadcrumbs
- veal sweetbreads
- veal meat
- mushrooms
- butter
- fresh parsley
- artichokes or asparagus
- salted water
- white meat broth
- shrimp tails and legs
- buttered sauce

## Directions

1. Lightly grate the crust of round rolls the size of pastries
2. Cut a small crown-sized lid in the center and hollow out the interior completely
3. Dip the roll in slightly salted egg, roll in fine breadcrumbs and quickly fry until nicely golden
4. Prepare minced veal from veal sweetbreads and veal meat, cutting into pea-sized cubes
5. Cut six cleaned mushrooms into similar cubes and braise with butter and fresh parsley
6. Cook artichokes or asparagus in salted water and set aside the small roses and tips
7. Keep warm in white meat broth
8. Clean 25-30 shrimp tails and legs, set aside the most beautiful tails, chop the rest along with the artichokes and asparagus into cubes
9. Prepare a buttered sauce, set aside half when done
10. Into the other half, add the veal meat, sweetbreads, mushrooms, artichokes, and shrimp
11. Cook the sauce well and fill the fried rolls with this mixture

title: Zsemlepástétomok Borral (Roll Pastries with Wine)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 small round rolls
- 10 dekagrams almonds
- 10 dekagrams finely ground sugar
- 1 lemon, finely chopped peel
- 1 whole egg
- 3 egg yolks
- 7 deciliters red wine, well sweetened
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Grate the crust of small round rolls, cut a small lid, and hollow out the center completely.
2. Fry the rolls in fat.
3. Mix 10 dekagrams almonds and 10 dekagrams finely ground sugar with the finely chopped peel of 1 lemon.
4. Beat in 1 whole egg and 3 egg yolks until frothy.
5. Fill the rolls with this mixture and replace the lids.
6. Place the rolls in a pan with 7 deciliters of well-sweetened red wine.
7. Place embers on top and braise until the rolls have absorbed all the wine but are not dried out.

title: Zsemlés torma-mártás (Bread Horseradish Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one bread roll, crust
- good stock
- fresh butter
- grated horseradish

## Directions

1. Grate the crust of one bread roll
2. Soften it in good stock and cook well
3. Smooth it so that no lumps remain
4. Melt a small amount of fresh butter and add the finely cooked bread roll together with grated horseradish to it
5. Cook for several more minutes

title: Zsigerleves (Lamb Offal Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb liver
- lamb lungs
- vegetables
- parsley
- carrot
- salt
- white roux
- fresh green parsley
- hard-boiled eggs
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Wash the lamb liver and lungs and put on to cook
2. Add vegetables, parsley, carrot, and a little salt
3. When cooked, strain the broth and chop the lungs and liver finely
4. Make a white roux, add finely chopped fresh green parsley, pour in the broth and add the chopped lungs and liver
5. Hard-boil a pair of eggs, chop them together, add to the soup and let it come to a boil
6. Sour cream can be added when serving

title: Zöld borsó párban (Green Peas in a Water Bath)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fine small sugar peas
- salt
- 28 dekagrams sugar
- 8 deciliters water

## Directions

1. Wash the peas and salt them
2. After half an hour, dry them with a clean cloth
3. Fill the jars, but not completely full
4. Boil the sugar with eight deciliters of water for about six jars of peas until dissolved
5. When the peas are filled in the jars and the sugar water has cooled, pour enough of it over the peas to just cover them
6. Tie the jars closed and place them in a water bath for two minutes

title: Zöld borsót szárítani (Dried Green Peas)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 deciliters green peas
- 1 tablespoon finely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Calculate one tablespoon of finely ground sugar per seven deciliters of peas
2. Braise the peas with the sugar in a pan until the peas, which release all their moisture during braising, become completely dry
3. Stir the peas continuously during braising
4. Spread them in a thin layer on strong paper, preferably sugar paper
5. Dry them in the oven after bread baking in an oven that is no longer very hot, without letting the peas brown
6. Use them in winter as fresh peas

title: Zöld Mandolát Befőzni (Preserved Green Almonds)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds when still soft
- half kilogram sugar
- almonds equal in quantity to the sugar
- one part white vinegar
- two parts water
- one glass of water
- silver spoon

## Directions

1. Preserve almonds when their kernel is still soft. For half kilogram of sugar, take an equal amount of almonds. Clean them with cloth so the coarse part comes off. Cook them in one part white vinegar and two parts water until they can easily be pierced with a pin, then place on a sieve to dry. Cook the sugar with one glass of water until it threads, then cook the almonds in it for a quarter hour, turning them with a silver spoon on all sides so they absorb the sugar well. Remove them and cook the syrup a bit longer. Place the almonds in jars, pour the slightly cooled syrup over them, leave the jars open, and for four days every twenty-four hours bring the syrup to a boil. On the fifth day, when the preserve is completely cool, seal the jars.

title: Zöld olaszdiót befőzni (Preserved Green Walnuts)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- half-ripe walnuts
- sugar (equal weight to walnuts)
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take half-ripe walnuts, which can be cut through very easily.
2. Prick each walnut several times with a feather knife.
3. Place the walnuts immediately in fresh well water, and change the fresh water daily several times, cleaning them of the coarse flavor until the water remains clear.
4. Boil the walnuts in the water for as long as you boil soft-boiled eggs.
5. Take as much sugar as the walnuts.
6. Place the walnuts immediately into the sugar with several split cloves and broken cinnamon, and boil until they are completely softened.
7. Remove the scum from it, then remove the walnuts; boil the syrup a bit more, during which place the walnuts in the glass, and when the syrup has cooled slightly, pour it over them, and place shavings crosswise on top so the syrup is always above the fruit.
8. Finally, tie it with paper, pierced through with a needle.

title: Zöld paszuly (Green Bean)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tender green beans
- fat
- parsley
- red onion, small amount
- garlic, small amount
- sour cream
- pork cutlet for garnish

## Directions

1. Clean the tender green beans and cut into slices
2. Blanch with hot water and set aside
3. Heat two full spoonfuls of fat
4. Add good amount of parsley and small amount of chopped red and garlic onion
5. Add the beans if well drained
6. Braise until tender with frequent stirring
7. Dust with a small amount of flour and braise further
8. Pour on a small amount of good broth and sufficient sour cream
9. Garnish with pork cutlet

title: Zöld paszulyt (babot) télre megtartani (Preserved Green Beans for Winter)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tender beans
- water
- 2 parts white vinegar
- 1 part water
- 14 dekagrams salt per 2 liters
- fresh beef fat or butter

## Directions

1. Clean the tender beans by removing the strings
2. Bring water to a boil in a pot and add the beans
3. Cook for a short quarter hour, then drain quickly and place in cold water
4. When completely cooled, remove and spread on clean cloth to dry
5. When dry, place in an earthenware pot
6. Take two parts water and one part white vinegar with fourteen dekagrams salt per two liters
7. Boil the beans only a few times so they don't become too soft
8. Boil the water only until the salt is completely dissolved, then remove from heat
9. When cooled, pour over the beans
10. Pour melted fresh beef fat or butter over it so that the fat forms a hard protective layer that air cannot penetrate
11. When the fat has hardened, tie the pots closed and store in a cool place
12. When using, take out as much as needed and place in lukewarm water an hour before use
13. Close the pot with freshly melted lard, otherwise the rest would spoil

title: Zöld és fehér kartifiola (Green and White Cauliflower)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- meat broth, well salted
- butter sauce
- ham roll
- thin bacon slice

## Directions

1. After cleaning the cauliflower and cutting the stem short, cook it in well-salted meat broth so that the florets remain whole and beautiful
2. Prepare a small amount of butter sauce
3. Cook the cut cauliflower stems in meat broth until so tender they can be pressed through a fine sieve
4. Mix the pressed stems with the butter sauce
5. Bring to a boil for a few minutes so it looks like light baby puree
6. Place this mixture on the bottom of the plate
7. Arrange the green cauliflower florets in the center
8. Place the white florets closely beside them so they form a cone shape about a finger-long
9. Insert the end of the ham roll with a knife under the floret
10. The thin bacon slice comes up to form a ribbon shape

title: Zöld-borsó (Green Peas)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- fresh parsley, finely chopped
- freshly shelled peas
- flour
- meat broth
- sugar
- salt
- filled sausages (for garnish)

## Directions

1. Melt butter in a pan and slowly stew the finely chopped fresh parsley and the cleaned shelled peas
2. Stir frequently to prevent browning
3. Sprinkle with a little flour
4. Once the flour is absorbed, pour in broth and add a piece of sugar
5. Garnish with filled sausages
6. To keep the green peas green, cook in an uncovered pot; once cooked, drain quickly and pour cold water over them
7. The sauce should be prepared separately: melt a little butter, add a small spoonful of flour and brown it, add finely chopped fresh parsley and fry for one minute, dilute with good meat broth and cook, add the green peas with two spoons of sugar, shake frequently but do not let it cook long so as not to lose color

title: Zöldborsó-leves (Green Pea Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- tender green peas in their pods
- butter
- fried bread slices
- 2 or 3 fried pike heads
- salt
- clear pea broth

## Directions

1. Braise tender green peas in their pods in butter until tender
2. Crush them together with fried bread slices and fried pike heads in a mortar
3. Place in a pot and pour good clear pea broth over it
4. Salt and cook well
5. Pass through a sieve and return to the pot
6. Bring to a boil again and serve over fried bread or fried bread cubes

title: Zöldborsóleves (Green Pea Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- butter the size of an egg
- 1 liter green peas
- green parsley, finely chopped
- a piece of sugar
- a little flour
- meat broth
- salt
- 1/4 liter sweet milk

## Directions

1. Put butter the size of an egg in a pot; when hot, add the washed green peas, finely chopped green parsley, and a piece of sugar
2. Stew covered over gentle heat
3. When soft, sprinkle a little flour on top and let it brown slightly
4. Thin with meat broth, season with salt
5. Add 1/4 liter of sweet milk

title: Zöldség-leves (Vegetable Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- One beautiful yellow cabbage
- One yellow carrot
- Lard, a small amount
- Meat broth
- One egg yolk
- Two tablespoons milk

## Directions

1. Cut the yellow cabbage and carrot into thick noodle-like pieces
2. Put a small amount of lard in a pot and when hot, add the carrot and let it braise slowly
3. When the carrot is halfway soft, add the cabbage and continue braising
4. When both are very soft, thin with meat broth and bring to a boil
5. Mix one egg yolk with two tablespoons of milk and pour the soup over it

title: Zöldséges pecsenye (Vegetable Roast)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram beef loin slice
- one spoon fat
- salt
- two decilitres vinegar
- one carrot, round-cut
- parsley root
- kohlrabi
- celery
- one onion
- several peppercorns
- four large potatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Place beef loin slice in a pot with one spoon fat, salt, and two decilitres vinegar
2. Add carrot, parsley root, kohlrabi, celery, one onion, and several peppercorns
3. Heat and cook until tender, always replenishing the liquid with a little cold water if it reduces
4. When the roast is soft, add four large potatoes, peeled and cut into slices, with enough water to cover them
5. Cover again and cook without stirring until the potatoes are soft and their fat is released
6. Cut the roast into slices and serve the vegetables and potatoes mixed together; this is a very tasty roast

title: Zöldségleves (Vegetable Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- common soup vegetables
- butter
- pea broth
- salt
- nutmeg
- four egg yolks
- two spoonfuls sour cream
- butter
- toasted breadcrumbs
- fried fish roe or pike liver

## Directions

1. Clean and wash common soup vegetables as on meat days.
2. Chop them as finely as possible.
3. Braise them in butter in a pot.
4. Mix with one liter of pea broth, salt, and nutmeg.
5. Before serving, beat four egg yolks, two spoonfuls of sour cream, and a piece of butter together.
6. Pour the hot broth over it and serve over toasted breadcrumb slices.
7. Top with sliced fried fish roe or pike liver if desired.

title: Zöldségleves (Vegetable Soup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- vegetables
- butter
- flour
- salt
- spices
- eggs
- sour cream
- bread

## Directions

1. For soup for six people, clean the vegetables well, chop finely
2. Melt a piece of butter, braise the vegetables with it, sprinkle in little flour, salt and season well, and let cook thoroughly
3. Mix together two egg yolks, little sour cream and a piece of butter, pour the soup over it, place on the fire and stir until it begins to thicken
4. Serve over fried bread pieces

title: Ánizs-pereez (Anise Pretzels)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams flour
- 10 dekagrams butter
- 14 dekagrams crushed sugar
- 1 lemon, finely chopped rind
- Few anise seeds
- 2 whole eggs
- Egg white for brushing

## Directions

1. Mix flour and butter on board with roller
2. Add crushed sugar, finely chopped lemon rind, and a few anise seeds
3. Beat 2 whole eggs on top
4. Assemble the dough
5. Form into pretzel shapes
6. Brush with egg white
7. Bake at gentle heat

title: Ánizscipó (Anise Roll)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagrams butter beaten to foam
- 10 egg yolks
- 14 dekagrams yeast dissolved in warm cream
- 2 deciliters lukewarm cream
- salt
- 2 tablespoons ground sugar
- flour as needed
- egg white
- coarsely broken anise and sugar for sprinkling
- sugar and finely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to foam and gradually mix in the egg yolks
2. Add the dissolved yeast and lukewarm cream
3. Add salt, ground sugar and enough flour so the dough is as thick as beaten egg noodle dough
4. Stir until it separates from the spoon, place in a warm spot and let it rise
5. Form small rolls on a fat-greased baking sheet
6. Brush with egg white and sprinkle thickly with coarsely broken anise and sugar, or with sugar and finely chopped almonds
7. Bake until golden

title: Árpaczukor (Barley Sugar)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen dekagrams barley
- two deciliters water
- one kilogram sugar
- almond oil

## Directions

1. Put fourteen dekagrams barley into two deciliters of water and bring to fire, cooking down to one cup
2. Strain through cloth onto one kilogram sugar
3. Cook the sugar down and pour onto a stone plate coated with almond oil
4. Let the sugar rest for a few minutes, then fold it together
5. Place on a board coated with almond oil
6. Cut into pieces 15-20 centimeters long with scissors
7. Pull lengthwise and place on sheet metal

title: Árpanyák (Barley Milk)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- barley grits
- water
- lemon juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Soak barley grits in water
2. Wash by hand and rub to remove the flour
3. Pour water over it and cook for two hours
4. Strain off the thin milk
5. Dilute the remainder by adding hot water
6. Add lemon juice and sugar

title: Ászpikot készíteni (Aspic)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram fresh pork skin, cleaned of all fat
- piece of veal knuckle
- 3 liters fresh water
- salt
- vegetables: root vegetables, red onion, carrot, kohlrabi, 2 heads white cabbage
- several peppercorns and cloves
- 3-4 deciliters strong vinegar
- 4 egg whites
- 1 whole egg in shell

## Directions

1. Rinse the pork skin several times in warm water and clean it of all fat
2. Place it in a pot with a piece of veal knuckle, pour 3 liters fresh water over it, add salt and vegetables
3. Clean off the scum frequently and simmer until half-cooked, then refill the pot with warm water
4. Continue boiling until the skins are so soft they can be pressed apart and the aspic is reduced to about 1.5 liters
5. In a separate pan, sauté sliced bacon, 1 kilogram soup meat, red onion, carrot, kohlrabi, and 2 heads white cabbage, all cut into slices, with several peppercorns and cloves
6. Cover and braise, stirring occasionally, until nicely browned without burning
7. Pour the liquid in which the skins were cooked into this, add 3-4 deciliters strong vinegar and any remaining pork skins not yet softened
8. Cook everything until reduced to about 1.5 liters
9. Beat 4 egg whites in a pot until foamy and beat in 1 whole egg with its shell
10. Pour the previously strained, fat-free, warm aspic into the eggs and beat well with a whisk during pouring
11. Let it stand quietly in a warm place for three hours
12. Strain slowly and pass through a stretched cloth
13. If not completely clear, strain once more
14. Cool it in a cold place and use as desired for meat dishes, mayonnaise, or preparation of cold foods

title: Édes omlós tészta (Sweet Crumbly Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fat
- sugar
- flour
- two decagrams of yeast
- four egg yolks
- ground cloves
- ground cinnamon
- milk
- jam

## Directions

1. Use equal amounts of fat and sugar, flour equal to the combined amount
2. Add two decagrams of yeast, four egg yolks, a knife-point of ground cloves, and twice as much ground cinnamon
3. Add milk and mix into a soft dough, working quickly
4. Spread half the dough thinly into a baking pan
5. Coat generously with jam
6. Roll the other half into finger-thick sticks
7. Arrange over the jam-covered dough
8. Bake in moderate heat until golden yellow
9. It is hard the first day but becomes softer the longer it stands, keeping 8-10 days
10. Slice as desired
11. Can also be filled with apple

title: Égő Pudding (Flaming Pudding)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one half grated rolls
- four and one half decilitres of good cream
- seven dekagrams of soaked finely ground almonds
- sugar
- seven dekagrams of butter
- three egg yolks and two whole eggs
- five dekagrams of citronât cut into large cubes
- preserved green walnuts, green plums, pitted cherries and cornelian cherries
- three egg whites
- rum for filling

## Directions

1. Pour four and one half decilitres of good cream over one and one half grated rolls
2. Add seven dekagrams of soaked finely ground almonds and enough sugar for sweetness
3. Cook to a paste with continuous stirring and cool
4. Mix in seven dekagrams of butter, three egg yolks and two whole eggs one after another
5. Add five dekagrams of citronât cut into large cubes, preserved green walnuts, green plums, pitted cherries and cornelian cherries in equal amounts and as much as so the roll is not overly noticeable
6. Finally fold in a little stiff white of three eggs
7. Put the pudding in a buttered ring mould and steam for one hour
8. Turn out when serving and at that moment fill the hollow in the pudding with good rum, add a few sugar lumps, set alight and serve quickly flaming
9. This paste is especially suitable for suppers
10. Arrange the bottom of the mould attractively with half-moon shaped citronât pieces and soaked almonds
11. Serve with orange juice sweetened with a little sugar or wine sauce

title: Égő Tészta (Burning Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 deciliters hot milk
- 4 deciliters fine flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the flour into the hot milk with continuous stirring so it does not become lumpy
2. Add a little salt, smooth it out, and when the dough separates from the spoon, set it aside and fry it
3. Once the dough has cooled, pound it fine in a clean mortar with 6 whole eggs
4. Press it from a pastry bag into hot fat and fry until a beautiful light brown color

title: Élesztős Kifli Ánizszsal (Yeast Croissant with Anise)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 280 grams flour
- 210 grams butter
- salt
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- 36 grams cheese yeast dissolved in milk
- 5 tablespoons sour cream
- small raisins
- raisins
- chopped almond
- sugar
- citron peel
- egg white
- sugar rice
- anise

## Directions

1. Layer in 280 grams flour 210 grams butter leaf by leaf, smooth it well with a roller on a board, and salt it
2. Prepare the dough with 1 whole egg, 2 egg yolks, 36 grams cheese yeast dissolved in milk, and 5 tablespoons sour cream
3. Roll out twice and cut into rectangular leaves
4. Fill with small raisins, raisins, chopped almond, sugar and citron peel
5. Fold together, brush with egg white, place on a pan and let rise slowly
6. Bake in the oven at gentle heat
7. Prepare sugared rice to sprinkle on top, sprinkle with anise, and put back in the oven to dry

title: Élesztős Kádkák Mákkal (Yeast Kádkák with Poppy)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 grams butter
- 5 egg yolks
- salt
- 1 tablespoon ground sugar
- 36 grams melted fresh yeast
- 4 deciliters lukewarm sour cream
- flour
- 2 deciliters finely ground poppy
- 1 lemon rind finely chopped
- knife-tip amount of cinnamon
- 120 grams sugar
- half coffee cup sour cream

## Directions

1. Whip 100 grams butter until foamy and mix in 5 egg yolks, little salt, 1 tablespoon ground sugar, 36 grams melted fresh yeast and 4 deciliters lukewarm sour cream
2. When all is well mixed, add so much flour that the dough is rather soft than hard and stir until it pulls away from the spoon
3. Mix together 2 deciliters finely ground poppy with 1 lemon rind chopped finely, knife-tip of cinnamon and 120 grams sugar, and cook this with half a coffee cup sour cream
4. Butter the kádkák molds, put some dough in them, fill with the poppy mixture, cover again with dough, not letting the molds be completely full
5. Let rise in a warm place and bake nicely
6. When baked, turn out onto a plate and sprinkle with sugar

title: Élesztős Szeletek (Yeast Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 dekagrams butter
- 1 whole egg
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 dekagrams yeast
- 6 tablespoons lukewarm cream
- 7 dekagrams powdered sugar
- 25 dekagrams flour
- salt
- melted butter
- finely chopped almonds
- coarsely broken sugar
- marmalade

## Directions

1. Beat 14 dekagrams butter until foamy, then slowly add 1 whole egg and 4 egg yolks
2. Dissolve 2 dekagrams yeast in 6 tablespoons lukewarm cream and fold in gradually
3. Mix in 7 dekagrams powdered sugar, 25 dekagrams flour and a little salt
4. Spread the mixture evenly on a long baking sheet brushed with melted butter using a large knife
5. Let rise, then brush with beaten egg, sprinkle with finely chopped almonds and coarsely broken sugar
6. Bake until beautifully golden yellow, then cut into rectangular pieces and fill with marmalade of your choice

title: Élesztős írós-tészta (Yeast Butter Dough)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram fine flour
- 6 whole eggs
- 30 grams yeast
- 2 tablespoons sugar powder
- 1 teaspoon salt
- warm milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix together one kilogram fine flour, 6 whole eggs, 30 grams yeast, 2 tablespoons sugar powder, and one teaspoon salt
2. Add enough warm (but not hot) milk so the dough is neither too stiff nor too soft
3. Knead well and let rise slightly
4. When the dough begins to spread, stretch with a rolling pin to about one finger-thickness
5. Brush with good butter
6. Fold in the four corners so that the four corners meet in the middle
7. Let rest again for 10-15 minutes
8. After rest, stretch again and brush with butter
9. Finally, after it rises slightly, stretch one more time to barely a finger-thickness
10. Cut short strips, barely one span long and two fingers wide from the dough
11. Each strip should be brushed with egg and sprinkled with almonds
12. Place curved butter dough on a baking sheet or flat pan and bake immediately at normal heat without rising, for this way it will be nicely layered

title: Ívek vajas tésztából (Wafers from Puff Pastry)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry folded five times
- egg white
- soaked almond, chopped
- vanilla sugar, finely ground
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare puff pastry folded five times
2. Roll it out to knife-thickness
3. Cut into strips a span long and two fingers wide
4. Brush with egg white
5. Sprinkle with soaked chopped almond and finely ground vanilla sugar
6. Bake on a wafer form until golden
7. Before cooling, sprinkle with sugar and serve on the table
8. In the same way from the puff pastry cut long, narrow sticks
9. Brush these also with egg white, sprinkle with almond and vanilla sugar
10. Bake on a pan, sprinkle with sugar while hot, and pile high on the plate

title: Ökörfark (Oxtail)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- oxtail
- salt
- red onion
- carrot
- lemon zest
- peppercorns
- bay leaves
- thyme
- clove pepper
- vinegar
- water
- melted fat
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut the oxtail into thin pieces
2. Wash it well with warm water and salt it
3. Place it in a pot with red onion, carrot, lemon zest, several peppercorns, bay leaf, thyme, and clove pepper
4. Fill the pot with vinegar and water and cook the oxtail until it is half soft, then remove the fat
5. Brown two spoonfuls of flour in melted fat and dilute it with the above sauce, which you have first strained
6. Add the oxtail with three spoonfuls of sour cream and braise until completely tender
7. This can also be served in a puff pastry vol-au-vent

title: Öntött perecz (Soaked Pretzels)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- six dry anise-free pretzels or same amount of horns
- hot salted water
- seven dekagrams pork fat or butter
- two decilitres sour cream
- crumbled salty cheese
- salt

## Directions

1. Break six dry anise-free pretzels or the same amount of horns into pieces or small slices
2. Pour hot salted water over them in a pot
3. Then cover and immediately strain off the water, leaving them covered in their own steam for quarter of an hour
4. Meanwhile, melt seven dekagrams of pork fat or butter, pour in two decilitres of sour cream, pour this over the pretzels, sprinkle with crumbled salty cheese, and shake the whole thing well together

title: Öszibaraczk Párban (Peaches in Syrup)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful ripe but not soft garden peaches
- sugar
- water
- 9 dekagrams sugar per jar

## Directions

1. Peel beautiful ripe but not soft garden peaches thinly and cut them in half
2. Arrange them neatly in jars so that each peach half falls on top of the already placed one, until the jars are filled to the neck
3. Calculate 9 dekagrams sugar per jar and cook with enough fresh water to fill the prepared jars, then cool and pour over
4. Arrange and bind as described in instructions 961, let boil for 2 minutes only
5. Once the peaches appear soft, do not cool them in the basin but quickly wrap the jars in warmed cloths and set aside
6. Once completely cooled, clean and store as with the others

title: Öszibaraczk-szeletek (Peach Slices)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Sponge cake batter
- 10 dekagrams blanched, dried, and finely chopped almonds
- 10 dekagrams crushed sugar
- Well-ripened, peeled peaches
- Chocolate icing

## Directions

1. Prepare sponge cake batter and spread on buttered baking sheet two knife-widths thick
2. Bake slowly at gentle heat
3. When the sponge cake has stood in the oven for a few minutes and a skin has formed, thickly sprinkle with sugared almonds without removing the sheet completely from the oven
4. Continue baking until golden yellow
5. Remove and cut immediately with sharp thin knife into pieces the size of playing cards
6. Peel from the sheet
7. Cook well-ripened, peeled peaches with equal amount of crushed sugar into marmalade
8. Coat half of the slices with marmalade one knife-width thick
9. Place the other half on top
10. Draw black stripes one finger width wide on four sides with chocolate icing
11. When dry, arrange neatly on serving plate

title: Öszibaraczkszalat (Peach Paste)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- so-called quince-peach fruits, well ripened
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Select so-called quince-peaches that are well ripened
2. Peel and halve them
3. If sufficiently softened, push through a sieve; if very firm, cook for a few minutes first
4. For half a kilogram of peaches, use equal amount of sugar with two deciliters water in a preserving pan, cook until it strings and throws large bubbles while boiling
5. Add the peaches, whose juice has been well drained, stirring them in
6. Cook well over charcoal with continuous stirring, then cool
7. Form into balls the size of half an egg
8. Place on white paper thickly sprinkled with sugar, sprinkle with fine sugar, and let stand in a dry, warm place for several days
9. Press flat, sprinkle with sugar again, and when dried, roll thinner each day, repeatedly sprinkling with sugar, until the pieces are wafer-sized
10. When fine and dry, layer in rolls, sprinkle with sugar, and store in a dry place

title: Öszibaraezk-kompót (Autumn Apricot Compote)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight apricots
- Approximately ten dekagrams sugar
- Half a glass of water
- Rum

## Directions

1. Boil the apricots in hot water for one minute, then place them in cold water
2. When cooled, peel them, cut them in half, and remove the pits
3. Cook approximately ten dekagrams of sugar with half a glass of water for eight apricots
4. Once the sugar has dissolved, add the apricots and cook until they absorb the sugar on both sides
5. Arrange them neatly in a cup and continue cooking the syrup
6. Add a coffee spoon of good rum to it
7. When the syrup begins to thicken, pour it over the apricots and place the whole dish on ice or in a cellar

title: Ötvözött metélt (Blended Noodles)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Two eggs
- Fine noodle dough
- One liter hot milk
- Five dekagrams butter
- Four deciliters cream
- Four egg yolks
- Sugar
- Vanilla

## Directions

1. Make fine noodle dough from two eggs and roll it out
2. Cut wide noodles from it and cook in one liter hot milk
3. When sufficiently cooked, remove from heat and beat in five dekagrams butter
4. Place in a baking dish lined with butter
5. Pour four deciliters cream mixed with four egg yolks over it
6. Place in oven and bake until light golden yellow
7. Sprinkle well with sugar and vanilla before serving

title: Özcomb Sülve (Roasted Venison Haunch)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch
- salt
- bacon for larding
- vinegar
- water
- cloves
- red onion
- carrot slices
- lemon peel
- sour cream
- flour

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the venison haunch well, remove the membrane, salt it
2. Lard it neatly and cover with brine made of 1 part boiled vinegar and 3 parts boiled water with cloves, red onion, carrot slices, lemon peel, and salt
3. Thread the roast onto a spit and baste it frequently with the brine during roasting, which is placed in a pan beneath the roast so the bacon fat drips into it
4. When it is slowly roasted through, make the sauce: whip 4 tablespoons sour cream with a spoonful of flour, then mix it with the brine until it is thick
5. Baste the roast several more times with this sauce, put the small amount of remaining sauce in the dish, and place the roast on top

title: Özhát Sülve (Roasted Venison Back)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- venison back
- vinegar
- red onion
- bacon for larding
- paper
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare it in exactly the same way as the haunch
2. Marinate it with vinegar and red onion, and let it lie in the brine for one or two days
3. Remove the skin, lard it with bacon, wrap it in paper, and roast it slowly
4. Baste it several times with the brine and sour cream
5. Serve it on the dish in the same way as the venison haunch

title: Özhát Vagdalék (Venison Loin Hash)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted venison loin or haunch
- bacon slices
- carrot slices
- onion slices
- cloves
- flour
- brown broth
- lemon zest
- good sour cream
- capers
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Remove meat from bones and cut into large cubes
2. Place bacon, carrot, and onion slices and a few cloves in a pot, add the venison bones on top
3. Cover and braise until nicely light brown
4. Sprinkle with two small tablespoons flour and brown
5. Add a ladleful of brown broth, some lemon zest, and a coffee cup of good sour cream
6. Cook well and pass through a sieve
7. Add one tablespoon capers and the minced meat
8. Cook with frequent stirring until the sauce thickens well
9. Pile in the center of the dish and surround with potato rounds

title: Ürüczomb franczia módra (Lamb Leg French Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb leg
- salt
- green parsley
- onion
- thyme
- pepper
- bacon
- thread
- brown broth
- onions
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Remove the bone from the leg and trim the fat from the skin, then salt it
2. Chop green parsley, onion, and thyme finely, add pepper, and prepare strips of bacon, coating them in this mixture
3. Make holes in the leg with a sharp knife and lard it with bacon, then wrap it completely with thread so the bacon does not fall out
4. Place parsley, onion, and several peppercorns in a pot, add the leg, fill with brown broth, cover and braise until completely tender
5. Remove the leg, strain the sauce and boil it until only one or two spoonfuls remain
6. Return the leg to the sauce and turn it several times in the small amount of sauce
7. Chop six or seven onions coarsely, melt a piece of butter and place the onions in it with pepper, fry until golden
8. Sprinkle flour over and brown it, add several spoonfuls of broth so it does not burn, add another spoonful of broth and cook for half an hour longer
9. Place the sauce on the serving plate and the leg on top

title: Ürüczomb Paradicsommártással (Young Lamb Leg with Tomato Sauce)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- young lamb leg
- bacon
- red onion, sliced
- thyme
- ivy leaf
- water
- vinegar
- 24-30 tomatoes
- flour
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Place the lamb leg in a copper pan lined with bacon, red onion slices, thyme, and ivy leaf
2. Pour water and vinegar over it
3. Braise until tender
4. Once the liquid has reduced, add the tomatoes
5. Cover and cook until very soft
6. Remove the meat
7. Sprinkle flour into the liquid and brown it
8. Pour good meat broth over it
9. Pass through a sieve over the meat
10. Boil together repeatedly

title: Ürüczomb rizszsel (Lamb Leg with Rice)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful lamb leg
- salt
- anchovy fillets
- thyme
- marjoram
- half kilogram rice
- salted water
- 42 dekagrams bacon
- thread

## Directions

1. Clean the leg thoroughly of its membrane, peel the meat from the bone, trim it slightly, salt it, make holes with a narrow knife and lard with anchovy that has been well soaked and cleaned of bones
2. Take equal quantities of thyme and marjoram, rub the leg well with these, place in a wooden vessel, cover with a fitted lid, place a stone on top and leave in a cool place throughout the whole day
3. The next day cook half kilogram rice in salted water until the grains burst
4. Cut 42 dekagrams bacon into fine slices and line the pot with it, place half the rice on it and cover the rice with bacon slices
5. Put the lid on the pot and seal it well with dough, braise for three hours at even heat and place a little coals on the lid
6. After three hours open the pot, remove the bacon, strain its fat and turn the whole thing out onto a large plate

title: Ürügerinez Körülrakva (Roast Saddle of Venison Surrounded)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- one saddle of venison
- twine
- carrots
- lettuce
- beef stock
- salt
- pepper
- sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Tie the venison saddle with twine to maintain its shape and roast it.
2. Arrange it on the plate and surround it with carrots and lettuce.
3. Cut the carrots into equal balls, wash them, and braise them with beef stock, salt, pepper, and sugar.
4. Wash the lettuce, clean away the stems, braise with butter, crush with a knife, and arrange in squares on the plate.

title: Ürühús Juhászosan Főzve (Mutton Cooked Shepherd Style)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton meat
- Salt
- Paprika
- Water

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into beautiful pieces
2. In a kettle or pot, add salt and paprika proportional to the amount of meat, then add the meat and water so it does not completely cover it
3. Do not stir with a spoon, but shake the pot and turn over several times while cooking

title: Őszi baraczk hálóköntösben (Autumn Apricot in Nightgown)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- 28 dekagram butter for buttered pastry
- Large autumn apricots
- Finely ground sugar
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Prepare buttered pastry with 28 dekagram butter, fold it three times
2. Roll out to the thickness of a knife blade
3. Cut into equal rectangles with a warm knife
4. Peel large autumn apricots, halve them, remove the pit
5. Dip the apricot halves in finely ground sugar
6. Place in the center of the cut pastry
7. Brush the four corners with egg white
8. Fold up, press together and bake nicely
9. While still warm, sprinkle with sugar
10. For preserved apricots: remove from the jar a quarter hour before, strain the juice well
11. When placing in the pastry center, dip in ground sugar
12. Cook the preserved juice with sugar until thick
13. When the pastry is baked, pour through the small holes that form during baking using a coffee spoon
14. Sprinkle with sugar and serve quickly and hot to the table

title: Őszi baraczk-fagylalt (Autumn Peach Ice Cream)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- fully ripe, very juicy autumn peaches
- sugar
- half a coffee cup Tokaji wine
- 56 dekagrams strained peach pulp
- 42 dekagrams sugar
- 1 liter water
- juice of 4 lemons
- elderflower liqueur

## Directions

1. Peel completely ripe autumn peaches and cut into segments
2. Coat well with sugar in a deep bowl
3. Pour half a coffee cup of Tokaji wine over them
4. Let stand for 3-4 hours and press through a sieve
5. Cook 56 dekagrams strained peach pulp with 42 dekagrams sugar and 1 liter water until it forms large drops
6. Pour this into the peach juice
7. Press in the juice of 4 lemons
8. Dissolve a little elderflower liqueur in it for a pale red color
9. Pour into a freezing container

title: Őszibaraczk-Marmelád (Peach Jam)
author: Rézi néni
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- water
- sugar, same amount of sugar as peaches, half kilogram

## Directions

1. Place the peaches in boiling water, bring to a boil several times, remove the skins, remove the pit, press through a sieve, and prepare the sugar as you would for other fruits.
2. Add the sieved peaches to the clarified sugar, mix well with it, and cook slowly until a drop tested on a plate remains firm.
